### 2.4.2.1 Power Saving Bandwidth Part Switch Control

#### 2.4.2.1.0-2 Power Saving Bandwidth Part Switch Control Overview of Power Saving Bandwidth Part switch state machine (ID: 11153110)

**2.4.2.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11153115`)

Power Saving Bandwidth Part Switch Control
When a UE is configured with at least one power saving bandwidth part (PS BWP), L2-PS shall control the bandwidth part switching (between Power saving BWP and larger BWP) dynamically using DCI 1\_1. To begin UE always starts from larger BWP and algorithm to monitor the BWP switch activity shall get triggered upon receiving PS BWP usage information from CP-RT during PsUser\_UserModifyReq + PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq OR PsUser\_UserSetupReq as explained in 5G\_L2\_9844\_replaced([11153158](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24sQm_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

UE state transitions within Large and Power saving BWP is as follows

---

#### 2.4.2.1.0-3 Power Saving Bandwidth Part Switch Control State machine to control UE's Bandwidth Part switching (ID: 11153121)

**2.4.2.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11153146`)

Power Saving Bandwidth Part Switch Control
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two state machine diagrams, one for "DL Sch EO" (Downlink Scheduling Execution Order) and another for "UL Sch EO" (Uplink Scheduling Execution Order). Both diagrams illustrate the states and transitions of a User Equipment (UE) in relation to Bandwidth Part (BWP) switching and HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) feedback.
**DL Sch EO Diagram:**
This diagram is enclosed in a light blue background.
\* **States:**
\* BWP Default State
\* BWP Recovery State
\* Evaluation State
\* Wait for DL & UL HARQ Free
\* Wait for UL HARQ Free
\* Wait for DL HARQ Free
\* Ready for DCI Tx
\* Wait for BWP Switch Complete
\* **Transitions and Conditions:**
\* From "BWP Default State" to "Evaluation State" if "UE can support Power saving BWP".
\* From "BWP Recovery State" to "Evaluation State".
\* From "Evaluation State" to "BWP Recovery State".
\* From "Evaluation State" to "Wait for DL & UL HARQ Free" if "UE eligible for BWP switch and DL HARQ not free".
\* From "Evaluation State" to "Wait for UL HARQ Free" if "UE eligible for BWP switch and DL HARQ free".
\* From "Wait for DL & UL HARQ Free" to "Wait for UL HARQ Free" if "UE is with DL HARQ free".
\* From "Wait for DL & UL HARQ Free" to "Wait for DL HARQ Free" if "UE is with UL HARQ free".
\* From "Wait for UL HARQ Free" to "Ready for DCI Tx".
\* From "Wait for DL HARQ Free" to "Ready for DCI Tx" if "UE is with DL HARQ free".
\* From "Ready for DCI Tx" to "Wait for BWP Switch Complete" after "transmitting BWP Switch DCI".
\* From "Wait for BWP Switch Complete" to "Evaluation State" if "DTX received in HARQ Feedback of Switch DCI".
\* From "Wait for BWP Switch Complete" to "Evaluation State" if "NACK received in HARQ Feedback of Switch DCI" and "UE successfully moved to Target BWP".
\* There's also a transition from "Evaluation State" to "Wait for UL HARQ Free" with the condition "UE eligible for BWP switch send Free HARQ and Stop Scheduling to UL Sch".
**UL Sch EO Diagram:**
This diagram is enclosed in a light green background.
\* **States:**
\* BWP Default State
\* BWP Recovery State
\* Evaluation State
\* Wait for UL HARQ Free
\* Waiting for completion
\* **Transitions and Conditions:**
\* From "BWP Default State" to "Evaluation State" if "UE can support Power saving BWP".
\* From "BWP Recovery State" to "Evaluation State".
\* From "Evaluation State" to "BWP Recovery State" with the condition "resumeScheduling OnNewBwp".
\* From "Evaluation State" to "Wait for UL HARQ Free" with the condition "Free HARQ and Stop Scheduling received from DL Sch".
\* From "Wait for UL HARQ Free" to "Evaluation State" if "UE is with UL HARQ free Send BWP HARQ free To DL Sch".
\* From "Wait for UL HARQ Free" to "Waiting for completion" if "UE is with UL HARQ free".
\* From "Waiting for completion" to "Evaluation State" with the condition "Resume scheduling received from DLSch UE Successfully moved to Target BWP".
\* There's an initial transition from outside the diagram to "BWP Recovery State" with the condition "stopScheduling ForBwpRematch".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1887.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

#### 2.4.2.1.1 UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch

#### 2.4.2.1.1-1 UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch

**2.4.2.1.1-1.0-3**  (ID: `11153258`)

UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch
1. BWP default state (No BWP Evaluation) : This is the default state. Same conditions as per DL Scheduler apply to this state handling also.

2.  Evaluation state (Evaluation for BWP Switch) : This is the state which maintains the UE in normal scheduling conditions in a given BWP. BWP switch evaluation happens at DL Scheduler and UE maintains this state until there is a trigger from DL Scheduler to free the UL HARQ transmissions using DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate.BWPSwitchUpdate with request Type as "Free HARQ and Stop Scheduling". Such request shall change the UE to next state " Waiting for UL HARQ Free "

1. Waiting for UL HARQ Free : Moved to this state if UE is found eligible for BWP switch and UL Scheduler has received DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate.BWPSwitchUpdate with request Type as "Free HARQ and Stop Scheduling". Here in this state the UE is blocked for new UL HARQ transmissions and checked for any pending UL HARQ retransmissions. A maximum of 64 slots are considered as wait period to clear all pending UL HARQ retransmissions. Beyond this wait period, the UE frees pending UL HARQ IDs. Once all the UL HARQ pending retransmissions are cleared, the UL Scheduler shall send UlToDlIntraSchedUpdate.BWPSwitchHARQFree by adding this CRNTI and change the state to "Waiting for completion".
2. Waiting for completion : UL scheduler waits in this state until it receives DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate.BWPSwitchUpdate with request Type as "Resume Scheduling in Large BWP" or "Resume Scheduling in PS BWP". Upon such message, its confirmed that the UE has now moved to the Target BWP and UL scheduler can resume new transmissions getting scheduled in same way as done in DL after updating UE context with new BWP ID. Now the UE is moved to evaluation state.

Steps 5 and 6 are applicable only if NRBTS/NRCELL.actInactiveBwpScanning is set to TRUE (When BWP scanning is enabled to help UE recover from possible BWP mismatch situation)

1. UL Sched EO monitors UE’s response to grants issued for SR requests. When 5 such consecutive UL grants are DTXed, it implies the UE could may not be in the BWP what gNB assumes it to be. At this stage, UE state is moved to BWP recovery state UL Sched EO sends the message stopSchedulingForBwpRematch to DL Sched EO and UE’s BWP is updated with the target BWP. gNB issues UL DCI 0\_1 to monitor PUSCH over this new target BWP (gNB assumed inactive BWP).
2. Upon receiving 5 non DTX responses from UE towards the UL grants issued in response to SR, UL Sched EO assumes the UE to be in target BWP. This step implies the gNB and the UE are now aligned with respect the UE’s BWP. UL Sched EO sends the message resumeSchedulingOnNewBwp to DL Sched EO and UE is moved out of BWP recovery state.

[03147365]

When BWP scanning is enabled(NRBTS/NRCELL.actInactiveBwpScanning is set to TRUE) and UE is under BWP scanning procedure, if L2-PS recevies MAC control element , C-RNTI for the UE. Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16104\_replaced([11111313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpMPyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

UL sched EO sends message resumeSchedulingOnActiveBwp to DL Sched EO. This indicates reception of MAC control element, C-RNTI for UE and L2PS has to roll back to source BWP(at start of BWP scanning) and stop/abort BWP scanning procedure.

[End 03147365]

---

##### 2.4.2.1.1.0-1 UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch Default state (ID: 11153158)

**2.4.2.1.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11153169`)

UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch
1. BWP default state (No BWP Evaluation) : This is the default state. The UE stays in this state till the time CP-RT indicates the usage of Power Saving Bandwidth Part (PS BWP) using bwpConfig (5G\_L2\_IF\_408\_replaced([10975247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_3Dfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) within PsUser\_UserModifyReq + PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq OR PsUser\_UserSetupReq as explained below.

(i) In case of Initial Context Setup (ICS) and Intra DU handover cases, L2-PS receives PsUser\_UserModifyReq containing bwpConfig to indicate PS BWP usage or ' 2 bit ' BWP indicator usage in the DCI for the UE. Moves to the next state "Evaluation for BWP Switch" after the reception of PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq to indicate the RRC Reconfiguration Complete from the UE side.

(ii) In case of Inter DU or Inter gNB handover cases the L2 PS receives PsUser\_UserSetupReq containing bwpConfig to indicate PS BWP usage or ' 2 bit ' BWP indicator usage in the DCI for the UE. Moves to the next state "Evaluation for BWP Switch" [Before 03573400] immediately [03573400] once the RACH procedure is completed. [End 03573400].

---

##### 2.4.2.1.1.0-2 UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch Evaluation state (ID: 11153176)

**2.4.2.1.1.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11153202`)

UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch
2. Evaluation state (Evaluation for BWP Switch) : This is the state which evaluates the timing and other conditions for the BWP switch to be triggered in either of the directions(Larger to PS BWP OR PS to Larger BWP). First time initiation of this state will be with the UE in larger BWP and L2-PS shall start UE level timer with value measurementDurationToPsBwp. Next iterations are those instances when the UE completes the BWP switch and enters Evaluation state. Here the timer shall be restarted with value equal to measurementDurationFromPsBwp when the UE enters PS BWP OR measurementDurationToPsBwp when the UE enters larger BWP. Evaluation is performed using conditions mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1870\_replaced([11111163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when UE is in PS BWP and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1872\_replaced([11111200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when UE is in large BWP. Evaluation criteria decides if the UE requires a BWP switch.

Along with the evalution criteria mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1870\_replaced([11111163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(when UE is in PS BWP) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1872\_replaced([11111200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(when UE is in large BWP), an additional check is applied such that

* UE shall not be in RRC Reconfiguration Transient phase and should have completed the RRC Reconfguration procedure, to be considered eligeable to preform BWP switch.

If the UE is within the large BWP, and it fulfills the evaluation criteria as per conditions mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1872\_replaced([11111200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), an additional check is applied such that

If ( UE is configured with at least one VoNR flow )

If ( ( Existing number VoNR UEs in PS BWP +  *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving ) <* maxNumberVoNRPowerSaving )\*\*

&& ( Existing number UEs in PS BWP +  *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving ) <* maxNumberUesPowerSaving ) )\*\*

Evaluation for moving to PS BWP is successful

Else

Evaluation for moving to PS BWP has failed. UE to continue in large BWP with measurements for the next cycle.

Else

If ( Existing number UEs in PS BWP +  *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving ) <* maxNumberUesPowerSaving\*\*

Evaluation for moving to PS BWP is successful

Else

Evaluation for moving to PS BWP has failed. UE to continue in large BWP with measurements for the next cycle.

If evaluation criteria are successful either for large to PS BWP or PS to large BWP switch, below steps (i) to (iv) are followed

(i) If the UE is moving from large to PS BWP, increment the count *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving* *and increment the count* TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving if the UE is configured with at least one VoNR flow\*\*\*\*

(ii) Change state to "Waiting for UL and DL HARQ Free" if all DL HARQ processes are not free OR change state to "Waiting for UL HARQ Free" if all DL HARQ processes are free.

(iii) Include this UE in  to inform UL scheduler to free up all HARQ Processes for this CRNTI (by sending request type as "Free HARQ and Stop Scheduling" for particular CRNTI). UL SCH to reply back in  by adding this particular CRNTI once all UL HARQ are free. UL scheduler to stop scheduling for this UE after freeing up all HARQ processes. 

(iv) Stop UE level evaluation timer

**[CB007880-C]**

If UE is already in PS BWP which is smaller than C\_srs for SRS positioning (5G\_UP\_ALG\_27064\_replaced([11107865](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kojWmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when precise positioning is to be configured,5G-L2-PS shall switch the UE's BWP to the dedicated BWP that is higher than C\_srs when UL BWP switch from power saving BWP.

**[End CB007880-C]**

[CNI-130786]

If evaluation criteria are successful either for large to PS BWP or PS to large BWP switch as [11153131](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showArtifactPage&artifactURI=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fresources%2FBI_24sQmPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ&oslc.configuration=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), as stated in[12702850](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BX8XQF44EfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), 5G-L2-PS shall intentionally add extra bits to the FDRA size of the M first repetitions of BWP switch DCI (where M is rdTestModeBwpSwitchFailureRepetitions: [12702888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_rIbgwF46EfCLrcnPCspVyQ)).   
Note, maximum repetitions of BWP switch DCI is capped by rdNumberOfBwpSwitchRepetitions. Here for CNI, it needs to malform FDRA size upto rdTestModeBwpSwitchFailureRepetitions times.  
Expectation, if  rdTestModeBwpSwitchFailureRepetitions < rdNumberOfBwpSwitchRepetitions , then bwp switch should be successful.  
If rdTestModeBwpSwitchFailureRepetitions >= rdNumberOfBwpSwitchRepetitions, then BWP switch should fail as depicted in CNI document. And bwp scanning and bwp recovery procedure should follow.

About extra bits to the FDRA size, add three extra bytes in calculate FDRA size. Expectation UE will not be able to decode DCI FDRA size is malfomed.

[End CNI-130786]

Note :

1. *rdSwitchTimertoPsBwp and* rdSwitchTimerFromPsBwp are used to maintain UEs for minimum durations within larger and PS BWP respectively. This period is also referred to as hysteresis duration in the given BWP.\*\*
2. *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving depicts the number of UEs which are currently moving from Larger to PS BWP after their evaluation criteria was fulfilled and not yet switched into PS BWP. Such UEs are tracked to allow their BWP transition during HARQ freeing and other states involved before they finally enter PS BWP.*
3. The 5QI 1/5QI**-65/5QI-66 enables the support of IMS conversational voice services on NR. Hence 5QI 15QI**-65/5QI-66 **is referred to as VoNR flow and UEs configured with at least one VoNR flow are considered as VoNR UEs.**\*\*\*\*
4. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1872\_replaced([11111200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) limits the number of VoNR UEs (UEs with at least one VoNR flow) entering PS BWP upto maxNumberVoNRPowerSaving. Inline with this condition, UEs during their transition into PS BWP need to be differentiated based on thier VoNR and non-VoNR criteria. *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving count considers combined total number of VoNR and Non-VoNR UEs.* TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving depicts the number of UEs which are configured with at least one VoNR flow and are currently moving from Larger to PS BWP after their evaluation criteria was fulfilled and not yet switched into PS BWP.\*\*

5.When UE is in Large BWP and has any SCell active then evaluation shall not started if possible or fail. Evaluation shall be failed at the earliest possible phase. UE is treated as not requiring BWP switch, hence restart of evaluation time shall be done.

1. This Requirement Id of BWP switching is applicable between PS and any of the dedicated BWP which can be either Large or Mid or Small BWP.

---

##### 2.4.2.1.1.0-3 UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch Other states (ID: 11153207)

**2.4.2.1.1.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `13455498`)

UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch
3. Waiting for UL and DL HARQ Free : Moved to this state if the UE is found eligible for BWP switch with both UL and DL HARQ pending retransmissions. After all the pending DL Retransmissions are cleared, the UE moves to the next state "Waiting for UL HARQ Free".

1. Waiting for UL HARQ Free : Moved to this state if UE is found eligible for BWP switch with only UL HARQ pending retransmissions.
2. Waiting for DL HARQ Free : Moved to this state if UE is found eligible for BWP switch with only DL HARQ pending retransmissions. A maximum of 64 slots are considered as wait period to clear all pending DL HARQ retransmissions. Beyond this wait period, the UE frees pending DL HARQ IDs and moves to the next state 'Ready for DCI Tx'

Note : In all the states when UE is waiting to clear the HARQ retransmissions (DL / UL / Both DL and UL) addition of UE in CS1 list to be skipped if UE's candidate List Priority index maps to NewTx. This allows UE to block scheduling any new transmissions and to continue only pending retransmissions.

1. Ready for DCI Tx : Moved to this state if the UE has no pending retransmissions for both UL and DL HARQs. When UE gets added in CS1 list from the candidate list, the TD scheduling checks the UE state to be "Ready for DCI Tx" and UE becomes eligible to get a DCI for BWP Switch. If BWP Switch DCI is scheduled, TD scheduler will change the Ue state to "Waiting for BWP Switch Completion" and will inform pre scheduler with "Scheduling Done" event adds an entry in updateList which leads to adding the UE in priority list post current scheduling.
2. Waiting for BWP Switch Completion(N + K0\_PS\_BWP + K1 period) : This state indicates that the UE has been scheduled a DCI 1\_1 in order to perform the BWP switch and gNB is awaiting a response from the UE to confirm the switch completion. During this period, UE remains in the candidate list and not added in the CS1 List for traffic scheduling. A maximum of 64 slots are considered as wait period to clear pending DL HARQ retransmission for the DCI switch transmission. Beyond this wait period, the UE frees pending HARQ IDs and moves to the previous state 'Ready for DCI Tx'.

Upon receiving HARQ feedback during the current state,

(i) If DTX or ACK received, change state to "Ready for DCI Tx". As normal DTX process, UE shall be scheduled another DCI attempting the BWP switch again. If this continues multiple times and UE does not switch the BWP, eventually RLF gets triggered after continuous DTX (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3350\_replaced([11151610](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksai1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_IF\_530\_replaced([10977521](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VNSY_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In order to avoid useless interaction with ReTx L2Ps internal mechanism all retransmitted DCI for BWP switch will be set as fresh Tx with new harqId

(ii) If NACK received, it indicates successful BWP switch and UE acknowledging the DCI switch information it received earlier. L2 PS shall perform the steps (a) to (e)

(a) If the UE has moved from Large to PS BWP, decrement the count *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving and decrement the count* TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving if the UE is configured with at least one VoNR flow\*\*

(b) If the UE has moved from Large to PS BWP, increment the count for number active UEs in PS BWP

(c) Update UE context to new BWP retaining the UE in the candidate list

(d) Indicate UL scheduler to resume scheduling on the new BWP by sending DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate.BWPSwitchUpdate request type as "Resume Scheduling in BWP Large BWP" or "Resume Scheduling in BWP PS BWP" containing the UE CRNTI

(e) Move the UE to "Evaluation state"

Steps 8 and 9 are applicable only if NRBTS/NRCELL.actInactiveBwpScanning is set to TRUE (When BWP scanning is enabled to help UE recover from possible BWP mismatch situation)

1. DL Sched EO receives the message stopSchedulingForBwpRematch from UL Sched EO. This indicates UE has failed to use(DTXed) 5 consecutive UL grants issued in response to SRs. Upon message reception, DL Sched moves the UE to BWP recovery state and suspends DL scheduling.
2. DL Sched EO receives the message resumeSchedulingOnNewBwp from UL Sched EO. This indicates UE has responded after the BWP switch was done internally at the gNB and PUSCH grants on the updated BWP were non DTX. This step implies the gNB and the UE are now aligned with respect the UE’s BWP. UE is now moved out of BWP recovery state, DL scheduling is resumed and UE’s BWP is updated with the target BWP.

When BWP scanning is enabled(NRBTS/NRCELL.actInactiveBwpScanning is set to TRUE) and UE is under BWP scanning procedure, if L2-PS recevies MAC control element , C-RNTI for the UE. Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16104\_replaced([11111313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpMPyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DL sched EO receives the message resumeSchedulingOnActiveBwp from UL Sched EO. This indicates reception of MAC control element, C-RNTI for UE and L2PS has to roll back to source BWP(at start of BWP scanning) and stop/abort BWP scanning procedure.

Note :

1. If the UE was moving from Large to PS BWP and was in any state other than "Evaluation state", and the UE context is deleted due to RLF or any scenario triggering PsUser\_UserDeleteReq(5G\_L2\_IF\_5365\_replaced([10976800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VIZ7Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2 PS decrements the count *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving and decrements the count* TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving if the UE is configured with at least one VoNR flow during the user deletion procedure.\*\*
2. VoNR flow deletion during PS BWP transition : If the UE is already considered in the count of *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving and the last VoNR flow is removed, decrement the count* TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving\*\*
3. VoNR flow addition during PS BWP transition : If the UE is already considered in the count of *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving and is not considered in the count* TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving and it is added with the first VoNR flow, increment the count *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving*\*\*
4. If BWP scanning is enabled (NRBTS/NRCELL.actInactiveBwpScanning is set to TRUE), then for UEs that have triggered by L2-PS the BWP scanning procedure, the PS BWP counters are pegged only when the BWP scanning procedure successfully completes. The following  AVG\_UE\_ACTIVE\_PWR\_SAVING\_BWP and MAX\_UE\_ACTIVE\_PWR\_SAVING\_BWP are derived from PS BWP counter
5. K0\_PS\_BWP = 6 except for the frame structure of tdLteConfig0, for tdLteConfig0 K0\_PS\_BWP is 8, refer to [11111082: DCI-based BWP switch procedure](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[05568582] 6. When PDCCH muting is triggered (see [13242857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2uvgIMKhEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ) and [13242859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_jt_WkMKiEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ)), the DCI 1\_1, where k0 is equal to 6 may be scheduled on muted symbols ([11360543](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucvIOfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End 05568582]

#### 2.4.2.1.2 PM counter update for PS BWP

##### 2.4.2.1.2.0-1 PM counter update for PS BWP (ID: 11153269)

**2.4.2.1.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11153277`)

PM counter update for PS BWP
Below mentioned PM counters shall be updated by L2-PS

1. MAX\_UE\_ACTIVE\_PWR\_SAVING\_BWP (M55351C00001) : L2 PS shall report to PM agent, the maximum number of active UEs in PS BWP every sampling interval. Each sampling interval can elapse across several slots. L2 PS computes maximum number of active UEs in PS BWP between each slot and shall report to PM-Agent once in every sampling interval.

Note: PM agent further reports the maximum number of active UEs in PS BWP to OAM every measurement period (could be several sampling intervals)

1. AVG\_UE\_ACTIVE\_PWR\_SAVING\_BWP (M55351C00002) : L2 PS shall report to PM agent, the average number of active UEs in PS BWP every sampling interval. Each sampling interval can elapse across several slots. L2 PS computes average number of active UEs in PS BWP between each slot and shall report to PM-Agent once in every sampling interval. During the computation if the average value results in a fraction, it will be either Ceiled Or Floored to its nearest value.

The example illustrates the L2-PS behaviour for counter updates. The example uses some potential values for number of active UEs in PS BWP during each slot and illustrates how L2-PS uses those to compute max and average values it reports to PM agent.

Sampling interval time duration : 1 second

Number of slots in 1 sampling interval : 2000

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two rows of data, each containing a sequence of numbers. The top row shows two identical sequences, each starting with the numbers 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and ending with "1999". The second row, labeled "No of Active Uses in PS BWP", contains two distinct sequences of numbers. The first sequence is: 2, 4, 5, 4, 6, 7. The second sequence is: 3, 0, 2, 3, 2, 3, 3, 2.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_1908.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

L2 PS reports MAX\_UE\_ACTIVE\_PWR\_SAVING\_BWP to PM agent for

(i) Sampling interval 1 = 7

(ii) Sampling interval 2 = 3

L2 PS reports AVG\_UE\_ACTIVE\_PWR\_SAVING\_BWP to PM agent for

(i) Sampling interval 1 = (2 + 4 + 5 + 4 + 6 + 7 ... + 3) / 2000

(ii) Sampling interval 2 = (0 + 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 3 ... + 2) / 2000

File name : PS\_BWP\_PM\_Counter\_Illustration.vsdx

Picture can be found here : <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements?csf=1&web=1&e=vxrqTw>

Note:

1. PM agent further reports the maximum and average number of active UEs in PS BWP to OAM every measurement period (could be several sampling intervals).
2. PM agent computes the average using the formula

Average = Value / Weight

where,

Value = Sum of average values received from L2 PS for 'n' sampling intervals

Weight = Total number of sampling intervals, which is 'n'.

1. L2 PS does not provide weight values to PM agent explicitly. PM agent applies a weight of '1' for every sample it receives during a sampling interval.

---

### 2.4.2.2 UE and L2-PS Active BWP

#### 2.4.2.2.0-1 UE and L2-PS Active BWP Active BWP Modification (ID: 13470131)

**2.4.2.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13470133`)

UE and L2-PS Active BWP
Modification of UE active BWP can be triggered by C-Plane, 5G-L2-PS or by User Equipment (UE) itself according to [11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Not every modification of the UE’s active Bandwidth Part (BWP) leads to an RRC Reconfiguration. The system uses different mechanisms depending on the nature of the change. Lightweight, dynamic changes between pre-configured dedicated BWPs are handled by DCI (see [11111082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), while more fundamental changes to a BWP's parameters or recovery from certain states require a full RRC Reconfiguration.

The modification of an active Bandwidth Part (BWP) is a comprehensive process that extends far beyond just the "core" parameters of bandwidth and frequency location which are defined in bwpConfig structure (see [10975247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_3Dfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) in PsUser\_UserModifyReq.cellConfigurationForModification structure (see [10976438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkufu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

A BWP's configuration defines a complete operational environment for the UE, and this includes a wide range of UE-specific parameters for physical channels and procedures such as:

• PDSCH: Configuration from pdschConfig, including mcsTable and dmrsType.

• PUSCH: Configuration from pusch-Config, including PUSCH region definition (longPucchStartPuschPrb, longPucchNumPuschPrb) and mcsTable (see [10976438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkufu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• PUCCH: UE-specific configuration from pucch-Config (see [10976438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkufu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• PRACH: Configuration based on the presence of the prachConfigNotPresent parameter (see [10975247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_3Dfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• SRS: Configuration from srsConfig, isTasSrsForSubbandBmConfigured and isPeriodDoubledForSrs (see [10975247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_3Dfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• Configured Grant: Configuration ulCgConfigType2 from PsUser\_UserModifyReq message (see [10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), if the feature is active (NRCELL/actUlCgType2 is TRUE).

• Scheduling Request: Configuration from the perSrConfig[] array (see [10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• Measurement Gap: Configuration from the measGapConfig structure (see [10975833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VC6Wfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• DRX: Configuration from the drxConfig structure (see [10975509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBFLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• CSI-RS: Configuration from perCsiReportConfig, perCsiRsResConfig, and actAperCsi within the cellConfigurationForModification structure (see [10976438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkufu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

---

#### 2.4.2.2.0-2 UE and L2-PS Active BWP Active BWP during the RA procedure (ID: 11153291)

**2.4.2.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11153298`)

UE and L2-PS Active BWP
Informative: see Active BWP management during the Random Access procedure in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1862\_replaced([11110853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-SvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.2.2.0-3 UE and L2-PS Active BWP Active BWP update with the CBRA RA-Msg3 information (ID: 11153304)

**2.4.2.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11153310`)

UE and L2-PS Active BWP
\* Upon reception of LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd (with isUlCcchData = true, UL CCCH message containing RA Msg3) form L2-LO, L2-PS shall update UE context with active BWP set to initial BWP (BWP-0).

In that case, C-Plane will follow up with setting up remaining part of UE context (PsUser\_UserSetupReq, 5G\_L2\_122\_replaced([11143796](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_232jEvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), alongside with UE's configured dedicated BWP. L2-PS shall compare this BWP with initial BWP set as the above on the CCCH data reception, and check if there is the need to perform BWP switch and which resources are available for UE scheduling after the RA procedure is completed (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17939\_replaced([11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Rationale: Unlike the CFRA procedure where the UE and its active BWP are known at the RA-Msg1 reception, the UE active BWP for CBRA procedure shall be determined as above.

* Upon the reception of LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd (with isMacCrntiCe = true) from L2-LO, L2-PS recognizes that uplink C-RNTI MAC Control Element was received (the UE is RRC connected), and checks in the UE context associated with RNTI received, which BWP is active as follows:
* The UE active BWP context is not the BWP-0 and it is linked to a BWP without RACH configuration. This means that the UE triggered an autonomous BWP switch to the initial BWP and the L2-PS shall update the current UE active BWP to BWP-0 in the UE context.
* The UE active BWP context is not the BWP-0 and it is linked to a BWP configured with RACH. The L2-PS shall assume that the valid UE active BWP is already defined in the UE context.
* The UE active BWP context corresponds to the BWP-0. The L2-PS considers that the current UE active BWP is the BWP-0.

In that case, the C-Plane already created the UE context in the U-Plane before the reception of the RA-Msg3 (via PsUser\_UserSetupReq, 5G\_L2\_122\_replaced([11143796](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_232jEvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). In this state, L2-PS shall maintain the current UE active BWP and will manage the modification(s) if needed (5G\_L2\_11487\_replaced([11153317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24tetvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17939\_replaced([11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The remaining actions triggered by the L2-PS on the reception of MAC CE CRNTI inside a RA Msg3 are described in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3436\_replaced([11152921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjFsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17933\_replaced([11110870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.2.2.0-4 UE and L2-PS Active BWP Active BWP after the RA completion with or without DL-CCCH or DL-DCCH transmission (ID: 11153317)

**2.4.2.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11153324`)

UE and L2-PS Active BWP
The L2-PS shall monitor and maintain the current UE active BWP until the UE context is released, as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17939\_replaced([11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.2.3 Initial Bandwidth Part

#### 2.4.2.3.0-1 Initial Bandwidth Part Initial Bandwidth Part - general information (ID: 11153336)

**2.4.2.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11153343`)

Initial Bandwidth Part
Informative: see the rules for configuration, usage and switching of Initial BWP under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1817\_replaced([11110897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.2.3.0-2 Initial Bandwidth Part Bandwidth part switching from initial BWP (ID: 11153349)

**2.4.2.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11153354`)

Initial Bandwidth Part
Bandwidth part switching from initial BWP via RRC procedure (DL-CCCH or DL-DCCH message)

Active BWP switch by RRC is triggered by the C-Plane. L2-PS is checking for possible switch, when:

* First, it has configured active BWP as Initial BWP already in the UE context, as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1818\_replaced([11110901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),
* Then, it is informed about the ongoing RRC procedure (through DL-CCCH or DL\_DCCH message, see 5G\_L2\_11487\_replaced([11153317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24tetvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17939\_replaced([11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). L2-PS shall check the first active BWP information received (if signaled first active BWP is different from current BWP stored in UE's context, L2-PS sets current BWP to first active BWP).

If the first active BWP is not present in the request to the L2-PS, the L2-PS considers that there will be no change of the current UE active BWP maintained in the UE context (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17939\_replaced([11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1819\_replaced([11110909](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17939\_replaced([11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.2.4 Bandwidth Part switch (by RRC or DCI)

### 2.4.2.4-1 Bandwidth Part switch (by RRC or DCI)

**2.4.2.4-1.0-1**  (ID: `11153415`)

Bandwidth Part switch (by RRC or DCI)
If NIDD parameter ueConnectionEstablishmentMode is configured as useInitialBwp, that means first active BWP in RRCSetup is Initial Bwp, first active BWP in RRCReconfiguration is Dedicated Bwp.

In L2-PS between user setup request from C-Plane and first rrc reconfiguration complete messages from C-Plane, UE is in Initial bwp.

During this period, DL scheduler allocate fixed resources for MACCE similar as SRB.

During this period, UL scheduler allocate fixed resources for SR and ulTaGrant similar as SRB.

During this period, UL scheduler don't notify CS1 event for ulProactiveSched when proActTimer expired,  CS1 event for ulProactiveSched will be notified after UE switch to dedicated BWP(first rrc reconfiguration complete received).

---

#### 2.4.2.4.0-1 Bandwidth Part switch (by RRC or DCI) Bandwidth Part switch - general information (ID: 11153368)

**2.4.2.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11153375`)

Bandwidth Part switch (by RRC or DCI)
Informative: see the rules for Bandwidth Part switch under 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1846\_replaced([11111047](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJzefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.2.4.0-2 Bandwidth Part switch (by RRC or DCI) RRC BWP switch procedure (ID: 11153380)

**2.4.2.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11153386`)

Bandwidth Part switch (by RRC or DCI)
The C-Plane informs the L2-PS about a BWP switch when the first active BWP in the C-Plane request (PsUser\_UserSetupReq/PsUser\_UserModifyReq) is not the one maintained by the L2-PS.

In that case, the L2-PS detects the presence of the RRC message in the DL buffer, which is indicated to L2-PS by the L2-LO via message:

* LoCtrl\_CcchDataInd (SRB0 - DL-CCCH, see 5G\_L2\_11433\_replaced([11181207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27SRkvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or,
* LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd (SRB1 - DL-DCCH, see 5G\_L2\_11432\_replaced([11181217](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27SRlPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

respectively, and activates the new configuration corresponding to the first active BWP after the HARQ acknowledgement of the last part of the RRC message is received. See the detailed procedure in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17973\_replaced([11111051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJzevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

According to the RRC message type, the 5G-L2-PS applies the procedures described in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17940\_replaced([11153286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kslh8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17973\_replaced([11111051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJzevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.2.4.0-3 Bandwidth Part switch (by RRC or DCI) Trigger RRC BWP switch from initial BWP (BFR Scenarios in BWP without PRACH case) (ID: 11153394)

**2.4.2.4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11153400`)

Bandwidth Part switch (by RRC or DCI)
If a BWP (example: PS-BWP) does not include PRACH configured, then UE undergoing Beam Failure in that BWP will switch to Initial BWP and trigger Beam Failure Recovery. (Ref: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17933\_replaced([11110870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

When an implicit BWP switch to initial BWP is detected, during BFR msg3 handling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3437\_replaced([11152927](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjFs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the gNB updates UE presence as initial BWP and performs a BWP switch to the nominal BWP.

This BWP switch is done by 5G-L2-PS informing 5G-CP-RT that there is a need to change the currently active BWP to Nominal BWP

Steps:

* First active BWP is the initial BWP at 5G-L2-PS (considering the implicit BWP switch).
* Flush on-going HARQ processes for the UE and Suspend DRB traffic in DL and UL (Message reference: LoCtrl\_StopFlowControlReq, LoCtrl\_StartFlowControlReq with PCell, CRNTI ).
* If the initial BWP configuration doesn't include dedicated configuration OR if it includes\*\* dedicated configuration
* the UE dedicated configuration is set “disabled (ueDedicatedConfiguration = disabled)” and only common channels could be used as indicated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21806\_replaced([11110950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJMYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Trigger CR and after the contention resolution is completed :
* Send PsUser\_UeBwpSwitchInd- 5G\_L2\_IF\_5239\_replaced([10978303](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VSx8Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for BWP switch request to 5G-CP-RT. L2PS to set ueDedicatedConfiguration = inProgress
* Wait for SRB1 “RRC Reconfiguration” - containing BWP switch from 5G-CP-RT when ueDedicatedConfiguration = inProgress according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18234\_replaced([11153699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 After RRCReconfiguration is sent - Following 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18234\_replaced([11153699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) after timeout DedicatedConfiguration set to “active” and switch UE BWP to Nominal BWP.

DL and UL DRB traffic is started (flow control activated).

CPRT will indicate completion of RRC Reconfiguration for BWP switch with psUser\_RRCReconfCompletedReq

Note : \*\*If Initial BWP contains Dedicated configuration till the DCI based BWP switching is defined in Initial BWP the same steps as above is to be followed.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21200\_replaced([11111062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJzffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

## 2.4.3 Beam Control

### 2.4.3.0-1 Beam Control FA: Beamforming/MIMO​ Document Owner: Michael 1. Wang (NSB); Michal Fiejka (Nokia) (ID: 11153427)

### 2.4.3.1 Concept

**2.4.3.1.0-1**  (ID: `11153438`)

Concept
[CNI-84537] Beamforming and massive MIMO (mMIMO) are key technologies in 5G. Beamforming means that a signal as sent or received by many antenna elements is multiplied with a dedicated complex factor per antenna element which is called beamforming weight. The set of these weights is called  beamforming weight vector. The resulting radiation pattern is then called a beam.

Beamforming is applied at different phases within the signal processing chain. Depending on whether it is applied in the digital part, it could be digital beamforming or analog beamforming. Within digtial beamforming, it could also be FD beamforming or TD beamforming.

NOTE:

* On gNB side, Beamforming is applied in RU, currently only 8T8R and MAA RU support beamforming.
* For FR2, only analog beamforming is applied.
* For FR1, digital beamforming is applied. If fronthaul interface is CPRI or OBSAI, only TD beamforming is possible. FD beamforming is feasible only when fronthaul interface is eCPRI.

For detail, please refer to 5G\_UP\_896\_replaced([11353334](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub-6Q_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[END CNI-84537]

---

### 2.4.3.2 Configuration

#### 2.4.3.2.0-1 Configuration Received and deduced Parameters (ID: 11153450)

**2.4.3.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12715344`)

Configuration
5G-L2-PS receives following parameter in PsCell\_CellGroupSetupReq(5G\_L2\_IF\_3117\_replaced([10966509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UPCB_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• *numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks*

5G-L2-PS receives following cell level parameters in PsCell\_CellSetupReq(5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• *actBeamforming*

• *numOfSubSectors*

• *numOfSuperSubcells*

• *dlSubcellList[0].dlSubcellType*

• *bfSetup.beamSet*

[CB014301-A] Note: L2-PS may receive PsCell\_BeamConfigUpdateReq from CP-RT ([12536044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_wLou8R9PEfCQJ5daVHVkgg)) in case of beamSet NIDD parameters online modification [End CB014301-A]

5G-L2-PS deduces *rrmBeamformingType according to* dlSubcellType:\*\*

If *dlSubcellList[0].dlSubcellType = D2, then* rrmBeamformingType is analogBf, else *rrmBeamformingType is digitalBf.*\*\*

---

#### 2.4.3.2.0-2 Configuration Beamforming Behavior (ID: 11153463)

**2.4.3.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11153475`)

Configuration
There is different spatial operating mode of a cell, please see 5G\_UP\_910\_replaced([11353407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub_hU_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more detail. 5G-L2-PS shall decide how to control the behavior of beamforming for different spatial operating mode during cell setup:

• Non-Beamforming (Non-BF): Supported for FR1 FDD and FR1 TDD

If (*actBeamforming = False) and (*numOfSubSectors = 0) and *numOfSuperSubcells = 0, then cell is in Non-Beamforming mode:*\*\*

• Beamforming is disabled.

• Single SSB is used.

• Analog Beamforming (Analog-BF): Supported for FR2 TDD

If (*rrmBeamformingType =* analogBf), then cell is in Analog Beamforming mode:\*\*

• Beamforming is enabled, Analog Beamforming is used.

• One or more SSB transmission is used.

• Only beamforming with SSB Beam is applied.

• If actBeamforming = False, beamforming with single sector beam is applied.

• Digital Beamforming (Digital-BF): Supported for FR1 TDD

If  (*rrmBeamformingType = digitalBf) and (*actBeamforming = True) and (*numOfSubSectors = 0), then cell is in Digital Beamforming mode*\*\*

• Beamforming is enabled, Digital Beamforming is used.

• One or more SSB transmission is used.

• More type of beams could be used depends on various beamforming related feature flags, like: SSB Beams, Refine beams, PMI beams, SRS based 2D-DFT beams, etc.

NOTE: There is still one special case for FR1 TDD, if *actBeamforming = False, but RU is configured with basic beam set #1 (*bfSetup.beamSet = BEAMSET\_1), single SSB transmission applied by beamforming with sector beam. [CB014301-B] In case of basicBeamSet can be online modifeid, and then L2-PS shall receive PsCell\_BeamConfigUpdateReq from CP-RT ([12536044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_wLou8R9PEfCQJ5daVHVkgg)).[End CB014301-B]\*\*

• Subsectorization: Supported for FR1 TDD

If (*actBeamforming = True) and (*numOfSubSectors = 2 or 4), then cell is in Subsectorization mode:\*\*

• Digital Beamforming is used.

• Only beamforming with SSB Beam is applied.

• Single SSB beam per each sub-sector formed by beamforming.

• Super cell: Supported for FR1 TDD

If (*actBeamforming = False) and* numOfSuperSubcells is in {2,3,4,5,6} **and (*numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks is in {2,3,4,5,6}), then cell is Super cell Mode:***\*\*

• Beamforming is not really applied.

• Multiple SSB transmission is used.

• Each subcell has its own SSB with unique index.

NOTE: *numOfSuperSubcells value equal to 0 (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicates supercell is disabled i.e. NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/actSuperCell is set to false, otherwise supercell is enabled.*

**[CB009543-A]**

• Super cell: Supported for FR1 FDD

If (*actBeamforming = False) and* numOfSuperSubcells is in {2,3,4,5,6} **then cell is Super cell Mode:**\*\*

• Beamforming is not applied.

• All subcells have the same SSB (1 SSB for supercell).

NOTE: *numOfSuperSubcells value equal to 0 (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicates supercell is disabled i.e. NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/actSuperCell is set to false, otherwise supercell is enabled.*

**[End CB009543-A]**

---

#### 2.4.3.2.0-3 Configuration Relation of csirsPatternId and antenna ports (informativ) (ID: 11153481)

**2.4.3.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11153486`)

Configuration
Following information/notes are copied from description field of IE startCsirsPmiPatternId of PsCell\_CellSetupReq to here.

Note: 4 patternIDs are needed for one CSI-RS resource, and each patternID serves the scheduling of 4 CSI-RS antenna ports on a certain OFDM symbol:

- csirsPatternId[0] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6} on symbol 0

- csirsPatternId[1] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7} on symbol 0

- csirsPatternId[2] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6} on symbol 1

- csirsPatternId[3] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7} on symbol 1

see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_832 for some detail about this mapping.

When 32 port CSI RS enable (act32PortCsiRs is True), 16 patternIDs(from startCsirsPmiPatternId to startCsirsPmiPatternId+15) are used for one 32 port CSI-RS resource.

* csirsPatternId[0] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6,p16,p18,p20,p22} on symbol 0
* csirsPatternId[1] -> {p8,p10,p12,p14,p24,p26,p28,p30} on symbol 0
* csirsPatternId[2] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7,p17,p19,p21,p23} on symbol 0
* csirsPatternId[3] -> {p9,p11,p13,p15,p25,p27,p29,p31} on symbol 0
* csirsPatternId[4] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6,p16,p18,p20,p22} on symbol 1
* csirsPatternId[5] -> {p8,p10,p12,p14,p24,p26,p28,p30} on symbol 1
* csirsPatternId[6] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7,p17,p19,p21,p23} on symbol 1
* csirsPatternId[7] -> {p9,p11,p13,p15,p25,p27,p29,p31} on symbol 1
* csirsPatternId[8] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6,p16,p18,p20,p22} on symbol 2
* csirsPatternId[9] -> {p8,p10,p12,p14,p24,p26,p28,p30} on symbol 2
* csirsPatternId[10] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7,p17,p19,p21,p23} on symbol 2
* csirsPatternId[11] -> {p9,p11,p13,p15,p25,p27,p29,p31} on symbol 2
* csirsPatternId[12] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6,p16,p18,p20,p22} on symbol 3
* csirsPatternId[13] -> {p8,p10,p12,p14,p24,p26,p28,p30} on symbol 3
* csirsPatternId[14] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7,p17,p19,p21,p23} on symbol 3
* csirsPatternId[15] -> {p9,p11,p13,p15,p25,p27,p29,p31} on symbol 3

Note, csirsPatternId[x] is:

csirsPatternId[0] -> startCsirsPmiPatternId

csirsPatternId[1] -> startCsirsPmiPatternId+1

...

csirsPatternId[15] -> startCsirsPmiPatternId+15

---

### 2.4.3.3 Beam Management

#### 2.4.3.3.1 Initial beam selection

##### 2.4.3.3.1.0-1 Initial beam selection Initial beam selection from PRACH (ID: 11153505)

**2.4.3.3.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11153532`)

Initial beam selection
5G-L2-PS receives cell level rach configuration parameters in PSCell\_CellSetupReq(5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) message:

prachConfigurationIndex

nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion

msg1Fdm

numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks

5G-L2-PS receives preamble dedicated parameters in UlData\_PrachReceiveInd (refer to 5G\_L1\_IF\_234\_replaced([11514309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR2oWvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

sfn

slot

prachPreambleTimeOccasion

prachPreambleFreqOccasion

Algorithm:

 Calculate NumberOfPrachOccasionsPerSlot based on prachConfigurationIndex parameter

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1959\_replaced([11111903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRIOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Calculate NumberOfPrachSlotsInAssociationPeriod

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1962\_replaced([11111925](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRIPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Calculate rrmPrachSlotIndex:

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1965\_replaced([11111936](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Calculate rrmPrachOccasionIndex:

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1965\_replaced([11111936](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

  Calcalute associated SSB

Refer to 5G\_UP\_1970\_replaced([11377269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueVDrvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note:  When NRBTS/NRCELL/actPrachC2Scs15kHzTddFr1 is set to TRUE, PRACH scs is set to 15 kHz in TDD FR1 (refer 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_13950\_replaced([11279220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUps_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).In this case related calculation of prach occasion shall be based on cell UL slot.

OutPut:

The beamID of rach occasion which the preamble is received.

In case of [CB009543-A] TDD [End CB009543-A] supercell mode enabled according to 5G\_L2\_7884\_replaced([11153463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24uFzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the beamId derived from rach occasion represents the associated L1 subcell. There is a mapping for supercell between beamId and pair of DL L1 subcellId and UL L1 subcellId of given L1 subcell, refer to 5G\_UP\_1851\_replaced([11375846](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueQLIfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In case of Pusch is 8Rx or 16Rx(numOfRxPusch=8 or 16, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the first raBeamId selection is the same as legacy. The secondRaBeamId selection is the same as ulSubCellType=C4. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3388\_replaced([11152417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

•  DL: the usage of beamId is same as legacy after raBeamId/secondRaBeamId selection.

•  UL PUCCH 4RX (numOfRxPucch=4, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): the usage of beamId is raBeamId + secondRaBeamId, the same as legacy ulSubCellType C4.

•  UL PUCCH/PUSCH 8RX (numOfRxPucch=8/numOfRxPusch=8, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): the usage of beamId is raBeamId + highCorrelation beam + two half sector beam. See 5G\_L2\_9602\_replaced([11155694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466E_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_12901\_replaced([11155736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466G_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In case of Pusch is 4Rx(numOfRxPusch=4, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the first raBeamId selection is the same as legacy. The secondRaBeamId selection is the same as ulSubCellType=C4. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3388\_replaced([11152417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DL/UL: the usage of beamId is same as legacy ulSubCellType C4 after raBeamId/secondRaBeamId selection.

---

#### 2.4.3.3.2 beamGroup selection

##### 2.4.3.3.2.0-1 beamGroup selection beamGroup selection in FR2 SA (ID: 11153545)

**2.4.3.3.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11153550`)

beamGroup selection
In FR2 SA, 5G-L2-PS instance, shall proceed accordingly to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20447\_replaced([11113612](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpcHW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for initial beamGroup selection procedure.

In FR2 SA CA, if 5G-L2-PS instance receives PsUser\_UserModifyReq with cellConfigurationForModification.pdschConfig.beamGroupSize set to 16 for PCell during SCell Addition procedure, then 5G-L2-PS instance (according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23110\_replaced([11113799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpd8jPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall:

•  calculate beamGroup and targetSsbBeam, based on received pdschConfig.beamGroupSize;

•  fill and send PsUser\_UserModifyResp with calculated beamGroup and targetSsbBeam;

•  not send PsUser\_BeamGroupChangeInd according to 5G\_L2\_IF\_531\_replaced([10977540](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VNSZvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), because beamGroup and targetSsbBeam are already sent in PsUser\_UserModifyResp.

---

##### 2.4.3.3.2.0-2 beamGroup selection Mapping of the Initial Beam to a Beam Group (ID: 11153556)

**2.4.3.3.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11153562`)

beamGroup selection
L2-PS shall store mapping tables for beam groups including beam ids and configured beam set as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2220\_replaced([11113602](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpcHWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.3.3.3 Beam tracking and beam refinement

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1 SSB and Refine Beam Management

###### 2.4.3.3.3.1.1 Configuration

###### 2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-1 Configuration SSB Beam (ID: 11153581)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11153607`)

Configuration
5G-L2-PS receives SSB related parameters in PsCell\_CellGroupSetupReq(5G\_L2\_IF\_3117\_replaced([10966509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UPCB_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• *numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks and* ssBurstSetPeriod\*\*

5G-L2-PS also receives cell level parameters from BfSetup struct in PsCell\_CellSetupReq(5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• *beamSet*

• *beamWeightVector and* ulBeamIdPatternIdMapping\*\*

• *fullSectorBeamPatternId, halfSectorBeamPatternId and quarterSectorBeamPatternId*

• *ulSsbCorrelatedBeamSet, numberOfRxPucch*

• *ulRefinementCorrelatedBeamSet*

• *mMimoPSBeamWeightVector.patternId*

If Beamforming is enabled according to 5G\_L2\_7884\_replaced([11153463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24uFzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G-L2-PS shall

• Store *numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks and* ssBurstSetPeriod for SS block transmission.\*\*

• Store corresponding *beamSet which include parameters related to the configuration of beams, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1930\_replaced([11111654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpPTCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1931\_replaced([11111662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpPTC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

• Store *beamWeightVector and* ulBeamIdPatternIdMapping which identify the mapping between beamId and patternId for DL and UL, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1939\_replaced([11111782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpQhIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

NOTE: the mapping also includes refine beams if beam refinement is enabled.

• Store refine beam to the corresponding SSB beam according to *beamWeightVector, which will be used for beam extension defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21306\_replaced([11113376](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpbgTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if beam refinement is enabled.*

• Store *fullSectorBeamPatternId,* halfSectorBeamPatternId, quarterSectorBeamPatternId for sector beam usage, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2037\_replaced([11112372](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

• Store *ulSsbCorrelatedBeamSet/numberOfRxPucch for SSB beam usage. For each SSB (coarse) beam, CPlane will calculate two highest correlation beam. L2PS use legacy SSB beam and ulSsbCorrelatedBeamSet to extend one SSB beam or two SSB beam to four beam for 8RX. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2032\_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

• Store *ulRefinementCorrelatedBeamSet for SSB/refine beam usage. For each SSB (coarse)/refine beam, CPlane will calculate two highest correlation beams(5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_11171\_replaced([11297028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqGFwfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). L2PS use legacy SSB/refine beams and ulRefinementCorrelatedBeamSet to extend one SSB/refine beam or two SSB/refine beams to four beams for PUSCH scheduling for 8RX. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21306\_replaced([11113376](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpbgTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

If **[CB009543-A] TDD** [End CB009543-A] supercell is enabled according to 5G\_L2\_7884\_replaced([11153463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24uFzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G-L2-PS shall\*\*\*\*

• Store *numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks and* ssBurstSetPeriod for SS block transmission, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4106\_replaced([11173741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1d_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

•  Store corresponding *beamSet which include parameters related to the configuration of beams, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13175\_replaced([11111819](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpQhLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

[CB014301-AA] 5G-L2-PS may receive beam related parameters in PsCell\_BeamConfigUpdateReq 5G\_L2\_IF [12536044: PsCell\_BeamConfigUpdateReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_wLou8R9PEfCQJ5daVHVkgg) after online modification of beamset NIDD parameters[End CB014301-AA]

---

###### 2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-2 Configuration Refine Beam (ID: 11153614)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11153618`)

Configuration
[CNI-84537] 5G-L2-PS receives Refine beam parameter in PsCell\_CellSetupReq(5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• nrBtsBeamRefinementP2

If Digital Beamforming is enabled according to 5G\_L2\_7884\_replaced([11153463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24uFzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 is true, 5G-L2-PS shall enable beam refinement:

• Apply CSI-RS transmission for beam refinement, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21843\_replaced([11159700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktLX1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L2-PS receives UE specific refine beam parameter from IE cellConfiguration in PsUser\_UserSetupReq(5G\_L2\_IF\_389\_replaced([10974645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or PsUser\_UserModifyReq(5G\_L2\_IF\_453\_replaced([10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• appliedBeamRefinementP2

If Beam refinement is enabled and appliedBeamRefinementP2 is true, 5G-l2-PS shall apply beam refinement for this UE:

• Send request for Beam management based on CRI-RSRP, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3604\_replaced([11159975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktNNBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

• Receive response for CRI Beam report, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3617\_replaced([11160013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktNNC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

• Filter beam power measurement and do beam selection, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1999\_replaced([11112098](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpS9avvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE:

• For digital beamforming, SSB and Refine beams configuration may be different for PCell and SCell, 5G-L2-PS shall deal wtih them separately.

• If appliedBeamRefinementP2 is updated on P(S)Cell due to SCell addtion/release, special procedure during the reconfiguration transient phase need to be applied, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4196\_replaced([11174380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4826\_replaced([11195069](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv5UqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [END CNI-84537]

---

###### 2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-3 Configuration Beam Fallback and Sweeping (ID: 11153626)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11153630`)

Configuration
[CNI-84537] 5G-L2-PS shall set appliedUlBeamSweeping, appliedUlBeamFallback, and appliedDlBeamProbingBeforeChange according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1888\_replaced([11111451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[END CNI-84537]

---

###### 2.4.3.3.3.1.2 Beam fallback for UL

###### 2.4.3.3.3.1.2.0-1 Beam fallback for UL UL Beam probing and fallback (ID: 11153642)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.2.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12591677`)

Beam fallback for UL
If *appliedUlBeamFallback == TRUE (5G\_L2\_1848\_replaced([11212918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-ogjvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_L2\_1851\_replaced([11212942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-pHkvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):*

5G-L2-PS shall conduct,

1. If a target beam was not defined by a beam report, the procedures of uplink beam fallback as defined by this requirement (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2041\_replaced([11112416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZr_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall not be conducted.

2. If dlMimoMode is CL 8x4MIMO,beam for UL receiver shall still be from Beam report, the procedures of uplink beam fallback as defined by this requirement and retrigger UL fall back procedure defined in 5G\_L2\_8179\_replaced([11153750](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24wiAvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall still be conducted based on SSB/refined beam changing from beam report.

3. If UEs which are configured with periodic SRS and state machine for BM is NOT under the state of SRS\_BM\_AGED (see 5G\_L2\_5339\_replaced([11153981](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24xwKvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the UL beam fallback and probing algorithms shall be skipped because UL beams are selected based on periodic SRS but not beam reports from UEs.

4. If FR1 SCell with UL configured according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2698\_replaced([11120363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqP_rPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), UL beam fallback and probing algorithms shall be conducted.

5. If Pusch is 4Rx(numOfRxPusch=4, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

5G-L2-PS shall conduct UL Beam probing and fallback as in 5G\_L2\_8177\_replaced([11153681](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24v69fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

else if Pusch is 8Rx(numOfRxPusch=8, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

5G-L2-PS shall conduct UL Beam probing and fallback as in 5G\_L2\_13275\_replaced([11153692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24v69_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

else Pusch is 16Rx(numOfRxPusch=16, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

5G-L2-PS shall conduct UL Beam probing and fallback as in 5G\_L2\_17762\_replaced([11153703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24v6-fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

else

***\*a.In case of Probing***\*

The old UL source beam ID shall be stored in UE context before the UL beam changes. Instantaneous SINR values of a) the last scheduled UL slot before and b) the first scheduled UL slot after an updated beam ID is applied shall be measured and stored in the UE context.

**NOTE: an updated beam ID shall be applied when a MAC CE PDCCH TCI state indication is HARQ ACKed or finally HARQ NACKed, the timing refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29442\_replaced([11112392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

**NOTE: updated beam PMI ID application shall not be impacted by MAC CE PDCCH TCI state indication is HARQ ACKed or finally HARQ NACKed.**

If the UL SINR value of the new beam ID is better than the value of the old beam ID or the PUSCH on old beam is DTX'ed, then the new beam ID shall be applied further as serving UL beam for PUCCH Format 3, PUSCH, and to AP-SRS for SU-MIMO. Normalized SINR values, as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2048\_replaced([11112469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpWAuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), shall be compared.

***\*b.In case of UL Beam Fallback***\*

If SINR of the new candidate beam is equal or lower than the SINR value of the old beam, **or the PUSCH on new candidate beam is DTX'ed, the old beam ID shall be applied in next slots for PUCCH, PUSCH, and to AP-SRS for SU-MIMO. If the UL beam falls back to the old beam ID, this shall be conducted directly in the next slot without any latency and without signaling to the UE.**

Endif

6. If UCA cell (5G\_UP\_921\_replaced([11353688](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucBWiPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for FR1-TDD, eCPRI, C4, appliedUlBeamFallback shall be FALSE (5G\_L2\_8050\_replaced([11153626](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24vT7PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and UL beam selection is performed according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2212\_replaced([11113055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpaSI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and does not rely on beam report.

Note:

 Any UL symbols between above two UL slots, e.g., PUCCH symbols in special slots, shall use the serving beam as defined without the PR4830646. That old beam ID shall be applied before the updated beam ID is applied. The new beam ID shall be applied after that point in time.

 UL slots used for (re-)probing are not considered by to OLLA and ILLA, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3078\_replaced([11130719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krANl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3060\_replaced([11129299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3153\_replaced([11137866](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcScfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 5G-L2-PS shall apply beam pattern Ids to physical channels including Long PUCCH as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2031\_replaced([11112294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUyl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2215\_replaced([11113083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpaSLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 PM counters NR Beamforming/UL\_SERV\_BEAM\_ID\_BEAM\_xx (M55305C010xx) shall be updated with current serving beam for all probing allocations (i.e. not with the beam which is probed)**.**

 Regarding any DTX of PUSCH mentioned in this requirement, the corresponding response is not applicable if DTX determined for any of the following and UE is configured for skippable grants (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4839\_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

o skippable Proactive grant

o fake SR (SR indication received from L2-HI)

o TA Grant

o UL grant after a zero BSR was received

[CB014005-A]

Note: In any UL beam probing and fallback case mentioned above, for beam report with ReportQuantity::ssb\_Index\_RSRP or ReportQuantity::cri\_RSRP for UL, stop/restart the UL beam probing and rollback when cell beam update as [12592060: Beam probing stop and re-start](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_MFNe8DbrEfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[End CB014005-A]

###### 2.4.3.3.3.1.3 Beam fallback for DL

###### 2.4.3.3.3.1.4 UL beam sweeping

###### 2.4.3.3.3.1.4.0-1 UL beam sweeping 1.Trigger for beam sweeping. (ID: 11153786)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11153798`)

UL beam sweeping
when appliedUlBeamSweeping == TRUE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1888\_replaced([11111451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

If UE’s CSI report for beam management received from the message of PuschReceiveRespU is DTX’ed (5G\_UP\_ALG\_22403\_replaced([11187538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvbanvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and/or its CRC failed continuously for more than rdUlBeamSweepingTriggerCounts defined in 5G\_L2\_1241\_replaced([11183642](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27lMhvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

5G-L2-PS shall conduct SSB beam sweeping until a target beam is able to receive the CSI report for beam management in a NON-DTX’ed manner.

Note:

For UEs which are configured with periodic SRS and state machine for BM is NOT under the state of SRS\_BM\_AGED (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2136\_replaced([11154035](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksqac_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the UL beam sweeping shall NOT be triggered

UL beams are selected based on periodic SRS but not on beam reports from UEs

 In case ulSubcellType is C4  [CB007595-C] or C4\_2[End CB007595-C]  and UE is configured with 2 SSB beams and beam refinement is disabled for   the UE and periodic SRS is not configured for the UE, UL beam sweeping  shall not be triggered.
