###### 2.4.3.3.3.5.0-8 TAS SRS-based short-term sub-band beam management for DL data (PDSCH) Less number of PRB checking (ID: 12715459)

**2.4.3.3.3.5.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `12715460`)

TAS SRS-based short-term sub-band beam management for DL data (PDSCH)
In eCPRI7-2e ([11155228: Abbreviation definition in eCPRI 7-2a and eCPRI 7-2e](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2432wvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)),

Due to L1's restriction (two subband BF UEs can't be allocated in same bf subband).

For RAT0 there is no such issue , it is because based on 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_10967\_replaced([11289526](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqBNR_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), CP shall adapt bfSubbandSize, and make sure that  bfSubbandSize is no bigger than RBG size, also RBG size is 2,4,8,16.

But for RAT1 ( transient phase triggered or dedicate resource allocation  ) , the minimum PRB allocation is 1, and startPrb  can be inside of BF SB.

To workaround this issue  L1 will use adjacent subband's weight, Then L2PS shall make sure at least the allocated PRBs is >= bfSubbandSize.

IF numOfPrb<bfSubbandSize, then UE Beam shall fallback ([11155506: UE Beam fallback](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_245r8PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

Else, the SRS quality is further checked to determine if subband beam can be used or not.  (see [11171429: SRS Quality Measurement](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26N6nPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

where

bfSubbandSize is the beamforming subband size.

numOfPrb is the number of allocated PRB.

SFS refers [11156802: Criteria for subband BF usage](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks3OzvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

###### 2.4.3.3.3.5.0-9 TAS SRS-based short-term sub-band beam management for DL data (PDSCH) Beam reset (ID: 11155544)

**2.4.3.3.3.5.0-9.0-3**  (ID: `11155561`)

TAS SRS-based short-term sub-band beam management for DL data (PDSCH)
Following short-term beamforming relevant parameter shall be reset by 5G-L2-PS during user delete procedure or wideband SRS switch to suband SRS during userModify

[CB009527-A] {  In eCPRI 7-2a ( 5G\_L2\_18130\_replaced([11155228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2432wvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

* Sub-Band BWVs calculate result
* bwvReportId and Ping - Pong patternIDs for UE}  [End CB009527-A]

[CB009527-A]  {In eCPRI 7-2e ( 5G\_L2\_18130\_replaced([11155228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2432wvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

* rtBfUeIndex  for UE
* SrsQualityTable  for UE}    [End CB009527-A]

[CB009527-A] Note: Due to supporting SB SRS for SB BF , Wideband SRS here means SB BF SRS,  subband SRS here means WB BF SRS , for habitual understanding, the rewording of names is not done. [End CB009527-A]

---

#### 2.4.3.3.4 Beam selection for physical channels

##### 2.4.3.3.4.0-1 Beam selection for physical channels Overview (ID: 11155622)

**2.4.3.3.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11155626`)

Beam selection for physical channels
In each slot, 5G-L2-PS shall select corresponding beams represented by patternId for physical channels on this slot. PatternId is a 16 bit index for a specific beamforming weight vector. The selected patternIds shall be indicated to 5G-L1-DL by 5G\_L2\_482\_replaced([11155914](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248INfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For Analog Beamforming, the beams shall be decided for each symbol on all carriers of this cell group.

For Digital Beamforming (FR1 only), the beams shall be decided for each scheduled UE or configured resource of corresponding DL/UL channels. Please note, in case of TD Beamforming, there is only one beam per symbol per stream per subcell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10617\_replaced([11179139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4A9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10697\_replaced([11199387](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwM2rvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

##### 2.4.3.3.4.1 Analog Beamforming

###### 2.4.3.3.4.1.0-1 Analog Beamforming DL channels (ID: 11155638)

**2.4.3.3.4.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11155642`)

Analog Beamforming
 5G-L2-PS shall select the configured SSB beam of corresponding SSB index for SSB symbols  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2329\_replaced([11114748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnthvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 5G-L2-PS shall select the configured beam for TRS symbols with corresponding TRS resource (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3583\_replaced([11159730](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktLX3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 5G-L2-PS shall select the configured beam for CSI-RS symbols with corresponding cell-specific CSI-RS for beam management resources  (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21843\_replaced([11159700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktLX1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 5G-L2-PS shall select the determined SSB beam of corresponding UE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2031\_replaced([11112294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUyl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_L2\_9544\_replaced([11155775](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_247hIvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for CSI-RS symbols with those UE specific CSI-RS for channel measurement resources.

 5G-L2-PS shall select the determined X-pol beams of the DL scheduling slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4250\_replaced([11175627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuke9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for those PDCCH symbols with DL grants, and also for those PDSCH symbols on same DL slot.

 5G-L2-PS shall select the determined X-pol beam of the corresponding UL scheduling slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4865\_replaced([11196363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCelPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for PDCCH symbols with UL grants.

---

###### 2.4.3.3.4.1.0-2 Analog Beamforming UL Channels (ID: 11155647)

**2.4.3.3.4.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11155653`)

Analog Beamforming
 5G-L2-PS shall select the SSB beam of mapped SSB index for PRACH symbols with related PRACH transmission occasion (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2339\_replaced([11114812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpoUmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 5G-L2-PS shall select the determined SSB beam of corresponding UE for PUCCH symbols with SR, ACK/NACK, or CSI report resources (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2031\_replaced([11112294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUyl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_L2\_9544\_replaced([11155775](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_247hIvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 5G-L2-PS shall select the determined X-pol beam of the UL scheduling slot (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4865\_replaced([11196363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCelPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for PUSCH symbols on same UL scheduling slot.

---

##### 2.4.3.3.4.2 Digital Beamforming

###### 2.4.3.3.4.2.0-1 Digital Beamforming DL/UL Beam Usage (ID: 13312720)

**2.4.3.3.4.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12825307`)

Digital Beamforming
If FDD with actBeamforming = TRUE, beam usage is as per is as per [12823081: Beam usage for UL](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_GGTJ4KpBEfCrANRYxAlR5A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9);

otherwise in this REQ [11155658: Digital Beamforming](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_246TDfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

###### 2.4.3.3.4.2.1 DL Channels

###### 2.4.3.3.4.2.1.0-1 DL Channels PDCCH (ID: 11155668)

**2.4.3.3.4.2.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12537034`)

DL Channels
For each UE, the PDCCH beam shall be selected separately:

 The PDCCH beam is firstly determined by selected initial beam from PRACH (see 5G\_L2\_8569\_replaced([11153505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24us1vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 If rdDisableBestSrsBeamForPdcch (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14772\_replaced([11114093](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kphm4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is false, and actDlSrsBm is true and state machine for codebook SRS based DL beam management isn't SRS\_DL\_BM\_AGED (see 5G\_L2\_6711\_replaced([11154583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_240zdPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the selected best beam using SRS covariance matrix (see 5G\_L2\_6564\_replaced([11154888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_242BlPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be use for PDCCH transmission.

 [Before CNI-137616-C]Else if rdDisableBestPmiBeamForPdcch (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8458\_replaced([11113959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpfxvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is false, and UE is in ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4 MIMO mode, and CSI feedback is not outdated (See 5G\_L2\_4072\_replaced([11155155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_243Pu_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the 1st beam of selected PMI beams (see 5G\_L2\_4068\_replaced([11155170](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_243PvfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be use for PDCCH transmission.[End CNI-137616-C]

 Else the PDCCH beam shall be selected based on beam reports from UE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1987\_replaced([11112050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpSWY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

###### 2.4.3.3.4.2.1.0-2 DL Channels PDSCH (ID: 11155681)

**2.4.3.3.4.2.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11155685`)

DL Channels
For each UE, the PDSCH beam shall be selected separately:

 The PDSCH beam is firstly determined by selected initial beam from PRACH (see 5G\_L2\_8569\_replaced([11153505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24us1vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 If actDlSrsBm is true and state machine for codebook SRS based DL beam management isn't SRS\_DL\_BM\_AGED (see 5G\_L2\_6711\_replaced([11154583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_240zdPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the selected beams using SRS covariance matrix (see 5G\_L2\_6564\_replaced([11154888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_242BlPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be use for PDSCH transmission.

 Else if UE is in ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4 MIMO mode, and CSI feedback is not outdated (See 5G\_L2\_4072\_replaced([11155155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_243Pu_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the selected PMI beams (see 5G\_L2\_4068\_replaced([11155170](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_243PvfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be use for PDSCH transmission.

 Else the PDSCH beam shall be selected based on beam reports from UE (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1987\_replaced([11112050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpSWY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

###### 2.4.3.3.4.2.2 UL Channels

###### 2.4.3.3.4.2.2.0-1 UL Channels PUSCH (ID: 11155694)

**2.4.3.3.4.2.2.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `12618991`)

UL Channels
PUSCH beam is firstly determined by initial beam selection from PRACH, see 5G\_L2\_8569\_replaced([11153505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24us1vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If actUlSrsBm is true and state machine for codebook SRS based UL beam management isn't SRS\_UL\_BM\_AGED, see 5G\_L2\_5339\_replaced([11153981](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24xwKvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), beams which are decided using SRS covariance matrix by 5G\_L2\_3589\_replaced([11154211](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24y-SvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be: [Before CB015692-A] should be applied for PUSCH transmission. [CB015692-A]

* If SRS BM 3rd/4th beam probing is [enabled](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_INefIDJbEfCfjdcwDn2YOQ) and PUSCH = 8RX, the 1st and 2nd beams shall be applied for PUSCH transmissions. 3rd and 4th beam shall be applied according to 3rd/4th beam probing, see [12580839: Beam probing for 8RX](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_M08itDIdEfCfjdcwDn2YOQ),
* Otherwise all beams shall be applied for PUSCH transmissions.

[End CB015692-A]

TCI MAC CE shall not be triggered for beam update.

If actUlSrsBm is false or state machine for codebook based UL beam management is SRS\_UL\_BM\_AGED, UL beams selected based on beam reports from UE, see  5G\_UP\_ALG\_1987\_replaced([11112050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpSWY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), shall be applied for PUSCH transmission.

Four UL beams shall be used for PUSCH/SRS for PUSCH 8RX. os-DFT beam or SSB beam combined with high correlation  beam/sector beam will be used. See the following table for PUSCH usage (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2032\_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Scenario** | **SRS Coma** |  | **beam valid** | **PUSCH Beam Usage** |  |
| PUSCH = 8RX PUCCH = 8RX | Valid |  | / | 4 os-DFT beams |  |
| Invalid |  | 2 SSB beams | a1 + a2 + b1 + b2 |  |  |
| 2 refine beams | a + ax + b + bx |  |  |  |  |
| 1 SSB beam | A + A1 + 2 half sector beam or a-1 + a-2 + a-3 + a-4 according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26331\_replaced([11113585](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpcHU_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (1 beam valid) |  |  |  |  |
| 1 refine beam | a-1 + a-2 + a-3 + a-4 |  |  |  |  |
| PUSCH = 8RX PUCCH = 4RX | Same as above |  |  |  |  |
| PUSCH = 16RX | Valid | / |  |  | 8 os-DFT beams**or os-DFT beams with subpanel beams(5G\_UP\_ALG\_20242\_replaced([11154912](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksuE0fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))** |
| Invalid | 2 SSB beams |  |  | a-1 + a-2 + a-3 + a-4 + b-1 + b-2 + b-3 + b-4 |  |
| 1 SSB beam or 2 same SSB beams |  |  | A + A1 + 2 half sector beam or a-1 + a-2 + a-3 + a-4  or a1 + a2 + b1 + b2 according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26331\_replaced([11113585](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpcHU_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (1 beam valid) |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |

Note:

the concept of “PUSCH=8RX”or “PUSCH=16RX” is described in 5G\_L2\_12903\_replaced([11155753](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466HvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

A/B is UE reported SSB coarse beam.

High correlation beam A1 and B1 is the highest correlation beam of A/B informed by CPlane during cellsetup.

a1/a2 are high correlation refine beam of A and b1/b2 are high correlation refine beam of B informed by CPlane in ulRefinementCorrelatedBeamSet during cellsetup.

a/b are UE reported refine beams.

a1/b1 are first high correlation refine beam of a/b informed by CPlane in ulRefinementCorrelatedBeamSet during cellsetup. a2/b2 are second high correlation refine beam of a/b informed by CPlane in ulRefinementCorrelatedBeamSet during cellsetup.

If a1 != b, ax = a1; else, ax = a2.

If b1 != a and b1 != ax, bx = b1; else if b2 != a and b2 != ax, bx = b2; else “a-1 + a-2 + a-3 + a-4” shall be used.

a-1/a-2/a-3/a-4 are four refine beams of the related SSB beam of the refine beam a. For example, ony 1 refine beam(beamId = 8) valid. The related SSB of this beam(8) is 0. And four refine beams of this SSB beam(0) are 8/16/24/32. So a-1/a-2/a-3/a-4 in this case are 8/16/24/32.

When target SSB beam changes, a TCI State Indication for UE-specific PDCCH MAC CE shall be sent. After TCI MAC CE is HARQ ACKed or finally HARQ NACKED, the timing refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29442\_replaced([11112392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and if appliedUlBeamFallback == TRUE, PUSCH beam probing should be triggered. The new beam ID shall be applied for PUSCH only if conditions in 5G\_L2\_8176\_replaced([11153642](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24vT7_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are met. Otherwise, old beam ID shall be applied for PUSCH. UL beam shall be re-probing until UL beam doesn't fall back to old beam ID, see 5G\_L2\_8179\_replaced([11153750](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24wiAvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If appliedUlBeamSweeping == TRUE, UL beam sweeping should be triggered according to 5G\_L2\_7995\_replaced([11153786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24wiCfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). DL-L2-PS should notify UL-L2-PS to trigger beam sweeping. In each period of beam sweeping the candidate SSB beam shall not be applied to PDCCH channel which is used for UL grant.  If 5G\_L2\_8034\_replaced([11153898](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24xJHPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is met, 5G-L2-PS shall use the beam with highest measured SINR as the serving UL beam for PUSCH in UL slots which are NOT sweeping slots. If CSI report for BM is received and if this UCI meets condition in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1992\_replaced([11112073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpS9Y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) that it can trigger a beam change , 5G-L2-PS shall stop beam sweeping and notify DL-L2-PS with new target beam ID.

In case that PUSCH = 8RX,  if UE is configured with 2 SSB beams(beamset with only 2 SSB beams configured) and beam refinement is disabled for the UE and no valid SRS beams **for the UE, "2 SSB beams" in the table should be chosen and 2 beams in beamset should be used for extention no matter one or two beams are valid. For “PUSCH=16RX”, beam extent to 4 or 8 beams as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26331\_replaced([11113585](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpcHU_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

---

###### 2.4.3.3.4.2.2.0-2 UL Channels PUCCH (ID: 11155736)

**2.4.3.3.4.2.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11155749`)

UL Channels
Four UL beams shall be used for PUCCH 8RX, Two UL beams shall be used for PUCCH 4RX. Sector beam or SSB beam combined with high correlation  beam will be used. See the following table for PUCCH usage (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2218\_replaced([11113106](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpa5MvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Scenario | pucchF3BeamformingEnabled is true |
| PUSCH=8RX**[CB010708-D] or 16Rx** [End CB010708-D]\*\*\*\* PUCCH=8RX | F1: four quarter sector beam   F3:   A + A1 + B + B1 (2 beams valid)   If A1 is the same as B1, then:   A + A1 + B + B2 (B2 is the second high correlation beam of B)     A + A1 + two half sector beam (RU region limited case or 1 beam valid)   Note: A, B is SSB coarse beam after ordering as legacy when fill pucchReiveReq |
| PUSCH=8RX  PUCCH=4RX | F1: two half sector beam   F3:   A + B (2 beams valid)   A + randomNeighbourBeam (1 beam valid)   A + full sector beam (RU region limited case)   Note: the same as ulSubCellType is C4. A, B is SSB coarse beam after ordering as legacy when fill pucchReiveReq |

Note: the concept of “PUCCH=8RX” or “PUCCH=4RX” is described in 5G\_L2\_12903\_replaced([11155753](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466HvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). A/B is UE reported SSB coarse beam. High correlation beam {A1, A2} and {B1, B2} is the highest correlation beam of A/B informed by CPlane during cellsetup.

1 beam valid for PUCCH = 8RX:

1 valid PUCCH beam in UE context OR

(UE reports only 1 beam instead of 2 (nrOfReportedRS = n1 in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2684\_replaced([11303281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tqj_0PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) AND appliedUlBeamFallback = FALSE) OR

2 SSB for PUCCH are the same

1 beam valid for PUCCH = 4RX(same as legacy):

1 valid PUCCH beam in UE context OR

UE reports only 1 beam instead of 2 (nrOfReportedRS = n1 in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2684\_replaced([11303281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tqj_0PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) OR

2 SSB for PUCCH are the same

In case that PUCCH = 8RX , if UE is configured with 2 SSB beams(beamset with only 2 SSB beams configured), " A + A1 + B + B1"/"A + A1 + B + B2" in the table should be chosen and 2 beams in beamset should be used for extention no matter one or two beams are valid for PUCCH F3. Sector beams shall always be used for PUCCH F1.

---

###### 2.4.3.3.4.2.2.0-3 UL Channels Concept of PUSCH 16RX, PUSCH 8RX, PUCCH 8RX and PUCCH 4RX (ID: 11155753)

**2.4.3.3.4.2.2.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11155772`)

UL Channels
The num of beams for PUSCH/PUCCH is equal to half of number of spatial stream for corresponding channel.

The detail information of PUCCH 4Rx/8Rx and PUSCH=4Rx/8Rx[CB010708-C] /16Rx [End CB010708-C] see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.3.3.4.3 Apply Updated Beam ID to Physical Channels

###### 2.4.3.3.4.3.0-1 Apply Updated Beam ID to Physical Channels (ID: 11155780)

**2.4.3.3.4.3.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `13413667`)

Apply Updated Beam ID to Physical Channels
**1.**SSB Beam ID apply\*\*\*\*

o Applied time

i. An updated beam ID shall be applied to PDCCH, PDSCH, CSI-RS (CSI), PUCCH, PUSCH, and to AP-SRS for SU-MIMO when MAC CE PDCCH TCI state indication is HARQ ACKed or finally HARQ NACKed if The MAC CE is triggered.

ii.The timing for both uplink and downlink channels shall refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29442\_replaced([11112392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

o Selection of serving beam IDs in UL and DL

i. defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2041\_replaced([11112416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZr_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

o Triggered condition

i. Beam report on UCI

o Before the first beam update is triggered

i. the beam Id associated to the RACH resources shall be used in which the last UE's RACH preamble was received as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3388\_replaced([11152417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

o new beam update time

i. Two beams cases

1. If for a given user two beams are applied for a transmission and first beam has changed then new 2nd beam shall be taken into use with the same timing as 1st beam.

2. If for a given user two beams are applied for a transmission and only 2nd beam has changed then then new 2nd beam shall be updated immediately.

3. Time when new beam is used

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| scenario |  | 1st beam |  | 2nd beam |  | Time when new beam is used |  |  |
| 1. |  | remains |  | changes |  | Just after the report or measured. |  |  |
|  | 2. |  | changes |  | remains |  | After MAC-CE HARQ feedback |  |
|  | 3. |  | changes |  | changes |  | After MAC-CE HARQ feedback |  |
|  | 4. |  | Remains |  | remains |  | NA |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

ii. updated refined beam case

1. If an updated refined beam has the same related SSB index as the serving beam, the updated beam ID shall be immediately applied just after the report to PDCCH, PDSCH, CSI-RS (CSI), PUCCH, PUSCH, and to AP-SRS for SU-MIMO. This shall be valid for the 1st beam and the 2nd beam if for a given user two beams are applied.

2. Time when new beam is used

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| scenario |  | 1st beam |  | 2nd beam |  | Time when new beam is used (with same related SSB as the serving beam) |  |  |
| 1. |  | remains |  | Changes |  | Just after the report or measured. |  |  |
|  | 1a. |  | changes |  | Remains |  | Just after the report or measured. |  |
|  | 1b. |  | changes |  | Changes |  | Just after the report or measured. |  |
|  | 4. |  | Remains |  | Remains |  | NA |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

o Inter work with Beam Sweeping and Probing

i. if *appliedUlBeamSweeping = TRUE (5G\_L2\_1240\_replaced([11183670](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27lMi_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if* appliedUlBeamFallback = TRUE (5G\_L2\_1238\_replaced([11183760](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27lzn_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), or if *appliedDlBeamProbingBeforeChange= TRUE (5G\_L2\_1244\_replaced([11183675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27lMjPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*\*\*

o Inter work with CA

i. If **actCellCsirsTddOnSsbBf is true and numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks is less than 4 in PCell or SCell, the updated beam ID from Beam Management report with report quantity = "SSBRI-RSRP"(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1992\_replaced([11112073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpS9Y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall also follow the above rule. The beam usage for cell specific CSI-RS resources,  please refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9731\_replaced([11113316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpa5P_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

**2.**DL beams PatternID apply\*\*\*\*

o defined in [11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZr_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

**3.**UL beams PatternID apply\*\*\*\*

o If NIDD actUlSrsBm is true, for UEs that are configured with periodic SRS, beams determined using SRS covariance matrix (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2168\_replaced([11154601](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kss2uPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB015692-A] and determined by [12580839: Beam probing for SRS BM 8RX](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_M08itDIdEfCfjdcwDn2YOQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), if beam probing active (see [12581649: Configuration](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_INefIDJbEfCfjdcwDn2YOQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)  
) [End CB015692-A] shall be applied immediately to PUSCH

o TCI MAC CE for beam change shall not be triggered

***\*4. eCPRI Pusch/Pucch/Srs/Prach is 4Rx(see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))* \*with FD BF enabled**\*\*\*\*

o Two UL beams shall be used for PUSCH, short PUCCH & A-SRS

o The serving beam is used as first UL beam, the second UL beam shall be as same as second DL best beam according to UE report.

o Before the first beam update is triggered, the beam Id associated to the RACH resources shall be used as first UL beam in which the last UE's RACH preamble was received as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3388\_replaced([11152417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

o Before first beam update is triggered or if second DL best beam is not available, the second UL beam shall be a random neighbor beam of the first UL beam.

o If appliedUlBeamSweeping = TRUE or appliedUlBeamFallback = TRUE, the UL beam selection is determined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2040\_replaced([11112412](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2061\_replaced([11112525](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpWnzvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

o If PUCCH Format 3 beamforming is enabled (pucchF3BeamformingEnabled==True), SSB beams shall be used, and 2nd SSB beam shall be different than the 1st one. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2218\_replaced([11113106](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpa5MvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details

o PUSCH data can be still received over refined beams, if applicable

o In case *Pusch/Pucch/Prach is 4Rx(see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))* *and* UE is configured with 2 SSB beams\*\*\*\*

- For PUSCH, if beam refinement is disabled for the UE and no valid SRS beams for the UE, the two SSB beams shall be used on PUSCH.

- For PUCCH F3&F1, the two SSB beams shall be used on it no matter beam refinement is enabled or disabled or SRS beams are valid or not.

- For PRACH, duplicated beams shall be used*.*

**5.**Supercell\*\*\*\*

o **If MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actSupercell = True with Long PUCCH on TDD FR1,   then for PUCCH Format 1 and PUCCH Format 3 resources the virtual beam handling of supercell is applicable; i.e. resources shall be received on the virtual SSB beam a UE is connected to (indicated in UE context), respectively the associated L1 subcell is used**

**Else**

If actLongPucchF1F3BfOnOranExtT6 is True, then PUCCH F1 and PUCCH F3 resources shall be received with SSB beams, for details see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2215\_replaced([11113083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpaSLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**6.**Beam for RIM RS\*\*\*\*

o If NRCELL.actNrRim is not equal to 0-disable, for remote interference monitoring request in each consecutive *rdRImonitorPRB number of PRB regions per UL symbol in monitoring slot , the pattern ID of beam weight is determined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9328\_replaced([11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*

o If NRCELL.actNrRim is equal to 3-“framework-0”, the beam for RIM RS detecting and the 2nd beam of PUSCH & AP-SRS for SU-MIMO shall be determined by **5G\_UP\_ALG\_13190\_replaced([11162584](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**

**7.**Usage of sector beam\*\*\*\*

o Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2037\_replaced([11112372](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**8.**Note: In case of UCA cell (5G\_UP\_921\_replaced([11353688](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucBWiPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for FR1-TDD, eCPRI, C4, UL beam selection is performed according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2212\_replaced([11113055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpaSI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and does not rely on beam report.\*\*\*\*

**9.****eCPRI Pusch/Srs is 8Rx, Pucch is 8Rx/4Rx(see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with FD BF enabled**\*\*\*\*

o PUSCH/SRS beam usage refer to 5G\_L2\_9602\_replaced([11155694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466E_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**.**

o PUCCH beam usage refer to 5G\_L2\_12901\_replaced([11155736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466G_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**10.****eCPRI Pusch/Srs is 16Rx, Pucch is 8Rx(see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ] with FD BF enabled**\*\*\*\*

o ueReceiverSize refer to 5G\_L2\_17840\_replaced([11179917](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27JHqfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

o PUSCH/SRS beam usage refer to 5G\_L2\_9602\_replaced([11155694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466E_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**.**

o PUCCH beam usage refer to 5G\_L2\_12901\_replaced([11155736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466G_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.3.3.4.4 Usage of Sector Beam

###### 2.4.3.3.4.4.0-1 Usage of Sector Beam Sector beam (ID: 11155830)

**2.4.3.3.4.4.0-1.0-8**  (ID: `12715186`)

Usage of Sector Beam
**1. Single sector beam**

a. FD beamforming is not enabled(Note : FD BF not enabled implies that either (dlEcpriFdBeamforming && ulEcpriFdBeamforming) is false or actFdmEnhancedScheduling is false)

i. if actDlMuMimo in PsCell\_CellSetupReq is true, the UL beam for receiving HARQ feedback over short PUCCH shall be switched to the sector beam.

ii. If more than one UEs’ CSI feedback in one symbol need to be received in one UL subcell and these users have different beams, the UL beam for that subcell shall be switched to the sector beam.

iii. The pattern ID of sector beam is provided by the parameter halfSectorBeamPatternId/ fullSectorBeamPatternId in bfSetup (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2480\_replaced([11288965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp-J8fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2483\_replaced([11289095](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp-xBfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

b. FD beamforming is enabled

i. If FD beamforming is enabled (ulEcpriFdBeamforming==True, 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2525\_replaced([11297471](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqGs3_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) AND Long PUCCH is used for FR1 TDD, then the usage of sector beam for Long PUCCH is defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2215\_replaced([11113083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpaSLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**2. Two sector beam**

a. two half-sector beams can be combined into single 4RX sector beam used for PUCCH Format 1 resources

b. single full-sector beam can be used as 2nd Xpol of 4RX receiver for PUCCH Format 3 resources

c. The sector beam patternID is obtained as follows:

If cell.numOfSubSectors >0,

the sector beam patternID is provided  in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1924\_replaced([11111609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpOsAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else,

the patternId of sector beam is provided by the parameters fullSectorBeamPatternId/halfSectorBeamPatternId in bfSetup (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2480\_replaced([11288965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp-J8fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 2483 and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2218\_replaced([11113106](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpa5MvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

d. UCA cell

i. for FR1-TDD, eCPRI, Pusch is 4Rx(numOfRxPusch=4, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **, same UL beam selection is performed for all UL channels according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2212\_replaced([11113055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpaSI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and PUCCH in 4Rx(numOfRxPusch=4, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) case shall be configured with the same UL specific beams as other UL channels, instead of sector beam (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6\_replaced([11094165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaBcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2475\_replaced([11288953](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp9i9vvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

e. In case Pusch is 4Rx(numOfRxPusch=4, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UE is configured with 2 SSB beams, refer to 5G\_L2\_12949\_replaced([11155780](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_247hI_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

f.      In case actUca is set to false (see [10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), actBeamforming is set to true (see [10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks is set to 1 (see [10966509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UPCB_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and numberOfRxBeamforming is set to 4RX (see [10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G-L2-PS shall use two half-sector beams on PUSCH and PRACH (see [13256670](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_UInXtMlGEfCYBIz6acpPHw)).

**3. Four sector beam**

a. four quarter-sector beams can be combined into single 8RX sector beam used for PUCCH Format 1 and Format3 resources (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2037\_replaced([11112372](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

b. Beam Selection for PUCCH

The sector beam patternID is obtained as follows:

If cell.numOfSubSectors >0,

the sector beam patternID is provided in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1924\_replaced([11111609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpOsAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else,

the patternId of sector beam is provided by the parameters quarterSectorBeamPatternId in bfSetup (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19228\_replaced([11113847](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpejmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2480\_replaced([11288965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp-J8fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2483\_replaced([11289095](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tp-xBfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

c. In case  Pusch/Pucch is 8Rx,*or Pusch is 16Rx  (see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if UE is configured with 2 SSB beams(beamset with only 2 SSB beams configured) Refer to 5G\_L2\_9602\_replaced([11155694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466E_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or PUSCH and 5G\_L2\_12901\_replaced([11155736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466G_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PUCCH. [CB013442-D]If actBeamformingFdd (see [10967202: PsCell\_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , sector beam for 8RX is as per [12704269: Beam ID for UL](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_AKvQAWB3EfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). [End CB013442-D] [CB008577-A]If FDD Cell with actBeamforming = TRUE, sector beam usage for 8RX is as per [12823081: Beam usage for UL](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_GGTJ4KpBEfCrANRYxAlR5A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).[End CB008577-A]*

Note: L2-PS may receive fullSectorBeamPatternId, halfSectorBeamPatternId, quarterSectorBeamPatternId via PSCell\_BeamConfigUpdateReq from CP-RT ([12536044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_wLou8R9PEfCQJ5daVHVkgg)) in case of online change of leftEdgeAngle or rightEdgeAngle according to [11288965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_zFZoUfvXEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

### 2.4.3.4 Mapping between Beam ID and Pattern ID

**2.4.3.4.0-4**  (ID: `11155907`)

Mapping between Beam ID and Pattern ID
The actual physical grid of beams layout design is described in a separate document, shared with the RU.

The document is stored in:

 mMIMO RU platforms product definition: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/SmartRadiobelow6GHzTechnologyDevelopment/eCPRIcommonimplementation/Shared%20Documents/MmimoRadioPlaformProductDefinition.pptx?d=w4cdde7b5c739476384cb857d8ca8c123&csf=1&e=VF4Kq5>

[CNI-59628] CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1912\_replaced([11111535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpOE7_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CNI-59628]

#### 2.4.3.5 Indication of the RF Pattern ID and DL/UL Beam Direction to 5G-L1-DL

##### 2.4.3.5.0-1 Indication of the RF Pattern ID and DL/UL Beam Direction to 5G-L1-DL Sending message DlData_PatternConfigReq (ID: 11155919)

**2.4.3.5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11155931`)

Indication of the RF Pattern ID and DL/UL Beam Direction to 5G-L1-DL
[Before 5GC001626-B] At each NR-Slot, 5G-L2-PS shall send one message DlData\_PatternConfigReq to ABIL #0 in [5GC000425-A] to configure the beam (Pattern ID) and its direction (transmission or reception) on OFDM or RACH symbol basis. [End 5GC001626-B]

[5GC001626-B] At each NR-Slot, [PR504708] out of eCPRI FR2 case [End PR504708][PR566435] and out of CPRI FR2 case [End PR566435],

5G-L2-PS shall send one message DlData\_PatternConfigReq on each configured subcell on each L1 instance. [End 5GC001626-B]

[PR566435] in FR2, CPRI only and [End PR566435] [PR504708] in FR2, eCPRI only, 5G-L2-PS shall send one message DlData\_PatternConfigReq on only one configured subcell in each L1 instance. L2 shall use l1PoolId to determine which subcell is activated on which L1 instance and how many L1 instances are active. See 5G\_L2\_IF\_75\_replaced([10967982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UVvtvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details. There shall not be any assumption about the subcellId values used. [End PR504708]

[Before PR468484] [5GC000920-BA] If no beamforming is applied, [Before PR585488, PR582875] i.e. ("actBeamforming = False" && "beamFormingType = None" received via PsCell\_CellSetupReq) [PR585488, PR582875] see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2183\_replaced([11112736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZEBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_907\_replaced([11353384](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub-6UvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End PR585488, PR582875], there is no need for BF antenna pattern configuration as well as RF calibration even for TDD. Therefore, DlData\_PatternConfigReq is not needed and it not sending to L1. [End PR468484] [End 5GC000920-BA]

[PR468484] [5GC000920-BA] [5GC001091-L] If TDD, DlData\_PatternConfigReq is always sent to L1, even if beamforming is not applied [Before PR585488, PR582875] ("actBeamforming = False" && "beamFormingType = None" received via PsCell\_CellSetupReq) [PR585488, PR582875] see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2183\_replaced([11112736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZEBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_907\_replaced([11353384](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub-6UvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End PR585488, PR582875] BFCM messages from L1 to RU will then be sent, and be discarded by RU if not needed. [End PR468484] [End 5GC000920-BA] [End 5GC001091-L]. Exception: in case of [CB009543-A] TDD or FDD [End CB009543-A]supercell mode DlData\_PatternConfigReq is not sent, see 5G\_UP\_5471\_replaced([11353418](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub_hVfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2183\_replaced([11112736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZEBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_7884\_replaced([11153463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24uFzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[5GC000920-AA] In FDD, DlData\_PatternConfigReq is never sent. [End 5GC000920-AA]

[Before 5GC001626-B] In case of 2 ABIL communicating with one L2-RT instance, each ABIL shall receive a DlData\_PatternConfigReq message. Message DlData\_PatternConfigReq shall be identical. [End 5GC001626-B]

[5GC001626-B] In case of 2 L1 instances communicating with one L2-RT instance, each L1 instance shall receive same set of DlData\_PatternConfigReq messages. Expect subcellId value which is different in each DlData\_PatternConfigReq message, set of messages DlData\_PatternConfigReq received by each L1 instance shall be identical.

* How to set content of the DlData\_PatternConfigReq message is defined in 5G\_L2\_486\_replaced([11155958](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248IO_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* How to divide DlData\_PatternConfigReq in a subcell multiplicity and send it to 5G-L1-DL is defined in 5G\_L2\_500\_replaced([11156046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248vUvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Please check also the interface chapter for the complete description of the message and for the timing requirements (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_53 and 5G\_RAN\_UP\_3949 respectively).

[End 5GC001626-B]

[Before 5GC001626-B] Selection of the destination address:

The destination addresses are provided in DlData\_AddressResp for each subcell handled by the L2-PS instance but L2-PS has to send one DlData\_PatternConfigReq per ABIK (each handling two subcells from each L2-PS instance) -> L2-PS shall send only one DlData\_PatternConfigReq for two subcells which have same address (i.e. one message per unique address).

[End 5GC001626-B]

Note: patternID could be determined by different schedulers for different physical channels of the same NR Slot (DL scheduler for some physical channels and UL scheduler for others), therefore a coordination is needed between them to fill properly the message content.

The information exchange can happen at the earliest after the beam selection phase. The details are left to the implementation.

---

**2.4.3.5.0-2**  (ID: `11155945`)

Indication of the RF Pattern ID and DL/UL Beam Direction to 5G-L1-DL
Compilation of DlData\_PatternConfigReq for Refined Beams

If beam refinement is switched on, see NIDD parameter SBTS/NGNODEB/NGCELL/nrBtsBeamRefinementP2, CSI-RS for L1 RSRP measurement are transmitted on the same symbols as SS/PBCH blocks, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4130\_replaced([11173889](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuXDnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Whereas for the SS/PBCH block the same beam ID is used for 4 consecutive symbols, the CSI-RS transmissions use 4 different refined beam directions on these 4 symbols, i.e., one direction per symbol. The beam ID valid for these CSI-RS transmissions is derived from the beam ID if the SS/PBCH block transmitted on the same symbols such that the refined beams belong to the SSB beams of this SS/PBCH block as displayed in the following figure for a single slot with 2 SS/PBCH blocks:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid-like structure, likely representing a resource allocation or scheduling diagram, possibly for wireless communication systems. The grid is divided into cells, with some cells colored and containing text labels. The top row of the grid is numbered from 0 to 13, indicating columns.
There are several colored blocks within the grid:
- Red blocks are located in columns 1, 2, 3, and 4. Each red block contains text "beam ID" followed by a four-digit number.
- Column 1: "beam ID 00001000"
- Column 2: "beam ID 00010000"
- Column 3: "beam ID 00011000"
- Column 4: "beam ID 00100000"
- Yellow blocks are located in columns 7, 8, 9, and 10. Each yellow block contains text "beam ID" followed by a four-digit number.
- Column 7: "beam ID 00001001"
- Column 8: "beam ID 00010001"
- Column 9: "beam ID 00011001"
- Column 10: "beam ID 00100001"
- Green blocks are located below the red blocks, spanning columns 2, 3, and 4, and also below the yellow blocks, spanning columns 8 and 9.
- The green block in columns 2-4 has the text "beam ID 00000000" below it.
- The green block in columns 8-9 has the text "beam ID 00000001" below it.
- A blue rectangular block is located within the green block in columns 3 and 4.
- A yellow rectangular block is located in column 1, below the red block.
- A blue rectangular block is located within the green block in columns 8 and 9.
Below the grid, there are three colored labels:
- A yellow rectangle with the text "PSS".
- A blue rectangle with the text "SSS".
- A green rectangle with the text "PBCH".
The grid itself is composed of many empty white cells, suggesting that the colored blocks represent allocated resources within a larger grid. The numbering at the top indicates a temporal or frequency division.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2288.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

For the relation between beam IDs of SS/PBCH block and related refined beams, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11982\_replaced([11111700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpP6FfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1937\_replaced([11111754](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpP6H_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The 4 beam IDs for the refined beams shall be ordered increasingly with increasing symbol number.

The pattern ID per symbol is derived from the beam ID as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1939\_replaced([11111782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpQhIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

These pattern IDs have then to be added to message DlData\_PatternConfigReq, see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_60, to the second vector, i.e., second X-Pol beam, of [Before 5GC001626-B] patternIdPol0List and patternIdPol1List [5GC001626-B] patternIdPolList. For symbols not used for CSI-RS transmission, the pattern IDs in the second vector remain unchanged.

NOTE: The parameter rdSyncSignalsTxMode as described in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_827 defines on which polarization the CSI-RS are actually transmitted. If one polarization is skipped, the setting in the respective polarization list will be ignored by 5G-L1-DL and/or 5G-RU.

For testing purpose, the power of single CSI-RS transmissions may be reduced artificially similar as it is done on slot level, see 5G\_L2\_569\_replaced([11157594](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25GgR_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The respective values have to be added to the parameter pwrReductionPerSymb\_dB of message DlData\_CsiRsSendReq.

NOTE: Earlier (before R15 of 3GPP), some symbols had been excluded from CSI-RS transmission by 3GPP. Therefore, it had been foreseen to send CSI-RS to two different directions in the symbols used for CSI-RS transmission for beam refinement. However, now all symbols are allowed for that purpose and hence only 4 AxC instead of 6 are needed, i.e., 2 for SS/PBCH block transmission and 2 for CSI-RS transmission, both polarizations each.

Originally, the intention was to occupy the whole carrier for CSI-RS transmission but leaving the PRBs allocated by SS/PBCH block punctured. However, 3GPP did not consistently introduce this option and it won’t be fixed in R15; therefore, only either the PRBs below the SS/PBCH blocks or those above can be used. Which part to be used is controlled by NIDD parameter csirsBmMgmtSubband. StartPRB and numofPRBs have to be set accordingly. The idea to have that adjustable is to configure adjacent cell differently in order to avoid interference. In addition, also the RE allocation inside the PRBs can be adjusted differently.

The source for this figure can be found at:

File: CSI-RS for Beam Refinement.xlxs

Folder: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane?csf=1&web=1&e=V8ishU>

---

##### 2.4.3.5.1 Setting content of DlData_PatternConfigReq

###### 2.4.3.5.1.0-1 Setting content of DlData_PatternConfigReq The most relevant IEs of the DlData_PatternConfigReq message are set as described in followings sections: (ID: 11155958)

**2.4.3.5.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11155987`)

Setting content of DlData\_PatternConfigReq
Setting txRxBitmapPol IE:

* it shall contain the sequence bits 0..(numOfPatternIdPol-1)
* bit 0:

 set to 1 if signal must be transmitted in DL at OFDM symbol 0,

 set to 0 if signal must be received in UL at OFDM symbol 0 or in case of no tx/rx at this symbol;

* bit 1:

 set to 1 if signal must be transmitted in DL at OFDM symbol 1,

 set to 0 if signal must be received in UL at OFDM symbol 1 or in case of no tx/rx at this symbol;

* ..
* bit (numOfPatternIdPol-1):

 set to 1 if signal must be transmitted in DL at OFDM (numOfPatternIdPol-1),

 set to 0 if signal must be received in UL at OFDM or in case of no tx/rx at this symbol;

* where bit 0 is the least significant bit of txRxBitmapPol IE, and so on.
* For PRACH, the UL OFDM symbols refer to PRACH symbols.

---

**2.4.3.5.1.0-1.1.0-3**  (ID: `11156008`)

Setting Pattern IDs informations:
Mapping of patternIdPolList per channel type inside a subcell

---

**2.4.3.5.1.0-1.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11156020`)

Setting Pattern IDs informations:
In below section, first XPolBeams corresponds to (bfcmIndex[0], bfcmIndex[1]), second XPolBeams corresponds to (bfcmIndex[2], bfcmIndex[3]).

o For dlMimoMode = 'CL 2x2 MIMO' or 'OL 2x2 MIMO' and for ulMimoMode = 'CL 2x2 MIMO':

 Polarizations +45° and -45° are associated with respectively L1 subcell PI antenna ports 0 and 1.

 For the case that dlSubcellType is ‘C4’, two XPolBeams are associated to the DL subcell.

* For symbols associated to PDCCH, if L2-PS needs to send DCIs to two beam directions with one C4 subcell, the pattern IDs for fist XPolBeam of the subcell is the beam at one direction, the pattern IDs for the second XPolBeam of the subcell is the beam at another direction, and pdcchPrecodingOption4x4 shall be set to repetitionTwoXPol (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_77).
* For symbols associated to PDSCH,

 L2-PS can send PDSCH to first and/or second XPolBeam.

 If PDSCH is transmitted with streamIndex {0, 1}, the pattern ID of the first XPolBeam of the subcell shall be associated with the best beam.

 If PDSCH is transmitted with streamIndex {2, 3}, the pattern ID of the second XPolBeam of the subcell shall be associated with the best beam.

o For dlMimoMode = 'OL 4x4 or 4x2 MIMO' or 'CL 4x4 or 4x2 MIMO':

 For dlSubcellType ‘C4’, two XPolBeams are associated to the DL subcell.

* For symbols associated to PDCCH,

 if numOfSubSectors = 2, first and second XPolBeams  (see column beamIndex) shall be set as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1924\_replaced([11111609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpOsAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) else,

 first XPolBeam (lowest index) of the subcell is the best beam selected (see mapping in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_57 and 5G\_UP\_2501\_replaced([11389876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufQQtvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). When rdPdcchPrecodingOption4X4 value is '2' (single beam), the pattern IDs for the second XPolBeam of the subcell are set to 0xFFFF (unused). Otherwise the second XPolBeam of the subcell is associated to the second best beam.

* If L2-PS needs to send DCIs to two beam directions with one C4 subcell, the pattern IDs for fist XPolBeam of the subcell is the beam at one direction, the pattern IDs for the second XPolBeam of the subcell is the beam at another direction, and pdcchPrecodingOption4x4 shall be set to repetitionTwoXPol (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_77).
* For symbols associated to PDSCH,

 if numOfSubSectors = 2, first and second XPolBeams (see column beamIndex) shall be set as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1924\_replaced([11111609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpOsAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) else

 The first XPolBeam of the subcell is the best beam selected (see mapping in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_57).

 If the rank of the scheduled PDSCH transmission is 3 or 4, the second XPolBeam of the subcell is associated to the second best beam.

 Otherwise, the second XPolBeam of the subcell is associated to the second best beam.

 rdPortSelectionPattenIdOff is replaced by rdPdschPrecodingOption4x4 to decide about portSelection.

 L2-PS can send PDSCH to first and/or second XPolBeam.

 If PDSCH is transmitted with streamIndex {0, 1}, the pattern ID of the first XPolBeam of the subcell shall be associated with the best beam.

 If PDSCH is transmitted with streamIndex {2, 3}, the pattern ID of the second XPolBeam of the subcell shall be associated with the best beam.

 If PDSCH is transmitted with streamIndex {0, 1, 2, 3}, the pattern ID of the first XPolBeam of the subcell shall be associated with the best beam, the second XPolBeam of the subcell is associated to the second best beam. The second XPolBeam of the subcell is associated to the second best beam. About definition of streamIndex, please see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_111.

* For symbol(s) associated to CSI-RS for channel measurement,

if numOfSubSectors = 2, first and second XPolBeams (see column beamIndex) shall be set as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1924\_replaced([11111609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpOsAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) else

 For symbol(s) associated to CSI-RS for channel measurement, first XPolBeam is the best beam selected (see mapping in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_57). The second XPolBeam is associated to the second best beam.

o  When dlMimoMode "CL 8x4 MIMO" , one or two PMI beams are used for the channels PDSCH, PDCCH. CSI-RS beams do not have the same patternIds as PDSCH and PDCCH.

For dlSubcellType ‘C4’, two XPolBeams are associated to the DL subcell. Refer to 5G\_L2\_497\_replaced([11156027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248vT_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

o For SSBlocks, the first XPolBeam of the subcell shall be used. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1894 for symbol mapping.

[Before 5GC002538-D] For the PDSCH transmitted in the SSB slot and the dlSubcellType is C4, the PDSCH shall be transmitted with streamIndex {2,3}, the pattern ID of the second XPolBeam of the subcell shall be associated with the best beam.

[End 5GC002538-D]

o if dlSubcellType is set to 'C4':

 If messages DlData\_CsiRsSendReq with field trsInfo set to ENABLED, then XPolBeam associated to the CSI-RS carried on this subcell are used for tracking. Mapping of symbol and beamId, associated to ssbBeamIndex, are defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2343\_replaced([11114897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpo7p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). AxC and CPRI mapping are described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1896\_replaced([11114916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpo7rPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in table below.

o If message DlData\_CsirsSendReq used for beam refinement is sent to a subcell during a slot, then XPolBeam associated to the CSI-RS carried on this subcell is defined in 5G\_L2\_484\_replaced([11155945](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248IOfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (symbol and BeamId mapping). CPRI mapping is defined in table below.

o Pattern IDs of unused streams in the DlData\_PatternConfigReq message shall have the value 65535 = 0xFFFF. This value indicates a special beamforming pattern which lets the RF blank these streams by applying a beamforming coefficient with zero amplitude in DL and UL.

o In order to have a minimum duration for a beam, the Pattern ID associated with a GP shall be equal to the Pattern ID of the following UL symbol, whatever the type of slot (GP before PUSCH, GP before PUCCH, GP before PRACH if any).

o For conformance test, the Pattern ID associated with a GP shall be set to 0xFFFF.

o Note: if two kinds of CSI-RS share the same subcell (for example CSI-RS for BM and CSI-RS for tracking) then all resources will be listed in the same message type DlData\_CsiRsSendReq and CSI-RS for different subcells are sent in different messages.

o For PRACH, the UL OFDM symbols refer to PRACH symbols (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1302 for the PRACH formats) and the Pattern ID for symbols s = 0 to 13 shall be set according to SSB beam associated with the PRACH occasion and to the PRACH config index. For all unused entries, Pattern ID shall be set to 0xFFFF.

o For symbol(s) associated to PUCCH CSI reporting,

If (there’s only one UE allocated with CSI F2 resources in this UL subcell )

The XPolBeams of the UL subcell should select UE’s best beam.

Else if (all UEs allocated with CSI F2 resources in this UL subcell have the same best beam )

The XPolBeams of the UL subcell should select UE’s best beam.

Else

The XPolBeams of the UL subcell should select sector beam.

---

**2.4.3.5.1.0-1.1.0-4**  (ID: `11156027`)

Setting Pattern IDs informations:
Mapping of patternIdPolList per channel type inside a subcell for Closed Loop 8x4 MIMO

---

**2.4.3.5.1.0-1.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11156033`)

Setting Pattern IDs informations:
When dlMimoMode "CL 8x4 MIMO", one or two PMI beams are used for the UE's PDSCH, PDCCH. CSI-RS beams do not have the same patternIds as PDSCH and PDCCH.

Scheduling of PDSCH, PDCCH for Paging/SI has the same setting as rank 1, except the beam is sweeping all the SSB beams that are configured to the cell.

Scheduling of PDSCH, PDCCH for msg2/4 shall be the same setting as rank 1, default precoding and beam, refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3189\_replaced([11139327](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For dlSubcellType ‘C4’, two XPolBeams are associated to the DL subcell. Pattern IDs for unused XPolBeams shall be set to 0xFFFF (unused).

o For symbols associated with PDCCH for DL scheduling, best beam is selected for each UE based UE's CSI feedback, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2201\_replaced([11113014](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), or default beam refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3188\_replaced([11139322](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Each PDCCH need 2 fronthaul streams.

[Before 5GC000524-L]

Pattern IDs for the first XPolBeam shall be filled and PdcchPrecodingOption4X4 (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_77) shall be set to repetitionOneXPol.

[5GC000524-L]

To transmit PDCCH signal, L2-RT shall follow below rules to fill pattern IDs and precoding options:

1) If DCI is transmitted with the first XPolBeam of the subcell, pattern IDs for the first XPolBeam shall be filled and pdcchPrecodingOption4x4 shall be set to repetitionOneXPol.

2) If DCI is transmitted with the second XPolBeam of the subcell, pattern IDs for the second XPolBeam shall be filled and pdcchPrecodingOption4x4 shall be set to repetition2ndXPol.

[End 5GC000524-L]

o For symbols associated with PDCCH for UL scheduling, related beam is SSB/refined beam for UL which is selected by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2018\_replaced([11112156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpTkdvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Each PDCCH need 2 fronthaul streams.

Pattern IDs for the first XPolBeam shall be filled and PdcchPrecodingOption4X4 (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_77) shall be set to repetitionOneXPol.

o For symbols associated with PDSCH, first/second/best beam is selected for each UE based UE's CSI feedback, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2201\_replaced([11113014](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), or default beam refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3188\_replaced([11139322](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 If the rank of the scheduled PDSCH transmission is 2 (without port selection) or 3 or 4, the first and second XPolBeam of the subcell is associated to the two PMI beams with the order reported by UE. The streamIndex (see 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_111) shall be set to {0, 1, 2, 3}.

[Before 5GC000524-L]

 If the rank of the scheduled PDSCH transmission is 1, pattern IDs for the first XPolBeam shall be filled and streamIndex shall be set to {0, 1}.

[5GC000524-L]

 If the rank of the scheduled PDSCH transmission is 1 or 2 (with port selection):

1) If PDSCH is transmitted with the first XPolBeam of the subcell, pattern IDs for the first XPolBeam shall be filled and streamIndex shall be set to {0, 1}.

2) If PDSCH is transmitted with the second XPolBeam of the subcell, pattern IDs for the second XPolBeam shall be filled and streamIndex shall be set to {2, 3}.

[End 5GC000524-L]

o For symbol(s) associated to CSI-RS for channel measurement, 4 patternIDs for each CSI-RS refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2194\_replaced([11112829](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 If this is the first symbol of row8 CSI-RS, the two XPolBeams shall be set as the two CSI-RS beams of port3000,3001,3004,3005 of the first symbol according to port-AxC mapping order defined in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_832. The first XPolBeam is beam for port 3000 and 3004, the second XPolBeam is beam for port 3001 and 3005.

 Else (i.e. the second symbol) the first and second XPolBeam shall be set as the two CSI-RS beams of port3000,3001,3004,3005 of the second symbol according to port-AxC mapping order defined in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_832.

---

##### 2.4.3.5.2 Transport of DlData_PatternConfigReq message to 5G-L1-DL

**2.4.3.5.2.0-3**  (ID: `11156069`)

Transport of DlData\_PatternConfigReq message to 5G-L1-DL
CPRI: Computation of numOfXpolBeams, bfcmOffset and patternIdPolList based on bfcmIndex

As indication and only for FR1, here is one formula proposed for numOfXpolBeams calculation in a given DlData\_PatternConfigReq message sent to DL subcellid i:

 for each slot:

o numOfXpolBeams = ((MAX(bfcmIndex) - MIN(bfcmIndex)) + 1) /2

o bfcmOffset = MIN(bfcmIndex)

With:

o bfcmIndex is taken from PsCell\_CellSetupReq-> dlSubcell (5G\_L2\_IF\_75\_replaced([10967982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UVvtvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

As indication and only for FR1 here is one formula proposed for patternIdPolList IE calculation in a given DlData\_PatternConfigReq message sent to DL subcell for a given symbol s:

 for each slot:

* patternIdPolList IE[s][0 .. 2\* numOfXpolBeams – 1] = index belonging to range [ bfcmOffset; bfcmOffset + 2 \* numOfXpolBeams - 1].

Note: During UL symbol, patternIdPolList values are computed based on UL subcells information in (5G\_L2\_23011\_replaced([11155919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248INvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_L2\_486\_replaced([11155958](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248IO_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), but it is transmitted on message DlData\_PatternConfigReq going to 5G-L1-DL which is using only DL subcell related parameter. With these 2 proposed formulas, all patternIdPolList computed during UL symbol is sure to be totally transported over the different patternIdPolList IE addressed to the DL subcells because there are always same or more DL streams than UL one. Please see 5G\_UP\_2490\_replaced([11389795](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufPpsvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to see rules to be applied by OAM to bfcmIndex values received on each subcells to have these formulas valid.

For FR2, only index 0 and 1 of patternIdPol have patternId value different from 0xFFFF. It means there shall be at least one message DlData\_PatternConfigReq sent per ABIL having numOfXpolBeams = 1 and bfcmOffset = 0. Other DlData\_PatternConfigReq messages sent per ABIL can have numOfXpolBeams = 0.

Here are two examples in FR1 with different bfcmIndex IE received for UL subcells:

First example: 1 L1 instance with 1 C4 on each NrCellGrp (Mapping table in 5G\_UP\_2501\_replaced([11389876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufQQtvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

PsCell\_CellSetupReq-> dlSubcell:

* dlSubcellId 0, subcellType = C4, numBfcmIndex = 4, bfcmIndex = [0,1,2,3]
* dlSubcellId 1, subcellType = C4, numBfcmIndex = 4, bfcmIndex = [4,5,6,7]

PsCell\_CellSetupReq-> ulSubcell:

* ulSubcellId 0, subcellType = C2, numBfcmIndex = 2, bfcmIndex = [0,1]
* ulSubcellId 1, subcellType = C2, numBfcmIndex = 2, bfcmIndex = [4,5]

 on each slot:

o DlData\_PatternConfigReq send to DL subcellId 0:

 numOfXpolBeams = (3 – 0 + 1)/2= 2

 bfcmOffset = 0

 patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = index belonging to range [0 .. 3]

 During DL symbol, patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = bfcmIndex = [0,1,2,3] of dlSubcellId = 0.

 During UL symbol, patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = bfcmIndex = [0,1] of ulSubcellId = 0 and patternIdPolList[s][2 .. 3] = 0xFFFF.

o DlData\_PatternConfigReq send to DL subcellId 1:

 numOfXpolBeams = (3 – 0 + 1)/2= 2

 bfcmOffset = 4

 patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = index belonging to range [4; 7]

 During DL symbol, patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = bfcmIndex = [4,5,6,7] of dlSubcellId = 1.

 During UL symbol, patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = bfcmIndex = [4,5] of ulSubcellId = 1 and patternIdPolList[s][2 .. 3] = 0xFFFF.

Second example: 1 L1 instance with 1 C4 on each NrCellGrp (Mapping table in 5G\_UP\_2501\_replaced([11389876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufQQtvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with different bfcmIndex IE received for ulSubcellId 1:

PsCell\_CellSetupReq-> dlSubcell:

* dlSubcellId 0, subcellType = C4, numBfcmIndex = 4, bfcmIndex = [0,1,2,3]
* dlSubcellId 1, subcellType = C4, numBfcmIndex = 4, bfcmIndex = [4,5,6,7]

PsCell\_CellSetupReq-> ulSubcell (different bfcmIndex mapping):

* ulSubcellId 0, subcellType = C2, numBfcmIndex = 2, bfcmIndex = [0,1]
* ulSubcellId 1, subcellType = C2, numBfcmIndex = 2, bfcmIndex = [2,3]

 on each slot:

o DlData\_PatternConfigReq send to DL subcellId 0:

 numOfXpolBeams = (3 – 0 + 1)/2= 2

 bfcmOffset = 0

 patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = index belonging to range [0 .. 3]

 During DL symbol, patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = bfcmIndex = [0,1,2,3] of dlSubcellId = 0.

 During UL symbol, patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = bfcmIndex = [0,1,2,3] of ulSubcellId =0 and 1.

o DlData\_PatternConfigReq send to DL subcellId 1:

 numOfXpolBeams = (3 – 0 + 1)/2= 2

 bfcmOffset = 4

 patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = index belonging to range [4; 7]

 During DL symbol, patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = bfcmIndex = [4,5,6,7] of dlSubcellId = 1.

 During UL symbol, patternIdPolList[s][0 .. 3] = 0xFFFF.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating a data flow within a system, likely related to telecommunications or signal processing. The diagram shows several components and their interactions, with arrows indicating the direction of data or control signals.
Here's a breakdown of the components and their relationships:
**Left Side:**
\* **OAM:** A green square labeled "OAM" (likely Operations, Administration, and Maintenance) is the starting point, with an arrow pointing to "CP-RT".
\* **CP-RT:** A cyan rectangle labeled "CP-RT" (likely Control Plane - Real-Time) receives input from OAM. It has two outputs, each labeled "C4".
\* The top "C4" output is associated with a box containing "listBfcmIndex = {0;1;2;3}".
\* The bottom "C4" output is associated with a box containing "listBfcmIndex = {4;5;6;7}".
**Middle Section (L2-RT):**
\* A yellow rectangle labeled "L2-RT: 2 NrCellGrp each one with 1 C4" encloses two sub-components.
\* **NrCellGrp 0:** A blue rectangle on top, receiving input from the top "C4" from CP-RT.
\* **NrCellGrp 1:** An orange rectangle below, receiving input from the bottom "C4" from CP-RT.
\* Each "NrCellGrp" has an output labeled "C4" that feeds into a data structure representation.
\* **NrCellGrp 0's C4 output:** Leads to a data structure labeled "DIData\_PatternConfigReq (numOfXpolBeams = 2, bfcmOffset = 0)". This data structure is depicted as a 2D array or matrix. The rows are indexed from 0 to 13 (indicated by "numOfPatternldPol(14)"). The columns are indexed from 0 to 3, and each cell contains a value. The top row shows values 0, 1, 2, 3. The bottom row also shows 0, 1, 2, 3. The label "2*numOfXpolBeams(2)" suggests the width of the data.
\** *NrCellGrp 1's C4 output:* *Leads to a data structure labeled "DIData\_PatternConfigReq (numOfXpolBeams = 2, bfcmOffset = 4)". This data structure is also a 2D array. The rows are indexed from 0 to 13. The columns are indexed from 0 to 3. The top row shows values 4, 5, 6, 7. The bottom row also shows 4, 5, 6, 7. The label "2*numOfXpolBeams(2)" is also present.
**Right Side (Loner L1 SW):**
\* A large gray rectangle labeled "Loner L1 SW" (likely Layer 1 Software in a "Loner" component) receives inputs from the "C4" outputs of the "NrCellGrp" components.
\* Two arrows, labeled "bfcmOffset = 0" and "bfcmOffset = 4", point to specific positions within a data structure inside the "Loner L1 SW" block.
\* The data structure within "Loner L1 SW" is a 2D array representing a "BFCM control vector (VSD)".
\* The columns are indexed from 0 to 7 and beyond (indicated by "...").
\* The rows are indexed from 0 to 13 (indicated by "Nb Control block -1=13").
\* The top row shows values 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and a final cell labeled "D".
\* The bottom row shows values 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and a final cell labeled "D".
\* The label "MAX\_CONTROL\_VECTOR\_PER\_CONTROL\_BLOCK -1" is present near the top right.
\* The label "D = Default value OXFFFF" explains the meaning of "D".
\* Two arrows originate from the "Loner L1 SW" block and point outwards, suggesting further processing or output.
The diagram visually represents how configuration requests, including beam information and offsets, are processed by different layers (L2-RT and L1 SW) to generate a control vector. The `listBfcmIndex` seems to define which beams are configured, and `bfcmOffset` determines the starting point for these configurations within the control vector.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2307.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
