##### 2.4.3.5.3 eCPRI

###### 2.4.3.5.3.0-3 eCPRI Ecpri FCP optimization on L2-L1 interface (ID: 11156092)

**2.4.3.5.3.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11156110`)

eCPRI
[Before CB008205-F]

For Ecpri FCP optimization, following action shall be taken by 5G-L2-PS DL scheduler.

 During each DL and special slot, considering slot IE provided in L1 message, for each active DL subcell (see 5G\_UP\_1760\_replaced([11372591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ud93TfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), considering only messages in DL\_Scheduler\_To\_DL\_Subcell list, L2 shall count number of messages in DL\_Scheduler\_To\_DL\_Subcell list sent to this DL subcell:

o If count is 0, DlData\_EmptySendReq (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_103) shall be sent to this subcell with lastMsg IE = 1.

o Otherwise only on last message sent to this subcell, lastMsg IE shall be set to 1.

o If lastMsg IE is set to 1, numberOfMsg IE shall be filled with total number of messages sent to this DL subcell including DlData\_EmptySendReq.

o lastMsgCount IE shall always be filled to 2 as UL scheduler may also send DlData\_PdcchSendReq to this DL subcell.

 During each UL slot,  considering slot IE provided in L1 message, for each UL subcell setting up, considering only message in DL\_Scheduler\_To\_UL\_Subcell list, L2 shall count number of message in UL\_Scheduler\_To\_UL\_Subcell list sent to this UL subcell:

o If count is 0, UlData\_EmptySendReq (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_261) shall be sent to this subcell with lastMsg IE = 1.

o Otherwise only on last message sent to this subcell, lastMsg IE shall be set to 1.

o If lastMsg IE is set to 1, numberOfMsg IE shall be filled with total number of message sent to this UL subcell including UlData\_EmptySendReq.

o lastMsgCount IE shall always be filled to 2 as UL scheduler may also sent a message from UL\_Scheduler\_To\_UL\_Subcell list to this UL subcell

 DlData\_EmptySendReq can also be sent as a compensation for a “last message” from DL\_Scheduler\_To\_DL\_Subcell list that was planned to be sent but finally result to not in the given slot for some reason. In any case, the use of DlData\_EmptySendReq as an indication of « last message » and not « no message » as it is described previously shall not increase number of message sent to L1 as it shall always be sent instead of a forecast message. Here are some scenarios identified:

o no more data buffer for the only user scheduled in a slot

o No more HARQ process available.

o In FR2 on ABIO only, overload mecanism prevent sending a given message.

 This requirement is applicable also for CPRI case.

Message in DL\_Scheduler\_To\_DL\_Subcell list to be considered (DlData\_XXXSendReq message type):

 DlData\_PdcchSendReq

 DlData\_PdschSendReq

 DlData\_SsBlockSendReq

 DlData\_CsiRsSendReq

Message in DL\_Scheduler\_To\_UL\_Subcell list to be considered (UlData\_XXXReceiveReq message type):

 UlData\_PucchReceiveReq

See 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_112 for lastMsg, lastMsgCount and numberOfMsg definition.

Note: this functionality is coded for all hardwares to avoid as much as possible HW dependent code at L2 side but functionality will never be used in ABIL as 5G-L1 ABIL (Loner) has not planned to implement it.

[End CB008205-F]

---

### 2.4.3.6 UL neighbor beam random selection for FR1 (digital beamforming)

**2.4.3.6.0-2**  (ID: `11156130`)

UL neighbor beam random selection for FR1 (digital beamforming)
Out of determined neighbor beams shall be selected randomly 1 beam.

Information about randomly selected beam shall be stored in UE context.

Note: This algorithm is used e.g. in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2032\_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.3.7 UL Beam Control from Periodic SRS Measurement

#### 2.4.3.7.1 P SRS CoMa Handling

##### 2.4.3.7.1.0-1 P SRS CoMa Handling (ID: 13482991)

**2.4.3.7.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13452539`)

P SRS CoMa Handling
[CB015122-A]When actSrsBmCalcInL1 is set to True in PsCnfg\_AddressDistributionReq ([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), then 5G-L2-PS shall receive only the instantaneous CoMa validity indication (see [11154046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24yXNfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Further part of the requirement applies only to the actSrsBmCalcInL1 set to False.[End CB015122-A]

After scheduling P SRS in 5G-L2-PS, L1 shall report UlData\_SrsReceiveRespBmPs (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_257\_replaced([11514467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR32cvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which carries Covariance Matrix (CoMa).

CoMa of each UE shall be extracted from UlData\_SrsReceiveRespBmPs according to UE RNTI [CNI-157588-H](see [11214253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-xqcvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))[End CNI-157588-H]. Then CoMa shall be processed as defined in [11154028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksqacvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Note:

1. if UE is configured with 2 SRS ports, there will be 2 RNTI with same value map to same UE different cyclic shifts.
2. CaMa values from L1L2 interface (5G\_L1\_IF\_257\_replaced([11514467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR32cvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) need to be processed before be used.

CoMa value in horizontal = CovarianceMatrixHorizontal \* 2^scalingHorizontal

CoMa value in vertical = CovarianceMatrixVertical \* 2^scalingVertical

Do not care about Q(1.0.15) in interface definition.

---

#### 2.4.3.7.2 P SRS CoMa Processing Timing

##### 2.4.3.7.2.0-1 P SRS CoMa Processing Timing (ID: 11156158)

**2.4.3.7.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13452568`)

P SRS CoMa Processing Timing
[CB015122-A]When actSrsBmCalcInL1 is set to True in PsCnfg\_AddressDistributionReq ([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), then 5G-L2-PS shall receive only the instantaneous CoMa validity indication (see [11154046](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24yXNfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Further part of the requirement applies only to the actSrsBmCalcInL1 set to False.[End CB015122-A]

Since there maybe hundreds of UEs with P SRS are scheduled in same OFDM symbol same slot, CoMa handling may time consuming. When to process CoMa need to be considered based on system processing capablity.

Step1: CoMa shall be separated for each UE after receiving L1 feedback UlData\_SrsReceiveRespBmPs. For eCPRI 7-2a (NRBTS.actEcpri72e is set to False), **Store CoMa of first polarization, then do average over polarization/cyclic shift after receiving CoMa of second polarization.** Then do validation of instance CoMa, **If NRBTS.actEcpri72e is set to True, validate the CoMa according to 5G\_L2\_3947\_replaced([11154130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24y-Q_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, then average it according to **5G\_L2\_3948\_replaced([11154107](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24yXP_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** do IIR filtering if it is valid. Store filtered CoMa for each UE.***\******\***

Step2: When rrmUlBeamSelectTimer expires, beam calculation of best and second best beam based on filtered CoMa shall be excuted in L2PS UL scheduler after both DL and UL scheduling, i.e. after post scheduling.  How many UEs can be calculated per slot depends on how many CPU cycle left after reserving 30us (maybe changed after more testing) for other processing

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the "General Flow of CoMa Processing for BF per UE (informative)". It depicts a timeline with various events and processes related to Beamforming (BF) and Channel State Information (CSI) measurements (CoMa) for a User Equipment (UE).
The timeline runs horizontally from left to right. Several vertical lines represent specific points in time or events.
At the beginning of the timeline, there's a downward arrow labeled "UE ATTACH and P SRS configed" originating from "SRS\_BM\_AGED". This signifies the UE attaching and being configured with P SRS.
Following this, there are multiple upward blue arrows, each labeled with "CoMa" followed by a number (CoMa 1, CoMa 2, CoMa 3, CoMa 4, CoMa 5, CoMa 6, CoMa 7, and then "CoMa x" indicated by dots). These likely represent the reception of CoMa reports.
Above the timeline, there are several labels and dotted lines indicating processes:
- "SRS\_BM\_VALID" with an upward pink dashed arrow.
- "Calculate Beam(s)" in green, with a dotted green line extending to the right.
- ".......rrmUIBeam Select Timer" in black dotted text, with a black arrow pointing right. This timer seems to be associated with beam selection.
- "SRS\_BM\_CHGED" with an upward pink dashed arrow.
- "rrmCoMaAgedTimer" in green, with a green dotted line and green arrows indicating a timer that resets or is triggered.
Below the timeline, there are additional labels:
- A horizontal arrow with a dotted line and text "after rrmNumOfCoMa valid CoMa" pointing left, suggesting a condition related to the number of valid CoMa reports.
- A blue upward arrow labeled "last CoMa received" indicating the reception of the final CoMa.
- Another label "rrmCoMaAgedTimer" in green, positioned further to the right.
- "SRS\_BM\_AGED" with an upward pink dashed arrow on the far right.
The diagram uses different colored arrows and lines to represent different types of events or processes:
- Black arrows: Indicate direction of time or a process completion.
- Blue upward arrows: Represent reception of CoMa.
- Pink dashed vertical lines: Indicate state changes like "SRS\_BM\_VALID" or "SRS\_BM\_CHGED" and "SRS\_BM\_AGED".
- Green dotted lines with arrows: Show the duration or effect of timers like "rrmCoMaAgedTimer" and "rrmUIBeam Select Timer".
The overall flow suggests that after UE attachment and SRS configuration, CoMa reports are received sequentially. These CoMa receptions trigger or interact with timers like "rrmUIBeam Select Timer" and "rrmCoMaAgedTimer", which in turn influence beam calculation and potentially lead to state changes like "SRS\_BM\_VALID", "SRS\_BM\_CHGED", or "SRS\_BM\_AGED". The diagram also highlights the concept of a "last CoMa received" and a condition based on the number of valid CoMa reports.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2325.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

---

### 2.4.3.8 UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam

#### 2.4.3.8.0-1 UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam Maintain of dominant UL Beam for UE (ID: 11156193)

**2.4.3.8.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13341693`)

UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam
[CB008577-E] For TDD: [End CB008577-E]

5G-L2-PS need to maintain dominant UL DFT  beam info for each UEs in Cell, including bestH, bestV, the beam updated Xsfn.

If UE configured with period codeBook SRS:

1) if valid DFT beam decoded by SrsEo, 5G-L2-PS need to save latest best DFT beam for the UE:

'ulSrsBmBestBeamIndexH' will saved to bestH, and

'ulSrsBmBestBeamIndexV' will saved to bestV, the two IE defined in  'UlSrsBeamSelectionInd' msg.

'validBeamUpdateXsfn' will update as current Xsfn

2) if receive 'UlSrsBeamSelectionInd' msg with srsBmState = aged from SrsEo, and last DFT beam is valid:

5G-L2-PS need to start a timer for the UE, the timer length equal to 'ulAoAMonitorWindow'.

2.1) if there is valid DFT beam decoded during the timer, 5G-L2-PS need to stop the timer, and save the valid DFT beam as Step 1);

2.1) if there is no valid DFT beam decoded during the timer, 5G-L2-PS need to set bestH/bestV/validBeamUpdateXsfn to invalid value after timeout.

Dominant UL SSB/refine beam should always be the primary SSB/refine beam used for PUSCH transmission.

Note: If 'ulAoAMonitorWindow' is reconfigured by PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq, timer has been started before reconfiguration will keep using the old value. Only timer started after reconfiguration will use the new parameter.

[CB008577-E] For FDD: [End CB008577-E]

5G-L2-PS need to use UE's latest maintaining PUSCH  beam type (PMI , refinement of fixed beam#4, or fixed beam #4 ), with related Beam's  bestH, bestV.

If UE with valid PMI beam , then related full panel PMI beam's bestH and bestV shall be recorded.

Else if UE with refinement of fixed beam#4, then the one is related  fixed beam#4 's bestH and bestV shall be recorded.

else UE with fixed beam#4, then the default full sector beam's bestH and bestV shall be recorded.

endif

ENDIF

Note : For FDD Beamforming (NRCELL/actBeamforming = True and NRCELL.cellTechnology is equal to 'FDD')

1, the ulAoAMonitorWindow will not be configured to L2PS.  Always the latest maintaining PUSCH channel related beam's bestH and bestV shall be used.

2, Because  the DL beam's doaInfo will only be configured to L2PS, then L2PS shall map the PUSCH channel related beam to DL beam first.

CP2 refer [11113859: Determination of dominant UL beam for Positioning](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpejnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

Informative refer  how CP calculate AoA :[11290451: LCS AoA of PMI beams generation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqCbb_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)  , [11289401: LCS AoA Value extracting and transforming for SSB and/or refinement beam](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqAmMfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

#### 2.4.3.8.0-2 UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam Response UL AOA to CPRT (ID: 11156212)

**2.4.3.8.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `13341717`)

UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam
When received PsUser\_RanMeasurementReq with 'angleOfArrivalNrRequired' equal to true,

**[CB008577-E] For TDD:**[End CB008577-E] 5G-L2-PS need to check whether there is valid DFT beam bestH and bestV saved for the UE in the past time duration of 'ulAoaMonitorWindow', if exist valid DFT beams, only the latest DFT beam corresponding aoaH and aoaV will response to CPRT. **If there has no valid DFT beams, dominant UL SSB/refine beam should be used for AoA measurement and corresponding aoaH and aoaV will be replied to CPRT.**\*\*\*\*

**[CB008577-E] For FDD:**[End CB008577-E]  5G-L2-PS will response the recorded latest beam's aoaH and aoaV of PUSCH channel to CPRT.\*\*\*\*

Note:  aoaH and aoaV for  SSB/refine [CB008577-E ] /full sector/ default fix beam#4 / DL PMI/ [End CB008577-E] beam is received in [Before CB008577-E ] PsCell\_CellSsbRefineBeamDoaInd [CB008577-E ]  PsCell\_CellSsbRefinePmiBeamDoaInd [End CB008577-E] from CPRT. Refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_5996\_replaced([10974196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U6Xefu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.3.8.0-3 UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam Response UL SSB beam Id to CPRT in case of supercell/subsector (ID: 11156229)

**2.4.3.8.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11156234`)

UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam
Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25920\_replaced([11113880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpfKo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

### 2.4.3.9 FDD Supercell management

#### 2.4.3.9.0-1 FDD Supercell management Initial serving subcell in UL (ID: 11156245)

**2.4.3.9.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156251`)

FDD Supercell management
L2-PS shall select the initial serving subcell in UL for a UE based on the PRACH based serving subcell selection procedure as per [11170357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26JCG_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).Initial serving subcell shall act as serving subcell for the UE until the first SINR report is processed from PUCCH F3 response from the L1-UL.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-2 FDD Supercell management Serving subcell in UL (ID: 11156255)

**2.4.3.9.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11156261`)

FDD Supercell management
L2-PS shall select the serving subcell in the UL for a UE based on the SINR reports recevied in PUCCH F3 as described in [12582938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GtKjwDScEfCKaNlcHOQWmg) and [12584579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_1-sdQTVJEfCKaNlcHOQWmg)

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-3 FDD Supercell management UL message scheduling (ID: 11156266)

**2.4.3.9.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11156270`)

FDD Supercell management
L2-PS shall request UL messages for PRACH **[CB009543-B] and PUCCH F3** [End CB009543-B] from all the UL subcells(serving and non-serving subcells) of the UE. All other UL messages are requested only from UE's UL serving subcell.\*\*\*\*

Rationale : As explained in 5G\_UP\_13262\_replaced([11353566](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucAvc_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), PRACH reception and **[CB009543-B] PUCCH F3 measurements** [End CB009543-B] shall be performed on all subcells only to facilitate initial serving subcell selection **[CB009543-B] and serving subcell selection respectively** [End CB009543-B]. Other UL message receptions over PUSCH/PUCCH shall be only through UL serving subcell of the UE.***\****\*

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-4 FDD Supercell management PUCCH reception request updates (ID: 11156276)

**2.4.3.9.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11156282`)

FDD Supercell management
L2-PS shall perform PUCCH reception only on UE's initial UL serving subcell or UL serving subcell. L2-PS shall set isServingSubcell to true for all PUCCH formats for TDD supercell, FDD supercell and non-supercell cases while sending UlData\_PucchReceiveReqL1sw to L1-UL.

[CB009543-B]

Only PUCCH F3 is requested for reception from all UL subcells. Other PUCCH formats are requested reception only from UE's UL serving subcell.

If a UE is scheduled for PUCCH F3 resources on its serving subcell, the same PUCCH resources are allocated on all the non-serving subcells. This way there will be multiple UlData\_PucchReceiveReqL1sw messages(One for serving subcell and one for each non-serving subcell) carrying the same UE's information. Each UlData\_PucchReceiveReqL1sw also multiplexes multiple UEs requesting PUCCH F3 and other formats towards the same subcell. All other requirements regarding PUCCH reception as per [11150467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_D7stgPvMEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) are applicable for FDD supercell.

[CB009543-C1]

In CA cases PUCCH F3 resource allocation shall be skipped for a UE if its UE's non-serving subcell over its SCell.

[End CB009543-C1]

The parameter ' isServingSubcell ' (within UlData\_PucchReceiveReqL1sw.PucchResourceAllocationConfigL1sw) is used to indicate the L1-UL about the UE's serving subcell applicability. This IE shall indicate whether PUCCH resource processing should be Decode+Measurement on serving subcell(isServingSubcell =true) or only Measurement on non-serving subcell(isServingSubcell =false).

L2-PS shall set 'isServingSubcell' as indicated below

1. PUCCH F3 in FDD supercell for UE's UL serving subcell, 'isServingSubcell' is set to true
2. PUCCH F3 in FDD supercell for UE's UL non serving subcell, 'isServingSubcell' is set to false
3. Other PUCCH formats in FDD supercell for UE's UL serving subcell, 'isServingSubcell' is set to true
4. All PUCCH formats in TDD supercell for UE's UL serving subcell, 'isServingSubcell' is set to true
5. All PUCCH formats in non-supercell (normal cell) for UE's UL serving subcell, 'isServingSubcell' is set to true

[End CB009543-B]

[CB009543-C1]

Note: Requesting UlData\_PucchReceiveReqL1sw for non-serving subcells are valid only when P-CSI reporting for the Pcell scheduled interval. Otherwise, non serving subcell related measurements request shall be ignored either Scell P-CSI or HARQ Feedback via PUCCH F3 Reporting Interval.

[End CB009543-C1]

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-5 FDD Supercell management PUCCH reception response handling (ID: 12582938)

**2.4.3.9.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `12582944`)

FDD Supercell management
L2-PS receives UlData\_PucchReceiveRespL1sw from all the requested subcells. PUCCH Responses with F3 containing SINR values shall be used to select the best UL serving subcell. L2-PS maintains these parameters to be used for filtered SINR computation and subcell switch decision making

i. Filtered SINR list - Maintained per UE, the list of latest computed filtered SINR values for all the configured subcells(one SINR value for one subcell). This list is used across processing cycles(slots) and is initialized with value 0dB.

ii. Target subcell ID - Maintained per UE and used only within a processing cycle(slot). Holds the target subcell ID until the subcell switch is completed.The value for all the UEs is initialized to 'Invalid subcell ID' before the first UlData\_PucchReceiveRespL1sw is handled in a processing cycle(slot).

iii. Best SINR - Maintained per UE, its the temporary highest value of filtered SINR across all the configured subcells within a processing cycle(slot). The value for all the UEs is initialized to -23dB before the first UlData\_PucchReceiveRespL1sw is handled in a processing cycle(slot).

iv. ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch - Maintained per cell, the list of UEs which have a possibility to switch their serving subcell. This list gets updated and flushed every processing cycle(slot).

For each subcell's decoded control data (pucchResources mapped to a UE) within each UlData\_PucchReceiveRespL1sw containing format 3 in every slot, L2-PS shall follow these steps

1. Computes the new filtered SINR of the UE and corresponding subcell as per [11113918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vpITkfvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
2. Stores/Updates the filtered SINR list with the new filtered SINR as per [11113918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vpITkfvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
3. If the new filtered SINR is greater than the Best SINR,

a. Update the Best SINR with the new filtered SINR

b. Update UE's Target subcell ID with the subcell ID corresponding to the new filtered SINR

c. Add the UE to "ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch" list if it was not added earlier. Else skip adding the UE again.

After handling all the received UlData\_PucchReceiveRespL1sw, L2-PS shall use the above parameters for subcell switching decision during the post processing as described in [12584579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_1-sdQTVJEfCKaNlcHOQWmg)

Note:

1. New filtered SINR is the value 'SINR(u,s)' depicted on LHS of the equation in [11113918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vpITkfvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

1. 'measuredSINR(u,s)' depicted in [11113918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vpITkfvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) is the SINR received in the first element of SINR[] in message UlData\_PucchReceiveRespL1sw
2. L2-PS shall follow the above mentioned steps only for PUCCH F3 in FDD supercell. All other PUCCH formats to follow non-supercell handling.
3. L2-PS does not wait for a response from all the configured UL subcells. Only the received UlData\_PucchReceiveRespL1sw messages within a processing cycle(slot) are handled for serving subcell switching decisions.
4. Above steps are applicable for UlData\_PucchReceiveRespL1sw received from UE's serving and non-serving subcells
5. SINR has a valid range between -23dB to 40dB. If out of range SINR is reported, L2-PS shall truncate the SINR value the nearest valid value before using it to compute filtered SINR. Example : (i) SINR reported as -25dB, L2-PS considers it as -23dB for filtered SINR computation. (ii) SINR reported as 45dB, L2-PS considers it as 40dB for computing filtered SINR
6. All samples of SINR are considered within the UlData\_PucchReceiveRespL1sw message irrespective of their CRC and DTX status. SINRs from samples with DTX or CRC\_NOK are also considered valid for filtered SINR computation.
7. L2-PS shall limit the lowest value for an entry in Filtered SINR list as -23dB.
8. L2-PS shall limit the highest value for an entry in Filtered SINR list as 40dB.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-6 FDD Supercell management UL Serving subcell switch selection (ID: 12584579)

**2.4.3.9.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12584580`)

FDD Supercell management
UE's UL serving subcell needs to be reviewed for a possible switch during the post-processing. L2-PS shall use the SINR values (processed as per[12582938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GtKjwDScEfCKaNlcHOQWmg)) in the below mentioned steps to check serving subcell switch for each UE in ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch.

For every UE in the ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch L2-PS shall check

1. If (TargetSubcellSINR is greater than ThresholdSINR) and (Target subcell ID is not equal to current subcell ID), Consider the UE eligible for UL serving subcell switch and indicate UL scheduler about the target subcell ID. Add an entry in PsInternal\_DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate.ueSubcellSwitchSuperCellFdd with UE's rnti and Target subcell ID (Set ueSubcellSwitchSuperCellFdd.rnti = UE's rnti, ueSubcellSwitchSuperCellFdd.targetUlServingSubcellId = Target subcell ID).
2. Update L2 tracing event with UE specific data (rnti of trace data mapped to UE's rnti)

a. ulServingSubcellSwitchDataSuperCellFdd.targetUlServingSubcellId = Target subcell ID if condition at step 1 was true. Else use UE's current UL serving subcell ID

b. ulServingSubcellSwitchDataSuperCellFdd.subcellId[0..n] = subcellId[0..n] configured for FDD supercell

c. ulServingSubcellSwitchDataSuperCellFdd.filteredSINR[0..n] = UE's filtered SINR[0..n]

1. Trigger the L2 tracing event ulServingSubcellSwitchDataSuperCellFdd.
2. Update Target subcell ID with 'Invalid subcell ID'
3. Update Best SINR with value -23dB
4. Remove the UE from ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch list

Note:

1. TargetSubcellSINR = SINR in the UE's Filtered SINR list (processed as per[12582938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GtKjwDScEfCKaNlcHOQWmg)) corresponding to the Target subcell ID
2. ThresholdSINR = SINR in the UE's Filtered SINR list (processed as per[12582938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GtKjwDScEfCKaNlcHOQWmg)) corresponding to the current serving subcell ID + *rdSubcellSwitchSINRThreshold is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28478\_replaced([11114169](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpiN-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*
3. Condition mentioned in step 1 effectively checks for the subcell switch criteria mentioned in [11113928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vt--YPvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Also implies the UE does not need a subcell switch if condition check at step 1 fails
4. ' n ' refers to configured number of subcells in FDD supercell. Refer [12654808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_HiAKoEcfEfC0Ye3M8ARUWQ) for details about the trace event IEs. ~~The remaining elements in the trace data are filled with default values as indicated in [12654808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_HiAKoEcfEfC0Ye3M8ARUWQ)~~
5. L2 tracing event ulServingSubcellSwitchDataSuperCellFdd shall be updated and triggered for every UE which is part of ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-7 FDD Supercell management UL Serving subcell switch execution (ID: 12636880)

**2.4.3.9.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `12636882`)

FDD Supercell management
Upon receiving the intra scheduler message PsInternal\_DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate, L2-PS shall update the UE's UL serving subcell ID with the ueSubcellSwitchSuperCellFdd.targetUlServingSubcellId. All the UL messages to be handled by serving subcell are now handled by the new UL serving subcell. L2-PS shall reset CFO and TA values after switching to new UL serving subcell.

1. Carrier frequency offset(CFO) metric rrmAvgCfoPrevious is reset to 0 as per [11150991](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GXnWUPvMEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)
2. Timing advance (TA) metric is reset to 0 as per [11150033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_B4anIPvMEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Note:

1. L2-PS uses the new UL serving subcell for issuing UL grants for the new transmissions and ongoing retransmissions over PUSCH. It updates PuschHarqConfig.freshHarqTrans according to the transmission type (True = New/Initial transmission, False = Retransmission), PuschHarqConfig.absoluteHarqProcessIndex value is not modified and PuschDecoderConfig.rvIndex according to legacy algorithms (No special handling just for FDD Supercell)
2. Due to subcell switch, the ongoing retransmissions(Grants on PUSCH) are now switched to new subcell. This can cause the packet drop as the new subcell may have partial data regarding the retransmissions and can lead to decoding failures.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-8 FDD Supercell management Pattern ID handling for DL and UL messages (ID: 12519755)

**2.4.3.9.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `12519976`)

FDD Supercell management
In case of FDD supercell is enabled, 'Pattern Id' is set to 0 for all relevant UL and DL messages towards L1.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-9 FDD Supercell management PMaxSubcell Handling for EIRP measurements and control (ID: 12700607)

**2.4.3.9.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `12700608`)

FDD Supercell management
PMaxSubcell is the maximum power transmitted for each subcell. This value is received in Cell setup as an integer value. However L2-Ps converts it to dBm by dividing by 10. L2-PS stores values of these in a local array PMaxSubcell[] and uses them only when EIRP is enabled either through cell setup or cell reconfiguration.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-10 FDD Supercell management Weakest segment for EIRP (ID: 12700618)

**2.4.3.9.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `12700620`)

FDD Supercell management
Each subcell represents a segment and PMaxSubcells[] are mapped to their respective segments in EIRP calculations. Segment with the least value of "Effective\_PMaxSubcell" shall be marked as the weakest segment.

Effective\_PMaxSubcell[i] in dBm = PMaxSubcell[i] in dBm [Before PR885352] - [PR885352] + [End PR885352] PowerReductionFactor[i] in dB

Where i is the segment index

In case if multiple segments have the same Effective\_PMaxSubcell, the one with lower subcell index is chosen as the weakest segment.

[PR885352]

Note :

1. Since the range of PowerReductionFactor is from -25dB to 0dB, the received values at L2-PS are in -ve scale. Hence the above formula adds the PowerReductionFactor to PMaxSubcell
2. Implementation of the above formula can be done in linear scale (Addition in dB gets implemented as multiplication in linear scale)

[End PR885352]

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-11 FDD Supercell management EIRP normalization with respect to primary DL segment (ID: 12710411)

**2.4.3.9.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `12710416`)

FDD Supercell management
EIRP values when computed for each segment which is not mapped to the 'primary DL subcell', there is a need for normalization proportional to its effective difference of PMaxSubcell of the primary DL subcell. More information can be found in [11118904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_ZTvJcPvJEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

The normalization is applied for each non primary DL segment(mapping to non primary DL subcell) ' i ' as per the formula mentioned below

Normalized EIRP of non primary DL segment(i) in dBm = Normalized EIRP of Primary DL segment(P) in dBm + Effective normalization factor segment(i) in dB

Effective normalization factor of (i) in dB = (PMaxSubcell[i]) in dBm - (PMaxSubcell[P]) in dBm

Where P = Primary DL segment mapping to primary DL subcell

Note :

1. Computation is applied in dB scale. It can be implemented in linear scale if required.
2. Effective normalization factor depicts the difference in power values. It can be +ve or -ve value in dB depending on the power values.
3. Since L2-PS allocates resources only for Primary DL subcell, the other subcells need to get the EIRP values from Primary DL subcell as a reference point.
4. Normalization is not applied for EIRP of primary DL segment.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-12 FDD Supercell management EIRP budget from the weakest segment (ID: 12701397)

**2.4.3.9.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `12701359`)

FDD Supercell management
The EIRP budget per beam,segment is represented by rrmNormalizedEirpBudget (b,s) and computed based on the equation

rrmNormalizedEirpBudget (b,s) = powerReductionFactor\_lin(s) / normalizedBeamGain(b,s)

as per [11118921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_ZdTVIPvJEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

For FDD supercell, EIRP budget is considered from the weakest segment such that "powerReductionFactor\_lin(s)" is taken from s=weakest segment and "normalizedBeamGain(b,s)" is 1. This will make the EIRP control function based on the most challenging value of PMaxSubcell which is configured for the weakest segment(subcell)

Note :

1. In case of cell reconfiguration, the new values for powerReductionFactor or normalizedBeamGain are used as per [11118723](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_XzKHgPvJEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

---

### 2.4.3.10 FDD Beamforming L3 Call

#### 2.4.3.10.0-1 FDD Beamforming L3 Call Beam ID for DL (ID: 12697999)

**2.4.3.10.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12697996`)

FDD Beamforming L3 Call
If actBeamformingFdd (see [10967202: PsCell\_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), the beam applied for DL channels/signals is as bellow according to [11113948: FDD mMIMO beamforming PatternID](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpfxufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9):

SSB: ssbBeamPatternId[0] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), 1 beam for only 1 SSB as per [11094949: Mapping of antenna ports to antenna elements for FDD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmhWN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

CSI-RS for tracking: keep the same as SSB ssbBeamPatternId[0] and populated as per above rationality and [11159736: CSI-RS for Tracking in SSBURST slot](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktLX3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [11193236: patternId and ceAxCIndex choices](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvqrKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

PDCCH: DL full beam from downlinkBeamPatternId[0], no matter the PDCCH is for common channel or UE specific data transmission as per [11193236: patternId and ceAxCIndex choices](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvqrKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

PDSCH: 2 panel split beam for DL in downlinkBeamPatternId[1], downlinkBeamPatternId[2], no matter the PDSCH is for common channel or UE specific data transmission as per above rationality and [11139560: Precoding options](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krn4q_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

CSI-RS for channel acquisition:  2 panel split beam for DL in downlinkBeamPatternId[1], downlinkBeamPatternId[2] and populated as per as per above rationality and [11193236: patternId and ceAxCIndex choices](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvqrKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

#### 2.4.3.10.0-2 FDD Beamforming L3 Call Beam ID for UL (ID: 12704269)

**2.4.3.10.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12704270`)

FDD Beamforming L3 Call
If actBeamformingFdd (see [10967202: PsCell\_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), the beam applied for UL channels/signals is as bellow according to [11113948: FDD mMIMO beamforming PatternID](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpfxufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9):

PRACH: uplinkBeamPatternId[0] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and populated for 4RX as per above rationality and [11094230: FDD FR1 - subcell type and number of antennas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaogfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

PUSCH: uplinkBeamPatternId[1~4] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for 8RX as per above rationality and [11112346: Apply Updated Beam ID to 8RX Physical Channels](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

PUCCH:  uplinkBeamPatternId[1~4] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for 8RX as per above rationality and [11112346: Apply Updated Beam ID to 8RX Physical Channels](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

A-SRS:  uplinkBeamPatternId[1~4] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for 8RX as per above rationality and [11112346: Apply Updated Beam ID to 8RX Physical Channels](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

### 2.4.3.11 FDD Beamforming

#### 2.4.3.11.0-1 FDD Beamforming Beam usage for DL (ID: 12823080)

**2.4.3.11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12823082`)

FDD Beamforming
If FDD cell with actBeamforming = TRUE, the beam for all DL channel/signal shall be as bellow:

SSB: fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

CSI-RS for tracking: fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

PDCCH:fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)[CB008577-B] if dlMimoMode != CL\_32x4MIMO, otherwise choose best PMI beam or fullSectorBeamPatternId[0], or best beam from PRACH probing for DL PDCCH [CB008577-C] and UL PDCCH[End CB008577-C] as per [12777257: FDD: Maximum Two beams from CSI feedback](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ldAtcI1KEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)[End CB008577-B];

PDSCH: as bellow table

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| dlMimoMode | beam for common PDSCH | beam for PDSCH before CSI is not available | beam for PDSCH with CSI available |  | beam for PDSCH when CSI aging |  |
| [Before CB008577-B] not CL\_32x4MIMO[End CB008577-B] | fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] and coppied for 4 stream | If actPrachProbing = FALSE, 2 identical beam from fullSectorBeamPatternId[0]; If actPrachProbing = TRUE, 2 idnetical DL beam mapping from PRACH probed best beam | beamset4PatternId[0~1] are selected from beamset#4 according to [11152417: SS/PBCH Index identification and association to an internal Beam ID in the gNB](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) |  | beamset4PatternId[0~1] are selected from beamset#4 according to [11152417: SS/PBCH Index identification and association to an internal Beam ID in the gNB](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) |  |
| [CB008577-B]CL\_32x4MIMO [End CB008677-B] | one beam fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] for 2 stream | If actPrachProbing = FALSE, fullSectorBeamPatternId[0]; If actPrachProbing = TRUE, one DL beam mapping from PRACH probing best beam | initial TX：PMI beam from CSI report according to [12777257: FDD: Maximum Two beams from CSI feedback](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ldAtcI1KEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) | reTx: if initial TX is on PMI beam,  latest PMI beam from CSI report if latest reported rank = last reported rank; otherwise the same beam as initial TX if the initial beam is valid or fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] if the initial beam is invalid. | initial TX: one beam fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] for 2 stream | reTX: same beam as initial TX |

The mapping between DL beam and PRACH probing best beam is as the mapping between UL beam and Dl beam in beamset4PatternId as in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

CSI-RS for channel acquisition: beamset4PatternId[0~1] as per [11112298: Apply Updated Beam ID to Physical Channels](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

---

#### 2.4.3.11.0-2 FDD Beamforming Beam usage for UL (ID: 12823081)

**2.4.3.11.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12823083`)

FDD Beamforming
If FDD cell with actBeamforming = TRUE, the beam for all UL channel/signal shall be as bellow::

PRACH: if actPrachProbing =  FALSE fullSectorBeamPatternId[1], otherwise beamset4PatternId[4~7] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

PUSCH: if actPrachProbing =  TRUE, UL counterpart refine beam for best beam from PRACH detection and if actPrachProbing = FALSE, beamset4PatternId[4~7] [CB008577-C] without valid PMI beam from CSI, and after PMI beam from CSI , shall use split beam of UL PMI beam[End CB008577-C] as per [12771852: FDD: four beams for 8RX receiver](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__UhkgYlZEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

PUCCH: beamset4PatternId[4~7] as [12770624: FDD: Beams applicable to long PUCCH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_JBaoA4iTEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

A-SRS: the same as PUSCH as per [11112346: Apply Updated Beam ID to 8RX Physical Channels](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

### 2.4.3.12 Test Support

#### 2.4.3.12.0-1 Test Support patternId Offset for Test Usage (ID: 11156292)

**2.4.3.12.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156299`)

Test Support
After 5G-L2-PS has determined the patternId according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1939\_replaced([11111782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpQhIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), it shall add the value of parameter rdPatternIdOffset (5G\_L2\_1103\_replaced([11183652](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27lMiPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to the patternId before using it in message DlData\_PatternConfigReq (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_53). It is left for tester responsibility to set the parameter to a reasonable value.

---

## 2.4.4 DRX Control

### 2.4.4.0-1 DRX Control FA: [Internal Domain] DRX and Measurement Gap Control Document Owner: Yao Wang (NSB); Yuanying Xie (NSB) (ID: 11156309)

### 2.4.4.1 DRX Control for non-CA UEs

#### 2.4.4.1.0-1 DRX Control for non-CA UEs 2.4.4.1.1 drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL (ID: 11156320)

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156326`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
2.4.4.1.1.1 general of drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL

---

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156330`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL is one per DL/UL HARQ process, maintained in L2PS DL/UL separately, the purpose is to keep a window within which the UE listens to the PDCCH for the corresponding HARQ. the detail value refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_426\_replaced([10975509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBFLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. actDrxRetransTimerSupport

This is the NIDD parameter for the CB009531 feature flag, for the different behavior with true or false of the actDrxRetransTimerSupport, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15848\_replaced([11114393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2270\_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2271\_replaced([11114484](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. start timer in L2PS DL/UL

The trigger of the start of drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL in L2PS DL/UL, which is after the end symbol of drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15848\_replaced([11114393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),  5G\_UP\_ALG\_2270\_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Define two types of drx-RetransmissionTimerUL

1) type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL: started by PUSCH CRC OK(ACK) with NIDD parameter actDrxRetransTimerSupport = true.

2) type2 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL: started by PUSCH CRC KO(NACK).

1. stop timer in L2PS DL/UL

The drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL started by NACK should be stopped when the retransmission of corresponding HARQ is scheduled in L2PS DL/UL with high priority, so these kinds of timer exist quite short duration, which make it no necessary to exchange between  L2PS DL/UL.

1. type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL

When NIDD parameter actDrxRetransTimerSupport = true, the drx-RetransmissionTimerUL can be started by PUSCH CRC OK, which is different from DL that only started at the NACK of DL HARQ feedback.

L2PS chooses the harqId for new transmission from 0 - 15 sequencely per UE, the minimum interval of  same harqId(do not consider retransmission) of the UE is about 80slots for TDD 1116 pattern and 16slots for FDD pattern. Based on that fact, L2PS starts/restarts type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL per UE, not per harq, which means there is no stop type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL proactively, except for the expiration of type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL.

---

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11156336`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
2.4.4.1.1.2 UE DRX status impacted by drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL

---

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11156343`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
The drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL is introduced by 5GC000772 and 5GC002060, which say that "while UE DRX onDuration timer and inactivity timer are both off, the HARQ process can retransmit only when its DRX retransmission timer is running. ", so the UE DRX active state does not contain the drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL. We continue to keep this idea, and give some more chance to be scheduled in L2PS DL/UL when the drx-RetransmissionTimerUL is started by PUSCH CRC OK(type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL) with NIDD parameter actDrxRetransTimerSupport = true, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2270\_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11156349`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
2.4.4.1.1.3   drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL length with slot1/slot2

---

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11156357`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
There is timing delay for DL AN/UL CRC result received in L2PS. with drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL length of slot1/slot2, it can happen that the drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL has already expired before the DL AN/UL CRC result received in L2PS. If no other DRX timer( (drx-onDurationTimer and drx-InactivityTimer)) is running, then there is no chance for retransmission until next drx-onDurationTimer, which is as expected and accepted. Below picture shows an example, with drx-RetransmissionTimerDL length = 8slot.

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11156363`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
2.4.4.1.1.4   enable drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL

---

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11156367`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
L2PS starts UE DRX activation procedure when receiving PsUser\_UserModifyReq with drxConfig structure is present and IE 'drxConfig' of drxConfig structure is set to 1, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2263\_replaced([11114367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Actually when UE receives RRC reconfiguration msg for drx configuration, UE may change to DRX deactive immediately, which leads to DRX status mismatch between gNB and UE. It may be worse with the introduction of drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL, so it’s decided to start drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL after receiving PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq in L2PS, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2270\_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DrxConfigurationMode with value of activated is involved in the PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq msg, refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_2575\_replaced([10977732](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VOghfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.4.1.0-2 DRX Control for non-CA UEs 2.4.4.1.2 drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL (ID: 11156373)

**2.4.4.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11156378`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
2.4.4.1.2.1 general of drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL

---

**2.4.4.1.0-2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156385`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL is one per DL/UL HARQ process, the purpose is to determine the start symbol of the drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2271\_replaced([11114484](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). As currently the slot based scheduling is supported in L2PS, for the simplicity, the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL is multiple of 14 symbols, which values are introduced by new NIDD parameter drxProfile1/drxHarqRttTimerDl, drxProfile2/drxHarqRttTimerDl, drxProfileNrAnr/drxHarqRttTimerDl, drxProfile1/drxHarqRttTimerUl, drxProfile2/drxHarqRttTimerUl, drxProfileNrAnr/drxHarqRttTimerUl, and sent to L2PS by PsUser\_UserSetupReq/PsUser\_UserModifyReq, refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_426\_replaced([10975509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBFLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. start timer

The start of drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL would be in the beginning of the slot boundary. Actually there is timing delay for DL L2PS receiving the DL AN on PUCCH/PUSCH, and also timing delay for UL L2PS receiving PUSCH CRC result from L1.  The timing delay is caused by scheduling advance in L2PS and receiving delay for L1 processing time. For scheduling advance, refer to 5G\_L2\_455\_replaced([11152781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24rCcvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Considering the timing delay in FDD, TDD FR1, TDD FR2, it's decided that the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL is restricted with max value of 42 symbols, which is no larger than the timing delay, so there is no need to start the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL. These are the following benefits with this handling:

1) the retransmission could be scheduled without delay caused by the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL.

2) the implementation would be more simple in L2PS without the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL.

Below picture shows an example of the timing delay for TDD 1116 pattern,  there is 4 slot delay that the DL AN on PUCCH for slot14 received in slot18 in DL L2PS. So with this delay, the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL has already expired when DL AN on PUCCH received in DL L2PS.

1. stop timer

No need to stop drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL as the timer is not started.

link: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/ECE_RD_1_CN/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B7FB0879A-2F1E-4036-89A2-50D976D6F67A%7D&file=cb009531-retx%20timer.xlsx&action=default&mobileredirect=true>

#### 2.4.4.1.0-3 DRX Control for non-CA UEs 2.4.4.1.3 drx-InactivityTimer (ID: 11156391)

**2.4.4.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11156394`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
2.4.4.1.3.1 general of drx-InactivityTimer

---

**2.4.4.1.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156400`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs
drx-InactivityTimer is one per UE, the purpose is to keep a window within which UE listens to the PDCCH. L2PS should start/restart drx-InactivityTimer when new transmission DL or UL grant is scheduled, the value is in unit of ms, refer to  5G\_L2\_IF\_426\_replaced([10975509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBFLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The drx-InactivityTimer is maintained in DL/UL L2PS separately, and sync the drx-InactivityTimer status to each other when the status is changed, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2267\_replaced([11114433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

As UE start drx-InactivityTimer after the PDCCH symbol, then the timer would be stopped at the same symbol of the last drx-InactivityTimer subframe at UE side, but L2PS stops the drx-InactivityTimer at the latter part of the last subframe, which means L2PS stop the drx-InactivityTimer later than the UE side. So if in the last subframe of drx-InactivityTimer window, L2PS gives grant in the PDCCH symbol index that larger than the PDCCH symbol index where UE starts the drx-InactivityTimer, then UE will miss this grant, DTX happen.

For the drx-InactivityTimer back off mechanism, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2268\_replaced([11114436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.4.2 DRX Control for CA UEs

#### 2.4.4.2.0-1 DRX Control for CA UEs 2.4.4.2.1 drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL (ID: 11156409)

**2.4.4.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156414`)

DRX Control for CA UEs
2.4.4.2.1.1 general of drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL

---

**2.4.4.2.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156419`)

DRX Control for CA UEs
Each Scell maintains its own drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL separately, no need to sync to other Scells and Pcell.

1. actDrxRetransTimerSupport

The actDrxRetransTimerSupport shall be sent to Scell with PsUser\_UserSetupReq message. For the different behavior with true or false of the actDrxRetransTimerSupport in Scell, which is the same as Pcell, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15848\_replaced([11114393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2270\_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2271\_replaced([11114484](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. start and stop timer in each Scell

For the start and stop of drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL in each scell(including DL and UL L2PS), which is the same as Pcell, refer to 5G\_L2\_14575\_replaced([11156326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24-kevvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**2.4.4.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11156425`)

DRX Control for CA UEs
2.4.4.2.1.2   enable drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL

---

**2.4.4.2.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11156430`)

DRX Control for CA UEs
Same as 5G\_L2\_14579\_replaced([11156363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_LgPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),  Scell drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL shall be started after receiving PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq in L2PS with the drxConfigurationMode = activated for DRX setup, inlcuding inter-gNB CA Scell, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2270\_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: For DRX setup is done before Scell addition, DRX config will be sent to Scell with PsUser\_UserSetupReq in Scell addition procedure, while no DRX config sent to Pcell with PsUser\_UserModifyReq. For this case, drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL already started in Pcell, then Scell drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL could be started after PsUser\_UserSetupReq, no PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq with the drxConfigurationMode = activated would be received in Scell L2PS.

---

### 2.4.4.3 DRX Control (InactivityTimer) for Multi L2-RT CA UEs

#### 2.4.4.3.1 DRX Overview for FR1 CA

##### 2.4.4.3.1.0-1 DRX Overview for FR1 CA Common DRX State management (ID: 11156444)

**2.4.4.3.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12798802`)

DRX Overview for FR1 CA
For FR1 CA,

Each Scell also has UE DRX configuration and maintain DRX lifecycle as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2262\_replaced([11114321](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDJPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

Each SCell also shall maintain the inactivity timer status by exchanging its downlink and uplink(if Scell is DL+UL joint Scell)  inactivity timer status, as 5G\_UP\_4751\_replaced([11356011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucVfl_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2274\_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

To manage DRX status per serving cell, below DRX states is proposed:

Common Status for both Pcell and Scell**:**

OnDuration timer status: it is UE level configuration, each CC has same onDuration time pattern as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2262\_replaced([11114321](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDJPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Local DRX InactivityTimer status: Each CC has both local DL and UL(if Scell is configed with DL+UL).

Pcell Local DRX inactivityTimer status management is defined in 5G\_L2\_8371\_replaced([11156458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ylPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

Scell is defined by 5G\_L2\_8380\_replaced([11156603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZq_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

Local DRX InactivityTimer should be synced between DL and UL as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2267\_replaced([11114433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

Besides Local DRX InactivityTimer Status, each CC also need known remote cell's DRX inactivityTimer status in order to sync with UE final DRX status, the reason is the UE inactivityTimer will be extended as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2267\_replaced([11114433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) once latest grant is received from any serving cell.

Remote DRX InactivityTimer states in Pcell:

*RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i]:*

remote Scell i local DRX InactivityTimer status;  By default is OFF when SCELL activated, updated based on Reporting from Scell once state change as 5G\_L2\_8381\_replaced([11156653](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25BAsvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

*InactivityTimerPsPeer (in Pcell):  combined all Scell's* RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[] as per 5G\_L2\_8421\_replaced([11156499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ymfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*

Remote DRX InactivityTimer states in Scell:

*InactivityTimerPsPeer (in Scell):*

By default is OFF. Update based on received broadcast message from Pcell for UE level overall DRX inactivityTimer state change in 5G\_L2\_8418\_replaced([11156623](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZrvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Based on Local Drx inactivityTimer status and remote DRX inactivityTimer status, UE level final DRX inactivityTimer status is determined in Pcell and synced to Scells:**

UE level Final DrxInactivityTimerState in Pcell:

Pcell DL as DRX master and make decision of UE level overall DRX inactivityTimer status according to 5G\_L2\_8422\_replaced([11156522](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ynvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Pcell **broadcast it to all serving Scells DL and UL at same time as 5G\_L2\_8372\_replaced([11156550](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZpPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), once CHANGED(ON to OFF or OFF to ON)**

UE level Final DrxInactivityTimerState in Scell:

Scell decide status based on 5G\_L2\_8419\_replaced([11156638](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZsfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , combined Scell local DRX inactivityTimer status and *InactivityTimerPsPeer (in Scell).*

Note that if *InactivityTimerPsPeer is changed from* *ON to OFF, local DRX inactivityTimer state will force to OFF. As 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2277\_replaced([11114541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), all scell stop scheduling once Pcell broadcast Final* DrxInactivityTimerState is OFF, to avoid mismatch/out of sync between Pcell and Scells due to DRX sync message delay.\*\*\*\*

For SR triggered DRX inactivityTimer in Pcell, Pcell will broadcast Final *DrxInactivityTimerState to scell once SR is verified as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2281\_replaced([11114555](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpl4UfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

Notes:

This requirement is the overall description based on latest DRX sync framework. For new introduced functions, refer to CFAM content of related feature. **[Before CNI-137616-B]This DRX sync framework is controlled by R&D parameter rdEnable6137Fr1CaDrxSolution defined as 5G\_L2\_10442\_replaced([11184728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27tIWfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**[End CNI-137616-B]\*\*\*\*

---

#### 2.4.4.3.2 P(S)Cell DRX handling

##### 2.4.4.3.2.0-1 P(S)Cell DRX handling Pcell local DRX Inactivity Timer Status Management (ID: 11156458)

**2.4.4.3.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11156494`)

P(S)Cell DRX handling
For FR1 CA, each serving cell shall maintain its local DRX inactivityTimer. Each serving cell only use its own grant and HARQ feedback information to update the local DRX inactivityTimer state. Details refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15848\_replaced([11114393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2267\_replaced([11114433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2274\_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

PCell shall maintain a pair of timestamp in XSFN format [lastOffXsfn, lastOnXsfn] to record latest local DRX inactivityTimer state change time XSFNchange as follows:

if state change from ON to OFF:

update lastOffXsfn value to the state change time XSFNchange , update lastOnXsfn value to INVALID value.

else if state change from OFF to ON:

update lastOnXsfn value to the state change time XSFNchange .

else:

no action.

The INVALID value is the largest value that the underlaying type of XSFN can represent.

The value of XSFNchange should aligned to TDD FR1 30kHz subcarrier spacing SCStddFr1 according to PCell's local time XSFNpcell and subcarrier spacing SCSpcell as:

XSFNchange = XSFNpcell  \* SCStddFr1 / SCSpcell

If DRX inactivityTimer reset indication triggered by PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) refer to 5G\_L2\_8422\_replaced([11156522](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ynvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), PCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) may sync new DRX state to DL by message PsInternal\_UlToDlIntraSchedUpdate due to UL inactivityTimer state change caused by new UL grant. In order to avoid furher mismatch between PCell DL and UL and also avoid further mismatch between PCell and SCells, PCell DL shall ignore this DRX sync message crossed with DRX inactivityTimer reset indication.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline or schedule, likely related to telecommunications, with two main rows labeled "PCELL DL" (Primary Cell Downlink) and "PCELL UL" (Primary Cell Uplink). Each row is divided into time slots labeled "M", "M+1", "M+2", "M+3", "M+4", "M+5", and "M+6". The time slots are colored in alternating yellow and green.
Within the "PCELL DL" row, there are annotations and arrows indicating specific events or conditions:
\* **M+1 to M+2:** A blue arrow points from "M+1" to "M+2" with the text "DIToUlIntraSchedUpdate inactivityTimer roll back".
\* **M+2 to M+3:** A red arrow points from "M+2" to "M+3" with the text "UlToDlintraSchedUpdate (DrxState=On)".
\* **Overlapping M+2 and M+3:** A red text box states, "DL shall ignore this intra update message due to it's crossed with InactivityTimer roll back Indication". This text is positioned between the "M+2" and "M+3" time slots, suggesting a conflict or condition occurring during this period.
\* **M+5 to M+6:** A green arrow points from "M+5" to "M+6" with the text "UlToDlintraSchedUpdate (DrxState=On)".
\* **Overlapping M+5 and M+6:** A green text box states, "DL shall handle this intra update message due to it's not crossed with InactivityTimer roll back Indication". This text is positioned between the "M+5" and "M+6" time slots, indicating a different outcome compared to the M+2/M+3 scenario.
The "PCELL UL" row also shows the same time slot divisions and colors but lacks the detailed annotations seen in the "PCELL DL" row.
The overall impression is a visual representation of how different messages or updates are handled based on their timing and potential conflicts with timers or states like "inactivityTimer roll back" and "DrxState=On".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2380.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[PR767017] The keyword XSFN used above is just an expression way of timestamp. DEV team could select proper data type to implement it. For example, use XHFN instead as XSFN type can only be compared within 5.12s time range.  [End PR767017]

---

##### 2.4.4.3.2.0-2 P(S)Cell DRX handling Pcell remote DRX Inactivity Timer Status Management (ID: 11156499)

**2.4.4.3.2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11156515`)

P(S)Cell DRX handling
PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall maintain remote DRX inactivityTimer state RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] for each remote SCells.

i: remote cell config index in PCell 5G-L2-PS.

RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i]  is updated by following event:

- PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL)  received message PsPeerCtrl\_ScellDrxInactivitityTimerStatusInd refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_2774\_replaced([10980770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VlF2Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from corresponding remote SCell.

If SCell release refer to 5G\_L2\_2872\_replaced([11160006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25UiufvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) triggered for remote SCell i , UE context on remote SCell may be removed during DRX inactivity timer state ON, so no more PsPeerCtrl\_ScellDrxInactivitityTimerStatusInd could be received for such SCell to set RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i]  to OFF. In order to handle this case, PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall set RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i]  to OFF after SCell release process finished.

PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall maintain one remote DRX inactivityTimer overall state InactivityTimerPsPeer for all remote SCells. When any value of RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] is ON, InactivityTimerPsPeer  is ON. Otherwise, InactivityTimerPsPeer  is OFF. InactivityTimerPsPeer  is updated by following event:

- RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] value is changed.

PCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) shall maintain one remote DRX inactivityTimer overall state InactivityTimerPsPeer  for all remote SCells. InactivityTimerPsPeer  in PCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) is aligned with same sate in PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) by DL to UL intra update message PsInternal\_DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate once InactivityTimerPsPeer  value in PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) is changed.

PCell shall maintain a pair of timestamp in xSFN format [lastOffXsfn, lastOnXsfn] for each remote serving cells to record remote serving cells latest DRX inactivityTimer state change timing indicated by message PsPeerCtrl\_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStautsInd refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_2774\_replaced([10980770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VlF2Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as follows:

For each state report received in PsPeerCtrl\_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStautsInd:

if state change from ON to OFF:

update lastOffXsfn value to the state change time XSFNchange, update lastOnXsfn value to INVALID value.

else if state change from OFF to ON:

update lastOnXsfn value to the state change time XSFNchange.

else:

no action.

The INVALID value is the largest value that the underlaying type of XSFN can represent.

The value of XSFNchange shall align to TDD FR1 30kHz subcarrier SCStddFr1 according to SCell's local time XSFNscell  got from message PsPeerCtrl\_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStautsInd and subcarrier spacing SCSscell  as:

XSFNchange = XSFNscell  \* SCStddFr1 / SCSscell

[PR767017] The keyword XSFN used above is just an expression way of timestamp. DEV team could select proper data type to implement it. For example, use XHFN instead as XSFN type can only be compared within 5.12s time range.  [End PR767017]

---

##### 2.4.4.3.2.0-3 P(S)Cell DRX handling Pcell Decide UE Level Final DRX Inactivity Timer Status (ID: 11156522)

**2.4.4.3.2.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11156543`)

P(S)Cell DRX handling
Whenever PCell local DRX inactivityTimer state changed or inactivityTimerPsPeer changed, PCell shall decide final DRX inactivityTimer state DrxInactivityTimerState on PCell.

DrxInactivityTimerState is also GNB decided UE level final DRX inactivityTimer state.

DrxInactivityTimerState on PCell is ON if either PCell local DRX inactivityTimer state or InactivityTimerPsPeer  is ON.

Otherwise, DrxInactivityTimerState is OFF.

As specified in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2277\_replaced([11114541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the conditions for DrxInactivityTimerState turn to OFF should take into account DRX inactivityTimer sync delays between SCell and PCell.

PCell shall maintain DRX inactivityTimer state change history for each serving cells as specified in 5G\_L2\_8371\_replaced([11156458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ylPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_8421\_replaced([11156499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ymfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) so that PCell could decide DrxInactivityTimerState according to DRX inactivityTimer state on each serving cells on the same slot.

After PCell updated DRX inactivityTimer state change time XSFNchange  for any serving cells as specified in 5G\_L2\_8421\_replaced([11156499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ymfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), PCell shall search all [lastOffXsfn, lastOnXsfn] pairs to find if any overlapping slot exist among all serving cells. If so, DrxInactivityTimerState change to OFF.

Example refer to below diagram:

[xlsx\_desc]--- Sheet1 ---
When PCELL receive latest DRX inactivityTimer state 0 (OFF) from SCELL, PCELL's state may already changed to 1 (ON) due to newTx. NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN

NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN

File: FR1\_CA\_DRX\_sync\_delay.xslx

Folder: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?cid=d33b59e8%2Da075%2D4ed6%2D80ea%2D361c804b20a1&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F4%2E%20DRX%20Control&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

After PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) decide DrxInactivityTimerState change to OFF caused by remote SCell sync message, besides sync the state change to all SCells as specified in 5G\_L2\_8372\_replaced([11156550](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZpPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall stop local ongoing inactivityTimer if exist, reset all DRX inactivityTimer state and sync a DRX inactivityTimer reset request to PCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) by DL to UL intra update message. PCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) shall stop local ongoing inactivityTimer if exist and reset all DRX inactivityTimer state after receive request from PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL).

[PR767017]

Once PCELL decide UE level final DRX inactivityTimer to OFF, in addition to sync the state to each serving cell. PCELL shall reset local maintained DRX inactivityTimer related state for each serving cells.

- RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] state reset to 0.

- lastOnXsfn reset to invalid value and lastOffXsfn reset to current on air XSFN.

The keyword XSFN used above is just an expression way of timestamp. DEV team could select proper data type to implement it. For example, use XHFN instead as XSFN type can only be compared within 5.12s time range.

[End PR767017]

---

##### 2.4.4.3.2.0-4 P(S)Cell DRX handling Pcell Sync UE Level Final DRX Inactivity Timer Status to Scell (ID: 11156550)

**2.4.4.3.2.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11156559`)

P(S)Cell DRX handling
[Before CB006137-AC]

To synchronize status of DRX Inactivity Timer between the 5G-L2-PS instances, PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) sends the InactivityTimer status to SCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) in the PsPeerCtrl\_PCellStatusInd.

The DRX InactivityTimer status is ON if either PCell UL InactivityTimer or/and PCell DL inactivityTimer is running.

PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) sends its DRX InactivityTimer status:

- on reception of harq feedback for scell.

- or when it changes to OFF

The InactivityTimer status can change to OFF on one of following events:

- PCell (DL) receives a notification from UL scheduler that UL timer status is OFF

- DL Inactivity Timer expires

- Rollback happens when DTX as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2274\_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB006137-AC]

To synchronize status of DRX inactivityTimer between 5G-L2-PS instances, PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall broadcast DrxInactivityTimerState to SCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) and SCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) by message PsPeerCtrl\_PCellStatusInd when state changed.

The DrxInactivityTimerState can change to ON/OFF on one of following event:

- PCell (DL) receives a notification from UL scheduler that UL timer status is ON/OFF

- DL Inactivity Timer start or expire

- Rollback happens when DTX as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2274\_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

-     PCell (DL) received new remote SCell state by message PsPeerCtrl\_ScellDrxInactivitityTimerStatusInd cause InactivityTimerPsPeer  value changed as specified in 5G\_L2\_8421\_replaced([11156499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ymfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB006137-AC]

---

##### 2.4.4.3.2.0-5 P(S)Cell DRX handling Scell DRX Sync Message Validation in Pcell (ID: 11156564)

**2.4.4.3.2.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11156592`)

P(S)Cell DRX handling
[Before CB006137-AC]

When P(S)Cell InactivityTimer status changes to OFF in PCell 5G-L2-PS, PCell L2PS notifies SCell L2PS via the message PsPeerCtrl\_PCellStatusInd. However before receiving the notification from PCell, SCell 5G-L2-PS may grant the UE (please refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_479\_replaced([11197286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Bf5nO_vREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more information on slot timing).

To avoid that PCell 5G-L2-PS changes the DRX state of the UE  (and start traffic)  based on a wrong grant on SCell,  PCell 5G-L2-PS shall not start InactivityTimer on the reception of  ScellUeSchedulingInd if its InactivityTimer status is OFF (UL and DL InactivityTimer are not running) and the SCell grant(s) were sent on DRX OFF period (according to the DRX cycle).

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a timeline diagram illustrating the states of "Active" and "sleep" for a system, along with the states of "sCell" and "pCell" over time. The time axis is marked with slots denoted by "M" and "N" followed by increments (e.g., M+1, N+2).
The "Active" state is represented by a solid black line, indicating it is active for a certain duration and then becomes inactive. The "sleep" state is represented by a dotted line, showing its corresponding inactive period.
The "sCell" row shows a sequence of colored blocks representing different time slots: "M" and "M+1" are yellow, followed by "M+2" through "M+5" in green, and then "M+6" through "M+13" alternating between green and yellow.
The "pCell" row also shows colored blocks representing time slots: "N" is yellow, followed by "N+1" and "N+2" in green, then "N+3" in yellow, "N+4" in green, and "N+5" and "N+6" alternating between green and yellow.
There are two arrows indicating communication or state changes:
1. A blue arrow originates from the "pCell" row at slot "N+1" and points upwards towards the "sCell" row, with the label "PCellStatusInd (DrxState=OFF)".
2. An orange arrow originates from the "sCell" row at slot "M+5" and points downwards towards the "pCell" row, with the label "ScellUeSchedulingInd".
Additionally, there is a blue upward arrow pointing to the "pCell" row at slot "N+4".
A text box at the bottom of the image provides a detailed explanation: "Since the ScellGrant was sent during a Drx ON period (Slots M+4/M+5 => N+2 in Pcell numrelogy), L2PS Pcell can restart the Timer even if the the InactivityTimer / ULInactivityTimer has been stopped at N+1 on Pcell."
The table structure can be represented as follows:

| Row/Column | M | M+1 | M+2 | M+3 | M+4 | M+5 | M+6 | M+7 | M+8 | M+9 | M+10 | M+11 | M+12 | M+13 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Active |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| sleep |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| sCell | Yellow | Yellow | Green | Green | Green | Green | Green | Green | Green | Green | Green | Green | Green | Green |
| pCell | Yellow | Yellow | Green | Green | Yellow | Green | Yellow |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
