#### 2.4.7.2.8 SCell DL Scheduling prevention

##### 2.4.7.2.8.0-1 SCell DL Scheduling prevention Impact for Scell Scheduler (ID: 13323693)

**2.4.7.2.8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13323694`)

SCell DL Scheduling prevention
if actFddDlSchedPrevent is True and PCell is FR1 TDD and SCell is FDD and UE's caConfigDu parameter is “DlAndUl”  
    L2PS does not send LoCtrl\_StartFlowControlReq Msg to L2LO under conditions mentioned in [11121583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqZJmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), [11121880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqa-y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)  
end

For NUM\_TIME\_DL\_SCH\_PREV\_CA\_SCELL  counter update refer [13323608](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_t1MIQNHFEfCVOOJMfwsFiw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

Note: When LoCtrl\_StartFlowControlReq Message is not sent to L2LO due to above mentioned reason, then LoCtrl\_StopFlowControlReq message should also not be sent to L2LO e.g during SCell Deactivation or Suspend procedures.

---

#### 2.4.7.2.9 UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG

##### 2.4.7.2.9.0-1 UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG Overview (ID: 13414174)

**2.4.7.2.9.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13414214`)

UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG
Timing alignment procedure is performed for UEs for which SCell is in different TAG than PCell (tagId in [10974781: CellConfigParam structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_jjfKsPu4Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to obtain Timing Advance (see: [13345951: Timing Alignment via CFRA](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_L_qpQOSSEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ)). Procedure is initiated on PCell side when there is need for Uplink traffic on SCell. Only when this procedure is succesfully finished Uplink transmission can be triggered.  
Procedure consists of steps:

* Step 1: PCell L2-PS requests SCell L2-PS to perform Timing Alignment procedure.
* Step 2: SCell L2-PS requests CP-RT to assign CFRA preamble for PDCCH order
* Step 3: SCell L2-PS orders UE to perform CFRA with DCI format 1\_0
* Step 4: Upon reception of preamble SCell L2-PS requests RAR transmission with MSG3 grant from PCell L2-PS.
* Step 5: When first uplink transsmission (MSG3) from UE in SCell is received (with CRC OK or NOK) SCell L2-PS shall inform PCell L2-PS about successfull procedure completion and release preamble in CP-RT.

For reference see flowchart: [13415683: Timing Alignment procedure for SCell in different TAG](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GHt_khPTEfG3gf_qmG8Vww)

Additional details:

1. Timing Alignment procedure is requested with PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUeSchedulingReq with modifType set to requestUlTimingAlignmentForSTag (see: [10979437: PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUeSchedulingReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_D6EQcPu5Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. Upon reception of Timing Alignment request L2-PS shall start timer for Timing Alignment procedure. Timer neeeds to be longer than max time for CFRA procedure handling (considering retries) computed as per [13414220: Timing Alignment for SCell with Different TAG procedure timeout calculation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_HCt11RLvEfG3gf_qmG8Vww).
3. Time of above timer shall be propagated to CP-RT as per [13414243: Filling Reserve Preamble Request](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_ELMSQBLzEfG3gf_qmG8Vww). Rationale: CP-RT needs to implement mechanism to release pramble on it's own to ensure robustness. Because time of processing CFRA can vary significatnly based on RACH configuration.
4. At Step 3 result of receiving DCI format 1\_0 by UE is solely preamble transmission thus there is no associated PDSCH, PUSCH or PUCCH transmission.
5. Step 4 shall be repeated for each transmission of preamble.
6. SCell L2-PS shall inform PCell about failed procedure with PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUeSchedulingResp with NOK status.
7. Procedure from SCell perspective is considered failed in following cases:
8. CP-RT could not reserve preamble for CFRA procedure.
9. timer for Timing Alignment procedure expired.
10. If PCell L2-PS receives PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUeSchedulingResp with NOK status for this procedure it shall retry after backoff time (see: [13411425: rdUlTimingAlignmentForSTagBackoffTime](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_CO90FBFcEfG3gf_qmG8Vww))

---

##### 2.4.7.2.9.0-2 UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG Timing Alignment for SCell with Different TAG procedure timeout calculation (ID: 13414220)

**2.4.7.2.9.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13414221`)

UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG
This requirement describes time needed by L2-PS to finish timing alignment procedure.

First, L2-PS shall determine largest possible distance (in slots) between PRACH occasions that could be used by UE to perform Random Access procedure based on steps:

1. Based on frame structure (for TDD only, see [10968427: tddFrameStructure structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_49Ez4Pu3Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and prachConfigurationIndex , L2-PS shall determine all PRACH slots (see: [11111903: Parameters used in PRACH mapping functions](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_gVg_kPvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. Next for each configured SSB beam ID:
3. Determine list of prach occasions for SSB beam ID (and slots they belong to) within rrmPrachAssociationPeriod ([11111925: PRACH Association Period](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_gfwgsPvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
4. exclude all occasions based on ssbOccasionMaskIndex parameter. (example: if there are 4 occasions for SSB beam and ssbOccasionMaskIndex is 10 occasions with ids 1 and 3 can be used)
5. For each occasion compute following variables:
   1. durationRarWindow - defined as duration in slots from occasion slot until Random Access Response Window ends (see: [11174520: Random Access Response window identification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GO7CcPvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
   2. endRarWindowSlot - defined as slot number within rrmPrachAssociationPeriod corresponding to slot in which Rar Window expires in its period. Can be calculated as follows: endRarWindowSlot = (occasion slot + durationRarWindow) mod rrmPrachAssociationPeriod
   3. nextOccasionSlot - defined as slot number of nearest occasion from filtered list (see step 2.b) after endRarWindowSlot. If there is no more occasions in rrmPrachAssociationPeriod search shall start from beginning of period. In this case L2-PS shall set periodOverflown = 1 otherwise periodOverflown = 0
   4. preambleRetransmissionDistance - distance between occasions in slots defined as preambleRetransmissionDistance = durationRarWindow + nextOccasionSlot - endRarWindowSlot + periodOverflown \* rrmPrachAssociationPeriod
6. Distance between occasions from above steps (preambleRetransmissionDistance) shall be added to list (preambleRetransmissionDistancesList)
7. Largest value from preambleRetransmissionDistancesList shall be used as baseDistance (in slots).

Then L2-PS shall calculate actual value of timout (in ms) using formula:

pdcchOrderProcedureTimeout = baseDistance \* preambleTransMax / (2μ) + rdPdcchOrderProcedureProcessingOverhead

where:

* prachConfigurationIndex, preambleTransMax, raResponseWindow, ssbOccasionMaskIndex are paremters from RACH config ([10968124: rachConfig structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_279QsPu3Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* rdPdcchOrderProcedureProcessingOverhead is additional time L2-PS may need to ensure not abandoning procedure too early (due to internal processing overhead, see: [13411426: rdUlTimingAlignmentForSTagProcessingOverheadTime](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_LoUwJxFcEfG3gf_qmG8Vww))
* μ is numerology used in SCell configuration (see: [11346879: Duplex Mode and Numerology](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vqVLcPveEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.7.2.9.0-3 UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG Storing timing alignment timout value in UE context (ID: 13429310)

**2.4.7.2.9.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13429311`)

UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG
Because of following reasons:

1. RACH configuration can be online modified (see raConfiguration parameter in [10971056: PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_KPrkLPu4Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. RACH configuration is not updated for already connected UEs
3. UL Timing Alignment Procedure can be delayed

L2-PS shall calculate timer value (see: [13414220: Timing Alignment for SCell with Different TAG procedure timeout calculation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_HCt11RLvEfG3gf_qmG8Vww)) once at time of reception of PsUser\_UserSetupReq with ueAddCause SCellAddition (see: [10974651: PsUser\_UserSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_inbyIPu4Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and store it in context. As long as UE is connected to this SCell, stored value shall be used as pdcchOrderProcedureTimeout duration.

---

##### 2.4.7.2.9.0-4 UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG Filling Reserve Preamble Request (ID: 13414243)

**2.4.7.2.9.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `13414244`)

UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG
L2-PS shall fill following IEs of PsUser\_ReserveCfraPreambleReq:

* ssbBeamId - best beam for UE as from Beam report populated from PCell (see: [11160013: Reception of CSI report and Beam Report](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_0REyQfvMEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), [10979797: SCell Physical Uplink Control Channel Receive](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GvhaoPu5Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* reservationTime - value calulated as per [13414220: Timing Alignment for SCell with Different TAG procedure timeout calculation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_HCt11RLvEfG3gf_qmG8Vww), maximum time needed by L2-PS to finish CFRA procedure (including retries and overhead)

Other parameters shall be filled according to specifics of UE preamble is requested for.

---

##### 2.4.7.2.9.0-5 UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG Aborting Timing Alignment Procedure (ID: 13491669)

**2.4.7.2.9.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `13491670`)

UL Timing Alignment for SCells in Different TAG
In case of Beam Failure Recovery on PCell L2-PS on SCell shall abort timing alignment procedure as described in [13345951: Timing Alignment via CFRA](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_L_qpQOSSEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ). This is performed as follows:

1. PCell L2-PS detects Beam Failure Recovery by reception of MSG 3 with known RNTI as described in [11152927](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_PoJtkPvMEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).
2. PCell L2-PS shall request SCell L2-PS to abort Timing Alignment procedure by sending PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUeSchedulingReq  with modifType equal to abortUlTimingAlignmentForSTag.
3. SCell L2-PS shall release preamble and abort any other processing related with Timing Alignment Procedure (including sending PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUeSchedulingResp with modifType equal to requestUlTimingAlignmentForSTag).
4. SCell L2-PS reponds with PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUeSchedulingResp with modifType equal to abortUlTimingAlignmentForSTag and OK status.
5. Upon reception of response PCell L2-PS shall consider procedure aborted and continue in similar fashion as after failure: shall restart procedure after backoff time (see: [13411425: rdUlTimingAlignmentForSTagBackoffTime](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_CO90FBFcEfG3gf_qmG8Vww))

For reference see flowchart: !!! ADD FLOWCHART LINK HERE !!!

---

### 2.4.7.3 SCell deactivation

#### 2.4.7.3.0-1 SCell deactivation Interworking with DPA algorithm (ID: 11159276)

**2.4.7.3.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11159296`)

SCell deactivation
Algorithm for deactive Scell if DPA is enabled:

(1) When Pcell is ready to send MAC-CE to UE, Pcell start timer with Measurement Processing Time (see 5G\_L2\_8707\_replaced([11158171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25KKo_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) + [Before CB012528-D] rdSchedulePUCCHCycle\_msec [CB012528-D] rrmSchedulePucchCycle\_msec [End CB012528-D]), this is the worse case to make sure already alocated DPA(Ctai/Tdai) still be valid for other Scells;

(2) Pcell stop DPA for this Scell which will be deactived;

(3) After timer out, Pcell continute deactive Scell to UE(send MAC-CE).

Notes: this is common way for 2CC or mutlple CCs.

---

#### 2.4.7.3.1 SCell candidate selection for deactivation due to UE Overheated

##### 2.4.7.3.1.0-1 SCell candidate selection for deactivation due to UE Overheated SCell candidate selection for deactivation (ID: 11159307)

**2.4.7.3.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11159317`)

SCell candidate selection for deactivation due to UE Overheated
L2-PS shall select the SCell(s) for deactivation as many as required to meet the value of[Before CB006137-BB] MaxNumofActiveSCells [CB006137-BB] MaxNumofActiveSCellsDL and MaxNumofActiveSCellsUL [End CB006137-BB] in the following order:

1. SCell(s) in the overheating state and there is more SCells in the overheating state than required for deactivation (for fulfill the[Before CB006137-BB] MaxNumofActiveSCells [CB006137-BB] MaxNumofActiveSCellsDL and MaxNumofActiveSCellsUL [End CB006137-BB] requirement), they all shall be deactivated;

[CB006137-BB]

1. If MaxNumofActiveSCellsUL < NumofActSCellsUL, the SCell with uplink activated shall be deactivated.

[End CB006137-BB]

1. SCell of “bad” Radio Link Quality[CB006137-BB] in downlink [End CB006137-BB].
2. [CB006137-BB] DL only [End CB006137-BB]SCells in the order from lowest to highest Available DL Resources, [CB006137-BB] the SCell with uplink activated is listed in the last. [End CB006137-BB].
3. SCells in the order from highest sCellIndex to lowest sCellIndex.

Note: This selection algorithm is used only for SCell deactivation when there is room for active SCell(s).

---

#### 2.4.7.3.2 ext-SCell deactivation due to Xp-U link status change to unavailable

##### 2.4.7.3.2.0-1 ext-SCell deactivation due to Xp-U link status change to unavailable Bulk ext-SCell deactivation due to Xp-U link status change to unavailable (ID: 11159326)

**2.4.7.3.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11159332`)

ext-SCell deactivation due to Xp-U link status change to unavailable
When primary 5G-L2-PS change Xp-U link status to unavailable by himself (as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12815\_replaced([11122016](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqcM4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or receives indication from ext-SCell 5G-L2-PS in PsPeerCtrl\_ScellMeasurementRep with IE xpLinkStatus set to unavailable (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2118\_replaced([10979995](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Ve_Pfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) due to detection of Xp-U link unavailability (according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12816\_replaced([11122039](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqcM5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), primary 5G L2 PS should initiate ext-SCell deactivation for every UE, which have this ext-SCell active, using scell deactivation procedure (see 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_311\_replaced([11195870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfxrYfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and IE modifType in PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUeScheduleReq set to deactivateExtScellXpLinkUnaval (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_656\_replaced([10979441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VbU1vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

##### 2.4.7.3.2.0-2 ext-SCell deactivation due to Xp-U link status change to unavailable ext-SCell deactivation procedure robustness (ID: 11159337)

**2.4.7.3.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11159343`)

ext-SCell deactivation due to Xp-U link status change to unavailable
When Bulk SCell deactivation procedure, due to Xp-U link status change to unavailable, is triggered (see. 5G\_L2\_8469\_replaced([11159326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25QRRvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) it may happen that due to Xp-U link unavailability some messages can or will be lost. To mitigate this, following enhancements to deactivation procedure are introduced:

* limit of retries for ext-SCell deactivation procedure (see. 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_311\_replaced([11195870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfxrYfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is introduced and controlled by R&D parameter rdMaxNumDeactReqRetransForExtScell (see 5G\_L2\_8540\_replaced([11184795](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27tvZfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* after number of retries is exhausted primary 5G L2 PS assumes that procedure is finished successfully and ext-SCell is deactivated.

---

#### 2.4.7.3.3 ext-SCell deactivation with sCellActivationMethod=blind

##### 2.4.7.3.3.0-1 ext-SCell deactivation with sCellActivationMethod=blind ext-SCell deactivation when sCellActivationMethod=blind on primary 5G-L2-PS (ID: 11159354)

**2.4.7.3.3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11159364`)

ext-SCell deactivation with sCellActivationMethod=blind
[Before CB008140-GK]

When primary 5G-L2-PS receives UeSchedulingNotify with modifType=deactivateReq via PsPeerCtrl\_SCellStatusInd (see 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_331\_replaced([11196055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfySe_vREe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) from ext-SCell 5G-L2-PS for a UE that has sCellActivationMethod=blind, primary 5G-L2-PS should ignore this notification and should not initiate ext-SCell deactivation for that UE.

Note: This should only happen if on ext-gNB this UE has sCellActivationMethod=bufferBased

[CB008140-GK]

When primary 5G-L2-PS receives UeSchedulingNotify with modifType=deactivateReq via PsPeerCtrl\_SCellStatusInd (see 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_331\_replaced([11196055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfySe_vREe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) from ext-SCell 5G-L2-PS for a UE that has sCellActivationMethod=blind, primary 5G-L2-PS should deactivate SCell and SCell shall not be re-activated again.

[End CB008140-GK]

---

#### 2.4.7.3.4 SCell deactivation due to SAG state transition

##### 2.4.7.3.4.0-1 SCell deactivation due to SAG state transition SCell deactivation due to UE entering SAG ON state (ID: 11159374)

**2.4.7.3.4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11159391`)

SCell deactivation due to SAG state transition
If NRBTS/NRCELL.sCellDeactInPoorRf = ‘Deact\_Scells\_VoNr\_SAg’ at PCell, Deactivate all of the UE's SCells when UE enters TP enabled::SAG ON state, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19252\_replaced([11205370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwo7h_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else if the UE is configured**[CB006139-CG] or reconfigured**[End CB006139-CG] with FDD + TDD FR1 [Before CB013553-C] R16 [End CB013553-C] UL Tx switching, and TDD SCell with UL Tx switching is activated, deactivate the TDD SCell with UL Tx switching, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19699\_replaced([11122124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqcz_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

Notes:

After CB008236-B-UPD003, blind mode SCell deactivation/activation shall not be impacted by UE entering TP enabled::SAG ON state.

---

#### 2.4.7.3.5 SCell deactivation due to active UE load for FR2

##### 2.4.7.3.5.0-1 SCell deactivation due to active UE load for FR2 Active UE load metric (ID: 11159401)

**2.4.7.3.5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11159411`)

SCell deactivation due to active UE load for FR2
The goal of active UE load metric:

* Provides a mechanism to limit the number of active SCells for each UE in the cell group;
* Aims to maximize the active carrier number per beam and avoid PRB resource without allocation on some specific carriers;

For the active UE load metric definition and how it shall be maintained in L2-PS, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26307\_replaced([11122140](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqdbAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3 active UE load metrics introduced:

* activeUeLoadPerBeamPerCarrierDL
* activeUeLoadPerBeamPerCarrierUL
* totalActiveUeLoad

The activeUeLoadPerBeamPerCarrierUL shall replace legacy UL counter - instantNumOfActiveCellUEUL as the criteria of selecting the lowest or highest active UE load SCell in UE’s best beam for SCell activation or deactivation in uplink direction.

The activeUeLoadPerBeamPerCarrierDL shall replace legacy DL counter - instantNumOfActiveCellUEDL as the criteria of selecting the lowest or highest active UE load SCell in UE’s best beam for SCell activation or deactivation in downlink direction.

The totalActiveUeLoad shall be used as the criteria for the measurement of the whole cell group’s total active UE load, when it exceeds the threshold(definition refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26309\_replaced([11122151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqdbAvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), those UEs with more active SCell number than maximum allowed number shall trigger SCell deactivation.

A matrix is maintained in L2\_PS DL to record the active UE load per beam per carrier, for simplicity following table is an example with some limitation:

* maximum beam number = 6, beam id ranges from 0 to 4;
* maximum carrier number = 8, the carrier id ranges from 0 to 7;
* totally 16 CA UE connected, the UE id ranges from 1 to 16;
* all UE’s PCell located in carrier id 0;
* all UEs have 4 DL active carriers except for UE-9 and UE-14, these two UEs have 5 DL active carriers;
* only DL UE load taken into account;

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a table with data related to active UE load per carrier and beam. It also includes a JSON-like structure and a smaller table detailing active UE load deactivation.
The main table has rows for "Beam [0]" through "Beam [4]" and columns for "Carrier [0]" through "Carrier [7]", plus a "Total Active UE Load" column. Each cell within the carrier columns contains a list of numbers, presumably representing UE IDs or load values. The "Active UE Load per CC" row at the bottom shows a single number for each carrier.
The JSON-like structure shows two objects, each with "ueId", "isPSCell", and "operationalMode" fields.
The smaller table has columns "Active UE Load Deactivation", "Legacy Metric (active #UEs/CC)", "New Metric (active #UEs/CC/Beam)", and "Best Beam". It lists two entries: "UE-9" and "UE-14".
There are also two lines of text above the JSON-like structure:
"activeUeLoadPerBeamPerCarrierDL[2][1] = 2"
"activeUeLoadPerBeamPerCarrierUL[2][1] = 1"
Here is the table in markdown format:

| Beam | Carrier [0] | Carrier [1] | Carrier [2] | Carrier [3] | Carrier [4] | Carrier [5] | Carrier [6] | Carrier [7] | Total Active UE Load |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Beam [0] | [13, 2, 7] | [7, 13] | [7] | [2] | [7] | [7] | [2, 13] | [2] |  |
| Beam [1] | [16, 9, 10] | [9] | [10, 16] | [9, 10] | [9] | [16] | [16] | [9, 10] |  |
| Beam [2] | [6, 1, 5, 14] | [1, 14] | [13] | [1, 6] | [5, 6] | [6, 14] | [5, 14] | [1, 5] | 66 |
| Beam [3] | [11, 15, 8] | [15] | [11, 8] | [11] | [8] | [11, 15] | [8] | [15] |  |
| Beam [4] | [3, 12, 4] | [4, 12] | [3] | [3] | [4, 12] | [3] | [12] | [4] |  |
| Active UE Load per CC | 16 | 8 | 7 | 7 | 7 | 7 | 7 | 7 |  |
| Here is the smaller table in markdown format: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

| Active UE Load Deactivation | Legacy Metric (active #UEs/CC) | New Metric (active #UEs/CC/Beam) | Best Beam |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| UE-9 | CC[1] | CC[3] or CC[7] | Beam[1] |
| UE-14 | CC[1] | CC[1] or CC[5] or CC[7] | Beam[2] |

File "FR2 activeUeLoad based Scell deactivation.xlsx", link in sharepoint:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F7%2E%20Carrier%20Aggregation%20Control%2F3%2E%20SCell%20Deactivation&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

1). During SCell activation procedure, activeUeLoadPerBeamPerCarrierDL and activeUeLoadPerBeamPerCarrierUL in current UE’s best beam shall be taken into account, so the active UE shall be distributed across over all the carriers with load balanced.

2). When periodic active UE load check starts, since now totalActiveUeLoad(66) > threshold(64), maxAllowedCC = 4, so UE-9 and UE-14 shall select one Scell to trigger deactivation individually.

3). For UE-9, if with legacy criteria of SCell selection, carrier-1 shall be selected, this will cause carrier-1 without active UE in beam-1. But with new SCell selction metric, carrier-3 or carrier-7 shall be the candidates for deactivation, one of them shall be selected finally. The similar case for UE-14.

Below events shall trigger the metrics updates in the matrix:

* UE setup
* UE release
* SCell activated
* SCell deactivated
* SCell released
* Cell delete
* Beam change
* Beam failure recovery

---

##### 2.4.7.3.5.0-2 SCell deactivation due to active UE load for FR2 Active UE load triggered Scell deactivation (ID: 11159417)

**2.4.7.3.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11159426`)

SCell deactivation due to active UE load for FR2
For algorithm level descriptions of active UE load triggered SCell deactivation, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26306\_replaced([11122135](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqcz__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When sCellActivationMethod is set to ‘bufferBased’ or ‘bufferAndRLQualityBased’, if active UE load based SCell deactivation trigger condition fulfilled in cell group, the active UE load check shall work together with buffer and RLQ check in L2-PS DL, which shall impact the final check result in DL, more information can refer to 5G\_L2\_14217\_replaced([11159743](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25StifvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When sCellActivationMethod is set to ‘blind’, active UE load based SCell deactivation shall work as well, UE’s SCell number shall not exceed the max allowed SCell number, L2-PS DL then select candidate SCell(s) with highest active UE load metric in current UE’s best beam and do SCell deactivation. Similar as buffer and RLQ check, in blind mode active UE load triggered SCell deactivation shall work together with periodic checking as before, but in each period only 1 time check shall be executed, L2-PS DL shall trigger SCell deactivation accordingly.

Following diagram is an example of how active UE load triggered SCell deactivation interworks with blind/bufferBased/bufferAndRadioLinkQualityBased SCell deactivation, impact to new Scell activation demonstrated as well.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline illustrating the behavior of four User Equipments (UEs) in a cellular network, specifically focusing on Secondary Cell (SCell) activation and deactivation mechanisms. The timeline is segmented into Super Frame Numbers (SFNs) and slots within each SFN. Each UE's activity is depicted on a separate horizontal track, showing events like `sCellActivationMethod`, `DL CC`, `UL CC`, `UE Group`, `Current DL active SCell number`, `prohibit timer expiry`, `deactivation`, `MAC-CE ACK`, `rdPeriodScellCheck`, `scell deactivation delay period`, `activation delay expiry`, and `rdProhibitScellCheck`.
The top part of the image provides a general condition for Ue load management: "When total active Ue load >= nbActiveCarriers Threshold". It also outlines steps for calculating the maximum allowed active SCell number per UE, selecting a new maximum allowed active SCell, starting the `auLoadReductionTimer` in the Cell Group, and setting the `auLoadBasedSCellDeactCheck` flag.
Below this, the timeline for each UE is detailed:
**UE-1:**
\* **Configuration:** `sCellActivationMethod = blind`, `8 DL CC + 4 UL CC configured`, added to `UE Group 7`, current `DL active SCell number = 7`.
\* **Events:** A `prohibit timer expiry` occurs around slot 14. A `Check result = active Ue load deactivation` is noted, leading to the selection of the highest DL load SCell for deactivation. `deactivation MAC-CE` and `MAC-CE ACK` events follow.
**UE-2:**
\* **Configuration:** `sCellActivationMethod = bufferAndRLQualityBased`, `8 DL CC + 4 UL CC configured`, added to `UE Group 1`, current `DL active SCell number = 7`.
\* **Events:** `rdPeriodScellCheck` occurs. A `Buffer and RLQ check result = rlq deactivation` is indicated, with no need to select an SCell for active Ue load deactivation, and a start to deactivate RLQ bad SCell(s). `deactivation MAC-CE` and `MAC-CE ACK` events are shown. The `scell deactivation delay period` is also depicted.
**UE-3:**
\* **Configuration:** `sCellActivationMethod = bufferAndRLQualityBased`, `8 DL CC + 4 UL CC configured`, added to `UE Group 0`.
\* **Events:** A `prohibit timer expiry` is shown. `rdPeriodScellCheck` events are marked. A `Buffer and RLQ check result = activation` is noted, with `auLoadBasedSCellDeactCheck = 1` and `auLoadReductionTimer is running`. `Scell activation not allowed` is stated, followed by resetting `auLoadBasedSCellDeactCheck` to 0, and a `Final check result = no action`. The `scell deactivation delay period` is also shown.
**UE-4:**
\* **Configuration:** `sCellActivationMethod = bufferAndRLQualityBased`, `8 DL CC + 4 UL CC configured`, added to `UE Group 2`.
\* **Events:** `activation delay expiry` is marked. A `Buffer and RLQ check result = activation` is indicated, with `total active Ue load < nbActiveCarriers Threshold`. `No auLoadBasedSCellDeactCheck and auLoadReductionTimer limitation` is stated, and the `Final check result = activation`. The selection of one least load SCell to activate based on active Ue load metric per beam is noted. `activation MAC-CE` and `MAC-CE ACK` events occur, followed by the `scell activation delay period`. Another section shows `Buffer and RLQ check result = activation`, `auLoadBasedSCellDeactCheck = 1` and active SCell number max allowed, `Scell activation not allowed`, `Reset auLoadBasedSCellDeactCheck to 0`, `Final result = no action`, and `Start prohibit timer, waiting for next check period`.
The timeline spans across multiple SFNs, indicating continuous operation and periodic checks. The color coding in the top SFN bar represents different states: 'D' for Downlink, 'U' for Uplink, and 'S' for Sleep.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2815.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

File "FR2 activeUeLoad based Scell deactivation.xlsx", link in sharepoint:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F7%2E%20Carrier%20Aggregation%20Control%2F3%2E%20SCell%20Deactivation&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

(1). sCellActivationMethod of UE-1 is configured as ‘blind’, when active UE load based SCell deactivation triggered in cell group, in the next nearest periodic checking opportunity in L2-PS DL after cell group periodic active UE load check, UE-1 shall select one highest active UE load SCell in best beam to deactivate.

(2). sCellActivationMethod of UE-2 is configured as ‘bufferAndRLQualityBased’, and current buffer and RLQ check cross over the boundary of active UE load check in cell group, but since the last buffer and RLQ check is later than active UE load check, the final buffer and RLQ check result shall be impacted by active UE load condition, if buffer and RLQ check result = rlq deactivation, then no additional Scell deactivation required due to active UE load, those bad RLQ Scell deactivation shall fulfill the active UE load condition.

(3). sCellActivationMethod of UE-3 is configured as ‘bufferAndRLQualityBased’, after active UE load check in cell group, the first buffer and RLQ check result shows one additional SCell is required, but due to this buffer and RLQ check within the scope of auLoadReductionTimer, no new SCell activation allowed this time. After the time of rdProhibitScellCheck, new round of buffer and RLQ check still require one new SCell activation, then this time SCell activation shall be allowed if total active UE load not exceeding the active UE load threshold.

(4). sCellActivationMethod of UE-4 is configured as ‘bufferAndRLQualityBased’, after active UE load check in cell group, previous SCell activation delay period has not been expired. After activation period expiry, if new round of buffer and RLQ check requires one new SCell activation, auLoadReductionTimer has expired this time. but SCell activation shall be not allowed due to auLoadBasedSCellDeactCheck not cleared ever. After this time of buffer and RLQ check, auLoadBasedSCellDeactCheck reset to 0.

Notes:

1. All CA UEs with sCellActivationMethod equal to blind or bufferBased or bufferAndRLQBased are added evenly in UE distribution table.

2. NRBTS.sCellActivationMethod supports on-line modification, and the modified value shall take effect on new users rather than existing users. The sCellActivationMethod is stored per UE context.

3. When UCI multiplexing is enabled for different TDD FR2 CC, the SCell activation delay period shall get longer, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1794\_replaced([11110605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.7.3.6 Interworking with SRS based DL BF for intra-band TDD CA

#### 2.4.7.3.6-1 Interworking with SRS based DL BF for intra-band TDD CA

##### 2.4.7.3.6-1.0-1 Interworking with SRS based DL BF for intra-band TDD CA For ext-SCell deactivation due to synchronization status change to OutOfSync, see 5G_L2_16188_replaced( 11163147 ). (ID: 11159452)

#### 2.4.7.3.6-2 Interworking with SRS based DL BF for intra-band TDD CA

##### 2.4.7.3.6-2.0-1 Interworking with SRS based DL BF for intra-band TDD CA For DL CA, when SCell deactivation is caused by 11122234: Evaluation of Scell scheduler processing load for Scell deactivation for DL branch trigger in 12678142: SCell deactivation checking when SCell is overload , SCell shall report it to PCell by deactReqType = overloadDeactiveReq(see 10979873: PsPeerCtrl_ScellStatusInd message content: ). For UL CA ,  when SCell deactivation is caused by 11122241: Evaluation of Scell scheduler processing load for Scell deactivation for UL branch trigger, PCell shall record the SCell status. when PCell decides to deactivate the SCell, if the UE's status on the SCell is deactReqType = overloadDeactiveReq or UL overload for the last zero buffer split for UL CA, the PCell should increase PM Counter in 12533986: Total number of times the DL countermeasure type 9 is active . (ID: 12531642)

##### 2.4.7.3.6.0-1 Interworking with SRS based DL BF for intra-band TDD CA Impact for Scell deactivation (ID: 11159437)

**2.4.7.3.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11159443`)

Interworking with SRS based DL BF for intra-band TDD CA
When SCell deactivation procedure is triggered on PCell’s 5G-L2-PS instance (see 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_311\_replaced([11195870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfxrYfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and SCell is present in srsBfSyncStatusForScells, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall proceed with 5G\_L2\_16753\_replaced([11154964](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_242oofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (see “SCell deactivation” section) for PCell and with 5G\_L2\_16755\_replaced([11154989](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_242opfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SCell.

---

### 2.4.7.4 Radio Link Quality Monitoring

#### 2.4.7.4.1 Radio Link Quality Monitoring for FR1 CA

##### 2.4.7.4.1.0-1 Radio Link Quality Monitoring for FR1 CA Scell report DL RL quality change to Pcell (ID: 11159467)

**2.4.7.4.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11159472`)

Radio Link Quality Monitoring for FR1 CA
SCell 5G-L2-PS shall report the change of RL quality between "bad" and "good" to P(S)Cell 5G-L2-PS, not only when the SCell permanent deactivation required. The RL quality is the main input parameter for the selection which SCell shall be deactivated due to overheating assistance information reported by UE.

---

##### 2.4.7.4.1.0-2 Radio Link Quality Monitoring for FR1 CA Scell UL RLQ monitoring and evaluation (ID: 11159478)

**2.4.7.4.1.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12472896`)

Radio Link Quality Monitoring for FR1 CA
When SCell activated, SCell UL will start to monitor and evaluate SCell UL radio link quality periodically for SCell activation method: “blind”, “bufferBased” or “bufferAndRLQualityBased”.

After SCell receives activation request, the CA UE was added to one UE group in UE distribution table of SCell UL, UE group number is identified by rdPeriodScellCheckFR1.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a hierarchical diagram illustrating a distribution table for User Equipment (UE) groups within a cell. The top level of the diagram features a central node labeled "SCell\_UL\_UE\_Distribution\_Table". From this central node, lines extend downwards to ten circular nodes, each representing a "UeGroup" numbered from 0 to 9.
Below these "UeGroup" nodes, a second level of the diagram depicts the actual distribution of UEs within each group. Each "UeGroup" is represented by a dashed rectangular box containing several smartphone icons.
Specifically:
- UeGroup\_0 contains four smartphone icons arranged in a vertical column.
- UeGroup\_1 contains three smartphone icons arranged in a vertical column.
- UeGroup\_2 contains three smartphone icons arranged in a vertical column, with one of the icons highlighted in red and enclosed in a green circle. A green arrow points from UeGroup\_1 to UeGroup\_2.
- UeGroup\_3 contains three smartphone icons arranged in a vertical column.
- UeGroup\_4 contains three smartphone icons arranged in a vertical column.
- UeGroup\_5 contains three smartphone icons arranged in a vertical column.
- UeGroup\_6 contains three smartphone icons arranged in a vertical column.
- UeGroup\_7 contains two smartphone icons arranged in a vertical column.
- UeGroup\_8 contains two smartphone icons arranged in a vertical column.
- UeGroup\_9 contains two smartphone icons arranged in a vertical column.
The diagram visually represents how UEs are organized and distributed across different groups within a cell, with a specific focus on UeGroup\_2 being highlighted.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2827.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Following parameters define the UL RLQ evaluation opportunities:

 rdPeriodScellCheckFR1 – refer to 5G\_L2\_1388\_replaced([11184635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27shR_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 rdNumberOfScellChecksFR1 – refer to 5G\_L2\_1390\_replaced([11184651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27shTPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 rdProhibitScellCheck – refer to 5G\_L2\_1392\_replaced([11184664](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27shTvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For each slot, the UE(s) in the UE group corresponding to this slot will be evaluated for UL RLQ status if it’s valid for evaluation.

UL RLQ evaluation conditions:

**[Before CNI-137616-C]**

 UL RLQ enabled, refer to 5G\_L2\_9371\_replaced([11184784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27tvY_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**[End CNI-137616-C]**

 SCell in activated state

 Current evaluation slot not in prohibit slots defined by rdProhibitScellCheck

 Valid UL MCS (not initial or default MCS)

 This SCell not in overheating status

 This SCell not in bad RLQ status

[image-1][image\_desc]The image depicts a diagram illustrating the "SCell UL RLQ Evaluation" process. It shows a timeline divided into slots, labeled from 0 to 19, and then wrapping around to 0 and 1. Different types of slots are indicated by letters: 'D' for Downlink, 'S' for Special, and 'U' for Uplink. The diagram is divided into three repeating segments, each representing a cycle of evaluation.
Below the slot timeline, there are annotations indicating parameters and conditions related to the evaluation. These include:
- `rdPeriodScellCheckFR1(=10 slots(default))`
- `rdNumberOfScellChecksFR1 (=1(default))`
- `rdProhibitScellCheckFR1 (8 slots, default: 0 slots)`
These parameters are associated with specific time intervals within the slot sequence, indicated by brackets and arrows. The diagram also shows three labels: "1 RLQ sample" below each of the three main segments.
At the bottom of the diagram, there is a formula: `(rdPeriodScellCheckFR1 * rdNumberOfScellChecksFR1 + rdProhibitScellCheckFR1) rrmNumMcsCheckForRLQinUL / (2^µ) >= sCellChannelEstimationPeriod(2000ms)`.
Finally, there are three legend entries with corresponding colored arrows:
- Green arrow: "Slot when conditions are fulfilled for UL RLQ evaluation"
- Red arrow: "Slot for RLQ evaluation but in prohibit slots"
- Blue arrow: "Slots when UE is prohibited from UL RLQ evaluation"
The diagram visually represents how different parameters influence the timing and conditions for evaluating Secondary Cell (SCell) Uplink (UL) Radio Link Quality (RLQ). The repeating pattern suggests a periodic evaluation process with specific windows for checking and potential prohibitions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2827.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Link:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=V8ishU&cid=9206de7d%2Df76b%2D4167%2D95a0%2D9ed94135ffa1&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F7%2E%20Carrier%20Aggregation%20Control%2F4%2E%20Radio%20Link%20Quality%20Monitoring&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

Following variables are used to evaluate the RLQ status:

 rrmNumMcsCheckForRLQinUL - this is the RLQ check number in each whole evaluation period, if RLQ evaluation condition check pass, it's increased by 1

 rrmSamplesScellBadRLQinUL - this is the bad RLQ check number in each whole evaluation period, if MCS is lower than the threshold, it's increased by 1

 rrmScellRLQStatusUL - when each whole evaluation period complete(the sample number reached), the RLQ status will be evaluated

When SCell activated, rrmNumMcsCheckForRLQinUL and rrmSamplesScellBadRLQinUL are initialize as 0, rrmScellRLQStatusUL = “good”

When each RLQ evaluation complete(*(rdPeriodScellCheckFR1 [5G\_L2\_1388\_replaced([11184635](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27shR_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))] \* rdNumberOfScellChecksFR1 [5G\_L2\_1390\_replaced([11184651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27shTPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))] + rdProhibitScellCheckFR1 [5G\_L2\_1392\_replaced([11184664](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27shTvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))]) \* rrmNumMcsCheckForRLQinUL / (2^µ) >= NRBTS/NRCELL.sCellChannelEstimationPeriod), rrmNumMcsCheckForRLQinUL and rrmSamplesScellBadRLQinUL are re-initialize as 0, rrmScellRLQStatusUL will be set according to evaluation result.*

For the evaluation procedure, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11868\_replaced([11121619](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqZwovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11871\_replaced([11121633](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqZwpfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For each bad RLQ sample, the instant and average or filtered DL pathloss will be recorded according to local or forwarded PHR, the filtered DL pathloss will be uesed for SCell UL RLQ resume, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13950\_replaced([11121669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqZwrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When the RLQ evaluation is bad, SCell UL will notify PCell UL via psPeerCtrl\_ScellUlControlInd, refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_2944\_replaced([10980976](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Vm7A_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note:

1. For some scenarios(UL LA expiry, overheating, etc), SCell RLQ evaluation may be interrupted, the evaluation would continue when SCell recovery from these scenarios.
2. The scaledMcs is used for bad RLQ sample judgement, which is calculated according to the assinged PRB and before MCS downgrade if it's activated.

---

##### 2.4.7.4.1.0-3 Radio Link Quality Monitoring for FR1 CA Scell Ul scheduling suspend (ID: 11159508)

**2.4.7.4.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11159512`)

Radio Link Quality Monitoring for FR1 CA
If PCell Ul receives the bad Ul RLQ status via PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUlControlInd, PCell will trigger buffer split stop refer to 5G\_L2\_7784\_replaced([11161228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25aCSfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and adjust the split tx power refer to 5G\_L2\_8639\_replaced([11161904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25dFnPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For SCell Ul, the bad RLQ status will be stored, and resume procedure will start to run, increase Ul RLQ counter "SCELL\_UL\_SUSPEND\_RLQ" by 1.

If PsPeerCtrl\_PcellUlBufferSplitInd with rrmBufferedDataUlTotalBytes=0 received in SCell Ul, new transmission will stop and re-transmission could continue, SCell Ul inactivity timer will start.

---

##### 2.4.7.4.1.0-4 Radio Link Quality Monitoring for FR1 CA Scell Ul scheduling resume (ID: 11159518)

**2.4.7.4.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11159524`)

Radio Link Quality Monitoring for FR1 CA
Different from SCell Dl RLQ resume procedure, which could use Ul MCS comparing with the RLQ resume threshold, since the CQI measurement is still available even SCell Dl scheduling suspend.

SCell Ul RLQ resume have to compare the instant Dl pathloss with a threshold instread of Ul MCS, because after Ul scheduling supend the Ul MCS will be reset to default value in case Ul LA expriy and it will have no chance to update, here the Dl pathloss is not a pre-defined parameter, it's calculated by the instant Dl pathloss of each bad RLQ sample during the evaluation period, refer to the formula in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13950\_replaced([11121669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqZwrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In case n times consecutive check pass, Ul RLQ status resume from bad to good, resume procedure stop:

filtered Dl pathloss - instant Dl pathloss > δ dB

- n:  specified by rdNumOfChecksforRecovScellULFR1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12689\_replaced([11124148](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqoaJPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- δ:  specified by rdPathlossDeltaforRecovScellULFR1 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12691\_replaced([11124155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqoaJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note:

 When calculate and record the Dl pathloss in RLQ evaluation period, PHR could be virtual or real, and local or forwarded, and this instant Dl pathloss used to calculate filtered Dl pathloss, it will the Dl pathloss threshold in RLQ resume procedure.

 When start RLQ resume procedure, only PCell forwarded virtual PHR could be used for Dl pathloss calculation, and this instant Dl pathloss will be used to compare with stored filtered Dl pathloss.

SCell Ul sends good Ul RLQ status to PCell Ul via PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUlControlInd, and increase Ul RLQ counter "SCELL\_UL\_RECOV\_RLQ" by 1.

When PCell Ul receives good Ul RLQ status, PCell Ul may tirgger buffer split refer to 5G\_L2\_7625\_replaced([11161003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25YNH_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and power split procedure refer to 5G\_L2\_7106\_replaced([11161721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25b3evvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If SCell Ul receives PsPeerCtrl\_PcellUlBufferSplitInd with rrmBufferedDataUlTotalBytes>0, SCell Ul will stop Ul inactivity timer.

---

#### 2.4.7.4.2 Buffer based and Radio Link Quality Monitoring

##### 2.4.7.4.2.0-1 Buffer based and Radio Link Quality Monitoring Update the pathloss information (ID: 11159533)

**2.4.7.4.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11159545`)

Buffer based and Radio Link Quality Monitoring
[CNI-71344]Requirement moved from CP2[End CNI-71344]

5G-L2-PS shall update the DL pathloss as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2746\_replaced([11121446](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqYikPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when the valid power headroom report for PSCell is received from 5G-L2-LO in LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd message.

5G-L2-PS shall update the UL pathloss as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2748\_replaced([11121531](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqYik_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when the valid power headroom report for PSCell is received from 5G-L2-LO in LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd message, and when the rx power of PUSCH for PSCell is received from 5G-L1-UL in UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs message.

For CA UEs, Multiple Entry PHR is used. 5G-L2-PS shall only consider the PHR of PSCell with V=0 (see 5G\_L2\_9489\_replaced([11215735](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-6NVfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as valid one.

5G-L2-PS UL shall ignore the reported values for the unknown LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd.phrSCellIndex in case that Multiple Entry PHR is used.

5G-L2-PS UL shall always indicate the latest DL pathloss value to 5G-L2-PS DL when it is updated.

---

#### 2.4.7.5 Common handling for SCell activation / Deactivation

###### 2.4.7.5.1 Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation

###### 2.4.7.5.1.0-1 Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation Coordination of SCell activation/deactivation (ID: 11159562)

**2.4.7.5.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11159571`)

Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation
Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2778\_replaced([11120862](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqU4JfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the overview.

RL Quality based SCell activation/deactivation parameters: mcsDeactSCellDl, mcsDeactSCellUl, pathlossActSCellDl, pathlossActSCellUl can be changed on-line. A change will take affect only for the new UEs for which the UE context setup was established after the change. Already connected UEs will be handled according to the method and parameters that were valid at the time of UE context setup.

Additional trigger for SCell deactivation is reception of PsCell\_CellStopSchedulingReq followed by PsCell\_CellDeleteReq. This procedure is originated by OAM Cell deletion procedure and triggered by 5G-CP-RT (see 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_89\_replaced([11178190](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BeXWLfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Upon reception of PsCell\_CellStopSchedulingReq, 5G-L2-PS shall stop scheduling PDSCH/PUSCH as well as CSI-RS in the given cell (same implementation as for PSCell is applied for SCells). If cell to be deleted is the SCell that was just activated and rdProhibitScellCheck timer is started just before cell deletion, 5G-L2-PS shall stop scheduling on that SCell and at the time of rdProhibitScellCheck expiry new validation cyclus will start and at the last validation SCell (corresponding to the deleted cell) will be deactivated. For all the UE's configured with the Cell to be deleted as SCell, 5G-L2-PS shall trigger SCell deactivation. In order to avoid triggering of huge number of SCell deactivation MAC CEs simultaneously, SCell deactivation timing shall follow regular deactivation evaluation scheme and deleted cell shall be tagged for the deactivation that shall be handled with same priority as radio link quality based deactivation. E.g. for each UE configured with affected cell as SCell, regular evaluation will be done and at the time of decision deactivation of deleted SCell shall be triggered. In case multiple cells are deleted, deactivation shall be done with single MAC CE for all deleted cells. If simultaneous triggers for SCell(s) deactivation due to radio link quality and cell(s) deletion is received all the affected SCells shall be deactivated with single MAC CE. SCell(s) deactivated due to cell deletion shall be marked as unavailable and activation of this SCells is not possible.

Note: This is workarround solution applicable only until SCell(s) release procedure is implemented (no within 5G18A release).

Additional trigger for SCell deactivation is reception of PsUser\_UserStopReq followed by PsUser\_UserModifyReq. This procedure is originated by some User Modification cases to modify/release SCell triggered by 5G-CP-RT (see 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_192\_replaced([11188767](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfNDofvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Upon reception of PsUser\_UserStopReq, 5G-L2-PS shall stop scheduling PDSCH/PUSCH as well as CSI-RS for UE on the target SCell. If rdProhibitScellCheck timer of the SCell is started, and at the time of rdProhibitScellCheck expiry new validation cyclus will start and at the last validation SCell will be deactivated. SCell deactivation timing shall follow regular deactivation evaluation scheme and shall be handled with same priority as radio link quality based deactivation. If simultaneous triggers for SCell(s) deactivation due to radio link quality and cell(s) deletion is received all the affected SCells shall be deactivated with single MAC CE. SCell(s) deactivated shall be released and the configuration of SCell(s) shall be removed from UE context.

Note: Originally, we planned to release SCell but due to simplification and avoidance of performance degradation we decided to keep the SCell configured and permanently deactivate the cell. Once UE reconnect new SCell selection will be done and in this case deleted Cell will not be considered.

[CB012734-B]

When active Ue load based SCell deactivation is enabled in FR2, 5G L2-PS DL shall do the active Ue load check together with buffer and radio link quality check, the SCell activation and deactivation result shall be impacted by the active Ue load condition.

[End CB012734-B]

---

###### 2.4.7.5.1.0-2 Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation Scell activation checking procedure (ID: 11159577)

**2.4.7.5.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11159581`)

Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation
Basically the activation checking procedure is same for FR2 and FR1, including checking period and prohibit period. And all RRC UEs are distributed in different slot of a distribution table to avoid too high cpu load when checking.

But the different radParameters are configured to different frequency range. All relative radParameters with default value are listed in below table.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **FR2 DL/UL** | **radParam: default value** | **FR1 DL** | **radParam: default value** | **FR1 UL** | **radParam: default value** |
| rdPeriodScellCheck: 10 | rdPeriodScellCheckFR1: 10 | reuse rdPeriodScellCheckFR1 |  |  |  |
| rdNumOfScellChecks: 3 | rdNumOfScellChecksFR1: 1 | rdNumberOfScellChecksFR1UL: 3 |  |  |  |
| rdProhibitScellCheck: 80 | rdProhibitScellCheckFR1: 0 | No prohibit period |  |  |  |

FR2 CA refer to 5G\_L2\_23203\_replaced([11158998](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25OcEPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

FR1 UL CA refer to 5G\_L2\_7769\_replaced([11159027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25OcFPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.7.5.1.0-3 Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation SCell deactivation checking when SCell is overload (ID: 12678142)

**2.4.7.5.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12678143`)

Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation
For FR1 DL, a UE on SCell shall take the SCell as not overload when the SCell is activated. When checking a UE for SCell deactivation if the UE's inactivity timer is not running:

If  the SCell turns to overload as [11122234: Evaluation of Scell scheduler processing load for Scell deactivation for DL branch](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqeCF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), L2-PS shall send LoCtrl\_StopFlowControlReq to L2LO, stop DL scheduling and clear all the harq, start inactivity timer.

After L2-PS send LoCtrl\_StopFlowControlReq to L2LO for the UE, then if  SCell turns to not overload as [11122234: Evaluation of Scell scheduler processing load for Scell deactivation for DL branch](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqeCF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), L2-PS shall send LoCtrl\_StartFlowControlReq to L2LO, re-start DL scheduling when data comes from L2LO and restart the inactivity timer as [11122234: Evaluation of Scell scheduler processing load for Scell deactivation for DL branch](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqeCF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

Note:

1. The flow control stop/start process may overlap with other flow control stop procedure(when bad RLQ as in [11121583: Evaluation of Scell Channel Quality (Scell)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqZJmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) or overheating in [11121319: Scell Deactivation based on Scell Inactivity Timer](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqX7c_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), do not send repeat LoCtrl\_StopFlowControlReq/LoCtrl\_StartFlowControlReq to L2LO. I.e. if a LoCtrl\_StopFlowControlReq is sent due to bad RLQ or overheating or overload, do not sent another  LoCtrl\_StopFlowControlReq, if LoCtrl\_StartFlowControlReq is needed due to bad RLQ recover or overheating recover or overload recover, make sure all RLQ/overheating/overload are in good status then send it.
2. The inactivity timer start/re-start process mentioned above may interwork with bad RLQ and overheating. When overload happens, do not start the inactivity timer if SCell is in bad RLQ or overheating status, when overload recovers,  do not re-start the inactivity timer if the SCell is in bad RLQ or overheating status.
3. The overload status handle for deactReqType = overloadDeactiveReq(see [10979873: PsPeerCtrl\_ScellStatusInd message content:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VeYJfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall be done if only when inactivity timer started by overload expires and the subsequent deactivation is triggered.

---

###### 2.4.7.5.1.0-4 Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation UE activation/deactivation checking opportunity distribution (ID: 11159586)

**2.4.7.5.1.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `11159630`)

Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation
5G-L2-PS (both DL and UL) shall maintain a table for the UE activation checking opportunity distribution. The length of the table is rdPeriodScellCheck (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2778\_replaced([11120862](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqU4JfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for FR2, refer to 5G\_L2\_20224\_replaced([11159636](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25SGefvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for FR1. In implementation, the table size is the max value of rdPeriodScellCheck, but only use the size according to current rdPeriodScellCheck value. Note, the 5G-L2-PS shall NOT support runtime modification for the value of rdPeriodScellCheck.

In each position of distribution table, there is a list of UE that will be checked at this position, and a value that count the number of UE exist in this position. Each UE can be only in the UE list of 1 position in the distribution table, and the position indicate the checking resource allocated to this UE.

For FR2 CA when a UE context setup with SCell activation method as 'buffer based', 5G-L2-PS UL shall find the position in the distribution table which have the least UE count. If more than 1 position have same count, then select the one with smaller index. 5G-L2-PS shall put the UE into the list of the selected position. When 5G-L2-PS UL perform the 1st check, it will inform 5G-L2-PS DL about the checking TTI number, 5G-L2-PS DL shall calculate the correct position according to this TTI number from UL (+2 in the position according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2778\_replaced([11120862](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqU4JfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and record the UE into its own distribution table.

For FR2 CA when a UE context setup with SCell activation method as 'bufferAndRLQualityBased’, 5G-L2-PS shall add the UE in the distribution table as same as activation method is ''buffer based'.

For FR1 UL CA, DL and UL maintain a UE distribution table seperately, but the UE should be at the same position in the two distribution tables. UE is added to DL distribution table firstly with an checkResourceIndex which is index of the table, then send the checkResourceIndex to UL by DlToUlIntraUpdate with CmdType FR1\_START\_CHECK\_CMD. UL add UE in UL distribution table with the checkResourceIndex  after receiving FR1\_START\_CHECK\_CMD. 2 slots advanced to DL is implemented when every TTI by using checkResourceIndex - 2, this operation make sure that DL and UL have same position for a specific UE, but UL is 2 slots advanced than DL, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2778\_replaced([11120862](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqU4JfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a process involving "PCELL UL" and "PCELL DL". There are two main steps depicted:
1. **From PCELL DL to PCELL UL:**
\* "1. AddUe to DL distribution table"
\* "2. Send index in the table to UL"
2. **From PCELL DL to PCELL UL (implied, as it's a separate box):**
\* "AddUe to UL distribution table with the index"
The diagram suggests a flow where a UE (User Equipment) is added to a distribution table in PCELL DL, and then an index related to this addition is sent to PCELL UL. Subsequently, the UE is added to a UL distribution table using this index.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2847.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Every TTI, 5G-L2-PS shall take the list of UE from distribution table at the index equal to current TTI number mod rdPeriodScellCheck for FR2 or rdPeriodScellCheckFR1 for FR1.

For FR2, each UE in the list shall trigger the operation of checking opportunity to the activation manager state machine (5G\_L2\_3546\_replaced([11159803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25TUk_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of this UE.

For FR1 refer to 5G\_L2\_20224\_replaced([11159636](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25SGefvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When a UE context setup with SCell activation method as "blind", it will not be added in the distribution table.

When this scell already activated, check will not be proceed anymore.

Below is an example to illustrate how the distribution table works. In this example the rdPeriodScellCheck = 10, so the first 10 ueGroupInfo is used as the position of CA UE. Every ueGroupInfo can store maximum 300 UE since 300 = 3000/10.

Every TTI scheduler use Xsfn mod rdPeriodScellCheck to get the index of ueGroupInfo to check Activation/Deactivation.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image depicts a diagram illustrating a data structure or system related to user equipment (UE) management. The diagram is organized into several sections, each representing different components and their relationships.
At the top, there are parameters defined: `rdPeriodScellCheck = numOfGroup = 10`, `maxUeNum = 3000`, and `currentSizeOfGroup = maxUeNum / numOfGroup = 300`.
Below these parameters, there's a representation of `ueGroupInfo<array>`, which is divided into segments labeled `index1`, `index2`, `index3`, `index10`, and `index100`. Each segment, except for `index100`, is shown as a blue rectangle, suggesting groups of data. Arrows point from these segments to different parts of the `ueList<vector>`. The arrows are labeled with `startIndex` and `sizeOfGroup` values. For `index1`, `startIndex=0` and `sizeOfGroup` are indicated. For `index2`, `startIndex=300` and `sizeOfGroup` are indicated. For `index3`, `startIndex=600` and `sizeOfGroup` are indicated. For `index10`, `startIndex=2700` and `sizeOfGroup` are indicated.
The `ueList<vector>` is depicted as a series of white and orange squares, representing individual UEs. The orange squares appear to highlight specific UEs within a group. Numbers below the squares in the `ueList<vector>` indicate indices, such as `0`, `1`, `300`, `600`, and `2700`.
A yellow highlighted box contains the text `ueIndexInUeList = startIndex + sizeOfGroup`, explaining how an index within the `ueList` is calculated.
Below the `ueList<vector>`, there are three rows labeled `Xsfn = 1/10`, `Xsfn = 1/11`, and `Xsfn = 1/25`. Each of these rows shows a smaller representation of a list of squares, some of which are filled with a pinkish-red color. These rows also have numbers below them, such as `0`, `1`, `300`, and `1200`, suggesting different states or groupings of UEs based on `Xsfn` values.
The overall diagram visually represents how groups of UEs are managed, indexed, and potentially accessed or processed, with specific parameters and formulas guiding the operations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2847.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

File: FR1\_DLUL\_checking\_opportunity\_distribution.vsdx

[Before CNI-134375-M]

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&e=29w0aZ&cid=0126e015%2Dde62%2D4cf8%2D87bc%2Db20aedb011c5&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L1%20and%20L2%20Entity%20Level%20Flows%2F8%20User%20Plane%20Data%20Transfer%2F8%2E5%20UL%20CA%20%28MAC%20level%2C%20Inter%20L2%2DPS%20case%2C%20FR1%29&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c&OR=Teams%2DHL&CT=1655087780002&params=eyJBcHBOYW1lIjoiVGVhbXMtRGVza3RvcCIsIkFwcFZlcnNpb24iOiIyNy8yMjA1MDEwMTAwOSJ9>

[CNI-134375-M]

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/5Gdoc/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B0ECDAC10-08E6-46F5-BBA4-CABA16B75908%7D&file=FR1_DLUL_checking_opportunity_distribution.vsdx&action=default&mobileredirect=true&DefaultItemOpen=1>
