###### 2.4.7.5.1.0-5 Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation UE activation/deactivation checking limitation for FR1 (ID: 11159636)

**2.4.7.5.1.0-5.0-5**  (ID: `13336922`)

Common procedure for Buffer and Buffer/Quality based SCell Activation/Deactivation
For FDD and TDD FR1, the UE distribution table length depends on rdPeriodScellCheckFR1, and L2-PS shall NOT support runtime modification for the value of rdPeriodScellCheckFR1.

The UE distribution table of FDD can refer to 5G\_L2\_3539.

When rdCaLoadControlFeatController(5G\_L2\_21408) activates the functionality of bi-directional slot CA check exclusion for TDD FR1, in order to decrease the CA load further, the bi-directional scheduling slot shall not be allowed to do buffer check for Scell addition/activation/deactivation, then the length of UE distribution table for TDD FR1 shall be restricted to the following value:

* rdPeriodScellCheckFR1 – maxNumOfMixedSchedSlot \* (rdPeriodScellCheckFR1 / 10)

The definition of maxNumOfMixedSchedSlot is the maximum possible number of bi-directional scheduling slot within a 10-slot period of each slot pattern, for simplicity it can be identified as the UL slot number within 10-slot period except for FR1 TDD frame structure tdLteConfig0. For tdLteConfig0 frame structure the scheduling slot offset of DL and UL is flexible, DL and UL scheduler are distributed in different slot, the exception is one bi-directional slot, refer to 5G\_L2\_23169.

The bi-directional scheduling slot means the slot has valid K1 and K2 value, but not requiring this slot really scheduled both in DL and UL, by taken into account the K1/K2 impacts by SSB/TRS slot multiplexing with PDSCH, PRACH multiplexing, special slot PDSCH scheduling, RIM and other functionalities, for simplicity we will use the slot with valid K2 as the criterion of possible bi-directional scheduling.

When start checking on one specific slot, if this is bi-directional slot, just skip this slot. Otherwise, this shall be an eligible slot for checking, choose one UE group index from distribution table in the way of round-robin:

* The first eligiable slot for checking in each rdPeriodScellCheckFR1 period shall start from UE group index 0.
* The following eligible slots for checking shall choose the UE group index with increment one by one until the last UE group.

The figure below shows how the UE group index mapping to the slot taken the bi-directional slot into account, the frame structure of this example is 5GC001208, other frame structures will use the similar way:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating a Time Division Duplex (TDD) UE distribution table and its relation to scheduling.
At the top, there's a table with "5GC001208" as a header. The table has columns representing slots numbered from 0 to 19. The rows are:
- **Slot Type**: D, D, D, S, U, D, D, D, S, U, D, D, D, S, U, D, D, S, U
- **Scheduling Type**: DU, I/D, I, D, DU, I/D, I, D, DU, I/D, I, D, DU, I/D, I, D, DU, I/D, I
- **UE Group Index**: 0, 1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
Below this table, there's a section with "scheduling time advance" indicating "2 slots". It also lists "k1" with a value of "3" and "k2" with a value of "2". To the right of this, there are labels: "D", "S", "U" stacked vertically.
Further down, there are definitions for slot types:
- **Downlink slot**
- **Special slot**
- **Uplink slot**
- **Downlink and uplink slot**
- **Idle slot**
- **Idle slot or downlink slot depends on idle scheduling enable or not**
The main part of the diagram shows a "UE distribution table" with "UE group index" as the header for a row of numbers: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. Each of these UE group indices is connected by a line to a cylindrical structure representing a cell or a group of UEs. These cylindrical structures contain multiple stacked rectangles, each with a smaller rectangle inside, presumably representing UEs within different sub-slots or resource blocks. There are eight such structures shown, with ellipses indicating that more continue.
At the bottom, there's a label "FR1 TDD UE distribution table" and "rdPeriodScellCheckFR1 = 10".
The table with scheduling information has some cells highlighted in yellow:
- Slot 3, Scheduling Type: DU
- Slot 5, Slot Type: DU
- Slot 15, Slot Type: DU[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2852.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

File: "TDD FR1 UE distribution table.xlsx", link in sharepoint:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=V8ishU&cid=9206de7d%2Df76b%2D4167%2D95a0%2D9ed94135ffa1&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F7%2E%20Carrier%20Aggregation%20Control%2F5%2E%20Common%20Handling%20of%20SCell%20activation%20and%20deactivation&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

When actCaOverloadCtrlEnh(5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rdActCaOverloadCtrlEnh(5G\_L2\_21410) are both TRUE and rdCaLoadControlFeatController(5G\_L2\_21408) activates the FR1 CA check reduction functionality, to save CPU cost on CA UE checking, if actCaOverloadCtrlEnh is TRUE the UE number in this UE group of distribution shall be further restricted for both FDD and TDD FR1 in downlink direction only, the algorithm of selecting the UEs for checking in the list or corresponding UE group shall follow the way of round-robin and the notes as below:

* Starting from the first UE in the list with the order when UE added into this list, up to rdMaxUeNumPerCheckingIndex (defined in 5G\_L2\_20014) of UEs shall be selected for each slot opportunity.
* Remaining UEs in the list shall wait for the opportunity in next checking period.
* When all UEs in the list have been checked, continue to select UE and check from the first UE in the list.

notes:

1). The UEs for Scell addition and Scell activation check shall be selected together with UE number limitation according to the order when UE added to UE distribution table. **Once buffer monitoring for UE SCell addition is started by [Before CB010741-A] PsUser\_DlBufferMonitoringInd [CB010741-A] PsUser\_BufferMonitoringInd [End CB010741-A], no matter the UE is CA UE or nonCA, even its buffer monitoring for SCell additon may be stopped temporarily by overheating or SCell deactivation or BWP switch as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29593\_replaced([11120432](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqQmxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[CB010741-A]/**[12639255](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_isFx8EC_EfCal_QUJn4xrg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)[End CB010741-A], the access to the UE in the slot opportunity for SCell addition check should be counted as one check.

2). The UE number in the list updates dynamically with UE release and UE setup.

3). The interval between two checks for specific UE shall get longer if the UEs in the list of one UE group more than rdMaxUeNumPerCheckingIndex.

4).  For those UEs in the list with active Scell(s), their Scell activation check shall not be restricted by the UE number defined by rdMaxUeNumPerCheckingIndex.

---

###### 2.4.7.5.2 SCell selection for Activation/Deactivation

###### 2.4.7.5.2.1 SCell activation/deactivation decision based on the checking result in DL and UL (FR2 CA)

###### 2.4.7.5.2.1.0-1 SCell activation/deactivation decision based on the checking result in DL and UL (FR2 CA) Scell activation/deactivation decision (ID: 11159692)

**2.4.7.5.2.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11159718`)

SCell activation/deactivation decision based on the checking result in DL and UL (FR2 CA)
SCell activation/deactivation decision is done in 5G-L2-PS DL, and before the decision is made, 5G-L2-PS shall get the information about the activation/deactivation proposal for both DL (see 5G\_L2\_14217\_replaced([11159743](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25StifvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and UL (5G\_L2\_14216\_replaced([11159722](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25SthvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the proposal include the action and the target cells.

[Before CB012734-B]

The proposal can be made by either radio link quality based check or buffer based check.

There is priority for the different checking results. For buffer based check, the priority from high to low is: activation, no action, deactivation, 0 buffer

When activation method is 'bufferAndRLQualityBased', then Radio link quality based deactivation has higher priority than any other check result. And buffer based check result will be ignored when RLQ deactivation exist at the same check period. (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2742\_replaced([11121361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqX7e_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21698\_replaced([11121774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqaXt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[CB012734-B]

The proposal can be made by either radio link quality based check or buffer based check.

There is priority for the different checking results.

For buffer based check, the priority from high to low is:

* activation,
* no action,
* deactivation,
* 0 buffer

When activation method is 'bufferAndRLQualityBased', then Radio link quality based deactivation has higher priority than any other check result.

And buffer based check result will be ignored when:

* RLQ deactivation exist at the same check period(5G\_UP\_ALG\_2742\_replaced([11121361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqX7e_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21698\_replaced([11121774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqaXt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Or there are SCell(s) to be deactivated due to active UE load for current UE when active UE load periodic checking is enabled(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26306\_replaced([11122135](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqcz__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[End CB012734-B]

---

###### 2.4.7.5.2.1.0-2 SCell activation/deactivation decision based on the checking result in DL and UL (FR2 CA) Uplink activation proposal behavior (ID: 11159722)

**2.4.7.5.2.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11159735`)

SCell activation/deactivation decision based on the checking result in DL and UL (FR2 CA)
There are 2 types of activation methods:

1) “Buffer and RLQ based" is when NRBTS.sCellActivationMethod is set to’ bufferAndRLQualityBased’.

2) “Buffer based” is when NRBTS.sCellActivationMethod is set to 'buffer based’.

The activation proposal behavior for L2 PS UL is described below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Priority | Activation method | Check result | Proposal action for L2 PS UL |
| 1 (highest) | Buffer and RLQ based | RLQ deactivation for any active SCell | L2 PS UL shall give the proposal action RLQ deactivation to L2 PS DL, together with a cell mask which contains all the SCells for which the UL RLQ is bad. And end this procedure. |
| 2 | Buffer based | activate | L2 PS UL shall give the proposal action Activation and also provide the cell mask which contains all the inactive SCells will minimal cell load to L2 PS DL[CB012734-C], if active UE load based SCell deactivation is disabled. L2 PS UL shall give the proposal action Activation and all bits set to 0 in cell mask to L2 PS DL, if active UE load based SCell deactivation is enabled, L2 PS DL will calculate the minimal UL cell load SCell(s) based on beam/carrier matrix maintained in DL. [End CB012734-C] |
| Buffer and RLQ based | L2 PS UL shall give the proposal action Activation to L2 PS DL and provide the cell mask which contains all the inactive SCells which fulfil the UL RLQ activation criteria to L2 PS DL, instead of SCells with least load. |  |  |
| 3 | Buffer and RLQ based | deactivate | L2 PS UL shall give the proposal action Deactivation and provide the cell mask with all the inactive SCells which fulfil the UL RLQ activation criteria together with cell mask for deactivation (all active SCells with maximal cell load). These 2 masks can be merged together [CB012734-C], if active UE load based SCell deactivation is disabled. L2 PS UL shall give the proposal action Deactivation and provide the cell mask with all the inactive SCells which fulfil the UL RLQ activation criteria, L2 PS DL will calculate the maximum UL cell load SCell(s) based on beam/carrier matrix maintained in DL. [End CB012734-C] |
| 0 buffer | L2 PS UL shall send the same information to L2 PS DL as Buffer based deactivation, and shall send extra information to L2 PS DL to indicate 0 buffer. (this info is carried in LSB of the cell mask sent to L2 PS DL. |  |  |
| Buffer based | deactivate | L2 PS UL shall give the proposal action Deactivation to L2 PS DL. together with the cell mask which contain all active SCells with maximal cell load[CB012734-C], if active UE load based SCell deactivation is disabled. L2 PS UL shall give the proposal action Deactivation to L2 PS DL, all bits set to 0 in proposed cell mask to L2 PS DL, if active UE load based SCell deactivation is enabled, L2 PS DL will calculate the maximum UL cell load SCell(s) based on beam/carrier matrix maintained in DL. [End CB012734-C] |  |
| 0 buffer | L2 PS UL shall send the same information to L2 PS DL as Buffer based deactivation, and shall send extra information to L2 PS DL to indicate 0 buffer. (this info is carried in LSB of the cell mask sent to L2 PS DL. |  |  |
| 4 | Buffer and RLQ based | No action | L2 PS UL shall give the proposal action No Action to L2 PS DL, together with a cell mask which contains all the inactive SCells which fulfil the UL RLQ activation criteria. |
| buffer based | L2 PS UL will not send the UL proposal to L2 PS DL, also no proposed mask. |  |  |

[Before CB012734-C]

Note, for DL-only SCells, L2 PS UL shall not do any activation check for them and will not include any DL-only SCell in the cell mask to L2 PS DL.

[CB012734-C]

Notes:

* For DL-only SCells, L2 PS UL shall not do any activation check for them and will not include any DL-only SCell in the cell mask to L2 PS DL.
* For the minimal cell load calculation in activation or maximum cell load calculation in deactivation, if active UE load based SCell deactivation is enabled, it shall be based on active Ue load per beam per carrier.

[End CB012734-C]

---

###### 2.4.7.5.2.1.0-3 SCell activation/deactivation decision based on the checking result in DL and UL (FR2 CA) Downlink activation proposal behavior (ID: 11159743)

**2.4.7.5.2.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11159751`)

SCell activation/deactivation decision based on the checking result in DL and UL (FR2 CA)
The activation proposal behavior for L2 PS DL is described below:

* L2 PS DL shall receive the proposed action and proposed cell mask from L2 PS  UL. (see 5G\_L2\_14216\_replaced([11159722](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25SthvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

o If the UL proposal and cell mask is not received, L2-PS DL shall consider the UL proposal as No Action.

o If activation method is 'bufferAndRLQualityBased', L2-PS DL shall consider UL proposed cell mask is 0, and prevent any SCell activation.

* If any active SCell has the check result RLQ deactivation, L2 PS DL shall get the cell mask which contain all the SCells for which the DL RLQ is bad.
* If there is DL RLQ deactivation or UL proposed action is RLQ deactivation, L2 PS DL shall merge the DL cell mask with UL proposed cell mask with 'bitwise OR' operation (if one branch doesn't have RLQ deactivation, the corresponding cell mask considers as all 0), and makes the final decision as RLQ deactivation with cells in merged cell mask as target cells.

The procedure ends here[Before CB012734-B].[CB012734-B], if any one of below conditions fulfilled:

o Active UE load based SCell deactivation is disabled

o Active UE load based SCell deactivation is enabled, nSCellTobeDeactDueToAuLoad = 0

o Active UE load based SCell deactivation is enabled, nSCellTobeDeactDueToAuLoad <= nSCellDeactDueToRLQ

* If active UE load based SCell deactivation is enabled, nSCellTobeDeactDueToAuLoad > 0:

o If activation method is 'bufferBased', L2-PS DL shall select nSCellTobeDeactDueToAuLoad SCell(s) to deactivate according to maximum DL carrier load in current UE's best beam.

The procedure shall end here.

o If activation method is 'bufferAndRLQualityBased', and nSCellTobeDeactDueToAuLoad > nSCellDeactDueToRLQ, L2-PS DL shall select (nSCellTobeDeactDueToAuLoad - nSCellDeactDueToRLQ) SCell(s) to deactivate according to the maximum DL carrier load in current UE's best beam.

The procedure shall end here. [End CB012734-B]

* If activation method is 'bufferAndRLQualityBased', L2 PS DL shall extract the cell mask for the inactive DL+UL SCells which fulfil the UL RLQ activation criteria from the UL proposed cell mask. And if UL proposed action is buffer based deactivation, [Before CB012734-C]the remaining cells in the UL proposed cell mask are the most loaded active SCells to do buffer based deactivation from UL.[CB012734-C]

o if active UE load based SCell deactivation is disabled, the remaining cells in the UL proposed cell mask are the most loaded active SCells to do buffer based deactivation from UL

o if active UE load based SCell deactivation is enabled, the most loaded UL active SCells are deduced from the beam/carrier matrix maintained in DL

[End CB012734-C]

L2 PS DL shall combine the DL buffer based check result and UL proposed action and cell mask [CB012734-B] together with SCell activation limitation due to active UE load[End CB012734-B], and do the final decision according to 5G\_L2\_14560\_replaced([11159758](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25StjvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB012734-B]

Notes:

* nSCellTobeDeactDueToAuLoad - denotes the number of SCell(s) that shall be deactivated due to active UE load, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27443\_replaced([11120929](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqVfM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* nSCellDeactDueToRLQ - denotes the SCells that shall be deactivated due to DL and UL bad RLQ, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27443\_replaced([11120929](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqVfM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* SCell activation limitation due to active UE load, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27443\_replaced([11120929](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqVfM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB012734-B]

---

###### 2.4.7.5.2.1.0-4 SCell activation/deactivation decision based on the checking result in DL and UL (FR2 CA) SCell activation/deactivation decision for different DL/UL proposal (ID: 11159758)

**2.4.7.5.2.1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11159770`)

SCell activation/deactivation decision based on the checking result in DL and UL (FR2 CA)
According to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21698\_replaced([11121774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqaXt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21703\_replaced([11121800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqaXvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the following table define the decision of final action and the target cell for different DL and UL proposals.

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | DL Action |  |  |  |
| ACT | DEACT | 0BUF | NA |  |  |
| UL  Action | ACT | A or G | A or E | A or F | A |
| DEACT | B | C | C + F | No Action |  |
| 0BUF | B | C | D | No Action |  |
| NA | B | E | F | No Action |  |

Description for the items in table:

A. [CB012734-B]

If active UE load based Scell deactivation is enabled and any one of the following conditions fulfilled, then new SCell activation shall be rejected:

1).  auLoadReductionTimer is running

2).  number of activated SCell(s) >= maxAllowedActiveScells

3).  instant totalActiveUeLoad [PR787445] + activePendingCcNum + 1 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_30184\_replaced([11122182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqdbEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End PR787445]>= scellReductionThreshold

4).  auLoadBasedScellDeactCheck was set to 1

Otherwise, [End CB012734-B]DL shall select a cell with min DL load count in the UL proposed cell mask.

If sCellActivationMethod is ‘bufferAndRLQualityBased’, the selected cell should fulfils RLQ activation criteria in both UL (from UL proposed cell mask) and DL. UL cell load is not considered in this case. If no cell can be found based on RLQ activation criteria for both DL and UL, do the action after 'or' in the table if there is, otherwise no action.

B. [CB012734-B]

If active UE load based Scell deactivation is enabled and any one of the following conditions fulfilled, then new SCell activation shall be rejected:

1).  auLoadReductionTimer is running

2).  number of activated SCell(s) >= maxAllowedActiveScells

3).  instant totalActiveUeLoad [PR787445] + activePendingCcNum + 1 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_30184\_replaced([11122182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqdbEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End PR787445]>= scellReductionThreshold

4).  auLoadBasedScellDeactCheck was set to 1

Otherwise, [End CB012734-B]DL shall select a DL-only cell with min DL load count, if such cell can't be found, select a DL+UL cell with min DL load count. UL cell load is not considered in this case.

If sCellActivationMethod is ‘bufferAndRLQualityBased’, the selected DL+UL cell shall also fulfil RLQ activation criteria in both UL (from UL proposed cell mask) and DL.

C. DL shall select a cell with max DL load count in the UL proposed cell mask for deactivation.

D. {L2 PS DL shall perform additional check (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2740\_replaced([11121344](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqX7efvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and if internal counter for postponing SCell deactivation (deactSkipCount) is less than burst counter value (burstCount), SCell deactivation shall be skipped ("No action" is forced) and if internal counter deactSkipCount is equal to burstCount,} DL shall deactivate all active SCells, including DL-only and DL+UL cells.

E. DL shall deactivate 1 DL only cell with max DL load.

F. DL shall deactivate all active DL-only SCells.

G. DL shall activate a DL-only SCell with min DL load which fulfils the RL Quality activation criteria in DL.

[CB012734-B]

Notes:

* When active UE load based SCell deactivation is enabled, the DL and UL cell load calculation shall be based on activeUeLoadPerBeamPerCarrier rather than instantNumOfActiveCellUE, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21698\_replaced([11121774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqaXt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* When active UE load based SCell deactivation is enabled, the UL proposed cell mask shall calculate from the activeUeLoadPerBeamPerCarrierUL maintained in DL, refer to 5G\_L2\_14216\_replaced([11159722](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25SthvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for different cases.
* For the definition of auLoadReductionTimer, maxAllowedActiveScells, totalActiveUeLoad, scellReductionThreshold, auLoadBasedScellDeactCheck, [PR787445]activePendingCcNum[End PR787445], refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26306\_replaced([11122135](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqcz__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* [End CB012734-B]For all the decision above, if no target cell according to the table can be found, the final decision shall be no action.

---

###### 2.4.7.5.3 Delay for SCell Activation/Deactivation

##### 2.4.7.5.4 Algorithm for special cases

**2.4.7.5.4.0-1**  (ID: `11159785`)

Algorithm for special cases
SCell Activation/Deactivation algorithm modification to avoid UE caused RL failure

In 5G19A testing it was observed that in case SCell deactivation results in PSCell and active SCell beeing noncontiguous in frequency with two inactive SCells between PSCell and SCell, UE experienced an issue in decoding DCI on PSCell and this leads to RLF.

This issue is reported and under investigation by UE vendor.

As a workaround following modifications are introduced within 5G19A System Release:

1. Special setting of R&D parameter rdDataStableForBurstCntResetTime – value 0 is introduced (See 5G\_L2\_1157\_replaced([11184680](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27tIUPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 If R&D parameter rdDataStableForBurstCntResetTime is set to 0, coordination of SCell activation/deactivation and evaluation of the load condition in DL branch is performed according to the algorithm specified before PR484008 modification. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2778\_replaced([11120862](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqU4JfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2709\_replaced([11120941](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqVfNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 If R&D parameter rdDataStableForBurstCntResetTime is set to the value different than 0, coordination of SCell activation/deactivation and evaluation of the load condition in DL branch is performed according to the algorithm specified within PR484008

2. Single SCell Activation/Deactivation for all configured SCells is introduced based on the setting of the new R&D parameter rdOneStepAllScellActDeact (See 5G\_L2\_1159\_replaced([11184330](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27qFDvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 If R&D parameter rdOneStepAllScellActDeact is set to true, all SCells configured in the UE will be activated in one step, upon buffer based evaluation triggers first SCell activation (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2787\_replaced([11121779](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqaXuPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Once all SCells are active and buffer based evaluation triggers SCell deactivation, all SCells are deactivated in one step (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2789\_replaced([11121804](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqaXvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 If R&D parameter rdOneStepAllScellActDeact is set to false, step vise SCell activation/deactivation is performed as defined prior to this modification.

---

##### 2.4.7.5.5 Activation Status and Channel Status Information Handling

**2.4.7.5.5.0-3**  (ID: `11159813`)

Activation Status and Channel Status Information Handling
When the target Cell to be deleted is marked as SCell, then during the Cell deleting procedure, the target cell status will be marked as inactive by cell deletion handling in L2 PS.

There are several steps to processing the Scell deletion

Step 1:  UL PS CA SCell Activation/Deactivation manager handling.

In the UL L2-PS CA SCell Activation/Deactivation manager during finial checking status (the last checking step to decide the Scell should be active or de-active), UL PS mark this Scell is in the status of “Cell outage”, and sent to DL PS SCell Activation/Deactivation manager with the result ul\_decision\_noaction

Step 2:  DL PS CA SCell Activation/Deactivation manager handling.

In the DL L2-PS CA SCell Activation/Deactivation manager during finial checking status (the last checking step to decide the Scell should be active or de-active), DL PS mark this Scell is in the status of “Cell outage”, DL Activation/Deactivation manager should not consider any UL decision and trigger the MAC CE for de-active sending.

When the DL L2-PS decide to de-active the Scell due to Cell deletion, the period resource for CSI resource and period PUCCH resource should NOT remove from the per slot processing queue (because the target resource has already been cleaned in the cell deletion phase).

Step 3:  CA SCell Activation/Deactivation manager processing for the outage Cell.

For the next checking phase, if the target Cell is in the status of outage, the checking will be skipped and this Scell will NEVER come back to active.

For the CA SCell Blind activation mode, the UL PS CA SCell Activation/Deactivation manager is still running for the SCell configured user, but during the checking phase, only check the SCell is active or not. If the SCell is not active, the MAC CE for de-active is still need to be sent for the deleting Scell.

---

###### 2.4.7.5.6 Multiple Scell's activation/deactivation concurrency

###### 2.4.7.5.6.0-1 Multiple Scell's activation/deactivation concurrency SCell activation and deactivation process (ID: 11159825)

**2.4.7.5.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11159830`)

Multiple Scell's activation/deactivation concurrency
Both SCell activation and SCell deactivation process are handled by PCell L2PS, but SCell deactivation is triggered by SCell L2-PS.

For the same CA UE with more than one SCell, different SCells’ activation and deactivation or different SCells’ deactivation and deactivation may occur meanwhile. As only an activation or deactivation can be handled by PCell L2PS each time, a new coming Scell deactivation event will be delayed, and cannot be executed until the previous event finish. Include two main scenarios,

(1) If one Scell is being deactivated, Pcell receive another Scell’s deactivation.

(2) If one Scell is being activated, Pcell receive another Scell’s deactivation.

---

### 2.4.7.6 SCell addition

#### 2.4.7.6.1 SCell UE Context Update for FR1 CA during SCell Addition

##### 2.4.7.6.1.0-1 SCell UE Context Update for FR1 CA during SCell Addition UE Context for CA UE (ID: 11159846)

**2.4.7.6.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11159856`)

SCell UE Context Update for FR1 CA during SCell Addition
[Before 5GC001729-A]In case FDD and FR1 TDD CA [5GC001167-H] or FDD and FDD CA [End 5GC001167-H] feature flag is activated according to 5G\_UP\_1273\_replaced([11357240](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucbmNfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) while SCell 5G-L2-PS is deployed over different L2RT instance as P(S)Cell,[End 5GC001729-A] UE context for CA UE shall be setup over SCell 5G-L2-PS for CA UE SCell FR1 TDD [5GC001167-H] or SCell FDD [End 5GC001167-H] addition.

---

##### 2.4.7.6.1.0-2 SCell UE Context Update for FR1 CA during SCell Addition Add DL+UL SCell (ID: 11159861)

**2.4.7.6.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11159869`)

SCell UE Context Update for FR1 CA during SCell Addition
SCell: Receive msg PsUser\_UserSetupReq. For the case of Scell addition of SCell (refer to 5G\_L2\_140\_replaced([11143950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_233KKPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If caConfigDu=0 in struct CellConfigParam, this indicates a CA UE with DL/UL CA configured in this serving cell as SCell. PS should forward the msg to the UL and store the UE context in the UL.

PCell: Receive msg PsUser\_UserModifyReq. For the case of Scell addition of PCell (refer to 5G\_L2\_5473\_replaced([11144396](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_235mcfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  if the caConfigDu in remoteCellConfigParam is set to 0, this indicates a DL/UL SCell is adding. The received servingCell(PCell) should forward the msg to the UL and store the UE context in the UL.

CP2 requirement:

5G\_UP\_ALG\_2670\_replaced([11120289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqPYmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

5G\_UP\_ALG\_2698\_replaced([11120363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqP_rPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

5G\_UP\_ALG\_22730\_replaced([11123985](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqnMA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating the process of SCell (DL+UL) Addition in a cellular network. The diagram depicts interactions between different network entities, including PCell (Primary Cell) and SCell (Secondary Cell) components like Sgnl (Signaling), DL (Downlink), and UL (Uplink). It also shows CPRT (Control Plane Relay Terminal).
The diagram is divided into two main sections, representing different stages of the SCell addition process.
The top section, titled "UL CA UserModification on Scell," shows a sequence of messages exchanged between entities related to the SCell. It starts with a `PsUSer_UserSetupReq` message, followed by `PsInternal_UserSetupReq` and `PsInternal_UserSetupResp` messages. A yellow annotation box explains that if `ueAddCaus` is "SCellAddition" and `caConfigDu` is 0, it indicates a CA UE with DL/UL CA configured in the serving cell as SCell. The sequence concludes with a `PsUSer_UserSetupResp` message.
The bottom section, titled "UL CA Setup on Pcell," illustrates the setup of UL CA on the PCell. It begins with a `PsInternal_UserModifyReq` message, followed by `PsInternal_UserModifyResp`. Then, another `PsInternal_UserModifyReq` is sent, and a yellow annotation box explains that if the message contains the pair `(numOfRemote ScellToAdd, numOfRemote ScellToRelease)=(1,0)` and `caConfigDu = 0`, it indicates a DL/UL SCell is being added. This is followed by a `PsInternal_UserModifyResp` and finally a `12_ps_cell_UserModifyResp` message.
On the left side of the diagram, there are annotations indicating the purpose of certain steps: "Record remote Scell relationship," "Build remote Pell relationship," "Record remote UL Scell relationship," and "Build remote Scell relationship."
The diagram uses different arrow types to represent message flows, including solid arrows for requests and dashed arrows for responses or acknowledgments. The overall diagram visually represents a complex signaling procedure for adding a Secondary Cell with both Downlink and Uplink capabilities.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2885.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

File "6137 Scell Addition ul+dl(2cc).signalling" stored at:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L1%20and%20L2%20Entity%20Level%20Flows%2F6%20Control%20of%20UEs%2F6%2E1%20User%20Procedures%2F6137%20Scell%20Addition%20ul%2Bdl%282cc%29%2Esignalling&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c&parent=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L1%20and%20L2%20Entity%20Level%20Flows%2F6%20Control%20of%20UEs%2F6%2E1%20User%20Procedures>

#### 2.4.7.6.2 UE Context Update for FR2 CA during SCell Addition

##### 2.4.7.6.2.0-1 UE Context Update for FR2 CA during SCell Addition Add SCell for FR2 SA CA (ID: 11159879)

**2.4.7.6.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11159884`)

UE Context Update for FR2 CA during SCell Addition
SCell Addition procedure is triggered only by PsUser\_UserModifyReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_453\_replaced([10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if all conditions are satisfied:

•  ueAddCause is not present,

•  cellConfigurationForAddition contains configurations of SCell(s) to be added,

•  cellConfigurationForModification contains PCell configuration, which 5G-L2-PS uses to update all parameters needed for SCell addition procedure.

---

#### 2.4.7.6.3 Transient Phase of SCell Addition

##### 2.4.7.6.3.0-1 Transient Phase of SCell Addition PCell DL scheduling via DCI 1_0 during the Transient Phase of SCell Addition (ID: 11159898)

**2.4.7.6.3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11159915`)

Transient Phase of SCell Addition
**FR1:**

For FR1 CA where PCell is a TDD or FDD **one, on PCell side, during the Transient Phase from single serving cell configuration (i.e. non-CA UE) to multiple serving cell configuration (i.e. CA UE), 5G-L2-PS shall use DCI 1\_0 to schedule DL SRB and/or DRB.**

Transient Phase is started upon PsUser\_UserModifyReq reception with all following conditions satisfied:  
- current serving cell is a FR1 TDD one  
- no SCell configured to this UE before PsUser\_UserModifyReq reception  
- *numOfRemoteScellToAdd IE is set to a value > 0*

**[CB013742-A] During this transient phase, L2-PS shall also follow the DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion procedure specified by 5G\_L2\_22365\_replaced([11170588](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3TfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).** [End CB013742-A]

Transient Phase is stopped upon PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq reception **[CB013742-A] or completion of DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion (5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))** [End CB013742-A] .***\****\*

For detail requirement about how to carry out DL scheduling via DCI 1\_0 during this Transient Phase, please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8181\_replaced([11153814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksolSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4629\_replaced([11192250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvjWZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**[CB013742-A], 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** [End CB013742-A].

CP2 requirements:  
- 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2673\_replaced([11120310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqPYnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  
- 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8181\_replaced([11153814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksolSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  
- 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4629\_replaced([11192250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvjWZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))\*\*\*\*

**[CB013742-A]**

* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**[End CB013742-A]**

**[CB013742-A] During this transient phase, L2-PS shall also follow the DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion procedure specified by 5G\_L2\_22365\_replaced([11170588](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3TfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**[End CB013742-A]\*\*\*\*

**FR2 SA:**

For FR2 SA CA, during Transient Phase, 5G-L2-PS shall use DCI1\_0 to schedule DL SRB and/or DRB. For details refer to 5G\_UP\_7826\_replaced([11357617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucdbbfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Transient Phase is started upon PsUser\_UserModifyReq reception with both conditions satisfied:

- at least one SCell configuration is received inside *cellConfigurationForAddition,*

- no SCell is already configured for this UE before reception of PsUser\_UserModifyReq.

Transient Phase is stopped upon reception of PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq.

---

#### 2.4.7.6.4 C-RNTI collision check

##### 2.4.7.6.4.0-1 C-RNTI collision check Check in SCell or ext-SCell (ID: 11159926)

**2.4.7.6.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11159932`)

C-RNTI collision check
[Before CB008140-I-UPD008]

In case of inter-gNB CA, during ext-SCell addition procedure, ext-L2-PS shall check if C-RNTI received in PsUser\_UserSetupReq message is already used in SCell being added for an UE. If it is already used, the ext-L2-PS shall reject the SCell addition procedure and send PsUser\_UserSetupResp with cause IE set to ECause5G\_CRntiCollision, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7252\_replaced([11120260](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqPYkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In this case needForRollback IE should be set to FALSE.

[CB008140-I-UPD008]

In case of intra-gNB CA or inter-gNB CA, during SCell or ext-SCell addition procedure, 5G-L2-PS in (ext-)SCell shall check if C-RNTI received in PsUser\_UserSetupReq message is already used in (ext-)SCell being added for an UE (see 5G\_L2\_12153\_replaced([11170442](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26JpK_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):  
Then:

If C-RNTI is already used:

Then:

5G-L2-PS in (ext-)SCell shall reject the (ext-)SCell addition procedure and send PsUser\_UserSetupResp with cause IE set to ECause5G\_CRntiCollision, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7252\_replaced([11120260](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqPYkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In this case needForRollback IE should be set to FALSE.

Else:

5G-L2-PS in (ext-)SCell shall follow C-RNTI allocation procedure (5G\_L2\_12153\_replaced([11170442](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26JpK_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and continue the (ext-)SCell addition procedure.

[End CB008140-I-UPD008]

Note: For remote L2-PS one PsUser\_UserSetupReq message configures only one SCell at a time, see 5G\_L3\_5252\_replaced([11058173](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BCRP5P_KEe-D3seUwaI0tg)).

---

#### 2.4.7.6.5 DL only SCell Configuration

##### 2.4.7.6.5.0-1 DL only SCell Configuration Configuration check (ID: 11159943)

**2.4.7.6.5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11159960`)

DL only SCell Configuration
[Before CNI-71344] 5G-L2-PS receives for each Scell CaConfigDu parameter. If this parameter is set to 1 (DL\_ONLY), sCellConfigDedicated.uplinkConfig should not be configured, then the L2 PS UL should ensure that for DL ONLY Scell scheduling in UL is not activated.

Note: For FDD + FR1 TDD CA and FDD + FDD CA FR1 CA UE, DL\_ONLY shall be configured for TDD Scell or FDD SCell so 5G-L2-PS will schedule the TDD Scell or FDD SCell with DL only carrier. [CNI-71344] L2-PS shall check CaConfigDu parameter for UL scheduling activation as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2698\_replaced([11120363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqP_rPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* In FR1, for every SCell configuration received in cellConfiguration (in PsUser\_UserSetupReq, see 5G\_L2\_IF\_390\_replaced([10974651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* In FR2, for every SCell configuration received in cellConfigurationForAddition (in PsUser\_UserModifyReq, see 5G\_L2\_IF\_453\_replaced([10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).[End CNI-71344]

---

#### 2.4.7.6.6 SCell Upgrade from DL to DL_UL

##### 2.4.7.6.6.0-1 SCell Upgrade from DL to DL_UL SCell Upgrade Preparation Phase (ID: 13349809)

**2.4.7.6.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13349812`)

SCell Upgrade from DL to DL\_UL
Details of the message exchange involved in SCell upgrade are as in Ref: [13325677](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_DskKsNK3EfCnq6aYkXj8Pw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

1) At PCell, UE's SCell upgrade is detected based on ueModifyCause as given in [13336155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_cBtQMdlzEfCmxLW1M7HMrQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9); CaConfigDu will be set to 1 (DL\_UL) in [10976333](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VF9o_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

2) PsUser\_userModifyReq will be be processed after a delay -

a) to stop DPA and

b) to deactivate the Upgraded SCell (if the SCell being upgraded is active)

Ref: [11120999](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqVfP_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) following [11195870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfxrYfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

The Delay will be : rrmUserModifyDelayTimer = 3 \* rrmSchedulePucchCycle\_msec, where rrmSchedulePucchCycle\_msec sets the length of the DPA scheduling cycle, as defined in [11123971](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqnMAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Waiting 3 scheduling cycles should guarantee that the Scell(s) consume all the already-allocated PUCCH resources and the SCell deactivation would be completed.

3) After the timer expiry and deactivation of to-be-upgraded SCell, P(S)Cell will proceed with userModifyReq handling and uprade the SCell with UL Configurations and update any retained other SCell's configurations from the message.

4) Till the SCell upgrade is not completed other SCell Activations are blocked for the UE.

Rational:   
      a) If we allow other SCell activation, it will result in additional SCells in Active state when the Upgraded SCell is also activated.   
      b) after the Upgrade of the SCell is completed and re-activated – Only, if needed, the Buffer based SCell activation can activate other SCell’s considering the activated upgraded SCell.

5) CPRT will indicate PsUser\_userModifyReq towards SCell to indicate Upgrade

Note:

If the UE is in SAG ON state or Over heat detected state - and an SCell upgrade (from DL to DL\_UL) is being processed for such UE- then only the SCell upgrade steps will be performed (no deactivation or activation)– Activation will be left to SAG/OH feature flow (for all activation methods i.e Blind or buffer based cases)

---

##### 2.4.7.6.6.0-2 SCell Upgrade from DL to DL_UL SCell Upgrade completion Phase (ID: 13350940)

**2.4.7.6.6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13350941`)

SCell Upgrade from DL to DL\_UL
1) Pcell receives PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq (sCellAdditionInd=1), all the updated configuration will be taken into use and the SCell will be treated as DL+UL.

If SCell was deactivated as part of SCell upgrade process then this SCell will be re-activated immediately without waiting for buffer based activation Ref:[11120833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqURHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

2) SCell also receives PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq to indicate completion of Upgrade procedure.

---

### 2.4.7.7 SCell change

#### 2.4.7.7.0-1 SCell change Scell change preparation phase (ID: 11159971)

**2.4.7.7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11159982`)

SCell change
[Before CNI-109641-N]

scell change transient phase occurs on scell change process. Both inter L2RT and intra L2RT are supported scell change.

In scell change process:

Step1: scell change Pre-handling:

When Pcell receive PsUser\_UserModifyReq with size of remoteSCellToAddList greater than 0 and size of remoteScellToRelease greater than 0, Do the step as follow:

if check message content of PsUser\_UserModfyResp unsuccessfully(refer to Error case in 5G\_L2\_153\_replaced([11144383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_235mb_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

reject UserModifyReq with Exception case;

else

(1). buffer PsUser\_UserModifyReq message for later handling;

(2). start DPA delay timer to guard DPA interaction:

[Before CB012528-D]

rrmUserModifyDelayTimer = 2\*rdSchedulePUCCHCycle\_msec

[CB012528-D]

rrmUserModifyDelayTimer = 2\*rrmSchedulePucchCycle\_msec

[End CB012528-D]

since if UE has DPA resource allocated for actived Scell, Scell will have DL scheduler with DCI\_1\_1 during Scell change phase, it will/may cause mismatch with UE. To reduce complexity, the common handling is done for CA configed UE.

Pcell handle PsUser\_UserModifyReq message until this timer expired.

(3). stop subsequent DPA allocation for this UE (if there is actived scell for this UE), but let previous DPA resource to be used during delay timer runing.

Step2: DPA delay timer expiration handling  :

When delay timer expired, it means Scell will stop scheduler since there is no DPA resource.

Pcell could go into transient phase: Pcell schedule only, as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6587\_replaced([11120811](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqURGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

Step3: scell change transient phase

(1). handle scell change PsUser\_UserModifyReq.

(2). send PsUser\_UserModfyResp message.

(3). keep released scells configuration on hold, stop released scells active/deactive estimation,  ignore released scell's period CSI.

(4). cache new Scell's configuration and Pcell's changed configruation.

(5). Pcell can not be DL scheduled when ACK/NACK multiplexing with Pcell's PCsi or Scell's PCsi.

Exception case:

When Pcell check the PsUser\_UserModifyReq failed, send the PsUser\_UserModifyResp with suitable casue. Pcell shall not proceed scell change process.

[CNI-109641-N]

The Scell change procedure is triggered in L2-PS by reception of PsUser\_UserModifyReq by the P(S)Cell, with size of remoteSCellToAddList greater than 0 and size of remoteScellToRelease greater than 0.

Both inter L2RT CA and intra L2RT CA support Scell change.

If the check on PsUser\_UserModifyReq content is not successful (refer to Error case in 5G\_L2\_153\_replaced([11144383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_235mb_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) L2-PS sends PsUser\_UserModifyResp with suitable error cause and the procedure is stopped, otherwise L2-PS proceeds as below.

The interworking with DPA requires that, for this reconfiguration, L2-PS delays the starting of the reconfiguration transient phase till the DL scheduling is restricted to the P(S)Cell only. Why this is required and how this is achieved is described in 5G\_L2\_16303\_replaced([11158861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25NN9_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When the reconfiguration transient phase is started, in addition to what is described in step2 of 5G\_L2\_16303\_replaced([11158861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25NN9_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), L2-PS shall:

(1). handle scell change PsUser\_UserModifyReq.

(2). keep released scells configuration on hold, stop released scells active/deactive estimation,  ignore released scell's period CSI.

(3). cache new Scell's configuration and Pcell's changed configuration.

(4). during the transient phase L2-PS shall avoid multiplexing ACK/NACK bits with PCell/Scell CSI (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4196\_replaced([11174380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End CNI-109641-N]

---

#### 2.4.7.7.0-2 SCell change SCell change complete phase (ID: 11159988)

**2.4.7.7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11159992`)

SCell change
When Pcell receive PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq with sCellChangeInd=1, the Pcell end the transient phase. handle the message normally. add the follow step for scell change process.[CB008015-E]both inter L2RT and intra L2RT are supported scell change[end CB008015-E]

(1). clean up resources of released scells in pcell.

(2). new scell configuration and pcell's changed configuration take into use.

(3). stop scell change transient phase.

Exception case:

When the PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq arrives earlier than the delay timer expired. Pcell shall stop the delay timer. Pcell handle the stored PsUser\_UserModifyReq and then processing PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq in sequence.

---

#### 2.4.7.7.0-3 SCell change Handling for FR1 UL CA (ID: 11159998)

**2.4.7.7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11160001`)

SCell change
When Pcell receive PsUser\_UserModifyReq with remoteScellToAddList not empty and remoteScellToReleaseList not empty(5G\_L2\_IF\_453\_replaced([10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), Scell change procedure will start. That means Scells in remoteScellToAddList will be released, and SCells in remoteScellToReleaseList will be added and can be activated later.

For releasing SCell handling, depending on the type of releasing SCell, two cases as follow.

1. Releasing DL+UL Scell

As only UL 2CC is supported, PCell and SCell will handle in different way.

PCell will stop buffer split and recycle the releasing SCell's buffer for CA UE, and stop power split without power limitation in overlapping slots.

Scell will stop CA UE's UL scheudler in overlapping slots and clear CA UE's UL HARQ re-transmission.

Refer to 5G\_L2\_6912\_replaced([11160050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25VJxfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

1. Releasing DL only Scell and a DL+UL SCell still exist

If the releasing SCell is DL only, PCell need to inform to handle pCSI transient phase and delete releasing SCell's pCSI, because the existing DL+UL SCell may scheduler releasing SCell's pCSI.

PCell will inform the existing DL+UL SCell transient phase after receiving PsUser\_UserModifyReq and PsUser\_RrcReconfCompleteReq.

SCell will handle transient start and stop phase seperately.

Refer to 5G\_L2\_7293\_replaced([11160073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25VJyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

---

### 2.4.7.8 SCell release

#### 2.4.7.8.0-1 SCell release Interworking with SRS based DL BF for intra-band TDD CA (ID: 11160011)

**2.4.7.8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11160017`)

SCell release
Impact for Scell release

---

**2.4.7.8.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11160022`)

SCell release
When SCell release procedure is triggered on PCell’s 5G-L2-PS instance (see 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_4522\_replaced([11182654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BestWfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and SCell is present in srsBfSyncStatusForScells, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall proceed with 5G\_L2\_16753\_replaced([11154964](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_242oofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (see “SCell release” section) for PCell and with 5G\_L2\_16755\_replaced([11154989](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_242opfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SCell.

---

#### 2.4.7.8.0-2 SCell release Delaying of the Reconfiguration Transient phase for a DPA UE (ID: 11160026)

**2.4.7.8.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11160045`)

SCell release
[Before CNI-109641-N]

When P(S)Cell 5G-L2-PS receive Scell release prepareation message (PsUser\_UserModifyReq) for a CA UE, it shall remove this CA UE from subsequent DPA allocation. The active serving cells can continue scheduling for this CA UE for N \* [Before CB012528-D] rdSchedulePUCCHCycle\_msec [CB012528-D] rrmSchedulePucchCycle\_msec [End CB012528-D](maximum value of N is 2) until the allocated PUCCH resources are totally used. The value N depends on the time length between receiving PsUser\_UserModifyReq and DPA starting allocation time, and maximum time length is bigger than 1 but not bigger than 2  [Before CB012528-D] rdSchedulePUCCHCycle\_msec [CB012528-D] rrmSchedulePucchCycle\_msec [End CB012528-D]. The value 2 is used for N, and that is not a absolutely exact value.

[CNI-109641-N]

The Scell release procedure is triggered in L2-PS by reception of PsUser\_UserModifyReq by the P(S)Cell, with size of remoteScellToRelease greater than 0.

The interworking with DPA requires that, for this reconfiguration, L2-PS delays the starting of the reconfiguration transient phase till the DL scheduling is restricted to the Pcell only. Why this is required and how this is achieved is described in 5G\_L2\_16303.

[End CNI-109641-N]

When receiving PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq (ScellReleaseInd = true), P(S)Cell 5G-L2-PS exit Transient Phase for this CA UE. P(S)Cell 5G-L2-PS can re-start DPA allocation for this CA UE if still other active Scell(s) exists.

Notes: only applied for FR1 CA. For DPA suspend and resume, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22730\_replaced([11123985](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqnMA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.7.8.0-3 SCell release Relese DL+UL SCell (ID: 11160050)

**2.4.7.8.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11160067`)

SCell release
For this case of SCell Release (refer to 5G\_L2\_5473), if the releasing SCell is a CA UE’s DL/UL SCell [Before CNI-106089], after PCell receives the msg in step1, delays handling this msg(refer to 5G\_L2\_16303), then:

1) PCell should recycle the unscheduled data buffer splitted to Scell;

2) PCell should stop the buffer split algorithm;

3) PCell sends rrmBufferedDataUlTotalBytes=0 to SCell to stop UL scheduling in SCell UL by msg PsPeerCtrl\_PcellUlBufferSplitInd (5G\_L2\_IF\_2147\_replaced([10980201](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Vg0Zfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

4) PCell sends ifHarqNeedClear=TRUE to SCell by msg PsPeerCtrl\_PcellUlBufferSplitInd, for the scheduling UL harq in SCell, if the feedback is not Ack, the harq's retransmit num should be set to the Max Retransmit Num, the harq will be released by harq process;

5) Scell UL deletes UE context after receiving msg PsUser\_UserDeleteReq.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating the "Scell(DL+UL) Release" process. The diagram shows interactions between different components, including PCell (Primary Cell) and SCell (Secondary Cell) with their respective signaling (Sgnl), downlink (DL), and uplink (UL) interfaces, as well as a Control Plane (CP).
The diagram is divided into two main phases: "Preparation Phase" and "RRC Completion Phase".
**Preparation Phase:**
- The process begins with `PsUser_UserModifyReq` messages exchanged between PCell and SCell.
- A key event is `PsPeerCtrl_PcellUIBufferSplitInd` sent from PCell to SCell, indicating buffer splitting status. This message includes parameters `rrmBufferedDataUITotalBytes=0` and `ifHarqNeedClear=TRUE`.
- Accompanying this are notes: "1.PCell should recycle the buffer splitted to Scell." and "2.PCell should stop the buffer split algorithm."
- Another note indicates: "3.SCell stop scheduling." and "4.SCell set the max retransmit num to the ul Harq when feedback is Nack."
- The preparation phase concludes with `PsUser_UserModifyResp` from SCell to PCell.
**Release SCell RRC:**
- A prominent orange bar signifies the "Release SCell RRC" action.
**RRC Completion Phase:**
- This phase starts with `PsUser_RrcReconfCompleteReq` messages exchanged between PCell and SCell.
**Delete Scell UE context:**
- Following the RRC completion, a sub-process labeled "Delete Scell UE context" is depicted.
- This involves a series of `PsUser_UserStopReq`, `PsUser_UserStopResp`, `PsUser_UserDeleteReq`, and `PsUser_UserDeleteResp` messages exchanged between PCell and SCell.
- A note indicates "delete UL contex." during the `PsUser_UserDeleteReq` message.
The table below summarizes the messages and their interactions:

| Source | Destination | Message | Parameters | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PCell\_PS | SCell\_DL | 1. PsUser\_UserModifyReq | (5g\_L2\_IF\_453) with ScellRelease |  |
| PCell\_DL | SCell\_DL | PsUser\_UserModifyReq |  |  |
| PCell\_DL | SCell\_DL | PsUser\_UserModifyReq |  | 1.PCell should recycle the buffer splitted to Scell.   2.PCell should stop the buffer split algorithm. |
| PCell\_DL | SCell\_DL | 2. PsPeerCtrl\_PcellUIBufferSplitInd | (5g\_L2\_IF\_2147), (rrmBufferedDataUITotalBytes=0, ifHarqNeedClear=TRUE) |  |
| SCell\_DL | PCell\_PS | 3. PsUser\_UserModifyResp | (5g\_L2\_IF\_458) | 3.SCell stop scheduling.   4.SCell set the max retransmit num to the ul Harq when feedback is Nack. |
| PCell\_PS | SCell\_DL | 8. PsUser\_RrcReconfCompleteReq | (5g\_L2\_IF\_2575) |  |
| PCell\_PS | SCell\_DL | PsUser\_RrcReconfCompleteReq |  |  |
| PCell\_PS | SCell\_DL | PsUser\_RrcReconfCompleteReq |  |  |
| SCell\_DL | PCell\_PS | 4. PsUser\_UserStopReq | (5g\_L2\_IF\_2569) |  |
| SCell\_DL | PCell\_PS | PsUser\_UserStopReq |  |  |
| SCell\_DL | PCell\_PS | PsUser\_UserStopReq |  |  |
| SCell\_DL | PCell\_PS | 5. PsUser\_UserStopResp | (5g\_L2\_IF\_2554) |  |
| SCell\_DL | PCell\_PS | 6. PsUser\_UserDeleteReq | (5g\_L2\_IF\_473) |  |
| SCell\_DL | PCell\_PS | PsUser\_UserDeleteReq |  | delete UL contex. |
| SCell\_DL | PCell\_PS | PsUser\_UserDeleteReq |  |  |
| SCell\_DL | PCell\_PS | 7. PsUser\_UserDeleteResp | (5g\_L2\_IF\_474) |  |

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?cid=d410b5bd%2D48a0%2D4541%2Da099%2Dc40c5fbb2f41&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L1%20and%20L2%20Entity%20Level%20Flows%2F6%20Control%20of%20UEs%2F6%2E1%20User%20Procedures%2F6137%20Scell%20release%20ul%2Bdl%282cc%29%2Esignalling&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c&parent=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L1%20and%20L2%20Entity%20Level%20Flows%2F6%20Control%20of%20UEs%2F6%2E1%20User%20Procedures>

Note: For DPA suspend and resume, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22730\_replaced([11123985](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqnMA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CNI-106089] see 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_4524\_replaced([11182660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BestWvvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CNI-106089]

---

#### 2.4.7.8.0-4 SCell release Relese DL Only Scell under UL CA (ID: 11160073)

**2.4.7.8.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11160093`)

SCell release
In case of CA UE with UL/DL SCell and DLOnly Scell, if the DLOnly SCell is to be released, the release flow refer to [Before CNI-106089] 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1141\_replaced([11182255](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Beq4IPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the CSI of the releasing SCell will be in transient state.

For L2 PS, in Preparation Phase, PCell PS receives the PsUser\_UserModify msg with (size of remoteSCellToAddList, size of remoteScellToRelease)=(0,1) and the releasing SCell is a DLOnly SCell, PCell shall send the CSI of the releasing SCell with (cause=Release and status=TransientStart) to the UL/DL SCell by msg psPeerCtrl\_PcellCsiStatusInd(5G\_L2\_IF\_2339\_replaced([10980559](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VjQqvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The UL/DL SCell receiving the msg and mark the received CSI to Transient state, which will lead the SCell UL can't be scheduled on the slot of these CSI. In complete phase, PCell receive the msg PsUser\_RrcReconfCompleteReq(5G\_L2\_IF\_542\_replaced([10977666](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VN5fPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), PCell should send the CSI of the releasing SCell with (cause=Release and status=TransientStop) to the UL/DL SCell by msg PsPeerCtrl\_PcellCsiStatusInd. The UL/DL SCell receiving the msg and clear these CSI.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating the "Scell(DLOnly) Release" process. The diagram depicts interactions between different components, including PCell (Primary Cell) and SCell (Secondary Cell) with various logical units like Sgnl (Signal), DL (Downlink), UL (Uplink), and DL\_Only. There are also components labeled "CP" (Control Plane).
The diagram is divided into three main phases: "Preparation Phase", a middle section without a specific phase label, and "Completion Phase".
**Preparation Phase:**
- `PsUser_UserModifyReq` is sent from PCell to SCell1.
- `paPeerCtrl_PcellCsiStatusInd` with status `(Release+TransientStart)` is sent from PCell to SCell1.
- `PsUser_UserModifyResp` is sent from SCell1 to PCell.
**Middle Section (DLOnly Scell Release):**
- `PsUser_UserStopReq` is sent from PCell to SCell1 and SCell2.
- `PsUser_UserStopResp` is sent from SCell1 to PCell.
- `PsUser_UserDeleteReq` is sent from PCell to SCell1 and SCell2.
- `PsUser_UserDeleteResp` is sent from SCell2 to PCell.
- A highlighted bar indicates "Release SCell RRC".
**Completion Phase:**
- `PsUser_RrcReconfCompleteReq` is sent from PCell to SCell1.
- `psPeerCtrl_PcellCsiStatusInd` with status `(Release+TransientStop)` is sent from PCell to SCell1.
- `PsUser_RrcReconfCompleteReq` is sent from PCell to SCell1.
There are also two text boxes on the left side of the diagram:
- "Msg PcellCsiStatusInd with cause-Release and status-TransientStart. Contain pcSI to add remove change."
- "Msg PcellCsiStatusInd with cause-Release and status-TransientStop. Contain pcSI to add remove change."
The table below represents the interactions shown in the diagram:

| Source | Destination | Message | Phase |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PCell | SCell1 | PsUser\_UserModifyReq | Preparation Phase |
| PCell | SCell1 | paPeerCtrl\_PcellCsiStatusInd (Release+TransientStart) | Preparation Phase |
| SCell1 | PCell | PsUser\_UserModifyResp(5G\_L2\_IF\_447) | Preparation Phase |
| PCell | SCell1 | PsUser\_UserStopReq | Middle Section |
| PCell | SCell2 | PsUser\_UserStopReq | Middle Section |
| SCell1 | PCell | PsUser\_UserStopResp | Middle Section |
| PCell | SCell1 | PsUser\_UserDeleteReq | Middle Section |
| PCell | SCell2 | PsUser\_UserDeleteReq | Middle Section |
| SCell2 | PCell | PsUser\_UserDeleteResp | Middle Section |
| PCell | SCell1 | PsUser\_RrcReconfCompleteReq | Completion Phase |
| PCell | SCell1 | psPeerCtrl\_PcellCsiStatusInd (Release+TransientStop) | Completion Phase |
| PCell | SCell1 | PsUser\_RrcReconfCompleteReq | Completion Phase |

File "6137 Scell release dlOnly (3cc).signalling" stored at:

### 2.4.7.9 HARQ bits calculation and fake HARQ bit filter for DPA UE

#### 2.4.7.9.0-1 HARQ bits calculation and fake HARQ bit filter for DPA UE HARQ bits calculation and fake HARQ bit filter algorithm (ID: 11160104)

**2.4.7.9.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11160451`)

HARQ bits calculation and fake HARQ bit filter for DPA UE
For FR1 TDD CA where DPA is always enabled, P(S)Cell 5G-L2-PS shall calculate the [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "O ACK" in a stylized font. The letters are colorful, with a gradient effect. The "O" is on the left, followed by "ACK". The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2927.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] for the expected HARQ bits and [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, abstract representation of the letters "Vs" in a gradient of colors, primarily blues, oranges, and yellows. The letters are stylized and appear to be in a bold, sans-serif font. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2927.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]for filtering fake HARQ feedback per HARQ bundling window according to the following procedure, the input are V\_C\_DAI , V\_T\_DAI(5G\_L2\_601) and HARQ bundling windows size, for more detailed, pls refer to 3GPP 38.213 Chapter 9.1.3

Implementation note – informative: [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "O ACK" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of blue and yellow, and they appear to be outlined in black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2927.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] and [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up shot of the letter "V" with a subscript "s" below it. The letters are rendered in a gradient of colors, with shades of orange, yellow, blue, and purple. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2927.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]calculation could be triggered multiple times on gNB side with following pseudocode, to achieve a consistent calculation result no matter how many times the calculation is triggered, following rules must be followed:

- For each round of calculation, calculation should take the scheduling of all serving cells (c) upon all monitoring occasions (m) into consideration

- Last Calculation is defined as the round of calculation with such a scheduling instance involved: the scheduling instance relates to (m, c) pair with largest value of (m\*N + c), where N is the total number of serving cells configured to current UE [image-4][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_2927.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4].

- Only the calculation result of Last Calculation is taken into usage for later processing, and the calculation result of all other calculation round (if existed) is ignored.
