##### 2.4.7.12.6.0-4 UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA) efficiencyPerPRB calculation (ID: 11161689)

**2.4.7.12.6.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11161695`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
efficiencyPerPRB is an intermediate variable used for calculating splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep during overpower state. efficiencyPerPRB is calculated by tbsPerPRB / nonPowerLimitedTxPowePerPRB as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10875\_replaced([11119847](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For tbsPerPRB=TBS / maxPRBonCarrier, some input parameter are described as follow.

1. The value of maxPRBOnCarrier equlas to ulBandwidth (refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) minus PUCCH's PRB.
2. correctedMCS = wideband MCS + adjustedMCS, and correctedMCS is calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5016\_replaced([11201086](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwULYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with rrmNumPuschPrb set to Max (maxPrbBasedOnSplitTxPower, 1). The rrmPhrScaled in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5016\_replaced([11201086](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwULYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) indicates the degree of full bandwidth power limitation. The value of correctedMCS is 0 unless rrmPhrScaled value is smaller than 0.

As efficiencyPerPRB is just used for calculating splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep, so the scenarios and times that trigger calculation of efficiencyPerPRB are the same as splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep during overpower state.

Notes:

1. Both Pcell and Scell need to calculate efficiencyPerPRB itself. Scell will sent its calculation result in psPeerCtrl\_ScellEfficiencyReportInd (refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_2926\_replaced([10980930](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VmT-vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to Pcell.
2. Scell's efficiencyPerPRB is not multiplied by NIDD weightRatioOfUePwrScellVsPcell on Pcell, in order to reduce the interaction between Pcell and Scell.

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1918\_replaced([11201901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgUeCPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

##### 2.4.7.12.6.0-5 UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA) splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep calculation (ID: 11161699)

**2.4.7.12.6.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11161706`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
the splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep calculation is triggered in below scenario

1. overpower state transform in the overpower check scenario, including non-overpower to overpower, overpower to non-overpower
2. PHR received in pcell or forward from scell in overpower state

In the overpower to non-overpower scenario, there is no need to request efficiencryRequest of scell, as the pcell/scell splitTxPowerAdjstmentStep equals to NIDD step ueSplitPowerAdjStep directly.

In other scenarios except overpower to non-overpower, pcell will sent efficiencyRequest with PsPeerCtrl\_PcellUlPhrForwardind to request scell efficiencyPerPrb, and scell response with PsPeerCtrl\_ScellEfficiencyReport. Once pcell receive the efficiencyPerPrb from scell, the cacluation of pcell efficiencyPerPRB and pcell/scell splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep will be triggered.

Notes:

1. the servingCell j0 with higher efficiencyPerPRB(5G\_L2\_8663\_replaced([11161689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25b3dPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) will treat splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep(i, j0, t) equals to NIDD parameter ueSplitPowerAdjStep, and the servingCell j1 with lower efficiencyPerPRB (5G\_L2\_8663\_replaced([11161689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25b3dPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value will calculate a new step splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep(i, j1, t) as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10877\_replaced([11119861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep(i, j0, t), splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep(i, j1, t) is only caculated on Pcell, and Pcell will send the result with psPeerCtrl\_PcellUlPowerCtrlInd(refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_2926\_replaced([10980930](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VmT-vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to Scell.
3. When comparing efficiencyPerPRB value of Pcell and Scell, Scell's efficiencyPerPRB should be multiplied by weightRatioOfUePwrScellVsPcell, as notes description in 5G\_L2\_8663\_replaced([11161689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25b3dPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
4. the rdMinSplitTxPowerAdjStep is always smaller than ueSplitPowerAdjStep, which make sure the efficiencyPerPRB take effect in the lower efficiencyPerPRB cell's splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep calculation in over power state.

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1918\_replaced([11201901](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgUeCPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

##### 2.4.7.12.6.0-6 UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA) The maximum PRBs based on the split TX power (ID: 11161711)

**2.4.7.12.6.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11161716`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
For a UE configured with FR1 UL CA, based on the split TX power, the maximum PRBs for Ul transmission with the required TX power is calculated as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10883\_replaced([11119896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower is changed in below scenario:

1. splitTxPower change
2. PHR report

[CB006137-BC]

1. TPC change when closed loop power control is enabled.

[End CB006137-BC]

For the implementation simplicity purpose, maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower is calculated at the time to be used. e.g.  used for calculating requireMorePower in Pcell and Sell, refer to 5G\_L2\_8741\_replaced([11161801](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ceivvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Notes: maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower is calculated independently in Pcell and Scell.

---

##### 2.4.7.12.6.0-7 UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA) initialSplitTxPower for Pcell and Scell (ID: 11161721)

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11161727`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Overview scenarios for intial power split

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11161737`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
According to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10879\_replaced([11119799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for a UE configured with FR1 Ul CA [CB006137-C]and dynamic power split enabled (refer to 5G\_L2\_9564\_replaced([11161442](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25bQZfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB006137-C], split TX power shall be initialized for Pcell and Scell in below cases:

·    Case1: First time UE transmission buffer split is started after Scell activation;

·    Case2: UE transmission buffer split is started after rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower milliseconds since UE transmission buffer split is stopped specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10919\_replaced([11119940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

·    Case3: First time UE transmission buffer split is started after Ul scheduling is resumed on Scell in Ul overlapping slots specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

·    Case4: Ul scheduling is resumed for a UE configured with FDD + FR1 TDD UL CA on Pcell in Ul overlapping slots specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

·    Case5: Ul Scell is recovered from overheating status specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2874\_replaced([11123313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqiThPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

Message flow refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1829\_replaced([11202015](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgVsE_vREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11161742`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
case1 start 1st buffer split after UL Scell activation

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11161755`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
case1 scenario:

according to Trigger-2 of Transmission Buffer Split 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10281\_replaced([11122608](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqf3TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), transmission buffer split shall not be started before PHR report based on a reference PUSCH transmission for SCell is received.

1. the pcell splitTxPower should set to min([image-0][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3102.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0],[image-1][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible objects, text, or features.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3102.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]), scell splitTxPower set to 0 in the scell addition phase, for the

purpose that no overpower check and no power adjustment before case1 power split of scell activation.

1. for the time FR1\_ACTIVATION\_ACK is received in pcell ul sent from pcell dl, L2-PS should check as 5G\_L2\_9234\_replaced([11161041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25Y0JvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. for the time BSR is received from PCell UL scheduler via LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd 5G\_L2\_IF\_875\_replaced([10984687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2WBxzvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUlInfoUpdateInd 5G\_L2\_IF\_2159\_replaced([10980370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2ViChPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), L2-PS

should check as 5G\_L2\_9237\_replaced([11161070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25Y0K_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1811\_replaced([11202026](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgVsFfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `11161760`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
case2 buffer split restart after rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11161763`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
case2 scenario:

After rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout, set reInitialPowerSplitForBufferStop  = true. For the time BSR is received from PCell UL scheduler via LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd 5G\_L2\_IF\_875\_replaced([10984687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2WBxzvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUlInfoUpdateInd 5G\_L2\_IF\_2159\_replaced([10980370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2ViChPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [CB006137-BB] or when PCell Ul receives the Ul RLQ resume status from SCell Ul via PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUlControlInd refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_685\_replaced([10979873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VeYJfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB006137-BB], L2-PS should check as 5G\_L2\_9237\_replaced([11161070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25Y0K_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Notes:

1. when buffer split stopped, whether Pcell start rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timer, depend on if Scell UL retransmission is possible, it is defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10919\_replaced([11119940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. As scell suspend in CB006137 will start rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower, Scell resume will also go to this case2 initialSplitTxPower scenario.

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-4**  (ID: `11161769`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
case3 buffer split restarted after Ul power resumed on Scell Ul overlapping slots

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11161774`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
case3 scenario:

case 3 is not used in this F+T UL CA, only appllied in the case that Scell has both UL overlapping and non Overlapping slot, when Scell is suspened in UL overlapping slot, non overlapping slot is still schedulable, which means buffer split should not be stopped. For F+T UL CA, when Scell is suspened, buffer split will be stopped since Scell has only UL overlapping slots. According to the description that "Split TX power shall be initialized based on case2 on Pcell and Scell, if UE transmission buffer split is started after rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower milliseconds since UE transmission buffer split is stopped, regardless of case3 or case4.", case2 initialSplitTxPower caculation will be chosen in this F+T UL CA scell resume scenario.

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-5**  (ID: `11161780`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
case4 Ul Power resumed on Pcell UL overlapping slots for a UE configured with FDD + FR1 TDD UL CA

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11161785`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
case4 scenario:

Scell UL check as below:

After pcell resume check pass with rdNumForMinSplitTxPowerCheck in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

the case4 initialSplitTxPower should be triggered directly.

Note:

pcell resume check refer to 5G\_L2\_9230\_replaced([11162010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25eTsPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-6**  (ID: `11161790`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
case5 overheating resume

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-7.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11161795`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
case5 scenario:

After PCell Ul received the overheating resume status from PCell Dl via DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate, which is reported from SCell Dl via PsPeerCtrl\_ScellStatusInd, check if send buffer split conditions can fulfill:

 no:  PCell Ul don't send PsPeerCtrl\_PcellUlBufferSplitInd to SCell Ul with rrmBufferedDataUlTotalBytes=0, but wait for next PCell BSR to trigger buffer split and power split, and set reInitSplitTxPowerForOHResume=true.

 yes:  PCell Ul start powerSplit and bufferSplit(splitRatio for SCell is 0.1, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9591\_replaced([11122888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqhFYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and send PsPeerCtrl\_PcellUlBufferSplitInd with rrmBufferedDataUlTotalBytes>0, and initialSplitTxPower could be calculated according to the formula for case5 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10879\_replaced([11119799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

##### 2.4.7.12.6.0-8 UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA) UE Over Power Status Check (ID: 11161801)

**2.4.7.12.6.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11161806`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
requireMorePower status in Pcell

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-8.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11161818`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
ue requireMorePower in Pcell

below are the conditions should be checked for the pcell requireMorePower in ul scheduling slot:

[Before CB006137-C]

1. Pcell powerState !=suspend (there is no ul scheduling in overlap slot if pcell suspend)
2. Scell powerState !=suspend or (scell powerstate ==suspend and powerSplitTimerHandle != invalid) (require more power if scell suspend before the rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout)
3. PrbTobeSchedule > maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower for new tx  in ul overlap slot
4. maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower < minPrb for new tx in ul overlap slot
5. Prb >maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower for new tx ul non-overlap slot in pcell

[CB006137-C]

1. UE is in power split mode refer to 5G\_L2\_9563\_replaced([11161436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25bQZPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower < minPrb for new tx in ul overlap slot
3. PrbTobeSchedule > maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower for new tx in ul overlap slot and PCell's splitTxPower is less than PCell's maximum UL transmission power considering MPR and DeltaMPR256QAM, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
4. Prb >maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower for new tx ul non-overlap slot in pcell and PCell's splitTxPower is less than PCell's maximum UL transmission power considering MPR and DeltaMPR256QAM, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Where:

* MPR and DeltaMPR256QAM refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11120\_replaced([11130873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krA0p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

[End CB006137-C]

and the logic should be handled as

[Before CB006137-C]

if((1 and 2 and (3 or 4))  or 5 )

[CB006137-C]

if(1 and (2 or 3 or 4))

[End CB006137-C]

requireMorePower = true

Note:

1. PrbTobeSchedule refer to case2 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. minPrb refer to case1 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
3. maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower calculation refers to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10883\_replaced([11119896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
4. Prb in ul non-overlap slot is not limited by maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower, as there is no power split in non-overlap slot.

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11161826`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)

---

4. maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower < minPrb for new tx in ul overlap slot

2. PrbScheduled > maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower for new tx  in ul overlap slot and SCell's splitTxPower is less than SCell's maximum UL transmission power considering MPR and DeltaMPR256QAM, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Where:

* MPR and DeltaMPR256QAM refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11120\_replaced([11130873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krA0p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

[End CB006137-C]

and the logic should be handled as

[Before CB006137-C]

If ((1 and 2) and (3 or 4)

[CB006137-C]

If (1 and 2)

[End CB006137-C]

requireMorePower = true

Note:

1. PrbScheduled refer to case1 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. minPrb refer to case1 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
3. maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower calculation refers to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10883\_replaced([11119896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
4. Scell sync Scell requireMorePower status to Pcell by PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUlHousekeepingReportInd->ScellIncreasingPower

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11161844`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Pcell Determine UE Over Power based on pcell&scell requireMorePower status

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-8.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11161855`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
For a UE configured with FR1 Ul CA, L2PS on Pcell evaluates whether the UE is in over power state periodically when [Before CB006137-C]UE TX power is split on Pcell and Scell in Ul overlapping slots[CB006137-C]UE is in power split mode (refer to 5G\_L2\_9563\_replaced([11161436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25bQZPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB006137-C]. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10903\_replaced([11119931](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuP_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Pcell run over power checking with below steps:

1. requireMorePower status is updated per Pcell(5G\_L2\_9196\_replaced([11161806](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25cei_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and Scell (5G\_L2\_9198\_replaced([11161826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25cejvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), requireMorePower for Scell is from PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUlHousekeepingReportInd->ScellIncreasingPower. if ScellIncreasingPower exist in PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUlHousekeepingReportInd, then requireMorePower for Scell is true for this slot.
2. once set to true  in the Pcell ul scheduling within the rdPeriodForOverPowerEva period, the final requireMorePower for Pcell should be true in this rdPeriodForOverPowerEva period, which is denoted as Pcell requireMorePower |= true.
3. this is the same for scell as pcell in step2.
4. when the rdPeriodForOverPowerEva check point reached, do the over power check based on the requireMorePower value from step2

if overpower state == non-overpower

if ((Pcell requireMorePower == true) and (Scell requireMorePower == true))

overpower state == overpower

else

if ((Pcell requireMorePower == false) or (Scell requireMorePower ==false))

checkCounter++

else

checkCounter = 0

if (checkCounter >= rdNumForNonOverPowerCheck)

overpower state == non-overpower

1. after the over power check, the final requireMorePower for Pcell and Scell should be reset to false to start a new journey of the next rdPeriodForOverPowerEva

Note:

1. An evaluation period (rdPeriodForOverPowerEva) for the UE is defined by rdPeriodForOverPowerEva, refers to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11993\_replaced([11120016](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqM8U_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. Checking whether UE need more power than the split TX power in Ul overlapping slots refers to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
3. Defines the number of evaluation periods to check whether an FR1 Ul CA UE is in non over power state or not is defined by rdNumForNonOverPowerCheck, refers to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10944\_replaced([11120023](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqM8VfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
4. the start sfn/slot of over power check point of ue  is depended on where the ue added to the ue distribution table,  and the check point repeat each rdPeriodForOverPowerEva interval

---

##### 2.4.7.12.6.0-9 UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA) Adjust split TX power for UL Overlapping Slot Scheduling (ID: 11161861)

**2.4.7.12.6.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11161865`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Power increase from Pcell scenario

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-9.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11161879`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
For a UE configured with FR1 UL CA, if one Ul scheduling on any cell j in an Ul overlapping slot requires more power than the given split power, then the split TX power is adjusted, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10881\_replaced([11119868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

below are the conditions should be checked before the split TX power adjustment:

[Before CB006137-C]

1. Pcell powerState !=suspend (there is no ul scheduling in overlap slot if pcell suspend)
2. Scell powerState !=suspend or (scell powerstate ==suspend and powerSplitTimerHandle != invalid) (no need to adjust power if scell suspend before the rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout)
3. PrbTobeSchedule > maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower for new tx  in ul overlap slot
4. maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower < minPrb for new tx in ul overlap slot
5. pcellSplitTxPower + pcellAdjStep <= min(Pcmax - rdUePowerReserved - rdMinSplitTxPower, servingcellPcmax)
6. rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower is invalid

[CB006137-C]

1. UE is in power split mode, refer to 5G\_L2\_9563\_replaced([11161436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25bQZPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. PrbTobeSchedule > maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower for new tx  in ul overlap slot
3. maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower < minPrb for new tx in ul overlap slot
4. pcellSplitTxPower + pcellAdjStep <= min(Pcmax - rdUePowerReserved - rdMinSplitTxPower, servingcellPcmax)
5. rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower is invalid

[End CB006137-C]

and the logic should be handled as

[Before CB006137-C]

if(1 and 2 and (3 or 4)  and 5 and 6)

[CB006137-C]

if(1 and (2 or 3)  and 4 and 5)

[End CB006137-C]

pcellSplitTxPower += pcellAdjStep

Note:

1. PrbTobeSchedule refer to case2 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. minPrb refer to case1 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
3. Pcmax refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10877\_replaced([11119861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
4. servingcellPcmax refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10873\_replaced([11119621](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
5. pcellAdjStep refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10877\_replaced([11119861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
6. powerSplitTimerHandle is valid when rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower is running

After receive PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUlHousekeepingReportInd->ScellIncreasingPower, do the following:

if(ScellIncreasingPower > 0)

pcellSplitTxPower -= ScellIncreasingPower

Scell requireMorePower |= true

else

Scell requireMorePower |= true

Note:

Scell requireMorePower refer to 5G\_L2\_9199\_replaced([11161844](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25dFkvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1836\_replaced([11202366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgXhSfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11161886`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Power increase from Scell scenario

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-9.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11161897`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
For a UE configured with FR1 UL CA, if one Ul scheduling on any cell j in an Ul overlapping slot requires more power than the given split power, then the split TX power is adjusted, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10881\_replaced([11119868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

below are the conditions should be checked before the split TX power adjustment:

[Before CB006137-C]

1. scell powerState != suspend (there is no ul scheduling in overlap slot if Scell suspend)
2. pcell powerState !=suspend (no need to adjust power if pcell suspend,Scell has max power)
3. PrbTobeSchedule > maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower for new tx  in ul overlap slot
4. maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower< minPrb for new tx in ul overlap slot
5. scellSplitTxPower + scellAdjStep <= min(Pcmax - rdUePowerReserved - rdMinSplitTxPower, servingcellPcmax)

[CB006137-C]

1. UE is in power split mode, refer to 5G\_L2\_9563\_replaced([11161436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25bQZPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. PrbScheduled > maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower for new tx  in ul overlap slot
3. scellSplitTxPower + scellAdjStep <= min(Pcmax - rdUePowerReserved - rdMinSplitTxPower, servingcellPcmax)

[End CB006137-C]

and the logic should be handled as

[Before CB006137-C]

if(1 and 2 and (3 or 4)  and 5)

[CB006137-C]

if(1 and 2 and 3)

[End CB006137-C]

scellSplitTxPower += scellAdjStep

Note:

[Before CB006137-C]

1. PrbTobeSchedule refer to case2 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB006137-C]

1. PrbScheduled refer to case1 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB006137-C]

1. minPrb refer to case1 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10890\_replaced([11119904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. Pcmax refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10877\_replaced([11119861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
3. servingcellPcmax refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10873\_replaced([11119621](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

After receive psPeerCtrl\_PcellUlPowerCtrlInd->pcellIncreasingPower, do the following:

if(pcell powerstate !=suspend)

scellSplitTxPower -= pcellIncreasingPower

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1836\_replaced([11202366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgXhSfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

##### 2.4.7.12.6.0-10 UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA) FULL TX power to Pcell while UE transmission buffer split stopped (ID: 11161904)

**2.4.7.12.6.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11161910`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
General concept for Pcell with full power(max power)

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-10.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11161916`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
For a UE configured with FR1 UL CA, while UE transmission buffer split is stopped, UE TX power is adjusted on Pcell in Ul overlapping, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10919\_replaced([11119940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1.Timer rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower is reserved for Scell UL re-transimission with existed split power, Pcell kept existed split power during timer is running.

1. After the timer rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower expiration or if no timer is needed:

pcellSplitTxPower be set to min(Pcmax,servingcellPcmax);

pcell shoule be resumed in UL overlapping slot if Pcell status is suspend.

Note:

1. For different bufferSplit = 0 cases, rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower is needed or not and how Pcell power is adjusted, refer to 5G\_L2\_9224\_replaced([11161921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25dsoPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_9222\_replaced([11161934](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25dsovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_9220\_replaced([11161946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25dspPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1841\_replaced([11202516](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgYvYPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-10.0-2**  (ID: `11161921`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Adjust Pcell power when bufferSplit = 0 triggered by BSR

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-10.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11161928`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
bufferSplit = 0 triggered by BSR scenario

below scenario can happen:

1. bufferSplit =0 triggered by BSR, Start rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower if the timer does not exist. When the rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower is running, existed Pcell splitTxPower should be used and power adjustment should be stopped
2. bufferSplit != 0 triggered by BSR before rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout, stop the timer, existed Pcell/Scell splitTxPower should be used and power adjustment  should be started again
3. after rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout, set Pcell splitTxPower = servingcellPcmax
4. bufferSplit != 0 triggered by BSR after rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout, the case2 initialSplitTxPower shoulde be triggered

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1841\_replaced([11202516](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgYvYPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-10.0-3**  (ID: `11161934`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Adjust Pcell power when bufferSplit = 0 triggered by housekeeping or SCell UL RLQ suspend

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-10.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11161941`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
bufferSplit = 0 triggered by housekeeping scenario [CB006137-BB]or SCell UL RLQ suspend[End CB006137-BB]

below scenario can happen:

1. bufferSplit =0 triggered by housekeeping [CB006137-BB] or SCell UL RLQ suspend [End CB006137-BB], Start rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower if the timer does not exist. When the rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower is running, existed Pcell splitTxPower should be used and power adjustment should be stopped
2. bufferSplit != 0 triggered by housekeeping [CB006137-BB] or SCell UL RLQ resume [End CB006137-BB] before rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout, Stop rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower, existed Pcell/Scell splitTxPower should be used and power adjustment  should be started again
3. after rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout, set Pcell splitTxPower = servingcellPcmax
4. bufferSplit != 0 triggered by housekeeping [CB006137-BB] or SCell UL RLQ resume [End CB006137-BB] after rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout, the case2 initialSplitTxPower shoulde be triggered

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1841\_replaced([11202516](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgYvYPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-10.0-4**  (ID: `11161946`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Adjust Pcell power for Speical bufferSplit =0 cases

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-10.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11161951`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
In 5G\_L2\_7784\_replaced([11161228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25aCSfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), Speical Buffer Split Cases with Zero Buffer for procedure control, several cases are defined.

Once conditions/procedures are triggered for such cases, Pcell should:

1.Stop UL Buffer split as normal bufferSplit = 0 scenario(a.k.a based on BSR and housekeeping).

2.Start timer rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower except for case2 and case4 for which ifHarqNeedClear = true in 5G\_L2\_7784\_replaced([11161228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25aCSfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Existed pcell splitTxPower be used before rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout, set Pcell splitTxPower = servingcellPcmax after rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout.

3.For case2 and case4 with ifHarqNeedClear = true in 5G\_L2\_7784\_replaced([11161228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25aCSfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), there are no need to start rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower for the purpose of possible scell retransmission, and set Pcell splitTxPower = servingcellPcmax immediately.

4.Pcell should restart buffer split (5G\_L2\_9232\_replaced([11161035](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25Y0JfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))/power split(5G\_L2\_7106\_replaced([11161721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25b3evvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) after procedure(in 5G\_L2\_7784\_replaced([11161228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25aCSfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is completed.

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1841\_replaced([11202516](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgYvYPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

##### 2.4.7.12.6.0-11 UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA) Resume or Suspend Scell in UL Overlapping slot (ID: 11161959)

**2.4.7.12.6.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `11161963`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Scell suspend

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11161969`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Suspend Ul scheduling in overlapping slots on Scell due to too low split TX power, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10921\_replaced([11119951](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Scell suspend scenario

below are the steps should be checked by Pcell before suspension as condition1 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10921\_replaced([11119951](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

1. Pcell requires to increase power which lead to exceed max allowed power(not enough power remained to Scell);
2. efficiency ratio less then rdEffiThreshForUlScheSuspension (scell efficiency is not good enough);
3. overpower

the logic should be handled as :

if(1 and 2 and3)

set scell powerState = suspend

Start rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower if not start

send bufferSplit = 0 to scell

Note:

1. for the pcell requiring to increase power, refer to 5G\_L2\_8627\_replaced([11161861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25dFlfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. for the implementation simplicity, use splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep(i, pcell, t) to replace efficiencyPerPRB(i, pcell, t),  splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep(i, scell, t) to replace efficiencyPerPRB(i, scell, t) in step 2.
3. In the case efficiencyPerPRB(i, scell, t) > efficiencyPerPRB(i, pcell, t), and the differential between rdMinSplitTxPowerAdjStep and ueSplitPowerAdjStep is not large enough(ueSplitPowerAdjStep /rdMinSplitTxPowerAdjStep < efficiencyPerPRB(i, scell, t)/efficiencyPerPRB(i,pcell, t)). It may result in that step 2 satisfied with the efficiencyPerPRB replaced by splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep but not satified if directly using efficiencyPerPRB. if this happen, it's treated scell suspend as step satisfied.

[CB006137-BB]

Note:

If SCell suspend due to no split tx power, and buffer split to SCell Ul is also stopped, then the SCell Ul inactivity timer will start, this may lead to SCell deactivated due to both SCell Dl and Ul inactivity timer expired.

In such case, PCell Ul should start a prohibit timer to prevent frequent ping-pong in the cycle as:

uplink buffer request --> split TX power is below the minimum split TX power on SCell --> SCell deactivation due to inactivity timer expiration --> SCell activation due to uplink buffer request again

Only the prohibit timer expired if this timer has started, PCell Ul could start to evaluate the Ul buffer status.

The prohibit timer is specified by the R&D parameter rdProhibitBufEvalULFR1, refer to see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12693\_replaced([11124165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqoaKPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB006137-BB]

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1850\_replaced([11202840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgZ9lPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-11.0-2**  (ID: `11161975`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Scell resume

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-11.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11161988`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Scell resume scenario

Resume Scell Ul scheduling in overlapping slots on Pcell where the scheduling is suspended due to too low split TX power, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Scell resume check is performed on Pcell after rdTimerForReinitSplitTxPower timeout, based on [Before CB006137-C]prbBasedOnSpecifiedSplitPower[CB006137-C]specifiedSplitPower[End CB006137-C] denoted as if there is stable remained power for scell, [Before CB006137-C]prbBasedOnSpecifiedSplitPower[CB006137-C]specifiedSplitPower[End CB006137-C] is calculated on Pcell according to [Before CB006137-C]5G\_UP\_ALG\_10883\_replaced([11119896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with splitTxPower set to the value according to [End CB006137-C]5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Scell resume on Pcell run with below step:

1. Prb is not limited by maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower as pcellSplitTxPower equal to min(Pcmax,servingcellPcmax)
2. requireMorePowerForResume is updated per ul schedule based on [Before CB006137-C]prbBasedOnSpecifiedSplitPower[CB006137-C]specifiedSplitPower and reqPowerBasedOnSchePRBs, denoted as the required power based on scheduled PRBs on Pcell, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB006137-C]
3. once set to true in the ul scheduling overlap slot within the rdPeriodForOverPowerEva period, the final requireMorePowerForResume should be true in this

rdPeriodForOverPowerEva period, which is denoted as requireMorePowerForResume |= true

1. When the rdPeriodForOverPowerEva check point reached，the resume check(ue distribution table) will start based on the final requireMorePowerForResume value(step3)

if requireMorePowerForResume == false

resumeCounter ++

else

resumeCounter = 0

when the resumeCounter reached rdNumForMinSplitTxPowerCheck, then the resume success, set scell powerState = resume, wait for BSR to trigger

case2 intialSplitTxPower and bufferSplit.

1. after the resume check, requireMorePowerForResume should be reset to false to start a new journey of the next rdPeriodForOverPowerEva

Note:

1. the start sfn/slot of resume check point of ue  is depended on where the ue added to the ue distribution table,  and the check point repeat each rdPeriodForOverPowerEva interval

[CB006137-C]

1. SpecifiedSplitPower is the threshold for reqPowerBasedOnSchePRBs when evaluating SCell UL resume, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB006137-C]

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1850\_replaced([11202840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgZ9lPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

##### 2.4.7.12.6.0-12 UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA) Resume or Suspend Pcell  in UL Overlapping slot (ID: 11161992)

**2.4.7.12.6.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `11161999`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Pcell Suspend

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-12.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11162003`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Suspend Ul scheduling in overlapping slots on Pcell due to too low split TX power, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10921\_replaced([11119951](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10933\_replaced([11197837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwH-KfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Pcell suspend scenario

below are the conditions should be checked by Scell before suspension:

[CB006137-BC]

condition1:

[End CB006137-BC]

1. Scell requires to increase power which lead to exceed max allowed power(not enough power remained to Scell);
2. efficiency ratio less then rdEffiThreshForUlScheSuspension (pcell efficiency is not good enough);
3. overpower

the logic should be handled as :

if(1 and 2 and 3)

set pcell powerState = suspend

set scellSplitTxPower = min(pcmax, servingcellPcmax) at next slot

send pcell powerState:suspend to pcell with psPeerCtrl\_ScellUlControlInd

Note:

1. For the scell requiring to increase power, refer to 5G\_L2\_8627\_replaced([11161861](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25dFlfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. For the implementation simplicity, use splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep(i, pcell, t) to replace efficiencyPerPRB(i, pcell, t),  splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep(i, scell, t) to replace efficiencyPerPRB(i, scell, t) in step 2.
3. In the case efficiencyPerPRB(i, pcell, t) > efficiencyPerPRB(i, scell, t), and the differential between rdMinSplitTxPowerAdjStep and ueSplitPowerAdjStep is not large enough(ueSplitPowerAdjStep /rdMinSplitTxPowerAdjStep < efficiencyPerPRB(i, pcell, t)/efficiencyPerPRB(i, scell, t)). It may result in that condition 2 satisfied with the efficiencyPerPRB replaced by splitTxPowerAdjustmentStep but not satified if directly using efficiencyPerPRB. if this happen, it's treated pcell suspend as condition2 satisfied.

[CB006137-BC]

condition2:

1. For SCell: PrbTobeScheduled > maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower(based on scellSplitTxPower), requireMorePower = true in Ul scheduling for overlapping slot t-1
2. For PCell: the pcellSplitTxPower -= scellIncreasingPower, and (nonPowerLimitedTxPowerPerPRB + TPC) \* minNumPrbsUl > max(pcellSplitTxPower, rdMinSplitTxPower) in slot t received the scellIncreasingPower from SCell.

Note: if closed loop power control is activated in PCell, TPC is the value to be transmitted, otherwise it's 0.

The PCell suspend procedure is handled as following:

if (1 and 2)

set the pcell powerState = suspend

set the scellSplitTxPower = min(pcmax, servingcellPcmax) at next overlapping slot

PCell send pcellSuspend to SCell via psPeerCtrl\_PcellUlPowerCtrlInd

end

[End CB006137-BC]

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1847\_replaced([11202756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgZ9gfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-12.0-2**  (ID: `11162010`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Pcell Resume

---

**2.4.7.12.6.0-12.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11162021`)

UL Power Split (FR1 UL CA)
Pell resume scenario

Resume Pcell Ul scheduling in overlapping slots on Scell where the scheduling is suspended due to too low split TX power, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Pcell resume check is performed on Scell, based on [Before CB006137-C]prbBasedOnSpecifiedSplitPower[CB006137-C]specifiedSplitPower[End CB006137-C] denoted as if there is stable remained power for Pcell, [Before CB006137-C]prbBasedOnSpecifiedSplitPower[CB006137-C]specifiedSplitPower[End CB006137-C] is calculated on Scell according to [Before CB006137-C]5G\_UP\_ALG\_10883\_replaced([11119896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLuOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with splitTxPower set to the value according to [End CB006137-C]5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before CB006137-C]Here splitTxPower for calculating prbBasedOnSpecifiedSplitPower[CB006137-C]SpecifiedSplitPower[End CB006137-C] in SCell depends on these three parameters in PCell:

- nonPowerLimitedTxPowerPerPRB

- TPC, if CLPC is activated

- minNumPrbsUl

For simplicity, if PCell is in suspend state due to no split tx power, then each time when nonPowerLimitedTxPowerPerPRB is updated in PCell, PCell will calculate the splitTxPower(minPrbSplitTxPower) for minimal Ul PRBs scheduling according to these three factors: nonPowerLimitedTxPowerPerPRB, TPC and minNumPrbsUl, and send this minPrbSplitTxPower to SCell via psPeerCtrl\_PcellUlPowerCtrlInd.

SCell will calculate the new [Before CB006137-C]prbBasedOnSpecifiedSplitPower[CB006137-C]specifiedSplitPower[End CB006137-C] based the the minPrbSplitTxPower received from PCell.

Note: if closed loop power control in PCell is activated, the TPC value will use the maximum TPC step 3 dB, otherwise TPC value is 0.

Pcell resume on Scell go with below step:

1. Prb is not limited by maxPrbBasedOnSplitPower as scellSplitTxPower equal to min(pcmax,servingcellPcmax)
2. requireMorePowerForResume is updated per ul schedule based on [Before CB006137-C]prbBasedOnSpecifiedSplitPower[CB006137-C]specifiedSplitPower and reqPowerBasedOnSchePRBs, denoted as the required power based on scheduled PRBs on Scell, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB006137-C]
3. once set to true in the ul scheduling overlap slot within the rdPeriodForOverPowerEva, the final requireMorePowerForResume should be true in this

rdPeriodForOverPowerEva period, which is denoted as requireMorePowerForResume |= true

1. When the rdPeriodForOverPowerEva check point reached，the resume check(ue distribution table) will start based on the final requireMorePowerForResume value(step3)

if requireMorePowerForResume == false

resumeCounter ++

else

resumeCounter = 0

when the resumeCounter reached rdNumForMinSplitTxPowerCheck, then treat the Pcell resume as success, set pcell powerState = resume, sent pcell powerState:resume to pcell with msg psPeerCtrl\_ScellUlControlInd

1. after each resume check, requireMorePowerForResume should be reset to false to start a new journey of the next rdPeriodForOverPowerEva

Note:

1. the start sfn/slot of the resume check point of ue is depended on where the ue added to the ue distribution table,  and the check point repeat each rdPeriodForOverPowerEva interval

[CB006137-C]

1. SpecifiedSplitPower is the threshold for reqPowerBasedOnSchePRBs when evaluating PCell UL resume, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10923\_replaced([11119968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqMVRPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB006137-C]

Refer to 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_1847\_replaced([11202756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgZ9gfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for message follow.

---

#### 2.4.7.12.7 pSRS for TDD SCell (FR1 UL CA)

##### 2.4.7.12.7.0-1 pSRS for TDD SCell (FR1 UL CA) UL scheduling in overlapping slot with pSRS (ID: 11162032)

**2.4.7.12.7.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12472144`)

pSRS for TDD SCell (FR1 UL CA)
When SRS transmission overlapped with PUCCH, PUSCH, SRS will have low priority of UE transmission power, refer to 5G\_UP\_5280\_replaced([11358825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucjiD_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or TS38.213 section 7.5.

Periodic SRS resource allocation:

**(1)**For pCSI over PUCCH overlapped with pSRS\*\*\*\*

Regardless whether UE support parallelTxSRS-PUCCH-PUSCH or not, one UE’s pCSI and pSRS can’t be allocated in the same slot, refer to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2763\_replaced([11306109](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tq1soPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**(2)**For PUSCH overlapped with pSRS\*\*\*\*

 pSRS is configured in TDD special slot with TDD symbol 12[CB014465-B], or 10,11,13[End CB014465-B].

 Pcell need to know remote Scell’s pSRS configuration(period/slotOffset) in remoteCell ConfigParam via PsUser\_UserModifyReq refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_456\_replaced([10976239](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VFWlPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), then PCell UL scheduling will skip the last one for puschMappingType-A or two symbols for puschMappingType-B in these FDD UL slots overlapped with pSRS, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2422\_replaced([11115762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpxei_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9351\_replaced([11115859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpyspPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). if UE doesn’t support **parallelTxSRS-PUCCH-PUSCH for FDD PCell, the above skip logic shall be applied too, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5062\_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**.\*\*\*\*

Link:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=V8ishU&cid=9206de7d%2Df76b%2D4167%2D95a0%2D9ed94135ffa1&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F7%2E%20Carrier%20Aggregation%20Control%2F12%2E%20CA%20UE%20Scheduling&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams illustrating different radio frame structures for mobile communication systems: FDD UL (Frequency Division Duplex Uplink) and TDD (Time Division Duplex). Both diagrams represent a time-frequency grid, with the horizontal axis indicating time slots (numbered 0 to 13) and the vertical axis representing frequency resources.
The FDD UL diagram shows a single frame structure labeled "U". It depicts the following channels:
- A wide orange band at the top labeled "U".
- A yellow band below it labeled "PUCCH" (Physical Uplink Control Channel).
- A brown band below PUCCH.
- A large orange area in the middle, spanning multiple time slots and frequency resources, labeled "PUSCH" (Physical Uplink Shared Channel). Two black squares are shown within the PUSCH area, possibly representing resource allocations.
- A yellow band below the PUSCH.
- A blue band at the bottom.
A red star is positioned below the time slot number 5.
The TDD diagram shows a frame structure divided into two parts, labeled "S" and "U".
The "S" part, on the left, represents a downlink transmission. It includes:
- A yellow band at the top labeled "S".
- A green area labeled "PDSCH" (Physical Downlink Shared Channel), occupying a significant portion of the time and frequency resources.
- A narrow light green column to the left of PDSCH, labeled "PDCC H".
- A gray column to the right of PDSCH, labeled "P", "S", "R", "S" vertically.
The "U" part, on the right, represents an uplink transmission. It includes:
- A yellow band at the top labeled "U".
- A blue band below it.
- A green band below the blue band.
- A yellow band below the green band.
- A large orange area in the middle, spanning multiple time slots and frequency resources, labeled "PUSCH".
- A blue band at the bottom.
Both diagrams have numerical scales along the bottom, indicating time slots from 0 to 13. The TDD diagram has two such scales, one for the "S" part and one for the "U" part, indicating a cyclical nature of the frame structure.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3154.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Link:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=V8ishU&cid=9206de7d%2Df76b%2D4167%2D95a0%2D9ed94135ffa1&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F7%2E%20Carrier%20Aggregation%20Control%2F12%2E%20CA%20UE%20Scheduling&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

**(3)**For DL A/N overlapped with pSRS\*\*\*\*

Regardless whether UE support parallelTxSRS-PUCCH-PUSCH or not, if DL A/N over PUCCH in FDD Cell overlapped with pSRS in TDD special slot, no special handling will be taken, according to the UE power allocation priority, pSRS may not be transmitted at all or no enough power to transmit, so DL and UL will switch to DL PMI/SSB beam and UL SSB beam.

Above description for PUSCH symbol skipping should be applied to NON UL TX switch UE.

For UL TX switch UE with P-SRS in TDD special slot, the FDD PUSCH symbol should be shortened according to 5G\_UP\_11397\_replaced([11356891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uca_JfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the implementation logic in FDD slot N can be as below:

if (the first TDD slot from two TDD slots that overlaps with next FDD PUSCH slot (N+K2+1) is UL slot) and (there is P-SRS in TDD special slot that overlaps with FDD PUSCH slot(N+K2))

FDD PUSCH in current slot N+K2 should be shortened with last 3 [CB014465-B] or 4 [End CB014465-B]symbols.

---

##### 2.4.7.12.7.0-2 pSRS for TDD SCell (FR1 UL CA) Start or stop pSRS receiving when SCell activated or deactivated (ID: 11162090)

**2.4.7.12.7.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11162101`)

pSRS for TDD SCell (FR1 UL CA)
When joint DL&UL SCell starts to activate, if SCell Ul received FR1\_Activation\_ACK via l2\_ps\_internal\_DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate, this CA UE will be added to SrsToBeScheduled list of SCell.

Then SCell Ul scheduler will check SrsToBeScheduled list and start to schedule pSRS if there are UEs configured with SRS resource. UE will report pSRS via Uldata\_SrsReceiveRespBmPs to SCell Ul, in case actDlSrsBm=true [CNI-83499]or actUlSrsBm=true[End CNI-83499], SCell Ul will forward Uldata\_SrsReceiveRespBmPs to SrsBM EO for those UEs configured with [Before CNI-83499]pSRS for Dl BM[CNI-83499]pSRS for Dl/UL BM[End CNI-83499].

When received valid CoMa, the Ul BM state change could refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2137\_replaced([11154039](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksqadPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and the Dl BM state change could refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6964\_replaced([11155671](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksxIIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When joint DL&UL SCell starts to deactivate, if SCell Ul received FR1\_Deactivation\_Decision via l2\_ps\_internal\_DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate from SCell Dl, the CA UE will be removed from the SrsToBeScheduled list of SCell, then this UE will not be scheduled to report SRS any more.  [Before CNI-83499]So the Ul BM will be reset to Srs\_Bm\_Aged state.[CNI-83499]So the Ul BM will be reset to Srs\_Bm\_Aged state.[End CNI-83499]

SCell Dl will also notify SrsBm EO about the deactivate decision via l2\_ps\_internal\_DlToSrsBmIntraUpdate with SCellActivationStatus=Deactive, [Before CNI-83499]in case pSRS for Dl BM is configured, then the Dl BM state will be reset to Srs\_Bm\_Aged state.[CNI-83499]in case pSRS for Dl or UL BM is configured, then the corresponding Dl or UL BM state will be reset to Srs\_Bm\_Aged state.[End CNI-83499]

---

#### 2.4.7.12.8 pSRS for TDD PCell (FR1 UL CA)

##### 2.4.7.12.8.0-1 pSRS for TDD PCell (FR1 UL CA) UL scheduling in overlapping slot with pSRS (ID: 11162112)

**2.4.7.12.8.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12461549`)

pSRS for TDD PCell (FR1 UL CA)
When SRS transmission overlaps with PUCCH/PUSCH, SRS will have low priority of UE transmission power, refer to 5G\_UP\_5280 or TS38.213 section 7.5.

Periodic SRS resource allocation:

TDD pSRS allocation follows the legacy procedure as TDD pSRS is configured in the user access phase, when it's not know whether there is TDD + FDD UL CA or not in later phase. During the FDD scell addition for TDD + FDD UL CA, the pSRS may be reconfigured to other period/slotOffset(keep no change is also OK). Anyway, there may be power limitation issue for TDD pSRS overlapping with FDD PUSCH for TDD + FDD UL CA.

Note: As pSRS is allocated in the special slot of TDD pcell, there is no PUCCH overlapping with pSRS in TDD + FDD UL CA.

**FDD SCell PUSCH overlapping with TDD PCell pSRS**

1. pSRS is configured in symbol 12 [CB014465-B] or 13 [End CB014465-B]of TDD special slot, which overlaps with FDD symbol 13[CB014465-B] when FDD SCell puschMappingType-A for example as bellow. The rules for detailed SLIV usage is according to [11202287: SLIV for FDD PCell or SCell in FR1 UL CA overlap with TDD P-SRS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcHPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)[End CB014465-B].

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams illustrating frequency division duplex (FDD) configurations.
The top diagram, labeled "FDD UL", depicts the uplink spectrum. It is divided into several horizontal bands representing different channels. The topmost band is labeled "U". Below this are bands labeled "PUCCH" and "PUSCH". The "PUSCH" band occupies the majority of the spectrum and is colored orange. There is a black block on the right side of the "PUSCH" band, possibly indicating a reserved or unused portion. The diagram includes a numerical scale from 0 to 13 along the bottom, with a red star symbol positioned at the end of the scale.
The bottom diagram, labeled "DD", illustrates the downlink spectrum. This diagram is divided into two main sections, labeled "D" and "S". The "D" section is colored green and is further subdivided into vertical columns. On the left side of the "D" section, there is a narrow vertical band labeled "PDCCH". The main area of the "D" section is labeled "PDSCH". The "S" section is colored yellow and is also subdivided into vertical columns. Similar to the "D" section, there is a narrow vertical band labeled "PDCCH" on the left side of the "S" section, followed by the "PDSCH" label in the main area. To the right of the "PDSCH" area in the "S" section, there are several narrow vertical bands colored grey, black, and light orange, with labels "P", "S", "R", and "S" within them. This diagram also has a numerical scale along the bottom, starting from 0 and extending to 13 for both the "D" and "S" sections, with an additional scale from 0 to 13 for the "S" section.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3162.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note: For TDD PCell with frame structure 5GC001208, pSRS can be allocated in symbol 12 of special slot 8/18, which overlaps with FDD symbol 6 in slot 4/9. In this case, there is no need to do FDD PUSCH symbol skipping as there is no FDD PUSCH scheduling in FDD slot 4/9 that overlaps with TDD UL slot 9/19.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams illustrating different cellular communication configurations: FDD UL (Frequency Division Duplex Uplink) and TDD (Time Division Duplex). Both diagrams use a grid-like structure with colored blocks representing different channels or time slots.
**FDD UL Diagram:**
This diagram shows a single block labeled "U" at the top, spanning the entire width of the diagram. Below this, a block labeled "PUCCH" (Physical Uplink Control Channel) is shown. The majority of the diagram is occupied by a large orange block labeled "PUSCH" (Physical Uplink Shared Channel), with two smaller black blocks within it. The horizontal axis is marked with numbers from 0 to 13. A red star is positioned below the number 5.
**TDD Diagram:**
This diagram is divided into two sections, labeled "S" and "U" on the top. The "S" section on the left is further subdivided. The leftmost part of the "S" section has vertical blocks labeled "P", "D", "C", "C", "H". The main part of the "S" section is a large green block labeled "PD SCH" (Physical Downlink Shared Channel), divided into multiple vertical segments. To the right of the "PD SCH" block, there are vertical blocks labeled "P", "S", "R", "S".
The "U" section on the right of the TDD diagram is similar to the FDD UL diagram. It has a block labeled "PUCCH" below the "U" label. The majority of this section is an orange block labeled "PUSCH". The horizontal axis for the TDD diagram is marked with numbers from 0 to 13 for the "S" section and then restarts from 0 to 13 for the "U" section.
**Textual Content:**
\* FDD UL
\* U
\* PUCCH
\* PUSCH
\* TDD
\* S

\* D
\* C
\* C
\* H
\* PD SCH

\* S

The diagrams visually represent the allocation of resources in different duplexing modes for cellular communication. The FDD UL diagram shows a configuration for uplink transmission, while the TDD diagram shows a configuration that includes both downlink (S) and uplink (U) transmissions, with a clear separation in time.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3162.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

1. FDD SCell needs to know remote TDD PCell’s pSRS configuration(period/slotOffset) in CellConfigParam[0] via PsUser\_UserSetupReq refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_392 **or remotePcellToModify via PsUser\_UserModifyReq refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_5208. With this information, FDD SCell UL scheduling will skip the last symbol for puschMappingType-A or last two symbols for puschMappingType-B in these UL slots overlapped with pSRS, applying for the case that [image-2][image\_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3162.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2](refer to 5G_UP_ALG_10873_replaced([11119621](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is less than [image-3][image\_desc]The image is entirely black, with no discernible features, text, or objects.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3162.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]which [image-4][image\_desc]The image is completely black, indicating it is either an empty image or a black screen. There is no discernible content, text, or table present.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3162.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10873\_replaced([11119621](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or** if UE doesn’t support **parallelTxSRS-PUCCH-PUSCH for FDD SCell, the above skip logic shall be applied too , refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5062\_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , if the slot offset of pSRS is changed in remotePcellToModify, both legacy and new SRS slot offset during the transient phase shall be skipped.** if it is criticalband for FDD SCell(tasSRSImpact**.txSwitchWithAnotherBand = true) and PUSCH is  overlapped with Pcell's periodic WB TAS SRS , last two symbols for puschMappingType-A/B shall be skipped, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5062\_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22912\_replaced([11115983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpz6xvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, SLIV refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2422\_replaced([11115762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpxei_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), pusch-TimeDomainAllocationList for NON-DSS refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9399\_replaced([11115853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpysovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), pusch-TimeDomainAllocationList for DSS with long PUCCH refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9401\_replaced([11115904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpzTsPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).***\******\***

Above description for PUSCH symbol skipping should be applied to NON UL TX switch UE.

For UL TX switch UE with P-SRS in TDD special slot, the FDD PUSCH symbol should be shortened according to 5G\_UP\_11397, the implementation logic in FDD slot N can be as below:

if (the first TDD slot from two TDD slots that overlaps with next FDD PUSCH slot (N+K2+1) is UL slot) and (there is P-SRS in TDD special slot that overlaps with FDD PUSCH slot(N+K2))

FDD PUSCH in current slot N+K2 should be shortened with last 3[CB014465-B] or 4 (depending on the TDD CC's periodic SRS symbol position) [End CB014465-B]symbols for both PUSCH mapping type A and B.

---

##### 2.4.7.12.8.0-2 pSRS for TDD PCell (FR1 UL CA) DL scheduling in overlapping slot with periodic WB TAS SRS for Critical band(txSwitchImpactToRx) (ID: 11162192)

**2.4.7.12.8.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11162211`)

pSRS for TDD PCell (FR1 UL CA)
if it is criticalband for FDD SCell(tasSRSImpact. txSwitchImpactToRx = true) and PDSCH is overlapped with Pcell's periodic WB TAS SRS , last two symbols for PDSCH shall be shorten, the slot for frameConfig1208(slot 4/9) shall be skipped , the following black bar is one example,SLIV refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14243\_replaced([11115315](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kptNFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

#### 2.4.7.12.9 A-SRS for TDD SCell (F+T FR1 UL CA )

##### 2.4.7.12.9.0-1 A-SRS for TDD SCell (F+T FR1 UL CA ) UL scheduling in overlapping slot with A-SRS (ID: 11162224)

**2.4.7.12.9.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11162249`)

A-SRS for TDD SCell (F+T FR1 UL CA )
When SRS transmission overlapped with PUCCH/PUSCH, SRS will have low priority of UE transmission power, refer to 5G\_UP\_5280\_replaced([11358825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucjiD_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or TS38.213 section 7.5.

For PUSCH overlapped with A-SRS:

L2-PS shall select K2 value for UE A-SRS scheduling on TDD SCell(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5057\_replaced([11201982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwa5GvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

There may be power limitation issue for TDD A-SRS overlapping with FDD PUSCH for FDD + TDD UL CA, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5062\_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). PCell UL scheduling will skip the last one symbol for puschMappingType-A or two symbols for puschMappingType-B in these FDD UL slots overlapped with TDD SCell's A-SRS, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2422\_replaced([11115762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpxei_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9351\_replaced([11115859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpyspPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If UE doesn’t support parallelTxSRS-PUCCH-PUSCH for FDD PCell, the above skip logic shall be applied too, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5062\_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

PCell and SCell are both puschMappingType-A:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams illustrating resource allocation in cellular communication systems, specifically for FDD UL (Frequency Division Duplex Uplink) and TDD (Time Division Duplex).
The top diagram, labeled "FDD UL", shows a single slot, "Slot 2", divided into subcarriers and symbols. The "U" at the top indicates uplink. The majority of the slot is allocated to "PUSCH" (Physical Uplink Shared Channel), depicted in orange. There are also colored bands representing other channels or control information. A text annotation points to a small gray area in the upper right portion of "Slot 2", stating: "At Scell aperiodic SRS symbol, skip the symbol for PCell PUSCH scheduling". This indicates that when a secondary cell (Scell) transmits an aperiodic Sounding Reference Signal (SRS), the primary cell (PCell) will skip scheduling PUSCH in that specific symbol. The numbers 0 through 13 at the bottom represent symbols within the slot.
The bottom diagram, labeled "TDD", shows two adjacent slots, "Slot 4" and "Slot 5", also divided into subcarriers and symbols. Both slots are labeled "U" at the top, indicating uplink. "PUCCH" (Physical Uplink Control Channel) is allocated in the upper portion of both slots, shown in blue and green bands. Below the PUCCH, the majority of the resource is allocated to "PUSCH", depicted in orange. A text annotation points to the boundary between "Slot 5" and the area below it, stating: "Scell aperiodic SRS symbol, PUSCH type A". This suggests that in TDD, an aperiodic SRS from a Scell might be associated with PUSCH of Type A.
The diagrams use color-coding to differentiate between various resource allocations, with orange for PUSCH, blue and green for PUCCH, and yellow for what appears to be control regions or other uplink channels. The grid-like structure represents the time-frequency resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3169.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

PCell is puschMappingType-A, while SCell is puschMappingType-B:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams illustrating different scheduling scenarios in a cellular network, likely related to LTE or 5G. Both diagrams represent time-frequency resources within slots.
The top diagram, labeled "FDD UL" (Frequency Division Duplex - Uplink), shows a single slot, "Slot 2," divided into subcarriers (represented by vertical columns) and OFDM symbols (represented by horizontal rows). The diagram indicates that the "U" (Uplink control region) occupies the top yellow row. Below this, there are several rows of colored blocks, with the "PUSCH" (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) occupying a large central orange area. A text annotation points to the rightmost part of the PUSCH region, specifically a small gray block in symbol 12 and 13, with the text "At Scell aperiodic SRS symbol, skip the symbol for PCell PUSCH scheduling." This suggests that when a secondary cell (Scell) transmits an aperiodic SRS (Sounding Reference Signal), the corresponding symbol in the primary cell (PCell) PUSCH is skipped. The horizontal axis is labeled with numbers 0 through 13, likely representing subcarrier indices.
The bottom diagram, labeled "TDD" (Time Division Duplex), shows two adjacent slots, "Slot 4" and "Slot 5," also divided into subcarriers and OFDM symbols. Both slots have a yellow "U" region at the top. Below the "U" region, the "PUCCH" (Physical Uplink Control Channel) occupies a narrow green row. The "PUSCH" then occupies a larger orange area below the PUCCH. A text annotation points to the rightmost part of Slot 5, specifically a dark blue vertical bar spanning multiple symbols, with the text "Scell aperiodic SRS symbol, PUSCH type B." This indicates that in TDD, a Scell aperiodic SRS symbol can be associated with PUSCH Type B transmission.
The diagrams use different colors to represent different channel types or resource allocations. The overall presentation is a visual representation of resource scheduling in uplink transmissions for different duplexing modes and specific scenarios involving aperiodic SRS.

Note:

1. For all TDD supported frame structures(5GC001208, 5GC001070, 5GC001116), A-SRS can’t be allocated for CA UE in TDD SCell even slot.

2. As A-SRS is transmitted with PUSCH, there is no PUCCH overlapping with A-SRS in FDD + TDD UL CA.

---

#### 2.4.7.12.10 A-SRS for TDD PCell (T+F FR1 UL CA)

##### 2.4.7.12.10.0-1 A-SRS for TDD PCell (T+F FR1 UL CA) UL scheduling in overlapping slot with A-SRS (ID: 11162262)

**2.4.7.12.10.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11162291`)

A-SRS for TDD PCell (T+F FR1 UL CA)
When SRS transmission overlapped with PUCCH/PUSCH, SRS will have low priority of UE transmission power, refer to 5G\_UP\_5280\_replaced([11358825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucjiD_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or TS38.213 section 7.5.

For PUSCH overlapped with A-SRS:

L2-PS shall select K2 value for UE A-SRS scheduling on TDD SCell(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5057\_replaced([11201982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwa5GvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

There may be power limitation issue for TDD A-SRS overlapping with FDD PUSCH for TDD + FDD UL CA, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5062\_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). SCell UL scheduling will skip the last one symbol for puschMappingType-A or two symbols for puschMappingType-B in these FDD UL slots overlapped with TDD PCell's A-SRS, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2422\_replaced([11115762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpxei_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9351\_replaced([11115859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpyspPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If UE doesn’t support parallelTxSRS-PUCCH-PUSCH for FDD SCell, the above skip logic shall be applied too, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5062\_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

PCell and SCell are both puschMappingType-B:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams illustrating different scheduling scenarios in a cellular network, likely related to LTE or 5G. Both diagrams use color-coded blocks to represent time slots and different types of data channels.
The top diagram is labeled "TDD" and shows two slots, "Slot 8" and "Slot 9".
- Slot 8 is divided into several vertical sections. The leftmost section is labeled "PDCH". To its right is a large green section labeled "PDSCH". The remaining sections are colored in shades of pink and orange.
- Slot 9 is divided into two main horizontal sections. The top section is labeled "U". Below this, there are two distinct areas: a yellow and light blue striped area labeled "PUCCH", and a larger orange area with a grid pattern labeled "PUSCH".
- A red text box with a pointer indicates "Pcell aperiodic SRS symbol, PUSCH type B" and points to the "PUSCH" area in Slot 9.
The bottom diagram is labeled "FDD UL" (likely indicating Frequency Division Duplex, Uplink). It shows a single slot, "Slot 4", which is divided into many smaller vertical blocks, numbered from 0 to 13 along the bottom.
- The top horizontal band of Slot 4 is labeled "U".
- Below the "U" band, there are several horizontal colored bands. The largest central area is colored orange with a grid pattern and is labeled "PUSCH".
- A light gray square block is present within the "PUSCH" area, towards the right side (around columns 11 and 12).
- A red text box with a pointer indicates "At Pcell aperiodic SRS symbol, skip the symbol for SCell PUSCH type B scheduling" and points to the light gray square block within the "PUSCH" area of Slot 4.
The diagrams visually represent how different data channels (PDCH, PDSCH, PUCCH, PUSCH) are allocated within time slots, and how specific scheduling events (like Pcell aperiodic SRS symbols) can affect the scheduling of other channels (like SCell PUSCH type B). The TDD diagram shows a PUSCH transmission in Slot 9, while the FDD UL diagram illustrates a scenario where a PUSCH transmission is skipped due to a Pcell aperiodic SRS symbol.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3173.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[CNI-134375-M]

Link:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=V8ishU&cid=9206de7d%2Df76b%2D4167%2D95a0%2D9ed94135ffa1&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F7%2E%20Carrier%20Aggregation%20Control%2F12%2E%20CA%20UE%20Scheduling&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

[End CNI-134375-M]

Note:

1. For all TDD supported frame structures(5GC001208, 5GC001070, 5GC001116), A-SRS can’t be allocated for CA UE in TDD PCell even slot.

2. As A-SRS is transmitted with PUSCH, there is no PUCCH overlapping with A-SRS in TDD + FDD UL CA.

---

#### 2.4.7.12.11 A-SRS for TDD PCell (T+T FR1 UL CA)

##### 2.4.7.12.11.0-1 A-SRS for TDD PCell (T+T FR1 UL CA) UL scheduling in overlapping slot with A-SRS (ID: 11162303)

**2.4.7.12.11.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11162330`)

A-SRS for TDD PCell (T+T FR1 UL CA)
When SRS transmission overlapped with PUCCH/PUSCH, SRS will have low priority of UE transmission power, refer to 5G\_UP\_5280\_replaced([11358825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucjiD_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or TS38.213 section 7.5.

For PUSCH overlapped with A-SRS:

L2-PS shall select K2 value for UE A-SRS scheduling on TDD SCell(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5057\_replaced([11201982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwa5GvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

There may be power limitation issue for A-SRS overlapping with PUSCH for TDD + TDD UL CA, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5062\_replaced([11202027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwbgJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). PCell UL scheduling will skip one symbol (for puschMappingType-A or UL fragmentation enabled on SCell) or two last symbols (for puschMappingType-B with UL fragmentation disabled on SCell) for these UL slots which overlap with SCell's A-SRS, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2415\_replaced([11115737](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpxehPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB006449-CC]

How many symbols is/are muted based on the configuration.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | SCell puschMappingType-B and UPlane fragmentation OFF | SCell Other combinations |
| PCell UPlane fragmentation ON | Two symbols | One symbol |
| PCell UPlane fragmentation OFF | Two symbols | One symbol |

[End CB006449-CC]

Different colors denote different types of channels: green for control channels, yellow for PUCCH (Physical Uplink Control Channel), orange for PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel), and blue for other control information.
The PCell grid shows PUCCH allocated in the first few rows and PUSCH in the middle rows. A single gray cell is highlighted in the PCell grid at symbol index 12 and a specific subcarrier row, with a text bubble pointing to it. This bubble states: "At SCell aperiodic SRS symbol, skip the symbol for PCell PUSCH scheduling." This indicates that when an aperiodic SRS (Sounding Reference Signal) is transmitted on the SCell, the corresponding symbol in the PCell is skipped for PUSCH scheduling.
The SCell grid also shows PUCCH in the upper rows and PUSCH in the middle rows. The rightmost columns of the SCell grid are colored purple, indicating a different type of resource allocation or a specific configuration. A text bubble points to the purple colored cells in the SCell grid, stating: "SCell aperiodic SRS symbol." This suggests that these purple cells represent the aperiodic SRS transmission on the SCell.
The numbers below each grid (0 to 13) represent the symbol indices within a time slot.
