##### 2.4.8.3.5 Dynamic Switching of DL MCS Table and/or DMRS Type

###### 2.4.8.3.5.0-1 Dynamic Switching of DL MCS Table and/or DMRS Type Stop and Re-start of the Channel Monitoring (ID: 13374150)

**2.4.8.3.5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13374917`)

Dynamic Switching of DL MCS Table and/or DMRS Type
As described in [11129283](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq7VHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [12483265](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_pPSrwA-UEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), under certain UE configurations the channel-based MCS-Table and DMRS-type re-configuration should not take place.  
L2-PS shall detect such cases and stop the channel monitoring when the RRC re-configuration needs to be prevented and re-start it when the changed UE configuration allows a switch of the MCS-table / DMRS-type.  
At any re-start the algorithm is reset (nbSamples = 0, [CB008949-D] nbSamples2 = 0, [End CB008949-D] timeOfLastSample = 0).

Below, it’s described which trigger is used in L2-PS to detect a configuration which shall stop/re-start the channel monitoring:

* Presence of an active VoNR call: detected through the reception of messages PsUser\_UserSetupReq, PsUser\_BearerSetupReq, PsUser\_BearerDeleteReq which set up / delete a VoNR DRB in L2-PS;
* Presence of an active HR DRB: detected through the reception of messages PsUser\_UserSetupReq, PsUser\_BearerSetupReq, PsUser\_BearerDeleteReq which set up / delete a HR DRB in L2-PS;
* Carrier Aggregation cases: see requirement [13133869](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2SeFsLwQEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

---

###### 2.4.8.3.5.0-2 Dynamic Switching of DL MCS Table and/or DMRS Type Interworking with Carrier Aggregation (ID: 13133869)

**2.4.8.3.5.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `13241740`)

Dynamic Switching of DL MCS Table and/or DMRS Type
In case of Inter-gNB Carrier Aggregation, the dynamic switching of the DL MCS Table ([11129283](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq7VHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) and the dynamic switching of the DMRS Type ([12483265](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_pPSrwA-UEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) are not supported on the Scell (also called: Ext-Scell), therefore the related channel monitoring (and following trigger to CP-RT) are not performed by L2-PS for the ue context in the Ext-Scell.

In case of FR1 Intra-gNB Carrier Aggreagation [Before CB008949-D] and except DL MCS table switching between 256Qam and 1024Qam in 26R2 [End CB008949-D], if (and on which trigger) the DL MCS Table / DMRS Type  is dynamically re-configured in the Scell depends on the setting of NIDD dlMcsTabAndDmrsSwitchImpCells for that cell. L2-PS performs the channel monitoring described in [11129283](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq7VHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) / [12483265](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_pPSrwA-UEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for the Scell only if dlMcsTabAndDmrsSwitchImpCells = "servingCell" for that cell. [Before CB008949-D] For DL MCS table switching between 256Qam and 1024Qam in 26R2, please refer to [13375032](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2hVr4fv9EfCPDo8cGBoqIw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). [End CB008949-D]

[CB008949-D] List of DL MCS table dynamic switching scenarios: qam64 from/to qam256, qam256 from/to qam1024, qam64 from/to qam1024. [End CB008949-D]

Note:

* L2-PS can recognize that an Scell is an Ext-Scell (to a certain Pcell) if IE pCellDlIgnbCaDestAddress is present in PsCell\_NrRelPCellSetupReq.
* dlMcsTabAndDmrsSwitchImpCells value is received within PsCell\_CellSetupReq and PsCell\_CellReconfgiurationReq. When the parameter is online modified the new value applies only to ue contexts instantiated on the Scell after the parameter's modification.

---

###### 2.4.8.3.5.0-3 Dynamic Switching of DL MCS Table and/or DMRS Type DL MCS Table Switching between 64Qam/256Qam and 1024Qam (ID: 13375032)

**2.4.8.3.5.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13378572`)

Dynamic Switching of DL MCS Table and/or DMRS Type
For both PCell/non-CA and SCell,

* [Before CB008949-D] L2-PS shall not monitor 64Qam from/to 1024Qam MCS index table switching.[End CB008949-D]
* In case of [Before CB008949-D] both dl1024QamDynamicSwitch ([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) and [End CB008949-D] pdschSupConfig.pdsch1024QamFr1 ([12876355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_dZT8QbC2EfCUL5QBTRN2zw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) [Before CB008949-D] are [CB008949-D] is [End CB008949-D] set to TRUE by L3, L2-PS shall monitor [CB008949-D] 64Qam from/to 1024Qam and [End CB008949-D] 256Qam from/to 1024Qam MCS index table switching as specified in [11129283](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq7VHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). “NRCELL.actDl1024Qam is equal to value other than 'disabled' and UE is DL 1024QAM capable“ condition of [11129283](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq7VHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) is indicated by  pdschSupConfig.pdsch1024QamFr1 parameter setting of PsUser\_UserSetupReq / PsUser\_UserModifyReq. Note: 64Qam from/to 1024Qam MCS index table switching also depends on actDlMcsTableAndDmrsSwitching setting, refer to [11129283](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq7VHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for detail.
* Otherwise, L2-PS shall not monitor [CB008949-D] 64Qam from/to 1024Qam or [End CB008949-D] 256Qam from/to 1024Qam MCS index table switching.

---

#### 2.4.8.3.6 PM Counter Support

##### 2.4.8.3.6.1 High Reliable Link Adaptation specific PM Counters

###### 2.4.8.3.6.1.0-1 High Reliable Link Adaptation specific PM Counters HR PM counters (ID: 11163645)

**2.4.8.3.6.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12787041`)

High Reliable Link Adaptation specific PM Counters
For HR LA, gNB extended to support URLLC specific MCS table (3GPP 38.214 §Table 5.1.3.1-3) or also known as LowSE (Low spectral efficiency) MCS table. The below counters are related to PDSCH transmission using LowSE table.

Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15997\_replaced([11230151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kysKBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for detailed description of below PM counter.

**1.**PDSCH\_INITX\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL1 (M55366C00001)\*\*\*\*

This PM counter is incremented whenever gNB transmits PDSCH using LowSE MCS table and UE configured with a HR DRB corresponding to hrProfile with PdschHrConfig.*nrPdschHRLaId = 1 (See 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))). All initial transmissions are considered with MCS index from 0 to 28.*

**2.**PDSCH\_INITX\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL2 (M55366C00003)\*\*\*\*

Incremented similarly as PDSCH\_INITX\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL1 **but here we check if the UE is configured with** hrProfile corresponding to PdschHrConfig.*nrPdschHRLaId = 2 (See 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))*\*\*\*\*

**3.**PDSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL1 (M55366C00002)\*\*\*\*

This PM counter is only incremented with successful initial transmissions **(HARQ with ACK) of PDSCH using LowSE MCS table and UE with HR DRB corresponding to hrProfile with PdschHrConfig.*nrPdschHRLaId = 1 (See 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).***

**4.**PDSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL2 (M55366C00004)\*\*\*\*

Incremented similarly as PDSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL1 but here we check if the UE is configured with **hrProfile corresponding to PdschHrConfig.*nrPdschHRLaId = 2 (See 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))***

An equivalent of those counters for HR LA for MCS table 64QAM is defined:

**1.**PDSCH\_INITX\_64TBL\_HRD\_PRFL1**(M55300C00029)**\*\*\*\*

**2.**PDSCH\_INITX\_64TBL\_HRD\_PRFL2**(M55300C00031)**\*\*\*\*

**3.**PDSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_64TBL\_HRD\_PRFL1 (M55300C00030)\*\*\*\*

**4.**PDSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_64TBL\_HRD\_PRFL2 (M55300C00032)\*\*\*\*

Note: If the UE is configured with LowSE MCS table, we need to make sure the legacy PM counters related to 64QAM table and 256QAM table [CB008949-C] and 1024QAM [End CB008949-C] shouldn't be pegged. The list of legacy MCS table specific counters wrt PDSCH transmission are mentioned in 5G\_PM\_868 under section "Legacy MCS Counters".

---

##### 2.4.8.3.6.2 service aware BLER target PM Counters

###### 2.4.8.3.6.2.0-1 service aware BLER target PM Counters service aware BLER target PM counters (ID: 11163663)

**2.4.8.3.6.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11163666`)

service aware BLER target PM Counters
There are two profiles for DRB specific BLER target(NRPDSCHBRLA profile1/profile2) in DL.

Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15997\_replaced([11230151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kysKBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for detailed description of below PM counter.

NRPDSCHBRLA profile1

1. PDSCH\_INI\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL1 (M55300C00041)

This counter is updated when HARQ feedback (ACK, NACK, or DTX) is received for initial transmission on PDSCH using DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPDSCHBRLA Profile1 for DL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

2. PDSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL1 (M55300C00042)

This counter is updated when HARQ ACK is received for initial transmission on PDSCH using DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPDSCHBRLA Profile1 for DL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

3. PDSCH\_SUCC\_ANY\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL1 (M55300C00043)

This counter is updated when HARQ ACK is received for any transmission/retransmission on PDSCH that was initially transmitted using DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPDSCHBRLA Profile1 for DL Outer Loop Link Adaption.

4. PDSCH\_UNSUCC\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL1 (M55300C00044)

This counter is updated when HARQ feedback (NACK or DTX) is received for the final HARQ retransmission on PDSCH that was initially transmitted using DRB specific target defined in NRPDSCHBRLA Profile1 for DL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

NRPDSCHBRLA profile2

1. PDSCH\_INI\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL2 (M55300C00045)

This counter is updated when HARQ feedback (ACK, NACK, or DTX) is received for initial transmission on PDSCH using DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPDSCHBRLA Profile2 for DL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

2. PDSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL2 (M55300C00046)

This counter is updated when HARQ ACK is received for initial transmission on PDSCH using DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPDSCHBRLA Profile2 for DL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

3. PDSCH\_SUCC\_ANY\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL2 (M55300C00047)

This counter is updated when HARQ ACK is received for any transmission/retransmission on PDSCH that was initially transmitted using DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPDSCHBRLA Profile2 for DL Outer Loop Link Adaption.

4. PDSCH\_UNSUCC\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL2 (M55300C00048)

This counter is updated when HARQ feedback (NACK or DTX) is received for the final HARQ retransmission on PDSCH that was initially transmitted using DRB specific target defined in NRPDSCHBRLA Profile2 for DL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

---

#### 2.4.8.3.7 Conservative Link Adaptation at Cell Edge in FDD

##### 2.4.8.3.7.0-1 Conservative Link Adaptation at Cell Edge in FDD Activation procedures (ID: 11163678)

**2.4.8.3.7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11163683`)

Conservative Link Adaptation at Cell Edge in FDD
Upon reception of PsCell\_CellSetupReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_ 1988) with DlLinkAdaptation structure (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_103\_replaced([10968509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UYL_Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) containing:

 actDlForceRankDowng IE, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall:

o if the IE’s value is equal to 5:dlNonGbrBrOnly, then activate the mechanism of forced rank downgrade at low CQI and high BLER (see 5G\_L2\_22721),

o else if the IE’s value is equal to 0:disabled, then not activate or deactivate this mechanism.

 deltaCqiOffsetAdjHo IE, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall:

o if the IE’s value is not 0, then activate the mechanism of additional delta CQI offset at incoming handover (see 5G\_L2\_22725),

o else if the IE’s value is equal to 0, then not activate or deactivate this mechanism.

 seThrTargetBlerReduct IE, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall:

o if the IE’s value is not 0, then activate the mechanism of lowered BLER target in low spectral efficiency region (see 5G\_L2\_22727).

o else if the IE’s value is equal to 0, then not activate or deactivate this mechanism.

---

##### 2.4.8.3.7.0-2 Conservative Link Adaptation at Cell Edge in FDD Conservative CQI Offset at incoming handover (ID: 11163689)

**2.4.8.3.7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11163692`)

Conservative Link Adaptation at Cell Edge in FDD
When the mechanism of additional delta CQI offset at incoming handover is activated (see 5G\_L2\_22708), then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall apply the additional CQI offset for the downlink transmission at incoming handover:

proceed with further steps only if all the conditions are met:

o processing cell is FDD cell, and,

o UE has no GBR or VoNR DRB configured, and,

o UE is configured with basic reliability [PR815391](IMS for 5QI-5 is included)[End PR815391], i.e. high reliability is not configured for any DRB (see 5G\_L2\_11104 for HR DRB),

apply an additional delta CQI offset for the next transmission, i.e. reduce the value of iniDeltaCqi (for initial delta CQI, see 5G\_L2\_11111) by the non-zero offset value received in deltaCqiOffsetAdjHo IE received from 5G-CP-RT (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_103\_replaced([10968509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UYL_Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For more details, please follow 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17528\_replaced([11129358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78KfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.8.3.7.0-3 Conservative Link Adaptation at Cell Edge in FDD Lowered BLER target at low Spectral Efficiency (ID: 11163698)

**2.4.8.3.7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11163704`)

Conservative Link Adaptation at Cell Edge in FDD
When the mechanism of lowered BLER target in low Spectral Efficiency regions is activated (see 5G\_L2\_22708), then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall follow the procedure below for the upcoming downlink transmission:

 proceed with further steps only if all the conditions are met:

o processing cell is FDD cell, and,

o UE has no GBR or VoNR DRB configured, and,

o UE is configured with basic reliability [PR815391](IMS for 5QI-5 is included)[End PR815391], i.e. high reliability is not configured for any DRB (see 5G\_L2\_11104 for HR DRB),

 check the values of seThrTargetBlerReduct IE and targetBlerReductFactor IE received from 5G-CP-RT (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_103\_replaced([10968509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UYL_Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and divide the received values ​​by 10 to adjust them to the NIDD values,

 if UE’s calculated spectral efficiency (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26012\_replaced([11129266](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq7VG_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) falls below the non-zero value of seThrTargetBlerReduct, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall adjust the BLER target for the UE, by multiplying the current BLER target value by targetBlerReductFactor IE’s value, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26677\_replaced([11130068](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq-YbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.8.4 UL Link Adaptation

#### 2.4.8.4.1 Overview

##### 2.4.8.4.1.0-1 Overview Uplink Link Adaptation Overview (ID: 11163718)

**2.4.8.4.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `11163781`)

Overview
The uplink link adaptation works based on:

1. HARQ feedback for PUSCH transmissions.

2. SRS/PUSCH SINR reports.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to wireless communication, specifically focusing on SINR (Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio) calculations and MCS (Modulation and Coding Scheme) selection in a 5G network.
The flowchart depicts several interconnected blocks, each representing a function or calculation. Arrows indicate the flow of data or control between these blocks.
Here's a breakdown of the components:
**Inputs:**
\* **SRS SINR Report:** A report related to Sounding Reference Signal (SRS) SINR.
\* **PUSCH SINR Report:** A report related to Physical Uplink Shared Channel (PUSCH) SINR.
\* **HARQ ACK/NACK:** Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request (HARQ) Acknowledgement/Negative Acknowledgement signals.
\* **UE PHR:** User Equipment (UE) Power Headroom.
**Processing Blocks:**
1. **Estimate Instance SU-SINR for MU-MIMO (5G\_L2\_6250):** This block takes the PUSCH SINR Report as input and estimates the SU-SINR for MU-MIMO.
2. **UL ILLA (Uplink Inter-Layer Link Aggregation):** This is a larger container block that includes two sub-blocks:
\* **rrmAvgSinrUl calculation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3078):** Calculates the average SINR for the uplink. It receives the SRS SINR Report as input and outputs `rrmAvgSinrUl`.
\* **rrmCorrAvgSinr calculation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3087):** Calculates a correlated average SINR. It receives `rrmAvgSinrUl` from the previous block and outputs `rrmDeltaSinr`.
3. **UL OLLA (Uplink Other Layer Link Aggregation):** This container block also has two sub-blocks:
\* **rrmDeltaSinr calculation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_11058):** Calculates a delta SINR. It receives the HARQ ACK/NACK signal as input and outputs `rrmDeltaSinr`.
\* **UL OLLA MU-MIMO:** This sub-block also performs `rrmDeltaSinr calculation (5G_UP_ALG_11058)`, taking the HARQ ACK/NACK signal as input and outputting `rrmDeltaSinr`.
4. **SINR rescaling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3090):** This block takes the output `rrmDeltaSinr` from the `rrmCorrAvgSinr calculation` block and the `UE PHR` as inputs. It performs SINR rescaling and outputs `rrmRescaled CorrAvgSinr`.
5. **SINR to MCS LUT calculation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3112):** This block takes the `rrmRescaled CorrAvgSinr` as input and uses a Look-Up Table (LUT) to calculate SINR to MCS. It outputs a value that feeds into the "proposed UI MCS" block.
6. **MU-SINR Calculation for MU-MIMO (5G\_L2\_6242):** This block takes the `rrmRescaled CorrAvgSinr` and the `rrmDeltaSinr` (from the UL OLLA MU-MIMO block) as inputs to calculate MU-SINR for MU-MIMO.
7. **MCS Compensation for MU-MIMO (5G\_L2\_6243):** This block takes the output of the "proposed UI MCS" block and the `MU-SINR Calculation for MU-MIMO` as inputs to perform MCS compensation for MU-MIMO.
**Outputs:**
\* **proposed UI MCS:** The proposed User Interface MCS.
\* **...:** An unspecified output from the "SINR to MCS LUT calculation" block, which then feeds into the "MCS Compensation for MU-MIMO" block.
The overall diagram shows a complex interplay of SINR measurements, calculations, and adjustments to determine an appropriate MCS for uplink transmissions, with specific considerations for MU-MIMO scenarios.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3415.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Uplink link adaptation can be further split to three parts, namely:

1. Outer loop link adaptation (OLLA) aims to eliminate systematical errors based on HARQ feedback (i.e., CRC-OK, CRC-NOK or DTX) for uplink transmission. OLLA computes ‘rrmDeltaSinr’ value as the output based on step-down/step-up filter (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11058\_replaced([11137940](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcSfvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The conditions when OLLA update could be skipped is defined in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11054\_replaced([11137899](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcSd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Initial value of ‘rrmDeltaSinr’ is set according to requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11060\_replaced([11138025](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krc5hvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

‘rrmDeltaSinr’ is computed for the current UL LA mode (high reliability / basic reliability). 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15603\_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) specifies when high reliability or basic reliability UL LA applies for an UE.

When the UL LA operates in HR mode, HARQ-ACK feedbacks associated to a PUSCH transmission with reduced MCS/PRBs (as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5102\_replaced([11203503](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are not ignored by OLLA but considered within offset rrmDeltaSinrForMcsDowngrade (as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25848\_replaced([11138261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which will also contribute to the calculation of the corrected average SINR (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3087\_replaced([11130243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq_mhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Additionally to what is requested in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25848\_replaced([11138261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), L2-PS shall reset rrmDeltaSinrForMcsDowngrade also when the UL LA switches from HR to BR. Reason: to be more robust when the corrected average SINR is calculated for BR and to avoid possible misunderstandings by displaying ulLaDeltaSinr.rrmDeltaSinrForMcsDowngrade != 0 in tti trace during BR mode.

There are two separate OLLA entities, each having its own state/counters. One is primary, used in most cases (denoted as UL OLLA), other one is additional OLLA  (denoted as UL OLLA MU-MIMO) used only for UEs scheduled in MU-MIMO mode [Before CB010708-C] in C8 subcells [CB010708-C]Pusch is 8Rx or 16Rx (numOfRxPusch=8 or 16, see 5G\_L2\_16925) [End CB010708-C]  as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11052\_replaced([11137872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcScvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Other responsibilites of UL OLLA:

· iniDeltaSinr: Initial delta SINR is a cell specific parameter which is assigned to each UE for when a context is set up to give a boost to get up to the right value of rrmDeltaSinr by assuming that the UE’s in the cell have similar rrmDeltaSinr. (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11060\_replaced([11138025](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krc5hvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

· CRC NACK flood: If we start receiving continuous CRC NACK/DTX indication from Layer-1 (due to bad radio condition), in such scenario UL LA follows requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3158\_replaced([11138143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdglfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

TTI trace event “ulLaDeltaSinr” defined in 5G\_L2\_1629 is traced with UL OLLA specific information.

2. Inner loop link adaptation (ILLA) aims to compute averaged and OLLA-corrected SINR for MCS selection. ILLA computes 'rrmAvgSinrUl' which is an output of exponential filter, by evaluating the instance SU-SINR . By considering 'rrmDeltaSinr' (output of OLLA) and 'rrmAvgSinrUl', ILLA computes 'rrmCorrAvgSinr' according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3087\_replaced([11130243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq_mhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In addition, uplink link adaptation stores the Power headroom report shared by UE in MAC CE.

Instance SU-SINR:

For SU UE:

instance SU-SINR is SINR reported from PUSCH/SRS(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3078\_replaced([11130719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krANl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For MU UE:

If SINR reported from SRS, instance SU-SINR is SINR reported.

If SINR reported from PUSCH, SU-SINR should be calculated as 5G\_L2\_6250.

‘rrmCorrAvgSinr’ is computed for the current UL LA mode (high reliability / basic reliability). 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15603\_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) specifies when high reliability or basic reliability UL LA applies for an UE.

TTI trace event ulLaAverageSinr defined in 5G\_L2\_1629 is traced with UL ILLA specific information.

3.   MCS selection selects best MCS which maximize the spectral efficiency and code rate that satisfy the required SINR (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3112\_replaced([11132202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krFGHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), by evaluating ‘rrmRescaledCorrAvgSinr’ (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3090\_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for SU UE, ‘MU-SINR’ (see 5G\_L2\_6242) for MU UE value from ‘rrmCorrAvgSinr’ value and the Power Headroom value.

If the current UL LA mode is set to high reliability, the MCS is reduced if it is higher than a threshold, defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17281\_replaced([11131699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krD38_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15603\_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) specifies when high reliability or basic reliability UL LA applies for an UE.

4.   MCS should be adjusted for MU UE when ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch = 1(MCS compensation) according to 5G\_L2\_8751.

Other UL LA related TTI trace event:

a. laTimer (see 5G\_L2\_1635): Link adaptation timer handling.

If RIM UL Mitigation extension is triggered (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27175\_replaced([11163444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktZaQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), ILLA and OLLA are maintained separately for each UL slot and SINR measurements i.e. ‘rrmDeltaSinr’, ‘rrmAvgSinrUl’, ‘rrmCorrAvgSinr’ are computed separately for the first and the second UL slot after Special Slot, contributing to ILLA and OLLA of their respective slots (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27204\_replaced([11130149](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq-_efvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). As a result, UE specific ILLA evaluates UE specific ‘rrmAvgSinrUl’ per UL slot and UL slot specific LA evaluates MCS for first and second UL slots separately.

Note 1: Like DL link adaptation, FD scheduler can override ‘proposedUlMcs’ and select different MCS based on number of PRB(s) allocated.

Note 2:UL link adaptation is affected by Adaptive transmission bandwidth (ATB) which applies for power limited UE(s) (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17311\_replaced([11132134](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krFGEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17271\_replaced([11131543](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). As part of ATB, if the smallest MCS doesn’t satisfy the conditions on required SINR and coderate, then ATB reduces the number of PRBs allocated by evaluating ‘reScaledCorrAvgSinr’ for SU UE, ‘MU-SINR’ (see 5G\_L2\_6242) for MU UE value from ‘rrmCorrAvgSinr’ and the Power Headroom value. The ‘numOfProposedPrbs’ is the output value.

Link to diagram source:

[https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS\_Tech\_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/CB007113-5G\_L2\_REQs\_DrawingFiles/2.4%20Algorithm/8.%20Link%20Adaptation/2.4.8.4.1%20Uplink%20Link%20Adaptation%20Overview.docx?d=w45117e2224854efcbd5135ed8207fe04&csf=1&web=1&e=bZNrSv](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/CB007113-5G_L2_REQs_DrawingFiles/2.4%20Algorithm/8.%20Link%20Adaptation/2.4.8.4.1%20Uplink%20Link%20Adaptation%20Overview.docx?d=w45117e2224854efcbd5135ed8207fe04&csf=1&web=1&e=bZNrSv%20)

#### 2.4.8.4.2 Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation

##### 2.4.8.4.2.0-1 Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation MCS mapping tables (ID: 11163792)

**2.4.8.4.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11163802`)

Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation
Tables defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3112\_replaced([11132202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krFGHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are used uplink are used to select the proposedMcs value, as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17281\_replaced([11131699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krD38_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The tables shall be implemented with all PRBs listed in the tables in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3112\_replaced([11132202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krFGHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), plus all PRBs listed in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1794\_replaced([11110605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpGJHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If some PRBs are not listed in the tables in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3028\_replaced([11126310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9C_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the table shall be implemented using interpolated valued.

Rational:

The full list of Prbs is required to calculate the proposed,

[CNI-126636]

if UserSetupReq.actUlOptMcsSelection is Ture,

based on rdUlPRBMcsSelection (R&D parameter defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29011\_replaced([11138974](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krjnMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

else

[End CNI-126636]

based on system bandwidth, defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17281\_replaced([11131699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krD38_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CNI-126636]

if UserSetupReq.actUlOptMcsSelection is Ture,

The proposedMcs will be same as the scaled MCS

else

[End CNI-126636]

The proposedMcs will be used to calculate the scaled MCS, based on used bandwidth. Interpolation is only used to calculate the scaled MCS.

The definition of proposedMcs and scaledMcs can be found in 5G\_L2\_13540

---

##### 2.4.8.4.2.0-2 Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation 8Rx sinr offset for IRC Rdd Debiasing (ID: 11163807)

**2.4.8.4.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11163812`)

Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation
When actIrcRddDebiasing in PsCell\_CellSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to TRUE then 5G-L2-PS shall apply sinr offset to 8RX UL LA LUTs (rdIrcRddDebiasingSinrOffset (see 5G\_L2\_20389)).

---

##### 2.4.8.4.2.0-3 Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation Calculation of the UL proposed MCS before the FD Resource Allocation (ID: 12762941)

**2.4.8.4.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12762942`)

Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation
**Calculation of the UL proposed MCS in FDM**

---

**2.4.8.4.2.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12762943`)

Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation
If link Adaptation Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency for UL is enabled ([12472235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_OeKtoA1aEfCuhewgqkUVKQ)), in FR1 gNB-DU calculates the proposed MCS before the FD resources' allocation, such that the MCS utilized in FDM to estimate the number of required PRBs to drain the UE's buffer and the MCS used to calculate the TBS are (as much as possible) aligned.

For a UE i in the to-be-scheduled UE list (that is: after the ue selection), if all the following conditions are true:

* NRCELL.actLaEnhanceEfficiencyLatency == ‘UL’/‘DL\_UL’;
* The cell's technology is FR1 TDD/FDD;
* The UE is candidate for a new transmission;

L2-PS shall calculate the proposed mcs (and associated maximum number of PRBs) based on UE's SINR as described in [12472327](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_RTT0sw3cEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9): rrmUlProposedMcs and rrmMaxNumPrbUl.

For a non power-limited UE:

* rrmUlProposedMcs is the proposed MCS which will be used to estimate the number of required PRBs to serve the UE's buffer. After the FD resources allocation, rrmUlProposedMcs can be adjusted to fulfill the max CR requirement or to consider SINR limitations due to MU-MIMO paring (see [12762944](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_7EHiI4NtEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

For a power limited UE:

* (rrmUlProposedMcs, rrmMaxNumPrbUl) is the starting point to look for the (mcs, prb) combination which maximizes the UE's tbs (see [12762948](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Xv24Y4NvEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) in the FDM.

---

**2.4.8.4.2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12762948`)

Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation
**Power Limited UE: Determination of the (prb, mcs) Combination maximizing the TBS for the current Transmission**

---

**2.4.8.4.2.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12762949`)

Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation
Additionally to what described in [12762942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_7EHiIoNtEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), for a power limited UE, NRCELL.actLaEnhanceEfficiencyLatency enables in UL new algorithms to determine the (prb, mcs) combination which allows to drain as much data as possible from the UE's buffer for the current transmission.

The calculation of the number of required PRBs to transmit UE's data ([11199729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwPS7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) shall follow [11201086](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwULYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for a power limited UE. The output is the combination (PRB\_N, MCS\_N), which allows to transmit as much data as possible from the total UE's pending data. PRB\_N is the maximum number of PRBs which can be given to the UE when using MCS\_N.

When the Low Complexity WRR scheduler is enabled in UL, after that the power limited UE is served by the WRR-sch, L2-PS shall check if a reduction of the allocated PRBs allows a better TBS according to the procedure in [12495965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6HxRIBQ8EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

**2.4.8.4.2.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `12762944`)

Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation
Possible Adaptations of the UL proposed MCS after the allocation of the FD Resources

---

**2.4.8.4.2.0-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12762945`)

Inner Loop UL Link Adaptation
For a power limited UE, if the number of allocated PRBs is less than PRB\_N ([12762948](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Xv24Y4NvEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) due to lack of available resources, MCS\_N must be corrected according to the number of allocated PRBs, by executing the steps below:

* The corrected average SINR is re-scaled according to the number of allocated PRBs as per [11131543](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);
* With it L2-PS looks for the highest mcs index in the sinr2mcs Table which satisfies the SINR condition ([12492829](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_975c0RL5EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)). For this search the column in the LUT corresponding to the reference PRB number rdUlPRBMcsSelection is used ([11132208](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krFGHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9));

The resulting mcs is the valid proposed MCS for the power-limited UE.

For a non-power limited UE the above re-scaling is not needed and rrmUlProposedMcs ([12762942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_7EHiIoNtEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) is still the valid mcs.

If the UE is scheduled with an initial transmission and MU-MIMO paired with other UE(s) in the current slot, the proposed MCS shall also be corrected according to [11148556](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKrMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) or [11148930](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksLSS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), depending on NRCELL.ulMuMimoParaSet.ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch configuration.

Finally it is checked if the combination (proposed MCS, allocated PRB) fulfills the code rate limitation ([11132055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krEfB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)). This can lead to a further reduction of the proposed MCS.

---

#### 2.4.8.4.3 Outer Loop UL Link Adaptation

##### 2.4.8.4.3.1 Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA

##### 2.4.8.4.3.1-1 Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA

###### 2.4.8.4.3.1-1.0-1 Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA UL service aware BLER target configuration to L2PS (ID: 11163845)

**2.4.8.4.3.1-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11163851`)

Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA
L2PS receives the service aware(DRB specific) BLER target profile in PsUser\_UserSetupReq or PsUser\_BearerSetupReq with the presence of drbBRPuschConfig below the IE DrbAddReqPs, refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

One service aware BLER target profile contains the detail value of profile ID, Bler target and/or stepdown. When multi DRBs associated with service aware BLER target setup for the UE, there will be several service aware BLER target profiles existing in this UE. Currently there can be max two NRPUSCHBRLA profiles supported.

Note:

1. there can be several bearers associated with the same service aware BLER target profile.

[CB013303-C]2. this profile configuration also apply to UL CA SCELL.[End CB013303-C]

---

###### 2.4.8.4.3.1-1.0-2 Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA UL service aware BLER target profile selection (ID: 11163857)

**2.4.8.4.3.1-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11163861`)

Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA
As there may be max two NRPUSCHBRLA profiles configured for the UE, it needs deciding which profile takes effect for this UE, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16024\_replaced([11138277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The selection happens when profile appears in  PsUser\_UserSetupReq or PsUser\_BearerSetupReq, meanwhile when PsUser\_BearerDeleteReq is received, it shall do profile selection among the left bearers. when none of the left bearers is associated with service aware BLER target, then NRBTS/NRCELL/ullaBlerTarget and NRBTS/NRCELL/ullaDeltaSinrStepdown should be chosed, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16024\_replaced([11138277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note:

1.when the selected NRPUSCHBRLA profile does not contain the stepdown value, then cell level NRBTS/NRCELL/ullaDeltaSinrStepdown( when online change with PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq received in L2PS, new ullaDeltaSinrStepdown only take effect for new UE) will be used.

[CB013303-C]2. the profile selection also apply to UL CA SCELL.[End CB013303-C]

---

###### 2.4.8.4.3.1-1.0-3 Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA UL service aware BLER target interworking with VoNR and HR mode (ID: 11163866)

**2.4.8.4.3.1-1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11163877`)

Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA
Service aware BLER target cannot be configured for VoNR (5QI 1**[CB008118-CB]/5QI** 65/5QI 66**[End CB008118-CB]) bearer, refer to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_14190\_replaced([11252212](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tngr0fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). So for UE with 5QI1**[CB008118-CB]/5QI **65/5QI 66**[End CB008118-CB]bearer and DRB specific BLER target bearer configured, the NRBTS/NRCELL/ullaTargetBlerVoNR should be selected when NRBTS/NRCELL/actTargetBlerVoNR is set to TRUE, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26677\_replaced([11130068](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq-YbfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).***\******\***

For UE work in HR(high reliability) mode, DRB specific BLER target will not be used, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16024\_replaced([11138277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.8.4.3.1.0-1 Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA Enter NACK Flood and exit NACK Flood over one Carrier (ID: 11163827)

**2.4.8.4.3.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11163835`)

Handling of CRC NACK flood in UL OLLA
When NACK Flood happens on carrier as 5G\_ALG\_UP\_3158 for one UE, [CB008246-I] if the UE is in basic reliability mode, [End CB008246-I] the UE should be paused for newTx scheduling. The initial transimission is blocked in TD scheduler, and UE start periodic timer with period = rdPeriodUlScheduling if the carrier is PCell and period = rdPeriodUlSchedulingSCell if the carrier is SCell. When timer expire, the UE tries to scheduling one initial PUSCH transimission, the scheduling pause is temperally stopped.

If there is pending data on this carrier for the initial PUSCH transmission when timer expires, wait for this initial PUSCH transmission CRC result. If the CRC is ACK, then the UE exit NACK Flood scheduling pause, timer stopped; if the CRC is NACK/DTX, NACK Flood Defense goes on.

If there is no pending data on this carrier for the initial PUSCH transmission when timer expires, then UE exit NACK Flood Defense directly.

[CB008246-I] If the UE is in high reliability mode, the scheduling of the UE shall not be paused. If the UE with a high reliability bearer is in basic reliability mode, the recovery timer defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16077\_replaced([11138358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreusvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be stopped when the UE enters NACK Flood Defense and started again when the UE exit NACK Flood Defense. 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15603\_replaced([11125162](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) specifies when high reliability or basic reliability UL LA applies for an UE. [End CB008246-I]

---

#### 2.4.8.4.4 Link Adaption for FR1 F+T UL CA

##### 2.4.8.4.4.0-1 Link Adaption for FR1 F+T UL CA Impact of dynamic power split on UL SCell (ID: 11163887)

**2.4.8.4.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11163897`)

Link Adaption for FR1 F+T UL CA
For a FR1 UL CA UE which is in power split mode (refer to 5G\_L2\_9563), the procedure of UL ILLA on SCell in overlapping slot is updated as follows:

1. when PCell UL grant this UE in overlapping slot, PCell UL shall calculate PCell’s txPowerDistributed according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11120\_replaced([11130873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krA0p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and send it to PCell DL via UlToDlIntraSchedUpdate. PCell DL saves the txPowerDistributed and sends it to SCell DL on step3.
2. When SCell UL grant this UE in overlapping slot, the determination of the UL MCS considers the effect of split TX power, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3090\_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Scell UL shall store nonPowerLimitedTxPowerPerPRB (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10873\_replaced([11119621](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqLHKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for the selected HARQ process for use in step5.
3. PCell DL sends PsPeerCtrl\_PcellUlSchedulingInd to SCell DL at the time of PCell sending PuschReceiveReq to L1, piggyback PCell’s txPowerDistributed to SCell and SCell save it for use in step4.
4. When SCell DL sends PuschReceiveReq to L1, if there is parallel scheduling for this UE on PCell and SCell in Ul overlapping slot, SCell DL shall send UE’s txPowerDistributed to SCell UL via DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate. SCell UL stores it for corresponding HARQ process.
5. When SCell UL receives PuschReceiveRespPs from L1, SCell UL shall calculate the average SINR based on SCell real available TX power (calculated based on PCell’s txPowerDistributed and nonPowerLimitedTxPowerPerPRB saved for corresponding HARQ  process), refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11120\_replaced([11130873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krA0p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.8.4.5 L2-PS triggered switch between High Reliability and Basic Reliability

##### 2.4.8.4.5.0-1 L2-PS triggered switch between High Reliability and Basic Reliability Overview (ID: 11163905)

**2.4.8.4.5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11163912`)

L2-PS triggered switch between High Reliability and Basic Reliability
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process with two main states: "HR OLC mode" and "BR OLC mode". The flowchart depicts the transitions between these states based on specific input events and conditions.
Starting from the top, there is a box labeled "Input Events for HR to BR" with an arrow pointing down to a larger rectangular box. This box describes the conditions for switching "from HR to BR". The conditions are:
1. "PUSCH fall back triggered (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16426)"
2. "or"
3. "if alSelection is set to dynamic, UL PDCCH fall back triggered (5G\_UP\_ALG\_18296)"
An arrow curves from the bottom of this "Switch from HR to BR" box to the oval shape labeled "BR OLC mode".
From "BR OLC mode", an arrow curves upwards to another large rectangular box. This box describes the conditions for switching "from BR to HR". The conditions are:
1. "PUSCH recovery triggered (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16077)"
2. "and"
3. "if alSelection is set to dynamic, UL PDCCH recovery triggered (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17263)"
An arrow points upwards from this "Switch from BR to HR" box to a box labeled "Input Events for BR to HR".
Finally, an arrow curves from the right side of the "BR OLC mode" oval back to the "HR OLC mode" oval, indicating a potential loop or alternative path not explicitly detailed by the rectangular boxes. Similarly, an arrow curves from the left side of the "HR OLC mode" oval to the "Switch from HR to BR" box, suggesting the start of that transition.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3440.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Event list to HR to BR evaluation:

[CB008246-H]

 ulPdcchHRBlerOutagePeriod, if alSelection is set to Dynamic: when ulPdcchHRBlerOutagePeriod timer is elapsed (5G\_UP\_ALG\_18296\_replaced([11170717](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3PvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[End CB008246-H]

 ulHRBlerOutagePeriod: when ulHRBlerOutagePeriod timer is elapsed (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16426\_replaced([11138318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

 ulNackFlood: : when CRC NACK Flood even happens according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3158\_replaced([11138143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krdglfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

Event list to BR to HR evaluation:

 ulHRBlerRecoverPeriod: when uHRBlerOutagePeriod timer is elapsed for PUSCH(5G\_UP\_ALG\_16077\_replaced([11138358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreusvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB008246-H] and PDCCH(5G\_UP\_ALG\_17263\_replaced([11170766](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008246-H]

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/CB008246%20-%205G_L2_Entity_Level%20Module%20-%20Drawings/2.4%20Algorithm/StateMachine_5G_L2_13221.xlsx?d=w3b65a98b3f0f429d94332ebd0f17c116&csf=1&web=1&e=jh17Yb>

---

##### 2.4.8.4.5.0-2 L2-PS triggered switch between High Reliability and Basic Reliability PUSCH triggered Fallback/Recovery (ID: 11163918)

**2.4.8.4.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11163923`)

L2-PS triggered switch between High Reliability and Basic Reliability
Samples Collection during the Channel Monitoring

---

**2.4.8.4.5.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11163933`)

L2-PS triggered switch between High Reliability and Basic Reliability
The metric used to monitor the UE PUSCH channel of a serving cell is the wide-band Transport Block size i.e. the Transport Block size derived from rrmUlProposedMcs (proposed MCS provided by UL LA from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3090\_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rrmMaxNumPrbUl (proposed max number of UL PRBs provided by UL LA from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3090\_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

L2-PS already calculates this value in the TD-scheduler, when the PF metric is calculated for the UE (refer to rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4856\_replaced([11195916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv_bQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Every time that the UE is scheduled in UL (with a new transmission), the latest calculated (rrmCurrentUlUeDataRate\_BytesPerSlot \* 8) is taken as valid sample for the purpose of the channel monitoring.

[CB008246-R]

If two consecutive samples (example: wbTbs[t] and wbTbs[t+delta]) are distant in time more than rdOversamplingInterval ms (5G\_L2\_15707), the gap is filled with oversampling N = delta/rdOversamplingInterval times wbTbs[t+delta].

Last Sample:

When the monitoring period is over (ulHRBlerOutagePeriod/ulHRBlerRecoverPeriod timer has expired or ulHRBlerOutagePeriod has been stopped due to NACK flood), if the last calculated wide-band proposed TBS (wbTbs[tlast]) is older than rdOversamplingInterval ms, a new wide-band proposed TBS is calculated and taken deltaLatestSample/rdOversamplingInterval times as valid sample, where deltaLatestSample is the difference between the time when the monitoring is stopped and tlast.

First Sample:

For the 1st wide-band TBS calculated within the monitoring period it is not required to perform backwards oversampling.

The oversampling aims to find a viable solution to the case that we have few samples available because the UE is rarely scheduled (mainly due to its traffic profile). It tries to reach similar results as sampling at regular intervals the latest value stored in the UE Database, w/o increasing SW effort or CPU load too much.

Oversampling is done only if rdOversamplingInterval > 0.

[End CB008246-R]

---

##### 2.4.8.4.5.0-3 L2-PS triggered switch between High Reliability and Basic Reliability TTI trace event for switching between High Reliability and Basic Reliability (ID: 11163939)

**2.4.8.4.5.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11163987`)

#### 2.4.8.4.6 Switch between LA mode and Transform Precoding

##### 2.4.8.4.6.0-1 Switch between LA mode and Transform Precoding Overview (ID: 11163997)

**2.4.8.4.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11164006`)

Switch between LA mode and Transform Precoding
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating different modes and transitions related to "LA mode" and "Transform Precoding" in a 5G context. The flowchart uses blue rectangles for modes and blue ellipses for transition conditions. Green arrows indicate transitions from conditions to modes, and red arrows indicate transitions from modes to conditions.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Modes (Blue Rectangles):**
\* **High-Reliabiliy LA mode (Transform Precoding Disabled)**: Located on the left.
\* **Basic-Reliabiliy LA mode (Transform Precoding Disabled)**: Located in the center.
\* **Basic-Reliabiliy LA mode (Transform Precoding Enabled)**: Located on the right.
**Transition Conditions (Blue Ellipses):**
\* **PUSCH recovery (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16077) AND UL PDCCH recovery (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17263)**: Located at the top center.
\* **Transform Precoding Enabled to Disabled (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3284)**: Located at the top right.
\* **PUSCH fallback (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16426) OR UL PDCCH fallback (5G\_UP\_ALG\_18296)**: Located at the bottom left.
\* **Transform Precoding Disabled to Enabled (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3282)**: Located at the bottom right.
**Transitions (Arrows):**
\* A green arrow goes from "PUSCH recovery (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16077) AND UL PDCCH recovery (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17263)" to "High-Reliabiliy LA mode (Transform Precoding Disabled)".
\* A green arrow goes from "Transform Precoding Enabled to Disabled (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3284)" to "Basic-Reliabiliy LA mode (Transform Precoding Disabled)".
\* A red arrow goes from "High-Reliabiliy LA mode (Transform Precoding Disabled)" to "PUSCH fallback (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16426) OR UL PDCCH fallback (5G\_UP\_ALG\_18296)".
\* A red arrow goes from "Basic-Reliabiliy LA mode (Transform Precoding Disabled)" to "Transform Precoding Disabled to Enabled (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3282)".
\* A red arrow goes from "Basic-Reliabiliy LA mode (Transform Precoding Enabled)" to "Transform Precoding Disabled to Enabled (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3282)".
The flowchart visually represents how different conditions trigger transitions between various LA modes, with a focus on the state of "Transform Precoding" (enabled or disabled).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3449.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The transition between the disabled and enabled states of Transform precoding, as specified in requirements 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3284\_replaced([11140751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krvNbvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3282\_replaced([11140672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krvNZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), is viable when the User Equipment (UE) operates within the Basic-Reliability link adaptation (BR LA) mode (i.e: UE shifting from High-Reliability Link adaptation (HR LA) to Basic-Reliability link adaptation, in accordance with requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16426\_replaced([11138318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreHrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18296\_replaced([11170717](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3PvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The transitioning between different Link Adaptation (LA) modes and Transform precoding states is illustrated in the above flow chart.

Note: Upon transitioning to the state of Transform precoding being enabled, it is required to discontinue the monitoring of the recovery mechanism outlined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16077\_replaced([11138358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kreusvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17263\_replaced([11170766](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The recovery mechanism is resumed when Transform precoding is disabled, as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3284\_replaced([11140751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krvNbvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/CB008246%20-%205G_L2_Entity_Level%20Module%20-%20Drawings/2.4%20Algorithm/StateMachine_5G_L2_14689.xlsx?d=wa81581a5ce0740488a028649e1cb00ed&csf=1&web=1&e=U0xDVP>

---

#### 2.4.8.4.7 PM Counter Support

##### 2.4.8.4.7.1 High Reliable Link Adaptation specific PM Counters

###### 2.4.8.4.7.1.0-1 High Reliable Link Adaptation specific PM Counters HR uplink LA PM counters (ID: 11164020)

**2.4.8.4.7.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11164026`)

High Reliable Link Adaptation specific PM Counters
For HR LA, gNB extended to support URLLC specific MCS table (3GPP 38.214 §Table 5.1.3.1-3) or also known as LowSE (Low spectral efficiency) MCS table. The below counters are related to PUSCH transmission using LowSE table.

Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15997\_replaced([11230151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kysKBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for detailed description of below PM counter.

1. PUSCH\_INITX\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL1 (M55367C00001)

This PM counter is incremented whenever UE transmits PUSCH using LowSE MCS table and UE configured with a HR DRB corresponding to hrProfile with PuschHrConfig.nrPuschHRLaId = 1 (See 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))). All initial transmissions are considered with MCS index from 0 to 28.

2. PUSCH\_INITX\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL2 (M55367C00003)

Incremented similarly as PUSCH\_INITX\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL1 but here we check if the UE is configured with hrProfile corresponding to PuschHrConfig.nrPuschHRLaId = 2 (See 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

3. PUSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL1 (M55367C00002)

This PM counter is only incremented with successful initial transmissions (HARQ with ACK) of PUSCH using LowSE MCS table and UE with HR DRB corresponding to hrProfile with PuschHrConfig.nrPuschHRLaId = 1 (See 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

4. PUSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL2 (M55367C00004)

Incremented similarly as PUSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_LOWSE\_HRD\_PRFL1 but here we check if the UE is configured with hrProfile corresponding to PuschHrConfig.nrPuschHRLaId = 2 (See 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[CB008246-I] An equivalent of those counters for HR LA for MCS table 64QAM is defined:

1. PUSCH\_INITX\_64TBL\_HRD\_PRFL1 (M55302C00029)

2. PUSCH\_INITX\_64TBL\_HRD\_PRFL2 (M55302C00031)

3. PUSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_64TBL\_HRD\_PRFL1 (M55302C00030)

4. PUSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_64TBL\_HRD\_PRFL2 (M55302C00032)

[End CB008246-I]

Note: If the UE is configured with LowSE MCS table, we need to make sure the legacy PM counters related to PUSCH 64QAM, 256QAM and DFTS-OFDM 64QAM table shouldn't be pegged. The list of legacy MCS table specific counters wrt PUSCH transmission are mentioned in 5G\_PM\_954 under section "Legacy MCS Counters".

Note: These PM counter updates are skipped

[CB008246-L]

for grants described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5074\_replaced([11202363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for UE configured with skippable grants (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4839\_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[End CB008246-L]

[CB008246-J]

when actUlCgType2 is set to "true" and L2-PS ignores DTX received for CG grants as it assumes to be skipped by the UE based on the evaluation of the UL buffer status in L2-PS.

[End CB008246-J]

---

##### 2.4.8.4.7.2 service aware BLER target PM Counters

###### 2.4.8.4.7.2.0-1 service aware BLER target PM Counters service aware BLER target PM counters (ID: 11164037)

**2.4.8.4.7.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11164043`)

service aware BLER target PM Counters
There are two profiles for DRB specific BLER target(NRPUSCHBRLA profile1/profile2) in UL.

Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15997\_replaced([11230151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kysKBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for detailed description of below PM counter.

NRPUSCHBRLA profile1

1. PUSCH\_INI\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL1 (M55370C00042)

This counter is updated when the PUSCH detection result is received for the initial transmission on PUSCH using the DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPUSCHBRLA Profile1 for UL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

2. PUSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL1 (M55370C00043)

This counter is updated when PUSCH CRC-OK is detected for initial transmission on PUSCH using DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPUSCHBRLA Profile1 for UL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

3. PUSCH\_SUCC\_ANY\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL1 (M55370C00044)

This counter is updated when PUSCH CRC-OK is detected for any transmission/retransmission on PUSCH that was initially transmitted using DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPUSCHBRLA Profile1 for UL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

4. PUSCH\_UNSUCC\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL1 (M55370C00045)

This counter is updated when PUSCH isn't successfully decoded for the final HARQ retransmission that was initially transmitted using the DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPUSCHBRLA Profile1 for UL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

NRPUSCHBRLA profile2

1. PUSCH\_INI\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL2 (M55370C00046)

This counter is updated when the PUSCH detection result is received for the initial transmission on PUSCH using the DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPUSCHBRLA Profile2 for UL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

2. PUSCH\_SUCC\_INI\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL2 (M55370C00047)

This counter is updated when PUSCH CRC-OK is detected for initial transmission on PUSCH using DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPUSCHBRLA Profile2 for UL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

3. PUSCH\_SUCC\_ANY\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL2 (M55370C00048)

This counter is updated when PUSCH CRC-OK is detected for any transmission/retransmission on PUSCH that was initially transmitted using DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPUSCHBRLA Profile2 for UL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

4. PUSCH\_UNSUCC\_TX\_BRD\_PRFL2 (M55370C00049)

This counter is updated when PUSCH isn't successfully decoded for the final HARQ retransmission that was initially transmitted using the DRB specific BLER target defined in NRPUSCHBRLA Profile2 for UL Outer Loop Link Adaptation.

---

#### 2.4.8.4.8 Online Reconfiguration of High Reliable UL Link Adaptation

##### 2.4.8.4.8.0-1 Online Reconfiguration of High Reliable UL Link Adaptation HR uplink LA online reconfiguration (ID: 11164053)

**2.4.8.4.8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11164057`)

Online Reconfiguration of High Reliable UL Link Adaptation
There are three different sets of parameters that could be online modifiable wrt HR UL LA:

- HR UL LA Profile: Upon online modification of the NRBTS.NRPUSCHHRLA (defined in requirement 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), in such case CP-RT doesn’t trigger any indication to L2-PS. UE(s) which were already added prior to online modification still retains the old HR profile configuration. New UE(s) added later is configured with updated HR profile by CP-RT.

- Cell NIDD parameter: On modification of NRBTS.NRCELL.puschHrLaConfig parameters (defined in requirement 5G\_L2\_IF\_4431\_replaced([10970149](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UhV4_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then CP-RT triggers PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq towards L2-PS with updated parameters. In such case, L2-PS shall follow steps defined in requirement 5G\_L2\_11689.

- Feature Activation/Deactivation: If the feature is deactivated when a cell is active, then CP-RT triggers PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq towards L2-PS. In such case, L2-PS follows 5G\_L2\_11689.

---

### 2.4.8.5 Test Support

#### 2.4.8.5.0-1 Test Support Set of supported MCS Indices in DL and UL Scheduling (ID: 11164067)

**2.4.8.5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12825521`)

Test Support
The set of MCS indices, which are available in downlink scheduling and uplink scheduling, are defined by the R&D parameter rdSupportedDl64QamMcs (see 5G\_L2\_1019), rdSupportedDl256QamMcs (see 5G\_L2\_1020), [CB008949-A] rdSupportedDl1024QamMcs (see [12825519](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_nGNHQKvzEfCyQasEtWkQ4A?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), [End CB008949-A] rdSupportedUlMcs (see 5G\_L2\_1021 for 64QAM) and rdSupportedUl256QamMcs (see 5G\_L2\_1022), respectively. 5G-L2-PS shall read the R&D parameters during the initialization phase of the base station and shall setup for each transmission direction a list of supported MCS indices. Separate lists shall be setup in DL/UL related to the 64QAM MCS index table, the 256QAM MCS index table [CB008949-A] and the 1024QAM MCS index table (DL only) [End CB008949-A]. The DL/UL list related to the 256QAM MCS index table is [CB008949-A] and the 1024QAM MCS index table (DL only) are [End CB008949-A] only applicable for bands below 6GHz.

List of supported DL MCS indices related to 64QAM MCS index table: ~~rrmSuppDl64QamMcsList~~ rdSupportedDl64QamMcs

List of supported DL MCS indices related to 256QAM MCS index table: ~~rrmSuppDl256QamMcsList~~ rdSupportedDl256QamMcs

[CB008949-A] List of supported DL MCS indices related to 1024QAM MCS index table: rdSuppDl1024QamMcsList [End CB008949-A]

List of supported UL MCS indices related to 64QAM MCS index table: ~~rrmSuppUlMcsList~~ rdSupportedUlMcs

List of supported UL MCS indices related to 256QAM MCS index table: ~~rrmSuppUl256QamMcsList~~ rdSupportedUl256QamMcs

The MCS indices shall be sorted for each list in ascending MCS index order starting with the smallest MCS index.

The lists of supported MCS indices shall be made available for the DL scheduler (~~rrmSuppDl64QamMcsList~~ rdSupportedDl64QamMcs, ~~rrmSuppDl256QamMcsList~~ rdSupportedDl256QamMcs  [CB008949-A] and rdSupportedDl256QamMcs [End CB008949-A]) and UL scheduler (~~rrmSuppUlMcsList~~ rdSupportedUlMcs and ~~rrmSuppUl256QamMcsList~~ rdSupportedUl256QamMcs), respectively.

The rules for configuring the set of supported MCS indices are:

 The set of configured MCS indices is implemented in L1

 Typically, the lists comprise the same set of MCS indices for DL and UL

 Unsymmetrical lists and subset of supported L1 MCS indices are supported as well

Furthermore, the initial MCS defined by dllaIniMcs for DL and ullaIniMcs for UL in PsCell\_CellSetupReq shall be corrected if it is not included in the lists of supported MCSes. For this case 5G-L2-PS shall downgrade the initial MCS defined by dllaIniMcs or ullaIniMcs to the next smaller MCS available in the list of supported MCSes. The smallest available MCS shall be applied if no suitable MCS can be found in the list of available MCSes. The so determined initial MCS shall be used instead of the MCS defined by dllaIniMcs or ullaIniMcs.

## 2.4.9 Rank Adaptation

### 2.4.9.0-1 Rank Adaptation FA: Beamforming/MIMO​ Document Owner: Michael 1. Wang (NSB); Michal Fiejka (Nokia) (ID: 11164089)

### 2.4.9.1 Rank adaptation algorithm

#### 2.4.9.1.0-1 Rank adaptation algorithm Adaptation based on received Rank Indicator (ID: 11164099)

**2.4.9.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11164105`)

Rank adaptation algorithm
For system level requirements and reasoning please see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3210\_replaced([11139690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krpGx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

L2-PS shall perform RI adaptation procedure for RI received in CSI report if during Cell Setup procedure:

- PsCell\_CellSetupReq.dlMimoMode == "OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2" OR "OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4"

And RD flag rd4x4MimoRAEnabled  == true.

Please see following requirements for Rank Indicator reception details:

- PUCCH - 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3626\_replaced([11160044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktN0EfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- PUSCH - 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3657\_replaced([11160598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPCOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Following flags shall be considered by the algorithm:

- rd4x4MimoRAEnabled  (See 5G\_L2\_1265)

- rdLayer4CqiThreshold (See 5G\_L2\_1266)

- rdLayer4DeltaCqiThreshold (See 5G\_L2\_1268)

- rdLayer3DeltaCqiThreshold (See 5G\_L2\_1267)

- rdRank3CqiOffset (See 5G\_L2\_1269)

For CSI report reception please see following messages in L1 IF:

- UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_216\_replaced([11513990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MIfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

### 2.4.9.2 DL Rank Adaptation

#### 2.4.9.2.0-1 DL Rank Adaptation Forced Rank Downgrade at Low CQI and High BLER in FDD (ID: 11164116)

**2.4.9.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11164118`)

DL Rank Adaptation
When the mechanism of forced rank downgrade at low CQI and high BLER is activated (see 5G\_L2\_22708), then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall apply the rank downgrade procedure for the upcoming downlink transmission:

 proceed with further steps only if all the conditions are met:

o processing cell is FDD cell, and,

o UE has no GBR or VoNR DRB configured, and,

o UE is configured with basic reliability **[PR815391](IMS for 5QI-5 is included)**[End PR815391], i.e. high reliability is not configured for any DRB (see 5G\_L2\_11104 for HR DRB),\*\*\*\*

 check the values of related parameters:

o for low CQI threshold, depending on current UE’s rank, i.e. rdLowCqiThresholdRankX where X corresponds to UE’s rank:

 R&D parameter rdLowCqiThresholdRank1, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31518\_replaced([11139047](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krkOQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 R&D parameter rdLowCqiThresholdRank2, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31522\_replaced([11139057](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krkORPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 R&D parameter rdLowCqiThresholdRank3, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31525\_replaced([11139066](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krkORvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 R&D parameter rdLowCqiThresholdRank4, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31527\_replaced([11139078](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krkOSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

o for high CQI threshold, depending on current UE’s rank, i.e. rdHighCqiThresholdRankX where X corresponds to UE’s rank:

 R&D parameter rdHighCqiThresholdRank1, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31529\_replaced([11139089](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krkOSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 R&D parameter rdHighCqiThresholdRank2, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31531\_replaced([11139101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krkOTPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 R&D parameter rdHighCqiThresholdRank3, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31533\_replaced([11139110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krkOTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 R&D parameter rdHighCqiThresholdRank4, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31535\_replaced([11139119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krk1UfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

o for short term BLER thresholds:

 shortTermBlerThreshold1, as received from 5G-CP-RT in DlLinkAdaptation structure (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_103\_replaced([10968509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UYL_Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

 shortTermBlerThreshold2, as received from 5G-CP-RT in DlLinkAdaptation structure (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_103\_replaced([10968509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UYL_Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

o rdBlerAvgCDlUe (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31553\_replaced([11194121](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvym-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and average BLER values for UE level DL BLER,

 check if BLER and CQI thresholds are properly defined, i.e.:

o shortTermBLERThreshold1 shall be greater or equal to shortTermBLERThreshold2, and,

o highCqiThresholdRankX shall be greater or equal to lowCqiThresholdRankX, where X corresponds to UE’s rank,

o if BLER or CQI thresholds are not defined properly, then raise dedicated warning,

 determine if the UE shall enter Rank Downgrade State and skip the BLER check if shortTermBlerThreshold1is 0:

o If:

 measured corrected CQI *is less than rdLowCqiThresholdRankX, defined under related R&D parameter, and,*

 measured BLER is greater than shortTermBlerThreshold1, and,

 minimal number of samples defined in rdBlerMinNumSamplesDl (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31551\_replaced([11194110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvym-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is valid,

o Then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall:

 enter UE into Rank Downgrade State, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9661\_replaced([11139510](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krn4ovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 adjust CQI for downgraded UE as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31666\_replaced([11126236](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9APvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), using related R&D parameter:

 rdCqiAdjustRank2To1 for reduction from rank 2 to rank 1, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31537\_replaced([11139130](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krk1U_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 rdCqiAdjustRank3To2 for reduction from rank 3 to rank 2, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31539\_replaced([11139139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krk1W_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 rdCqiAdjustRank4To2 for reduction from rank 4 to rank 2, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31541\_replaced([11139148](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krk1XfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 If rdI13SelectForRankDown (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31818\_replaced([11141851](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr1UDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to TRUE, then select i13 codeboox index (see 5G\_L2\_15487) in accordance with 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9661\_replaced([11139510](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krn4ovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 determine if the UE shall exit Rank Downgrade State and skip the BLER check if shortTermBlerThreshold2 is 0:

o If:

 measured corrected CQI *is greater or equal rdHighCqiThresholdRankX, defined under related R&D parameter, and,*

 measured BLER is less or equal shortTermBlerThreshold2, and,

 minimal number of samples defined in rdBlerMinNumSamplesDl (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31551\_replaced([11194110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvym-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is valid,

o Then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall exit UE from Rank Downgrade State and revoke CQI adjustment, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9661\_replaced([11139510](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krn4ovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
