###### 2.4.11.1.2.2.0-2 FD UE Selection UE Schedulablity Check (ID: 11165067)

**2.4.11.1.2.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11165072`)

FD UE Selection
UE Schedulablity Check (isUeSchedulable)

---

**2.4.11.1.2.2.0-2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11165088`)

FD UE Selection
If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False The UE schedulability check is follow the defination in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4292\_replaced([11177281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). But when actDlMuMimoEnh=True, the lastRbg availablility check is refer to 5G\_L2\_5520\_replaced([11165093](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25wnnfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and the resource availablity check is refer to 5G\_L2\_4512\_replaced([11165120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else

The UE schedulability check is follow the defination in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4292\_replaced([11177281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the resource availablity check is refer to 5G\_L2\_4512\_replaced([11165120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB010630-E] If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE ,

keep track of the used/remaining layer PRB resources during the process (rrmNumUsedPdschLayerPrbPerSlot/rrmRemainingPdschLayerPrbPerSlot).

The maximum available layer PRB resources is initialised in every slot to the value determined at the beginning of

every pooling period and the layer PRB usage shall be tracked also for PRB based RAT1 allocation scheme described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23663\_replaced([11177504](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB010630-E]

---

**2.4.11.1.2.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11165093`)

FD UE Selection
LastRbg Availablility Check for ReTxUE

---

**2.4.11.1.2.2.0-2.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11165114`)

FD UE Selection
When actDlMuMimoEnh=False and adaptive re-transmission scheme is not applied, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when an reTxUe was allocated with lastRbg(size < normal RBG size P) in it's initialTx, then it's reTx shall allocate with lastRbg(<P) too. So only 1 reTxUe that need lastRbg(<P) is allowed to schedule in an slot, other reTxUe that need lastRbg(<P) is excluded for this slot's scheduling if no lastRbg(<P) available.

When actDlMuMimoEnh=True , for simplify the MU paring, the restriction of lastRbg(<P) is not needed. It means, when adaptive re-transmission scheme is not applied, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), (case a) a reTxUe that allocated with lastRbg(<P) in it's initialTx is not request lastRbg(<P) allocate in it's reTx, vice versa, (case b) a reTxUe that NOT allocated with lastRbg(<P) in it's initialTx is allowed to allocate lastRbg(<P) in it's reTx.

It also means, when adaptive re-transmission scheme is applied, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), (case c) a ReTxUe that would require lastRbg(<P) for optimization wrt its target prb number is allowed to NOT allocate lastRbg(<P).

Note, for (case b), it's may has potential BLER issue and need to be verified in test, and it's can be further improvement in future.

When adaptive re-transmission scheme is not applied, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the number of RBG of reTx shall always be same as it's initialTx.

When the prb number of reTx is different with it's initialTx, the reserved MCS is used, refer to 5G\_L2\_4508\_replaced([11167076](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538XfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When adaptive re-transmission scheme is applied, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the number of RBG of reTx shall be the roundUp(number of PRBs (defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11948\_replaced([11184286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) divided by RBG size P).

Prb allocation of the adaptive retransmission is applied as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11948\_replaced([11184286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and reserved MCS is used as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12911\_replaced([11184825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRpnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB010630-E] when slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed isset to TRUE, the resource allocation are calculated in terms of layer PRBs as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11948\_replaced([11184286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB010630-E]

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_431\_replaced([11097993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km6-1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),  5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Adative retransmission is not applied for configured grant (CG) Harq process according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5079\_replaced([11202406](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**2.4.11.1.2.2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11165120`)

FD UE Selection
Resource Availablity Check

---

**2.4.11.1.2.2.0-2.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `13354948`)

FD UE Selection
If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False

Note that the UE's MU eligibility shall be checked prior to resource availablility check when actDlMuMimoEnh=True, refer to 5G\_L2\_4195\_replaced([11165691](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD4fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

There shall be has enough available PDSCH resource to satifiy the UE's minimum request resource.

The minimum request resouce is follow the defination in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4311\_replaced([11177467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for example:

* ReTx UE: minRequest[Prb|Rbg] is same number of Prb/Rbg as it's initialTx, both for prb and rbg when adaptive re-transmission scheme is not applied, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When adaptive re-transmission scheme is applied, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), minRequest[Prb] is prb target defined by  5G\_UP\_ALG\_12911\_replaced([11184825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRpnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and minRequest[Rbg] is corresponding number of rbg
* Srb UE: minRequest[Prb|Rbg] is the minimum number of [Prb|Rbg] to drain the SRB buffer. Use of prb or rbg is determined by UE's resource allocation type1 or type0.
* Drb UE: minRequest[Prb|Rbg] is the number of *rdFdResPerRoundDl  [prb|Rbg],* rdFdResPerRoundDl = 1 in default, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Use of prb or rbg is determined by UE's resource allocation type1 or type0.\*\*
* Nbr UE: **minRequest[Prb|Rbg] is the minimum resource calculated refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21689\_replaced([11179765](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ERvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**
* ...

For the available resource, following is an example of resource in BWP of normal DL slot:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a "BWP resource" divided into several segments: "allocated", "nonDistributable", "muEligible", "distributable", and "free". The "allocated" segment is black, "nonDistributable" is light blue, "muEligible" is a darker blue with a red border, "distributable" is light blue, and "free" is green. Arrows indicate that the entire span is the "BWP resource", and a bracket above shows "available[Prb | Rbg]". A bracket below indicates "reserved[Prb | Rbg]".
Below the diagram is a legend explaining the terms:
- "allocated": allocated[Prb | Rbg].
- "nonDistributable": reserved[Prb | Rbg] Non Distributable.
- "distributable": reserved [Rbg] Distributable.
- "muEligible": reserved[Rbg] MuEligible, it is consist of non Distributable and distributable RBGs, and inside of "reserved[Rbg] Non Distributable+reserved [Rbg] Distributablable".
- "free": free [Prb | Rbg], = available [Prb | Rbg] - reserved[Prb | Rbg].[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3634.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note, if multiple BWP enabled(actMultipleDedicatedBWPs=True or/and actPowerSavingBWP=True), the allocated/nonDistributable/distributable resource shall consider for each BWPs, and reserved[Rbg]MuEligible resource is only in large BWP.  Example in following picutre:

[xlsx\_desc]--- Sheet1 ---
Empty DataFrame
Columns: []
Index: [][/xlsx\_desc][xlsx\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/attachments/m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file3634\_1.xlsx[/xlsx\_path]

Note, actMultipleDedicatedBWPs and actPowerSavingBWP are OAM parameters received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq.

During the cs2List|toBeScheduledList UE selection in FDM, when a UE been selected and the schedulablity check is passed, it's will allocate or reserve resource, refer to 5G\_L2\_5527\_replaced([11165197](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOp_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For muEligible UE, it's reserved resource is adding to reserved[Rbg]MuEligible, for both reTx and newTx UE.

The UE is resource availablity check pass if it's satifiy:

IF UE is muEligible:

minRequest[Rbg] <= free[Rbg] + reserved[Rbg]MuEligible;

Else:

minReq[Prb|Rbg] <= free[Prb|Rbg];

End

The rational of adding reservedRbgOfMuEligibleUe for muEligible UE is, the RBG reserved by other muEligible UEs is possible to be used again, in case of they are MU paring.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4311\_replaced([11177467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for reTx, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for newTx.

Else

When actDlMuMimoEnh=True then refer 5G\_L2\_15356\_replaced([11177424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_267FM_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for resource availability check.

Note, if multiple BWP enabled (actMultipleDedicatedBWPs=True or/and actPowerSavingBWP=True), the allocated/nonDistributable/distributable resource shall consider for each BWPs, and reserved[prb]MuEligible resource is only in large BWP.  Example in following picture:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR) or a similar technology. It depicts different sub-areas (SubArea #1 and SubArea #0) and Carrier Resource Blocks (CRBs) from #0 to #N.
The diagram is structured with horizontal bands representing different types of resource blocks and their allocations.
**Key elements and their descriptions:**
\* **CRB #0 to CRB #N:** These labels indicate the range of Carrier Resource Blocks being considered.
\* **SubArea #1:** This section is on the left side of the diagram and appears to be an initial allocation.
\* **Initial BWP (Bandwidth Part):** This is a time duration indicated by a bracket, encompassing the initial allocation within SubArea #1.
\* **SA1 Available RB's:** Three horizontal green bands labeled "SA1 Available RB's" occupy the top portion of SubArea #1. These likely represent Resource Blocks available for a specific service or user.
\* **DCI 1\_0:** Light blue boxes labeled "DCI 1\_0" are positioned next to the "SA1 Available RB's". These likely represent Downlink Control Information (DCI) of type 1, version 0, which carries scheduling information.
\* **CC:** Red boxes labeled "CC" are located in the middle of SubArea #1, adjacent to "DCI 1\_0". "CC" could stand for "Control Channel" or "Carrier Component", indicating a specific type of resource.
\* **Allocated due to CC:** An arrow points from this text to the "CC" blocks, suggesting these resources are allocated as a consequence of the "CC" allocation.
\* **SubArea #0:** This section is on the right side of the diagram and appears to represent a subsequent or different allocation.
\* **SAO Available RB's:** A large green band labeled "SAO Available RB's" spans across the top of SubArea #0. This likely represents Resource Blocks available for a different service or user.
\* **SU Eligible:** Yellow boxes labeled "SU Eligible" are present within the "SAO Available RB's" and also in a lower band. This indicates resources that are eligible for scheduling of a "Single User" transmission.
\* **MU Eligible:** A light blue box labeled "MU Eligible" is located within the "SAO Available RB's", indicating resources eligible for "Multi-User" transmission.
\* **FreePrbs:** A grey box labeled "FreePrbs" is at the far right of the "SAO Available RB's", indicating available, unallocated Resource Blocks.
\* **Non-Distributable:** A grey box labeled "Non-Distributable" is located in the middle section of SubArea #0, suggesting resources that cannot be flexibly allocated.
\* **Orange Band:** A large orange band occupies a significant portion of the middle of SubArea #0, below the "SAO Available RB's". Its specific meaning is not explicitly labeled but it appears to be a distinct resource allocation area.
\* **BWP (Bandwidth Part) Indicators:**
\* **PS BWP SA#1:** A horizontal arrow with a label indicates the extent of a "PS BWP" (likely "Primary Secondary" or "Physical Signal" Bandwidth Part) for SubArea #1.
\* **Small BWP SA#1:** Another arrow indicates a "Small BWP" for SubArea #1.
\* **Large BWP SA#1:** A third arrow indicates a "Large BWP" for SubArea #1.
\* **PS BWP SA#0:** Similar indicators for SubArea #0.
\* **Small BWP SA#0:** Similar indicators for SubArea #0.
\* **Large BWP SA#0:** Similar indicators for SubArea #0.
The diagram visually represents how different types of resource blocks (available, control, eligible for SU/MU, free) are organized and allocated across different sub-areas and within different bandwidth parts. The arrows at the bottom indicate the temporal or frequency extent of these bandwidth parts.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3634.003.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Note, actMultipleDedicatedBWPs and actPowerSavingBWP are OAM parameters received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq.

During the cs2List|toBeScheduledList UE selection in FDM, when a UE been selected and the schedulablity check is passed, it will allocate or reserve resource, refer to 5G\_L2\_15358\_replaced([11177444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_267FNfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For muEligible UE, it's reserved resource is adding to reserved[prb]MuEligible, for both reTx and newTx UE.

The UE is resource availablity check pass if it's satisfy:

IF UE is muEligible [PR867736] or LargeBwp and Priority Class as N-GBR Priority [End PR867736]:

minRequest[prb] <= free[prb] + reserved[prb]MuEligible;

Else:

minReq[Prb] <= free[Prb];

End

The rational of adding reservedPrbOfMuEligibleUe for muEligible UE [PR867736] or LargeBwp and Priority Class as N-GBR Priority [End PR867736] is, the PRB reserved by [Before PR867736]other [PR867736] muEligible UEs are possible to be used again, in case of they are MU paring.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23663\_replaced([11177504](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for reTx, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for newTx.

If powerboost functionality enabled then

If MU UE's are eligible for Power Boost which is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_ 25136, along with required prb calculation , power boost PRB's also shall be calculated according to powerBoostScalingFactor.

Let’s take example, required PRB's are 6 and powerBoostScalingFactor 1.5 then total required PRB's are 6 x 1.5 = 9 PRB's .

If total remaining PRB's are equal to 6 ,such case power boosting shall not be applied otherwise remaining PRB's are greater than 6 then either partially or fully power boost shall be applied.

Note: Please refer 5G\_L2\_22946\_replaced([11177475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_267FOfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for available PRBs calculation

---

###### 2.4.11.1.2.2.0-3 FD UE Selection Resource Allocation or Reservation (ID: 11165197)

**2.4.11.1.2.2.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `11165231`)

FD UE Selection
**NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False**

After the schedulablity check pass for a UE as defined in 5G\_L2\_4511\_replaced([11165067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25wnl_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the resource is going to allocate or reserve for the UE as follows:

* Initial Bwp UE:
* allocate resource according to to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), same as actDlMuMimoEnh=False case.
* ReTx UE:
* type 0 UE:
* muEligible UE: reserve muEligible resource:

reserve[Rbg] = min(free[Rbg], request[Rbg]);

reserved[Rbg]NonDistributable += reserve[Rbg];

reserved[Rbg]MuEligible += reserve[Rbg].

Note,

a) request[Rbg] is equal to minRequst[Rbg] as defined in 5G\_L2\_4512\_replaced([11165120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For muEligible UE, it's will reserve request[Rbg] if free[Rbg] is enough, of else it's allow to reserve less RBG or 0 RBG because it has possiblility that this UE allocate with RBGs that reserved by other muEligble UEs if they are going to MU paring.

b) allowedRbgUe = request[Rbg], which is used in MU pairing.

* other UE (non-muEligible): allocate resource of request[Rbg], the request[Rbg] is equal to minRequest[Rbg] as defined in 5G\_L2\_4512\_replaced([11165120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note, due to the lastRbg limitation is removed when actDlMuMimoEnh=True as defined in 5G\_L2\_5520\_replaced([11165093](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25wnnfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the RBG allocated in lowToHigh ascend order, so that the lastRbg is only possible to be allocated in last allocation (the last allocation UE will be a newTx UE in most case).

* type 1 UE:
* reserve nonDistributable resource:

reserved[Prb]NonDistributable += request[Prb], same as actDlMuMimoEnh=False case. The request[Prb] is equal to minRequest[Prb] as defined in 5G\_L2\_4512\_replaced([11165120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* Srb UE:
* allocate with request[Prb|Rbg], refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Priority bearer UE (priority > nonGbr, e.g., 5QI5, 5QI1**[CB008118-A] ,5QI69/5QI65/5QI66** [End CB008118-A]):\*\*\*\*
* reserve nonDistributable resource:

reserved[Rbg]NonDistributable += min(free[Rbg], request[Rbg]), the request[Rbg] is minimum RBG to drain the priority bearers' buffer, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* NBR bearer UE (priority =NBR):

reserve[Rbg] = min(free[Rbg], request[Rbg]), the request[Rbg] is RBG calculated to drain the NBR bearers' buffer, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

reserved[Rbg]Distributable += reserve[Rbg];

IF muEligible UE: reserve muEligble resource:

reserved[Rbg]MuEligible += reserve[Rbg];

Note, it's possible that the muEligible NBR UE reserves RBG for bearer again in MU WRR based on available RBGs and needed resources

* normal bearer UE (priority =nonGbr):

reserve[Rbg] = min(free[Rbg], number of *rdFdResPerRoundDl  Rbg);*

reserved[Rbg]Distributable += reserve[Rbg];

IF muEligible UE: reserve muEligble resource too:

reserved[Rbg]MuEligible += reserve[Rbg];

Note, it's possible that the muEligble UE reserve 0 RBG and this is allowed.

The resource reservation/allocation is in the UE's current active BWP if multiple BWPs enabled.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4500\_replaced([11179300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku4oDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**.**

Else

After the schedulability check pass for a UE as defined in 5G\_L2\_4511\_replaced([11165067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25wnl_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_15356\_replaced([11177424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_267FM_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), Refer 5G\_L2\_15358\_replaced([11177444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_267FNfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for resource reserve for the UE.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a wireless communication system. It depicts a timeline or spectrum divided into several segments, each representing a different type of resource. These segments are labeled as "nonDistributable", "Allocated", "Non-Distributable", "SU Distributable (SU-WRR)", "MU Distributable (MU-WRR)", and "Free Prbs". Arrows point to specific segments, indicating their relevance to the accompanying text.
Below the timeline, there are two distinct text boxes, each describing a process for updating reserved non-distributable resources.
The first text box, associated with "Initial BWP UE", outlines the following steps:
- Select SubArea within Initial BWP
- Reserve[prb] = min(free[PRB], request[PRB])
- Reserved[PRB] Non Distributable += Reserve[prb]
- Update Remaining PRB's Selected SA
The second text box, associated with "Dedicated BWP UE", describes a more complex process:
- Calculate Free[PRB] Resource in SA = (total subarea size - total allocated in Initial BWP + total reserved in Initial BWP + total reserved in dedicated BWP)
- If is MuEligibleUe = True:
- rserved[prb]MuEligible += rserved[prb];
- Else:
- Reserve[prb] = min(free[PRB], request[PRB])
- Reserved[PRB] Non Distributable += Reserve[prb]
- Update Remaining PRB's Selected SA
The diagram uses a combination of visual elements (colored blocks, arrows) and textual descriptions to explain the resource management logic for different types of User Equipment (UE) and resource categories.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3640.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

In a slot where UE has MAC-UE and/or with GBR and/or with N-GBR buffers , MAC-CE and GBR Reservation shall happen through Non-Distributable Resource and N-GBR bearer Reservation shall happen during SU-WRR Procedue through Distributable Resource and for N-GBR MuEligible bearer reservation shall happen during MU-WRR through MuEligible resources **[CB014861-B]**or during **Exhaustive DL Scheduling (if NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to DL or DLAndUL,refer to [12483324: Exhaustive Scheduling for RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qbsGEQ-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).**[End CB014861-B]***\****\*

**[CB009849-C]**

NRCELL.actRat1Alloc either True or False

Note 1: request[Rbg/Prb] shall be computed based on corrected proposedDlMcs and Rank when actDlForceRankDowng is set to dlVoNRSrbVoNRCtrl/dlVonrSrbVoNRCtrNonGbrBr /dlVoNROnly.(Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30473\_replaced([11126067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32930\_replaced([11125480](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvH0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note 2: proposedDlMcs and Rank shall be limited based on highest priority service class when multiple bearers buffer muliplexed within TBS ( SRB > 5QI5 > 5QI1) .

**[End CB009849-C]**

---

##### 2.4.11.1.2.3 Resource Distribution and Allocation

###### 2.4.11.1.2.3.0-1 Resource Distribution and Allocation Distribute Resources (FDM WRR) (ID: 11165243)

**2.4.11.1.2.3.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12563007`)

Resource Distribution and Allocation
If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False

WRR(Weighted Round Robin) algorithm is used for newTx nonGbr resource distribution, which is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When actDlMuMimoEnh=True, 2 stages for WRR resource distributioni:

* FDM WRR: it's resource distribution in FDM, only non-muEligible UE considered, same as actDlMuMimoEnh=False case. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* MU WRR: it's resource distribution after reTx UE scheduling in MU pairing procedure, only muEligble UE considered. Refer to 5G\_L2\_4604\_replaced([11166710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HLPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Before FDM WRR, the number of distributable resource shall be calculated:

* available[Rbg]Distributable = available[Rbg] - reserved[Rbg]NonDistributable,  it's total distributable resource.
* ~~available[Rbg]DistributableFdm = available[Rbg]Distributable \* weightSumNonMuEligibleUe/weightSum.~~
* available[Rbg]DistributableFdm = max(available[Rbg]Distributable \* weightSumNonMuEligibleUe/weightSum, numOfUeFdmWrr\**rdFdResPerRoundDl ).*

where,

* weightSum: is the sum of weight of all schedulable nonGbr UEs in cs2List|toBeScheduledList.
* weightSumNonMuEligble: is the sum of weight of all schedulable nonGbr non-muEligible UEs.
* numOfUeFdmWrr: number UEs involved in FDM WRR, there are non-muEligible UEs which has reserved resource in reserved[Rbg]Distributable.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating BWP (Bandwidth Part) resource allocation. The top portion shows a horizontal bar labeled "BWP resource" which is divided into five segments: "allocated" (black), "nonDistributable" (blue), "muEligible" (blue with red border), "distributable" (blue), and "free" (green). Above this bar, "available[Prb | Rbg]" is indicated, spanning across the "nonDistributable", "muEligible", "distributable", and "free" segments.
Below the main resource bar, a legend explains the different segments:
- "allocated": allocated[Prb | Rbg].
- "nonDistributable": reserved[Prb | Rbg] NonDistributable.
- "distributable": reserved[Rbg]Distributable.
- "muEligible": reserved[Rbg]MuEligible.
- "free": free [Prb | Rbg]
Further down, there's a secondary diagram showing a breakdown of "available[Rbg]Distributable". This is represented by a smaller horizontal bar divided into two segments: "fdmWrr Res" (cyan) and "muWrr Res" (dark blue). The legend provides definitions for these:
- "fdmWrr Res": available [Rbg]DistributableFdm, for FDM WRR.
- "muWrr Res": available[Rbg]DistributableMu, for MU WRR, it's will be updated after reTxUe MU, refer to 5G\_L2\_4604.
The overall presentation is clear and uses color-coding to differentiate resource types. The background is a light yellow.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3645.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The FDM WRR resource distribution is based on 1) available[Rbg]DistributableFdm and 2) the UE list of all non-muEligble nonGbr UEs, follow the algorithm defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The FDM WRR is in the UE's current active BWP if multiple BWPs enabled, and shall not exceed available[Rbg]DistributableFdm for all BWPs.

Else //RAT1

**[CB014861-B]For Exhaustive DL Scheduling (NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to DL or DLAndUL),**

exhaustive scheduling algorithm is used for newTx nonGbr resource distribution/allocation, refer to [12483324: Exhaustive Scheduling for RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qbsGEQ-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

else //WRR **[End CB014861-B]**

WRR(Weighted Round Robin) algorithm is used for newTx nonGbr resource distribution, which is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When actDlMuMimoEnh=True, 2 stages for WRR resource distribution:

* SU WRR: it's resource distribution in FDM, only non-muEligible UE considered, same as actDlMuMimoEnh=False case.
* MU WRR: it's resource distribution after reserved the resources for reTx MU UEs and only muEligble new Tx UEs are considered.

Please refer to 5G\_L2\_4604\_replaced([11166710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HLPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_15363\_replaced([11177481](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_267FOvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: If DL slice aware scheduler is active (*sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation is set to true (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))), then UE weights that make up weightSumNonMuEligibleUe & weightSum must be multiplied by slice weight (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23761\_replaced([11179826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7rUvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).*

###### 2.4.11.1.2.3.0-2 Resource Distribution and Allocation Allocate Resources (ID: 11165277)

**2.4.11.1.2.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11165289`)

Resource Distribution and Allocation
[CB008927-P] If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False [End CB008927-P] All UEs with resource reserved are allocated with resource in this step, except muEligble UEs. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for resource allocation.

When actDlMuMimoEnh=Ture, the restriction for lastRbg is not needed, resource allocated is lowToHigh.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008927-P] Else

For MuEligible Ues allocation please refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_15322\_replaced([11177767](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_268TXvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008927-P]

#### 2.4.11.1.3 MU Scheduling

##### 2.4.11.1.3.1 General

###### 2.4.11.1.3.1.0-1 General Virtual UE Concept (ID: 11165305)

**2.4.11.1.3.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11165326`)

General
[Before CNI-109641-K]

Virtual UE includes a group UEs which are paired in spatial domain and share the same RBG allocation. In a virtual UE, the first added UE is its root UE. One UE could be assigned to multiple virtual UEs.

Some examples of virtual UE are listed as below. In the first case, one virtual UE includes two UEs and two RBGs are allocated to them. In the second case, one virtual UE includes three UEs and three RBGs are allocated to them. In the third case, one virtual UE only includes one UE and two RBGs are allocated to it. It’s the case that a root UE cannot be paired with any MU candidate.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains three tables. Each table has a header row and multiple data rows. The header row contains the label "RBG" and numerical values. The data rows contain labels like "UE1", "UE2", and "UE3". Some cells in the tables are filled with a light green color.
Table 1:

| RBG | 0 | 1 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| UE1 |  |  |
| UE2 |  |  |
| Table 2: |  |  |

| RBG | 0 | 1 | 2 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| UE1 |  |  |  |
| UE2 |  |  |  |
| UE3 |  |  |  |
| Table 3: |  |  |  |

| RBG | 0 | 1 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| UE1 |  |  |

The size of virtual UE, virtualUeSizeA, represents the number of paired UE in virtual UE.

The layer number of virtual UE, layerNumVirtualUeA, is the sum of PDSCH layers of paired UEs in virtual UE.

[End CNI-109641-K]

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7444\_replaced([11142531](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.1.0-2 General Rank Downgade (ID: 11165331)

**2.4.11.1.3.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11165347`)

General
The UE using rank2 MU scheduling may has better performance than rank3/4 MU/SU scheduling in some case according to simulation, so rank downgrade is introduced for newTx to downgrade rank3/4 UE to rank2 UE for MU pairing. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7563\_replaced([11142677](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5ldPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Two type of rank is defined for muEligible UE:

* rankSuUe(i): is the DL rank of SU MIMO transmisison for UE(i), refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3163\_replaced([11139204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krlcZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* rankMuUe(i): is the DL rank of MU MIMO transmission for UE(i), it's shall be determine as below:

If dlMuMimoRankAdaption = 0(disabled):

rankMuUe(i) = rankSuUe(i);

ElseIf dlMuMimoRankAdaption = 1(always):

rankMuUe(i) = min(rankSuUe(i), 2);

ElseIf dlMuMimoRankAdaption = 2(adaptive):

[Before PR647623]

If rankSuUe(i)=4 And rrmAvgCqi >rdRankdowngradeCqiThd Then

rankMuUe(i) = rankSuUe(i);

Else

rankMuUe(i) = min(rankSuUe(i), 2);

End

[PR647623]

rankMuUe(i) = min(rankSuUe(i), 2);

[End PR647623]

End

Where,

* dlMuMimoRankAdaption: is NIDD parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.
* rdRankdowngradeCqiThd: is RD parameter define the CQI threshold for rank downgrade, default is 10. Refer to 5G\_L2\_5015\_replaced([11186782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_277x3vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* rrmAvgCqi: is average WB CQI defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7557\_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* muEligible: refer to 5G\_L2\_4196\_replaced([11165697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD6PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The rankSuUe(i) and rankMuUe(i) are calculated when UE(i) is add to rrmMuCandidateList, refer to 5G\_L2\_4196\_replaced([11165697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD6PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The UE(i)'s final PDSCH transmision rank, rankUe(i), is determined when it's first virtualUe(A) generation finished:

If UE(i)'s first virtualUe(A)'s virtualUeSize<2, then

rankUe(i) = rankSuUe(i); //the UE(i) is no possible for MU pairing in this slot. Example case, UE(i) is rootUe and can not pair with any other UE.

Else

rankUe(i) = rankMuUe(i); //the UE(i) is going to be MU UE.

End

If UE(i) is scheduled with rank downgraded (rankMuUe(i)<rankSuUe(i), rankMuUe(i) used) and rank2 used, then only 1 best beam is used. Following items shall be update accordingly:

* txPower: set accoding to rank2 defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2565\_replaced([11118485](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDLVvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2567\_replaced([11118493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDLW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) first, and then considering the MU txPowerFactor defined in 5G\_L2\_4767\_replaced([11167010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538V_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* MU SINR: The SU-SINR(rrmSinrUeFactori) in MU-SINR formula shall be compensated due to rank downgrade, refer to 5G\_L2\_4998\_replaced([11165581](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25yczfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* precoding: use 2x2 MIMO precoding codebook, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3175\_replaced([11139352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmqhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The origianl 8x4 or 4x4 codebook i1 is not used, only i2 is used and covert to 2x2 codebookIndex as following mapping table:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| i2 in PMI report (see tables 5.2.2.2.1-7 and 5.2.2.2.1-8 in 38.214) | Codebook index (see the table 5.2.2.2.1-1 in 38.214) |
| 0 | 0 |
| 1 | 1 |

Informative: following diagram illustrate how the precoding changed:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a diagram illustrating the process of downgrading a 4-port CSI-RS (Channel State Information Reference Signal) and PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) transmission from 3 or 4 layers to 2 layers.
The diagram is divided into two main sections, representing the initial state and the downgraded state.
**Top Section (Initial State):**
\* A base station (represented by an antenna icon) transmits CSI-RS and PDSCH.
\* **UE 4Port CSIRS: SSB Beam or SRS DFT Beam W₀**: This indicates that the UE receives CSI-RS using either an SSB (Synchronization Signal Block) beam or an SRS (Sounding Reference Signal) DFT beam, denoted by W₀. The beams are labeled as [B₀] (best beam) and [B₁] (second best beam).
\* **UE PDSCH(3 or 4 layers) Beam: W₀(B₀ and B₁) \* W₄Port\_1 \* W₄Port\_2**: The PDSCH is transmitted with 3 or 4 layers, using a beam formed by W₀, considering both B₀ and B₁, and further precoded by W₄Port\_1 and W₄Port\_2.
\* **UE report 3 or 4 layer PMI: W₄Port**: The UE reports a PMI (Precoding Matrix Indicator) for 3 or 4 layers, represented by W₄Port. This report is sent to the base station, indicated by a green arrow.
**Middle Section (Downgrade Process):**
\* A blue arrow points downwards, indicating "PDSCH downgrade from 3 or 4 layers (2 Beams) to 2 layers (1 Beam)".
**Bottom Section (Downgraded State):**
\* A base station (represented by an antenna icon) transmits PDSCH.
\* **UE PDSCH(2 layers) Beam: W₀(B₀) \* W₂Port**: The PDSCH is now transmitted with 2 layers, using a beam formed by W₀ (specifically considering the best beam B₀) and precoded by W₂Port.
\* A mobile device (UE) receives the PDSCH.
**Legend:**
The legend provides definitions for the terms used in the diagram:
\* **W₀**: The beam weight for UE-specific 4-port CSIRS. It specifies that if `actDISrsBeam=True` and the UE has 2 valid SRS beams, the SRS beam is used; otherwise, the SSB/Refined beam is used. B₀ is the best beam, and B₁ is the second best beam.
\* **W₄Port**: The precoding matrix of the UE report PMI based on 4-port CSIRS. It refers to specific tables in standard 38214 for 3 and 4 layers. W₄Port\_1 is the 2D DFT beam part (i₁), and W₄Port\_2 is the co-phase (phase offset) for 2 polarizations part (i₂).
\* **W₂Port**: The precoding matrix of the UE PMI based on 2-port CSIRS, referring to standard 38214 Table 5.2.2.2.1-1. The codebook index is deduced from i₂ of W₄Port when layer downgrade occurs.
The diagram visually explains how the PDSCH transmission is simplified from multiple layers and beams to a single layer and beam configuration, along with the corresponding changes in the precoding matrices and beam selection.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3655.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image depicts a diagram illustrating the process of 8-port CSI-RS (Channel State Information Reference Signal) and PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) downgrading from 3 or 4 layers to 2 layers. The diagram shows three scenarios, each originating from a base station antenna and directed towards a user equipment (UE) device.
The first scenario shows "Cell 8Port CSIRS: Beam Wo" being transmitted, with a blue beam labeled "Wo" and a purple beam labeled "[Bi]". A green arrow indicates "UE report 3 or 4 layers PMI: WsPort". The UE PDSCH is shown with a beam described as "UE PDSCH(3 or 4 layers) Beam: WsPort\_1 ⊗ Wo \* WsPort\_2".
The second scenario illustrates a downgrade where "SRS beam used instead of PMI beam (actDISrsBm=True and UE has 2 valid SRS beams)". The PDSCH beam is then described as "UE PDSCH(3 or 4 layers) Beam: WsrsBeam(bo and b₁) \* WsPort\_2". A blue arrow indicates "PDSCH downgrade from 3 or 4 layer (2 Beams) to 2layer (1 Beam)".
The third scenario shows the final downgraded state: "UE PDSCH(2 layers) Beam: WsrsBeam(bo) \* W2Port".
A legend at the bottom explains the notation used in the diagram:
- **Wo**: The beam weight for Cell specific 8Port CSIRS, where the beam Bi is configured and is the same beam for all 8 ports.
- **WsPort**: The precoding matrix of UE report PMI based on 8Port CSIRS. It refers to 38214 Table 5.2.2.2.1-7 for 3 layers and 5.2.2.2.1-8 for 4 layers. Wsport\_1 is the 2D DFT beam part (i1), and Wsport\_2 is the co-phase (phase offset) for 2 polarizations part (i2).
- **WsrsBeam**: The beam based on SRS, where bo is the best beam and b₁ is the second best beam.
- **W2Port**: The precoding matrix of UE PMI based on 2Port CSIRS, referring to 38214 Table 5.2.2.2.1-1. The codebook index is deduced from i₂ of WsPort when layer downgrade occurs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3655.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7563\_replaced([11142677](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5ldPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.1.0-3 General Rank Downgrade for ZF_MU (ID: 12567357)

**2.4.11.1.3.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13351982`)

General
5G\_L2\_PS shall decide the rank down paring for ZF-MU only  based on [ Before CB012971-F-UPD002] rdDlZfMuDisableRankDowngrade ,  [CB012971-F-UPD002]   Two UEs' correlation [End CB012971-F-UPD002] ,  without considering NIDD NRCELL/NRDLMUMIMO/dlMuMimoRankAdaption.

[ Before CB012971-F-UPD002] with rdDlZfMuDisableRankDowngrade =True, [End CB012971-F-UPD002] the max number of UEs can be added to a paring group , please refer [11142576: Overview](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-ZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

[ Before CB012971-F-UPD002] 5G\_L2\_PS shall  down grade to rank2 to multiplex sub band beamforming UEs in case of rdDlZfMuDisableRankDowngrade=false. [ End CB012971-F-UPD002]

Because L2PS isn't aware the final ZF-MU beam weights, the MU-SINR can't be estimated very correctly, so it can't be precise to decide if high rank SU (layer 3/4 ) or lower layer MU ( rank2) performance is better based on spectrum efficiency checking (see [11166474: Spectrum Efficiency Check](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_251gHvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).  Then L2\_PS shall check the [Before CB012971-F-UPD002] average rank for in only two UEs case, please see [12568847: Only two UEs paring with high rank for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YtX0kiy_EfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for detail [CB012971-UPD002] correlation of two UEs' in only two UEs case , please see [12568847: Only two UEs paring for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YtX0kiy_EfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) . [End CB012971-F-UPD002]

[Before CB012971-F-UPD002]

IF rdDlZfMuDisableRankDowngrade=True

rankMuUe(i)=rankSuUe(i)

ELSE rdDlZfMuDisableRankDowngrade=False

If  average rank for in only two UEs case is executed and rank downgrade ([12568847: Only two UEs paring for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YtX0kiy_EfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) is decided or more than two UEs cases

rankMuUe(i)=min( 2, rankSuUe(i) )

Else Two UEs will be  forced to SU scheduling.

EndIf

ENDIF

[CB012971-F-UPD002]

- If number of paired UEs in the same pairing group is more than 2, the rank downgrade shall be always enabled.

- If number of paired UEs in the same pairing group is 2, the rank downgrade shall be disabled if {(correlation value is NOT higher than rdDlMuZfTwoUePairingCorrelation ([13322074: rdDlMuZfTwoUePairingCorrelation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HGM-8dEVEfCVOOJMfwsFiw))) AND (numbers of valid SRS ports on all SRS subbands are not less than rankSuUe(i)) }, otherwise rank downgrade shall be enabled.

[End CB012971-F-UPD002]

Where Two type of rank is defined for muEligible UE:

* rankSuUe(i): is the DL rank of SU MIMO transmisison for UE(i), refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3163\_replaced([11139204](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krlcZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* rankMuUe(i): is the DL rank of MU MIMO transmission for UE(i)

CP2 reference:[11142677: DL rank adaption](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5ldPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.1.0-4 General Only two UEs paring for ZF_MU (ID: 12568847)

**2.4.11.1.3.1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `13351984`)

General
In only two UEs ZF-MU case, [Before CB012971-F-UPD002 ] that UEs are in high SU-MIMO rank [End CB012971-F-UPD002] . To avoid possible rank down grade for ZF\_MU  paring([12567357: Rank Downgrade for ZF-MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_W7elgCyFEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) , [Before CB012971-F-UPD002] The average of two UEs' SU-MIMO rank [CB012971-F-UPD002] The two UEs' correlation shall be checked , and SRS quality table shall be checked with UE's rankSuUe(i)  [End CB012971-F-UPD002] is checked first to decide if those two UEs can be ZF\_MU or not.

[Before CB012971-F-UPD002]

~~IF the only two UEs' average rank is checked in adding UEs to CS1 phase, this will decide if the UE is ZF\_MU eligible or not.~~

IF the only two UEs' average rank is checked in the ZF\_MU virtual UE construction phase, this will decide those two ZF\_MU eligible UEs can be paired or not.

[CB012971-F-UPD002]

- If number of paired UEs in the same pairing group is 2, the rank downgrade shall be disabled if {(correlation value is NOT higher than rdDlMuZfTwoUePairingCorrelation ([13322074: rdDlMuZfTwoUePairingCorrelation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HGM-8dEVEfCVOOJMfwsFiw))) AND (numbers of valid SRS ports on all SRS subbands are not less than rankSuUe(i)) }, otherwise rank downgrade shall be enabled.

[End CB012971-F-UPD002]

see  [12571009: ZF\_Mu Candidate List](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_m4HnYS8BEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for ZF MU eligible capability checking.

see [12567357: Rank Downgrade for ZF-MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_W7elgCyFEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for Rank downgrade for ZF\_MU

see [11139204: DL SU-MIMO](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krlcZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for UE's rank.

CP2 reference: [11144070: Virtual UE construction](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.1.0-5 General Beamforming Gain (ID: 11165354)

**2.4.11.1.3.1.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `13438616`)

General
Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7442\_replaced([11143159](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the DL BF gain of UE(i) with beam w can be calculated with the below equation:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$G\_{i,w} = |w^T h\_i|^2 = (w^T h\_i)(w^T h\_i)^H = w^T h\_i h\_i^H w^ *= w^H h\_i^* h\_i^T w = w^H R\_i w$
The equation defines a variable $G\_{i,w}$ and shows its equivalence to several other mathematical expressions. It involves vector notation ($w$, $h\_i$), transpose ($^T$), conjugate transpose ($^H$), complex conjugate ($^\*$), and a norm squared ($|$.$|^2$). $R\_i$ appears to be a matrix.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

* w is vector of beamforming weight.
* [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, stylized rendering of the letters "W" and "H" with "H" appearing as a superscript to "W". The letters are rendered in a gradient of blue, orange, and yellow, with a white outline and a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] is the Hermit transform of w.
* [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a stylized letter "R" with a subscript "i". The letter "R" is rendered in a gradient of colors, with shades of purple, blue, and orange. The subscript "i" is smaller and appears to be in a similar color scheme. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] is short-term CoMa of UE(i).
* [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, abstract graphic that appears to be a stylized representation of a bar chart or a series of vertical bars. The colors are muted and blend into each other, with shades of orange, blue, and yellow visible. There is a faint, blurry subscript "i" to the right of the graphic. The overall impression is one of movement or fluidity, with the colors bleeding into one another.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is channel coefficient vector of UE(i) which is measured over periodic SRS.

Considering L1 reports short-term CoMa on horizontal and vertical directions separately, L2PS uses IIR filter to calculate long-term CoMa, and DL beams are oversampled 2D-DFT beams, then the DL BF gain can be approximately calculated as below equation:

[image-4][image\_desc]The image contains three lines of mathematical equations.
The first line is:
$G\_{i,w\_h} = w\_h^H R\_i w\_h \approx w\_h^H \tilde{R}*{i,h} w\_h$
The second line is:
$G*{i,w\_v} = w\_v^H R\_i w\_v \approx w\_v^H \tilde{R}*{i,v} w\_v$
The third line is:
$G*{i,w} = G\_{i,w\_h} G\_{i,w\_v}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

* [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the letters "W" and "y" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of blue, orange, and yellow, and have a slight blur effect. The background is white, and the letters are outlined in black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] is vertical DFT beam of the beam [image-6][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry, and abstract representation of the letter "W". The colors are a mix of blues, oranges, and yellows, creating a somewhat iridescent or prismatic effect. The edges of the letter are soft and indistinct due to the blurriness. The background is a solid white, with a dark, blurred border around the entire image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6].
* [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the letters "W" and "h" in a stylized font. The letters appear to be in a gradient of colors, transitioning from a warm orange to a cool blue. The background is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7] is horizontal DFT beam of the beam [image-8][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry, and abstract representation of the letter "W". The colors are a mix of blues, oranges, and yellows, creating a somewhat iridescent or prismatic effect. The edges of the letter are soft and indistinct due to the blurriness. The background is a solid white, with a dark, blurred border around the entire image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.007.png[/image\_path][/image-8].
* [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical symbol. It is a capital letter R with a tilde above it, and subscripts i and v below and to the right of the R. The symbol is rendered in a blurry, stylized font with chromatic aberration, giving it a slightly iridescent or rainbow-like effect.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.009.png[/image\_path][/image-9]: long-term CoMa of UE(i) on vertical direction.
* [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is written in a stylized font with a slight blur and chromatic aberration, giving it a colorful, almost holographic appearance. The main element is a capital letter "R" with a tilde above it, indicating a conjugate or a related quantity. Below and to the right of the "R", there are two subscripted indices: "i" and "h". The "i" is positioned slightly lower than the "h". The overall expression appears to be a variable or a parameter in a mathematical context, possibly related to physics or engineering.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.010.png[/image\_path][/image-10]: long-term CoMa of UE(i) on horizontal direction.

During the DL SRS beam selection(5G\_UP\_ALG\_6995\_replaced([11156011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksy9U_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the [image-11][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Gi,wh" in a dark blue, blurry font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.011.png[/image\_path][/image-11]  and [image-12][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Gᵢ,wᵥ" in a dark blue, slightly blurry font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3657.012.png[/image\_path][/image-12] has been calculated, a table **rrmBfGainTable is defined to store all the G for a UE. So then the UE's G corresponding to each w can be fast calculated.  
[CB015912-A]  
When a DL SRS beam selection is completed and the R&D flag CAL\_CORRELATION\_DETAIL is set, then the 5G-L2-PS shall log the rrmBfGainTable to Syslog for the purpose of offline ML retraining (see [13375160](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_4Uv5hvwZEfCPDo8cGBoqIw)).  
[End CB015912-A]**

[image-13][image\_desc]The image displays two matrices side-by-side, illustrating a mathematical operation. Above the matrices, an equation is presented: $G\_{i,w\_{h1,v2}} = G\_{i,w\_{h1}} \* G\_{i,w\_{v2}}$. This equation indicates that an element in the first matrix is multiplied by an element in the second matrix to obtain a result, though the result matrix is not explicitly shown.
The left matrix has rows labeled $w\_{h0}, w\_{h1}, w\_{h2}, \dots, w\_{hj}, \dots$ and columns labeled $UE\_0, UE\_1, UE\_2, \dots, UE\_i, \dots$. The entries in this matrix are of the form $G\_{k,w\_{hx}}$, where $k$ is the column index (e.g., 0, 1, 2, i) and $w\_{hx}$ is the row label. The element $G\_{i,w\_{h1}}$ is highlighted with a red border.
The right matrix has rows labeled $w\_{v0}, w\_{v1}, w\_{v2}, \dots, w\_{vj}, \dots$ and columns labeled $UE\_0, UE\_1, UE\_2, \dots, UE\_i, \dots$. The entries in this matrix are of the form $G\_{k,w\_{vy}}$, where $k$ is the column index and $w\_{vy}$ is the row label. The element $G\_{i,w\_{v2}}$ is highlighted with a red border.
Two blue arrows originate from the equation and point to specific elements in the matrices. One arrow points from $G\_{i,w\_{h1}}$ in the equation to the highlighted element $G\_{i,w\_{h1}}$ in the left matrix. The other arrow points from $G\_{i,w\_{v2}}$ in the equation to the highlighted element $G\_{i,w\_{v2}}$ in the right matrix.
The matrices appear to represent some form of channel gains or related quantities in a communication system, with $UE\_k$ representing User Equipment and $w$ representing some form of weights or indices.
Here is the table representation of the left matrix:

|  | $UE\_0$ | $UE\_1$ | $UE\_2$ | $\dots$ | $UE\_i$ | $\dots$ |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| $w\_{h0}$ | $G\_{0,w\_{h0}}$ | $G\_{1,w\_{h0}}$ | $G\_{2,w\_{h0}}$ | $\dots$ | $G\_{i,w\_{h0}}$ | $\dots$ |
| $w\_{h1}$ | $G\_{0,w\_{h1}}$ | $G\_{1,w\_{h1}}$ | $G\_{2,w\_{h1}}$ | $\dots$ | $G\_{i,w\_{h1}}$ | $\dots$ |
| $w\_{h2}$ | $G\_{0,w\_{h2}}$ | $G\_{1,w\_{h2}}$ | $G\_{2,w\_{h2}}$ | $\dots$ | $G\_{i,w\_{h2}}$ | $\dots$ |
| $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ |
| $w\_{hj}$ | $G\_{0,w\_{hj}}$ | $G\_{1,w\_{hj}}$ | $G\_{2,w\_{hj}}$ | $\dots$ | $G\_{i,w\_{hj}}$ | $\dots$ |
| $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ |
| Here is the table representation of the right matrix: |  |  |  |  |  |  |

|  | $UE\_0$ | $UE\_1$ | $UE\_2$ | $\dots$ | $UE\_i$ | $\dots$ |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| $w\_{v0}$ | $G\_{0,w\_{v0}}$ | $G\_{1,w\_{v0}}$ | $G\_{2,w\_{v0}}$ | $\dots$ | $G\_{i,w\_{v0}}$ | $\dots$ |
| $w\_{v1}$ | $G\_{0,w\_{v1}}$ | $G\_{1,w\_{v1}}$ | $G\_{2,w\_{v1}}$ | $\dots$ | $G\_{i,w\_{v1}}$ | $\dots$ |
| $w\_{v2}$ | $G\_{0,w\_{v2}}$ | $G\_{1,w\_{v2}}$ | $G\_{2,w\_{v2}}$ | $\dots$ | $G\_{i,w\_{v2}}$ | $\dots$ |
| $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ |
| $w\_{vj}$ | $G\_{0,w\_{vj}}$ | $G\_{1,w\_{vj}}$ | $G\_{2,w\_{vj}}$ | $\dots$ | $G\_{i,w\_{vj}}$ | $\dots$ |
| $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ | $\dots$ |

Note that tapering beam Gain shall be stored in case of tapering is enabled, refer to 5G\_L2\_4981\_replaced([11165432](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycwPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
