###### 2.4.11.1.3.1.0-6 General Tapering (ID: 11165432)

**2.4.11.1.3.1.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `11165464`)

General
Tapering for horizontal and vertical is controlled by  NIDD  parameter dlMuMimoTaperingH and dlMuMimoTaperingV ,which are received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq message.

When tapering [Before CB013098-C]disalbed [CB013098-C] is not configured [End CB013098-C] , CPlane only send non-tapering DFT beams to L2PS in PsCell\_CellSetupReq.

When tapering [Before CB013098-C ]enabled [CB013098-C] is  configured [End CB013098-C] , CPlane send both non-tapering DFT beams and tapering DFT beams to L2PS in PsCell\_CellSetupReq, and there has one-to-one mapping relationship between non-tapering and tapering beams.

When tapering enabled [ Before CB013098-C] (dlMuMimoTaperingH != 0(NoTapering) and/or dlMuMimoTaperingV != 0(NoTapering)), [End CB013098-C],  the tapering best beam and beam pair are selected from tapering DFT beam set based on the selected nonTapering best beam and beam pair according to one-to-one mapping relationship between non-tapering and tapering beams table. The nonTapering best beam and beam pair selection is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6995\_replaced([11156011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksy9U_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* Usage of beam

After beams selected, both tapering beams and non-tapering beams shall be stored in UE context.

For MU PDSCH scheduling, the tapering beam is used. Note, the rrmBfGainTable defined in 5G\_L2\_4978\_replaced([11165354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1vfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be based on tapering beam.

For SU PDSCH scheduling, the non-tapering beam is used.

When tapering beam used for MU PDSCH scheduling, CSIRS for CSI acquisition is still not use tapering beam, so a compensation is needed for the suSinrLinear(which is based on CQI) in MuSinr calculation defined in 5G\_L2\_4277\_replaced([11165538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycyvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The compensation factor for tapering best beam shall be calculated as definition in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7835\_replaced([11142690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5ldvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that rrmTaperingCompensationi is only calculated and stored for best beam for UE(i), second best beam is not needed for simplify.

[CB013098-C]

Note:

1, DL tapering  is configured,

case1 when actDlSrsBm=True and ( dlMuMimoTaperingH or dlMuMimoTaperingV is existed in NIDD and it is not equals 0 ),

case2, when actDlSrsBm=True but dlMuMimoTaperingH is not existed in NIDD . ( default 25db will be used to generate tapering dft beam (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4849\_replaced([11294877](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqE3pPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).)

2, DL tapering  is enabled, when (dlMuMimoTaperingH != 0(NoTapering) and/or dlMuMimoTaperingV != 0(NoTapering)) in PsCell\_CellSetupReq,  and actDlMuMimoEnh=True in PsCell\_CellSetupReq or PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq message.

3, When DL tapering is configured but it is not enabled, then L2PS shall only  store the tapering beam' configuration , but shall not trigger the selection and  usage of tapering beam.  In cell setup phase,

4, When DL taparing is enabled, L2PS shall start the tapering beam selection and usage. If the DL tapering is enabled during cell Reconfiguration phase, the tapering beam selection will be delayed one or two SRS beam selection period (rrmDlBeamSelectTimer ) after the cell Reconfiguration phase is finished.

[End CB013098-C]

CP2 Reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7835\_replaced([11142690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5ldvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.1.0-7 General LT CoMa based correlation (for WB BF UEs) (ID: 11165470)

**2.4.11.1.3.1.0-7.0-4**  (ID: `13450799`)

General
The correlation between the **m-th layer of UE(i) and** UE(j) is determined as below:\*\*\*\*

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$corrLayer\_{i,m,j} = \sqrt{\frac{\sum\_{n \in {0, rankMuUe\_j-1}} G\_{i,w}(j,n)}{G\_{i,w}(i,m)}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3664.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

* G:  it represents DL BF gain, see definition in 5G\_L2\_4978\_replaced([11165354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1vfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* w(x,y): it represents the BF vector of the y-th layer of UE(x), see definition in 5G\_L2\_5017\_replaced([11165649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD0vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* rankMuUex: the PDSCH rank of UE(x), refer to 5G\_L2\_5008\_replaced([11165331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1u_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The correlation between UE(i) and UE(j) is determined as below:

**IF rankMuUei(i) > 2** THEN\*\*\*\*

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$corr\_{i,j} = \sqrt{corrLayer\_{i,j,b\_best} \* corrLayer\_{i,j,b\_second}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3664.003.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

b\_best and b\_second are the beam pair of UE(i) for ranks 3 or 4.

**ELSE**

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$corr\_{i,j} = \max\_{m \in {0, ..., rankM u e\_1 - 1}} corrLayer\_{i,j,m}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3664.004.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

**ENDIF**

Implementation proposal: the  [image-3][image\_desc]corrLayer²ᵢₘ,ⱼ[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3664.005.png[/image\_path][/image-3], [image-4][image\_desc]corr²ᵢ,ⱼ[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3664.006.png[/image\_path][/image-4] can be used to avoid the sqrt calculation.

CP2 Reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7436\_replaced([11143836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB015912-A]  
When a correlation is calculated between UEs and the R&D flag CAL\_CORRELATION\_DETAIL is set, then the 5G-L2-PS shall log UE beam information (i.e., bestPatternId, pairPatternId, bestBeamHV, pairBeamHV) to Syslog in the interval of once per slot for each UE, for the purpose of offline ML retraining (see [13375160](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_4Uv5hvwZEfCPDo8cGBoqIw)).  
[End CB015912-A]

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.1.0-8 General APS based correlation (for SB BF UEs) (ID: 11165521)

**2.4.11.1.3.1.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11165527`)

General
If  APS based correlation is enable  [11143707: Calculate UE correlation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), L2PS shall maintain a long term correlation table for all SB BF UEs as [11143707: Calculate UE correlation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

L2PS shall maintain agingTime and corrSubbandId(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32976\_replaced([11143715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in UL Scheduler to trigger correlation calculation request for SB BF UEs.

L2PS shall maintain LT correlation table(correlation value and continous invalid counter) in DL Scheduler and update it when ST correlation value is received from L1.

L2PS shall try to build SB BF UEs pairing group based on the LT correlation table .

For correlation calclation request, see [11180919: Setting IEs of structure srsRtBf](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27P1W_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

For correlation reception, see [11180948: Reception of UlData\_SrsReceiveRespRtBfPs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27QcYPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.1.0-9 General DL pairing group(for SB BF UEs) (ID: 12565792)

**2.4.11.1.3.1.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `12708948`)

General
The purpose of Pairing group is to manage the DL subband beamforming UE paring candidates in non-RT path.

To avoid introduce of rrmPairingGroupExitTimer , currently SW implements the high buffer status checking in post Pre-scheduling.

If NIDD:actDlMuPairingGroup = TRUE,  5G-L2-PS shall assign SB BF UEs  to a  pairing group if this is a Dl high buffer UE (see [11142557: DL high buffer UE](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-YfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) ) and meet correlation condition ( see [12442775: Pairing criteria of pairing group](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FVnv8AjUEfCT35inaBGo7g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) . The correlation is APS based correlation (see [11165521: APS based correlation (for SB BF UEs)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycx_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

[Before CB012971-SR-F-UPD002 ] In case of rdDlZfMuDisableRankDowngrade=false , [End CB012971-SR-F-UPD002]L2PS shall optimize paring groups by combining two Pairing group with size equal to two ,aims to improve the number of paring layers.  Because per rdDlMuCorrelationAgingTime (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_33042\_replaced([11149984](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksRY5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), all UEs' APS correlation shall be updated once, and To avoid introduce unnecessary load, then L2PS per periodically (rdDlMuCorrelationAgingTime), L2PS will find a UL slot to check the combination possibility.

if there are more than one pairing groups with PG\_Size=2, and L2Ps will try to add UE from one of those PGs (PG\_size=2) to other PGs (PG\_size < 4) as candidate PG  one by one，the bigger PG\_size the higher priority as candidate PG ）, if any UE is added successfully , the original related paring groups will be dismissed, the adding failure UE will be treated as non-PG UE and will be added based it's own LT APS-Correlation updating.

The long-term CFO  used in the DL high buffer UE checking is metric belong to best beam for PUSCH (see [11151021: LT CFO metric for PUSCH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksW4cfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

For detail  paring group management   refer SFS  [11142569: DL pairing group](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-Y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

[CB012971-F] Note, with supporting very few configured subband beamforming UEs, also UEs with different DMRS type can't be paired, when actDlMuPairingGroup=True, CP shall not configure DMRS type2 to subband beamforming UE ([11308195: gNB-DU shall determine TS 38.331 IE DMRS-DownlinkConfig->dmrs-Type](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrCg_fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).  Because NIDD:actDlMuPairingGroup can be online modification, L2PS shall avoid adding SB BF UE with DMRS type2 which was already configured  to paring group in case of  actDlMuPairingGroup from false to true ([12442775: Pairing criteria of pairing group](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FVnv8AjUEfCT35inaBGo7g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).  [End CB012971-F]

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.1.0-10 General Singular values from SRS channel (ID: 12738349)

**2.4.11.1.3.1.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `12738350`)

General
The singular value is corresponding to the dominant singular values of the singular vector when using singular value decomposition method to derive singular vectors of matrix of SRS channel estimation H.

Detail calculation see [11099803: SVD-RZF Calculation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knHzIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

5G\_L2\_PS will round robin to select subband beamforming SRS configured UEs to calculate singular values.

5G\_L2\_PS will inform L1 the selected UEs by  [11180683: Transmission of UlData\_SrsReceiveReq for periodic SRS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27OALvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

And L1 will report the calculated results.

L2\_PS shall make sure the number of selected UEs per slot doesn't exceed  radio's calculation capacity ([12580757: Singular value calculation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_U0YvMDIBEfCfjdcwDn2YOQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

Because singular values in this feature is only for feature analysis purpose. There is no need to make sure the selection of calculation UEs are very precisely periodicity.

Step1, Set  svdValue\_XSFN  as initial default invalid value for new assigned rtBfIndex UE.

Step2, During per subband beamforming  SRS'  scheduling occasion, The current SRS occasion's over the air XSFN is SRS\_XSFN,  5G\_L2-PS shall find all rtBfIndex assigned UE as potential UE for calculating singular value,

IF svdValue\_XSFN is valid , {

svdValue\_Age= SRS\_XSFN -  svdValue\_XSFN.

if svdValue\_Age >=srsPeriod,  Select  most aged UEs that it is required to calculate singular value. Until the Radio's capability is reached or  the UEs  with svdValue\_Age >=srsPeriod are all selected.

update svdValue\_XSFN=SRS\_XSFN

else , Skip this UE, update svdValue\_XSFN=SRS\_XSFN .

}

ELSE  svdValue\_XSFN is default invalid  value,

update svdValue\_XSFN=SRS\_XSFN.

End IF

where svdvalue is the singular value of SVD calculation.

Informative note: the svdValue\_Age is defined with srsPeriod is because there is no need to calculate in same SRS subband without CE updating.  XSFN and srsPeriod shall have same unit for calculating age.

SFS reference :[11156601: SRS Reception](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks2AovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

##### 2.4.11.1.3.2 Link Adaptioin

###### 2.4.11.1.3.2.0-1 Link Adaptioin MU SINR (ID: 11165538)

**2.4.11.1.3.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12527419`)

Link Adaptioin
**MU SINR of Layer of UE (Linear)**

The linear value of MU-SINR for m-th layer of UE(i) in virtualUe(A) can be calculated as the below:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation presented in three lines, with each line starting with an equals sign. The equations are written in a dark font on a white background.
The first line of the equation is:
$$ \mu\_{SinrLinearUe\_{im,A}} = \frac{1}{\frac{1}{sinrCompensationFactorUe\_i} \times suSinrLinearUe\_i} \times N + rdDIMuInfFactor \times \sum\_{j \in A \setminus {i}} \frac{\sum\_{n \in {0, ..., rankMuUe\_j - 1}} G\_{i,w(j,n)}}{G\_{i,w(i,m)}} $$
The second line of the equation is:
$$ = \frac{1}{suSinrLinearUe\_i} \times N + rdDlMuInfFactor \times \sum\_{j \in A \setminus {i}} corrLayer\_{im,j}^2 $$
The third line of the equation is:
$$ = \frac{1}{sinrFactorForMuUe\_i} \times N + rdDlMuInfFactor \times \sum\_{j \in A \setminus {i}} corrLayer\_{im,j}^2 $$
There are also some highlighted sections in the image:
- A pink highlight over "1 / sinrCompensationFactorUe\_i" in the first line.
- A pink highlight over "1 / suSinrLinearUe\_i" in the second line.
- A pink highlight over "1 / sinrFactorForMuUe\_i" in the third line.
- A light blue highlight over "corrLayer\_{im,j}^2" in the second and third lines.
- A light blue highlight over the fraction term "$\frac{\sum\_{n \in {0, ..., rankMuUe\_j - 1}} G\_{i,w(j,n)}}{G\_{i,w(i,m)}}$" in the first line.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3670.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

* *suSinrLinearUe*i : the linear value of SU-SINR which is calculated from rrmCorrAvgCqi without OLLA correction (rrmDeltaCqi = 0) and MU correction (rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo = 0).\*\*
* rrmCorrAvgCqi, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7557\_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* the mapping relationship between SINR and CQI are defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3009\_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), note that it's shall convert from DB scale to linear scale.
* *suSinrLinearUe*i *=* sinrCompensationFactorUe*i \*\** suSinrLinearUe*i* , it is the compensated SU-SINR of UE(i).***\****\*\*
* *sinrFactorForMuUe*i *= 1/*suSinrLinearUe*i* , is the SINR factor of UE(i) for MU-SINR calculation for  MU UE.**\***\*
* *sinrCompensationFactorUe*i : the compensation factor to adjust value of SU-SINR layer of UE(i). It's an internal variable, initilaize to 1.0. Refer to 5G\_L2\_4998\_replaced([11165581](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25yczfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*
* *N: virtualUeSize, it's defined as the number of UEs in virtualUe, refer to 5G\_L2\_4482\_replaced([11165305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1uPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note that it's can be updated as* 1/txPowerFactor in finnal MCS calculation as defined in 5G\_L2\_4508\_replaced([11167076](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538XfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*
* *G: DL BF gain, refer to 5G\_L2\_4978\_replaced([11165354](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1vfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*
* *w(x,y): it represents the BF vector of the y-th layer of UE(x), see 5G\_L2\_5017\_replaced([11165649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD0vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*
* *rankMuUe*j *: the PDSCH rank of MU UE(j), refer to 5G\_L2\_5008\_replaced([11165331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1u_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*
* *rdDlMuInfFactor: a factor to adjust ratio of interference from paired UEs. It's an RD parameter, default value 1.0, refer to 5G\_L2\_4784\_replaced([11186699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_277Kz_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

**MU SINR of UE** (DB)\*\*\*\*

The DB value of MU-SINR of UE(i) in virtualUE(A) can be calculated from MU-SINR of layers according to below equations:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a dark font on a white background. The equation is:
muSinrDble\_{im,A} = 10 log\_{10} muSinrLinearUe\_{im,A}[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3670.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

**[Before CNI-137616-B]IF rdDlMuSinrMethod = 0(avgMethod):**[End CNI-137616-B]\*\*\*\*

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in an italicized font. The equation is:
$muSinrDbUe\_{i,A} = \frac{\sum\_{m \in {0, rankMuUe\_i - 1}} muSinrDbUe\_{i\_m,A}}{rankMuUe\_i}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3670.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

**[Before CNI-137616-B] ELSE IF rdDlMuSinrMethod = 1(minMethod):**

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a dark gray, italicized font on a white background. The equation is:
$muSinrDbUe\_{i,A} = \min\_{m \in {0, rankMuUe\_i - 1}} muSinrDbUe\_{i\_m, A}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3670.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

END **[End CNI-137616-B]**

**[Before CNI-137616-B]The rdDlMuSinrMethod is defined as RD parameter with default value of 0(avgMethod), refer to 5G\_L2\_4792\_replaced([11186710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_277x0fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**[End CNI-137616-B]\*\*\*\*

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7440\_replaced([11143425](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.2.0-2 Link Adaptioin ZF_MU SINR (ID: 12574995)

**2.4.11.1.3.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12574996`)

Link Adaptioin
If  ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True) , If SB BF UEs are paired with SVD-RZF in virtualUE(A)，the linear value of MU-SINR for each UE(i) can be calculated based on UE level long term correlation ([11165521: APS based correlation (for SB BF UEs)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycx_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), Also 5G\_L2\_PS isn't aware final beam weight, roughly estimation of receiving relative power per layer due to DL pairing , assume TX power is equally  distributed over all  paring layers.

where:

* The base of suSinrUeLinear(i): the linear value of SU-SINR which is calculated from rrmCorrAvgCqi within ILLA without OLLA correction, the linear value of SU-SINR which is calculated from rrmCorrAvgCqi without OLLA correction (*rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf  = 0) ,  and MU correction is zero in this step (rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo = 0).*

-  corrMaxVue(A, i), the long term correlation and  the maximum correlation between UE(i) and other paired UEs in virtualUE(A) .

* rrmDlZfMuFactor ,  [12581849: rdDlZfMuFactor](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_GWPNsTJ8EfCfjdcwDn2YOQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

-rrmDlZfMuSinrFactor, [12581855: rdDlZfMuSinrFactor](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_IjIGkTJ9EfCfjdcwDn2YOQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

-rrmDlZfMuInfFactor, [12581857: rdDlZfMuIntfFactor](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_O44uwDJ9EfCfjdcwDn2YOQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

-*rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf  , [12577004: ZF\_MU OLLA](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kIKxgDBwEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).*

Informative:

1, Because  5G\_L2\_PS isn't aware final beam weight and actual channel, the SINR compensation ([11165581: SINR Compensation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25yczfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) due to rank downgrade ([11165581: SINR Compensation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25yczfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) can't be estimated.

Detail CP2 reference: [11143425: Calculate MU-SINR in virtual UE](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.2.0-3 Link Adaptioin SINR Compensation (ID: 11165581)

**2.4.11.1.3.2.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11165645`)

Link Adaptioin
The SU-SINR compensation factor sinrCompensationFactorUei is initialized to 1.0 for UE(i).

Subband RT Beam Compensation

In case of eCPRI 7-2a (5G\_L2\_18130\_replaced([11155228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2432wvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ,For UEs with valid subband beams:

In case of eCPRI 7-2e (5G\_L2\_18130\_replaced([11155228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2432wvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), For UEs with SB BF SRS configured,  refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7454\_replaced([11142605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-afvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) IWK with rdDlMuSbBfMode=1 for SB BF UE definition.

sinrCompensationFactorUei  = sinrCompensationFactorUei  / rdRtGainAdjust

Where,

* rdRtGainAdjust is RD parameter defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22194\_replaced([11149754](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksPjvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Tapering Compensation

If DL tapering is enabled [Before CB013098-C](dlMuMimoTaperingH != 0(NoTapering) and/or dlMuMimoTaperingV  != 0(NoTapering))  [CB013098-C] ,refer 5G\_L2\_4981\_replaced([11165432](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycwPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB013098-C]:

sinrCompensationFactorUei = sinrCompensationFactorUei \*  taperingCompensationoFactorUei rrmTaperingCompensationi

End

Where,

* taperingCompensationoFactorUei rrmTaperingCompensationi : the tapering cpmpensation factor of UE(i), refer to 5G\_L2\_4981\_replaced([11165432](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycwPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7835\_replaced([11142690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5ldvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* dlMuMimoTaperingH,dlMuMimoTaperingV: are  [Before CB013098-C] NIDD [End CB013098-C]parameters and received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq message.

Rank downgrade compensation

If rank adaption is enabled (dlMuMimoRankAdaption != 0(disable)) and UE is rank downgraded ( rankMuUe(i) <rankSuUe(i) ):

sinrCompensationFactorUei = sinrCompensationFactorUei \*  rankDowngradeSinrAdjustUei ;

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in black text on a white background. The equation is:
`rankDowngradeSinnAdjustUeᵢ = min (2 * Gᵢ,wstrong / Gᵢ,wweak , rdRankDowngradeSinnAdjustMax)`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3675.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

End

Where,

* rdRankDowngradeSinrAdjustMax: is RD parameter defined in 5G\_L2\_5002\_replaced([11186771](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_277x3PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), default value is 20.
* [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the word "Wrong" written in a dark blue, slightly blurry font. The letters are capitalized "W" followed by lowercase "r", "o", "n", "g".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3675.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1], is the weight of non-tapering best beam, it's the strongest beam of the UE.
* [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Wweak" in a dark blue, blurry font against a white background. The text is written in a cursive style, with the "W" being larger and capitalized, followed by the lowercase letters "weak".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3675.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2], is the weight of non-tapering second beam of the beam pair, it's the weakest beam of the UE.
* dlMuMimoRankAdaption: is NIDD parameters and received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq message.

Refer to 5G\_L2\_5008\_replaced([11165331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1u_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for rank downgrade.

The compensated SU-SINR of m-th layer of UE(i) is:

suSinrLinearUei = sinrCompensationFactorUei \* suSinrLinearUei

suSinrDbUei = 10\*log(suSinrLinearUei)

It's used in MU-SINR calculation (5G\_L2\_4277\_replaced([11165538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycyvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and SU-MCS calculation(5G\_L2\_4948\_replaced([11165673](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD1fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7440\_replaced([11143425](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.2.0-4 Link Adaptioin ZF_MU OLLA (ID: 12577004)

**2.4.11.1.3.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12577006`)

Link Adaptioin
If  ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True) ,  and UE(i) in a DL paring group , zero forcing MU MIMO specified  OLLA compensation delta CQI (*rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf[i, j, t, p]) shall be maintained.  The*rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf  's updating is same as *rrmDeltaCqi, but the rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf[i, j, t, p] needs reset to 0 when the specified conditions are meet, this is because we want maintain a paring group specific OLLA , to compensate the ZF\_MU SINR estimation.*\*\*

Note, for UE(i) in a Dl paring group, the OLLA compensation *rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf  and*rrmDeltaCqi  both shall be maintained because the paring status are decided per slot.\*\*

with

i: Index of the UE

j: Index of the carrier

t: Time when the summands change the value  
p:                                              Index of MU pairing group

*For  resetting  to 0 conditions of  rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf   see  [11142569: DL pairing group](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-Y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA).*

Detail  *rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf   updating see  [11129351: OLLA offset evaluation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78KPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA)*

CP2 refer [11125689: Correct average WB-CQI](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fcm%2Fstream%2F_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA) .

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.2.0-5 Link Adaptioin Precoding (ID: 11165649)

**2.4.11.1.3.2.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11165665`)

Link Adaptioin
Precoding with Port Selection

In case of actDlMuMimoEnh=True, port selection shall be used:

* For UE configured with 4 port UE-specific CSIRS

Port selection up to rank4 shall be used, pdschPrecodingOption4X4=2, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3180\_replaced([11139272](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for precoding, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2565\_replaced([11118485](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDLVvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for txPower.

* For UE configured with 8 port cell-specific CSIRS

Rank2 port selection shall be used, only 1 beam is needed. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3185\_replaced([11139392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmqi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for precoding, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2567\_replaced([11118493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDLW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for txPower.

BF Weight Vector of Layer of UE

The vector w(x,y) represents the effective BF vector of the y-th PDSCH layer on UE(x). In below table, [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "W\_{x,best}" in a dark blue font against a white background. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3677.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] is the BF vector of best DL beam of UE(x), [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Wx,secondbest". The text is dark blue and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are slightly blurred, but the text is still legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3677.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] is the BF vector of second-best DL beam of UE(x).

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a table with two columns: "rankUex" and "w(x, y)". The table has 5 rows, including the header.
| rankUex | w(x, y)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3677.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Note,

a) for 8x4 cell with actDlSrsBm=True, it is possible that the UEs configured with 8 port cell-specific CSIRS and the UEs configured with 4 port UE-specific CSIRS are co-exist and both are muEigible.

b) the BF vector table is valid for both MU and SU UE when actDlMuMimoEnh=True.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7278\_replaced([11112223](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpULhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.2.0-6 Link Adaptioin ZF_MU Precoding (ID: 12579923)

**2.4.11.1.3.2.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12579924`)

Link Adaptioin
**Precoding with Identity Precoding**

In case of ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True) , identity precoding shall be used.

Informative, the precoding for subband beamforming SU and MU are same.

CP2 reference:[11096976: Overview of spatial multiplexing up to RANK4 (Identity Precoding)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km0RIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.2.0-7 Link Adaptioin MCS Calculation (ID: 11165673)

**2.4.11.1.3.2.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `13342911`)

Link Adaptioin
\* **MU-MCS calculation (based on MU-SINR):**

1. Get rrmCorrAvgCqi[i,j,t] without rrmDeltaCqi[i,j,t] and rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo[i,j,t], refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7557\_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

That is:

rrmCorrAvgCqi[i,j,t] = rrmAvgCqi[i,j,t] + rrmDeltaAvgCqiDiffBeam[i,j,t] + rrmBlerCorrectionValue.

This is the corrected average CQI without OLLA correction and MU interference correction.

1. Calculate suSinrLinearUe:
2. [Before PR871264] [PR865959] If LA Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency is enabled in DL (see [12472220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HoCyEA1UEfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), rrmCorrAvgCqi is corrected by avgCqiRatio (as described in [11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), before calculating suSinrLinearUe. [End PR865959] [End PR871264]
3. Transfer rrmCorrAvgCqi to suSinrDbUe according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3009\_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
4. Transfer DB to Linear value:

[image-0][image\_desc]suSinrLinearUe = 10suSinrDbUe/10[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3679.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

1. Calculate muSinrDbUe based on suSinrLinearUe and MU pairing result, according to 5G\_L2\_4277\_replaced([11165538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycyvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note, muSinrDbUe is the avgMuSinrDbUe in case of final MCS calculation, refer to 5G\_L2\_4508\_replaced([11167076](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538XfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. Transfer muSinrDbUe to rrmCorrAvgCqiMu according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3009\_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  Note, the rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo = rrmCorrAvgCqiMu - rrmCorrAvgCqi.
3. Add OLLA correction:

rrmCorrAvgCqiMu = rrmCorrAvgCqiMu + rrmDeltaCqi

[PR871264] If LA Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency is enabled in DL (see [12472220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HoCyEA1UEfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), rrmCorrAvgCqiMu calculated above is corrected by the CQI Ratio described in [11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for TRS, CSI-IM and special slots. [End PR871264]

1. Calculate MCS from rrmCorrAvgCqiMu according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3015\_replaced([11126253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9A_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the calculation procedure is same as calculate MCS from rrmCorrAvgCqi.
2. **SU-MCS calculation (the SU-MCS calculation is same as the defination in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3004\_replaced([11125290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5sPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo=0):**
3. Get rrmCorrAvgCqi without OLLA correction and MU interference correction, same as step 1 of MU-MCS.

If tapering/rank downgrade applied,  the rrmCorrAvgCqi shall be updated as follows:

a) Calculate suSinrLinearUe, same as step 2 of MU-MCS.

b) Get suSinrDbUe with compensation of tapering/rank downgrade, refer to 5G\_L2\_4998\_replaced([11165581](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25yczfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

c) Get rrmCorrAvgCqi based on suSinrDbUe according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3009\_replaced([11126006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. Add OLLA correction, same as step 5 of MU UE:
2. rrmCorrAvgCqi = rrmCorrAvgCqi + rrmDeltaCqi
3. [PR865959] If LA Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency is enabled in DL (see [12472220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HoCyEA1UEfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), rrmCorrAvgCqi computed above is corrected by the CQI Ratio described in [11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for TRS, CSI-IM and special slots. [End PR865959]
4. Calculate MCS from rrmCorrAvgCqiMu, same as step 6 of MU UE.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7555\_replaced([11126154](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3045\_replaced([11125301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.2.0-8 Link Adaptioin ZF_MU MCS Calculation (ID: 12577605)

**2.4.11.1.3.2.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `13381631`)

Link Adaptioin
​​​If  ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True),

**MU-MCS calculation (based on ZF\_MU-SINR [12574995: ZF\_MU SINR](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_XKY7QC-mEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)):**

1. Get rrmCorrAvgCqi[i,j,t,p] without OLLA correction rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf[i, j, t, p] . And rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo[i,j,t,p]=0 in this step, refer to [11143425: Calculate MU-SINR in virtual UE](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

This is the corrected average CQI without OLLA correction and MU interference correction [11125689: Correct average WB-CQI](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

1. Calculate suSinrLinearUe:
2. Transfer rrmCorrAvgCqi to suSinrDbUe according to  [11126006: Conversion between CQI and SINR](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).
3. Transfer DB to Linear value:

[image-0][image\_desc]suSinrLinearUe = 10suSinrDbUe/10[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3679.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

1. Calculate muSinrDbUe based on suSinrLinearUe and MU pairing result, according to [12574995: ZF\_MU SINR](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_XKY7QC-mEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9):

Informative, DL paring with SVD-RZF virtual UE for SB BF UEs, one UE can only belong to one virtual UE (see [11142531: Concept of virtual UE](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).  Then muSinrDbUe is same as the avgMuSinrDbUe in case of final MCS calculation, refer to [11167076: Final MCS Calculation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538XfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

1. Transfer muSinrDbUe to rrmCorrAvgCqiMu according to [11126006: Conversion between CQI and SINR](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

Note, the rrmDeltaAvgCqiMuMimo = rrmCorrAvgCqiMu - rrmCorrAvgCqi.

1. Add ZF\_MU OLLA specified correction according [12577004: ZF\_MU OLLA](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kIKxgDBwEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) :

rrmCorrAvgCqiMu = rrmCorrAvgCqiMu + rrmDeltaCqiDlMuZf

[PR871264] If LA Enhancements for Efficiency and Latency is enabled in DL (see [12472220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HoCyEA1UEfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), rrmCorrAvgCqiMu calculated above is corrected by the CQI Ratio described in [11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)​​​​​​​ for TRS, CSI-IM and special slots. [End PR871264]

1. Calculate MCS from rrmCorrAvgCqiMu according to [11126253: Corrected average WB-CQI to MCS mapping tables](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqw9A_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), the calculation procedure is same as calculate MCS from rrmCorrAvgCqi.

CP2 reference: [11126154: CQI compensation for DL MU-MIMO](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqwV_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9),  [11125301: Determination of the DL MCS index](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5svvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9),  [11125290: Inner Loop DL Link Adaptation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5sPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

##### 2.4.11.1.3.3 MU VirtualUe Scheduling

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.1 MU Candidate List Build

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.1.0-1 MU Candidate List Build Mu Candidate List (ID: 11165697)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.1.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12571103`)

MU Candidate List Build
A rrmMuCandidateList is created at the beginning of MU scheduling, the list is consist of muEligible UEs in cs2List.

The UE's MU eligibility is checked during FDM UE selection stage when actDlMuMimoEnh=True. An UE is muEligiable only when satisfy all of following criterias:

* UE is in cs2list|toBeScheduledList.
* The DL traffic priority can be nonGbr only or intTx nonGbr with MacCe(s), e.g. DL traffic priority is harqRetransDrb(in case of rdDlEnableReTxUeMu=1(enable)) or drbTraffic only or dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic only or drbTraffic/dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic with MAC CE(s). refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4145\_replaced([11174005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuXqrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note, in case of rdDlEnableReTxUeMu=0(disable), reTx UE is not allowed for MU pairing, only newTx UE is allowed.  OTE: non-GBR InitTx includes both the NBR configured nonGBR InitTx (NBR category of drbTraffic, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4178\_replaced([11174242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuaG5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and normal nonGBR InitTx
* PDSCH beams are periodic SRS based oversample DFT beams, and not aged.  Or

[Before CB012971-C]

In eCPRI 7-2a (5G\_L2\_18130\_replaced([11155228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2432wvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

PDSCH beam is subband beamforming RT beam and rdDlMuSbBfMode=1 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_22164\_replaced([11149741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksPjvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In eCPRI 7-2e ( 5G\_L2\_18130\_replaced([11155228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2432wvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

UE is configured with SB BF SRS (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7454\_replaced([11142605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-afvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and ,

with rdDlMuSbBfMode=0  (5G\_UP\_ALG\_22164\_replaced([11149741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksPjvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),  it is not MU eligible.

With rdDlMuSbBfMode=1, it is MU eligible.

[CB012971-C]

IF actDlSrsSubbandBm = True: {

If actDlMuPairingGroup=True,

SB BF UE are not WB BF MU eligible.

Else

with rdDlMuSbBfMode=0  (5G\_UP\_ALG\_22164\_replaced([11149741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksPjvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), SB BF UE it is not MU eligible.

With rdDlMuSbBfMode=1, it is MU eligible.

}

Where the above SB BF UE, in eCPRI7-2a means UE has valid SB BWVs, in eCPRI 7-2e means UE is configured with SB BF SRS.

and actDlMuPairingGroup can only be enable in eCPRI7-2e.

[End CB012971-C]

Note,

* 1, it needs valid best and second best DFT beams for rank 3-4 UE and valid best DFT beam for rank1-2 UE.

-2, UE’s current active BWP is large BWP if multiple BWP enabled(actMultipleDedicatedBWPs=True or/and actPowerSavingBWP=True), due to periodic SRS only configure for large BWP.

* 3，UE is scheduling in PCell if CA enabled, due to perodic SRS only configure for PCell. When actDlSrsForCa is set to TRUE on both PCell and SCell, SRS DFT beams on SCell are available as PCell forwards relevant SRS DFT information to SCell UE via PsPeerCtrl\_ScellUeSchedulingReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_656\_replaced([10979441](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VbU1vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* UE gains from rrmBfGainTable are valid (see 5G\_L2\_17814\_replaced([11155074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_243Ps_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* CQI > dlMuMimoCqiThd.
* If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True PDSCH is scheduled with RAT1 else PDSCH is scheduled with RAT0.
* if actExcludeLowBufferUser is true ,UE with HARQ initial transmission shall NOT be considered as MU candidate if DL buffer condition is met defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7454\_replaced([11142605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-afvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

where,

-1, actExcludeLowBufferUser OAM parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq

-2，dlMuMimoCqiThd is OAM parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

-3, rdDlEnableReTxUeMu is RD parameter with default value 1(enable), refer to 5G\_L2\_4786\_replaced([11186727](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_277x1PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The disable of reTx UE MU pairing is for C&P purpose in case of high load, to tune the MU consuming time.

* 4，actMultipleDedicatedBWPs and actPowerSavingBWP are OAM parameters received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq.
* SRS DFT beams on SCell are available due to PCell send such beams to SCell UE when actDlSrsForCa is set to true on both Pcell and SCell. Additional gains are exchange between PCell and SCell.
* For UE paring need to check if gains are valid.
* If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True L2-PS shall check for minimum FD resources while pairing as mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26957\_replaced([11142663](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5lcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .

When a MU Eligible UE(i) is selected to be added to rrmMuCandidateList, the rankSuUe(i) and rankMuUe(i) shall be determined according to 5G\_L2\_5008\_replaced([11165331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1u_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In case of dlMuMimoRankAdaption=2(adaptive), the rankMuUe(i) has been downgraded to rank2 for high rank(rank3 and rank4) MU eligible UE(i) as defined in 5G\_L2\_5008\_replaced([11165331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1u_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

After all UEs in cs2list|toBeScheduledList has been MU eligiblity checked and rrmMuCandidateList has been created, then the number of muEilgible UE in this slot is known. If only 2 muEligble UEs, the rankMuUe(i) need reset back to it's rankSuUe(i) as it has the possibility of high rank MU, for the MU gain optimization puppose.

actRankAdaptive2UeMuOptimzation=False.

IF dlMuMimoRankAdaption=2(adaptive) AND numOfMuEligbileUe=2:

FOR each UE(i) in rrmMuCandidateList:

IFrankSuUe(i)=rank4 AND rrmAvgCqi >rdRankdowngradeCqiThd

rankMuUe(i) = rankSuUe(i).

actRankAdaptive2UeMuOptimzation=True.

END

END

END

where,

-1, dlMuMimoRankAdaption: is NIDD parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

-2, rdRankdowngradeCqiThd: is RD parameter define the CQI threshold for rank downgrade, default is 10. Refer to 5G\_L2\_5015\_replaced([11186782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_277x3vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

-3, rrmAvgCqi: is average WB CQI defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7557\_replaced([11125689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Refer to 5G\_L2\_4280\_replaced([11166042](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zq8vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the use of actRankAdaptive2UeMuOptimzation.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7454\_replaced([11142605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-afvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.1.0-2 MU Candidate List Build ZF_Mu Candidate List (ID: 12571009)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12688637`)

MU Candidate List Build
If NIDD:actDlMuPairingGroup is True, UE's ZF\_MU eligible shall be checked in two phases for different purpose.

first step, the UE's ZF\_MU eligible capability is checked during adding the UE to CS1 list phase by bucket algorithm, ( [12586255: UEs selection for UEs In Pairing Group](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_LTQLgDYEEfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).  when satisfy all of following criteria:

1. SB BF UE which is added in pairing group ([12565792: DL pairing group(for SB BF UEs)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HJrRACvSEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) and has valid quality SRS ports number are not less than MU rank on all SRS subbands. The all SRS subbands with valid SRS quality is because the UE's frequency domain allocation is unknown yet, detail SRS quality  measurement refer [11171429: SRS Quality Measurement](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26N6nPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). and  MU rank refer [12567357: Rank Downgrade for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_W7elgCyFEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)  [Before CB012971-E] Note: UEs was already added in CS1 with high priority request class ( like MAC-CE ), the SRS Quality table will not be checked in this step if related Pairing Group is not selected by Bucket algorithm.[End CB012971-E]
2. ~~If  criteria 1 is checked and passed, but only with two UEs from same pairing group, additional step of averaging rank of two UEs are checked to decide if can be ZF\_MU or not, see [12568847: Only two UEs paring with high rank for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YtX0kiy_EfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)~~

second step,  the UE's ZF\_MU eligible capability is checked  at the beginning of ZF\_MU scheduling phase, rrmZfMuCandidateList  is ZF\_MU Eligible UEs in cs2List.   The UE's ZF\_MU eligibility is checked during FDM UE selection stage.    An UE is ZF\_MU eligible only when satisfy all of following criteria:

1. [Before CB012971-E] IF UEs was already added in CS1 with high priority request class ( like MAC-CE ) the SRS Quality across all SRS Subbands shall be checked like in first step.[End CB012971-E]
2. UE's  ZF\_MU  capability was checked and passed in first step [Before CB012971-E] or criterion 1 of second step [End CB012971-E].
3. The common conditions same as legacy WB BF based MU still need checked,   but WB BF MU specified conditions shall be excluded . See WB BF MU eligible checking [11165697: Mu Candidate List](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD6PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)
4. The SB BF UEs in  a Pairing group ,  If the [Before PR863398] UE's demand PRB (MAC-CE buffer also be considered if it has) >= bfSubbandSize, [PR863398] if number of PRBs can be allocated to root SB BF UE is less than dlSubbandBfSize, UEs in the same pairing group shall not be paired. For WRR case, the number of PRBs is value of allowedPrbUe. For DL ES case, the number PRBs is value of MIN(allowedPrbUe, nbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea) where nbRemainingPrbsPdschSubArea is the maximum remaining PRBs among all subareas. [End PR863398],  [CB012971-SR-F-UPD002] and  UE's SV1/SV3 >=rdSv1ToSv3Ratio ([13322105: rdSv1ToSv3Ratio](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HO9c49EWEfCVOOJMfwsFiw) , then [End CB012971-SR-F-UPD002]this SB BF UE is ZF MU eligible.

-where common conditions:

* UE works in Basic Reliability (BR) mode in DL.
* PDSCH is scheduled only for  non-GBR (request class is drbTraffic or dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic) or non-GBR  multiplexed with MAC CE(s).    ​​​​​Note for ZF\_MU , [Before CB012971-E ] only initial TX  is considered as ZF \_MU eligible [CB012971-E] Re-TX MU eligible [CB012971-SR-F-UPD002]  is true by default  [Before CB012971-SR-F-UPD002] decided by rdDlZfMuSupportReTx ([12578190: rdDlZfMuSupportReTx](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Jf3DQTDKEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) [End CB012971-E] [End CB012971-SR-F-UPD002].
* For multiple BWP cases, DL MU-MIMO applies to large BWP: UE’s current active BWP is large BWP if multiple BWP enabled(actMultipleDedicatedBWPs=True or/and actPowerSavingBWP=True), due to periodic SRS only configure for large BWP.
* For CA case, DL ZF MU-MIMO only applies to UE in Pcell, because there is no beam or beam gain syncing  between Pcell and Scell like WB BF MU.
* ​​​​​​CQI > dlMuMimoCqiThd.  NOTE: the value of CQI is same as WB BF MU, it shall be calculated as sum of rrmAvgCqi and rrmDeltaCqi. (see  [11125689: Correct average WB-CQI](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqvu6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))
* PDSCH is scheduled with RAT1 because NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True.
* There is no need to consider actExcludeLowBufferUser true , because actExcludeLowBufferUser =false in case of NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True.
* Minimum FD resource for MU paring  [11142663: Minimum FD resources for MU pairing](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5lcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)
* [CB012971-E] If actSrsBeamFallbackByRetrx = true, then for non-GBR ReTx , the beam does not fallback to legacy PMI/SSB beam. Informative note, in eCPRI 7-2e, this requirement doesn't apply to SB BF UE, this is because L2PS will based on SRS quality table to determine to use RT beam or not. [End CB012971-E]

- Where the WB  BF MU specified conditions ( This is osDFT beam validation conditions. This is for informative only. it will not be maintained per release , detail please check Mu candidate List  ):

* It's need valid best and second best DFT beams for rank 3-4 UE and valid best DFT beam for rank1-2 UE.
* SRS DFT beams on SCell are available due to PCell send such beams to SCell UE when *actDlSrsForCa is set to* true on both Pcell and SCell. Additional gains are exchange between PCell and SCell.\*\*
* For UE paring need to check if gains are valid. UE gains from rrmBfGainTable are valid (see [11155074: Handling on SCell](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_243Ps_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

For initial Tx , the demand number of Prb  see [11181264: Determining the maximum number of FD resources which initial transmission UEs can get](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-HkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

For non-GBR InitTx multiplexed with MAC CE(s), the minimum number of [Prb] to drain the MAC CE buffer shall be included , see [11179675: Resource distribution to DL MAC CE of initial transmission UEs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku6dM_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

The demand PRB checking with rrmMuMinPrbNum, this is common with WB BF MU.

Note:

1,  rrmZfMuCandidateList  doesn't not need to be created. And  ZF\_MU eligible capability status per UE with pairing group ID  is recorded in UE DB.

SFS refer: [11142605: Build MU candidate list](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-afvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.1.0-3 MU Candidate List Build Correlation Table (ID: 11165729)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11165738`)

MU Candidate List Build
For each UE pair {UE(i), UE(j)} in rrmMuCandidateList,  the correlation of UE(i)\_layer(m) and UE(j) shall be calculated and stored, a table rrmCorrTable is calculated as follows:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a table with yellow headers and white cells. The table has 5 rows and 5 columns. The headers are "ue(0)", "ue(1)", "ue(2)", "...", and "ue(n)". The first column also has headers "ue(0)", "ue(1)", "ue(2)", "...", and "ue(n)".
The table content is as follows:

|  | ue(0) | ue(1) | ue(2) | ... | ue(n) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ue(0) | [1,1] | [corrLayer(0,0,1), corrLayer(0,1,1)] | [corrLayer(0,0,2), corrLayer(0,1,2)] | ... | [corrLayer(0,0,n), corrLayer(0,1,n)] |
| ue(1) | [corrLayer(1,0,0), corrLayer(1,1,0)] | [1,1] | [corrLayer(1,0,2), corrLayer(1,1,2)] | ... | [corrLayer(1,0,n), corrLayer(1,1,n)] |
| ue(2) | [corrLayer(2,0,0), corrLayer(2,1,0)] | [corrLayer(2,0,1), corrLayer(2,1,1)] | [1,1] | ... | [corrLayer(2,0,n), corrLayer(2,1,n)] |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | [1,1] | ... |
| ue(n) | [corrLayer(n,0,0), corrLayer(b,1,0)] | [corrLayer(n,0,1), corrLayer(n,1,1)] | [corrLayer(n,0,2), corrLayer(n,1,2)] | ... | [1,1] |

Note:

1. corrLayer(i,m,j): means corrLayer(targetUe\_i, targetUe\_i\_layer\_m, interferenceUe\_j) defined in 5G\_L2\_4230\_replaced([11165470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycxPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB008305-B]The corrLayer shall be calculated based on rankMuUe.[End CB008305-B]
2. According to defination of BF weight vector of each layer in 5G\_L2\_5017\_replaced([11165649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD0vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the correlation of each layer as follows:
3. 1 layer UE: corrLayer(i,0,j).
4. 2 layer UE: corrLayer(i,0,j)=corrLayer(i,1,j).
5. 3 layer UE: corrLayer(i,0,j)=corrLayer(i,2,j), corrLayer(i,1,j).
6. 4 layer UE: corrLayer(i,0,j)=corrLayer(i,2,j), corrLayer(i,1,j)=corrLayer(i,3,j).

So only max 2 correlation values is need for ue(j) to ue(i), corr(i,0,j) and corr(i,1,j) are stored in the table.

Implementation proposal:

- the corrLayer2 can be used to avoid the sqrt calculation.

- the correlation table can be calculate after the Max MU Layer Calculation(5G\_L2\_4500\_replaced([11165748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zq6fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), in case of the calculated maxDlMuMimoLayer<=1, then the correlation table is not needed due to there will be no MU pairing in this slot.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.2.0-1 Max MU Layer Max MU Layer Calculation (ID: 11165748)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11166017`)

Max MU Layer
[CB008927-P] If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False [End CB008927-P]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7446\_replaced([11142759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.2.0-2 Max MU Layer Max MU Layer Calculation for RAT1 (ID: 11166023)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11166028`)

Max MU Layer
The supported Max Mu layers is a NIDD parameter received from cell in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq message. The max number of layers calculation done based on the 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7955\_replaced([11142718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5lefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The max Mu layers calculation based on DL buffer size is not valid when NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True. Please refer in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7446\_replaced([11142759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.2.0-3 Max MU Layer Max MU Layer Calculation for ZF_MU (ID: 12580137)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12580138`)

Max MU Layer
​​​If  ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True), the max MU layers for ZF\_MU shall be calculated.

Due to For subband BF users, It supports up to 8 layers DL MU-MIMO with ZF algorithm, see [12193641: The feature overview can be found at](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_H_I-7QDcEfCESrsIqIgYfw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) <https://focalpoint-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/fp/workspace/1314/view/68773/tree/56898>

Then  maxDlZfMuMimoLayer=min(8, rrmMaxDlMuMimoLayerL1).

maxDlZfMuMiMoLayer will be used in Step2.2 MU Pair Limitation check for ZF\_MU virtual UE .(informative refer  [11166322: Layer Limitation Check](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2505DfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

where :

* maxDlZfMuMimoLayer the max MU layers of L1  for ZF\_MU.

-rrmMaxDlMuMimoLayerL1,  The supported Max Mu layers is a NIDD parameter received from cell in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq message. The max number of layers calculation done based on [11142718: Max MU layer supported by L1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5lefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) .

Informative note: The max Mu layers calculation based on DL buffer size is not valid when NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True. Please refer in [11142759: Max MU layer from DL buffer size](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

Virtual UE Construction
If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False **,the virtual UE scheduling precedure as follows:**

The virtual UE scheduling precedure as follows:

**[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to virtual UE (vUE) scheduling in a 5G network.
The flowchart begins with a "Start" node.
It then proceeds to a process block labeled "nongbr New UES MU WRR 5G\_L2\_5575".
The next step is a process block "Select SubArea 'i' for vUE Scheduling". This block has a dashed arrow pointing to a yellow rectangle labeled "vUeList.size=0" which contains a single blue rectangle representing an empty list.
From the "Select SubArea 'i' for vUE Scheduling" block, a solid arrow leads into a rounded rectangle containing three sequential process blocks:
1. "vUe(A) virtual UE construct"
2. "vUE(A) prb Allocation"
3. "Add vUe(A) to virtual UE list vUeList{}"
The third block, "Add vUe(A) to virtual UE list vUeList{}", has a dashed arrow pointing to a yellow rectangle labeled "vUeList.size=N". This rectangle contains multiple blue rectangles, labeled "vUe(0)", "vUe(1)", and "vUe(A)", with an ellipsis (...) indicating more elements.
After the "Add vUe(A) to virtual UE list vUeList{}" block, a solid arrow leads to a decision diamond labeled "Is done vUE scheduling for subarea 'N'".
This diamond has two outgoing arrows:
- One arrow loops back to the "Select SubArea 'i' for vUE Scheduling" block.
- Another arrow leads to the "End" node.
The overall process appears to describe the construction and addition of virtual UEs to a list for scheduling purposes, iterating through subareas until the scheduling for a specific subarea is complete.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3704.004.png[/image\_path][/image-1]**

**[CB013709-CB]**

5G-L2-Ps shall get the value of rrmMaxNumberVuePerTTI by referring to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32899\_replaced([11144184](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7aqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The following case shall be considered when 5G-L2-Ps update the rrmMaxNumberVuePerTTI tilll it is equal to zero during the construction of virtaul UE every round. When rrmMaxNumberVuePerTTI shall be updated to zero,no any Ue shall be allowed to add this virtual UE even if these UEs meet the the following condidition described in the procedure of Virtual UE construction.Example as following digram.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid-like structure, resembling a spreadsheet or a Gantt chart, with colored bars indicating resource allocation across different "PRB" (Physical Resource Block) columns.
The leftmost column is labeled "virtualUe No." and lists entries from "InitTx1" to "InitTx8", followed by "vUe limit".
The columns are labeled "PRB0", "PRB1", "PRB2", "PRB3", "PRB4", "PRB5", and "PRB7". Below these labels are numerical values: 1 under PRB0, 2 under PRB1, 3 under PRB2, 4 under PRB3, 5 under PRB4, and 6 under PRB5. The PRB7 column is empty.
The colored bars represent the allocation of resources for each "InitTx" entry across the PRBs:

\* **InitTx8**: A pink bar is partially visible in the PRB7 column.
The bottom row, "vUe limit", has numerical values below the PRB columns: 3 under PRB0, 2 under PRB2, and 0 under PRB4.

[CB012971-F]

Note, for SVD-RZF vUE, its maximum number per TTI is not limited by rrmMaxNumberVuePerTTI.  But the

[End CB012971-F]

The virtual UE construction as follows:

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process for creating a virtual UE (User Equipment) in a 5G network. The flowchart begins with a "Start" node and proceeds through several steps, each represented by a rectangular box containing a description of the action and a corresponding 5G\_L2 code.
Here's a breakdown of the flowchart:
1. **Start**: The process begins.
2. **Create a new virtual ue vUe(A)**: A new virtual UE named vUe(A) is created. This step is associated with a visual representation of vUe(A) having a size of 0.
3. **1) Select root ue rUe(i) from rrmMuCandidateList [subarea] 5G\_L2\_4288**: The process selects a root UE (rUe(i)) from a candidate list.
4. **Add root ue vUe(i) to virtual ue vUe(A)**: The selected root UE is added to the virtual UE.
5. **Create temprrmPairCandidateList = rrmMuCandidateList**: A temporary candidate list is created.
6. **Remove rUe(i) from rrmPairCandidateList**: The selected root UE is removed from the candidate list. This step is associated with a visual representation of vUe(A) having a size of 1, containing rUe(i).
7. **Is max layers/stream reached? 5G\_L2\_5546**: A check is performed to see if the maximum layers or streams have been reached.
\* If **No**: The process continues to step 2.1.
\* If **Yes**: The process loops back to the "Is max layers/stream reached?" check.
8. **2.1) select pair candidate UE pUe(j) min-max algo from rrmPairCandidateList 5G\_L2\_4290**: A pair candidate UE (pUe(j)) is selected using a min-max algorithm from the candidate list.
9. **Remove pUe(j) from rrmPairCandidateList**: The selected pair candidate UE is removed from the candidate list.
10. **2.2) MU Pair limitation check 5G\_L2\_4361**: A check is performed for MU (Multi-User) pair limitations.
11. **2.3) Spectrum Efficiency check 5G\_L2\_5467**: A check is performed for spectrum efficiency.
12. **2.4) Add pUe(j) to vUe(A) 5G\_L2\_5469**: The pair candidate UE is added to the virtual UE. This step is associated with a visual representation of vUe(A) having a size of 1, containing rUe(i), pUe(0), ..., pUe(j).
13. **3) SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation 5G\_L2\_4439**: Sub-cell, stream, and DMRS (Demodulation Reference Signal) are allocated.
14. **End**: The process concludes.
The flowchart uses dashed arrows to indicate data flow or state changes, particularly in relation to the size and content of vUe(A). The 5G\_L2 codes likely refer to specific functions or procedures within the 5G Layer 2 protocol stack.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3704.006.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

Note, in caes of actRankAdaptive2UeMuOptimzation=True as defined in 5G\_L2\_4196\_replaced([11165697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD6PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for the first MU candidate UE selected as root UE, if it's failed to MU pair with another MU candidate UE in step 2.1)corelation check or step 2.2) pair limitation check or step 2.3) Spectrum Efficiency check, it's need to downgrade rankMuUe(i) for both UE to min(rankSuUe(i), 2) again, and recalculate correlation table as defined in 5G\_L2\_4742\_replaced([11165729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD7vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), then do the MU pairing again with the downgraded rank.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.1 Step 1. Root UE Selection

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.1-1 Step 1. Root UE Selection

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.1-1.0-2**  (ID: `12560582`)

Step 1. Root UE Selection
The 1st selected UEs that add to a virtualUe(A) is the root UE,**[CB014861-B]For Exhaustive DL Scheduling (NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to DL or DLAndUL,refer to [12483324: Exhaustive Scheduling for RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qbsGEQ-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), root UE is the selected MU UE with highest TD metrics, else**[End CB014861-B]\*\*\*\* the root UE is selected from rrmMuCandidateList (5G\_L2\_4196\_replaced([11165697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD6PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the PdschSubarea[i] with following criterion:

* The selected root UE shall be allowedPrbUe>0 and < availablePrbsInPdschSubarea[i]
* Root UE is selected from rrmMuCandidateList should be reserved highest allowedPrbsUe from MU WRR.

Please refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7430\_replaced([11144070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more information.

Note: If the virtualUe(A).size is equal to 1 which means that this UE is not paired with any UE present in rrmMuCandidateList so then considered this MU non-paired UE as an SU eligible UE.

Allocation of resources for this non-paired MU UE need to do after completion of all virtualUes allocation.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.1-2 Step 1. Root UE Selection

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.1-2.0-2**  (ID: `12771478`)

Step 1. Root UE Selection
If NIDD:actDlMuPairingGroup is True, The ZF\_MU based  virtualUE shall be build, and the 1st selected UE that add to a virtualUe(A) is the ZF\_MU root UE, the root UE is selected from rrmZfMuCandidateList ([12571009: ZF\_Mu Candidate List](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_m4HnYS8BEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))  with following criterion:

* [CB012971-F] NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is other than DL or DLAndUL, [End CB012971-F] Root UE is selected from rrmZfMuCandidateList and should be reserved highest allowedPrbsUe after MU WRR.   [CB012971-F] NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is DL or DLAndUL, Root UE is selected from rrmZfMuCandidateList with highest TD metric. [End CB012971-F]
* The selected root UE shall be  allowedPrbUe>=bfSubbandSize.
* Force SU check for the root UE is executed, see [12627592: Force SU Check for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ZKCXITrQEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

where

bfSubbandSize: beamforming subband size that receives from high layer(see 5G\_L2\_IF\_115\_replaced([10968891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UaBKvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Please refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7430\_replaced([11144070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more information.

Note:

1, If the ZF\_virtualUe(A).size is equal to 1 which means that this subband beamforming UE is not paired with any UE present in rrmZfMuCandidateList, so then considered this ZF\_MU non-paired UE as an SU UE.

2, ZF\_MU UEs in different  pairing groups cannot be paired. WB BF UEs and SB BF UEs cannot be paired as well (see [11144070: Virtual UE construction](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

3, Also SB BF UEs which are already paired in SVD-RZF VUE shall NOT be allocated to any additional PRBs for PDSCH with SU mode( see [11145129: Allocate remaining RBGs/PRBs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_FBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), then allocation of resources for this non-paired ZF MU UE doesn't necessary to do after the completion of all virtualUes allocation.  But this leaves to SW implementation to decide.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.1.0-1 Step 1. Root UE Selection Select Root UE (ID: 11166138)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11166165`)

Step 1. Root UE Selection
[CB008927-P] If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False [End CB008927-P] The 1st selected UEs that add to a virtualUe(A) is the root UE, the root UE is selected from rrmMuCandidateList (5G\_L2\_4196\_replaced([11165697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD6PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with following criterion:

* Root UE is selected in priority descend order.
* The selected root UE shall be allowedRbgUe>0. The allowedRbgUe, is defined in 5G\_L2\_5527\_replaced([11165197](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOp_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for reTx UE, and defined in 5G\_L2\_4604\_replaced([11166710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HLPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for newTx UE.
* Specific restriction for reTx UE:

a) available PRB|RBG check

The selected UE(i) shall satisify:

(requestRbgUe(i) - allocatedRbgUe(i))<= availableRbg

where,

* request[Rbg]Ue: is the minRequest[Rbg] defined in 5G\_L2\_4512\_replaced([11165120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* allocated[Rbg]Ue: is the allocated RBG of the UE in previous virtualUes.
* available[Rbg]: is the available RBG in the BWP.

Note, this available RBG check is valid for pair UE selection too, refer to 5G\_L2\_4607\_replaced([11166370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2505FPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If the reTx UE is available RBG check failed, it's shall be remove from rrmMuCandidateList as it's cannot be scheduled in this slot.

[CB012605-E]

In case of DL  layerPrb pooling enabled (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE) ReTx shall satisfy additionally layerPrb availability condition:

(requestRbgUe(i) - allocatedRbgUe(i)) \* rbgSize \* layer(Ue(i)) <= rrmRemainingDlMuMimoLayerPrb

where

- rrmRemainingDlMuMimoLayerPrb is the reamining layer PRB capacity for MU-MIMO allocation in the cell as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27723\_replaced([11144557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8owvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note 1. In case of layerPrb pooling, cellPrb BBRM allocation for the cell  in current bbPoolingPeriod is  rrmMaxNumUsablePrb defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26582\_replaced([11214591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (see numAllocCellPrb value in 5G\_L2\_8852\_replaced([11243971](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3BzwXPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note 2. Corresponding CP2 requirement is 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7430\_replaced([11144070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB012605-E]

b) prioritize reTx UE with RBG partial allocated

When a reTx UE(i) is scheduled in a virtualUe(A), no matter as root UE or as pair UE, if the request RBG only partial allocated(requestRbgUe(i) - allocatedRbgUe(i) > 0) after the virtualUe(A) RBG allocation, this UE(i) shall be selected as root UE again in next vritualUe.

Special Case: If there has muilt reTx UEs are RBG partial allocated, all these UEs shall be included in next virtualUe, example as following digram. Note that in this case, the Force SU defined in 5G\_L2\_5545\_replaced([11166171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250R-fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall not apply on these RBG partial allocated reTx UEs, these muilt reTx UEs are still MU pairing in next virtual UE.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a diagram illustrating resource allocation for virtual UEs (User Equipment). The diagram is divided into two main sections, labeled "VirtualUE(A)" and "VirtualUE(B)".
In the "VirtualUE(A)" section, there are three rows representing different UEs: "UE1 (reTx)", "UE2 (reTx)", and "UE3 (reTx)". Each UE row contains two blocks: "allocatedRbg" (allocated Resource Block Group) and "requestRbg" (requested Resource Block Group). "UE1 (reTx)" is associated with "rootUe".
An orange arrow originates from a bracket encompassing "UE2 (reTx)" and "UE3 (reTx)" in the "VirtualUE(A)" section and points towards the "VirtualUE(B)" section. Text next to the bracket reads "UE2, UE3 be selected into next virtualUe directly".
The "VirtualUE(B)" section also has rows for "UE2 (reTx)", "UE3 (reTx)", and "UEx (..)". Similar to the left side, these rows contain "allocatedRbg" and "requestRbg" blocks. The "UE2 (reTx)" and "UE3 (reTx)" rows in "VirtualUE(B)" show a similar structure to their counterparts in "VirtualUE(A)", with the "allocatedRbg" and "requestRbg" blocks appearing to be transferred or mirrored. The "UEx (..)" row in "VirtualUE(B)" shows dashed outlines for "allocatedRbg" and "requestRbg", suggesting potential future allocations or a different state.
The diagram visually represents a process where UE2 and UE3 from VirtualUE(A) are selected to be part of the next virtual UE, indicated by the arrow and accompanying text. The blocks labeled "allocatedRbg" and "requestRbg" likely represent the resource management status for these UEs within their respective virtual UE contexts.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3709.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

MU WRR

Note, if it's first time to selected a newTx nonGbr UE as root UE, the MU WRR shall be execute accoding to 5G\_L2\_4604\_replaced([11166710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HLPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The first time of selected root UE is newTx UE is means, the reTx UE pairing is ended, there is pure newTx UE pairing remain. The MU WRR shall be execute before pure newTx UE pairing, and calculate allowedRbg for them.

Before MU WRR, the allowedRbgUe=0 for newTx UE, they can be seleted as pair UE by reTx root UE, but can not be seleted as root UE itself.

After MU WRR, If there still has free RBG not distributed, then it's means that the resource is enough to drain buffer for all UEs wihtout MU pairing,  so force to SU RBG allocation for all the new transmission UEs in next steps. While, for new transmisison UEs that already MU pairing with reTx UE and allocated RBGs, it's keep previous MU pairing and RBG allocation no change, just no MU pairing in next steps and only has SU RBG allocation.

[CB008927-P] Else

For Root UE selection please refer 5G\_L2\_20727\_replaced([11166182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250R-_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB008927-P]

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.1.0-2 Step 1. Root UE Selection Force SU Check (ID: 11166171)
