**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11166177`)

Step 1. Root UE Selection
For the selected root UE(i) for virtualUe(A), it's will force to SU RBG allocation in following case:

* rdDlMuMinRbgNum>0, and
* allowedRbgUe(i) < rdDlMuMinRbgNum, and
* not the special case of muilt reTx UEs with RBG partial allocated as defined in 5G\_L2\_5544\_replaced([11166138](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250R8vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Note, if there has any RBG partial allocated reTx UE with allocatedRbgUe(i)<rdDlMuMinRbgNum in the virtualUe, then the virtualUe shall not add more pair UEs.

rdDlMuMinRbgNum is RD parameter with default value 0(disable), refer to 5G\_L2\_4790\_replaced([11186749](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_277x2PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This is for C&P purpose, limite the minimum MU RBG can reduce the number of virtual UE, so that save the MU comsuming time.

For a root UE force to SU RBG allocation, pair UE selection is not needed, SU RBG allocated dirtectly, refer to 5G\_L2\_4441\_replaced([11166747](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252uMPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note, this is valid for pairing UE selection too, refer to 5G\_L2\_4362\_replaced([11166299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2505A_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7454\_replaced([11142605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-afvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2 Step 2. MU Pair UE Selection

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.1 Step 2.1. Pair Candidate UE Selection with Min-Max Algorithm

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.1.0-1 Step 2.1. Pair Candidate UE Selection with Min-Max Algorithm Virtual UE Limitation Check (ID: 11166206)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11166211`)

Step 2.1. Pair Candidate UE Selection with Min-Max Algorithm
Before select next pair candidate UE, the virtual UE limitation shall be shall be checked. Virtual UE is stop adding new pair UE if any of following limitation reachd:

1. virtualUe layer is reach the rrmDlMaxMuMimoLayer;
2. there is no available steam.

Refer to 5G\_L2\_4361\_replaced([11166294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2505AvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If none of the limitation reached, continue to select next pair candidate UE.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.1.0-2 Step 2.1. Pair Candidate UE Selection with Min-Max Algorithm Pair Candidate UE selection (ID: 11166216)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11166287`)

Step 2.1. Pair Candidate UE Selection with Min-Max Algorithm
[CB008927-P] While preparing the Pair Candidate List, there is no specific change for RAT1 when compared with RAT0.

Note: In the below text, the "RBG" word can be consider as in case of RAT0 it is in RBG granularity or in case of RAT1 it is in PRB granularity. [End CB008927-P]

Pair Candidate List

The pair candidate UE of virtualUe(A) is selected from rrmPairCandidateList(A), this list is created and initialize with rrmMuCandidateList after root UE selection, and root UE is exlcuded in the rrmPairCandidateList(A). When an UE is selected from rrmPairCandidateList(A), this UE is better to remove from the list for software efficiency purpose.

Implementation Informative:

Some limitation check defined in 5G\_L2\_4361\_replaced([11166294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2505AvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) can be check in advance during rrmPairCandidateList(A) creation/updating. Following figure is a example of creation and updating of this list:[xlsx\_desc]--- pairCandiateList ---
NaN NaN layer 4 2 2 2 2 2 2 4
NaN NaN rrmMuCandidateList UE0 UE1 UE2 UE3 UE4 UE5 UE6 UE7
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN virtualUe(A) creation ue0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN ue1 ue2 ue4 ue6 ue7 ue3 ue5
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN <1st round>\nue4 selected, MU check pass ue0 ue4 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN ue1 ue2 ue6 ue4 ue7 ue3 ue5
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN <2nd round>\nue2 selected, MU check fail ue0 ue4 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN
NaN NaN NaN NaN ue1 ue6 ue2 ue4 ue7 ue3 ue5[/xlsx\_desc][xlsx\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/attachments/m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file3720\_0.xlsx[/xlsx\_path]

Pair Candidate UE selection

If dlMuMimoCorrThd<1.0(max value), L2PS shall use below min-max algorithm to select optimal candidate which can be added into virtualUE(A).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in dark blue text on a white background. The equation is presented in two lines, with the second line being a simplification of the first.
The first line reads:
$i\_A = \underset{i \in {rrmPairCandidateList} \&\& i \notin virtualUe(A)}{\operatorname{argmin}} \left{ \underset{j \in {virtualUe(A)}}{\max} (\max(corr\_{i,j}, corr\_{j,i})) \right}$
The second line reads:
$ = \underset{i \in {rrmPairCandidateList} \&\& i \notin virtualUe(A)}{\operatorname{argmin}} {maxCorr\_{A,i}}$
There are two highlighted pink rectangular areas in the image. The first is around the expression $\underset{j \in {virtualUe(A)}}{\max} (\max(corr\_{i,j}, corr\_{j,i}))$. The second is around the expression ${maxCorr\_{A,i}}$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3720.002.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

* corr: the correlation value is defined in 5G\_L2\_4230\_replaced([11165470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycxPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_4742\_replaced([11165729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD7vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), based of the stored corrLayer in rrmCorrTable, [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in dark gray text on a light blue background. The equation is:
  $corr\_{i,j} = \max(corrLayer\_{i\_0,j}, corrLayer\_{i\_1,j})$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3720.003.png[/image\_path][/image-1].

Implementation proposal: the value of  [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "max (corr\_{i,j}, corr\_{j,i})". The text is dark blue and appears on a white background. The expression is written in a standard mathematical font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3720.004.png[/image\_path][/image-2] can be stored in rrmCorrTable too to improve the software efficiency.

The correlation value [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "maxCorr\_{A,i}". The text is in a dark blue color and is italicized. The letters are slightly blurred, and there is a faint upward arrow above the letter 'C'.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3720.005.png[/image\_path][/image-3] between the selected candidate [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a close-up of a digital display showing the letters "i" and "A". The letters are rendered in a pixelated, blocky style, with a gradient of colors, including blue, orange, and yellow, giving them a somewhat iridescent or metallic appearance. The background is white. The overall impression is that of a digital interface or a stylized font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3720.006.png[/image\_path][/image-4]and virtualUE(A) shall be less than dlMuMimoCorrThd, otherwise no candidate can be selected.

For example: assume there has 2 UEs (j1, j2) in virtual UE, and another 3 UEs (i1, i2, i3) in rrmPairCandidateList.

1) Calculate correlation for each MU candidate UE with virtual UE:

corrUe(i1) = max{ max(corr(i1, j1), corr(j1, i1)), max(corr(i1, j2), corr(j2, i1)) }

corrUe (i2) = max{ max(corr(i2, j1), corr(j1, i2)), max(corr(i2, j2), corr(j2, i2)) }

corrUe(i3) = max{ max(corr(i3, j1), corr(j1, i3)), max(corr(i3, j2), corr(j2, i3)) }

2) Select a UE(i) with smallest correlation with UEs in virtual UE, and minCorr < dlMuMimoCorrThd

minCorr = min {corrUe(i1), corrUe(i2), corrUe(i3)}

[image-5][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating correlations between different User Equipment (UE) entities. There are three UE entities on the left side, labeled UE\_i1, UE\_i2, and UE\_i3, arranged vertically. On the right side, there are two UE entities, labeled UE\_j1 and UE\_j2, arranged vertically.
Arrows indicate correlations between these UEs.
- A red arrow originates from UE\_i2 and points to UE\_j1, labeled "Corr\_i1j1". This label appears to be a typo and should likely be "Corr\_i2j1" given the arrow's origin.
- Another red arrow originates from UE\_i2 and points to UE\_j2, labeled "Corr\_i1j2". Similar to the previous label, this likely should be "Corr\_i2j2".
- A blue arrow originates from UE\_i3 and points to UE\_j1.
- Another blue arrow originates from UE\_i3 and points to UE\_j2.
The diagram visually represents potential interference or correlation relationships between different user devices in a network.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3720.007.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

Blind MU Pairing:

If dlMuMimoCorrThd=1.0(max value), blind MU pairing is enabled, then L2PS select pair UE from rrmPairCandidateList directly without min-max algorithm and correlation check, assume always correlation check pass. Also refer to 5G\_L2\_5467\_replaced([11166474](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_251gHvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SE check bypass for blind MU pairing.

Note, the dlMuMimoCorrThd is OAM parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7436\_replaced([11143836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2 Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-1 Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check Force SU Check (ID: 11166299)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11166317`)

Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check
**[CB008927-P] NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False,** [End CB008927-P] When a new pair candidate UE(i) is selected to add to virtual UE(A), the allowedRbgUe(i) shall not satisify:\*\*\*\*

* rdDlMuMinRbgNum>0, and
* allowedRbgUe(i) < rdDlMuMinRbgNum, and
* rootUe is reTx and Ue(i) is reTx, or rootUe is newTx. Note, this is consider for allowedRbgUe is 0 for newTx UE before MU WRR.

Refer to 5G\_L2\_5545\_replaced([11166171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250R-fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* rootUe and Ue(i) have different DL  Dmrs Config Type.

Note, the SU check can be execute during the step of pairing UE selection(5G\_L2\_5547\_replaced([11166216](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250SAfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) too, depend on implementation.

**[CB008927-P] Else**

* requiredPrbUe(i) >= rrmMuMinPrbNum.

Please refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26957\_replaced([11142663](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5lcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_22375\_replaced([11188719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28KbWPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of rrmMuMinPrbNum**.**

Please refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26503\_replaced([11144193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7aqvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for allowedPrbUe(i).

**[End CB008927-P]**

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-2 Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check Layer Limitation Check (ID: 11166322)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11166331`)

Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check
When a new pair candidate UE(i) is selected to add to virtual UE(A), the virtual UE layer shall not exceed maxDlMuMimoLayer:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical inequality written in italicized font. The text reads: "Layer\_{virtual Ue(A)} + Layer\_{i,i \in virtual Ue(A)} \leq maxD MuMimoLayer".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3727.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note:

* maxDlMuMimoLayer, refer to 5G\_L2\_4500\_replaced([11165748](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zq6fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* [CB008305-B] layeri, is equal to rankMuUe(i), refer to 5G\_L2\_5008\_replaced([11165331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1u_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB008305-B].

Note, the layer limitation check can be execute during the step of pairing UE selection(5G\_L2\_5547\_replaced([11166216](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250SAfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) too, depend on implementation.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7436\_replaced([11143836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-3 Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check SubCellIndex and StreamIndex Limitation Check (ID: 11166336)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11166342`)

Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check
When a new pair candidate UE(i) is selected to add to virtualUe(A), the subCellIndex and streamIndex shall be checked as follows:

IF ( UE(i) has subCellIndex and streamIndex already allocated )

a) Check if UE(i) is streamIndex confilicate with exist UEs in virtualUe(A).

ELSE

b) Check there has available subCellIndex and streamIndex for UE(i). [CB008305-C] Note, in case of DL subCell pooling enabled, the number of available DL subCell can be changed accoding to subCell pooling algorithm, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5230\_replaced([11210126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw3lB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008305-C]

c) If numberOfUeInToBeScheduledList > rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell, L2PS shall additional check the number of UE in the subCell is not exceed the limitation of rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell.  The numberOfUeInToBeScheduledList is the number of UE in Cs2List|toBeScheduledList for FDM scheduling, refer to 5G\_L2\_4282\_replaced([11165013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25wnk_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4327\_replaced([11177617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwsNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). numberOfUeInToBeScheduledList <=rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4478\_replaced([11178989](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2y2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl  and rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell.

END

Refer to 5G\_L2\_4439\_replaced([11166624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HJPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7436\_replaced([11143836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7955\_replaced([11142718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5lefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-4 Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check SubCellIndex and StreamIndex Limitation Check Informative (ID: 11166347)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11166364`)

Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check
Implementation Informative:

Per virtualUe, define:

* virtualUe(X).streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[]  :  used for UE with streamIndex already allocated. The Step3 streamIndex allocation is based on this variable too, refer to 5G\_L2\_4439\_replaced([11166624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HJPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Initialization: streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[] ={0000, 0000}; //assume 2 C4 subCell.
* Update: when a UE add to this virtualUe and this UE with streamIndex already allocated.
* virtualUe(X).numberOfAvailableStreamIndex  : used for all UEs add to the virtualUe, include both streamIndex allocated and NOT allocated UEs. When a UE add to virtualUe, it's need reserve corresponding number of streamIndex and update this variable.
* Initialization: numberOfAvailableStreamIndex = 2\*4; //assume 2 C4 subCell.
* Update: when a UE add to this virtualUe.
* virtualUe(X).numberOfReservedSubCellFor4StreamUe : used for rank3/4 UEs which need 4 streams when add to the virtualUe, include only streamIndex NOT allocated UEs.
* Initialization: numberOfReservedSubCellFor4StreamUe = 0;
* Update: when a rank3/4 UE add to this virtualUe.
* virtualUe(X).numberOfUeWithoutSubCell : used for UEs without subCell allocated.
* Initialization: numberOfUeWithoutSubCell = 0;
* Update: when a UE add to this virtualUe and this UE without subCell allocated.
* virtualUe(X).numberOfUeWithSubCell : used for UEs with subCell allocated.
* Initialization: numberOfUeWithSubCell= 0;
* Update: when a UE add to this virtualUe and this UE with subCell allocated.

Example:

* Assumption/Precondition:
* Some virtualUes has allocated already, and current virtualUe(A) already is:

virtualUe(A).streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[] = {00 00, 11 00} , only subCell0's streamIndex{2,3} used, so subCell1 can allocate for addition max 2 UEs and sbuCell0 and allocate for max addition 1 UE.

virtualUe(A).numberOfAvailableStreamIndex = 6.

virtualUe(X).numberOfReservedSubCellFor4StreamUe = 0.

virtualUe(X).numberOfUeWithoutSubCell  = 0.

virtualUe(X).numberOfUeInSubCell[]  = {0,1}.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation for virtual UEs (User Equipment) across two subcells, subCell1 and subCell0. The diagram uses a grid-like structure with labels and annotations to explain the conditions for adding virtual UEs.
The grid is divided into two main rows, representing subCell1 and subCell0. Each subcell row is further divided into columns. The leftmost column lists "stream 3", "stream 2", "stream 1", and "stream 0" for both subcells. The next column displays "numberOfUeSubCell(1)" for subCell1 and "numberOfUeSubCell(0)" for subCell0, indicating the number of UEs allocated to each subcell.
To the right of these columns, there are two distinct sections. The top section, colored blue, is labeled "allocated virtualUes virtualUe(A)". This section appears to represent the virtual UEs allocated to subCell1. The bottom section, colored light green, is labeled "allocated" and is associated with subCell0.
Red arrows point from specific cells within the "allocated virtualUes virtualUe(A)" section to textual annotations on the right. These annotations describe the conditions for adding more UEs to subCell1. Specifically, it states: "max 2 addition UEs can be add to subCell1 for virtualUe(A) if : (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell - numberOfUeSubCell(1)) >=2".
Another red arrow points from the blue section to the text "available streams for virtualUe(A)".
Similarly, red arrows originate from the light green "allocated" section for subCell0 and point to a textual annotation: "max 1 addition UEs can be add to subCello for virtualue(A) if : (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell - numberOfUeSubCell(0)) >=1".
Below the subcell rows, there is a bracketed label "rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell", suggesting this is a maximum limit for UEs across FDM (Frequency Division Multiplexing) in a subcell.
The table structure can be represented as follows:

| Subcell | Stream | Number of UEs | Allocated Virtual UEs (subCell1) | Allocated Virtual UEs (subCell0) | Condition for Adding UEs to subCell1 | Condition for Adding UEs to subCell0 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| subCell1 | stream 3 | numberOfUeSubCell(1) |  |  | max 2 addition UEs can be add to subCell1 for virtualUe(A) if : (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell - numberOfUeSubCell(1)) >=2 |  |
|  | stream 2 |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | stream 1 |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | stream 0 |  |  |  |  |  |
| subCell0 | stream 3 | numberOfUeSubCell(0) |  |  |  | max 1 addition UEs can be add to subCello for virtualue(A) if: (rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell - numberOfUeSubCell(0)) >=1 |
|  | stream 2 |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | stream 1 |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | stream 0 |  |  |  |  |  |
| The diagram visually represents a system where the number of virtual UEs that can be added to a subcell is constrained by a maximum limit (`rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell`) and the current number of allocated UEs in that subcell. The conditions are different for subCell1 and subCell0, indicating varying resource availability or configuration.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3732.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |  |  |  |

* Step of streamIndex check, when try add a canddate UE(i) to virtualUe(A) //current step.

a) If UE(i) with streamIndex allocated already. Assume rankMuUe(i)=2,  UE(i).numberOfStream=2, UE(i).subCellIndex=1, UE(i).streamIndexBitmap={1100}. Check the streamIndex confilication as follows:

isStreamIndexConflicate = False;

IF (virtualUe(A).numberOfAvailableStreamIndex < UE(i).numberOfStream )

isStreamIndexConflicate = True;

ELSE IF( streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[UE(i).subCellIndex] & UE(i).streamIndexBitmap )   // check if confilicate with allocated streamIndex

isStreamIndexConflicate = True;

ELSE IF( numberOfAllocateSubCell(virtualUe(A).streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[], UE(i).subCellIndex) + virtualUe(X).numberOfReservedSubCellFor4StreamUe > rrmNumDlSubcells)    // check if conflicted with reserved 4Stream Ue streamIndex

isStreamIndexConflicate = True;

END

Note, numberOfAllocateSubCell(streamIndexBitMapPerSubCell, subCellIndexOfNewCandidateUe) is a function to return the total to be allocated subCell number.

Example 1:  streamIndexBitMapPerSubCell={0000,1100}. subCellIndexOfNewCandidateUe=1, => return 2.

Example 1:  streamIndexBitMapPerSubCell={0000,1100}. subCellIndexOfNewCandidateUe=0, => return 1.

b) If UE(i) without streamIndex allocated yet. Assume rankMuUe(i)=2,  UE(i).numberOfStream=2, UE(i).streamIndexBitmask[]={1100,0011}. Check the streamIndex availablity as follows:

isStreamIndexAvailable = False;

IF (virtualUe(A).numberOfAvailableStreamIndex >= UE(i).numberOfStream AND

(UE(i).numberOfStream==2) OR (UE(i).numberOfStream==4 AND numberOfAllocatedSubCell(virtualUe(A).streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[]) + virtualUe(A).numberOfReservedSubCellFor4StreamUe < rrmNumDlSubCells))

)

FOR ( subCell s from 0 to rrmNumDlSubCells)

IF (streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[s] | UE(i).streamIndexBitmask[0] == 0 OR treamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[s] | UE(i).streamIndexBitmask[1] == 0)  // check if streamIndex available

isStreamIndexAvailable = True;

Return;

END

END

END

* Step of check max number of UE limitation of subCell, when try add a canddate UE(i) to virtualUe(A). //current step.

c) IF (numberOfUeInToBeScheduledList > rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell):

numberOfUeRemainAllowedOfAllSubCell = SUMs { numberOfUeRemainAllowedInSubCell[s] }, s is from subCell 0 to rrmNumDlSubcells-1;

numberOfUeRemainAllowedOfVirtualUe = rrmNumDlSubcells \* maxUePerSubCellPerVirtualUe - virtualUe(X).numberOfUeWithSubCell ;

isMaxNumberOfUeInAllSubCellReached = True; .

IF (virtualUe(A).numberOfUeWithoutSubCell +1 <= min{numberOfUeRemainAllowedOfAllSubCell,  numberOfUeRemainAllowedOfVirtualUe } )

isMaxNumberOfUeInAllSubCellReached  = False;

END

Where,

* numberOfUeRemainAllowedInSubCell[s] = rrmMaxNumUesFdmDlSubcell - numberOfUeSubCell[s],  the numberOfUeSubCell[s] is the number of all already allocated UEs in subCell[s].
* rrmNumDlSubcells, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10610\_replaced([11179079](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku3Z6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), is 2 subCells  in this example.
* maxUePerSubCellPerVirtualUe = 2 for C4 subCell.
* Step of success add UE(i) to virtualUe(A) //step 2.4, refer to 5G\_L2\_5469\_replaced([11166611](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HIvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

d) If UE(i) with streamIndex allocated already. Assume rankMuUe(i)=2,  UE(i).numberOfStream=2, UE(i).subCellIndex=1, UE(i).streamIndexBitmap={1100}.

Update virtualUe(X).streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[] = {1100, 1100};

Update virtualUe(A).numberOfAvailableStreamIndex -= UE(i).numberOfStream; //6-2=4.

Update virtualUe(X).numberOfUeWithSubCell+=1; //0+1=1

e) If UE(i) without streamIndex allocated yet. Assume rankMuUe(i)=2,  UE(i).numberOfStream=2.

Update virtualUe(A).numberOfAvailableStreamIndex -= UE(i).numberOfStream; //6-2=4.

Update virtualUe(A).numberOfUeWithoutSubCell +=1; //0+1=1

IF UE(i).numberOfStream==4:

virtualUe(X).numberOfReservedSubCellFor4StreamUe +=1;

END

[CB008701-A]

Note, if nrCellType is 16DLxUL (x can be any value), there should assume 4 C4 subCells in the above implementation informative.

[End CB008701-A]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7436\_replaced([11143836](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6zm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7955\_replaced([11142718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5lefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),[CB008701-A] 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11372\_replaced([11145207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_sEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11377\_replaced([11145267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_sGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB008701-A]

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-5 Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check Available PRB &amp; RBG Limitation Check for ReTx Ue (ID: 11166370)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-5.0-5**  (ID: `11166423`)

Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check
If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False When a new pair candidate UE(i) is selected, if it's a reTx UE, there shall has enough available PRB|RBGs to satisfy the reTx UE's request RBG:

(requestRbgUe(i) - allocatedRbgUe(i))<= availableRbg

Refer to 5G\_L2\_5544\_replaced([11166138](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250R8vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note, if the reTx UE is available RBG check failed, it shall be removed from rrmMuCandidateList as it cannot be scheduled in this slot.

Moreover,

if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed = TRUE, when a new pair candidate UE(i) is selected for VirtualUE(A)

L2-PS shall perform the following additional check:

 If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed = TRUE

o If UE(i) is reTx

 If (layer(VirtualUE(A)) + layer(Ue(i)) > 4) and (maxDlMuMimoLayer > 4) (i.e. VirtualUE(A) needs to be scheduled on 2 subcells)

 If allowedRbg(UE(i)) > rrmMuAvailableNumSubcellPrb/rbgSize – maxAllowedRbg(VirtualUE(A))

o UE(i) is not selected to add to virtualUE(A)

Where:

 maxAllowedRbg(vUE): [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a single line of mathematical text. The text is blurry and difficult to read. It appears to be a mathematical expression or formula.
The text reads: "max(allowedRbg (Ue(j))),vje ((UE(j) e vUE) and (UE(j) is reTx))"[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3738.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

 maxDlMuMimoLayer: maximum number of MU layers per slot, as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7446\_replaced([11142759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 rrmMuAvailableSubcellPrb: is the usable Subcell PRBs as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14518\_replaced([11144879](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr924_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 rbgSize: is the number of PRBs per RBG for RAT0, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE, when a new pair candidate UE(i) is selected for VirtualUE(A)

L2-PS shall perform the following additional check:

 If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE

o If Ue(i) is reTx

 If  PRB( allowedRbg(Ue(i))  )  \* layer(Ue(i)) +  PRB(maxAllowedRbg(VirtualUE(A))) \* layer(VirtualUE(A)) > rrmRemainingDlMuMimoLayerPrb

Ue(i) is not selected to add to virtualUE(A)

Where:

 maxAllowedRbg(vUE): [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a single line of mathematical text. The text is blurry and difficult to read. It appears to be a mathematical expression or formula.
The text reads: "max(allowedRbg (Ue(j))),vje ((UE(j) e vUE) and (UE(j) is reTx))"[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3738.001.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

 rrmRemainingDlMuMimoLayerPrb is the reamining layer PRB capacity for MU-MIMO allocation in the cell as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27723\_replaced([11144557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8owvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 PRB(x) is a function that converts the number of RBG into cell PRBs including also the non-full/partial last RBG

Else

In case of RAT1, for ReTx UEs L2-PS shall reserve the FD resources before WRR if enough availablePrbs are present else shall remove the ReTx UE from the rrmMuCandidateList as it can not be Schedule in curent slot.

[CB010630-E] Available Prb check for slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed enabled state is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23663\_replaced([11177504](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB010630-E]

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-6 Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check Available PRB &amp; RBG Limitation for newTx UE (ID: 11166430)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11166432`)

Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check
If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False and  slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = True when a newTx pair candidate UE(i) is selected for VirtualUE(A)

L2-PS shall perform the following additional step before selection:

       If allowedRbg(Ue(i)) \* RBG size \* layer(Ue(i)) +  PRB(maxAllowedRbg(VirtualUE(A))) \* layer(VirtualUE(A)) > rrmRemainingDlMuMimoLayerPrb

allowedRbg(UE(i)) = min{allowedRbg(Ue(i)), (rrmRemainingDlMuMimoLayerPrb -  PRB(maxAllowedRbg(VirtualUE(A))) \* layer(VirtualUE(A))) / (RBG size \* layer(Ue(i)))}

Where:

 maxAllowedRbg(vUE): max(allowedRbg(Ue(j))) where Ue(j) are all UEs already selected in VirtualUE(A)

 rrmRemainingDlMuMimoLayerPrb is the reamining layer PRB capacity for MU-MIMO allocation in the cell as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27723\_replaced([11144557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8owvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 PRB(x) is a function that converts the number of RBG into cell PRBs including also the non-full/partial last RBG

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-7 Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check VirtualUe Size Limitation Check for ReTx UE (ID: 11166438)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.2.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `12715142`)

Step 2.2. MU Pair Limitation Check
When a new pair candidate UE(i) is seleted, if it's a reTx UE or there has any other ReTx UE in virtualUE(A),

**[Before PR842908]**

IF rdRestrictReTxUeForVirtualUe is disable

L2RT shall do additional checking for each ReTx UE to ensure below condition is meet, otherwise, the candidate UE shall not be added into virtualUE(A).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression written in a blurry, italicized font. The text reads: "avgVirtualUeSizeOfUe; ≥ virtualUeSizea+ij ∈ {A, i) and Ue(j) is ReTx".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3743.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where:

* [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the text "avgVirtualUeSizeOfUe₁". The text is in a dark blue color and is slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3743.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1], it's the average virtual UE size of initial TX of the ReTx UE, refer to 5G\_L2\_4505\_replaced([11166982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538VPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* virtualUeSizeA : size of virtualUe(A), see 5G\_L2\_4282\_replaced([11165013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25wnk_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The rational of this limitation is to avoid the MU interference of reTx UE not become worse compare with it's initialTx.

Example 1: if InitTx of UE(i) is not paired, its ReTx shall not pair as well because its virtual UE size of InitTx is 1.

Example 2: If ReTx UE(i) is added into a virtualUE(A) as root UE, and average virtual UE size of its InitTx is 2, then virtualUE(A) can only two UEs MU.

END

Note, the default value for rdRestrictReTxUeForVirtualUe is enable, refer to 5G\_L2\_9455\_replaced([11186792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_278Y4PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7428\_replaced([11143900](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6znfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[ PR842908]**

The necessary of checking  virtualUeSize restriction refer SFS [11143900: Check SE of paired UEs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6znfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[End PR842908]

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.3 Step 2.3. SE(Spectrum Efficiency) Check

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.3.0-1 Step 2.3. SE(Spectrum Efficiency) Check Spectrum Efficiency Check (ID: 11166474)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.3.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11166602`)

Step 2.3. SE(Spectrum Efficiency) Check
Note, if dlMuMimoCorrThd=1.0(max value), blind MU pairing is enabled, the SE check defined in this section is not needed and assume it's always SE check pass.

[CNI-126638]

if dlMuMimoSpectralEffThd equals to 0, the spectrum efficiency related checks shall be skipped. refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7428\_replaced([11143900](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6znfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CNI-126638]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7428\_replaced([11143900](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6znfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.4 Step 2.4. Add Pair Candidate UE to VirtualUe

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.4.0-1 Step 2.4. Add Pair Candidate UE to VirtualUe Add Pair Candidate UE to VirtualUe (ID: 11166611)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.2.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11166617`)

Step 2.4. Add Pair Candidate UE to VirtualUe
If all steps (step 2.2 defined in 5G\_L2\_4361\_replaced([11166294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2505AvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), step 2.3 defined in 5G\_L2\_5573\_replaced([11166469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_251gHfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) check pass, the pair candidate UE(i) can be add to virtualUe(A).  Then continue next pair candidate UE selection (5G\_L2\_4290\_replaced([11166200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250R_vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.3 Step2a. ZF_MU Pair UE Selection

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.3.0-1 Step2a. ZF_MU Pair UE Selection Force SU Check for ZF_MU (ID: 12627592)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12627594`)

Step2a. ZF\_MU Pair UE Selection
IF zero forcing Mu function is enable (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True), during ZF\_MU virtual UE construction , the Force SU Check is executed.  This is to workaround L1's restriction (two subband BF (virtual)UEs can't be allocated in same bf subband).

To workaround this by:

Option 1,  L2PS may reduce several allocated PRB to align with  BF subband boundary, with minimum value of rrmZfMuMinPrbNum=Max( bfSubbandSize, rdDlMuMinPrbNum)  to avoid too much reducing cause this UE can't be scheduled. ( see [11177767: FD Resource Allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_268TXvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for detail).

Option 2, or L1 will use adjacent subband's weight. Then L2PS shall make sure at least the allocated PRBs is >= bfSubbandSize.  If  ZF MU eligible UE in [12571009: ZF\_Mu Candidate List](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_m4HnYS8BEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)  with AllowedPrbUei <  bfSubbandSize , this UE shall be forced to SU scheduling.

Option 2 is final selected.

Informative,  The purpose of force su checking in ZF-MU is not  for the C&P purpose.

where

-bfSubbandSize is beamforming subband size,  see [10968891: bfSetup structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UaBKvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

-  ZF\_MU UE's start PRB adjustment  see  [11177767: FD Resource Allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_268TXvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

-  AllowedPrbUei  see [11144637: Calculate total number of allowed PRB in virtualUE (A)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8oy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

Note, this is valid for both root and  pairing UE selection , [12620812: Step1a. Root UE Selection for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HOI88DlJEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)  ,[12582836: Step2a. ZF\_MU Pair UE Selection](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_b6bh1zSTEfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

CP2 reference:   [11142605: Build MU candidate list](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-afvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.3.0-2 Step2a. ZF_MU Pair UE Selection ZF_MU pair UE Selection (ID: 12630350)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12582846`)

Step2a. ZF\_MU Pair UE Selection
This step is executed when zero forcing Mu function is enable(actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True),  and Root UE is ZF\_MU eligible UE ( [12571009: ZF\_Mu Candidate List](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_m4HnYS8BEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) .

Step2a.1.  Pair candidate UE selection

- UE is ZF\_MU eligible and in the same paring group with root UE. ([12565792: DL pairing group(for SB BF UEs)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_HJrRACvSEfCNjfqfyUlvmw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) .

If there has only one ZF\_MU eligible UE from the same dl pairing group with root UE when rank downgrade is enabled, the  averaging rank of two UEs are checked to decide if can be ZF\_MU or not, see [12568847: Only two UEs paring with high rank for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YtX0kiy_EfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

Else continue following steps for ZF virtual UE  construction.

Step2a.2.  MU pair Limitation Check

-   Force SU check ,  Layer Limitation check, SubCellIndex and StreamIndex Limitation Check, Available PRB & RBG Limitation Check for ReTx Ue,Available PRB & RBG Limitation for newTx UE ,are same as WB BF MU which defined in step2.2.    And VirtualUe Size Limitation Check for ReTx UE will not be applied to ZF\_MU, [11186792: rdRestrictReTxUeForVirtualUe](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_278Y4PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) .

For force SU check, refer [12627592: Force SU Check for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ZKCXITrQEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

Step2a.3.  SE( Spectrum Check)

- There is no need execute SE checking for ZF\_MU UEs. ([11144070: Virtual UE construction](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))

Step2a.4.  Add Pair candidate UE to VirtualUe

- If all steps ( Step 2a.1 and Step 2a.2) check passes, the pair candidate UE(i) can be added to ZfVirtualUe(A). Then continue next pair candidate UE selection.

Stpe2a.5  Only two UEs paring with high rank check

- If  this ZfVirtualUe  construction finished, but only two UEs in this ZF virtual UE, additional step of averaging rank of two UEs are checked to decide if can be ZF\_MU or SU, see [12568847: Only two UEs paring with high rank for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_YtX0kiy_EfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.4 Step 3. SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.4.0-1 Step 3. SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation Allocation of subCell/stream/DMRS for a virtual UE (ID: 11166630)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11166689`)

Step 3. SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation
There are 4 steamIndex per C4 subCell. The number of available subCell in the cell, rrmNumDlSubcells, is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4478\_replaced([11178989](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2y2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note, in case of DL subCell pooling enabled, the rrmNumDlSubcells can be changed accoding to subCell pooling algorithm, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5230\_replaced([11210126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw3lB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008830-B] Here are some examples of DMRS port allocation with Dmrs Type 1 [End CB008830-B]

Following is a example of 2 C4 subCells case:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a table that illustrates the DMRS (Demodulation Reference Signal) allocation based on the number of layers, subcell index, and scrambling ID.
The table has the following structure:
\* **Header Row:** The top row contains the title "DMRS Allocation" spanning across the columns for the number of layers: "1 layer", "2 layers", "3 layers", and "4 layers".
\* **Leftmost Columns:**
\* The first column lists "subCellIndex 1" and "subCellIndex 0".
\* The second column, associated with each subcell index, lists "streamIndex 3", "streamIndex 2", "streamIndex 1", and "streamIndex 0".
\* **Middle Column (Scrambling ID):** This column indicates the scrambling ID associated with each subcell index. "subCellIndex 1" is associated with "scrmablingID1" (blue background), and "subCellIndex 0" is associated with "scrmablingID0" (green background).
\* **Data Cells (Port Allocation):** The cells within the table show the port allocation for DMRS based on the number of layers.
\* **For subCellIndex 1 (scrmablingID1):**
\* **1 layer:** port 2
\* **2 layers:** port 3, port 2
\* **3 layers:** port 2, port 1
\* **4 layers:** port 3, port 2, port 1, port 0
\* **For subCellIndex 0 (scrmablingID0):**
\* **1 layer:** port 0
\* **2 layers:** port 1, port 0
\* **3 layers:** port 1, port 0
\* **4 layers:** port 1, port 0
It appears there's a slight inconsistency in the "1 layer" and "2 layers" rows for both subcell indices when compared to the "3 layers" and "4 layers" rows. Specifically, the "1 layer" and "2 layers" rows for "subCellIndex 1" and "subCellIndex 0" seem to be grouped differently than the "3 layers" and "4 layers" rows.
Here's the table in markdown format:

|  | DMRS Allocation | 1 layer | 2 layers | 3 layers | 4 layers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | streamIndex 3 |  | port 3 | port 2 | port 3 |
|  | streamIndex 2 | port 2 | port 2 | port 1 | port 2 |
| subCellIndex 1 | streamIndex 1 |  | port 1 | port 0 | port 1 |
|  | streamIndex 0 | port 0 | port 0 | port 0 | port 0 |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | streamIndex 3 |  | port 3 | port 2 | port 3 |
|  | streamIndex 2 | port 2 | port 2 | port 1 | port 2 |
| subCellIndex 0 | streamIndex 1 |  | port 1 | port 0 | port 1 |
|  | streamIndex 0 | port 0 | port 0 | port 0 | port 0 |
| **Note:** The "scrmablingID1" and "scrmablingID0" labels are visually grouped with the "streamIndex" rows and the "port" allocations, but they don't fit neatly into a standard markdown table cell structure without further interpretation of their grouping. The markdown above attempts to represent the visual layout as closely as possible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3757.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |  |  |

Following is an example of 4 C4 subCells case:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a table that illustrates DMRS allocation. The table has a header row with "DMRS Allocation" spanning across four columns: "1layer", "2layers", "3layers", and "4layers".
The leftmost column is divided into four rows, each labeled with "subCellIndex" followed by a number from 3 down to 0 (subCellIndex3, subCellIndex2, subCellIndex1, subCellIndex0). Each of these "subCellIndex" rows is further subdivided into four rows, each labeled with "streamIndex" followed by a number from 3 down to 0.
The second column from the left contains "scramblingID" values. For subCellIndex3, subCellIndex2, and subCellIndex1, the scrambling ID is "scrmablingID1". For subCellIndex0, the scrambling ID is "scrmablingID0".
The remaining columns represent the DMRS allocation for different layer configurations.
Here is the table represented in markdown:

| subCellIndex | streamIndex | 1layer | 2layers | 3layers | 4layers |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| subCellIndex3 | streamIndex 3 | port2 | port3 | port2 | port3 |
|  | streamIndex 2 |  | port2 | port1 | port2 |
|  | streamIndex 1 | scrmablingID1 | port1 | port0 | port1 |
|  | streamIndex 0 | port0 | port0 | port0 | port0 |
| subCellIndex2 | streamIndex 3 | port2 | port3 | port2 | port3 |
|  | streamIndex 2 |  | port2 | port1 | port2 |
|  | streamIndex 1 | scrmablingID1 | port1 | port0 | port1 |
|  | streamIndex 0 | port0 | port0 | port0 | port0 |
| subCellIndex1 | streamIndex 3 | port2 | port3 | port2 | port3 |
|  | streamIndex 2 |  | port2 | port1 | port2 |
|  | streamIndex 1 | scrmablingID1 | port1 | port0 | port1 |
|  | streamIndex 0 | port0 | port0 | port0 | port0 |
| subCellIndex0 | streamIndex 3 | port2 | port3 | port2 | port3 |
|  | streamIndex 2 |  | port2 | port1 | port2 |
|  | streamIndex 1 | scrmablingID0 | port1 | port0 | port1 |
|  | streamIndex 0 | port0 | port0 | port0 | port0 |

[CB008830-B]

For Dmrs Type 2 examples of Dmrs allocation can be found in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25105\_replaced([11145336](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksATJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25224\_replaced([11145370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksATKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For Dmrs Type 2, DMRS allocation for UEs within a virtual UE is performed from  highest rank UE to lowest rank  UE , in order to optimize allocation of Dmrs Ports

The subcellIndex and streamIndex allocation is performed independantly from allocated Dmrs Port using same algorithm as for Dmrs Type 1 subcellIndex and stream index allocation.

For Dmrs Type 1 [End CB008830-B] the streamIndex/layer to DMRS port/scramblingID mapping is defined too, to simplify the DMRS allocation.

Value of the field ‘Antenna port(s)’ in DCI 1\_1 is defined in table 7.3.1.2.2-1 of TS 38.212 according to allocated DMRS port(s) per UE. The number of CDM group(s) without data is determined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3054\_replaced([11129150](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6uCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In case of  DL data and DMRS FDM enabled [Before CB009568](actDlDataDmrsFdm = True)[CB009568](actLocalDmrsFdm = True)[End CB009568]:

* MU UE:

-[CB008830-B] if Dmrs Type 1 is configured [End CB008830-B] DL data and DMRS FDM is not supported for MU UE, only "Number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data=2" is supported.

-[CB008830-B] if Dmrs Type 2 is configured, DMRS FDM is supported for DL Data and number of DMRS CDM Groups without data is computed according to requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25267\_replaced([11145445](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksATL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  [End CB008830-B]

* SU UE: both DL data and DMRS FDM("Number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data=1") and no FDM("Number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data=2") is supported. Note, for ReTx SU UE and its coresponding initialTx is MU, the DL data and DMRS FDM is supported for the SU ReTx too, reserved MCS is used due to RE difference, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Due to port selection is always used when actDlMuMimoEnh=True, so the number of stream allocated to UE's PDSCH as follows:

* 1 or 2 layers UE:  2 streams, streamIndex = {0,1} or {2,3}.
* 3 or 4 layers UE: 4 streams, streamIndex = {0,1,2,3}.

Refer to 5G\_L2\_6629\_replaced([11166695](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HKfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for precoding and  5G\_L2\_5017\_replaced([11165649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD0vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for port selection.

When a virtualUe construction finished, subCellIndex and streamIndex shall be allocated to each UEs in this virtualUe.

IF ( nrCellType is 16DLxUL (x can be any value) ):

The DMRS scrambling ID of UEs allocate in the first subCellIndex set to scramblingID0, and the remaining set to scramblingID1.  The value of scramblingID1 shall be set to C-RNTI, and C-RNTI is sent to CP-RT during user setup procedure, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11377\_replaced([11145267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_sGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

END

[CB008830-B]IF DmrsType is DmrsType 1 [End CB008830-B]

FOR ( each UE(i) in virtualUe(A) )

IF ( UE(i) is not allocated subCellIndex and streamIndex yet):

* Allocate available subCellIndex and streamIndex to UE(i).

Note, for UE request to allocate 2 streamIndexs, the subCell with some streamIndex already allocated shall be considered first for this UE. Following is an example of UE1's subCell/streamIndex allocation:

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a visual representation of resource allocation for two subcells, subCell1 and subCell0, before and after a change. Each subcell is divided into four streams: stream 3, stream 2, stream 1, and stream 0.
In the initial state (left side of the image), subCell1 has "ue1" allocated to stream 1. subCell0 has "ue0" allocated to stream 1.
The image shows an arrow indicating a transition to a new state (right side of the image). In the updated state, subCell1 still has "ue1" allocated to stream 1. However, subCell0 now has "ue1" allocated to stream 3 and "ue0" allocated to stream 1.
The table below summarizes the resource allocation:

| Subcell | Stream | Initial Allocation | Updated Allocation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| subCell1 | stream 3 |  |  |
| subCell1 | stream 2 |  |  |
| subCell1 | stream 1 | ue1 | ue1 |
| subCell1 | stream 0 |  |  |
| subCell0 | stream 3 |  | ue1 |
| subCell0 | stream 2 |  |  |
| subCell0 | stream 1 | ue0 | ue0 |
| subCell0 | stream 0 |  |  |

In the case of left picture, if next UE2 is a 4 layers UE, then it's fail to allocate subCell/streamIndex. The prefered allocation is as right picture.

* Allocate scramblingId and port based on the streamIndex and layer of UE(i), refer to the mapping table above.

END

END

[CB008830-B]

ELSE IF  DmrsType is DmrsType 2

FOR ( each UE(i) in virtualUe(A) sorted from highest rank to lowest rank )

IF ( UE(i) is not allocated subCellIndex and streamIndex yet):

* Allocate scramblingId and Dmrs port(s) according to requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25105\_replaced([11145336](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksATJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* Allocate available subCellIndex and streamIndex to UE(i).

Note, for UE request to allocate 2 streamIndexs, the subCell with some streamIndex already allocated shall be considered first for this UE. Following is an example of UE1's subCell/streamIndex allocation:

[image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a visual representation of resource allocation for two subcells, subCell1 and subCell0, before and after a change. Each subcell is divided into four streams: stream 3, stream 2, stream 1, and stream 0.
In the initial state (left side of the image), subCell1 has "ue1" allocated to stream 1. subCell0 has "ue0" allocated to stream 1.
The image shows an arrow indicating a transition to a new state (right side of the image). In the updated state, subCell1 still has "ue1" allocated to stream 1. However, subCell0 now has "ue1" allocated to stream 3 and "ue0" allocated to stream 1.
The table below summarizes the resource allocation:

| Subcell | Stream | Initial Allocation | Updated Allocation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| subCell1 | stream 3 |  |  |
| subCell1 | stream 2 |  |  |
| subCell1 | stream 1 | ue1 | ue1 |
| subCell1 | stream 0 |  |  |
| subCell0 | stream 3 |  | ue1 |
| subCell0 | stream 2 |  |  |
| subCell0 | stream 1 | ue0 | ue0 |
| subCell0 | stream 0 |  |  |

In the case of left picture, if next UE2 is a 4 layers UE, then it's fail to allocate subCell/streamIndex. The prefered allocation is as right picture.

END

END

END

[End CB008830-B]

Implementation Informative for streamIndex allocation:

A bitmap can be defined for each virtualUe(A):

1. Initialize: at virtualUe(A) creation, virtualUe(A).streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[] = {0000 , 0000}, assume 2 C4 subCells.
2. Update: when a UE(i) is successfully added to virtualUe(A), and UE(i) has allocated with subCell and streamIndex already, set the corresponding bitmap.

Example, UE(i).subCellIndex=0 and UE(i).streamIndex={2,3}, then bitmap update to virtualUe(A).streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[] = {0000,1100}.

1. Check: when a pair candidate UE(i) is going to be added to virtualUe(A), check the streamIndex availablility or conflict, refer to 5G\_L2\_4608\_replaced([11166336](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2505D_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. allocate: when the virtualUe(A) construction is finished, allocate subCell and stream to each UEs in virtualUe(A) from available stream based on virtualUe(A).streamIndexAllocatedBitmapPerSubCell[] = {0000,1100}.

Note, if nrCellType is 16DLxUL (x can be any value), there should assume 4 C4 subCells in the above implementation Informative for streamIndex allocation.

CP2 reference:5G\_UP\_ALG\_11372\_replaced([11145207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_sEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G\_UP\_ALG\_11377\_replaced([11145267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_sGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.4.0-2 Step 3. SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation Allocation of subCell/stream/DMRS for a ZF_MU virtual UE (ID: 12580223)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12580224`)

Step 3. SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation
There are 4 steamIndex per C4 subCell. The number of available subCell in the cell, rrmNumDlSubcells, is defined in [11178989: DL FDM Specific General Information](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2y2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

Note, in case of DL subCell pooling enabled, the rrmNumDlSubcells can be changed accoding to subCell pooling algorithm, refer to [11210126: Subcell Pooling (TDD only)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw3lB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) .

If ​​​ ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True), and ZF\_MU virtual UE is constructed, due to maxDlZfMuMimoLayer is up to 8 (see [12580137: Max MU Layer Calculation for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_71SMkTFsEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), then max two subCells can be allocated to ZF-MU virtual UEs , even there can be more subcells， based on currently implementation, first one or two subCell (subCellIndex 0/1) can be used.

Value of the field ‘Antenna port(s)’ in DCI 1\_1 is defined in table 7.3.1.2.2-1 of TS 38.212 according to allocated DMRS port(s) per UE. The number of CDM group(s) without data is determined in  [11129150: Determination of the number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq6uCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

In case of  DL data and DMRS FDM enabled actLocalDmrsFdm = True :

* MU UE:

**- if Dmrs Type 1 is configured** DL data and DMRS FDM is not supported for MU UE, only "Number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data=2" is supported.\*\*\*\*

* SU UE: both DL data and DMRS FDM("Number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data=1") and no FDM("Number of DMRS CDM group(s) without data=2") is supported. Note, for ReTx SU UE and its coresponding initialTx is MU, the DL data and DMRS FDM is supported for the SU ReTx too, reserved MCS is used due to RE difference, refer to  [11184193: Scheduling of a Re-transmission](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) .

Even the identity precoding is applied to ZF\_MU UEs ([12579923: ZF\_MU Precoding](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ORkl4DFmEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) , but  the number of streams allocated to UE's PDSCH as follows:

* 1 or 2 layers UE:  2 streams, streamIndex = {0,1} or {2,3}.
* 3 or 4 layers UE: 4 streams, streamIndex = {0,1,2,3}.

When a ZF\_MU virtualUe construction finished, sorting the UE pair based on the sum of APS\_correlation across UEs per ZF\_MU virtual UE from high to low first,  Then allocation DMRS port

subCellIndex and streamIndex shall be allocated to each UEs in this ZF\_MU  virtualUe based on the sorting list.

IF DmrsType is DmrsType 1

FOR ( each UE(i) in virtualUe(A) )

**IF ( UE(i) is not allocated subCellIndex and streamIndex yet):**

* Allocate available subCellIndex and streamIndex to UE(i).

Note, for UE request to allocate 2 streamIndexs, the subCell with some streamIndex already allocated shall be considered first for this UE. Following is an example of UE1's subCell/streamIndex allocation:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a visual representation of resource allocation for two subcells, subCell1 and subCell0, before and after a change. Each subcell is divided into four streams: stream 3, stream 2, stream 1, and stream 0.
In the initial state (left side of the image), subCell1 has "ue1" allocated to stream 1. subCell0 has "ue0" allocated to stream 1.
The image shows an arrow indicating a transition to a new state (right side of the image). In the updated state, subCell1 still has "ue1" allocated to stream 1. However, subCell0 now has "ue1" allocated to stream 3 and "ue0" allocated to stream 1.
The table below summarizes the resource allocation:

| Subcell | Stream | Initial Allocation | Updated Allocation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| subCell1 | stream 3 |  |  |
| subCell1 | stream 2 |  |  |
| subCell1 | stream 1 | ue1 | ue1 |
| subCell1 | stream 0 |  |  |
| subCell0 | stream 3 |  | ue1 |
| subCell0 | stream 2 |  |  |
| subCell0 | stream 1 | ue0 | ue0 |
| subCell0 | stream 0 |  |  |

In the case of left picture, if next UE2 is a 4 layers UE, then it's fail to allocate subCell/streamIndex. The prefered allocation is as right picture.

* Allocate scramblingId and port based on the streamIndex and layer of UE(i), refer to the mapping table above.

END

END

Note:

IWK with DMRS Type 2 is not support supported for when ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True).

CP2 reference: [12436704: DMRS port allocation algorithm (for SB BF UE)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Djh98AgpEfCT35inaBGo7g?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.4.0-3 Step 3. SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation Set stream and precoding info (ID: 11166695)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11166699`)

Step 3. SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation
The stream and precoding in DlData\_PdschSendReq set as follows for MU MIMO enhancement:

* 1 or 2 layers UE:
* numStreamIndex= 2, streamIndex = {0,1} or {2,3}.
* closedLoop3gppCodebook = 0(2portTypeICodebook)
* pdschPrecodingOption4x4=0(NoPortSelection)
* codebookIndex: 2x2 MIMO codebook transfered from i2 as follows:

**rank1 UE (based on table 5.2.2.2.1-1 in 3GPP 38.214)**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **i2** | **Codebook index** |
| 0 | 0 |
| 1 | 1 |
| 2 | 2 |
| 3 | 3 |

**rank2 UE (based on table 5.2.2.2.1-1 in 3GPP 38.214)**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **i2** | **Codebook index** |
| 0 | 0 |
| 1 | 1 |

* 3 or 4 layers UE:
* numStreamIndex= 4, streamIndex = {0,1,2,3}.

4Port CSIRS UE:

* closedLoop3gppCodebook = 1(4portTypeICodebook)
* pdschPrecodingOption4x4=2(PortSelectionUpToRank4)
* i2Codebook4AntPorts = [0,1]

8Port CSIRS UE:

* closedLoop3gppCodebook = 2(8portTypeICodebook)
* pdschPrecodingOption4x4=0(NoPortSelection)
* i2Codebook4AntPorts = [0,1]

Note, both MU and  SU UE are set as above when  actDlMuMimoEnh=True.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.4.0-4 Step 3. SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation Set stream and precoding info for ZF_MU UE (ID: 12580887)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12580888`)

Step 3. SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation
If ​​​ ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True), and ZF\_MU virtual UE is constructed,

The stream and precoding in DlData\_PdschSendReq set as follows for ZF\_MU MIMO UE:

* 1 layer UE:
* numOfLayers=numStreamIndex= 1, but streamIndex = {0,1} or {2,3}.
* 2 layers UE:
* numOfLayers=numStreamIndex= 2, and streamIndex = {0,1} or {2,3}.
* 3 layers UE:
* numOfLayers=numStreamIndex= 3, but streamIndex = {0,1,2,3}.
* 4 layers UE:
* numOfLayers=numStreamIndex= 4, and streamIndex = {0,1,2,3}.

Because ZF\_MU UE is still scheduled as subband beamforming,  other precoding setting is same as subband beamforming SU UE.

Note,  numOflayers=numStreamIndex is same for subband beamforming SU and MU UEs. but streamIndex setting is different.

For subband beamforming SU UE's setting refer [11184739: (19)DlData\_PdschSendReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Be3sfvvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.5 MU WRR

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.3.5.0-1 MU WRR MU WRR Resource Distribution for NonGbr NewTx UE (ID: 11166710)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.3.5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11166740`)

MU WRR
[CB008927-P] If NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False [End CB008927-P] The MU WRR is execute after reTx UE pairing, refer to 5G\_L2\_5544\_replaced([11166138](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250R8vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  All nonGbr newTx UEs in rrmMuCandidateList is involved in the resource distribution based on all available RBG (not allocated RBG) in the BWP.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a horizontal bar representing "BWP resource". This bar is divided into three colored sections, each with text. The leftmost section is black and labeled "allocated SU". The middle section is dark blue and labeled "allocated MU". The rightmost section is green and labeled "free". Below the "free" section, a blue bracket spans across it, with the text "availableRbg" written below the bracket. The entire "BWP resource" bar is outlined in red, with red arrows at both ends indicating its extent.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3763.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

There has 2 options for MU WRR resource distribution:

Option 1: Good Fairness Method

Available RBG Distributable for MU

Consider some newTx UEs may has allocated with some RBG during reTx UE MU pairing(RBG shared by reTx UE), these allocated RBGs shall be included for MU WRR resource distribution:

availableRbgDistributableMu = availableRbg;

FOR (virtualUe(A) in virtualUeList && has newTx UE in virtualUe(A))

{

availableRbgDistributableMu  += allocatedRbgOfVirtualUe(A);

}

Following picture is a example:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a "BWP resource" which is divided into several segments. The resource is represented by a horizontal bar.
The segments are labeled as follows, from left to right:
1. "allocated SU": This segment is colored black and is at the beginning of the resource bar.
2. A series of segments colored dark blue, labeled "reTx". These appear to be grouped.
3. A segment colored green, labeled "newTx".
4. Another segment colored green, labeled "newTx".
5. A segment colored dark blue, labeled "reTx".
6. A segment colored green, labeled "free". This segment is at the end of the resource bar.
Above the resource bar, there are several annotations:
\* "allocatedRbgOfMuUe": This annotation spans across a portion of the resource bar that includes the "reTx" and "newTx" segments. It is further subdivided into smaller blocks, some labeled "reTx" and some "newTx", arranged in a stacked manner.
\* "allocatedRbgHasNewTxUe": This annotation is a bracket pointing to a section of the resource bar that includes the "reTx" and "newTx" segments.
\* "availableRbg": This annotation is a bracket pointing to the "free" segment of the resource bar.
\* "availableRbgDistributableMu": This is a longer arrow spanning from the beginning of the "allocatedRbgHasNewTxUe" section to the end of the "availableRbg" section.
The diagram visually represents the allocation and availability of resources within a Bandwidth Part (BWP) for a mobile user equipment (MuUe). The "allocated SU" likely represents a statically allocated resource. The "reTx" and "newTx" segments within "allocatedRbgOfMuUe" indicate resources allocated for retransmissions and new transmissions, respectively. The "free" segment represents available resources. The annotations "allocatedRbgHasNewTxUe" and "availableRbgDistributableMu" provide further context on the nature and distribution of these allocated and available resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3763.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Note, the availableRbgDistributableMu may increased compare with it's value during FDM WRR resource distribution (5G\_L2\_5529\_replaced([11165243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOrvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), due to there may has RBG freed and/or shared during reTx UE MU pairing.

MU WRR

The MU WRR methods can be same as FDM WRR methods, with folowing WRR algorithm factors:

* availableRbgDistributableMu, as the total avaialble resouce can be distribute;
* nonGbr newTx UE list in rrmMuCandidateList, as the UEs involve in the MU WRR;
* For each UE, the requestRbg based on SU SINR is used, note that the requestRbg is the all nonGbr request RBG of this slot, inlcude the allocatedRbg too.

The output of MU WRR is distributeRbgMuUe for each UE.

Allowed RBG for newTx UE

Based on the output of  MU WRR, distributeRbgMuUe, the allocated RBGs shall be excluded:

a) Calculate Normalized Alllocated RBG for each UEs

Initialize allocatedRbgNormalizedUe(i) = 0 for each UEs;

FOR (virtualUe(A) in virtualUeList && has newTx UE in virtualUe(A)

{

allocatedRbgNormalizedOfVirtualUe(A) = allocatedRbgOfVirtualUe(A)/sizeVirtualUe(A);

FOR (UE(i) in virtualUe(A) && UE(i) is newTx UE)

{

allocatedRbgNormalizedUe(i) += allocatedRbgNormalizedOfVirtualUe(A);

}

}

b) Calcualte Allowed Rbg for each UEs

FOR (UE(i) in rrmMuCandidateList && UE(i) is nonGbr newTx UE)

{

allowedRbgUe(i) = max(distributeRbgMuUe(i) - rounddown(allocatedRbgNormalizedUe(i)) , 0);

}

Option 2: Less Fairness Method

Available RBG Distributable for MU

For some newTx UEs may has allocated with some RBG during reTx UE MU pairing(RBG shared by reTx UE), not consider these allocated RBGs in MU WRR resource distribution, only the availabe RBG is considered for distribution. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4504\_replaced([11179776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku7ESfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Following picture is a example:

[image-2][image\_desc]The image depicts a resource allocation diagram, likely related to wireless communication systems. A horizontal red bar labeled "BWP resource" spans the top, indicating the total available resource. Below this, the resource is divided into several segments, visually represented by colored rectangles with red borders.
From left to right, the segments are:
1. A black rectangle labeled "allocated SU".
2. A series of stacked rectangles representing allocated resources for "MuUe" (likely a User Equipment). These are further categorized:
\* The bottom row contains three blue rectangles labeled "reTx", "reTx", and "reTx".
\* The middle row contains two green rectangles labeled "newTx" and "newTx", and one blue rectangle labeled "reTx".
\* The top row contains one blue rectangle labeled "reTx" and one green rectangle labeled "newTx".
A bracket labeled "allocatedRbgOfMuUe" encompasses these stacked rectangles.
3. A blue bracket labeled "allocatedRbgHasNewTxUe" points to a portion of the stacked rectangles, specifically the "reTx" and "newTx" blocks in the bottom and middle rows.
4. A green rectangle labeled "free" occupies the rightmost portion of the resource.
5. A green bracket labeled "availableRbg" spans the "free" segment.
6. A longer green bracket labeled "availableRbgDistributableMu" extends from the beginning of the "free" segment to its end, indicating the total available distributable resource.
The diagram illustrates how a total resource ("BWP resource") is partitioned into allocated portions for different purposes (e.g., "allocated SU", "allocatedRbgOfMuUe") and a free portion. The allocated portions for "MuUe" are further broken down into "reTx" (re-transmission) and "newTx" (new transmission) activities. The "allocatedRbgHasNewTxUe" segment highlights a specific subset of these allocated resources. The "availableRbg" and "availableRbgDistributableMu" indicate the remaining free and distributable resources, respectively.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3763.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Note, the availableRbgDistributableMu may increased compare with it's value during FDM WRR resource distribution (5G\_L2\_5529\_replaced([11165243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOrvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), due to there may has RBG freed during reTx UE MU pairing.

MU WRR

The MU WRR methods can be same as FDM WRR methods, with folowing WRR algorithm factors:

* availableRbgDistributableMu, as the total avaialble resouce can be distribute;
* nonGbr newTx UE list in rrmMuCandidateList, as the UEs involve in the MU WRR;
* For each UE, the requestRbgRemain based on SU SINR is used, note that the requestRbgRemain is the request RBG of remain buffer, not include the allocatedRbg.
* the nonGbr newTx UE list is in priority decend order, so that the resource distribution can be start from highest prioirty UE.

The output of MU WRR is distributeRbgMuUe for each UE.

Allowed RBG for newTx UE

allowedRbgUe = distributeRbgMuUe.

Note,

* selection of option 1 or option 2 is depend on implemetation. (option 2 is selected)
* allowedRbgUe, is 0 before MU WRR for newTx UE, refer to 5G\_L2\_5544\_replaced([11166138](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250R8vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008927-P] Else

For RAT1 MU WRR please refer 5G\_L2\_5529\_replaced([11165243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOrvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB008927-P]

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.4 Virtual UE RBG Allocation

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.4-1 Virtual UE RBG Allocation

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.4-1.0-1 Virtual UE RBG Allocation For virtual UE PRB allocation please refer 5G_UP_ALG_26505_replaced( 11144618 ). (ID: 11166844)

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.4-2 Virtual UE RBG Allocation

**2.4.11.1.3.3.4-2.0-2**  (ID: `12792539`)

Virtual UE RBG Allocation
Because ZF\_MU virtual UE's concept is updated comparing WB BF MU virtual UE,  One UE can only belong to one SVD-RZF virtual UE. UEs in the same SVD-RZF virtual UE may have different number of allocated PRBs for PDSCH ( [11142531: Concept of virtual UE](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).  And For the case of SVD-RZF VUE for pairing SB BF UEs, the number of PRB that can be allocated to virtualUE(A) is the maximum number of requested PRB among all UEs in virtualUE(a)  (see [11144718: Allocate PRB to virtual UE](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr9P0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) .

Updating the reqPrbUeiand totalAllowedPrbVirtualUeA,

[CB012971-F]

IF  it is not Exhaustive DL Scheduling (NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to value other than  DL or DLAndUL,refer to [12483324: Exhaustive Scheduling for RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qbsGEQ-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ))

[End CB012971-F]

that are calculated in MU-WRR (see [11166705: MU WRR](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HK_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

reqPrbUei = min (totalAllowedPrbVirtualUeA ,reqPrbUei )

[image-0][image\_desc]reqPrbVirtualUe\_A = min (totalAllowedPrbVirtualUe\_A, max\_{i ∈ EA} (reqPrbUei))[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05261044.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[CB012971-F]

Else  it is Exhaustive DL Scheduling (NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to  DL or DLAndUL,refer to [12483324: Exhaustive Scheduling for RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qbsGEQ-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ))

that are calculated by ES Alg, detail refer [11144618: PDSCH allocation with RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8oyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

[End CB012971-F]

For Pooling and other limitation is same as WB BF based MU.

Note, some corner case, due to LayerPrb's limitation, final allocation maybe is smaller than bfSubbandSizes. L1 may treat this as abnormal ZF-MU scheduling.

where

- totalAllowedPrbVirtualUeA see [11144637: Calculate total number of allowed PRB in virtualUE (A)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8oy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

* reqPrbUeisee [11144666: Calculate request PRB of UE(i) in virtualUE(A)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8ozfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)
* bfSubbandSize: beamforming subband size that receives from high layer(see 5G\_L2\_IF\_115\_replaced([10968891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UaBKvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

ZF\_MU PRB allocation please refer SFS   [11144618: PDSCH allocation with RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8oyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.4.0-1 Virtual UE RBG Allocation Calculate totalAllowedRbgVirtualUe (ID: 11166753)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11166770`)

Virtual UE RBG Allocation
The totalAllowedRbgVirtualUe(A) is the total number of allowedRbgUe of each UEs in the virtualUe(A)[Before CNI-109641-K]:

[image-0][image\_desc]allowedRbgVirtualUe\_A = sum\_{i in A} allowedRbgUe\_i[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3767.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where,

* allowedRbgUe,  is defined in 5G\_L2\_5527\_replaced([11165197](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25xOp_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for reTx UE, and defined in 5G\_L2\_4604\_replaced([11166710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HLPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for newTx UE.

CP2 referernce:[CNI-109641-K] refer to [End CNI-109641-K] 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11853\_replaced([11144217](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7arvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.4.0-2 Virtual UE RBG Allocation Calculate nonGbrBufferDataUe (ID: 11166776)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.4.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11166788`)

Virtual UE RBG Allocation
The nonGbrBufferDataUe(i) is the total buffer data for all non-GBR bearers of UE(i).

Note:

If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True, for the computation of total non-GBR buffer data, the non-GBR DRBs and deprioritized GBR DRBs with  [Before CB008828-C] slice [CB008828-C] resource group [End CB008828-C] weight = 0 ([Before CB008828-C] rrmSliceWeight = 0 [CB008828-C] rrmRGWeight = 0 [End CB008828-C], see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7389\_replaced([11188798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be excluded.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.4.0-3 Virtual UE RBG Allocation Calculate requestRbg (ID: 11166792)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.4.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11166809`)

Virtual UE RBG Allocation
\* ReTx UE:

requestRbgUe(i) = allowedRbgUe(i);

* NewTx UE:

The requestRbgUe(i) is the request RBG of UE(i) considering only the remain buffer in non-GBR bearers:

requestRbgUe(i) = roundup((nonGbrBufferDataUe(i) - allocatedTbsUe(i)) / avgTbsPerRbgUe(i))

Where,

* nonGbrBufferDataUe(i), is defined as in 5G\_L2\_9183\_replaced([11166776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252uNPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));
* allocatedTbsUe(i), is the already allocated TBS of UE(i), this is the TBS allocated when the UE scheduled in previous virtualUes, and initialized to 0 of the slot.
* avgTbsPerRbgUe(i), is the TBS of a normal RBG (size P) of UE(i) in a virtualUe, it's calculate based on the MCS defined in 5G\_L2\_4948\_replaced([11165673](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD1fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before CB008940-H]

* Virtual UE:

The requestRbgVirtualUe(A) is the request RBG of virtualUe(A):

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in italicized text. The equation is:
$requestRbgVirtualUe\_A = min\_{i \in A} {requestRbgUe\_i}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3773.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[CB008940-H]

* Virtual UE:

The requestRbgVirtualUe(A) is the request RBG of virtuaUe(A). Its computation is specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11855\_replaced([11144425](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8Bt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008940-H]

CP2 Reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11854\_replaced([11144269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8Bs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11855\_replaced([11144425](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8Bt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.4.0-4 Virtual UE RBG Allocation Allocate RBG (ID: 11166814)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11166819`)

Virtual UE RBG Allocation
\* Virtual UE

The number of allocate RBG to the virtualUe(A) is:

allocatedRbgVirtualUe(A) = min(requestRbgVirtualUe(A), totalAllowedRbgVirtualUe(A), availableRbg);

Where,

* availableRbg, is the available RBG in the BWP.

The number of allocatedRbgVirtualUe(A) RBGs is allocated to virtualUe(A) in lowToHigh order, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note, when actDlMuMimoEnh=True, the rdEnableDlInterCellInterfAvoid shall be set to disable always, and the NIDD parameter actDlMuMimo shall be disable too,  so there is no any highToLow allocation, only lowToHigh allocation.

* UEs in virtualUe

The allocatedRbgVirtualUe(A) shall allocate to all UEs in the virtualUe(A) too, and check if satisfy for resource:
