FOR (UE(i) in virtualUe(A))

{

allocate same RBG as the virtualUe(A) to UE(i);

allocatedRbgUe(i) += allocatedRbgVirtualUe(A);

allocatedTbsUe(i) += allocatedRbgVirtualUe(A) \* avgTbsPerRbgUe(i); //Note, software can determine how to calculate the allocate TBS based on the complexity.

}

Note, allocatedRbgUe(i) and allocatedTbsUe(i) is initilized to 0 for UE(i) when this UE is first time selected scheduling in this slot.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11855\_replaced([11144425](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8Bt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.4.0-5 Virtual UE RBG Allocation Update allowedRbgUe (ID: 11166822)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.4.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11166832`)

Virtual UE RBG Allocation
The allowedRbgUe shall be deduted for the allocatedRbgVUe after RBG allocation for the virtual UE:

* Virtual UE with reTx UE as root

FOR (each UE(i) in virtualUe(A))

{

IF (UE(i) is reTx UE)

allowedRbgUe(i) -= allocatedRbgVirtualUe;

IF allowedRbgUe = 0: //satisfy for resource

remove this UE from rrmMuCandidateList;

End

Else //NewTx

IF allocatedTbsUe(i) >= nonGbrBufferDataUe(i):  //buffer drained, satisfy for resource

remove this UE from rrmMuCandidateList;

END

END

}

Note, the allowedRbgUe for newTx UE is 0 in this stage, refer to 5G\_L2\_5544\_replaced([11166138](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250R8vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* Virtual UE with newTx UE as root

The basic idea of how to update the allowedRbgUe is:

a) clear the allowedRbgUe for all buffer drained UE;

b) [case 1] if the total allowedRbgUe of buffer drained UEs is not enough for the virtualUe allocated RBG, there has allowedRbg shortage, other UEs in this virtual UE shall contribute/deduct allowedRbgs to fill the gap of shortage;

c) [case 2] if the total allowedRbgUe of buffer drained UEs is more than the virtualUe allocated RBG, there has allowedRbg remain, the extra remain allowedRbgs are share/add to other UEs in this virtual UE.

Following picture illustrate the idea of how to update allowedRbgUe:

[xlsx\_desc]--- allowedRbg ---
Empty DataFrame
Columns: []
Index: [][/xlsx\_desc][xlsx\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/attachments/m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file3777\_0.xlsx[/xlsx\_path]

The allowedRbgUe is updated as follows:

step 1)

allowedRbgOfSatisfyUesInVUe = 0;

FOR(each UE(i) in virtualUe(A))

{

IF allocatedTbsUe(i) >= nonGbrBufferDataUe(i):  //buffer drained, satisfy for resource

allowedRbgOfSatisfyUesInVUe += allowedRbgUe(i);

allowedRbgUe(i) = 0;

remove this UE from rrmMuCandidateList;

END

}

step 2)

allowedRbgRemainOfVUe = allowedRbgOfSatisfyUesInVUe - allocatedRbgVUe(A); //Note that allowedRbgRemainOfVUe can be positive or negative value.

IF (allowedRbgRemainOfVUe<0) //case 1

{

While(allowedRbgRemainOfVUe != 0)

{

FOR(each UE(i) in virtualUe(A) && allowedRbgUe(i)>0)

{

allowedRbgUe(i) -= 1;

allowedRbgRemainOfVUe += 1;

}

}

}

ELSE IF (allowedRbgRemainOfVUe>0) //case 2

{

IF (has UE in virtualUe(A) still has nonGbr buffer)

{

While(allowedRbgRemainOfVUe != 0)

{

FOR(each UE(i) in virtualUe(A) && allocatedTbsUe(i) < nonGbrBufferDataUe(i))

{

allowedRbgUe(i) += 1;

allowedRbgRemainOfVUe -= 1;

}

}

}

}

Note:

* nonGbrBufferDataUe(i), is defined as in 5G\_L2\_9183\_replaced([11166776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252uNPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11857\_replaced([11144475](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8Bu_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11858\_replaced([11144572](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr8oxPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.5 Virtual UE RBG Allocation Conditions and Subcell Resource Availability With PRB Pooling

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.5.0-1 Virtual UE RBG Allocation Conditions and Subcell Resource Availability With PRB Pooling Available FD Subcell Resources For MU-MIMO with PRB Pooling (ID: 11166854)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11166864`)

Virtual UE RBG Allocation Conditions and Subcell Resource Availability With PRB Pooling
The availability of FD subcell resources for MU-MIMO with PRB pooling shall be done according 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14518\_replaced([11144879](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr924_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In the check for potential restriction of the maximum number of allowed subcells that can be used for the next virtual UE scheduling:

[CB008927-P]

-if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = false AND (rrmMuAvailableNumSubcellPrb - rrmMuAvailableNumCellPrb) < rbgSize;

else,

* if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = true AND (rrmMuAvailableNumSubcellPrb - rrmMuAvailableNumCellPrb) < rrmMuMinPrbMargin

[End CB008927-P]

rbgSize is the size of any available RBG: It can be the full RBG size or the size of the last RBG.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.6 Remain RBG Allocation

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.6.0-1 Remain RBG Allocation (ID: 11166875)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.6.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12623623`)

Remain RBG Allocation
if NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = False After all virtualUes construction and RBG allocation are finished, if there still has free RBG remaining, these RBG shall be allocated to UEs that still has buffer in cs2List|toBeScheduledList or rrmMuCandidateList, in priority descend order. The remaining avaialble RBG is allocated in SU method. **Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7530\_replaced([11145129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_FBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for remaining RBG/PRB allocation in case of slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed  is TRUE.**

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a horizontal bar representing a "BWP resource". This resource is divided into three segments. The leftmost segment is black and labeled "allocated SU". The middle segment is dark gray and labeled "allocated MU". The rightmost segment is green and labeled "free". A blue bracket extends from the beginning of the "free" segment to its end, with the text "availableRbg" positioned below it. The entire bar is outlined in red, with red arrows at both ends indicating the total extent of the "BWP resource".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3789.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

FOR (each UE(i) in cs2List|toBeScheduledList or rrmMuCandidateList)

{

IF (availableRbg>0)

IF (UE(i) is newTx && (bufferDataUe(i) - allocatedTbsUe(i))>0):

//Allocate the remain available RBG to UE(i) to drain buffer, the detail can be determine in implementation, following is a example:

requestRbgUe(i) =roundup( (bufferDataUe(i) - allocatedTbsUe(i)) / avgTbsPerRbgUe(i));

toBeAllocateRbg =  min(requestRbgUe(i), availableRbg);

allocatedRbgUe(i) += toBeAllocateRbg;

allocate toBeAllocateRbg to UE(i).

Where,

* bufferDataUe(i), is the buffer data of all bearer of UE(i),

If sliceAwareSchedulerCellActivation = True, for the computation of buffer data, the non-GBR DRBs and deprioritized GBR DRBs with resource group weight = 0 **rrmRGWeight = 0** , see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7389\_replaced([11188798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kved7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be excluded.\*\*\*\*

* avgTbsPerRbgUe(i), is the TBS of a normal RBG (size P) of UE(i), it's calculated based on the MCS calculated from SU SINR, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3004\_replaced([11125290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqt5sPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

//Note, in case of a muEigible UE is selected for remaining RBG allocation, a new virtualUe with size=1 is needed for this reamain RBG allocation, facilitate for txPower(5G\_L2\_4767\_replaced([11167010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538V_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and final MCS(5G\_L2\_4508\_replaced([11167076](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538XfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) calculation.

//Decrease the remain available RBG

availableRbg -=toBeAllocateRbg;

END

ELSE

Break;

END

}

Note, for easy implementation:

1) Use cs2List|toBeScheduledList or rrmMuCandidateList can be determined by implementation, for simplify consideration. The rrmMuCandidateList is defined in 5G\_L2\_4196\_replaced([11165697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD6PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1) If cs2List|toBeScheduledList is used,

* the remain RBG allocation can be do only for nonGbr UEs in cs2List|toBeScheduledList too, can be determine by implementation.
* due to the UEs in cs2List|toBeScheduledList is stored per subCell, remain RBG allocation can be executed per subCell too, e.g., allocate RBG for all UEs in subCell0 first, then allocate RBG for all UEs in subCell1, and so on. The UEs in a subCell shall be still in priority descending order.

Else

At pdsch subarea level, L2-PS shall distribute the resources only for the highest prbs needed UE from the last MU paired virtual UE list or last MU non paired UE. This is because of immediate allocation of MU resources while virtual UE construction.

After MU allocation, if any remaining PRBs are available, then as part of SU redistribution, the remaining PRbs are allocated to any SU UE which have DL data in buffer one by one as per their PF metric priority order.

Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7530\_replaced([11145129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_FBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for remaining RBG/PRB allocation in case of slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed  is TRUE for RAT1, **[CB012971-C] if actDlMuPairingGroup is True, SB BF UEs which are already paired in SVD-RZF VUE shall NOT be allocated to any additional PRBs for PDSCH with SU mode.**

**[CB012971-C]**

End

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.7 Virtual UE Construction for SB BF based MU-MIMO PoC

###### 2.4.11.1.3.3.7.0-1 Virtual UE Construction for SB BF based MU-MIMO PoC General  description (ID: 11166923)

**2.4.11.1.3.3.7.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11166931`)

Virtual UE Construction for SB BF based MU-MIMO PoC
Detail impaction

---

**2.4.11.1.3.3.7.0-1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12575591`)

Virtual UE Construction for SB BF based MU-MIMO PoC
[Before CB012971-C]

1,   In eCPRI 7\_2e=True (see 5G\_L2\_18130\_replaced([11155228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2432wvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if actDlSrsSubbandBm =True, actDlMuMimoEnh =True, and rdEnableSbBfDlMuMimoL2 = true (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28945\_replaced([11149844](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksQKzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), which means  subband BF based MU-MIMO function is active.

2,  Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7454\_replaced([11142605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-afvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), to avoid PDSCH beam fallback due to the virtualUE's PDSCH PRBs are allocated in initial or poor quality srs subband mapping area,  Only SB BF UE which has valid SRS quality on all SRS subbands/ports can be considered as MU eligible (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26998\_replaced([11156904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks4c5PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). It means WB BF UE or SB BF UE without valid SRS quality on all SRS subbands/ports are not MU eligible.

During **MU Candidate List Build (see 5G\_L2\_4195\_replaced([11165691](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD4fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), The UE's MU eligibility is checked during FDM UE selection stage, and same criterias checking as osDFT beam based MU, with addtional criteria that only SB BF UE which has valid SRS quality on all SRS subbands/ports can be considered as MU eligible (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26998\_replaced([11156904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks4c5PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  This addtional criteria can be be checked first.**

Note, During this phase, the final PDSCH allocation area is unknown, then all SRS subbands' SrsSbstatus shall be NonInitial and all ports' quality are VAL (see 5G\_L2\_19849  [11171433: SRS Quality for SB Beamforming determination](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26N6nfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

3, Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7430\_replaced([11144070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), due to L1 radio's restrction,  5G-L2-PS shall only construct up to one virtual UE which includes paired SB BF UEs. For virtual Ue concept refer 5G\_L2\_4482\_replaced([11165305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1uPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

During  **Virtual UE Construction phase (see 5G\_L2\_4279\_replaced([11166035](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zq8fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),if  the vUeList.size=1, then exit the virtual UE consturction loop.**

4, Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7835\_replaced([11142690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr5ldvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),  no matter the DL tapering beam is enable or not, L2\_PS shall force rrmTaperingCompensation=1, this is because the final beam is subband beam, but the osDFT beam is still used in **MU Pair UE Selection phase**

(see 5G\_L2\_5464\_replaced([11166193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_250R_fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

5, Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7530\_replaced([11145129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_FBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), If [rdEnableSbBfDlMuMimoL2 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_28945\_replaced([11149844](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksQKzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is True], to simplify solution in L1 Radio, SB BF UEs which are already paired in VUE shall NOT be allocated to any PRBs with SU mode.

During Remian RBG RBG allocation  phase (see 5G\_L2\_5576\_replaced([11166870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_253VRPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),if  UEs that has been alocated RBG as virtual UE in Virtual UE RBG Allocation phase (5G\_L2\_4441\_replaced([11166747](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252uMPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) , those UEs shall be excluded from the remain RBG allocation. (Note, Due to ReTX UEs' PRB allocation restriction, rdDlEnableReTxUeMu=FALSE shall set in CB009527-H )

#### 6, Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2570\_replaced([11118507](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDLX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), Weight normalization will make sure the total power will not bigger than per TRX's max power, then there is no need for L2\_PS to set pdschPowerOffset based on paring UEs'  number.

#### If [rdEnableSbBfDlMuMimoL2 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_28945\_replaced([11149844](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksQKzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is True], pdschPowerOffset = 0dB.

#### Then the txPowerFactor calculation in TxPower calculation is can be skipped ( see 5G\_L2\_4767\_replaced([11167010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538V_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), even the txPowerFactor will be used to re-scale muSinrDbUe in final MCS calculation phase (5G\_L2\_4508\_replaced([11167076](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538XfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). This is because in CB009527-H, L2\_PS only constructs one Virtual UE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7430\_replaced([11144070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr7ao_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and SB BF UEs which are already paired in VUE shall NOT be allocated to any PRBs with SU mode (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7530\_replaced([11145129](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr_FBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then the step of re-scale muSinrDbUe in final MCS calculation phase is also unnecessary. This can be determined by SW implementation, because skipping or not will get same result.

7, During **SubCell/Stream/DMRS Allocation phase (see 5G\_L2\_4439\_replaced([11166624](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252HJPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), This is kept same algorithm as osDFT beam based MU.**

Then allocation of streams for PDSCH 1 or 2 layers UE:  2 streams, streamIndex = {0,1} or {2,3}.    but the numStreamIndex shall be same with numOfLayers in message dldata\_pdschsendreql1sw because this is subband beamforming scheduling.

Below are some examples:

case1 : RANK1 & RANK1 MU,"allocation" of streams are subcellId0 (stream0 & stream1),  subcellId0(stream2 & stream3),

this case numStreamIndex = numOfLayers =1  , and  subcellId0 &  streamIndex (0,1,0,0) for UE1,

numStreamIndex = numOfLayers =1  , and  subcellId0 & streamIndex (2,3,0,0) for UE2,

case2 : RANK1 & RANK2  MU,"allocation" of streams are  subcellId0 (stream0 & stream1),   subcellId0 (stream2 & stream3),

this case  numStreamIndex = numOfLayers =1  , and  subcellId0 &  streamIndex (0,1,0,0) for UE1,

numStreamIndex = numOfLayers =2  , and  subcellId0 & streamIndex (2,3,0,0) for UE2,

case3 : RANK1 & RANK2 & RANK1 &RANK 2 MU,"allocation" of streams are   subcellId0 (stream0 & stream1),  subcellId0(stream2 & stream3),   subcellId1 (stream0 & stream1),  subcellId1(stream2 & stream3)

this case   numStreamIndex = numOfLayers =1  , and  subcellId0 &  streamIndex (0,1,0,0) for UE1,

numStreamIndex = numOfLayers =2  , and  subcellId0 & streamIndex (2,3,0,0) for UE2,

numStreamIndex = numOfLayers =1  , and  subcellId1 &  streamIndex (0,1,0,0) for UE3,

numStreamIndex = numOfLayers =2  , and  subcellId1 & streamIndex (2,3,0,0) for UE4,

case 4:  RANK3 and RANK4 MU, "allocation" of streams are subcell0 & streamIndex = {0,1,2,3},  subcell1 & streamIndex = {0,1,2,3}.

numStreamIndex = numOfLayers =3 , and  subcellId0 &  streamIndex (0,1,2,3) for UE1,

numStreamIndex = numOfLayers =4 , and  subcellId1 &  streamIndex (0,1,2,3) for UE2,

8, The impaction to potential PM counter and TTI trace updating are not analyzed, because this is only a PoC feature.

[End CB012971-C]

---

##### 2.4.11.1.3.4 Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling

###### 2.4.11.1.3.4.0-1 Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling PM Counter (ID: 11166949)

**2.4.11.1.3.4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12630490`)

Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling
The following counter should be pegged during/after PRBs are allocated, the PRB in lastRbg is different with normal Rbg.

1) new counter :

M55308C34001 Num of PRBs for 1 layer tx for SU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_SU\_MIMO\_1LAYER

M55308C34002 Num of PRBs for 2 layer tx for SU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_SU\_MIMO\_2LAYER

M55308C34003 Num of PRBs for 3 layer tx for SU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_SU\_MIMO\_3LAYER

M55308C34004 Num of PRBs for 4 layer tx for SU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_SU\_MIMO\_4LAYER

M55308C34005 Num of PRBs for 2 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_2LAYER

M55308C34006 Num of PRBs for 3 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_3LAYER

M55308C34007 Num of PRBs for 4 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_4LAYER

M55308C34008 Num of PRBs for 5 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_5LAYER

M55308C34009 Num of PRBs for 6 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_6LAYER

M55308C34010 Num of PRBs for 7 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_7LAYER

M55308C34011 Num of PRBs for 8 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_8LAYER

M55308C34012 Num of PRBs for non paired UE tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_NOPAIR\_UE

M55308C34013 Num of PRBs for 2 UE pair tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_2PAIR\_UE

M55308C34014 Num of PRBs for 3 UE pair tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_3PAIR\_UE

M55308C34015 Num of PRBs for 4 UE pair tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_4PAIR\_UE

M55308C34016 Num of PRBs for 9 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_9LAYER

M55308C34017 Num of PRBs for 10 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_10LAYER

M55308C34018 Num of PRBs for 11 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_11LAYER

M55308C34019 Num of PRBs for 12 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_12LAYER

M55308C34020 Num of PRBs for 13 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_13LAYER

M55308C34021 Num of PRBs for 14 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_14LAYER

M55308C34022 Num of PRBs for 15 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_15LAYER

M55308C34023 Num of PRBs for 16 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_16LAYER

M55308C34024 Num of PRBs for 5 UE pair tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_5PAIR\_UE

M55308C34025 Num of PRBs for 6 UE pair tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_6PAIR\_UE

M55308C34026 Num of PRBs for 7 UE pair tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_7PAIR\_UE

M55308C34027 Num of PRBs for 8 UE pair tx for MU MIMO in DL  PRB\_DL\_MU\_MIMO\_8PAIR\_UE

M55308C34028 Total num of MU MIMO paired UEs for PDSCH per cell      TOT\_MU\_MIMO\_PAIR\_UE\_PDSCH

M55308C34029 Total num of MU MIMO eligible UEs scheduled on PDSCH per cell          TOT\_MU\_MIMO\_ELIG\_UE\_PDSCH

NOTE:

1. For PM counter TOT\_MU\_MIMO\_PAIR\_UE\_PDSCH, on each DL slot, the number of paired UEs is accumulated:

1) In each DL slot, if PDSCH is not scheduled, this counter is not incremented.

2) In each DL slot, if any PDSCH is scheduled:

-  If there are paired UEs, this counter is incremented by number of paired UEs.

-  If there is no paired UEs, this counter is not incremented.

2. For PM counter TOT\_MU\_MIMO\_ELIG\_UE\_PDSCH, on each DL slot, the number of scheduled eligible UEs is accumulated:

1)   In each DL slot, if PDSCH is not scheduled, this counter is not incremented.

2)   In each DL slot, this counter is incremented by number of MU MIMO eligible UEs scheduled on PDSCH.

3)   MU MIMO eligible UEs means UEs in MU candidate list, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7454\_replaced([11142605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-afvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

one example is as below:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a spreadsheet-like layout with two main sections, each depicting a resource allocation plan for different User Equipments (UEs) across Resource Blocks (RBs). Each section is further divided into rows representing "layers" and columns representing "RBG=>PRB" (Resource Block Groups to Physical Resource Blocks) from 0 to 17. Different UEs are assigned to specific RBG/PRB ranges, indicated by colored blocks.
To the right of each resource allocation plan, there are columns listing identifiers (e.g., M55308C34001), a category (NCELA), and descriptions of the number of PRBs for various transmission scenarios (SU MIMO, MU MIMO, paired/non-paired UEs). Numerical values and sometimes parenthetical expressions represent the PRB counts. Some cells also contain additional notes, such as "UE11/Partial UE7->SU" and "UE7/UE11".
**Section 1 (Top):**
This section shows a resource allocation for UEs labeled UE1, UE2, UE3, UE4, UE5, UE6, UE7, and UE8. The right side details the number of PRBs for SU MIMO transmissions with varying numbers of layers (1 to 4).
**Section 2 (Bottom):**
This section illustrates a different resource allocation for UEs labeled UE1, UE2, UE3, UE4, UE5, UE6, UE7, UE8, UE9, UE10, and UE11. The right side details the number of PRBs for MU MIMO transmissions with varying numbers of layers (2 to 8), as well as for non-paired UEs and UE pairs.
The table below summarizes the information from the right side of the second section, which details MU MIMO configurations:

| Identifier | Category | Description | PRB Count | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| M55308C34005 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 2 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL | 2 |  |
| M55308C34006 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 3 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL | 3 |  |
| M55308C34007 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 4 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL | 2 |  |
| M55308C34008 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 5 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL | 0 |  |
| M55308C34009 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 6 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL | 4 |  |
| M55308C34010 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 7 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL | 0 |  |
| M55308C34011 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 8 layer tx for MU MIMO in DL | 5 |  |
| M55308C34004 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 4 layer tx for SU MIMO in DL | (1+1) |  |
| M55308C34012 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for non paired UE tx for MU MIMO in DL | 1+1 | UE7/UE11 |
| M55308C34013 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 2 UE pair tx for MU MIMO in DL | (2+3+5+2) |  |
| M55308C34014 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 3 UE pair tx for MU MIMO in DL | 4 |  |
| M55308C34015 | NCELA | Num of PRBs for 4 UE pair tx for MU MIMO in DL | 0 |  |

**If nrCellType is 16DLxUL (x can be any value), one example is as below:**

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid representing Resource Block Groups (RBGs) and Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) allocated to different User Equipments (UEs) in a cellular network. The grid is organized by RBG index from 0 to 17 along the top and by "layer" on the left. Each cell in the grid is colored and labeled with a UE identifier (e.g., UE1, UE2, UE3, etc.), indicating the allocation of PRBs to specific UEs.
To the right of the grid, there are columns detailing the number of PRBs allocated for various transmission scenarios, such as MU MIMO (Multi-User Multiple-Input Multiple-Output) and SU MIMO (Single-User Multiple-Input Multiple-Output). These descriptions are associated with specific UE identifiers or combinations of UEs.
The table below is a representation of the data presented in the image:

2)legacy counter(only consider the physical PRB, spatial domain is not needed):

M55308C10001 PRBs used for PDSCH PRB\_USED\_PDSCH

M55308C29003 PRBs used for PDSCH for BWP2 PRB\_USED\_PDSCH\_BWP2

M55338C00001 PRBs used for PDSCH per PLMN PRB\_USED\_PDSCH\_PLMN

for detailed counter descpriton, pls refer to NIDD.

**[CB012971-C]**

If ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True), {

1, the ZF\_MU virtual UE supports max layer for ZF\_MU  please see  [12580137: Max MU Layer Calculation for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_71SMkTFsEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

2, the ZF\_MU virtual UE supports max subband BF UEs please see [11142569: DL pairing group](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4-Y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

3, for ZF MU eligible UEs refer [12571009: ZF\_Mu Candidate List](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_m4HnYS8BEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

Related PM counters shall also be pegged after ZF\_MU virtual UE's scheduling.

}

**[End CB012971-C]**

###### 2.4.11.1.3.4.0-2 Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling Average Virtual UE Size Calculation for each newTx UE (ID: 11166982)

**2.4.11.1.3.4.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11167005`)

Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling
[Before CNI-109641-K]

After all virtualUe constriction and RBG allocation done, average virtual UE size shall be calculated for all scheduled newTx MU candidate UEs.

An UE can be in one virtualUe, or in multi virtualUes with diffrent virtualUeSize. The average virtualUeSize of UE(i) is defined for newTx UE as follows:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
avgVirtualUeSizeOfUei = roundNearest (∑*{A∈{all virtualues of UE\_j}} virtualDeSize\_A \* allocatedRbgVirtualDe\_A) / (∑*{A∈{all virtualues of UE\_j}} allocatedRbgVirtualUe\_A)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3801.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where,

* virtualUeSizeA ,size of virtualUe(A), see 5G\_L2\_4282\_replaced([11165013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25wnk_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* allocatedRbgVirtualUeA, allocated RBG of virtualUE(A), refer to 5G\_L2\_4641\_replaced([11166814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_252uOvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CNI-109641-K]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7449\_replaced([11143103](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr6MhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.4.0-3 Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling TxPower Calculation (ID: 11167010)

**2.4.11.1.3.4.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11167073`)

Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling
[CB008927-P] While preparing the Tx Power Calculation, there is no specific change for RAT1 when compared with RAT0.

Note: In the below text, the "RBG" word can be consider as in case of RAT0 it is in RBG granularity or in case of RAT1 it is in PRB granularity. [End CB008927-P]

After all virtualUe constriction and RBG allocation done, an txPower factor is calculated for all MU UEs to compensate the MU impact.

The Tx power of PDSCH needs to be calculated for each UE after all virtual UEs are built. To simplify the solution, the PDSCH power density of all MU UEs shall be same over RBG allocated to MU UEs.

txPowerFactor for MU UE

The txPowerFactor is defined as PDSCH power compensation on top of SU-MIMO. The PDSCH power, pdschTbTransmitPower, for SU is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2556\_replaced([11118369](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqCkRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The PDSCH power with txPowerFactor compensation equals to pdschTbTransmitPower + 10log(txPowerFactor). See the detail in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2570\_replaced([11118507](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDLX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For SU UE, txPowerFactor = 1.0. For MU UE, the equation of txPowerFactor is defined as  in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7434\_replaced([11145459](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksA6MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Follow figure is an example of txPowerFactor calculation for MU:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the allocation of Resource Block Groups (RBGs) to different User Equipments (UEs) in a system. The top section shows a list of virtual UEs: `muVirtualUeList = virtualUeA, virtualUeB, virtualUeC, virtualUeD, virtualUeE`.
Below this, a table represents the RBGs, numbered from 0 to 9. Each row below the RBG numbers indicates the allocation of these RBGs to specific UEs.
- The first row shows `UE2` allocated to RBGs 0, 1, and 2.
- The second row shows `UE1` allocated to RBGs 3 and 4.
- The third row shows `ue0` allocated to RBGs 0, 1, 2, and 3.
- The fourth row shows `UE3` allocated to RBGs 4, 5, and 6.
- The fifth row shows `UE4` allocated to RBGs 7, 8, and 9.
The diagram also indicates that RBGs 0 through 6 are part of the "RBG of MU UEs" (Multi-User UEs), while RBGs 7 through 9 are part of the "RBG of SU UE" (Single-User UE).
Finally, there is a calculation for `txPowerFactor`, which is described as being averaged on RBGs across all MU UEs. The formula shown is:
`txPowerFactor = (3+1+2+1) / (3*3 + 2*1 + 2*2 + 1*1) = 0.4375`
The numbers in the numerator (3, 1, 2, 1) and the terms in the denominator (3*3, 2*1, 2*2, 1*1) likely correspond to the number of RBGs allocated to specific UEs or some weighting factor associated with them.
The visual representation uses different colors to distinguish the allocations for `UE2` (light green), `UE1` (orange), `ue0` (yellow), `UE3` (green), and `UE4` (blue).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3806.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the allocation of Resource Blocks (RBG) to different User Equipments (UEs). The top section shows a list of virtual UEs: `muVirtualUeList = virtualUeA virtualUeB virtualUeC virtualUeD virtualUeE`. Below this, a table represents the RBG allocation. The table has a header row labeled "RBG" with columns numbered 0 through 9. The subsequent rows show how different UEs are assigned to these RBGs.
Here's a breakdown of the RBG allocation:
\* **UE2** is allocated RBG 0 and 1.
\* **UE1** is allocated RBGs 2 and 3.
\* **ue0** is allocated RBGs 0, 1, 2, and 3.
\* **UE3** is allocated RBGs 4, 5, and 6.
\* **UE4** is allocated RBGs 7 and 8.
The diagram also indicates the "RBG of MU UEs" which spans RBGs 0 through 6, and "RBG of SU UE" which spans RBGs 7 and 8, with "freeRbg" for RBG 9.
Finally, there's a formula explaining how `txPower` is averaged across all MU UEs:
`txPowerFactor = (1+3+1+2+1) / (3*3+2*1+2*2+1*1) = 0.5`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3806.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

pdschPowerOfffset

Note, due to port selection is used when actDlMuMimoEnh=True, the pdschPowerOffset shall be update according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2565\_replaced([11118485](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDLVvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 4x4 and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2567\_replaced([11118493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDLW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 8x4,  and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13149\_replaced([11118478](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDLVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 1x1  for both SU and MU UEs. Refer to 5G\_L2\_5017\_replaced([11165649](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD0vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for port selection. This pdschPowerOffset is included in pdschTbTransmitPower, independent with txPowerFactor of MU.

Note, in case of 256QAM dynamic power enabled (rdActDynamicPowerBackoff=1), the rankOffset of MU UE is updated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2560\_replaced([11118403](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqCkTPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If EIRP control is enabled (actEirpControl = TRUE), the EIRP token per PRB shall consider the txPowerFactor due to MU pairing and txPower is changed, refer to 5G\_L2\_6349\_replaced([11157708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25HHXfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). actEirpControl is NIDD parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReConfigurationReq.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7434\_replaced([11145459](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksA6MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.4.0-4 Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling TxPower Calculation for ZF-MU (ID: 12572000)

**2.4.11.1.3.4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12572002`)

Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling
​​​If  ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True) ,

Because L1-RU will do the MU weight normalization w.r.t the maximal TRX power. Then there is no need for L2PS to calculate

txPowerFactor, which in PDSCH power compensation on top of DL SU-MIMO.

Then L2PS will apply txPowerFactor=1 in subband beamforming UEs.

SFS refer [11145459: Calculate Tx power](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksA6MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

For weight normalization refer [Massive MIMO Algorithm Specification.docx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/5gmax/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BC7D041A7-9D30-43D5-9233-3FE4F70347B7%7D&file=Massive%20MIMO%20Algorithm%20Specification.docx&action=default&mobileredirect=true)

---

###### 2.4.11.1.3.4.0-5 Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling Final MCS Calculation (ID: 11167076)

**2.4.11.1.3.4.0-5.0-5**  (ID: `13413963`)

Post MU VirtualUe Scheduling
[CB008949-F]

The Ue level parameter of isReachedLimittedMaxDlLayer1024Qam shall be reset as False every DL slot before the following algorithm.

[End CB008949-F]

For ReTx UE,

[CB008949-F]

IF( (Ue(i).mcstable == 1024QAM)&& Is16Dl2UlOr16Dl4Ul([13421199](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e53evxexEfGFMKAOrP5qqw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) )

{

FOR(virtualUe(A) in all virtualUes)

{

IF(virtualUeSizeA>1)

{

FOR(Ue(i) in virtualUe(A)

{

IF( (Ue(i).isReachedLimittedMaxDlLayer1024Qam == False)

AND (total layers of virtualUe(A)> rdMaxDlLayer1024Qam ([13363354: rdMaxDlLayer1024Qam](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_FSj4EPWpEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) ))

{

Ue(i).isReachedLimittedMaxDlLayer1024Qam = True

}

Note refer to [13363347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_u2DBAPWjEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

}

}

[End CB008949-F]

When adaptive re-transmission scheme is not applied, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), then:

* Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7435\_replaced([11145579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksA6PPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

When adaptive re-transmission scheme is applied, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11943\_replaced([11184268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRCjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), then:

* the MCS is same as its initialTx, when TBS computed with ReTx resources didn't changed compared to its initialTx one.
* reserved MCS is used, when TBS changed compared to its initialTx one, as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12911\_replaced([11184825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvRpnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). When reserved MCS used, specific reserved MCS shall be chosen accoding to modulation order, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

[CB008949-F]

IF(Ue(i).isReachedLimittedMaxDlLayer1024Qam ==True)

{

If MCS index of the UE is mapped to 1024QAM modulation order (i.e. MCS table is 1024QAM and MCS index is 23-26 or 31),

Then the MCS index of the UE shall be changed to 30, refer to [13363347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_u2DBAPWjEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

}

[End CB008949-F]

For NewTx UE,

Refer to 5G\_L2\_4948\_replaced([11165673](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD1fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for the MCS calculation.

* For SU UE, the final MCS is calculated based on SU SINR, refer to 5G\_L2\_4948\_replaced([11165673](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD1fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
* For MU UE, the final MCS is calculate as follows:

a) Calculate muSinrDbUe based on N=1/txPowerFactor

The muSinrDbUe defined in 5G\_L2\_4277\_replaced([11165538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycyvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), shall be re-calculate with N=1/txPowerFactor in each virtualUe with size>1(has MU).

FOR(virtualUe(A) in all virtualUes)

{

IF(virtualUeSizeA>1)

{

FOR(Ue(i) in virtualUe(A)

{

re-calculate muSinrDbUei,A with N=1/txPowerFactor;

[CB008949-F]

IF( (Ue(i).isReachedLimittedMaxDlLayer1024Qam == False)

AND (total layers of virtualUe(A)> rdMaxDlLayer1024Qam ([13363354: rdMaxDlLayer1024Qam](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_FSj4EPWpEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ)) )

AND Is16Dl2UlOr16Dl4Ul([13421199](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e53evxexEfGFMKAOrP5qqw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9))) )

{

Ue(i). isReachedLimittedMaxDlLayer1024Qam = True

}

Note refer to [13363347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_u2DBAPWjEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

[End CB008949-F]

}

}

}

Note, txPowerFactor is defined in 5G\_L2\_4767\_replaced([11167010](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2538V_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

b) Calculate average SINR

where,

* sinrDbUei,A: it is muSinrDbUei,A defined in 5G\_L2\_4277\_replaced([11165538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycyvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for MU virtualUe(virtualUeSize>1), or suSinrDbUei defined in 5G\_L2\_4948\_replaced([11165673](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD1fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for SU virtualUe(virtualUeSize=1).
* allocatedRbgVirtualUeA: is the number of allocated RBG of virtualUe(A).

Following figure is a example of avgMuSinrDbUe for UE(0):

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the concept of virtual UEs (User Equipments) and their associated SINR (Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio) values. The diagram is divided into three main sections labeled "virtualUeA", "virtualUeB", and "virtualUeC", representing different virtual UEs.
Within these sections, colored rectangular blocks represent SINR values for specific UEs.
In the "virtualUeA" section, there are two blocks:
- A yellow block labeled "muSinrDbUe(Ue1,A)"
- A green block labeled "muSinrDbUe(Ue0,A)"
In the "virtualUeB" section, there are three blocks:
- A gray block labeled "muSinrDbUe(Ue3,B)"
- A yellow block labeled "muSinrDbUe(Ue2,B)"
- A green block labeled "muSinrDbUe(Ue0,B)"
In the "virtualUeC" section, there is one block:
- A green block labeled "suSinrDbUe(Ue0,C)"
A dashed red rectangle encloses the green blocks labeled "muSinrDbUe(Ue0,A)", "muSinrDbUe(Ue0,B)", and "suSinrDbUe(Ue0,C)". An arrow points from this dashed rectangle to the text below.
Below the diagram, there is a text description: "UE0's final sinr is average over all sinr in virtualUe{A,B,C}". This indicates that the final SINR for UE0 is calculated by averaging the SINR values from virtual UEs A, B, and C.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-03061454.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

c) Calculate MCS accoding to 5G\_L2\_4948\_replaced([11165673](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD1fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

[CB008949-F]

d) Update MCS

IF (Ue(i).isReachedLimittedMaxDlLayer1024Qam == True)

{

If MCS index of the UE is mapped to 1024QAM modulation order (i.e. MCS table is 1024QAM and MCS index is 23-26),

then the MCS index of the UE shall be changed to maximal MCS index with modulation order lower than 10 and corresponding effective code rate not greater than 0.95. refer to [13363347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_u2DBAPWjEfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

}

[End CB008949-F]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7435\_replaced([11145579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksA6PPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4622\_replaced([11192072](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvivUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

---

##### 2.4.11.1.3.5 Interaction with layerPrb pooling

###### 2.4.11.1.3.5.0-1 Interaction with layerPrb pooling ​​​ (ID: 13412672)

#### 2.4.11.1.4 MU Aware TD Scheduling

##### 2.4.11.1.4.1 Overview

###### 2.4.11.1.4.1.0-1 Overview (ID: 11167155)

**2.4.11.1.4.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11167160`)

Overview
MU aware TD scheduling is enabled by NIDD parameter actMuAwareTdSch = true (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1871\_replaced([10968710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaE_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_L2\_IF\_2026\_replaced([10971449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UoDnfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) . With this feature, DL MU pairing ratio will be improved.

It happens in the stage of selecting UEs from Cs2List to toBeScheduledUeList.

With considering correlation between the candidate UEs and the UEs in toBeScheduledUeList, the UEs in toBeScheduledUeList will get a better pairing pre-condition so that pairing ratio will be improved.

The high priority UEs(higher than drbTraffic)  shall be selected first as legacy.

Only check correlation among mu-eligible UEs(5G\_L2\_4196\_replaced([11165697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD6PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and threshold defined as NIDD parameter dlMuAwareCorrThd(see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1871\_replaced([10968710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaE_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_L2\_IF\_2026\_replaced([10971449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UoDnfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If actMuAwareTdSch = true,  dlMuMimoRankAdaption (see 5G\_L2\_5008\_replaced([11165331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25x1u_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be 1(always) or 2(adaptive), can't be set as 0(disabled).

---

##### 2.4.11.1.4.2 Correlation Considering UE selection

###### 2.4.11.1.4.2.0-1 Correlation Considering UE selection Basic Procedure (ID: 11167171)

**2.4.11.1.4.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11167176`)

Correlation Considering UE selection
The basic procedure has 2 steps as below.

Step 1, Select UE from Cs2List with correlation check.

WHILE (NOT isMaxUesReached) // more UEs can be scheduled in this slot

{

UE = getOneUeForCheck   //see 5G\_L2\_14322\_replaced([11167182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_254javvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

check isEnoughFdSchedulingTimeAvailableForAdditionalUe

check isNumberOfCorrelationCalculationExcceeded //see 5G\_L2\_14363\_replaced([11167277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255KffvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

check isDeprioritizedTraffic //see 5G\_L2\_14566\_replaced([11167206](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_254jbvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

IF (isCorrelationCheckNeeded)  //see 5G\_L2\_14323\_replaced([11167213](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255KcPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

{

IF (NOT UE.isMuMimoEnhEligible)

add UE to nonCorrCheckUeList //see 5G\_L2\_14368\_replaced([11167236](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255KdvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

continue

ELSE

IF  (NOT correaltionCheck(UE)) //see 5G\_L2\_14354\_replaced([11167225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255Kc_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

add UE to CorrelationCheckBlockUeList  //see 5G\_L2\_14325\_replaced([11167194](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_254jbPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

continue

ELSE

PASS

}

check PUCCH/PDCCH/PDSCH/subCell as legacy // see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4297\_replaced([11177362](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuu3A_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

}

Step 2, Select UE from nonCorrCheckUeList without correlation check.

IF (NOT isMaxUesReached)

{

merge Ue lists into nonCorrCheckUeList //see 5G\_L2\_14368\_replaced([11167236](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255KdvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

WHILE (NOT isMaxUesReached)

{

get an UE from nonCorrCheckUeList as priority

select UEs as legacy with PUCCH/PDCCH/PDSCH/subCell checking

}

}

The detail procedure refers to selectUes.hpp under the link below.

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=f857xG&cid=140ace03%2D7720%2D46dc%2Daab8%2D3cc48f7132af&FolderCTID=0x012000D96F54C70C4C3547AE19DA5D399C6414&id=%2Fsites%2FASCOP%2FAS%20Documents%2FFS1TAs%2DCFAMs%2FCB%2FCB009000%20%2D%20CB009499%2FCFAM%2FCB009235%20Downlink%20MU%20MIMO%20service%20enhancement%2FCP3&viewid=73435348%2D8379%2D4262%2Da3aa%2Dffe0b7322e0c>

---

###### 2.4.11.1.4.2.0-2 Correlation Considering UE selection Get One UE for Check (ID: 11167182)

**2.4.11.1.4.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11167188`)

Correlation Considering UE selection
A UE shall be selected from CorrelationCheckBlockUeList or Cs2List.

1. Select a UE from CorrelationCheckBlockUeList if the UE marked as correlation not checked (see 5G\_L2\_14325\_replaced([11167194](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_254jbPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
2. If no UE is got from CorrelationCheckBlockUeList , select a UE from Cs2List.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.4.2.0-3 Correlation Considering UE selection CorrelationCheckBlockUeList (ID: 11167194)

**2.4.11.1.4.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11167200`)

Correlation Considering UE selection
If a UE correlation check failed, the UE shall be added into CorrelationCheckBlockUeList.

The UEs in CorrelationCheckBlockUeList shall be selected again when a new UE is added into toBeScheduledUeList.

The UEs in CorrelationCheckBlockUeList shall be marked as correlation checked when correlation check failed.

The UEs in CorrelationCheckBlockUeList shall be marked as correlation not checked when a new UE is added into toBeScheduledUeList.

The UE shall be removed from CorrelationCheckBlockUeList if it is added into toBeScheduledUeList.

The UE shall be set block reason as CS2\_CORRCHECKBLOCK (see 5G\_L2\_3858\_replaced([11204016](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29-ZQPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

CS2\_NONMUELIGIBLEBLOCK

Rationale: Based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23057\_replaced([11177334](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuu3AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), if the minimum correlation between a UE and the UEs in toBeScheduledUeList less than dlMuAwareCorrThd(see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1871\_replaced([10968710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaE_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_L2\_IF\_2026\_replaced([10971449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UoDnfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the UE can be added into toBeScheduledUeList. So although correlation check failed at the moment, it may be succeeded afterwards.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.4.2.0-4 Correlation Considering UE selection Deprioritized Traffic Check (ID: 11167206)

**2.4.11.1.4.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11167209`)

Correlation Considering UE selection
To keep traffic priority as legacy (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4144\_replaced([11174002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuXqrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the UEs with drbTraffic request class shall be selected before deprioritized traffic. So if all of the following conditions are met, UE selecting step1(see 5G\_L2\_14328\_replaced([11167171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_254jaPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be stopped and move to step2.

1) UE request class is deprioritizedVoip OR deprioritizedGbr

2) CorrelationCheckBlockUeList.size() >0 OR nonCorrCheckUeList.size()>0

Note: At this time, if there are still UEs with dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic request class, correlation check(5G\_L2\_14354\_replaced([11167225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255Kc_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) will not applied on these UEs for simplification.

Rationale: TD Mu aware schedule aims to high number of UEs with high traffic while dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic is the opposite situation.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.4.2.0-5 Correlation Considering UE selection Correlation Check is Needed or not (ID: 11167213)

**2.4.11.1.4.2.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11167219`)

Correlation Considering UE selection
When all conditions below are meet, mu-aware correlation check is needed.

1) actMuAwareTdSch = true

2) UE request class is drbTraffic or dtxOptimizedDrbTraffic

3) SUM(requiredPrbForMuAwareTdSch of UEs in toBeScheduledUeList) > totalPRB \* dlMuAwarePrbRatio

4) rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl - toBeScheduledUeList.size() < number of un-checked UEs in Cs2List  + CorrelationCheckBlockUeList.size() + nonCorrCheckUeList.size()

5) Cs2List.numberOfMuEligibleUes > 2

6) toBeScheduledUeList.size() < rdMaxNumMuUeInTbs

Note:

- actMuAwareTdSch and dlMuAwarePrbRatio, see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1871\_replaced([10968710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaE_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_L2\_IF\_2026\_replaced([10971449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UoDnfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- request class, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4144\_replaced([11174002](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuXqrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- requiredPrbForMuAwareTdSch is requiredPrb in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23036\_replaced([11177323](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuQAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- totalPRB, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23036\_replaced([11177323](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuQAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl , see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10610\_replaced([11179079](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku3Z6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- rdMaxNumMuUeInTbs, see 5G\_L2\_17495\_replaced([11186859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_278Y7PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

###### 2.4.11.1.4.2.0-6 Correlation Considering UE selection Correlation Check (ID: 11167225)

**2.4.11.1.4.2.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11167230`)

Correlation Considering UE selection
Correlation (see 5G\_L2\_4230\_replaced([11165470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25ycxPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be checked between the checking UE and the UEs in toBeScheduledUeList.

Non mu-eligible UEs shall skip correlation check.

[Before CB009235-A-UPD001]

To make checking more efficient, the UEs in toBeScheduledUeList shall be got from the end.

If Correlation(checking UE, UE in toBeScheduledUeList) < dlMuAwareCorrThd (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1871\_replaced([10968710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaE_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_L2\_IF\_2026\_replaced([10971449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UoDnfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the check can be regarded as pass and stop immediately, no need to continue checking the correlation between checking UE and the other UEs in toBeScheduledUeList.

[CB009235-A-UPD001]

Correlation between checking UE and UEs in toBeScheduledUeList refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23057\_replaced([11177334](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuu3AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If Correlation(checking UE, UEs in toBeScheduledUeList) < dlMuAwareCorrThd (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1871\_replaced([10968710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaE_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_L2\_IF\_2026\_replaced([10971449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UoDnfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the check can be regarded as pass.

[End CB009235-A-UPD001]

Performance shall be considered (see 5G\_L2\_13842\_replaced([11167247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255KePvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

###### 2.4.11.1.4.2.0-7 Correlation Considering UE selection nonCorrCheckUeList (ID: 11167236)

**2.4.11.1.4.2.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11167243`)

Correlation Considering UE selection
nonCorrCheckUeList is used for UE selecting in step2(see 5G\_L2\_14328\_replaced([11167171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_254jaPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in case toBeScheduledUeList in not full after step1. The following UEs shall be added into nonCorrCheckUeList.

1) Non mu-eligible UEs which have not checked PUCCH/PDCCH/PDSCH/subCell in step 1.The UE shall be set block reason as CS2\_NONMUELIGIBLEBLOCK(see 5G\_L2\_3858\_replaced([11204016](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29-ZQPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

2) UEs in CorrelationCheckBlockUeList(5G\_L2\_14325\_replaced([11167194](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_254jbPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) after step 1.

3) UEs in Cs2List which have not checked PUCCH/PDCCH/PDSCH/subCell after step 1.

---

##### 2.4.11.1.4.3 Performance

###### 2.4.11.1.4.3.0-1 Performance Intro (ID: 11167255)

**2.4.11.1.4.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11167261`)

Performance
There are maximum 50 UEs in Cs2List, maximum number of correlation calculation is 50\*49/2=1225, that's too much in a slot.

Refer to benchmark result: CB009235 performance evaluation.pptx under link <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB009000%20-%20CB009499/CFAM/CB009235%20Downlink%20MU%20MIMO%20service%20enhancement/CP3?csf=1&web=1&e=gePsGt.>

Methods need to be taken to avoid overload.

- Optimize correlation calculation and usage (see 5G\_L2\_14366\_replaced([11167267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255Ke_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- Number of correlation calculation limitation (see 5G\_L2\_14363\_replaced([11167277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255KffvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

###### 2.4.11.1.4.3.0-2 Performance Optimize Correlation Calculation and Usage (ID: 11167267)

**2.4.11.1.4.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11167272`)

Performance
Correlation calculation shall be simplified and re-used.

1. Correlation value in correlation table(5G\_L2\_4742\_replaced([11165729](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25zD7vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be calculated and set only when it is to be used.
2. Correlation table shall be re-used in mu-aware TD scheduling and mu-pairing.
3. Only calculate maximum rank2 correlation between UEs, which means rank downgrade is always enabled.

---

###### 2.4.11.1.4.3.0-3 Performance Number of Correlation Calculation Limitation (ID: 11167277)

**2.4.11.1.4.3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11167282`)

Performance
Total number of correlation calculation and number of correlation calculation per UE shall be limited.

1. If Total number of correlation calculation > rdNumberOfCorrelationCalculationTotal(see 5G\_L2\_14373\_replaced([11186831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_278Y5_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), TD MU aware correlation check shall be stopped. The rest UEs shall be selected without correlation check as step 2 in 5G\_L2\_14328\_replaced([11167171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_254jaPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. If number of correlation calculation of a UE > rdNumberOfCorrelationCalculationUe(see 5G\_L2\_14375\_replaced([11186814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_278Y5PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the UE shall be skipped in correlation calculation.

[CB009235-A-UPD001]

1. If number of correlaiton calculation is excceeded during correlaiton check (5G\_L2\_14354\_replaced([11167225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255Kc_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),  correlaiton check shall be continute, but N defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23057\_replaced([11177334](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuu3AfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be number of correlation calculated UEs with selecting Ue. The selecting Ue shall be added into CorrelationCheckBlockUeList(5G\_L2\_14325\_replaced([11167194](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_254jbPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in case N is 0.

[End CB009235-A-UPD001]

---

### 2.4.11.2 UL MU-MIMO

#### 2.4.11.2.1 Overview

##### 2.4.11.2.1.0-1 Overview Virtual UE Concept of UL MuMimo (ID: 11167299)

**2.4.11.2.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11167316`)

Overview
Virtual UE includes a group of UEs which are paired in spatial domain and share the same PRB allocation at the same slot.

The first added UE is root UE of the virtual UE.

A virtual UE may include one to several UEs, but each UE can be assigned to single virtual UE only in UL MuMimo procedure.

If a candidate UE group (see 5G\_L2\_7326\_replaced([11169781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EwqPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) paired with root UE, the start PRB for UEs which are included in a candidate UE group refer to 5G\_L2\_6465\_replaced([11168919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25_4LPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , otherwise the UEs in a virtual UE are expected to have the same starting PRB position in the FD scheduling.

Virtual UE should not overlap PRBs with other Ue/vUE

UEs in Virtual UE may be allocated different prbs based on required prb of each UE.

[CB008235-C] Multiple uegroups(beam group based,5G\_UP\_ALG\_29211\_replaced([11149207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksMgcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) pairing is supported. Refer to 5G\_L2\_20419\_replaced([11169896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26FXvfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB008235-C]

OK and NOK virtual UEs example as following.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a table comparing two scenarios, labeled "OK" and "NOK", across 25 "PRB" (Physical Resource Blocks) numbered 1 through 25. Each scenario shows the allocation of different user equipment (UE) units.
**OK Scenario:**
This scenario is represented by green bars.
- "vUe1" is allocated to PRB 1.
- "Ue1" is allocated to PRB 2.
- "Ue2" is allocated to PRB 3.
- "vUe2" is allocated to PRBs 4 through 7.
- "Ue3" is allocated to PRBs 5 through 9.
- "vUe3" is allocated to PRBs 15 through 25.
- "Ue4" is allocated to PRBs 15 through 21.
- "Ue5" is allocated to PRBs 15 through 22.
- "Ue6" is allocated to PRBs 15 through 23.
- "Ue7" is allocated to PRBs 15 through 24.
**NOK Scenario:**
This scenario is represented by red and white bars.
- "vUe1" is allocated to PRB 1.
- "Ue1" is allocated to PRB 2.
- "Ue2" is allocated to PRB 3.
- "vUe2" is allocated to PRBs 4 through 7.
- "Ue3" is allocated to PRBs 5 through 7.
- "vUe3" is allocated to PRBs 15 through 25.
- "Ue4" is allocated to PRBs 15 through 21.
- "Ue5" is allocated to PRBs 15 through 22.
- "Ue6" is allocated to PRBs 15 through 23.
- "Ue7" is allocated to PRBs 15 through 24.
The table visually contrasts the resource allocation between the "OK" and "NOK" scenarios, highlighting differences in UE assignments and their durations across the PRBs.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3843.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8267\_replaced([11145665](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksBhQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9548\_replaced([11147793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHn8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9145\_replaced([11201926](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwaSCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

##### 2.4.11.2.1.0-2 Overview Framework of Scheduler MuMimo (ID: 11167322)

**2.4.11.2.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11167331`)

Overview
UL MuMimo procedure should be executed in FDM Scheduler.

The UEs with allocated PDCCH will be the UL MuMimo algorithm input. The UEs also known as toBeScheduled UeList.

The UL MuMimo should be started before PRB allocation, the UEs only have required PRB at the moment.

Note: DL MuMimo started after PRB allocation.

Overall framework as following.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a scheduling process within a Time Division Duplex (TDD) and Frequency Division Duplex (FDD) system. The flowchart is divided into two main sections: "TD EO" (Time Division) and "FD EO" (Frequency Division).
Within the "TD EO" section, there are several boxes representing stages of the scheduling process:
1. **Pre Scheduler Candidate Set**: This is the initial input, likely representing a set of potential candidates for scheduling.
2. **Candidate Set1**: This box receives input from "Pre Scheduler Candidate Set".
3. **Candidate Set2 (beam Selection)**: This box receives input labeled "analog BF" from "Candidate Set1".
4. **PxCCH schedule**: This box is within a larger yellow-bordered area labeled "FDM". It receives input labeled "digital BF" from "Candidate Set1".
5. **toBeScheduled UeList**: This box is also within the "FDM" area and receives input from "PxCCH schedule". Inside this box, there are further sub-processes:
\* **required PRB**: This indicates the required Physical Resource Blocks.
\* **UL muMIΜΟ**: This likely refers to Uplink Multi-User MIMO.
\* **PRB distribution (number of RBGs/PRBs)**: This describes the distribution of PRBs, possibly into Resource Block Groups (RBGs).
\* **DL muMIMO**: This likely refers to Downlink Multi-User MIMO.
\* **PRB resource allocation (placement of resources)**: This indicates the final placement of the allocated resources.
An arrow flows from "toBeScheduled UeList" down to the "FD EO" section.
The "FD EO" section contains a single box:
1. **FD(final mcs)**: This box likely represents the final Modulation and Coding Scheme (MCS) determination in the FDD system.
The overall flow suggests a process where candidate sets are refined using different beamforming techniques (analog and digital), leading to a PxCCH schedule and a list of users to be scheduled. This list then undergoes PRB requirement analysis, MIMO consideration, and resource allocation before potentially influencing the final MCS in an FDD context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3845.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The editable file for the figure is in link : <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/11.%20MU-MIMO/5G_L2_6246_Framework%20of%20Scheduler%20MuMimo.xlsx?d=w49592c74ebea49b888dab81b7d908ff6&csf=1&web=1&e=R8trio>

---

##### 2.4.11.2.1.0-3 Overview UL MuMimo Procedure (ID: 11167338)

**2.4.11.2.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11167356`)

Overview
Overall pairing procedure of UL MiMimo algorithm.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to 5G network operations, specifically focusing on the construction and pairing of Virtual UEs (User Equipments). The flowchart begins with a "Start" node and progresses through various steps involving calculations, checks, and selections.
Here's a breakdown of the key elements and their flow:
**Start:** The process begins here.
**Initial Steps:**
\* **5G\_L2\_6239: Building Mu Candidate UE List:** This step likely involves creating a list of potential MU (Multi-User) candidates.
\* **5G\_L2\_6357: Calculating rrmavailablePrbForMu:** Calculation of available Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs) for MU.
\* **5G\_L2\_6358: Calculating rrmRequiredMuPrbNum:** Calculation of the required PRBs for MU.
\* **5G\_L2\_6359: Checking PRB to Decide Construct VUE or not []:** A decision point based on PRB availability to determine if a Virtual UE should be constructed.
\* **5G\_L2\_9647: Checking PRB to Decide Number of Subcells [End ]:** Further PRB checking related to subcell allocation.
**Core Logic:**
\* **5G\_L2\_6255: Maximum MU layers calculation:** Determines the maximum number of layers for MU, noted to be updated only at the first time per slot.
\* **5G\_L2\_6256: Root UE selection:** Selects a root UE and builds a temporary Virtual UE with it.
\* This step has a condition: "rrmUIMuCandidateList is empty". If true, it proceeds to this step.
\* **Add root UE to rrmNonPairedUeList, deconstruct temp Virtual:** These actions follow the root UE selection.
\* **Construct failed / Construct success:** These are outcomes of a construction attempt.
\* If "construct failed", it leads to "try to pair with ue group (8360-B)".
\* If "construct success", it leads to "5G\_L2\_6257: Pairing Candidate UE Selection".
**Pairing and Selection:**
\* **5G\_L2\_6257: Pairing Candidate UE Selection:** Selects a candidate UE for pairing.
\* This step is reached if "construct success" or if "no satifised ue" from a previous step.
\* **VUE size =1 / VUE size >1:** Decision points based on the size of the Virtual UE.
\* If "VUE size =1", it proceeds to "5G\_L2\_6258: Correlation Check".
\* If "VUE size >1", it proceeds to "no correaltion satifised ue" which then leads to "5G\_L2\_6258: Correlation Check".
\* **5G\_L2\_6258: Correlation Check:** Performs a correlation check.
\* **SE check failed, try to find another correaltion satifised ue:** If the SE (Spectrum Efficiency) check fails, it attempts to find another satisfactory correlation.
\* **5G\_L2\_6259: Spectrum Efficiency Check:** Performs a Spectrum Efficiency check.
**Further Construction and Updates:**
\* **Add candidate Ue to tempVirtulUe:** Adds a candidate UE to a temporary Virtual UE list.
\* **5G\_L2\_6478: Information Updating During and After Virtual Contruction:** Updates information during and after the virtual construction.
\* **5G\_L2\_6358: Calculating rrmRequiredMuPrbNum:** Recalculates required PRBs.
\* **5G\_L2\_6359: Checking PRB to Decide Construct VUE or not:** Another PRB check for VUE construction.
\* **Layer check: layers of the virtual UE >= maximum number of MU layers:** A check comparing the virtual UE's layers with the maximum MU layers.
\* If "yes", it proceeds to "5G\_L2\_6277: Dmrs Port Allocation".
\* If "no", it proceeds to "layer check in rrmDedicateList []".
**Branching and Termination:**
\* **5G\_L2\_6277: Dmrs Port Allocation:** Allocates DM (Demodulation Reference Signal) ports.
\* **5G\_L2\_6279: Virtual UE Information Updating, add tempVirtulUe to rrmvUeList:** Updates virtual UE information and adds the temporary virtual UE to the main list.
\* **continue construct VUE:** Indicates the continuation of VUE construction.
\* **no need to continue construct vUE:** Indicates the termination of VUE construction.
\* **stop pairing:** A node indicating the cessation of pairing.
\* **no need to construct:** A condition leading to "stop pairing".
\* **no satifised ue:** A condition leading to further selection or checks.
\* **layer check in rrmDedicateList [], 5G\_L2\_9813:PRB and Layer Check With PRB Pooling [End ]:** This branch handles checks related to dedicated lists and PRB pooling.
The flowchart uses standard flowchart symbols: rectangles for processes, diamonds for decisions, and arrows for flow. The text within the shapes describes the specific operations or conditions. The green highlighted box "try to pair with ue group (8360-B)" suggests a specific grouping or scenario.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3848.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The editable file for the figure is in link: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/11.%20MU-MIMO/5G_L2_9656_UL%20MuMimo%20Procedure.xlsx?d=w4eaa224ab632493f934b025d9ab6600f&csf=1&web=1&e=97CTyU>

Overview
Root Ue should try to pair with Ue group as following procedure.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to 5G network operations. The flowchart has a light green background.
The flowchart begins with a rounded rectangle labeled "Start".
From "Start", an arrow points down to a rectangular box containing the text "5G\_L2\_7328: The conditions of UEs in the candidate UE group".
Another arrow points down from this box to a rectangular box labeled "5G\_L2\_7329: construct the candidate UE group".
To the left of the "5G\_L2\_7329" box, there is a rectangular box labeled "construct failed". An arrow originates from the "5G\_L2\_7329" box and points left towards "construct failed", with the text "can't construct" written above the arrow.
From the "5G\_L2\_7329" box, an arrow points down to a rectangular box labeled "5G\_L2\_7330: MU-SINR estimation for the root UE and paired candidate UE group".
An arrow points down from the "5G\_L2\_7330" box to a rectangular box labeled "5G\_L2\_7331: Spectral efficiency check if root UE paired with candidate UE group".
From the "5G\_L2\_7331" box, there are two outgoing paths. One path goes downwards with an arrow labeled "pass" pointing to a rounded rectangle labeled "construct success". The other path goes to the right with an arrow labeled "failed" pointing back to the "5G\_L2\_7329: construct the candidate UE group" box.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3850.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

##### 2.4.11.2.1.0-5 Overview Simple reTx Ue Pairing (ID: 11167378)

**2.4.11.2.1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11167383`)

Overview
Flow chart

---

**2.4.11.2.1.0-5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12463368`)

Overview
[Before CNI-134356]

For the reTx UEs in CS2 which have been paired in former initial transmission should be paired as a virtual Ue directly. See 5G\_L2\_7317\_replaced([11169583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26C7e_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process named "selectUes(CS2)". The flowchart consists of several rectangular boxes representing steps in the process, connected by blue arrows indicating the flow of control.
Here's a breakdown of the steps:
1. **selectUes(CS2)**: This is the starting point of the process, indicated by the text in the top-left corner.
2. **Pick a Ue from CS2**: The first step involves selecting a "Ue" (likely User Equipment) from "CS2" (likely a component or context).
3. **checkUeSchedulability**: The selected Ue's schedulability is checked.
4. **add to toBeScheduledUeLists toBeDistributedUes/toBeAllocatedUes**: Based on the schedulability check, the Ue is added to lists for scheduling, distribution, or allocation.
5. **allocatePdcchResource**: A "Pdcch Resource" is allocated.
6. **reserveOrAllocatePxschResources**: "Pxsch Resources" are reserved or allocated.
7. **buildMuVirutalUes**: "Virtual Ues" are built.
8. **distributeResources**: Resources are distributed.
9. **allocateResources**: This is the final step, indicated by a yellow box, signifying the actual allocation of resources.
There is also a green rectangular box labeled **"reTx simple pairing"**. A blue arrow points from this box to the "Pick a Ue from CS2" box, suggesting that this "reTx simple pairing" might be a condition or an alternative path that leads back to selecting a Ue. Another blue arrow originates from the "add to toBeScheduledUeLists toBeDistributedUes/toBeAllocatedUes" box and points to the "reTx simple pairing" box, indicating a potential transition to this state.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3854.003.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The editable file for the flow chart is in link: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/11.%20MU-MIMO/5G_L2_10843_Flow%20chart%20of%20Simple%20reTx%20Ue%20Pairing.xlsx?d=w0c636c0cc9104f159562c72cf85e941c&csf=1&web=1&e=88hNvg>

[End CNI-134356]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process titled "reTx simple pairing". The flowchart has a green background and uses white rectangular boxes for process steps and decision points, connected by blue arrows.
The flowchart begins with a decision point: "is reTx Ue?".
- If the answer is "no", the process leads to an "End" state.
- If the answer is "yes", the process moves to the next decision point: "has initTx paired UE in CS2?".
From "has initTx paired UE in CS2?":
- If the answer is "no", the process leads to the "End" state.
- If the answer is "yes", the process moves to the next step: "checkUeSchedulability".
From "checkUeSchedulability":
- If the answer is "no", the process leads to the "End" state.
- If the answer is "yes", the process moves to the step: "build reTx virtual UE remove from CS2". After this step, there is a blue arrow that loops back to the decision point "has initTx paired UE in CS2?".
The text elements identified are:
- "reTx simple pairing" (title)
- "no" (decision outcome)
- "is reTx Ue?" (decision point)
- "yes" (decision outcome)
- "has initTx paired UE in CS2?" (decision point)
- "checkUeSchedulability" (process step)
- "End" (terminal state)
- "build reTx virtual UE remove from CS2" (process step)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3856.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

##### 2.4.11.2.1.0-6 Overview Normal reTx Ue Pairing (ID: 11167429)

**2.4.11.2.1.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `12463370`)

Overview
[Before CNI-134356]

For a reTx UE which can't be paired with simple reTx Paring(5G\_L2\_7317) will try to pair as below.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process. The flowchart starts with a box labeled "selectUes(CS2)". From this box, an arrow points to "Pick a Ue from CS2". Another arrow goes from "Pick a Ue from CS2" to "checkUeSchedulability (5G\_L2\_8243)". From "checkUeSchedulability", an arrow leads to "add to UeLists (5G\_L2\_8250)".
There is a loop in the flowchart. An arrow goes from "add to UeLists" to "reTx simpe pairing (5G\_L2\_7317)", and then an arrow goes from "reTx simpe pairing" back to "Pick a Ue from CS2".
Following the main flow from "add to UeLists", an arrow points to "allocatePdcchResource". From there, an arrow goes to "reserveOrAllocatePxschResources (5G\_L2\_8318, 5G\_L2\_8263)".
Below this, there is a green box labeled "buildReTxVirutalUe (5G\_L2\_7316)". An arrow from "reserveOrAllocatePxschResources" points to this green box.
From the green box, an arrow leads to "buildInitTxVirutalUe (5G\_L2\_6223)". Following this, an arrow points to "distributeResources". Finally, an arrow from "distributeResources" leads to "allocateResources", which is the last box in the flowchart.
The entire flowchart is enclosed within a larger rectangular border. The arrows are blue and indicate the direction of the process flow.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_3858.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The editable file for the flow chart is in link: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/11.%20MU-MIMO/5G_L2_8638_Normal%20reTx%20Ue%20Pairing.xlsx?d=wc56e51d3acf94e8aa765bd2c1e680565&csf=1&web=1&e=amIMkm>

[CNI-134356]

For a reTx UE shall try to pair as below.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process called "selectUes(CS2)". The flowchart consists of several rectangular boxes connected by downward-pointing arrows, indicating a sequence of steps.
Here is a breakdown of the steps:
1. **selectUes(CS2)**: This is the title of the process.
2. **Pick a Ue from CS2**: The first step involves selecting a "Ue" (likely User Equipment) from "CS2" (likely a component or context).
3. **checkUeSchedulability (5G\_L2\_8243)**: The next step is to check the schedulability of the selected Ue, with an associated identifier "5G\_L2\_8243".
4. **add to UeLists (5G\_L2\_8250)**: The Ue is then added to a list, identified by "5G\_L2\_8250".
5. **allocatePdcchResource**: This step involves allocating a "PdcchResource" (likely Physical Downlink Control Channel Resource).
6. **reserveOrAllocatePxschResources (5G\_L2\_8318, 5G\_L2\_8263)**: Resources are either reserved or allocated for "PxschResources" (likely Physical Uplink Shared Channel Resources), with associated identifiers "5G\_L2\_8318" and "5G\_L2\_8263".
7. **buildReTxVirutalUe (5G\_L2\_7316)**: This step, highlighted in green, involves building a "ReTxVirutalUe" (likely Retransmission Virtual UE) with identifier "5G\_L2\_7316".
8. **buildInitTxVirutalUe (5G\_L2\_6223)**: Following that, an "InitTxVirutalUe" (likely Initial Transmission Virtual UE) is built, identified by "5G\_L2\_6223".
9. **distributeResources**: The resources are then distributed.
10. **allocateResources**: Finally, resources are allocated.
The flowchart is contained within a larger rectangular border, suggesting it's a part of a broader system or diagram. The arrows are thick and blue, and the text is black and clearly legible within white rectangular boxes, except for the "buildReTxVirutalUe" box which is green.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-03280953.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
