##### 2.4.11.2.3.0-6 FDM Interworking WRR with vUE and ueGroups with LayerPrbPooling (ID: 13348802)

**2.4.11.2.3.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `13373506`)

FDM Interworking
When slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed is equal to true, during the WRR phase, the layerPrb consumption of a virtual UE (vUE) is accumulated and stored across all previous rounds. In the current round, the allocation of cellPrbs for the vUE is calculated as the sum of the resources received in previous rounds and the resources allocated in the current round.

The new LayerPrb consumption for the vUE is then computed and the difference from the previous round's consumption is assessed. If the difference remains within the LayerPrbs limit, then the allocation is accepted. If it exceeds, then we need to truncate some UEs within the vUE to reduce the excess of LayerPrbs.

Then, if truncation is successful, the allocation of this round is accepted. If it's not the case, then there is no allocation for the current round.

For truncating the virtual UEs, two possibilities are considered:

* If the Root UE is paired with an UE group, the cellPrbs allocation of this UEs can be reduced, but it can't fall below the minimum number of PuschPrbs.
* If the Root UE is paired with absolute UEs, we try to truncate the last UE.

[PR861028]

When a Virtual UE (vUE) is paired with beamGroups, L2-PS UL scheduler shall not exceed layerPrb limit. To respect this limit truncation might be needed and, in such case, L2-PS UL scheduler shall truncate MuUe of the last MuMimo Layers (refer to: [11200918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwS9SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

[End PR861028]

---

#### 2.4.11.2.4 UL Slice Aware Scheduler Interworking

##### 2.4.11.2.4.0-1 UL Slice Aware Scheduler Interworking Virtual UEs PRB bookkeeping in UL SAS (ID: 11168949)

**2.4.11.2.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11168954`)

UL Slice Aware Scheduler Interworking
When UL Slice Aware Scheduler (SAS) is active (ulResourceGroupConfig was present in PsCell\_CellSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988\_replaced([10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))), 5G-L2-PS shall execute additional steps in order to estimate resource usage of the MU MIMO paired UEs for the SAS algorithm PRB bookkeeping. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24609\_replaced([11208303](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwubFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

---

##### 2.4.11.2.4.0-2 UL Slice Aware Scheduler Interworking Virtual UE scheduling weight (ID: 11168957)

**2.4.11.2.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11168962`)

UL Slice Aware Scheduler Interworking
When UL SAS is active, 5G-L2-PS shall derive UE scheduling weight as a product of rrmRgWeight (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23167\_replaced([11207350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwsl7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and DRB schedulWeight for vUE weight calculation. See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9548\_replaced([11147793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHn8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

---

#### 2.4.11.2.5 Link Adaptation Interworking

##### 2.4.11.2.5.0-1 Link Adaptation Interworking Estimate Instance SU-SINR (ID: 11168971)

**2.4.11.2.5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11169001`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
For UE i, if at the previous UL scheduling slot t0, UE i is paired with other UEs. And at data slot t1, L1 will report corresponding SINR according to PUSCH measurement and L2RT should adjust this SINR before filtering as following if ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch =0 (MU-SINR estimation).

IF multiple UL subcells with ulSubcellType C4 configured AND two subcells are scheduled for the virtual UE(k) at slot t0

L2PS shall estimate UE(i)'s SU-SINR rmInstSinr on PUSCH which is included in virtual UE(k) in the corresponding data slot t1 for the same harqID according to the noise per prb and rxPower reported by L1 and send this estimated SU-SINR rmInstSinr to the filter exponent. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18098\_replaced([11148747](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKrOvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

ELSE IF[Before CB010708-C] subcell with ulSubcellType C8 is configured [CB010708-C] Pusch is 8Rx or 16Rx(numOfRxPusch=8 or 16, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB010708-C]

L2PS shall estimate UE(i)'s SU-SINR as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21886\_replaced([11148764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKrPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

ELSE

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a stylized font. The formula defines a variable `rrmInstSinr` as the minimum of two values. The first value is `rdTargetMaxSinr`. The second value is obtained by applying a `Linear2dB` function to the result of a `dB2Linear` function. The input to the `dB2Linear` function is the difference between `rrmInstSint` and `rrmDeltaSINRMuMimo`, divided by `rrmTaperingCompensation`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4023.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

- rrmDeltaSINRMuMimo is the delta between mu-sinr and su-sinr for the corresponding harq id, which is saved in the harq info. see 5G\_L2\_6242\_replaced([11169030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26AfPPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- rrmDeltaSINRMuMimo is equal to zero if UE i is not paired with other UEs for this transmission.

- If SINR is reported according to SRS measurement, there are no extra calculation on this instant SINR.

- rrmTaperingCompensation is sinr compensation for tapering for the corresponding harq id, which is saved in the harq info. It is calculated according to 5G\_L2\_8148\_replaced([11169139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26BtVfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and stored in 5G\_L2\_6242\_replaced([11169030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26AfPPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- rdTargetMaxSinr is RD parameter. The default value of rdTargetMaxSinr is 35dB. Refer to 5G\_L2\_11622\_replaced([11187110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27-1IPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

END

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21939\_replaced([11148794](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksLSQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.11.2.5.0-2 Link Adaptation Interworking Compensation for WbUlMcs (ID: 11169005)

**2.4.11.2.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169008`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
If ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch = 1(MCS compensation), wbUlMcs which is used to compute maximum number of resources for initial transmission(required prb) to UE should be compensated to SU-MCS.

1) Get the wbUlMcs in legacy as current mcs before adjusting.

2) Set current pair size as 0 since SU-MCS is calculated.

3) Get lastPairSize according to 5G\_L2\_7017\_replaced([11169493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26CUavvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

4) Adjust MCS to SU-MCS according to 5G\_L2\_7015\_replaced([11169434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26CUZvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5) Use the wbUlMcs after adjusting for required prb calculation as legacy.

---

##### 2.4.11.2.5.0-3 Link Adaptation Interworking Final MCS calculation (ID: 11169013)

**2.4.11.2.5.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11169017`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
MCS calculation

---

**2.4.11.2.5.0-3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11169026`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
[CB008235-A]

IF ulMaxMuMimoLayer(see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2296\_replaced([10968800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaH_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is larger than 4.

Only MCS compensation is used.

ELSE [End CB008235-A]

L2RT should calculate final mcs according to the OAM parameter ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch.

If ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch = 0, MU-SINR estimation algorithm is active.

Else if ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch = 1, MCS compensation is used.

ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch is OAM parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

[CB008235-A] END IF [End CB008235-A]

---

**2.4.11.2.5.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11169030`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
MU-SINR Based

---

**2.4.11.2.5.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169040`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
If ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch = 0

1) Get the rescaledSinr in dB based on allocated PRB in legacy code as SU-SINR. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3090\_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [PR672232]Note, rrmCorrAvgSinr in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3090\_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) should be the WB-SINR rrmCorrAvgSinr (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3074\_replaced([11130160](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq-_e_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) including rrmDeltaSinr[i,j,t](5G_UP_ALG_3151_replaced([11137856](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krbrbvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End PR672232]

[PR665241]

2) Use the correlation based on tapered beam or non-tapered beam according to latest isUsingTaperedBeam which is updated after virtual UE completed in 5G\_L2\_9344\_replaced([11169166](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26BtV_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End PR665241]

3) Convert the SU-SINR to MU-SINR according to  5G\_L2\_6712\_replaced([11169176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26BtWfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

4) Replace the SU-SINR in legacy code with MU-SINR to calculate the final mcs.

5) Calculate rrmDeltaSINRMuMimo and store it to the corresponding harqId, which will be used when receiving SINR from PUSCH(5G\_L2\_6250\_replaced([11168971](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26AfMvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). rrmDeltaSINRMuMimo is a negative value because MU-SINR should always be smaller than SU-SINR.

[CB008360-A]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a light-colored font against a white background. The equation is:
$rrrmDeltaSINRMuMimo = SINR\_{i,mu} - SINR\_{i,su}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4031.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[End CB008360-A]

[CB008360-B]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a stylized font. The formula is:
rrmDeltaSINRMuMimo = SINRi,mu - Liner2dB( dB2Linear(SINRi,su) \* rrmTaperingCompensation)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4031.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

6) store rrmTaperingCompensation to the corresponding harqId, which will be used when receiving SINR from PUSCH(5G\_L2\_6250\_replaced([11168971](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26AfMvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- rrmTaperingCompensation is sinr compensation for tapering. Refer to 5G\_L2\_8148\_replaced([11169139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26BtVfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008360-B]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3090\_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**2.4.11.2.5.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11169044`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
MCS Compensation based

---

**2.4.11.2.5.0-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11169049`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
If ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch = 1

1) Get the final mcs in legacy as current mcs before adjusting.

2) Adjust the mcs according to 5G\_L2\_7015\_replaced([11169434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26CUZvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3) Using the mcs after adjusting as final mcs for the UE.

4) Saving the current pair size to the harqInfo of the UE for this transmission. Which will be updated to the lastPairSize according to 5G\_L2\_7017\_replaced([11169493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26CUavvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3090\_replaced([11131515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krDQ5fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**2.4.11.2.5.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11169053`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
reTx UE MCS Decision

---

**2.4.11.2.5.0-3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11169056`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27799\_replaced([11148312](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksJdF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.11.2.5.0-4 Link Adaptation Interworking DMRS Front-load Symbols decision (ID: 11169060)

**2.4.11.2.5.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11169065`)

Link Adaptation Interworking
During selecting mcs, number of DMRS front-load symbols shall be calculated as 5G\_L2\_6480\_replaced([11168656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-qC_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.11.2.6 Load Control Interworking

##### 2.4.11.2.6.0-1 Load Control Interworking Overview (ID: 11169072)

**2.4.11.2.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11169077`)

Load Control Interworking
[CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO]

After virtual UE construction, UEs in virtual UE may can’t be scheduled due to load control(5G\_L2\_1820\_replaced([11212319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-k2IvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO]

---

##### 2.4.11.2.6.0-2 Load Control Interworking UlData_PuschReceiveReq when Load Control Happen (ID: 11169083)

**2.4.11.2.6.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11169096`)

Load Control Interworking
IEs in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq(PairedUe/ECpriConfig/eCpriFcpSectionConfig) should be sent according to the UEs which are really scheduled.

IF [Before CB010708-C] subcell with ulSubcellType C8 is configured in cell  [CB010708-C] Pusch is 8Rx or 16Rx(numOfRxPusch=8 or 16, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB010708-C]:

When MU-MIMO paired UE\_i cannot be scheduled, depending on UE pairing type (5G\_UP\_ALG\_19631\_replaced([11104203](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn52TfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS should:

For UEs remaining in virtual UE, in which UE\_i was paired :

In case of Ue Pairing (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7444\_replaced([11142531](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kr4XXvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) :

IF UE\_i is root UE

- L2-PS should select next paired UE as the root UE. PairedUE IE should be set according to 5G\_L2\_7035\_replaced([11179582](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27GrbfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- L2-PS shall re-allocate DMRS ports (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9536\_replaced([11147413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksGZyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- L2-PS shall re-select beams (5G\_UP\_ALG\_20049\_replaced([11148996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksL5UvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

ELSE

- L2-PS shall re-select beams (5G\_UP\_ALG\_20049\_replaced([11148996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksL5UvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

In case of Candidate Ue Group Pairing (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9690\_replaced([11148139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2BPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) :

IF UE\_i is root UE

- L2-PS should schedule the remaining UEs as SU-MIMO UEs. See Note1

Rationale: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9739\_replaced([11148209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksJdEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) ensures, that the sum of PRBs used by all Ues in candidate list should not exceed the PRBs used by root UE.

When root UE\_i cannot be scheduled, its PRBs can be used to schedule all remaining paired UEs via SU-MIMO, without exceeding cell's physical PRB limit.

ELSE

- No extra steps are needed, as DMRS ports (5G\_UP\_ALG\_9536\_replaced([11147413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksGZyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and beams selection (5G\_UP\_ALG\_20049\_replaced([11148996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksL5UvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) do not depend on number of UEs in candidate UE group

ELSE

For example, if UE1 and UE2 paired on subcell0, UE3 and UE4 paired on subcell1 according to virtual UE construction. But UE2 can’t be scheduled due to load control.

IEs for UE3 and UE4 will not be affected.

IEs for UE1 should be updated as SU-receiver will be used. See Note1

Note1 : When UE should be scheduled as SU-MIMO UE :

-PairedUe should be set to 0 according to 5G\_L2\_7035\_replaced([11179582](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27GrbfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

-ECpriConfig should be set as SU-IRC according to 5G\_L2\_7035\_replaced([11179582](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27GrbfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

-eCpriFcpSectionConfig should be set according to 5G\_L2\_8039\_replaced([11179370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27GEVvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). FcpNumPrbc should be the same as numOfPrb in puschAllocationConfig since no other UEs in the same subcell. FcpAddSectionEnable should be false.

---

##### 2.4.11.2.6.0-3 Load Control Interworking L2 trace when Load Control Happen (ID: 11169100)

**2.4.11.2.6.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11169103`)

Load Control Interworking
[CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO]

If all of the paired UEs are not scheduled, ulMuStatus in ulFdSchedData(5G\_L2\_8183\_replaced([11201619](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29xk5fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and ulMuUeData(5G\_L2\_9250\_replaced([11205114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-Ef1PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be set as ” muNonPaired\_overLoad”.

For example, a virtual UE has 3 UEs, UE1, UE2 and UE3. UE1 and UE2 can’t be scheduled due to load control. ulMuStatus of UE3 should be set to ” muNonPaired\_overLoad”.

[End CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO]

---

##### 2.4.11.2.6.0-4 Load Control Interworking Actions when Overload (ID: 11169109)

**2.4.11.2.6.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11169113`)

Load Control Interworking
Base on Load Severity monitoring (5G\_UP\_ALG\_31293\_replaced([11216055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxWtPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2PS shall stop the extra steps as following(TBD after benchmark).

- Simple reTx pairing (5G\_L2\_7317\_replaced([11169583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26C7e_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- Ue Group Pairing (5G\_L2\_7326\_replaced([11169781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EwqPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- Enhance Pairing On Constructed vUEs (5G\_L2\_20417\_replaced([11169888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26FXu_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- Spectrum Efficiency Check (5G\_L2\_6259\_replaced([11168277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_259b7PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- Enhance Pairing On Constructed vUEs (5G\_L2\_20417\_replaced([11169888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26FXu_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

#### 2.4.11.2.7 Throughput Pooling Interworking

##### 2.4.11.2.7.0-1 Throughput Pooling Interworking Overview (ID: 12875833)

**2.4.11.2.7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12875834`)

Throughput Pooling Interworking
**[PR853223]**

After virtual UE construction, UEs in virtual UE may not be scheduled due to Throughput pooling limit control. As defined in [11215118:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_dtPmsPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_UBmNVp5EEe2A-5U7tzedRA).

The below steps are controlled by the setting of rdTdThroughputPooling ([13353829](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_RrMZQO91EfC3ocOh_n7w7A?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

If rdTdThroughputPooling is True [PR872259] and there are UL MuMImo UE in candidateSet [End PR872259]

Steps in Throughput Pooling Limit Control to handle UL MuMimo UE's

1. Merge List of UE on the subcells
2. For all subcell\_i in availableSubcellIndexes
3. ueList: Merged list of UEs on the subcells (candidateSets.cs2Lists.at(subcell\_i))
4. Estimate throughput
5. For every UE\_i in ueList
6. aggregateTbs += Calculate TBS for UE
7. For New Transmission UE's, Estimation of  TBS shall be done for using the tdPhaseMcs(rrmUlTdMcs).

tdPhaseMcs in current slot is the rrmUlProposeMcs of previous schedule slot.

In all subsequent stages

[Before PR870929] the Min (FdDataMcs , tdPhaseMcs ) [PR870929]

If actLaEnhanceEfficiencyLatency ([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))  is not activated for UL ( i.e "Disabled" or "DL" )

the Min (FdDataMcs , tdPhaseMcs ) [End PR870929] is used to ensure that throughput pooling limit are not exceeded in the later stages of scheduling.

Where FdDataMcs , define the MCS calculated for the UE during FD Phase.

* For Re-Transmission UE's the TBS value is taken from previous HARQ Transmission.

-  For Msg3 UE's , the 64Qam MCS Table and fixed DRMS length (3) is used for TBS calculation.

1. Prepare Drop UEList based on Throughput Pooling Limit

If (aggregateTbs  > allocatedTbs)

If UE\_i is for New Transaction and has satisfies the Min PRB in UL condition

* UE\_i is scheduled from TD phase and eligible for PRB Shaving in FD
* Phase
* Add all the UE from UE\_i+1 to UE\_n in the droppedUeList
* [PR871972]
* Else if UE\_i is for ReTx , add UE\_i to droppedUeList and continue check for UE\_i+1 to UE\_n
* [End PR871972]
* Else
* Add all the UE from UE\_i to UE\_n in the droppedUeList.

Where , allocatedTbs defines the allocated data per slot for the scheduler (allowedDataPerSlot as in [11215118](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxRNrvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

1. Process MuMimo Ue Drop

If the UE in the droppedUeList is a Virtual UE

Virtual UE's dropped due to Throughput pooling limit are handled in the same way as Virtual UE dropped due to over load as defined in [11212319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-k2IvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

1. Update Counter and Trace information

Drop UE counter and TTI Trace information are update with the dropped UE information and reason

The handling of **UlData\_PuschReceiveReq** specified below are inline with the Overload handling mechanism.\*\*\*\*

**[End PR853223]**

---

##### 2.4.11.2.7.0-2 Throughput Pooling Interworking UlData_PuschReceiveReq when Throughput pooling limit Control Happen (ID: 12875835)

**2.4.11.2.7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12875836`)

Throughput Pooling Interworking
**[PR853223]**

IEs in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq(PairedUe/ECpriConfig/eCpriFcpSectionConfig) should be sent according to the UEs which are really scheduled.

Refer to requirement [11169083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26BGSPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

**[End PR853223]**

---

##### 2.4.11.2.7.0-3 Throughput Pooling Interworking L2 trace when Throughput Pooling Limit Control Happen (ID: 12875839)

**2.4.11.2.7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12875840`)

Throughput Pooling Interworking
**[PR853223]**

If all of the paired UEs are not scheduled, ulMuStatus in ulMuUeData(5G\_L2\_9250\_replaced([11205114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-Ef1PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be set as ” muNonPaired\_TpPoolLimit”.

If all of the paired UEs are not scheduled ,ulPreTdBlockUeData.reason (5G\_L2\_8183\_replaced([11201619](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29xk5fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  should be set as ” CS2\_PREFDBLOCKTHROUGHPUT\_DROP\_LIMIT\_REACHED”.

For example, a virtual UE has 3 UEs, UE1, UE2 and UE3. UE1 and UE2 can’t be scheduled due to throughput limit control. ulMuStatus of UE3 should be set to ” muNonPaired\_TpPoolLimit”.

**[End PR853223]**

---

#### 2.4.11.2.8 UL tapering

##### 2.4.11.2.8.0-1 UL tapering Dynamic UL tapering (ID: 11169121)

**2.4.11.2.8.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11169134`)

UL tapering
IF (actUlMuMimo && actUlMuMimoTapering && rescaledSinr >=  taperingSINRThreshold)

isUsingTaperedBeam = TRUE

ELSE

isUsingTaperedBeam = FALSE

- actUlMuMimo is OAM parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq.

- actUlMuMimoTapering is OAM parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

- rescaledSinr is rescaled Sinr based on required PRB, refer to 5G\_L2\_6239\_replaced([11167494](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YlvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- taperingSINRThreshold is for one of two rd parameters:rdTaperingSINRThreshold defined in 5G\_L2\_6970\_replaced([11187027](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_279nD_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when subcell type is C4 or rdTaperingSINRThreshold8RX defined in 5G\_L2\_15142\_replaced([11187037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27-OEfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when[Before CB010708-C] subcell type is C8 [CB010708-C] Pusch is 8Rx or 16Rx(numOfRxPusch=8 or 16, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB010708-C]

isUsingTaperedBeam need to be calculated and store, which will be used in correlation calculation, tapering compensation and beam selection.

IF isUsingTaperedBeam && UE is MU UE

UE shall use tapered beams to receive UL signals

ELSE

UE shall use non-tapered beams to receive UL signals

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9866\_replaced([11148389](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKEI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.11.2.8.0-2 UL tapering UL tapering compensation factor (ID: 11169139)

**2.4.11.2.8.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169161`)

UL tapering
IF (isUsingTaperedBeam && rdTaperingCompFlag && ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch = 0(MU-SINR estimation)), L2RT shall calculate tapering compensation factor as following.

[PR672232]

ELSE IF  (isUsingTaperedBeam && ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch = 0(MU-SINR estimation)), rrmTaperingCompensation shall always be equal to 0.5.

[End PR672232]

ELSE, rrmTaperingCompensation shall always be equal to 1.

rrmTaperingCompensation calculation:

If rdMuInfCorr is configured to true, [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a stylized font. The equation is:
$rrmTaperingCompensation = \frac{G\_{i,\hat{w}*{i,1}} + G*{i,\hat{w}*{i,2}}}{G*{i,w\_{i,1}} + G\_{i,w\_{i,2}}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4055.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

If rdMuInfCorr is configured to false, [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is:
`rrmTaperingCompensation = Gi,ŵi,1 / Gi,wi,1`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4055.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

- isUsingTaperedBeam is calculated and stored in 5G\_L2\_8061\_replaced([11169121](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26BtUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- rdTaperingCompFlag is RD parameter. The default value of rdTaperingCompFlag is false. Refer to 5G\_L2\_7810\_replaced([11187051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27-OFPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch is OAM parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

- rdMuInfCorr is RD parameter. The default value of rdMuInfCorr is false. Refer to 5G\_L2\_6382\_replaced([11186938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_279AA_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the letter "G" followed by a subscript that appears to be "i, w, 1". The colors are muted and indistinct, with hints of purple, yellow, and blue. The overall impression is of a digital or printed character that has been heavily blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4055.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] and [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "G i,w,1,2" in a stylized font with a gradient effect. The letters are slightly blurred, giving them a soft, almost watercolor-like appearance. The background is white, and the text is positioned in the center of the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4055.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is beamforming gain of the 1st and 2nd best tapered beam of UE(i).

- [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of the letter "G" followed by the subscript "i,w,1". The colors are muted and appear to have a slight rainbow effect, suggesting a digital artifact or a lens flare. The background is dark and out of focus.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4055.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] and [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "G i,w1,2" in a blurry, stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of purple, blue, and yellow. The text is positioned on a white background with a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4055.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] is beamforming gain of the 1st and 2nd best non-tapered beam of UE(i).

- When rdMuInfCorr is configured to true but the 2nd best beam is invalid, L2RT shall calculate rrmTaperingCompensation according to the algorithm in case of rdMuInfCorr is false.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13871\_replaced([11148444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKELfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

##### 2.4.11.2.8.0-3 UL tapering Tapering Status Update (ID: 11169166)

**2.4.11.2.8.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11169171`)

UL tapering
Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15693\_replaced([11148418](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKEKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If tapering shall not be applied to all UE according to degree between UEs, isUsingTaperedBeam = FALSE.

If isUsingTaperedBeam updated here, correlation should also be updated for final mcs calculation.

#### 2.4.11.2.9 MU-SINR

##### 2.4.11.2.9.0-1 MU-SINR MU-SINR estimation for the root UE and paired UEs (ID: 11169179)

**2.4.11.2.9.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11169222`)

MU-SINR
[Before CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO]

If (ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch == 0)(MU-SINR estimation algorithm is active), mu-sinr should be calculated.

[CB008360-A]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$SINR\_{i,mu} = Linear2dB(-\frac{1}{\frac{1}{dB2Linear(SINR\_{i,su})} + \alpha \* \sum\_{j \in J^\*} Corr\_{i,j}})$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4060.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[End CB008360-A]

[CB008360-B]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$SINR\_{i,mu} = Linear2dB(\frac{1}{\frac{1}{dB2Linear(SINR\_{i,su}) \* rrmTaperingCompensation} + \alpha \* \sum\_{j \in J^\*} Corr\_{i,j}})$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4060.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End CB008360-B]

- [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "SINR i,mu". The text is in a light color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred and have a chromatic aberration effect, giving them a rainbow-like outline.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4060.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] is the MU-SINR of UE(i) in dB.

- [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "SINR i,su". The text is in a light blue color and is set against a white background. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4060.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is SU-SINR in dB based on required PRB or allocated PRB according to the usage of mu-sinr. Refer to 5G\_L2\_6270\_replaced([11168337](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-C9_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SE check and 5G\_L2\_6242\_replaced([11169030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26AfPPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for final mcs calculation.

- [image-4][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract depiction of a corner of a document or screen. The left side features a gradient of colors, transitioning from a warm orange and yellow at the bottom left, through blues and purples, to a lighter, almost white hue at the top. This colorful section appears to be a stylized graph or chart, with vertical lines suggesting bars or data points. To the right of this colorful area, the image is predominantly white, with a soft, blurred glow emanating from the top right corner. There is no discernible text or table in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4060.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] is the set of paired UEs in one virtual UE excluding the UE i.

- [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Corrᵢⱼ". The text is blurry and has a slight color distortion.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4060.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] is the correlation from UE(j) to UE(i). The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4060.004.png[/image\_path][/image-7])).

##### 2.4.11.2.9.0-2 MU-SINR MU-SINR estimation for the root UE and paired candidate UE group (ID: 11169228)

**2.4.11.2.9.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11169318`)

MU-SINR
[Before CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO]

If (ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch == 0(MU-SINR estimation algorithm is active) &&

[Before 03313301]rdactUlMuMimoEnhance[03313301]actUlMuMimoGroupParing[End 03313301] &&

root UE(i) paired with a candidate UE group(Z) )

MU-SINR should be calculated

1) L2RT shall calculate MU-SINR of the UEs which are included in the candidate UE group(Z) according to 5G\_L2\_6734\_replaced([11169179](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26BtWvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), where [image-0][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract composition of colors and shapes. It appears to be a digital rendering or a photograph with a strong artistic filter applied. The dominant colors are white, with streaks and patches of blue, yellow, purple, and green. There are no discernible objects or text within the image. The overall impression is one of soft gradients and diffused light, with a blurred edge on the right side.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4063.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] is the root UE(i).

2)L2RT shall calculate MU-SINR of root UE(i) as following considering the proportion of required PRB:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula defines $SINR\_{i,mu}$ as a function of $Linear2dB$, $dB2Linear(SINR\_{i,su})$, $rrmTaperingCompensation$, $\alpha$, and a summation term involving $N\_j^{req}$, $N\_{vUE}^{req}$, and $Corr\_{i,j}$.
The formula is:
$SINR\_{i,mu} = Linear2dB(\frac{1}{\frac{1}{dB2Linear(SINR\_{i,su}) \* rrmTaperingCompensation} + \alpha \* \sum\_{j \in Z} \frac{N\_j^{req}}{N\_{vUE}^{req}} Corr\_{i,j}})$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4063.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

-     [03313301]actUlMuMimoGroupParing is OAM parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq[End 03313301]

- [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "SIN R i, mu". The text is in a light color against a white background, with a black border around the image. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4063.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] is the MU-SINR of UE(i) in dB.

- [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "SINR i,su". The text is in a light blue color and is set against a white background. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4063.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is SU-SINR in dB based on required PRB or allocated PRB according to the usage of mu-sinr. Refer to 5G\_L2\_6270\_replaced([11168337](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-C9_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SE check and 5G\_L2\_6242\_replaced([11169030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26AfPPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for final mcs calculation.

- [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Corrᵢⱼ". The text is blurry and has a slight color distortion.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4063.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] is the correlation from UE(j) to UE(i). Refer to 5G\_L2\_6264\_replaced([11167962](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_258NzPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- [image-5][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to provide a detailed description.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4063.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5] is a parameter, get from the table stored in 5G\_L2\_6462\_replaced([11169332](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26CUYfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) based on min(rdRescaledSuSINR, rounding-off([image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the text "SINR i,su". The text is in a light blue color and is set against a white background. The text is slightly blurry.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4063.004.png[/image\_path][/image-6])).

o rdRescaledSuSINR is RD parameter. The default value of rdRescaledSuSINR is 50dB. Refer to 5G\_L2\_6388\_replaced([11186968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_279nA_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- rrmTaperingCompensation is sinr compensation for tapering. Refer to 5G\_L2\_8148\_replaced([11169139](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26BtVfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a close-up of a mathematical expression. The expression appears to be "N\_i^{req}". The characters are slightly blurred and have a colorful, almost iridescent sheen, particularly around the edges. The "N" is large and bold, with a subscript "i" positioned below and to the right of it. Above and to the right of the "N\_i" is the superscript "req". The background is white, and the text is dark, likely black or a very dark gray.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4063.007.png[/image\_path][/image-7] is  the number of required PRBs of UE(j).

- [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry close-up of text. The visible text appears to be "N req" on the top line and "vu e" on the bottom line. The letters are in a sans-serif font and have a rainbow-like color gradient.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4063.008.png[/image\_path][/image-8] is the number of required PRBs of virtual UE.

- Z is the candidate UE group.

##### 2.4.11.2.9.0-3 MU-SINR Mu-Mimo Related SINR (ID: 11169323)

**2.4.11.2.9.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11169327`)

MU-SINR
1. Instantaneous SU-SINR request and report:

4Rx: see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21891\_replaced([11148754](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKrO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

8Rx/16Rx: see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21886\_replaced([11148764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKrPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. Input of instantaneous SU-SINR averaging: see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24987\_replaced([11149165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksMgYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. SU-SINR averaging: see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19820\_replaced([11130714](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krANlvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
3. MU-SINR to SU-SINR conversion: see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21939\_replaced([11148794](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksLSQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), only used when rdSuSinrFlag (5G\_UP\_ALG\_21893\_replaced([11149710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksPjt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))/ rdSuSinrFlag16RX (5G\_UP\_ALG\_27713\_replaced([11149730](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksPjuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is flase.
4. SU-SINR to MU-SINR: see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8348\_replaced([11148500](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksKEMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), a.k.a MU-SINR estimation in FD scheduler.

##### 2.4.11.2.9.0-4 MU-SINR Table for alpha in MU-SINR calculation (ID: 11169332)

**2.4.11.2.9.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11169425`)

MU-SINR
IF (only one UL subcell with ulSubcellType C4 configured) OR ((multiple UL subcells with ulSubcellType C4 configured) AND (only one subcell scheduled)) OR ([Before CB010708-C]UL subcell with ulSubcellType C8 configured [CB010708-C] Pusch is 8Rx or 16Rx(numOfRxPusch=8 or 16, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB010708-C] ), [image-0][image\_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract representation of a square with a gradient of colors. The colors range from a light beige on the left to a darker gray and black on the right. Within the square, there are hints of blue, red, and yellow, suggesting a colorful, possibly digital, design. The overall impression is one of a stylized logo or icon, but the blurriness makes it difficult to discern any specific details or text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4069.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] should be decided by option 1:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry shot of a colorful, abstract design. The left side of the image is dominated by a gradient of colors, transitioning from white at the top left to various shades of blue, green, yellow, and pink. These colors appear to be arranged in horizontal bands or blocks, creating a layered effect. The right side of the image is a solid black void, with a soft, blurred gradient separating it from the colorful section. The overall impression is one of a vibrant, abstract artwork partially obscured by darkness.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4069.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] is calculated assuming that when correlation is ulMuMimoCorrThd, paired UE number is rdMaxInterf, maximum sinr downgrade is rdMuSuSINRDiff.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula defines a variable 'a' as the minimum of 1.0 and a fraction. The numerator of the fraction is "dB2Linear(rdMuSuSINRDiff)". The denominator of the fraction is "dB2Linear(SINRisu) \* rdMaxInterf \* corrThd".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4069.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

- [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "SINR,su". The text is in a light color against a dark background. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4069.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] is SU-SINR, can be integer numbers in the range[0, rdRescaledSuSINR], rdRescaledSuSINR is RD parameter. The default value of rdRescaledSuSINR is 50dB. Refer to 5G\_L2\_6388\_replaced([11186968](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_279nA_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- rdMuSuSINRDiff is RD parameter. The default value of rdMuSuSINRDiff is 3dB. Refer to 5G\_L2\_6390\_replaced([11186980](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_279nBfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- rdMaxInterf is RD parameter. The default value of rdMaxInterf is 2. Refer to 5G\_L2\_6721\_replaced([11186990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_279nB_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- If NIDD:nrCellType = 'xDL4UL', corrThd = ulMuMimoCorrThd; If NIDD:nrCellType = 'xDL8UL', corrThd = ulMuMimoCorrThd2. ulMuMimoCorrThd/ ulMuMimoCorrThd2 is OAM parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

Implementation proposal: a table for [image-4][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry, and pixelated view of a logo. The logo appears to be a stylized letter or symbol with vibrant colors, including blue, red, yellow, and white, arranged in a geometric pattern. The background is white, and the right side of the image is black, suggesting it might be a cropped portion of a larger image or a graphic element with a dark border. The overall impression is of a digital graphic with a focus on abstract design and color.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4069.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] based on different SINR can be calcaulated and stored during the period of cell setup to improve software efficirency.

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a table with a yellow header row and a white data row. The header row has the following columns: "SU-SINR dB", "0", "1", "2", "......", and "rdRescaledSuSINR". The data row has the following entries corresponding to the header columns: "α", "......", "......", "......", "......", and "......". The table appears to be a representation of data related to Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) for a single user (SU). The "......" entries suggest that there are more data points or values that are not explicitly shown in this snippet.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4069.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

ELSE, [image-6][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry, and pixelated view of a logo. The logo appears to be a stylized letter or symbol with vibrant colors, including blue, red, yellow, and white, arranged in a geometric pattern. The background is white, and the right side of the image is black, suggesting it might be a cropped portion of a larger image or a graphic element with a dark border. The overall impression is of a digital graphic with a focus on abstract design and color.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4069.005.png[/image\_path][/image-6] shall be decided by option2:

Using RD parameters rdAlpha1~rdAlpha7(Refer to 5G\_L2\_11625\_replaced([11187119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27-1IvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Default value of  rdAlpha1~rdAlpha7 are as followings.

[image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a table with three columns: "SINRᵢ,su", "α", and "Default value". The table lists different ranges of SINR (Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio) values and their corresponding alpha values and default values.

| SINRᵢ,su | α | Default value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SINR <=15dB | rdAlpha1 | 0.978 |
| 15dB < SINR <= 20dB | rdAlpha2 | 0.397 |
| 20dB < SINR <= 25dB | rdAlpha3 | 0.372 |
| 25dB < SINR <= 30dB | rdAlpha4 | 0.196 |
| 30dB < SINR <= 35dB | rdAlpha5 | 0.101 |
| 35dB < SINR <= 40dB | rdAlpha6 | 0.055 |
| SINR > 40dB | rdAlpha7 | 0.014 |

Note: During SE check, since we don't know how many subcells will be scheduled finally,  [image-8][image\_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry, and pixelated view of a logo. The logo appears to be a stylized letter or symbol with vibrant colors, including blue, red, yellow, and white, arranged in a geometric pattern. If only one UL subcell with ulSubcellType C4 configured, option1 used; else, option2 used.

#### 2.4.11.2.10 MCS compensation

##### 2.4.11.2.10.0-1 MCS compensation (ID: 11169434)

**2.4.11.2.10.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11169488`)

MCS compensation
[Before CB008235-A] If (ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch == 1)(MCS compensation), [CB008235-A] If MCS compensation is used(5G\_L2\_11681\_replaced([11168322](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-C9PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), [End CB008235-A] mcs should be adjusted as follows.

1) Calculate deltaPairSize[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the following text: deltaPairSize = currentPairSize - lastPairSize[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4074.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

- Pair size for UE(i) in virtual UE(A) is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20424\_replaced([11149069](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksL5XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- lastPairSize can be got based on 5G\_L2\_7017\_replaced([11169493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26CUavvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[Before CB008235-A]

2) Get deltaMcs based on deltaPairSize

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a table with two columns and eight rows. The first column is titled "deltaPaireSize" and contains numerical values ranging from -3 to 3. The second column is titled "deltaMcs" and contains corresponding string values that appear to be variable names or identifiers.

| deltaPaireSize | deltaMcs |
| --- | --- |
| -3 | -mcsOffsetLayerDiffThree |
| -2 | -mcsOffsetLayerDiffTwo |
| -1 | -mcsOffsetLayerDiffOne |
| 0 | mcsOffsetLayerDiffZero |
| 1 | mcsOffsetLayerDiffOne |
| 2 | mcsOffsetLayerDiffTwo |
| 3 | mcsOffsetLayerDiffThree |

- mcsOffsetLayerDiffZero/mcsOffsetLayerDiffOne/mcsOffsetLayerDiffTwo/mcsOffsetLayerDiffThree are OAM parameters received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

- mcsOffsetLayerDiffOne/mcsOffsetLayerDiffTwo/mcsOffsetLayerDiffThree should be negative values.

3) Adjust current mcs using deltaMcs.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a light gray font against a white background. The equation is: MCS = MCS + deltaMcs. The text is slightly blurred, but all characters are legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4074.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8349\_replaced([11148930](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksLSS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB008235-A]

2) Adjust current mcs as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8349\_replaced([11148930](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksLSS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008235-A]

##### 2.4.11.2.10.0-2 MCS compensation Last Pair Size (ID: 11169493)

**2.4.11.2.10.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169498`)

MCS compensation
lastPairSize for UE is defined as follows.

- lastPairSize is initialized as 0.

- If UE processes SINR from pusch, lastPairSize will be set to the pair size in the corresponding harqInfo. Which is saved when calculating final mcs. Refer to 5G\_L2\_6243\_replaced([11169044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26AfPvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- If UE processes SINR from SRS, lastPairSize will be set to 0.

---

#### 2.4.11.2.11 Receiver Model

##### 2.4.11.2.11.0-1 Receiver Model (ID: 11169508)

**2.4.11.2.11.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11169527`)

Receiver Model
L2RT shall decide which subcell and receiver model will be used for the MU-MIMO and SU-MIMO UEs and indicate in UlData\_PuschReceiveReq(Refer to 5G\_L2\_7033\_replaced([11179604](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27HScfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_7035\_replaced([11179582](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27GrbfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- For the SU-MIMO UEs,

o Subcell, should always be primary subcell except ReTx UE, which will use the same subcell as initial transmission.

o Receiver Model, should always be Su-Receiver.

- For the MU-MIMO UEs,

o If  single C4 subcell is configured

§ Subcell, should always be primary subcell.

§ Receiver Model, should always be Mu-Receiver.

o Iftwo C4 subcells are configured

Refer to the table in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9550\_replaced([11146266](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksDWfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

§ If 2 UEs with rank1 paired, they will go to the primary cell.

§ If 2 UEs paired, and at least one of them has the rank larger than 1, they will go to different subcells.

§ When the virtual UE is constructed by 3 or 4 UEs and the number of layers for all of them is one, L2RT shall decide which two UEs use the same MU-IRC receiver model as following:

The UEs with minimum horizontal degree separation shall be assigned to the same MU-IRC receiver, the remaining UE(s) will be assigned to the other receiver.

§ If root UE is paired with a candidate UE group, SU-IRC receiver in the 1st subcell will be used by root UE and SU-IRC receiver in the 2nd subcell will be used by the UEs which are included in candidate UE group.

o [Before CB010708-E] C8 subcell is configured [CB010708-E] Pusch is 8Rx or 16Rx(numOfRxPusch=8 or 16, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB010708-E]

§ Receiver Model, should always be Mu-Receiver.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9550\_replaced([11146266](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksDWfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

#### 2.4.11.2.12 MU Receiver Beam Used for Overlapping PRB

##### 2.4.11.2.12.0-1 MU Receiver Beam Used for Overlapping PRB (ID: 11169541)

**2.4.11.2.12.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12639717`)

MU Receiver Beam Used for Overlapping PRB
If UE1 paired with UE2, these 2 UEs go to the same subcell, and MU-IRC receiver is used. Let's consider UE0 is allocated with PRB [startPRB , lastPRB\_UE0] ; UE1 is allocated with PRB [startPRB , lastPRB\_UE1] ; lastPRB\_UE1 > lastPRB\_UE0. If those UEs are scheduled in subcell with subcell type C4, beam used for [lastPRB\_UE0+1, lastPRB\_UE1] **[Before CNI-137616-B]is decided by rdUlMuReceiverBeamUsedOverlappingPRB.**

- If rdUlMuReceiverBeamUsedOverlappingPRB = true,it **[End CNI-137616-B]should  be received by the 2nd best beam of UE1.**

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a table with two rows and two columns. The first column contains the labels "UE0" and "UE1". The second column contains colored cells with text.
The first row, labeled "UE0", has two cells. The first cell is yellow and contains the text "1st best beam of UE0". The second cell is blue and contains the text "2nd best beam of UE1".
The second row, labeled "UE1", has one cell that spans the entire width of the second column. This cell is light blue and contains the text "1st best beam of UE1".
```markdown

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| UE0 | 1st best beam of UE0 | 2nd best beam of UE1 |
| UE1 | 1st best beam of UE1 |  |
| ```[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4086.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |

**[Before CNI-137616-B] - Else rdUlMuReceiverBeamUsedOverlappingPRB = false, it should  be received by the 1st best beam of UE0.**

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a table with two rows and two columns. The first column contains the labels "UE0" and "UE1". The second column is divided into two parts for the "UE0" row and one part for the "UE1" row.
The cell for "UE0" in the first part of the second column contains the text "1st best beam of UE0" and is colored yellow. The cell for "UE0" in the second part of the second column contains the text "1st best beam of UE0" and is colored orange.
The cell for "UE1" in the second column contains the text "1st best beam of UE1" and is colored light blue.
```md

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| UE0 | 1st best beam of UE0 |
| UE1 | 1st best beam of UE1 |
| ```[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4086.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] |  |

o rdUlMuReceiverBeamUsedOverlappingPRB is RD parameter. The default value of rdUlMuReceiverBeamUsedOverlappingPRB is TRUE. Refer to 5G\_L2\_8316\_replaced([11187071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27-OGPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). **[End CNI-137616-B]**

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11355\_replaced([11104143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kn5PNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11359\_replaced([11149352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksNHevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
