#### 2.4.11.2.14 reTx UE Pairing

##### 2.4.11.2.14.0-1 reTx UE Pairing Simple reTx Pairing (ID: 11169583)

**2.4.11.2.14.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12463374`)

reTx UE Pairing
[Before CNI-134356]

For the reTx UEs in CS2 in the same slot, if they are paired in former initial transmission, the reTx Ues  should be paired as a virtual Ue directly without correlation and SE check.

- the reTx Ues in the virtual Ue should be allocated same number of Prbs as initTx

- the reTx Ues in the virtual Ue should be allocated same mcs as initTx

- the reTx Ues in the virtual Ue should be allocated same subcell as initTx

- the reTx Ues in the virtual Ue should be allocated same start Prb

- totally reserved/allocated number of prbs of the virtual UE should be the max number of Prbs of all reTx Ues instead of sum.

Flow chart refer to 5G\_L2\_7312\_replaced([11167383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255xivvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), pairing procedure refer to 5G\_L2\_7314\_replaced([11167415](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255xjfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: if Ue is a MPR aware UE(5G\_L2\_5038\_replaced([11170086](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26HM5PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which should not be paired.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8890\_replaced([11145770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksBhRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CNI-134356]

---

##### 2.4.11.2.14.0-2 reTx UE Pairing Normal reTx Pairing (ID: 11169597)

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169602`)

reTx UE Pairing
Overview

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11169610`)

reTx UE Pairing
The reTx UEs shall try to pair with reTx UEs which have paired in initial transmission (5G\_L2\_7317\_replaced([11169583](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26C7e_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) firstly [Before PR710589][CB010659-B]If the adaptive retransmission feature is deactivated(NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcsEnh is set to FALSE).  Simple reTx Ue pairing procedure shall be skipped if the adaptive retransmission feature is activated (NRBTS/NRCELL/actAdaptiveReTxResMcsEnh is set to TRUE).[End CB010659-B][End PR710589].

The reTx Mu-eligible UEs(5G\_L2\_8241\_replaced([11169620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Dig_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) can pair with reTx UEs and/or initTx UEs.

The reTx root UE(5G\_L2\_8247\_replaced([11169690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EJkvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is selected from rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList as priority(5G\_L2\_8250\_replaced([11169669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26DijfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The pairing algorithm (5G\_L2\_8246\_replaced([11169678](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Dij_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) will continue with next retX Mu-eligible UE as root UE until rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList is NULL.

After each pairing round, related information should be updated(5G\_L2\_8258\_replaced([11169721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EJmfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as the input of next round.

The reTx UEs can't be paired with a UE group or be added in a UE group.

After reTx UEs pairing, initTx UEs pairing should be handled(5G\_L2\_6223\_replaced([11167490](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YlfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9509\_replaced([11146323](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksD9hPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11169615`)

reTx UE Pairing
Select UEs

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169620`)

reTx UE Pairing
Mu Eligible Criterion

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11169630`)

reTx UE Pairing
1. For initTx Ues, please refer to 5G\_L2\_6239\_replaced([11167494](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YlvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. For reTx Ues which should satisfy the conditions in 5G\_L2\_6239\_replaced([11167494](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YlvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the conditions in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11084\_replaced([11148246](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksJdE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to be a reTx Mu-eligible UE.

Note: required PRB of reTx UE[CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO] [CB010659-B] which is scheduled with the MCS of the initial transmission [End CB010659-B] [End CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO] is equal to initTx Prbs.

[CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO] [CB010659-B] required PRB of reTx UE using a reserved MCS is given by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11970\_replaced([11202476](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB010659-B] [End CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO]

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11169634`)

reTx UE Pairing
Ue Schedulable Criterion

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11169651`)

reTx UE Pairing
IF (actUlMuMimo = true AND UE is a muEligible UE(5G\_L2\_8241\_replaced([11169620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Dig_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) )

Select a schedulable UE and add to rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeList/rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList(5G\_L2\_8250\_replaced([11169669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26DijfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11510\_replaced([11196812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwFh6PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) except prb requirement as below.

minReqPrbs <=  available PRBs[i] + muEligiblePrbs[i]

ELSE

Select a schedulable UE and add to toBeScheduledUeLists/toBeAllocatedUes/toBeDistributedUes as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11920\_replaced([11196444](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCeo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

where,

minReqPrbs: Minimal required prbs is the least prbs for a UE to be shceduled.

For a nonGbr initTx UE, minReqPrbs should be rdMinSupportedNumPrbsUl + UciPuschPrbs.

For a reTx UE which is scheduled with the MCS of the initial transmission, minReqPrbs should equal to initTx Prbs.

For a reTx UE using a reserved MCS, minReqPrbs is given by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11970\_replaced([11202476](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

available PRBs[i] : Remaining Prbs in subArea i of BWP.

muEligiblePrbs[i]: The Prbs reserved for Mu-eligible UEs in subArea i of BWP (5G\_L2\_8318\_replaced([11169656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26DiivvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB012605-I] Note: if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed is TRUE, check of minReqPrbs is supported in both layerPrbs and cellPrbs, so that we don't exceed any limit. [End CB012605-I]

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11169656`)

reTx UE Pairing
Prbs Pre-Reservation

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11169664`)

reTx UE Pairing
With actUlMuMimo configured as true,  the reserved prbs and remaining prbs in each subArea should be updated as muEligiblePrbs and available PRBs as below.

For muEligible UEs (5G\_L2\_8241\_replaced([11169620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Dig_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), extra update as below.

muEligiblePrbs[i] +=min(available PRBs[i],requiredPRB)

For all UEs(including muEligible UEs and non-muEligible UEs).

available PRBs[i] -= min(available PRBs[i],requiredPRB)

where,

available PRBs[i] : Remaining Prbs in subArea i of BWP.

muEligiblePrbs[i]: The Prbs reserved for Mu-eligible UEs in subArea i of BWP.

Note:

-  muEligiblePrbs[i] initialize as 0 at the beginning of each UL slot.

-  available PRBs[i] initialize as max prbs of  subArea i of BWP at the beginning of each UL slot.

[CB012605-I] Note: if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed is TRUE, calculation of muEligiblePrbs and availablePrbs is done in both layerPrbs and cellPrbs, so that we don't exceed any limit. [End CB012605-I]

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11169669`)

reTx UE Pairing
Ue Lists for selected UEs

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11169673`)

reTx UE Pairing
With actUlMuMimo configured as true, two more Ue lists(rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList and rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeList) are introduced besides the legacy lists(toBeScheduledUeLists, toBeAllocatedUes,toBeDistributedUes).

A schedulable UE should be added to corresponding lists as below.

- All schedulable UEs should be added to toBeScheduledUeLists

- Schedulable non-MuEligble UEs with fixed prbs or no need more prbs should be added to toBeAllocatedUes

- Schedulable non-MuEligble UEs which need more prbs should be added to toBeDistributedUes

- Schedulable MuEligble reTx UEs  (5G\_L2\_8241\_replaced([11169620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Dig_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be added to rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList, and will be added to toBeAllocatedUes after pairing.

- Schedulable MuEligble initTx UEs (5G\_L2\_6239\_replaced([11167494](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YlvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be added to rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeList , and will be added to toBeDistributedUes/toBeAllocatedUes after pairing.

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11169678`)

reTx UE Pairing
reTx UE Pairing Algorithm

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11169682`)

reTx UE Pairing
Pairing Process

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11169687`)

reTx UE Pairing
Try to construct reTx virtual UE based on rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList and rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeList as below.

1. Deciding to construct vUE or not as 5G\_L2\_6254\_replaced([11167505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YmPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. Select root UE as 5G\_L2\_8247\_replaced([11169690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EJkvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
3. Construct rrmDedicateList as 5G\_L2\_6257\_replaced([11167673](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_257myfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
4. Check reTx UE subcell as 5G\_L2\_8256\_replaced([11169699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EJlPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
5. Check the correlation between root UE and the UEs in rrmDedicateList as 5G\_L2\_6258\_replaced([11167855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_258NyfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
6. Check reTx UE MuSinr/vUE layer as 5G\_L2\_8254\_replaced([11169706](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EJlvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
7. Check spectrum efficiency as 5G\_L2\_6259\_replaced([11168277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_259b7PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
8. Update numberOfRemainingPrbsForDistribution(5G\_L2\_8269\_replaced([11168778](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25_RG_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) before WRR(5G\_UP\_ALG\_5002\_replaced([11200325](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwRvLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11169690`)

reTx UE Pairing
reTx Root UE selection

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169695`)

reTx UE Pairing
The UE in rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList the with highest priority(5G\_UP\_ALG\_4769\_replaced([11194310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvz1JPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) should be selected as root UE.

Root UE should be removed from rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList after pairing, in spite of pairing succeeded or not.

Note :

For initTx root UE selection, refer to 5G\_L2\_6256\_replaced([11167657](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_257mwPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8890\_replaced([11145770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksBhRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11169699`)

reTx UE Pairing
Subcell Check

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11169702`)

reTx UE Pairing
IF (ulSubcellList.size()=2 AND ulSubcellList[0].l1PoolId != ulSubcellList[1].l1PoolId)

For a reTx Ue, it should be allocated the same subcell as initial transmission.

- if reTx root UE is rank2, reTx candidate UEs must be the different initTx subcell with reTx root UE.

- if reTx root UE is rank1, rank1 reTx UE with same subcell with reTx root UE can be the candidate.

- if reTx root UE is rank1, rank1 or rank2 reTx UE with different subcell with reTx root UE can be the candidate.

For initTx UE, subcell will be allocated after virtual UE constructed(5G\_L2\_7233\_replaced([11169508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26CUbfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

ELSE

reTx Ue can be allocated a different subcell with initial transmission. Subcell of UEs in a virtual Ue will be allocated as 5G\_L2\_7233\_replaced([11169508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26CUbfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: virtual UE total rank check refer to 5G\_L2\_6474\_replaced([11167679](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_257myvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11169706`)

reTx UE Pairing
MuSinr/layer Check

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11169717`)

reTx UE Pairing
IF (ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch = 0) // MU-SINR estimation

To simplify implementation(avoid prb compensation and don't use reserved mcs), Mu sinr of reTx UE in virtual UE should meet the condition below.

retx UE muSinr >= sinr of the UE in initial transmission

ELSE // ulMuMimoLinkAdaptionSwitch =1, MCS compensation

pairedLayers of reTx UE <= pairedLayers of the UE in initial transmission

where,

pairedLayers : Sum of layers other UEs in vUE.

[CB010708-C] Note : If Pusch is 8Rx/16Rx(numOfRxPusch=8/16, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), this check shall be skipped. [End CB010708-C]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9548\_replaced([11147793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHn8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11169721`)

reTx UE Pairing
Information Updating After Each Pairing Round

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11169725`)

reTx UE Pairing
Summary

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11169728`)

reTx UE Pairing
After each reTx or initTx UE pairing, that is a root UE tries to pair with reTx UEs and/or initTx UEs, whether a virtual UE is constructed or not, the following information should be updated.

1. Calculate the minimal required prbs(minReqPrbs) of UE(virtual UE can't be constructed with the UE as rootUE) or virtual UE (5G\_L2\_8261\_replaced([11169732](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EJnPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
2. Reserve prb for root UE(virtual UE can't be constructed) or virtual UE (5G\_L2\_8263\_replaced([11169746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EwoPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
3. Update UE lists (5G\_L2\_8265\_replaced([11169759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Ewo_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
4. Allocate subcell/mu-receiver/DMRS  (5G\_L2\_8267\_replaced([11169772](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EwpvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11169732`)

reTx UE Pairing
Minimal Required Prb of Root UE/Virtual UE

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11169741`)

reTx UE Pairing
Minimal required prbs(minReqPrbs) is the least prbs for a UE to be scheduled.

For a nonGbr initTx UE, minReqPrbs should be rdMinSupportedNumPrbsUl + UciPuschPrbs.

For a reTx UE [CB010659-B] which is scheduled with the MCS of the initial transmission [End CB010659-B], minReqPrbs should equal to initTx Prbs.

[CB010659-B] For a reTx UE using a reserved MCS, minReqPrbs is given by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11970\_replaced([11202476](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwdVX_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB010659-B]

IF (virtual UE is constructed)

IF (virtual Ue with ue group(5G\_L2\_7326\_replaced([11169781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EwqPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))))

minReqPrbs = Max(minReqPrbs of root Ue, Sum(minReqPrbs of Ues in ue group))

ELSE

minReqPrbs = Max(minReqPrbs of UEs in virtual UE)

ELSE

minReqPrbs = minReqPrbs of root Ue

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9548\_replaced([11147793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHn8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11169746`)

reTx UE Pairing
Prb Reservation For Root UE(virtual UE can't be constructed) or Virtual Ue

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11169755`)

reTx UE Pairing
Reserve minimal required prbs(minReqPrbs) for UE(virtual UE can't be constructed with the UE as rootUE) or virtual UE as below.

1. select a subArea

minReqPrbs<=  available PRBs[i] + muEligiblePrbs[i]

and

available PRBs[i] + muEligiblePrbs[i] is the smallest one in all subAreas.

1. update muEligiblePrbs in corresponding subArea

muEligiblePrbs[i] -= minReqPrbs

[CB007113-I]

1. If multiple UL subcells with ulSubcellType C4 configured AND slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE

update remainingSubcellResourcesForDistribution

If UE/vUE allocated in two subcell

remainingSubcellResourcesForDistribution -= 2 \* minReqPrbs

Else

remainingSubcellResourcesForDistribution -= minReqPrbs

[End CB007113-I]

where,

muEligiblePrbs[i] : The prbs reserved for Mu-eligible UEs in subArea i of BWP (5G\_L2\_8318\_replaced([11169656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26DiivvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

available PRBs[i] : Remaining Prbs in subArea i of BWP.

[CB007113-I]

remainingSubcellResourcesForDistribution : Total remaining subcell PRBs.

[End CB007113-I]

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `11169759`)

reTx UE Pairing
Ue List Updating

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11169768`)

reTx UE Pairing
1. Remove UEs from rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList / rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeList

IF (virtual UE is constructed)

Remove all UEs in virtual UE from rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList or rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeList

ELSE

Remove the UE from rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList or rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeList

1. Add UE(virtual UE can't be constructed with the UE as rootUE) or virtual UE to toBeAllocatedUes/toBeDistributedUes

IF ( UE is a reTx UE OR virtual UE is constructed with reTx root UE)

Add UE / virtual UE to toBeAllocatedUes

ELSE

Add  UE / virtual UE to toBeDistributedUes

[CB007113-I]

Add UE / virtual UE to rrmRootOnlyUeListForEst / rrmvUeListForEst

[End CB007113-I]

1. Remove un-schedulable UEs

With muEligiblePrbs[CB007113-I]/remainingSubcellResourcesForDistribution[End CB007113-I] updated(5G\_L2\_8263\_replaced([11169746](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EwoPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), UEs(in rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList or rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeList) schedulability should be rechecked.

If UE's minReqPrbs meet the condition as below, the UE should be removed from rrmReTxMuEligibleUeList/rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeList and toBeScheduledUeLists.

minReqPrbs > max (available PRBs[i]+muEligiblePrbs[i]) of all subArea

[CB007113-I]

OR

multiple UL subcells with ulSubcellType C4 configured AND slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE  AND minReqPrbs > remainingSubcellResourcesForDistribution

[End CB007113-I]

where,

minReqPrbs: Minimal required prbs is the least prbs for a UE to be shceduled.

For a nonGbr initTx UE, minReqPrbs should be rdMinSupportedNumPrbsUl + UciPuschPrbs.

For a reTx UE, minReqPrbs should equal to initTx Prbs.

available PRBs[i] : Remaining Prbs in subArea i of BWP.

muEligiblePrbs[i]: The Prbs reserved for Mu-eligible UEs in subArea i of BWP (5G\_L2\_8318\_replaced([11169656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26DiivvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB007113-I]

remainingSubcellResourcesForDistribution : Total remaining subcell PRBs.

[End CB007113-I]

Rationale: If a UE can't find a subArea with enough remaining prbs, it can't be scheduled.

Note: If UEs been removed from lists, rrmRequiredMuPrbNum[CB007113-I]/rrmRequiredMuSubcellPrbNum/rrmSchedWeights[End CB007113-I] should be updated. Refer to 5G\_L2\_6358\_replaced([11167527](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YnPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[CB007113-I]and 5G\_L2\_9644\_replaced([11167557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YofvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB007113-I].

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `11169772`)

reTx UE Pairing
Allocate Subcell/Receiver Model/DMRS Port

---

**2.4.11.2.14.0-2.0-4.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11169776`)

reTx UE Pairing
If virtual UE is constructed, the UEs in virtual UE should be allocated Subcell/Receiver Model/DMRS Port as below.

Subcell:

- For reTx UEs, refer to 5G\_L2\_8256\_replaced([11169699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26EJlPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- For initTx UEs, refer to 5G\_L2\_7233\_replaced([11169508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26CUbfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Receiver model: Refer to 5G\_L2\_7233\_replaced([11169508](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26CUbfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

DMRS : Refer to 5G\_L2\_6275\_replaced([11168724](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-qD_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9536\_replaced([11147413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksGZyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

#### 2.4.11.2.15 Ue Group Pairing

##### 2.4.11.2.15.0-1 Ue Group Pairing Overview (ID: 11169786)

**2.4.11.2.15.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11169804`)

Ue Group Pairing
If (actUlMuMimoGroupParing is true) And (NIDD:nrCellType is xDL8UL) And (2 subcells can be used for paring when multiple UL subcells with ulSubcellType C4 configured AND slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE according to 5G\_L2\_9652\_replaced([11167610](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256_qfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), ue group pairing shall be enabled.

[CB008235-C] if ulMaxMuMimoLayer(5G\_L2\_IF\_2296\_replaced([10968800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaH_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is larger than 4,ue group pairing shall be disabled. [End CB008235-C]

With ue group pairing enabled, if a root UE can't be paired with the UEs in rrmDedicateList(5G\_L2\_6257\_replaced([11167673](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_257myfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the root UE should try to pair with ue group.

Flow chart refer to 5G\_L2\_6226\_replaced([11167338](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255xhPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), pairing procedure refer to 5G\_L2\_7324\_replaced([11167362](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_255xh_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For ue group pairing, following rules should be obeyed.

- one root UE can be paired with only one ue group

- one ue group can be paired only  one root UE

- root Ue and ue group should be allocated to the different subcell

- all Ues(root Ue and ues in ue group) in the virtual UE should use SU receiver

- all Ues(root Ue and ues in ue group) should not be a MPR aware UE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_5038\_replaced([11201808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZq8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note: actUlMuMimoGroupParing is OAM parameter received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8267\_replaced([11145665](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksBhQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.11.2.15.0-2 Ue Group Pairing Candidate UEs for UE Group (ID: 11169808)

**2.4.11.2.15.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169811`)

Ue Group Pairing
Build Ue Group Candidate List

---

**2.4.11.2.15.0-2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11169816`)

Ue Group Pairing
The UEs in rrmUlMuCandidateList which are meet the following requirements should be added in rrmUeGroupCandidateList , these UEs will try to construct a ue group to pair with root Ue.

- The Ue's correlation with root Ue shall meet the correlation check (5G\_L2\_6258\_replaced([11167855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_258NyfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- Prb Requirement (5G\_L2\_7343\_replaced([11169820](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26FXsfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- DMRS Configuration Requirement (5G\_L2\_6267\_replaced([11167737](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_257m0fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10488\_replaced([11148176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2CvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**2.4.11.2.15.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11169820`)

Ue Group Pairing
Prb Requirement for Ue group Candicate

---

**2.4.11.2.15.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169841`)

Ue Group Pairing
[Before CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO]

The PRB Requirement of candidate UE to be added in rrmUeGroupCandidateList as following.

IF ( [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Nreq root". The text is in a stylized font with a gradient effect, appearing to be in shades of blue, orange, and yellow. The "N" is large and bold, while "req" is smaller and superscripted above it. "root" is positioned below the "N" and "req".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4145.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] < ulMinPrbThreshold )

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in absolute value bars. The expression is: $|N\_{root}^{req} - N\_{candidate}^{req}|$. The text is in a sans-serif font and appears to be in English. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4145.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] > ulMuMimoPrbThresholdAbsolute

ELSE

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a fraction. The numerator is the absolute value of the difference between two terms, $N\_{root}^{req}$ and $N\_{candidate}^{req}$. The denominator is $N\_{root}^{req}$. The expression can be written as:
$$ \frac{|N\_{root}^{req} - N\_{candidate}^{req}|}{N\_{root}^{req}} $$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4145.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]  > ulMuMimoPrbThresholdRatio

Where,

- [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Nreq root". The text is in a stylized font with a gradient effect, appearing to be in shades of blue, orange, and yellow. The "N" is large and bold, while "req" is smaller and superscripted above it. "root" is positioned below the "N" and "req".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4145.001.png[/image\_path][/image-3] : required PRB of root UE

- [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N req candidate". The text is in a dark color against a white background. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4145.004.png[/image\_path][/image-4] : required PRB of candidate UE which is in rrmUlMuCandidateList

- ulMinPrbThreshold : see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2296\_replaced([10968800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaH_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- ulMuMimoPrbThresholdAbsolute : see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2296\_replaced([10968800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaH_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- ulMuMimoPrbThresholdRatio : see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2296\_replaced([10968800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UZaH_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9739\_replaced([11148209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksJdEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO]

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9739\_replaced([11148209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksJdEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CNI-109641 #UL MU-MIMO]

---

##### 2.4.11.2.15.0-3 Ue Group Pairing Construct UE Groups (ID: 11169847)

**2.4.11.2.15.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11169861`)

Ue Group Pairing
Select the UEs from rrmUeGroupCandidateList to construct a UE group as following.

1.  Calculate priority for each UE in rrmUeGroupCandidateList.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
Priority$*{Ue(i)}$ = $\frac{N*{Ue(i)}^{req}}{Corr\_{i,root}}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4147.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Where,

- [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry mathematical expression. The expression appears to be $N\_{ue(i)}^{req}$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4147.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]: see note of 5G\_L2\_6239\_replaced([11167494](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YlvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Corr₁,root". The text is in a dark color against a light, slightly blurred background. The characters are slightly pixelated, but legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4147.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]: correlation between Ue(i) and root UE, see 5G\_L2\_6258\_replaced([11167855](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_258NyfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

1. Sort the Ues in rrmUeGroupCandidateList with [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Priorityue(t)". The text is in a light color against a white background, with some blurriness.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4147.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] descending order.
2. Pick the UEs from rrmUeGroupCandidateList sequentially to construct a UE group which meet 5G\_L2\_6268\_replaced([11167701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_257mzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9719\_replaced([11148145](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksI2BfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.11.2.15.0-4 Ue Group Pairing The Other Procedures (ID: 11169866)

**2.4.11.2.15.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11169870`)

Ue Group Pairing
Spectral Efficiency Check (MuSinr and prb compensation based): See 5G\_L2\_6259\_replaced([11168277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_259b7PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Update Virtual UE Scheduling Information: See 5G\_L2\_6279\_replaced([11168500](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-qBvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

PRB distribution for UEs in vUE: See 5G\_L2\_6282\_replaced([11168833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25_4J_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Start Prb: See 5G\_L2\_6465\_replaced([11168919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25_4LPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

DMRS Ports Allocation: See 5G\_L2\_6277\_replaced([11168728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25_REPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

eCpriSectionId: See 5G\_L2\_7076\_replaced([11179800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27IgmPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

#### 2.4.11.2.16 Very closed UEs pairing

##### 2.4.11.2.16.0-1 Very closed UEs pairing Overview (ID: 11169877)

**2.4.11.2.16.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11169882`)

Very closed UEs pairing
Before basic UE pairing, if number of  the MU eligible UEs is two(see 5G\_L2\_20385\_replaced([11167458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YkfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2PS shall try to pair them as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29228.

---

#### 2.4.11.2.17 Enhanced UE Pairing

##### 2.4.11.2.17.0-1 Enhanced UE Pairing Enhance Pairing On Constructed vUEs (ID: 11169888)

**2.4.11.2.17.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11169893`)

Enhanced UE Pairing
This requirement is the supplement of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29026\_replaced([11145918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksCIV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to elaborate the paring steps from entity level.

Step 1: Create two vUeGroup base on constructed vUEs(5G\_L2\_6223\_replaced([11167490](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YlfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

1. Create vUeGroup1, including vUEs which numOfMuMimoLayer(5G\_UP\_ALG\_29199\_replaced([11147973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksIO-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) > 4 and numOfMuMimoLayer < rrmMaxUlMuMimoLayer(5G\_L2\_6255\_replaced([11167631](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256_v_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) .
2. Create vUeGroup2, including vUEs which numOfMuMimoLayer < 4.

Step 2: Create beamGroups  and gapGroups based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29212\_replaced([11149214](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksMgevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 3: Add UEs from rrmUlMuCandidateList(5G\_L2\_6239\_replaced([11167494](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YlvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) into beamGroup.

1. The UE's twoAdditionalDmrsUl(5G\_L2\_IF\_401\_replaced([10975032](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_P9Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be true if numberOfUlDmrsAddPos is 1 or twoFlDmrsSupportUl(5G\_L2\_IF\_401\_replaced([10975032](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_P9Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be true if numberOfUlDmrsAddPos is 0.
2. The UEs shall be added into relevant beamGroup based on the UE's the 1st best horizontal beam .
3. Calculate numOfReqPrbPerBeamGroup of each beamGroup based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29226\_replaced([11149288](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksNHc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
4. Calculate rrmBeamGroupLayerStep based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29237\_replaced([11149309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksNHdfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Step 4: Select vUE.

1. Select vUE from vUeGroup1 first, then from vUeGroup2.
2. The order of vUEs within vUeGroup1 and vUeGroup2 is irrelevant, there is no need to sort them.

Step 5: Add beamGroup into vUE.

1. Select available beamGroups for current vUE based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29215\_replaced([11146059](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksCvZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. Add beamGroup into vUEs based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29101\_replaced([11145925](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksCIWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29109\_replaced([11145997](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksCvYvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3.To simplify implementation, the beamGroup may be excluded from the vUE if its inclusion would alter the vUE's WRR behavior (i.e., if WRR was not required before adding the beamGroup but becomes necessary afterward, or vice versa).

---

##### 2.4.11.2.17.0-2 Enhanced UE Pairing Beam Group Based Pairing (ID: 11169896)

**2.4.11.2.17.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169901`)

Enhanced UE Pairing
This requirement is the supplement of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29140\_replaced([11146093](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksCvZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to elaborate the paring steps from entity level.

Virtual UE concept is extended that multiple ue groups can be paired in single vUE, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8267\_replaced([11145665](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksBhQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 1: Available beamGroup

The beamGroup is created in step 2 of 5G\_L2\_20417\_replaced([11169888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26FXu_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and not added into vUEs will be used in this requirement.

Step 2: Construct beamGroup vUEs.

1. Construct vUEs as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29140\_replaced([11146093](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksCvZvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
2. There may more than one beamGroup vUEs.

---

##### 2.4.11.2.17.0-3 Enhanced UE Pairing Steps After vUEs are Constructed (ID: 11169905)

**2.4.11.2.17.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11169909`)

Enhanced UE Pairing
For the vUEs construced in 5G\_L2\_20417\_replaced([11169888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26FXu_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_20419\_replaced([11169896](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26FXvfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall take following steps.

Step 1: Update vUE information

update vUE ScheduleWeight as 5G\_L2\_6480\_replaced([11168656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-qC_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 2: Update UE information

1. Update Number of DMRS front-load of each UE in vUEs as 5G\_L2\_6480\_replaced([11168656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-qC_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. Set numOfDmrsCdmGroupWithoutData for all UEs in vUE as 2.
3. Allocate DMRS ports as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9535\_replaced([11147407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksGZx_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
4. Update number of beams to be used as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20045\_replaced([11148991](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksL5UfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step 3: Distribute Prb for vUEs

Refer to 5G\_L2\_6281\_replaced([11168825](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25_4JfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Prb distribution for UEs and vUEs.

Step 4: Distribute Prb for UEs in vUE

1. Distribute Prbs for normal UE or beamGroup in vUE in each rrmBeamGroupLayerStep as 5G\_L2\_6282\_replaced([11168833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25_4J_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. Distribute Prbs for UEs in beamGroup(s) in same rrmBeamGroupLayerStep as 5G\_L2\_6282\_replaced([11168833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25_4J_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30416\_replaced([11200918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwS9SfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
3. Allcate startPrb for UEs in a Virtual UE as 5G\_L2\_6465\_replaced([11168919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25_4LPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.11.2.18 PRB Estimation

##### 2.4.11.2.18.0-1 PRB Estimation Overview (ID: 11169918)

**2.4.11.2.18.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11169922`)

PRB Estimation
When multiple UL subcells with ulSubcellType C4 configured AND slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE AND 2 subcells can be used for MU, L2RT shall estimate PRBs for UEs in the lists(5G\_L2\_9767\_replaced([11169925](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-xPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when

- Each slot before pairing, or

- After a virtual UE with initial TX root UE completed

Procedure for PRB estimation:

Before virtual UE construction

1. Estimate PRBs as 5G\_L2\_9769\_replaced([11169944](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-yPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

2. Update lists and parameters as 5G\_L2\_9770\_replaced([11169958](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-yvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

After a virtual UE with initial TX root UE completed

1. Estimate PRBs for new vUE as 5G\_L2\_9769\_replaced([11169944](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-yPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

2. Update lists and parameters as 5G\_L2\_9770\_replaced([11169958](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-yvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3. Estimate PRBs for other UEs/vUEs as 5G\_L2\_9769\_replaced([11169944](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-yPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

4. Update lists and parameters as 5G\_L2\_9770\_replaced([11169958](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-yvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.11.2.18.0-2 PRB Estimation UE Lists for PRB Estimation (ID: 11169925)

**2.4.11.2.18.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11169930`)

PRB Estimation
UEs in the following lists should be estimated:

- rrmNonMuUeListForEst: initialized with non-MuEligble UEs which need more prbs(5G\_L2\_8250\_replaced([11169669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26DijfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and updated according to 5G\_L2\_9770\_replaced([11169958](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-yvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeListForEst: initialized with MuEligble initTx UEs(5G\_L2\_8250\_replaced([11169669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26DijfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- rrmvUeListForEst, which contain the virtual UE with initial TX root UE. Updated according to 5G\_L2\_8265\_replaced([11169759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Ewo_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_9770\_replaced([11169958](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-yvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- rrmRootOnlyUeListForEst, which contain the initial TX wich can’t be constructed. Updated according to 5G\_L2\_8265\_replaced([11169759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Ewo_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_9770\_replaced([11169958](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-yvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.11.2.18.0-3 PRB Estimation rrmSchedWeights (ID: 11169935)

**2.4.11.2.18.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11169940`)

PRB Estimation
When multiple UL subcells with ulSubcellType C4 configured AND slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE, total schedule weights(rrmSchedWeights) should be calculated when

- Each slot before pairing, or

- After a virtual UE with initial TX root UE completed

- After UE list updated(5G\_L2\_8265\_replaced([11169759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Ewo_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rrmSchedWeights = sum of schedulWeight of the UEs/vUEs in list below

- UEs in rrmNonMuUeListForEst

- UEs in rrmInitTxMuEligibleUeListForEst

- UEs in rrmRootOnlyUeListForEst

- vUEs in rrmvUeListForEst

Note:

- schedulWeight of UE is from NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight

- schedulWeight of vUE refer to 5G\_L2\_6480\_replaced([11168656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-qC_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9596

##### 2.4.11.2.18.0-4 PRB Estimation (ID: 11169944)

**2.4.11.2.18.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11169953`)

PRB Estimation
L2RT shall estimate PRBs according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9596.

- rrmAvailablePrbForMu: see 5G\_L2\_6357\_replaced([11167511](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YmfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- rrmSchedWeights: see 5G\_L2\_9768\_replaced([11169935](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26F-xvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "Nj estPrb". The text is blurry and appears to be in a light color against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4168.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]/[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text. The text appears to be "N est Pró" on the top line and "vUE(A)" on the bottom line.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4168.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]: defined as estNumPrbAllocationForUe

---

##### 2.4.11.2.18.0-5 PRB Estimation Update Lists and Parameters (ID: 11169958)

**2.4.11.2.18.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11169962`)

PRB Estimation
estNumPrbAllocationForUe for each UE/vUE in rrmNonMuUeListForEst / rrmRootOnlyUeListForEst / rrmvUeListForEst should be checked.

If estNumPrbAllocationForUe of UE/vUE ≥ required PRBs of UE/vUE

UE/vUE should be removed from the list

rmRequiredMuPrbNum = rmRequiredMuPrbNum - required PRB of UE/vUE

rrmRequiredMuSubcellPrbNum = rrmRequiredMuSubcellPrbNum – required subcell PRB of UE/vUE

rrmAvailablePrbForMu = rrmAvailablePrbForMu - required PRB of UE/vUE

rrmAvailableSubcelPrbForMu = rrmAvailableSubcelPrbForMu – required subcell PRB of UE/vUE

rrmSchedWeights = rrmSchedWeights – schedulWeight of UE/vUE

- required PRB of UE, refer to 5G\_L2\_6239\_replaced([11167494](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_256YlvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- required PRB of vUE, refer to 5G\_L2\_6478\_replaced([11168503](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-qB_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- required subcell PRB of UE = required PRB of UE

- required subcell PRB of vUE = alpha \* required PRB of vUE

alpha = 1, if vUE allocated in one subcell;

alpha = 2, if vUE allocated in two subcell;

- schedulWeight of UE is from NRBTS/NRDRB.schedulWeight

- schedulWeight of vUE, refer to 5G\_L2\_6480\_replaced([11168656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25-qC_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9573\_replaced([11147551](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksHA3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

##### 2.4.11.2.19.0-2 PMCounter Examples (ID: 11169992)

PMCounter
Some examples for how to update the counters.

Legend:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays two diagrams illustrating resource allocation, likely in a telecommunications context. Each diagram has a top row representing "prb index" with numbers 1 through 10 in green cells. Below this, there are rows indicating user equipment (UE) allocations.
The left diagram shows:
- "ue1 rank1" allocated across the entire range of PRB indices (1-10).
- "ue2 rank1" allocated across PRB indices 1-8, with the last two PRB indices (9 and 10) appearing unallocated or empty.
- Text to the right states "ue1 allocated 10 prb" and "ue2 allocated 8 prb".
The right diagram shows:
- "ue1 rank2" allocated across the entire range of PRB indices (1-10).
- Below "ue1 rank2", there is a row with three colored cells: "rank1" (yellow) in the first cell, "rank1" (yellow) in the second cell, and "rank1" (brown) in the third cell. These cells appear to correspond to PRB indices 1-3, 4-6, and 7-9 respectively, with PRB index 10 being unallocated or empty.
- Text to the right states "ue1 allocated 10 prb".
- Further text below states "3 ues in ue group, got 3,4,2 prbs respectively". This suggests that the three colored cells in the row below "ue1 rank2" represent the PRB allocations for three different UEs within a group, receiving 3, 4, and 2 PRBs respectively.
The diagrams use color coding to differentiate between PRB indices, UE allocations, and potentially different ranks or types of resource blocks.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4177.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Examples:

**Case 1:**
This case shows a grid of 10 numbered resource blocks (1-10) at the top. Below this, two rows are labeled "ue1 rank1" and "ue2 rank1". The "ue1 rank1" row spans all 10 resource blocks. The "ue2 rank1" row spans the first 8 resource blocks, with the last two blocks appearing empty.

Below this, a row is divided into colored segments: "rank1" (orange) spanning blocks 5-6, another "rank1" (orange) spanning blocks 7-8, and a final "rank1" (orange) spanning block 9. Block 10 is empty.
The metrics listed for Case 3 are:

**Case 4:**

Below this, a row is divided into colored segments: "rank1" (orange) spanning blocks 1-2, "rank1" (orange) spanning blocks 3-4, "rank2" (brown) spanning blocks 5-6, and an empty block 7. ## 2.4.12 Uplink Time Alignment Control

### 2.4.12.0-1 Uplink Time Alignment Control FA: QoS Document Owner: Santhosh Kumar Pulassery (Nokia) (ID: 11170033)

### 2.4.12.1 TA Timer Handling

#### 2.4.12.1.0-1 TA Timer Handling First TA Timer Start (ID: 11170042)

**2.4.12.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11170054`)

TA Timer Handling
Every time when 5G-L2-PS starts the TA timer for the first time after the UE has finished the RA procedure 5G-L2-PS shall set the runtime of the timer to

rdTacPeriodicity\_ms + ueSpecificTaTimerOffset\_ms

where rdTacPeriodicity\_ms is given in 5G\_L2\_1040\_replaced([11186652](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_277KxvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The offset ueSpecificTaTimerOffset\_ms depends on the UE's C-RNTI and ensures that the TA timers of multiple UEs expire in many different slots. This helps to reduce load peaks which occur when the TA timers of many UEs expire simultaneously. 5G-L2-PS shall determine the value of ueSpecificTaTimerOffset\_ms as follows.

[Before PR778137]

ueSpecificTaTimerOffset\_ms = (crnti mod 40) / 2^u

[PR778137]

ueSpecificTaTimerOffset\_ms = (crnti mod 41) / 2^u

[End PR778137]

where 2^u = scs / 15.

The crnti is received in the PsUser\_UserSetupReq (5G\_L2\_IF\_390\_replaced([10974651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The subcarrier spacing (scs) is received in the PsCell\_CellGroupSetupReq (5G\_L2\_IF\_3117\_replaced([10966509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UPCB_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: This requirement version has been created to backport the correction for PR627778 to the previous release. It has been created retroactively after the requirement version for PR627778.

---

#### 2.4.12.1.0-2 TA Timer Handling TA Timer Restart (ID: 11170059)

**2.4.12.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11170068`)

TA Timer Handling
After the TA timer has expired for the first time after the RA procedure (see 5G\_L2\_5034\_replaced([11170042](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Gl2_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) 5G-L2-PS shall carry out all subsequent restarts as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10195\_replaced([11150039](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksRY7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), i.e. with a runtime of rdTacPeriodicity\_ms (see 5G\_L2\_1040\_replaced([11186652](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_277KxvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[Before PR627778] Note: This requirement version has been created to backport the correction for PR627778 to the previous release. It has been created retroactively after the requirement version for PR627778. [End PR627778]

---

#### 2.4.12.1.0-3 TA Timer Handling TA transient phase handling for FR1 UL CA (ID: 11170074)

**2.4.12.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11170080`)

TA Timer Handling
If the skipping condition is matched as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3319\_replaced([11150376](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksTOFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the TAC sending will be blocked temporarily, and TA timer will be postponed and expire every 10 milliseconds to recheck the skipping condition until it’s not matched. If the TAC is no longer needs to be skipped, TAC command is computed and scheduled as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10195\_replaced([11150039](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksRY7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and the TA timer is restarted with rdTacPeriodicity\_ms expire time.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication process between a PCell and a UE, with a focus on a "SCell transient period". The diagram uses two horizontal lines to represent the PCell and the UE, with time progressing from left to right. Arrows indicate the flow of information or events.
The PCell line is annotated with several time-related labels: "rdTacPeriodicity\_ms" appears twice, indicating a periodic interval. Between these, there's a section labeled "SCell transient period". Within this transient period, there are multiple smaller intervals labeled "recheck", with curved arrows suggesting a feedback or re-evaluation loop. The "SCell transient period" spans across several "recheck" intervals and ends before the second "rdTacPeriodicity\_ms" label.
There are four diagonal arrows labeled "TAC", originating from the PCell line and pointing to the UE line. These arrows represent the transmission of "TAC" information. Two of these arrows occur before the "SCell transient period", and two occur after it, with one of the latter falling within the "SCell transient period" and the other after it. Dashed vertical lines are also present, marking specific points in time, possibly related to the start or end of certain phases or events.
The text in the image is:
SCell transient period
rdTacPeriodicity\_ms rdTacPeriodicity\_ms
recheck recheck
recheck rdTacPeriodicity\_ms
PCELL
UE
TAC
TAC
TAC
TAC[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4193.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note:

TAC retransmission is possible to occur during TAC skipping period. This corner case can be recovered by on demand TA after the end of the TAC skipping period.

---

### 2.4.12.2 PM Counter Support

#### 2.4.12.2.0-1 PM Counter Support TA Statistics PM Counter Histogram support (ID: 11170092)

**2.4.12.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13477751`)

PM Counter Support
[Before CB015279-F]

If a Timing Command (TAC) was sent (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3319\_replaced([11150376](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksTOFPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and acknowledged the PM Counters (described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6272\_replaced([11230269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kytYEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) related to Timing alignment Control shall be updated.

Note: PM Counter M55307C02001…M55307C02032, aka TA\_RRC\_CON\_BIN\_01 … TA\_RRC\_CON\_BIN\_32, describe the distribution of the absolute Timing Advance values sent to the RRC connected UE inside a subcell where the UE is attached to. The distribution is represented by a 32 bin-histogram.  
The counter(s) are defined per subcell and every UE receiving TAC commands in this subcell is contributing to these counters. PM Counters M55307C02001…M55307C02032 shall not be used to capture initialTa values sent in RA Msg2. initialTa values shall be captured by other PM counters (M55307C01001…M55307C01032).

When a Timing Command (TAC) was acknowledged by a UE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10195\_replaced([11150039](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksRY7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) first the relative Timing Offset (TO) is re-calculated by re-using the formula from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3317\_replaced([11150271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksSnDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

TO = TAC - 31

A TAC value shall be considered according to 5G\_L2\_IF\_3458\_replaced([10984546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2WBKufu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as 'taMacCeValue' from LoCtrl\_PduMuxReq message with a range of 0 ... 63, in units of 16/2^u\*Ts.  
The TO value has a range of -31 ... 0 ... 32 in units of 16/2^u Ts, while u depends on sub carrier spacing.

The first TA Counter Sample shall be set to the absolute initialTa value of the UE provided in RA Msg 2 (described in 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_233, with same unit 16/2^2\* Ts) plus the first TO value after the RA procedure computed above:

TA Counter Sample (new) = initialTa + TO.

Every succeeding TA Counter Sample shall be computed as the previous TA Counter Sample plus the TO value computed above:

TA Counter Sample (new) = TA Counter Sample (previous) + TO.

I.e. the TA Counter Sample value is the accumulation of the absolute initialTa plus all subsequent relative TO values.

The TA Counter sample value shall determine the correct one of the TA\_RRC\_CON\_BIN\_01 .. 32 bins.  
The value of the chosen TA\_RRC\_CONN\_BIN\_XX PM Counter bin is incremented by +1.

[CB015279-F]

This requirement describes alogrithm for choosing correct bin for Timing Advance related counters (specific counter in TA\_RACH\_RESP\_BIN\_01 .. TA\_RACH\_RESP\_BIN\_32 or TA\_RRC\_CON\_BIN\_01 .. TA\_RRC\_CON\_BIN\_32, denoted further as CNT\_BIN\_XX which references counters in any of those 2 sets).

[End CB015279-F]

There are two methods of determining which [Before CB015279-F]TA\_RRC\_CONN\_BIN\_XX [CB015279-F] CNT\_BIN\_XX [End CB015279-F] should be incremented.  
The method is chosen based on the value of NRBTS/NRPMRNL/NRPMCCP-cfgTAcounters parameter (taCountersConfig.cfgTAcounters in PsCell\_CellSetupReq (ref. 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq (ref. 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988\_replaced([10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))).

If cfgTAcounters = 0 (Bin ranges based on SCS) then:

Bins have constant width of 8 TA indexes (except last bin).  
Linear ranges for the [Before CB015279-F]TA\_RRC\_CONN\_BIN [CB015279-F] CNT\_BIN [End CB015279-F] bins:  
0 <= [Before CB015279-F]TA\_RRC\_CONN\_BIN\_01 [CB015279-F] CNT\_BIN\_01 [End CB015279-F] < 8  
8 <= [Before CB015279-F]TA\_RRC\_CONN\_BIN\_02 [CB015279-F] CNT\_BIN\_02 [End CB015279-F] < 16  
...  
240 <= [Before CB015279-F]TA\_RRC\_CONN\_BIN\_31 [CB015279-F] CNT\_BIN\_31 [End CB015279-F] < 248  
248 <= [Before CB015279-F]TA\_RRC\_CONN\_BIN\_32 [CB015279-F] CNT\_BIN\_32 [End CB015279-F] < infinite

Else if cfgTAcounters = 1 (Bin ranges based on expected cell size) then:

[Before CB015279-F]TA\_RRC\_CONN\_BIN\_XX [CB015279-F] CNT\_BIN\_XX [End CB015279-F] to increment is determined based on the value of NRBTS/NRCELL-expectedCellSize parameter (taCountersConfig.expectedCellSize in PsCell\_CellSetupReq (ref. 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq (ref. 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988\_replaced([10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))).

Firstly, based on TA index (taIndex) and SCS, distance traveled by signal between gNB and UE (dist) must be calculated:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$dist = \frac{talndex \* baseTaDist}{2^u} [m]$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4197.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where:  
baseTaDist = 78,0709526 [m]  
u depends on SCS:  
SCS = 15kHZ, u = 0  
SCS = 30kHZ, u = 1  
SCS = 60kHZ, u = 2  
SCS = 120kHZ, u = 3  
SCS = 240kHZ, u = 4

Secondly, range of single bin for given expected cell size (baseDistEcs) must be calculated:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a serif font. The equation is:
`baseDistEcs = baseTaDist * 2^x * Π_{i=1}^{n} w_i [m]`
The equation defines a variable `baseDistEcs` in terms of `baseTaDist`, a power of 2, and a product of terms `w_i` indexed from `i=1` to `n`, with an additional factor `[m]`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4197.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

where:  
baseTaDist = 78,0709526 [m]  
x, w, n depend on expected cell size (ECS):  
ECS = 2.1km, x = 0, w = [1]  
ECS = 5km, x = 1, w = [1]  
ECS = 10km, x = 2, w = [1]  
ECS = 15km, x = 3,  w = [1, 3/4]  
ECS = 30km, x = 4, w = [1, 3/4]  
ECS = 60km, x = 5, w = [1, 3/4]  
ECS = 100km, x = 6, w = [1, 3/4, 5/6]  
n = number of elements in w

Thirdly, ratio of dist to baseDistEcs (ratio) must be calculated:

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation displayed horizontally. The equation shows the derivation of a "ratio" through a series of equalities.
The equation is as follows:
ratio = $\frac{dist}{baseDistEcs}$ = $\frac{talndex \* baseTaDist}{2^u}$ = $\frac{talndex}{baseTaDist \* 2^x \* \prod\_{i=1}^{n} w\_i}$ = $\frac{talndex}{2^u \* 2^x \* \prod\_{i=1}^{n} w\_i}$
The equation involves variables such as "dist", "baseDistEcs", "talndex", "baseTaDist", "u", "x", "n", and "w\_i". The symbol $\prod$ represents a product.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4197.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Calculated ratio may not be integral number and/or may exceed number of bins.

Finally, number of [Before CB015279-F]TA\_RRC\_CONN\_BIN\_XX [CB015279-F] CNT\_BIN\_XX [End CB015279-F] (binNum) to be incremented may be determined:

[image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a light-colored font against a white background. The equation is:
`binNum = min(numberOfBins, floor(ratio) + 1)`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4197.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

where:  
numberOfBins = 32

End if

Note:

1) [38.213 ch 4.2 (R1-1805795)] Range of TA index signalled to UE in RA response is 0, 1, 2, ..., 3846. However cell range is limited by PRACH formats.

2) "TA index" refers to the TA command value sent in RAR. How to calculate the value from the L1 measurement is provided in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3417\_replaced([11152587](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kshQgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

3) TA Bins tables presenting distance/bin distribution may be found in the A&S CB008204 documents:  
<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/ASCOP/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B866DBF8D-C6ED-4FEE-85C0-D3A793B0D766%7D&file=TA%20Bins%20CB008204.xlsx>
