## 2.4.13 Carrier Frequency Offset Handling

### 2.4.13.0-2 Carrier Frequency Offset Handling CFO metric calculation (ID: 11170126)

**2.4.13.0-2.0-6**  (ID: `12706221`)

Carrier Frequency Offset Handling
CFO metric handling is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3322\_replaced([11150515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksT1LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If beamforming is enabled (actBeamforming = TRUE [CB013442-D] for TDD or actBeamformingFdd = TRUE[End CB013442-D]) then in requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3324\_replaced([11150688](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVDQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) index *k is a beam index – for* *C4 or C8 or C4\_2 or C8\_2 or C8\_4 [CB013442-D]or A8[End CB013442-D]UL cell serving beams have consecutive indexes starting from 0, for other UL cell types single beam is used and* k has value 0. For FR1 TDD cell with beamforming enabled 5G-L2-PS instance shall maintain separate CFO metrics for each beam (identified by unique beam attribute), not beam index, so the instance can properly map each beam to its CFO metric in beam change scenarios and online modification of beamset NIDD parameters in [12582669: Handling online modification of beamset parameters](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0nFmoTSEEfCKaNlcHOQWmg) specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9157\_replaced([11150941](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksWRZ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

[CB008577-A]

If actBeamforming = TRUE for FDD, A8 UL Cell handle CFO as per [12786877: CFO metric - FDD Beamforming handling](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_O4hO0pIWEfCoxNjZXyLbRA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[End CB008577-A]

If, for given UE, 5G-L2-PS uses SRS beams for PUSCH then:

The unique beam attribute is pattern ID.

If, for given UE, 5G-L2-PS uses SSB/Refined beams for PUSCH then:

The unique beam attribute is beam ID (5G\_UP\_ALG\_11982\_replaced([11111700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpP6FfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

### 2.4.13.0-3 Carrier Frequency Offset Handling CFO metric calculation for UL MU-MIMO (ID: 11170144)

**2.4.13.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13356812`)

Carrier Frequency Offset Handling
Instantaneous CFO calculation for UL MU-MIMO, refer to 5G\_L2\_6916\_replaced([11170152](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26Hz8PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before PR868318]

Long term CFO metric for PUSCH for UL MU-MIMO, refer to 5G\_L2\_14382\_replaced([11179614](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27HSdPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[PR868318]

For Long term CFO metric for PUSCH for UL MU-MIMO, refer to [11151086: LT CFO metric for MU-MIMO UEs](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksW4evvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

For LT CFO validity for PUSCH/PUCCH, refer to [11151157: LT CFO validity for PUSCH/PUCCH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

For setting of the IE longTermCfoMetric and longTermCfoMetricOfBeam, refer to 5G\_L2\_14382\_replaced([11179614](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27HSdPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End PR868318]

---

### 2.4.13.0-4 Carrier Frequency Offset Handling Instantaneous CFO calculation for UL MU-MIMO (ID: 11170152)

**2.4.13.0-4.0-6**  (ID: `11170180`)

Carrier Frequency Offset Handling
L2RT shall calculate rrmInstCfo based on UE and beams all the time.

For UE based CFO

rrmInstCfo[i,j] = shortTermCfoMetric (measured from PUSCH according to 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) - 'PuschMeasurementsResp' section, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6789\_replaced([11108106](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_konA4fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [CB007491-H]or PUCCH F3 according to 5G\_L1\_IF\_15485\_replaced([11514178](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR2BQfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) - 'PucchRespPsMeasL1sw' section, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8000\_replaced([11108191](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kooPAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB007491-H]).

For beam based CFO

- If UE i is scheduled in MU-IRC receiver, rrmInstCfo shall be caculated based on real used beams(5G\_UP\_ALG\_20045\_replaced([11148991](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksL5UfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20722\_replaced([11150880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVqb_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

rrmInstCfoOfAnt[i,j,k] = shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt[antIndex1] + shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt[antIndex2]

o j is for component carrier.

o k is the unique beam attribute of real used beams(5G\_UP\_ALG\_20045\_replaced([11148991](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksL5UfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of UE i

o antIndex1/ antIndex2 are the ant index used by UE i, which is related to beam k.

o shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt is measured from PUSCH according to 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) - 'PuschAntMeas' section.

[CB007491-H]Note: PUCCH is never scheduled in MU-IRC receiver, therefore this case covers only shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt sourced from PUSCH.[End CB007491-H]

- If UE i is scheduled in SU-IRC receiver, L2RT shall store CFO values of based on beams selected as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2135\_replaced([11154028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksqacvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

rrmInstCfoOfAnt[i,j,k] = shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt[antIndex1] + shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt[antIndex2]

o j is for component carrier.

o k is the unique beam attribute of beams selected as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2135\_replaced([11154028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksqacvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) of UE i.

o antIndex1/ antIndex2 are the ant index used by UE i, which is related to beam k.

o shortTermCfoMetricOfAnt is measured from PUSCH according to 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) - 'PuschAntMeas' section [CB007491-H]or PUCCH F3 according to 5G\_L1\_IF\_15485\_replaced([11514178](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR2BQfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB007491-H].

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9160\_replaced([11150769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVDS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note: 1)rrmInstCfo[i,j] here which is used in software for UE based rrmInstCfo corresponds to rrmInstCfo[i,j,0] in CP2. 2) rrmInstCfoOfAnt[i,j,k](k=0,1,2,3) here correspond to rrmInstCfo[i,j,k](k=1,2,3,4) in CP2.

---

### 2.4.13.0-5 Carrier Frequency Offset Handling CFO metric synchronization (ID: 11170186)

**2.4.13.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11170194`)

Carrier Frequency Offset Handling
If long PUCCH is enabled, CFO metric is used by UL and DL schedulers for sending PUSCH and PUCCH reception requests to L1 respectively. Moreover if rrmCfoAverageMode is set to Both CFO metric calculation is distributed among UL and DL schedulers. Required synchronization of CFO metric between schedulers shall be carried by PsInternal\_UlToDlIntraSchedUpdate and PsInternal\_DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate messages (5G\_L2\_5674\_replaced([11242413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3BqmbfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) using puschReceiveRespPsLight.longTermCfoMetricOfBeam and pucchReceiveRespPsLight.longTermCfoMetricOfBeam IEs respectively.

[Before CB007103-B]To ensure proper synchronization, CFO metric calculation should be performed as early as possible i.e. right after receiving NON\_DTX PUSCH or NON\_DTX PUCCH F3 response and before sending mentioned synchronization messages so both schedulers can work on the most recent calculated CFO metric. This is illustrated in 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_3707\_replaced([11200685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgNwUPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB007103-B]To ensure proper synchronization, CFO metric calculation should be performed as early as possible i.e. right after receiving:

- NON\_DTX PUSCH response or

- NON\_DTX PUCCH F3 reponse out of transient phase with PUCCH DMRS reconfiguration

and before sending mentioned synchronization messages so both schedulers can work on the most recent calculated CFO metric. This is illustrated in 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_3707\_replaced([11200685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgNwUPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB007103-B]

---

### 2.4.13.0-6 Carrier Frequency Offset Handling Relation between longTermCfoMetricOfAnt and longTermCfoMetricOfBeam (ID: 11170198)

**2.4.13.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `11170212`)

Carrier Frequency Offset Handling
Depending on Pusch receiver type (see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) longTermCfoMetricOfAnt defined in the SFS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3322\_replaced([11150515](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksT1LvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) corresponds to longTermCfoMetricOfBeam defined on the L1-L2 interface (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L1\_IF\_216\_replaced([11513990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MIfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in the following manner:

[CB010708-D]

If Pusch is 16Rx (numOfRxPusch=16, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[0] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[0]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[1] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[2]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[2] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[4]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[3] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[6]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[4] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[8]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[5] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[10]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[6] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[12]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[7] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[14]

Else [End CB010708-D] If  Pusch/Pucch is 8Rx (numOfRxPusch/numOfRxPucch=8, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[0] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[0]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[1] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[2]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[2] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[4]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[3] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[6]

Else If Pusch/Pucch is 4Rx (numOfRxPusch/numOfRxPucch=4, see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[0] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[0]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[1] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[1]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[2] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[2]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[3] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[3]

Note:  Special case subcell type is C8 and numberOfRxPucch is 4.

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[0] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[0]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[1] = longTermCfoMetricOfAnt[2]

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[2] = 0

longTermCfoMetricOfBeam[3] = 0

---

### 2.4.13.0-7 Carrier Frequency Offset Handling CFO extension for 8RX (ID: 11170217)

**2.4.13.0-7.0-4**  (ID: `11170240`)

Carrier Frequency Offset Handling
With pucchF3BeamformingEnabled is true, the maintenance of CFO for PUSCH is 8Rx, PUCCH is 4Rx or 8Rx *,* **[Before CB010708-D]or PUSCH is 16Rx**[End CB010708-D] (see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is as follows.**\***\*

PUSCH 8RX**[Before CB010708-D] or PUSCH 16Rx** *[End CB010708-D]  (5G\_L2\_12903\_replaced([11155753](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466HvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):*\*\*\*\*

If PUSCH use osDFT beams, CFO shall be maintained per osDFT beam. It is same as 5GC001086 and extend two patterns to four patterns.

If PUSCH use SSB, CFO shall be maintained per SSB beam. With two SSB valid case, CFO is maintained as [a1,a2, b1, b2]; With one SSB valid **case, CFO is maintained as [A, A1, half1, half2]** or [a-1 + a-2 + a-3 + a-4] according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26331\_replaced([11113585](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpcHU_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

If PUSCH use refine beam, CFO shall be maintained per refine beam. With two refine beam valid case, CFO is maintained as [a, ax, b, bx] or [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4]; With one refine beam valid case, CFO is maintained as [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4].

If beam switch, keep the same behavior of current CFO usage (eg: the old best beam CFO is used before new beam CFO is reported).

PUCCH 8RX (5G\_L2\_12903\_replaced([11155753](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466HvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

If PUSCH use osDFT beams, the CFO from the strongest beam is applied, it is same as 5GC001086.

If PUSCH use SSB **or refine beam, follow current PUCCH 4RX logic and extend 2 beam to 4 beam. See the following table for summary:**

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PUSCH beam | os-DFT valid | 2 SSB valid | 2 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid | 2 refine beams belong to different SSBs valid-different correlated beams can be found | 2 refine beams belong to different SSBs valid-different correlated beams can't be found | 2 refine beams belong to same SSBs valid-different correlated beams can be found | 2 refine beams belong to same SSBs valid-different correlated beams can't be found | 1 refine beam |
| PUCCH beam | / | 2 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid or regions limited by RRU | 1 SSB valid | 2 SSB valid | 2 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid |
| PUSCH CFO | [osDft1, osDft2, osDft3, osDft4] | [a1, a2, b1, b2] | [a1, a2, b1, b2] | [A, A1, half1, half2] **or [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4]** | [a, ax, b, bx] | [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4] | [a, ax, b, bx] | [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4] | [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4] |
| PUCCH CFO(F1) | [osDft1, osDft1, osDft1, osDft1] | [a1, a1, a1, a1] | [a1, a1, a1, a1] | [A, A, A, A] **or [a-1, a-1, a-1, a-1]** | [a, a, a, a] | [a-1, a-1, a-1, a-1] | [a, a, a, a] | [a, a, a, a] | [a-1, a-1, a-1, a-1] |
| PUCCH CFO(F3) | [osDft1, osDft1, osDft1, osDft1] | [a1, a2, b1, b2] or [b1, b2, a1, a2] | [a1, a1, a1, a1] | [A, A, A, A] **or [a-1, a-1, a-1, a-1]** | [a, ax, b, bx] or [b, bx, a, ax] | [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4] or [a-3, a-4, a-1, a-2] | [linear average(a, b), linear average(a, b), linear average(a, b), linear average(a, b)] | [linear average(a-1, a-3), linear average(a-1, a-3), linear average(a-1, a-3), linear average(a-1, a-3)] | [a-1, a-1, a-1, a-1] |

PUCCH 4RX (5G\_L2\_12903\_replaced([11155753](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466HvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

If PUSCH use osDFT beams, the CFO from the strongest beam is applied, it is same as 5GC001086.

If PUSCH use SSB **or refine beam, follow current PUCCH 4RX logic, only CFO of beam A** or a **and beam B** or b **from PUSCH are used.*****\****\*

See the following table for summary:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PUSCH beam | os-DFT valid | 2 SSB valid | 2 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid | 2 refine beams belong to different SSBs valid-different correlated beams can be found | 2 refine beams belong to different SSBs valid-different correlated beams can't be found | 2 refine beams belong to same SSBs valid-different correlated beams can be found | 2 refine beams belong to same SSBs valid-different correlated beams can't be found | 1 refine beam |
| PUCCH beam | / | 2 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid or regions limited by RRU | 1 SSB valid | 2 SSB valid | 2 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid |
| PUSCH CFO | [osDft1, osDft2, osDft3, osDft4] | [a1, a2, b1, b2] | [a1, a2, b1, b2] | [A, A1, half1, half2] **or [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4]** | [a, ax, b, bx] | [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4] | [a, ax, b, bx] | [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4] | [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4] |
| PUCCH CFO(F1) | [osDft1, osDft1] | [a1, a1] | [a1, a1] | [A, A]or [a-1, a-1] | [a, a] | [a-1, a-1] | [a, a] | [a, a] | [a-1, a-1] |
| PUCCH CFO(F3) | [osDft1, osDft1] | [a1, b1] | [a1, a1] | [A, A]or [a-1, a-1] | [a ,b] or [b, a] | [a-1, a-3] or [a-3, a-1] | [linear average(a, b), linear average(a, b)] | [linear average(a-1, a-3), linear average(a-1, a-3)] | [a-1, a-1] |

Note:

(1) Suppose beam A and beam B are UE reported SSB, A1 is high correlation of A and B1 is high correlation of B. a1/a2 are high correlation refine beam of A and b1/b2 are high correlation refine beam of B. Half1/half2 is half sector beam.

(2) Suppose refine beam a and refine beam b are UE reported refine beams, ax is first/second high correlation of a and bx is first/second high correlation of b.

(3) Suppose only one refine beam a reported by UE or can’t find different high correlated beams when two refine beams valid, a-1/a-2/a-3/a-4 are four refine beams of the related SSB of a. Refer to 5G\_L2\_9602\_replaced([11155694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466E_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for detail.

(4) The above rule is valid when rdBeamSpecificCfoEnabled is true. If rdBeamSpecificCfoEnabled is false, only first UL beam is used for CFO as legacy.

(5) Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21277\_replaced([11151260](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for detail PUCCH CFO usage and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21503\_replaced([11151025](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksW4cvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PUSCH CFO usage.

---

### 2.4.13.0-8 Carrier Frequency Offset Handling CFO extension for 8RX FDD (ID: 12706230)

**2.4.13.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `12706225`)

Carrier Frequency Offset Handling
If actBeamformingFdd (see [10967202: PsCell\_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), PUSCH is 8Rx, PUCCH is 8Rx for both format 1 and format 3, according to [12704269: Beam ID for UL](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_AKvQAWB3EfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [11102694: Mapping of antenna wise ST/LT CFO to spatial streams for SU receiver](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knltPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), the maintenance of CFO is as bellow:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| channel | beam | short term CFO as source of long term CFO | long term CFO to L1 | reference |
| PUSCH | panel split beam: [beam1, beam2, beam3, beam4] | process and maintain CFO for each beam [cfo\_beam1, cfo\_beam2, cfo\_beam3, cfo\_beam4] | apply long term CFO on each beam [cfo\_beam1, cfo\_beam2, cfo\_beam3, cfo\_beam4] | [11150775: Calculate Instantaneous PUSCH CFO from L1 measurement](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVDTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), [11151025: LT CFO metric for PUSCH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksW4cvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) |
| PUCCH F1 | / | / | apply long term CFO on each beam [cfo\_beam1, cfo\_beam2, cfo\_beam3, cfo\_beam4] | [11151297: LT CFO metric for Long PUCCH Format1, with F3 BF, without ORAN F1F3 BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGr_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and pucchF3BeamformingEnabled = TRUE as [10967202: PsCell\_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) |
| PUCCH F3 | panel split beam: [beam1, beam2, beam3, beam4] | process and maintain CFO for each beam [cfo\_beam1, cfo\_beam2, cfo\_beam3, cfo\_beam4] | apply long term CFO on each beam [cfo\_beam1, cfo\_beam2, cfo\_beam3, cfo\_beam4] | [11150688: CFO metric - Exponential average](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVDQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), [11150823: Calculate Instantaneous PUCCH F3 CFO from L1 measurement](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVqVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), [11151260: LT CFO metric for Long PUCCH Format3, with F3 BF, without ORAN F1F3 BF](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) |

[CB008577-A]If FDD Cell with actBeamforming = TRUE, PUSCH is 8Rx, PUCCH is 8Rx for both format 1 and format 3, according to [12823081: Beam usage for UL](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_GGTJ4KpBEfCrANRYxAlR5A?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), [11102694: Mapping of antenna wise ST/LT CFO to spatial streams for SU receiver](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knltPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), and  [11150556: CFO metric - Average mode](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksUcNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), the maintenance of CFO is as bellow:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| channel | beam | short term CFO as source of long term CFO | long term CFO to L1 | reference |
| PUSCH | Sector beam beamset4 if actPrachProbing = FALSE: [Sectorbeam1, Sectorbeam2, Sectorbeam3, Sectorbeam4] or split beam of beamset4 if actPrachProbing = TRUE: [Refinebeam1, Refinebeam2, Refinebeam3, Refinebeam4] [CB008577-C] or split beam of UL PMI beam based on valid CSI: [Splitbeam1, Splitbeam2, Splitbeam3, Splitbeam4] [End CB008577-C] | process and maintain CFO for each beam [cfo\_Xbeam1, cfo\_Xbeam2, cfo\_Xbeam3, cfo\_Xbeam4], X means "Sector" or "Refine" [CB008577-C] or "SplitBeam" [End CB008577-C]in beam description if rrmCfoAverageMode != "PUCCH\_Only" | apply long term CFO on each beam  [cfo\_Xbeam1, cfo\_Xbeam2, cfo\_Xbeam3, cfo\_Xbeam4] if rrmCfoAverageMode != "PUCCH\_Only",  else strongest long term CFO from PUCCH | [12786877: CFO metric - FDD Beamforming handling](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_O4hO0pIWEfCoxNjZXyLbRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) |
| PUCCH F1 | Sector beam beamset4: [Sectorbeam1, Sectorbeam2, Sectorbeam3, Sectorbeam4] | / | apply long term CFO on each beam [cfo\_Sectorbeam1, cfo\_Sectorbeam2, cfo\_Sectorbeam3, cfo\_Sectorbeam4] from PUSCH if rrmCfoAverageMode != "PUSCH\_Only",  else apply long term CFO from PUSCH [cfo\_Xbeam1, cfo\_Xbeam2, cfo\_Xbeam3, cfo\_Xbeam4] | pucchF3BeamformingEnabled = FALSE [12787612: LT CFO metric for Long PUCCH BF FDD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_4fDXkZLHEfCoxNjZXyLbRA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) |
| PUCCH F3 | Sector beam beamset4: [Sectorbeam1, Sectorbeam2, Sectorbeam3, Sectorbeam4] | If rrmCfoAverageMode != "PUSCH\_Only": process and maintain CFO for each beam [cfo\_Sectorbeam1, cfo\_Sectorbeam2, cfo\_Sectorbeam3, cfo\_Sectorbeam4] | apply long term CFO on each beam [cfo\_Sectorbeam1, cfo\_Sectorbeam2, cfo\_Sectorbeam3, cfo\_Sectorbeam4]  from PUCCH if If rrmCfoAverageMode != "PUSCH\_Only",  else apply long term CFO from PUSCH [cfo\_Xbeam1, cfo\_Xbeam2, cfo\_Xbeam3, cfo\_Xbeam4] | pucchF3BeamformingEnabled = FALSE [12787612: LT CFO metric for Long PUCCH BF FDD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_4fDXkZLHEfCoxNjZXyLbRA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) |

For rrmCfoAverageMode mentioned above, refer to [11150556: CFO metric - Average mode](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksUcNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[End CB008577-A]

---

### 2.4.13.0-9 Carrier Frequency Offset Handling CFO extension for 16RX (ID: 11170247)

**2.4.13.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11170255`)

Carrier Frequency Offset Handling
With pucchF3BeamformingEnabled is true, The maintenance of CFO for PUSCH is 16Rx, PUCCH is 8Rx or 4Rx *(see 5G\_L2\_16925\_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), is as follows.*

PUSCH is 16Rx (5G\_L2\_12903\_replaced([11155753](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466HvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

If PUSCH use osDFT beams,

**[CB010708-G] if rdUlSplitPanelModeFor16RX (5G\_UP\_ALG\_29390\_replaced([11114144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpiN8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  is set to** 0(none), **[End CB010708-G]CFO shall be maintained per osDFT beam. It is same as 5GC001086 and extend two patterns to eight patterns.**\*\*\*\*

**[CB010708-G]**

If rdUlSplitPanelModeFor16RX (5G\_UP\_ALG\_29390\_replaced([11114144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpiN8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  is set to 1( hybrid split mode), only the 1st best beam and 2nd best beam shall be maintained, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20493\_replaced([11150775](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVDTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If rdUlSplitPanelModeFor16RX (5G\_UP\_ALG\_29390\_replaced([11114144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpiN8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  is set to 2(UD split mode), the 1st to 4th best beam  shall be maintained, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20493\_replaced([11150775](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksVDTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[End CB010708-G]**

If PUSCH use SSB, CFO shall be maintained per SSB beam. With two SSB valid case, CFO is maintained as [a1,a2,a3,a4, b1, b2,b3,b4]; With one SSB valid **case, CFO is maintained as [A, A1, half1, half2]** or [a-1 + a-2 + a-3 + a-4] according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26331\_replaced([11113585](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpcHU_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).\*\*\*\*

If beam switch, keep the same behavior of current CFO usage (eg: the old best beam CFO is used before new beam CFO is reported).

PUCCH 8RX (5G\_L2\_12903\_replaced([11155753](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466HvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

If PUSCH use osDFT beams, the CFO from the strongest beam is applied, it is same as 5GC001086.

If PUSCH use SSB beams, follow current PUCCH 4RX logic and extend 2 beam to 4 beam. See the following table for summary:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PUSCH beam | os-DFT valid | 2 SSB valid | 2 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid |
| PUCCH beam | / | 2 SSB valid | 1 SSB valid or regions limited by RRU | 1 SSB valid |
| PUSCH CFO | [osDft1, osDft2, osDft3, osDft4，osDft5, osDft6, osDft7, osDft8] | [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4,  b-1, b-2, b-3, b-4] | [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4,  b-1, b-2, b-3, b-4] | [A, A1, half1, half2] **or [a-1, a-2, a-3, a-4]** |
| PUCCH CFO(F1) | [osDft1, osDft1, osDft1, osDft1] | [a-1, a-1, a-1, a-1] | [a-1, a-1, a-1, a-1] | [A, A, A, A] **or [a-1, a-1, a-1, a-1]** |
| PUCCH CFO(F3) | [osDft1, osDft1, osDft1, osDft1] | [a-1, a-2, b-1, b-2] or [b-1, b-2, a-1, a-2] | [a-1, a-1, a-1, a-1] | [A, A, A, A] **or [a-1, a-1, a-1, a-1]** |

Note:

(1) Suppose beam A and beam B are UE reported SSB, A1 is high correlation of A.

(2) a-1/a-2/a-3/a-4 are four refine beams of the related SSB beam A, b-1/b-2/b-3/b-4 are four refine beams of the related SSB beam B.

(3) Half1/half2 is half sector beam.

(4) The above rule is valid when rdBeamSpecificCfoEnabled is true. If rdBeamSpecificCfoEnabled is false, only first UL beam is used for CFO as legacy.

(5) Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21277\_replaced([11151260](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for detail PUCCH CFO usage and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21503\_replaced([11151025](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksW4cvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PUSCH CFO usage.

---

### 2.4.13.0-10 Carrier Frequency Offset Handling LT CFO validity for PUSCH/PUCCH in mode "Both" (ID: 11170261)

**2.4.13.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11170265`)

Carrier Frequency Offset Handling
If for given UE rrmCfoAverageMode is "Both" (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6877\_replaced([11150556](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksUcNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and enableUlPtrs is set to FALSE (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_430\_replaced([10975648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VCTQPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and dmrsAdditionalPosition is set to pos0 (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_430\_replaced([10975648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VCTQPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and

PUCCH F3 is scheduled and PUCCH F3 has not been scheduled for a certain time defined by rdPucchF3FoeResetThreshold\_ms (5G\_UP\_ALG\_28606\_replaced([11151505](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksZ7xPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

then:

5G-L2-PS shall invalidate LT CFO by setting:

* foeValid to FALSE
* [Before CB007491-H-UPD002]longTermCfoMetric and[End CB007491-H-UPD002][PR831459]longTermCfoMetric and[End PR831459] longTermCfometricOfAnt[0..7] to 0.
* rrmAvgCfoPrevious[i, j, 0..7] to 0.

otherwise, if any of the above conditions is not fulfilled then:

5G-L2-PS shall assume LT CFO is valid and set:

* foeValid to TRUE
* [Before CB007491-H-UPD002]longTermCfoMetric and[End CB007491-H-UPD002] [PR831459]longTermCfoMetric and[End PR831459] longTermCfoMetricOfAnt according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3331\_replaced([11151200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_UP\_ALG\_9182\_replaced([11151225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_UP\_ALG\_9183\_replaced([11151254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYGnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_UP\_ALG\_9184\_replaced([11151331](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksYtpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For more information see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3333\_replaced([11151157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksXfhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

## 2.4.14 Radio Link Monitoring

### 2.4.14.0-1 Radio Link Monitoring FA: QoS Document Owner: Anoop Maddi (Nokia) (ID: 11170275)

## 2.4.15 Random Access Procedure

### 2.4.15.0-2 Random Access Procedure C-RNTI cooling down protection algorithm for reusing after release (ID: 11170288)

**2.4.15.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11170300`)

Random Access Procedure
If the Cell is FR1 FDD cell  with one non-overlapping pool and one overlapping pool , the non-overlapping CRNTI table for Cell has no enough buffer for preventing reuse new CRNTI which is just released by L2-PS(e.g UE delete ).

[CB009058-A] If the Cell is FR1 TDD cell  with two non-overlapping pool and one overlapping pool , the non-overlapping CRNTI table for Cell has no enough buffer for preventing reuse new CRNTI which is just released by L2-PS(e.g UE delete ). [End CB009058-A]

C-RNTI cooling down protection is introduced to prevent allocating new released CRNTI and also make sure overlapping CRNTI is used only when no non-overlapping CRNTI is available.

CRNTI pending list is introduced for temp released CRNTI buffer.

When a CRNTI in non-overlapping list is released, push it back to a CRNTI pending list by steps bellow:

1. Get current time stamp, store the time stamp for the released CRNTI.
2. Check if there is CRNTI in pending list which has coolingDownTime more than rdCrntiCoolingDownTime(5G\_L2\_1429\_replaced([11187329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28ADTfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) seconds, if yes, then pop these CRNTI, and push them pack to non-overlapping list (free CRNTI table). here:

coolingDownTime= current time stamp - CRNTI released time stamp.

3.Push back the new released CRNTI into pending list with time stamp when the CRNTI is released.

When a new CRNTI is allocated using the free CRNTI from non-overlapping CRNTI list, and if the non-overlapping CRNTI list is run out(no free CRNTI), then continue the allocation by steps bellow:

1. Get current time stamp.
2. If the pending list is not empty and get the first pending CRNTI, check if its coolinDownTime(as step 2) is more than rdCrntiCoolingDownTime(5G\_L2\_1429\_replaced([11187329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28ADTfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) , if yes, pop this CRNTI to non-overlapping list and make the allocation.
3. If CRNTI allocation fails in step 2, then allocate CRNTI from overlapping CRNTI list according to  5G\_UP\_ALG\_3394\_replaced([11152003](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksdmIfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) that overlapping range is not used until C-RNTI from non-overlapping range is used up.

There is no need to cool down the overlapping CRNTI for FR1 FDD cells because number of CRNTI in this list is more than needed. And so every time one overlapping CRNTI is released, it could be push back into the end of the overlapping list directly.

For FDD cell with two non-overlapping pools, there is no cooling down algorithm at all. The CRNTI will be push back into the end of free CRNTI list and not be used immediately after released because the total number of CRNTI in the list has enough buffer than max UEs.

---

### 2.4.15.0-3 Random Access Procedure Duplicate Dedicated Preamble Handling (ID: 11170305)

**2.4.15.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11170309`)

Random Access Procedure
[PR585652]When processing the preambles received in UlData\_PrachReceiveInd message:

If a dedicated preamble (defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3382\_replaced([11152371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksfbXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) belongs to a known UE context which is currently performing a Random Access procedure, L2-PS shall discard the premable. [End PR585652]

---

### 2.4.15.0-4 Random Access Procedure UlData_PrachReceiveReq Transmission (ID: 11170313)

**2.4.15.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11170322`)

Random Access Procedure
UlData\_PrachReceiveReq shall be sent by the UL scheduler.

[CB009543-A] L2-PS shall send multiple UlData\_PrachReceiveReq (one for each UL subcell) with same PRACH data in case of FDD supercell [End CB009543-A]

### 2.4.15.0-5 Random Access Procedure rachStatus and prachPeakMetric Debugging Parameters (ID: 11170326)

**2.4.15.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11170331`)

Random Access Procedure
[CNI 82897]

rachStatus

A new parameter rachStatus is added as part of the following requests. The purpose of it is to indicate to L1 the relevant scheduled request is part of which Message from Random Access Process. The request can be carrying Message 2/ Message 3/ Message 4 or Message 5. This is used for debugging purpose on L1 side.

1. DlData\_PdcchSendReq - refer 5G\_L1\_IF\_74\_replaced([11512566](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRqbGvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. This is used in Message 2/3/4 or 5
2. DlData\_PdschSendReq - refer 5G\_L1\_IF\_87\_replaced([11512751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. This is used in Message 2/4
3. UlData\_PucchReceiveReq - refer 5G\_L1\_IF\_216\_replaced([11513990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR0MIfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. This is used in Message 4
4. UlData\_PuschReceiveReq - refer 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. This is used in Message 3/5
5. UlData\_PuschReceiveRespPs - refer 5G\_L1\_IF\_200\_replaced([11513831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRzlAPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. L1 uses this message to indicate to L2 whether it is sending a response for Message 3/4/5
6. UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs - refer 5G\_L1\_IF\_221\_replaced([11514182](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR2BQvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details. L1 uses this message to indicate to L2 whether it is sending a response for Message 3/4/5

[PR674171]

The value of this field rachStatus needs to be set to 0 in all the above requests for all the scenarios possible(including conformance test scenarios) except from Random Access Procedure messages. As this value only has a role for RACH process.

[End PR674171]

prachPeakMetric

A new parameter prachPeakMetric is added as part of request UlData\_PuschReceiveReq. L2 PS needs to add the value of metric received in the corresponding ul\_data\_prach\_receive\_ind into ul\_data\_pusch\_receive\_req for the Message 3 scheduling. This is further used in debugging during Random Access Procedure by L1. Refer 5G\_L1\_IF\_191\_replaced([11513756](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRy99_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

[PR674171]

The value of this field needs to be set to 0.0 in all non-RACH scenarios.

[End PR674171]

[End CNI 82897]

---

### 2.4.15.0-6 Random Access Procedure Random Access preamble reception (ID: 11170335)

**2.4.15.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11170339`)

Random Access Procedure
Algorithm for preambles selection

---

**2.4.15.0-6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11170352`)

Random Access Procedure
This requirement specifies the algorithm for preambles selection for Msg2 as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3383\_replaced([11152335](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksfbV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Upon reception of UlData\_PrachReceiveInd (defined in 5G\_L1\_IF\_234\_replaced([11514309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR2oWvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

 For every subcell in message :

o If numOfPrachPreambles = 0, go to next subcell in list

o Order all received preambles by absolute peakMetric in preamblesList[]

o If (NRCELL-nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion is in ["two", “four”]) AND (the number of preambles received > rdNbPrachPreambleProcessableForRachMsg2) AND (rdEvenPreambleSelectionBasedOnSsbBeamForMsg2 is 1)

 Call ‘even preamble selection’ algorithm processEvenPreambleSelection(preamblesList[], rdNbPrachPreambleProcessableForRachMsg2)

o Truncate preamblesList[] to its first rdNbPrachPreambleProcessableForRachMsg2 elements

Overview of ‘even preamble selection’ algorithm:

 Inputs:

o nbPreambleProcessable = rdNbPrachPreambleProcessableForRachMsg2 (see 5G\_L2\_1186\_replaced([11187391](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28AqWfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

o preamblesList[] : list of received preambles in prachReceiveInd message, sorted by descending peakMetric value

o Note: At algorithm input, PreamblesList[] size is equal to numOfPrachPreambles value in message UlData\_PrachReceiveInd.

 processEvenPreambleSelection(preamblesList[], nbPreambleProcessable)

o Output:

 preamblesList[] reordered by “even preamble selection” algorithm described below.

 Note: at algorithm output, PreamblesList[] size is equal to nbPreambleProcessable.

o Steps:

 nbBeamsDetected = computeNbBeamsDetected(preamblesList[])

 ifnbBeamsDetected = 1

 exit (same processing as when nbrSsbPerRo = 1)

 nbPreambleSelectedGlobally, nbPreambleSelectedEvenly computeNbPreamblesToBeSelectedGloballyAndEvenly(nbPreambleProcessable, nbBeamsDetected)

 sortedPreambleListPerSsb[][] = buildSortedPreambleListPerSsb(preamblesList[], nbPreambleSelectedGlobally)

 preamblesList[] = keepFirstPreamblesInListForGlobalSelection(preamblesList[], nbPreambleSelectedGlobally )

 preamblesList[] = completePreamblesListWithEvenSelection(preamblesList[], sortedPreambleListPerSsb[][], nbPreambleSelectedEvenly )

‘even preamble selection’ algorithm subfunctions:

 computeNbBeamsDetected(preamblesList[])

o Output:

 nbBeamsDetected , the number of distinct beams on which preambles of preamblesList[] have been detected.

 computeNbPreamblesToBeSelectedGloballyAndEvenly(nbPreambleProcessable, nbBeamsDetected)

o Output:

 nbPreambleSelectedGlobally : the number of preambles to be selected based on their absolute peakMetric value, regardless of the beam they were detected on.

 nbPreambleSelectedEvenly : the number of preambles to be selected based on the beamId they were detected on.

 Note: nbPreambleSelectedGlobally + nbPreambleSelectedEvenly = nbPreambleProcessable

o Steps:

 halfNbPreambleProcessable = floor(nbPreambleProcessable/2)

 if ((nbBeamsDetected> halfNbPreambleProcessable) and (nbBeamsDetected <= nbPreambleProcessable))

 nbPreambleSelectedEvenly = nbBeamsDetected

 Else

 nbPreambleSelectedEvenly = halfNbPreambleProcessable

 nbPreambleSelectedGlobally = nbPreambleProcessable – nbPreambleSelectedEvenly

o Note : From verification point of view, if nbBeamsDetected > nbPreambleProcessable, then obviously, the algorithm cannot ensure that at least one preamble for each detected SSB is selected (clarification of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3383\_replaced([11152335](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksfbV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 buildSortedPreambleListPerSsb(preamblesList[], nbPreambleSelectedGlobally)

o Output:

 sortedPreambleListPerSsb[][] : 2 dimensions table of preambles. First dimension is the “local” ssbIndex of the beamId the preamble belongs to, second dimension is the relative position of this preamble in preamblesList[] with respect to its beam. This table is built from preamblesList[] table, starting from element at index nbPreambleSelectedGlobally.

o Example of sortedPreambleListPerSsb[][] table construction:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating a process related to preambles and SSB (Synchronization Signal Block) indices. The diagram is divided into two main sections: the top section shows a list of preambles and their associated information, and the bottom section presents a table representing the sorted preambles per SSB.
**Top Section: Preamble List and Information**
This section starts with a horizontal arrow indicating "Start at index n = nbPreamblesSelectedGlobally". A list labeled "preamblesList[]" is shown, containing elements like "prmb0", "prmb(n-1)", and then a sequence from "prmb(n)" to "prmb(n+10)".
Below this list, several boxes describe the characteristics of specific preambles. These boxes are numbered from 1 to 8, corresponding to preambles from "prmb(n)" to "prmb(n+7)". Each box contains information about the preamble's "beamldx", "beamldy", "beamidz", or "beamldu" status, whether it's "unknown" or "already known", its association to a "new free local SSB index", and the column it's inserted into.
To the left of these boxes, a text block explains that "The first n elements of preamblesList[] are the ones with highest absolute peakMetric value, regardless of their SSB, i.e. the ones selected 'globally' by algorithm."
**Bottom Section: Sorted Preambles Per SSB Table**
This section is labeled "sortedPreamblesListPerSsb[][]". It's a table with columns representing SSB indices: "SSBidx0", "SSBidx1", "SSBidx2", and "SSBidx3". The rows are numbered from 0 to 5.
Each cell in the table contains a preamble identifier (e.g., "prmb(n)", "prmb(n+1)") and a number in a colored circle, indicating an "iteration x of sorting algo". The colors of the circles and the background of the preamble identifiers correspond to the legend.
**Legend**
A legend is provided to explain the color coding:
\* **Gray:** beamIdx / SSBIdx0
\* **Yellow:** beamldy / SSBIdx1
\* **Blue:** beamidz / SSBIdx2
\* **Green:** beamldu / SSBIdx3
\* **Blue Circle with 'x':** iteration x of sorting algo
**Table Representation:**

| SSB Index Dimension | SSBidx0 | SSBidx1 | SSBidx2 | SSBidx3 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | prmb(n) (1) | prmb(n+1) (2) | prmb(n+3) (4) | prmb(n+7) (8) |
| 1 | prmb(n+2) (3) | prmb(n+6) (7) |  | prmb(n+9) |
| 2 | prmb(n+4) (5) | prmb(n+10) |  |  |
| 3 | prmb(n+5) (6) |  |  |  |
| 4 | prmb(n+8) |  |  |  |
| 5 |  |  |  |  |
| *(Numbers in parentheses indicate the iteration number from the sorting algorithm.)* |  |  |  |  |
| The diagram visually represents how preambles are processed and sorted based on their characteristics and SSB associations, likely as part of a communication system's resource allocation or synchronization procedure.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4247.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |  |

Link to the source drawing file: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5GdocRD/RD%20NPS%20documents/09%20-%20Features/25R2-SR/CB012192-SR%20SSB%20to%20PRACH%20preamble%20mapping%20optimization/Archi/CP3%20material/CB012192-C%20-%20evenPreambleSelection%20Algorithm%20CP3.xlsx?d=w835e187f2bcb4d3eafb76da48dc53123&csf=1&web=1&e=AUHre7>

o Note: Because preambleList[] is sorted by descending peakMetric values, every column in sortedPramblesListPerSsb[][] is in descending peakMetric value by construction.

 keepFirstPreamblesInListForGlobalSelection(preamblesList[], nbPreambleSelectedGlobally)

o Output:

 preamblesList[] truncated to its nbPreambleSelectedGlobally first elements

 completePreamblesListWithEvenSelection(preamblesList[], sortedPreambleListPerSsb[][], nbPreambleSelectedEvenly)

o Output:

 preamblesList[] completed with nbPreambleSelectedEvenly preambles from sortedPreambleListPerSsb[][], parsed in a “per beam” order.

o Example of parsing of the sortedPreambleListPerSsb[][] table:

---

**2.4.15.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `11170357`)

Random Access Procedure
Preamble selection for FDD supercell

---

**2.4.15.0-6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11170362`)

Random Access Procedure
FDD supercell uses multiple L1-UL subcells to detect PRACH preambles. L2-PS can receive upto 'n' UlData\_PrachReceiveInd in response to earlier sent 'n' UlData\_PrachReceiveReq within a Preamble receive window (refer 5G\_L2\_23691\_replaced([11170367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26JCHfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), where n=number of UL super subcells in FDD. In such case, L2-PS needs to select the 'best' preambles among all the received UlData\_PrachReceiveInd messages to be used further in Msg2.

L2-PS maintains preamblesListFddSuperCell[] of the size MaxNumberofPreambles containing VitalParametersForPreambleSelection.

VitalParametersForPreambleSelection are

1. prachPreambleIndex

2. ulSubcellId corresponding to this UlData\_PrachReceiveInd

3. peakMetric corresponding to prachPreambleIndex

Entries in the preamblesListFddSuperCell[] are mapped 1-1 with the received prachpreamble indicies. 'Best preambles' across all the received UlData\_PrachReceiveInd messages are shortlisted and stored in preamblesListFddSuperCell[]. Steps to select and store 'best preambles' is explained below.

For every UlData\_PrachReceiveInd received with at least one valid prachPreamble, within the subcell data (only one subcell data supported in FDD supercell):

1. L2-PS checks every prachpreamble received in the message

2. If there is no prior entry in preamblesListFddSuperCell[] at the index mapping to the received prachpreamble index, L2-PS stores the 'VitalParametersForPreambleSelection' from the received prach preamble at such index.

3. If there is an existing entry in preamblesListFddSuperCell[] at the index mapping to the received prachpreamble index in the message, L2-PS compares the 'peakMetric' related to the received prach preamble and the one already stored in the corresponding entry of the preamblesListFddSuperCell[]. If the received 'peakMetric' is greater than the stored 'peakMetric' for a given prachpreamble, L2-PS overwrites the VitalParametersForPreambleSelection stored in preamblesListFddSuperCell[] with the ones received in the UlData\_PrachReceiveInd for the same prachpreamble index.

L2-PS stops processing the received UlData\_PrachReceiveInd messages when one of these conditions occurs

1. Preamble receive window has expired

2. At least one UlData\_PrachReceiveInd has already been processed from each of the 'n' configured UL super subcells.

L2-PS sorts the entries in preamblesListFddSuperCell[] in the descending order of the 'peakMetric' values among all the valid prachpreamble indicies. The entries which do not have valid VitalParametersForPreambleSelection are not considered for sorting and are pushed to the end of the list.

If sorted preamblesListFddSuperCell[] has valid entries greater than rdNbPrachPreambleProcessableForRachMsg2, truncate preamblesListFddSuperCell[] to its first rdNbPrachPreambleProcessableForRachMsg2 elements.

L2-PS now has the list of 'best' preambles among all the received UlData\_PrachReceiveInd messages and further uses them in Msg2. L2-PS maps each of these best preambles to their corresponding subcells (uLSubCellId from where the best preamble was reported) for deriving parameters to be used in Msg2. Further this ulSubcellId is marked as 'Initial serving subcell' for the preamble and its going to be established UE context.

All the entries in preamblesListFddSuperCell[] are reset after the transmission of Msg2.

Note :

1. L2-PS skips the steps related to storing the 'VitalParametersForPreambleSelection' if any of the parameters are invalid.
2. L2-PS skips the steps related to peakmetric comparsion if any of the 'VitalParametersForPreambleSelection' (related to prachpreambles being compared) are invalid.
3. L2-PS processes only the first UlData\_PrachReceiveInd received from a given UL subcell in the during the same PRACH occassion. Subsequently received UlData\_PrachReceiveInd messages shall be dropped during the same PRACH occassion.
4. During the Peakmetric comparison, if same preambles have the same peakMetric, storage of VitalParametersForPreambleSelection is skipped

---

**2.4.15.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `11170367`)

Random Access Procedure
Preamble reception window for FDD supercell

---

**2.4.15.0-6.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11170371`)

Random Access Procedure
L2-PS shall process all the UlData\_PrachReceiveInd messages received during 'preamble receive window' as per 5G\_L2\_23596\_replaced([11170357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26JCG_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Preamble receive window is the maximum allowable time for L2-PS to receive UlData\_PrachReceiveInd and process preambles to safely trigger Msg 2 towards the L1-DL within the given Random Access Response (RAR) window. Preamble receive window is calculated by tracking the preamble receive window start and end slots.The definition is explained below

Preamble receive window(measured in slots) => Time between ' Preamble receive window start ' and ' Preamble receive window end '

where

Preamble receive window start = slot when the first UlData\_PrachReceiveInd is received from any UL subcell in FDD supercell for the given PRACH occasion.

Preamble receive window end = minimum((Preamble receive window start + rdPrachRecIndReceiveWindowMultipleSubcells), Previous slot of the RAR window start slot)

Note:

1. Preamble receive window is specific to PRACH occasion, hence it needs to be refreshed and computed for every PRACH occasion.
2. All UlData\_PrachReceiveInd messages received outside/after 'preamble receive window' shall be ignored.
3. RAR window start slot is defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23061\_replaced([11174520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kucjKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
4. FDD Supercell does not extend or shorten the RAR window. Sufficient time is allowed to process PRACH receive indication and still send MSG2 within the RAR window like in non-supercell cases.

---

### 2.4.15.0-7 Random Access Procedure Multiple Preambles Handling in the same PRACH occasion (ID: 11170374)

**2.4.15.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11170393`)

Random Access Procedure
If /NRBTS/NRcell/actFdmSchedulingInitialBWP is true

**[Before CB012192-C]**

5G-L2-PS assemble a MAC PDU which bundling multiple RARs for mutiple preambles from the same prach occasion. 5G-L2-PS create a new RA-RNTI UE context to schedule rach Msg2 in Pre/Td/Fd scheduler and it would be deleted after rach Msg2 has been scheduled.

The detail as blow:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process with several stages: "Start", "Pre-scheduler", "TD Scheduler", "FD Scheduler", and "POST Scheduler". Arrows indicate the flow between these stages, with labels like "Prach slot" and "Msg2 slot" denoting transitions.
To the right of the flowchart, there are descriptions of actions associated with each stage.
- **Pre-scheduler**: Associated actions are "Creat RA-RNTI UE context" with a sub-action "start up Rach Msg2 StartTimer", and "Creat TC-RNTI/CRNTI UE context" with a sub-action "Cancel Rach Msg2 StartTimer".
- **TD Scheduler**: The action is "Schedule RACH Msg2 with RA-RNTI UE context, calculate TDRA and FDRA for multiple RARs". This action is highlighted with a light blue background.
- **FD Scheduler**: The action is "Schedule RACH Msg2 with RA-RNTI UE context, fill multipe RARs into one Pdschpayloadtbsendreq".
- **POST Scheduler**: There are two associated actions: "copy necessary Information From RA-RNTI UE context to TC-RNTI/CRNTI" and "Delete RA-RNTI UE context if all RARs scheduled out, or the left RARs will be store in UE context and scheduled in next msg2 slot".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4254.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3418\_replaced([11152611](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kshQhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[CB012192-C]**

5G-L2-PS assembles a MAC PDU which bundles multiple RARs for multiples preambles from the same prach occasion. 5G-L2-PS creates a new RA-RNTI UE context, and creates a currentBundlesUe context with same RA-RNTI/BeamId by taking RA-RNTI/BeamId of the first UE from this new RA-RNTI UE Context to schedule rach Msg2 in Pre/Td/Fd scheduler, the UEs with this RA-RNTI/beamId will be deleted after rach Msg2 has been scheduled. If there are other UEs in RA-RNTI UE context, a currentBundlesUe context will be created with the RA-RNTI/BeamId of the first UE in the list of RA-RNTI UE context and will be scheduled in the next msg2 slot. This creation/deletion procedure repeats until all bundles Ues Msg2 are scheduled.

Link to the source drawing file: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/5GdocRD/RD%20NPS%20documents/09%20-%20Features/25R2-SR/CB012192-SR%20SSB%20to%20PRACH%20preamble%20mapping%20optimization/Archi/CP3%20material/CB012192-C%20-%20Multiple%20preambles%20handling.pu?csf=1&web=1&e=8H1EIE>

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating a process related to RACH (Random Access Channel) preamble and Msg2 scheduling in a cellular network. The diagram consists of several states connected by transitions, with accompanying text boxes providing detailed descriptions of the actions performed in each state.
Here's a breakdown of the diagram:
**States:**
1. **Rach preamble:** This is the initial state, indicated by a filled circle.
2. **Pre Scheduler:** This state is reached after the "prach slot" transition.
3. **TD/FD Scheduler:** This state is reached after the "msg2 slot" transition.
4. **Post Scheduler:** This state is reached after a transition from the "TD/FD Scheduler".
5. **End State:** Represented by a filled circle within an unfilled circle, indicating the end of the process.
**Transitions and Descriptions:**
\* **Rach preamble** to **Pre Scheduler**: Labeled "prach slot".
\* **Description Box:** "At the moment of receiving UlData\_PrachReceivelnd from L1: Create RA-RNTI UE context, Start up Rach Msg2 StartTimer Create TC-RNTI/CRNTI Ue Context, Cancel Rach Msg2 StartTimer (note: The creation of Ra Rnti Ue Context and the management of Rach Msg2Cancel timer were added by feature CB009550-B, CB012192-C did not modify it)"
\* **Pre Scheduler** to **TD/FD Scheduler**: Labeled "msg2 slot".
\* **Description Box:** "Schedule Msg2 with currentBundlesUe containing the same RA-RNTI/Beamid Calculate TDRA and FDRA for multiples RARS fill multiples RARs into one pdschPayLoadTbSendReq"
\* **TD/FD Scheduler** to **Post Scheduler**: No explicit label on the transition arrow, but it follows the flow.
\* **Description Box:** "Copy necessary Information from RA-RNTI Ue context to TC-RNTI/C-RNTI context Delete RA-RNTI Ue context if all RARs scheduled out or the left RARs will be stored in RA-RNTI Ue Context, currentBundlesUe with the same RA-RNTI/Beamid will be built by taking the first Ue in the list and scheduled in next msg2 slot"
The diagram visually represents a sequence of operations performed by different schedulers in response to RACH preamble reception and Msg2 transmission.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4254.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3418\_replaced([11152611](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kshQhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_UP\_ALG\_16158\_replaced([11177911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIcPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**[End CB012192-C]**

### 2.4.15.0-8 Random Access Procedure PM Counters Support (ID: 11170397)

**2.4.15.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11170402`)

Random Access Procedure
RA setup time calculation

---

**2.4.15.0-8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11170408`)

Random Access Procedure
For 'NR Rach Usage's class PM Counters, the time taken for a successful RA procedure PM Counter shall be maintained by calculating them in the following cases:

M55306C09012: RA\_CF\_STP\_TIME record the time taken for a successful CF RA procedure.

in the case that one contention free preamble was received, record this msg1 time for the UE;

in the case that one contention free MSG3 was received with CRC OK, record this msg3 time for the UE;

The time between msg3 and msg1 in micro seconds is record for a successful CF RA procedure.

Average shall be calculated using the counter: RA\_COMP\_DED\_PRMBL

M55306C09013: RA\_CB\_STP\_TIME record the time taken for a successful CB RA procedure.

in the case that one contention based preamble was received, record this msg1 time for the UE;

in the case that one contention based MSG3 was received with CRC OK, record this msg3 time for the UE;

The time between above msg3 and msg1 in micro seconds is record for a successful CB RA procedure.

Average shall be calculated using the counter: RA\_MSG3\_RECEP

---

**2.4.15.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11170413`)

Random Access Procedure
Contention Based RA Procedure

---

**2.4.15.0-8.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11170419`)

Random Access Procedure
5G-L2-PS shall update counter RA\_CB\_CONT\_RESOLVED\_ALL (M55306C09015) by one, when the first UL transmission from the UE, performing initial access or Handover, is received over PUSCH by 5G-L2-PS. The message contains CRC-Ok indication thus Resolving Contention.

Note:

For reference check Contention Based RA Procedure 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_182\_replaced([11186977](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfDSpvvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The counter is incremented only for the first RA attempt performed by the UE making initial 5G connection. Any subsequent RA attempts using the same Preambles shall not be included in this counter.

---

**2.4.15.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11170422`)

Random Access Procedure
Contention Free RA procedure

---

**2.4.15.0-8.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11170428`)

Random Access Procedure
5G-L2-PS shall update counter RA\_CFRA\_MSG2\_SENT (M55306C09014) by one, when Msg2 is send to UE in response to Msg1 for CFRA.

Note:

For reference check Contention Free RA Procedure 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_184\_replaced([11187642](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfG9CPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**2.4.15.0-8.0-4**  (ID: `11170432`)

Random Access Procedure
Number of RA msg3 with CCCH1 receptions

---

**2.4.15.0-8.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11170437`)

Random Access Procedure
Measurement: NR RACH usage

NetAct name: RA\_MSG3\_CCCH1\_RECEP

PI ID: M55306C06002

NIDD link: <https://nidd-prod1.ext.net.nokia.com/NIDD/Trans/Counter/Details?id=9277776&pId=65523&vId=2>

Update time:

5G-L2-PS shall increment the counter when the gNB successfully receives (CRC-OK) the random access msg3 containing CCCH1.

Increment value:

5G-L2-PS shall increment the counter by 1.

---

### 2.4.15.0-9 Random Access Procedure C-RNTI allocation in SCell or ext-SCell (ID: 11170442)

**2.4.15.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11170446`)

Random Access Procedure
In case of intra-gNB CA or inter-gNB CA, during SCell or ext-SCell addition procedure:

 5G-L2-PS in (ext-)SCell shall reserve the UE's allocated C-RNTI by marking such as “in use” to avoid potential collision (see 5G\_L2\_8969\_replaced([11159926](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25T7qfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 If UE's allocated C-RNTI belongs to 5G-L2-PS (ext-)SCell list of free C-RNTIs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3403\_replaced([11152041](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksdmJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then the C-RNTI shall be removed from that cell’s list of free C-RNTIs.

---
