## 2.4.16 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management

### 2.4.16.0-2 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management Overview (ID: 11170471)

**2.4.16.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11170475`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
Informative:

According to received UL-CCCH content (5G\_L2\_11433\_replaced([11181207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27SRkvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_11432\_replaced([11181217](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27SRlPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS shall manage User Context and respective UL and DL resources as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18227\_replaced([11153313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kslh9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Depending on the RRC procedure triggered by the C-Plane, specific rules can be found in:

* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17951\_replaced([11153395](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (RRC Setup).
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17972\_replaced([11153597](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksnXKPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (RRC reconfiguration, RRC Re-establishment, RRC Reject, RRC Resume with an explicit state of UE dedicated configuration).
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion)

---

### 2.4.16.0-3 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management DL-CCCH: RRC setup procedure (ID: 11170481)

**2.4.16.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11170484`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
When RRC Setup is triggered by C-Plane (5G\_L2\_11433\_replaced([11181207](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27SRkvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17951\_replaced([11153395](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS shall execute the procedure as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17951\_replaced([11153395](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJBfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Specifically:

* Procedure start (potential BWP switch check, availability of UE’s dedicated configuration, computing expected TBS depending on CRI MAC CE presence, scheduling of RA Msg4 etc.) – see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17986\_replaced([11153416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Procedure completion detection – see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17985\_replaced([11153522](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmwHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For specific rules and scheduling restrictions until UE’s dedicated configuration is confirmed to be activated, see 5G\_L2\_11428\_replaced([11170534](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3QfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.16.0-4 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management DL-CCCH: RRC setup procedure increase AUG SRB priority (ID: 12568967)

**2.4.16.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12568972`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
Increase priority for AUG SRB

During RRC Setup procedure 5G-L2-PS shall boost priority of UL-DCCH transmission (SRB) when all below conditions are met:

* MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAugSrbPrioBoost is set true, and
* Call type which corresponds with callType IE in PsUser\_UserSetupReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_378) is equal to CT\_SAInitialAccess, and
* DRB is not established (during RRC Setup procedure it’s always before DRB establishment)[PR837690] or DedicatedConfigActivationTimer has expired (in such situation boost only one UL grant) [End PR837690], and
* SRB1 (Control PDU or Data PDU) has any pending data to schedule including MSG5 (RRC Setup/Resume/Reestablishment Complete) [CNI-127390-D], and
* There was no positive SR from UE after RrcProcessingDelayTimer expired (see 11153913, 11183250) [End CNI-127390-D]

Then 5G-L2-PS should boost UL messages by setting EntryAnticipatedUlGrantSrb in UL-pre-scheduler (see 5G\_L2\_1614\_replaced([11201458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29wWyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) (highestClassPriority = 6) and prioAnticipatedUlGrantSrb in UL-TD-Scheduler (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_107) (resReqClassPrio = 65).

[PR837690] Note1: After 5G-L2-PS receive PsUser\_UserSetupReq message (see 10974651) is set ueDedicatedConfigState IE to "disabled" or "inProgress".  
Note2: DedicatedConfigActivationTimer starts after sending all payload in MSG4. After timer expires 5G-L2-PS change ueDedicatedConfigState IE to "Activated" (parameter not present) and give UL grant for RRCResumeComplete / RRCReestablishmentComplete. [End PR837690]

[PR829916] Decrease number of MSG5 Schedule Requests (SR)

When SR is multiplexed with CSI report and when all below conditions are met:

* DRX is enabled (NRBTS.NRCELL.actCDrx is equals to true), and
* NRCELL.actAugSrbPrioBoost is equals to true, and
* UE is awaiting UL grant for MSG5, and
* CSI report occasion was skipped

then 5G-L2-PS shall not add ‘fake SR’ to CSI report multiplexed with SR and add shall boost priority for MSG5 [End PR829916]

---

### 2.4.16.0-5 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management DL-CCCH: RRC Setup processing delay (ID: 11170493)

**2.4.16.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11170501`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
Before sending the anticipatory UL grant during RRC Setup procedure, when all the following conditions are met:

* call type which corresponds with *callType IE in* PsUser\_UserSetupReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_378\_replaced([10974513](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U8zsvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equal to *CT\_SAInitialAccess*\*\*
* the PDU is being sent over SRB1
* the first DRB has not been established
* and *rdRrcProcessingDelayAUGSrb (see 5G\_L2\_21822\_replaced([11183250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iJO_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))* is set to *[Before CNI-127390-D] true [CNI-127390-D] 1:RRC delay enabled for RRC setup and RRC reconfiguration or 2:RRC delay enabled only for RRC setup[End CNI-127390-D],*

5G-L2-PS shall wait for 5 ms.

**Notes:**

1. RRC processing delay before allowing UL grant for MSG5 is handled by DedicatedConfigActivationTimer, which introduces a 16ms delay for regular RRC setup and a 10ms delay for Small RRC Setup. (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17940\_replaced([11153286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kslh8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
2. If the computed rrcProcessingDelay is less than 0, then don't start rrcProcessingDelayTimer. If rrcProcessingDelay is less than L1 delay, apply a delay equal to rrcProcessingDelay + L1 delay. (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_287\_replaced([11514945](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR6SuvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L1\_IF\_14384\_replaced([11514812](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR5rp_vxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

**R&D parameter for control delay for RRC Setup: 5G\_L2\_21822\_replaced([11183250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iJO_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**

**CP2 Reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**

---

### 2.4.16.0-6 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management DL-DCCH: RRC reconfiguration, Re-establishment, Reject, Resume (with an explicit state of UE dedicated configuration) (ID: 11170505)

**2.4.16.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `11170517`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
When RRC reconfiguration, Reestablishment, Reject or Resume is triggered by C-Plane (5G\_L2\_11432\_replaced([11181217](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27SRlPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3454\_replaced([11153602](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksnXKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/ 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18929\_replaced([11153613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksnXK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and there is no dedicated BWP configuration, L2-PS shall execute the procedure as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17972\_replaced([11153597](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksnXKPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Specifically:

* Procedure start (availability of UE’s dedicated configuration, DL Link Adaptation rules etc.) – see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3454\_replaced([11153602](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksnXKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_UP\_ALG\_18929\_replaced([11153613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksnXK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Procedure completion detection – see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17992\_replaced([11153671](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-NvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* Procedure for first DL-DCCH transmission - see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18233\_replaced([11153675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-N_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (specifically, DL-DCCH activating the UE dedicated configuration - see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18234\_replaced([11153699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For specific rules and scheduling restrictions until UE’s dedicated configuration is confirmed to be activated, see 5G\_L2\_11428\_replaced([11170534](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3QfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When RRC reconfiguration is triggered by C-Plane and there is already a dedicated BWP configuration, L2-PS shall execute the procedure as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26249\_replaced([11153730](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-PvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For specific rules and scheduling restrictions until UE’s dedicated configuration is modified, see 5G\_L2\_17046\_replaced([11170549](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3RPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before PR811432]

[CNI-127390-A] Increase priority for AUG SRB

When RRC reconfiguration is triggered by C-Plane by sending valid PsUser\_UserModifyReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_453\_replaced([10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to 5G-L2-PS, then 5G-L2-PS shall check if:

     MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAugSrbPrioBoost is set to true, and

     PsUser\_UserModifyReq message is without IE ueRRCReconfigurationNotNeeded

If all conditions are met, then 5G-L2-PS shall boost AUG SRB until:

     reception Data PDU, or

     reception scheduling request (SR) from UE, or

     reception of UE context release [End CNI-127390-A]

[End PR811432]

[Before PR807214][CNI-127390-B] RRC processing delay for AUG SRB

During RRC Reconfiguration procedure 5G-L2-PS shall:

 add 10ms before allow UL grant for RRC reconfiguration complete, and

 block scheduling UE data by blocking cs1 list with reason cs1\_ueRrcReconfigDelayAdded (see 5G\_L2\_16735\_replaced([11204151](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29_nUfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

when all below conditions are met:

 rdRrcProcessingDelayAUGSrb (see 5G\_L2\_21822\_replaced([11183250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iJO_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to true (default is true), and

 ueRRCReconfigurationNotNeeded in PsUser\_UserModifyReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_453\_replaced([10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is absent, and

 SRB1 or SRB3 Data PDU only, and

 DRB established (During RRC reconfiguration DRB is already established)

[End CNI-127390-B][End PR807214]

---

### 2.4.16.0-7 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management DL-DCCH: RRC Reconfiguration processing delay and boost priority for AUG SRB (ID: 11170522)

**2.4.16.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11170529`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
Delay State machine

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12516323`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
For proper handling RRC processing delay for RRC reconfiguration, there was introduced new Enum class: State which represents delay state machine (DSM).

Possible states of DSM:

* State 0: Disabled
* State 1: Waiting for Buffer (Enabled)
* State 2: Waiting for Grant
* State 3: Waiting for Harq Feedback
* State 4: Timer Ready
* State 5: Timer Running
* State 6: Not Applicable

Possible moves between states of DSM:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating a process with six states and seven transitions. The states are represented by light blue rounded rectangles, and the transitions are represented by arrows labeled with letters. There is also a yellow rectangle representing an event.
Here's a breakdown of the states and transitions:
**States:**
\* **(0) Disabled:** This is the initial state.
\* **(1) Waiting For Buffer (Enabled):** A state where the system is waiting for buffer enablement.
\* **(2) Waiting For Grant:** A state where the system is waiting for a grant.
\* **(3) Waiting For Harq Feedback:** A state where the system is waiting for HARQ feedback.
\* **(4) Timer Ready:** A state indicating the timer is ready.
\* **(5) Timer Running:** A state where the timer is currently running.
\* **(6) Not Aplicable (Default):** A state labeled as not applicable, serving as a default.
**Transitions:**
\* **G:** From state (5) Timer Running to state (0) Disabled.
\* **A:** From state (0) Disabled to state (1) Waiting For Buffer (Enabled).
\* **F:** From state (5) Timer Running to state (1) Waiting For Buffer (Enabled).
\* **B:** From state (1) Waiting For Buffer (Enabled) to state (2) Waiting For Grant.
\* **C:** From state (2) Waiting For Grant to state (3) Waiting For Harq Feedback.
\* **D:** From state (3) Waiting For Harq Feedback to state (4) Timer Ready.
\* **E:** From state (4) Timer Ready to state (5) Timer Running.
**External Event:**
\* **Timer HARQ Expiry or SR or reRACH or RRCReconfigCompleted Req:** This event is represented by a yellow rectangle.
\* **G':** A dotted arrow from this event points to state (0) Disabled.
\* **F':** A dotted arrow from this event points to state (1) Waiting For Buffer (Enabled).
The diagram shows a cyclical flow between states (1) through (5), with state (0) acting as a reset or initial state. State (6) appears to be an isolated state, possibly representing an outcome or condition where the described process is not relevant. The dotted arrows from the event suggest that this event can trigger transitions into states (0) and (1).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-01301418.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `12516336`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
Initialization of Delay State Machine, states 0,1,6

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12516346`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
After reception of PsCell\_CellSetupReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_73), 5G-L2-PS shall:

* Save received value of actRrcReconfProcessingDelay IE into cell DB

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the states and transitions related to a "UserSetupReq (init)" process. The flowchart consists of one yellow rectangular box at the top and three light blue rounded rectangular boxes at the bottom. Arrows connect the top box to the bottom boxes, indicating transitions.
The yellow box at the top contains the text "UserSetupReq (init)".
The three light blue boxes at the bottom contain the following text:
1. "(0) Disabled"
2. "(1) Enabled (Waiting For Buffer)"
3. "(6) Not Aplicable"
The transitions are labeled as follows:
- An arrow from "UserSetupReq (init)" points to "(0) Disabled" with the label "Activation + FAF OFF".
- An arrow from "UserSetupReq (init)" points to "(1) Enabled (Waiting For Buffer)" with the label "Activation + FAR ON".
- An arrow from "UserSetupReq (init)" points to "(6) Not Aplicable" with the label "DeActivation".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-04111217.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

After reception of PsUser\_UserSetupReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_378), 5G-L2-PS shall save actRrcReconfProcessingDelay value into UE DB actRrcReconfProcessingDelay and then it shall:

1. check if actRrcReconfProcessingDelay is quals to true, and
2. check if R&D parameter rdRrcProcessingDelayAUGSrb (see 5G\_L2\_21822\_replaced([11183250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27iJO_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is equals to 1 or 3

(1 - RRC delay enabled for RRC setup and RRC reconfiguration,

3 - RRC delay enabled only for RRC reconfiguration), and

1. check if UE call type is SA (callType IE equals to CT\_SAInitialAccess), and
2. check if serving cell is PCell for this UE

If at least one of these conditions are not met: (2), (3), (4) then:

* set DSM in state: (6) Not applicable

If all above conditions are met except condition 1 (actRrcReconfProcessingDelay is equal to false), then:

* set DSM in state: (0) Disabled.

IF all above conditions are met then:

* set delay state machine (DSM) in state: (1) Waiting for Buffer.

Note: Scell is not in scope of CNI-127390, due to there is no RRC communication between UE and Scell. The RRC connection is established and maintained with the PCell (primary serving cell), which acts as the central point of control for the UE.

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-4**  (ID: `12516347`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
Handling dynamic disabling (True à False) of RRC processing delay for RRC reconfiguration

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12516349`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
After reception PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_220) with actRrcReconfProcessingDelay IE equal to false, 5G-L2-PS shall check if its value is different than current value in Cell DB, if yes then it shall:

* update new value of actRrcReconfProcessingDelay in cell DB to False, and
* For all UEs on this cell:
* update UE DB: actRrcReconfProcessingDelay to False, and
* If UE’s Delay State Machine (DSM) is equal to 1, then change state to 0
* If UE’s DSM is equal to 2,3,4, or 5 then 5G-L2-PS shall continue RRC processing delay for RRC reconfiguration,
* If UE’s DSM is equal to 6 then do nothing.

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-5**  (ID: `12516350`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
Handling dynamic enabling (False à True) of RRC processing delay for RRC reconfiguration

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `12516354`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
After reception PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_220) with actRrcReconfProcessingDelay IE equal to True, 5G-L2-PS shall check if its value is different than current value in Cell DB, if yes then it shall:

* Update new value of actRrcReconfProcessingDelay in cell DB to True, and
* For all UEs on this cell:
* update UE DB: actRrcReconfProcessingDelay to True, and
* If UE’s Delay State Machine (DSM) is equal to 0, then change state to 1
* If UE’s DSM is equal to 6 then do nothing.

Note: UEs which were during RRC processing delay when 5G-L2-PS received PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq message will change DSM state after delay timer expired (after state 5).

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-6**  (ID: `12516370`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
Handling transition between delay state machine states

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `12710132`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
Move from state (1) Waiting for Buffer (Enabled) to à (2) Waiting for Grant, transition B

After reception from 5G-L2-LO LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_870) by 5G-L2-PS, it shall proceed with steps starting from (1), and go to next step only when is true:

1. rrcAction IE is equal to (2): [Before CNI-125276-A]RRCReconfigNonCA[CNI-125276-A]RRCReconfigOthers[End CNI-125276-A], or (3) RRCReconfigCA
2. UE is after first DRB establishment
3. last PsUser\_UserModifyReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_453) was not Cancellation, rollback or PCMD Cookie
4. ueDedicatedConfigState IE stored in UE DB equals to Activated

When all above conditions are met, then 5G-L2-PS shall:

* UE move from state (1) Waiting for Buffer (Enabled), to state (2) Waiting for Grant,
* [CNI-161127] fill TTI trace event processingDelayInfo.macLayerMonitoringStart (see [13360656: processingDelayInfo](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6y9bM_K_EfC0Z6kKit-MzQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) with SFN and slot received in LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd message,
* fill TTI trace event processingDelayInfo.macLayerMonitoringStop with SFN and slot when harqRrcMsgTimeoutTimer shall expire [End CNI-161127]

When 5G-L2-PS receive rrcAction IE equals to (2): [Before CNI-125276-A]RRCReconfigNonCA[CNI-125276-A]RRCReconfigOthers[End CNI-125276-A], or (3) RRCReconfigCA without corresponding and valid PsUser\_UserModifyReq message before (see point 3 above), then it shall not apply RRC processing delay and shall stay in state (1) Waiting for Buffer (Enabled). Additional for rrcAction IE equal to (3) RRCReconfigCA 5G-L2-PS shall rise warning.

Note: After start MAC layer monitoring (L2PS receive from 5G-L2-LO LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd message) each PsUser\_UserModifyReq message send from CP-RT to L2PS shall be ignored unit RrcProcessingDelayTimer expire.

Note2: CP-RT via L2-HI then L2-LO inform L2-PS about RRC message type (2): [Before CNI-125276-A]RRCReconfigNonCA[CNI-125276-A]RRCReconfigOthers[End CNI-125276-A] or (3) RRCReconfigCA in rrcAction IE (either initiated by gNB-CU or gNB-DU), but do not guarantee that it sent earlier correlated PsUser\_UserModifyReq message.

Note3. When adding, modifying, or releasing a Secondary Cell (SCell) in a Carrier Aggregation (CA) scenario, the PsUser\_UserModifyReq and PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq sequence is used. In case of measurement configuration, there is PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq without corresponding PsUser\_UserModifyRequest message.

Move from state (2) Waiting for Grant to  à  (3) Waiting for HARQ Feedback, transition C

When first fragment of RRC reconfiguration message has been scheduled for UE, then 5G-L2-PS shall:

* move UE’s Delay state machine (DSM) to state (3) Waiting for HARQ Feedback,
* [CNI-161127] fill TTI trace event processingDelayInfo.firstFragment with current SFN and slot [End CNI-161127]

Note4. RRC reconfiguration message can be fragmented, so in that situation gNB sends a few PDSCH messages to UE and all of them should be confirmed (ACK) and then UE respond with RRC reconfiguration complete message.

Move from state (3) Waiting for HARQ Feedback to  à (4) Timer Ready, transition D

When all grants (in case of fragmentation of RRC Reconfiguration message occurred, in other case only 1 grant) for RRC Reconfiguration have been acknowledgement (ACK) or finally HARQ NACK (isMaxRetransExceeded), then 5G-L2-PS shall change UE’s DSM to state 4

Move from state (4) Timer Ready to à (5) Timer Running, transition E

5G-L2-PS shall compute RrcProcessingDelayTimer (see [11153872: DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) as follows:

* depends on received rrcAction IE in LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd message set RrcProcessingDelayTimer:
* 10 ms when rrcAction IE equal to (2): [Before CNI-125276-A]RRCReconfigNonCA[CNI-125276-A]RRCReconfigOthers[End CNI-125276-A]
* 16 ms when rrcAction IE equal to (3): RRCReconfigCA
* Extend timer by 6ms when BWP config related parameters are present in PsUser\_UserModifyReq message (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_453) such as:
* parameters related to transform precoder switch, or
* scheduling request, or
* 3Tx mode, or
* Channel State Information (CSI) report, or
* CSI-RS (Channel State Information-Reference Signal), or
* Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) configuration, or
* Physical Uplink Shared Channel (PUSCH) configuration, or
* Physical Uplink Control Channel (PUCCH) configuration, or
* power control configuration for PUSCH , or
* Configured grant (CG grant )
* Reduce time with K1 value

When last fragment for RRC Reconfiguration is triggered then 5G-L2-PS shall:

* start RrcProcessingDelayTimer
* change UE’s Delay State Machine (DSM) to state 5,
* [CNI-161127] fill TTI trace event processingDelayInfo.rrcDelayStart with current SFN and slot,
* fill TTI trace event processingDelayInfo.rrcDelayStop with calculated SFN and slot when RrcProcessingDelayTimer shall expire.[End CNI-161127]

Move from state (5) Timer Running to à (0) Disabled, transition G

When RrcProcessingDelayTimer for UE will expire and actRrcReconfProcessingDelay in UE DB was changed and is equal to False, then 5G-L2-PS shall:

* change UE’s Delay State Machine (DSM) to state 0,
* if MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAugSrbPrioBoost is set to true, then boost priority of UL-DCCH transmission (SRB) for at least one AUG SRB or more according to proactive UL grant (see 12532487),
* [CNI-161127] trace whole TTI event processingDelayInfo [End CNI-161127]

Move from state (5) Timer Running to à (1) Waiting for Buffer (Enabled), transition F

When RrcProcessingDelayTimer for UE will expire and actRrcReconfProcessingDelay in UE DB is still equal to True, then 5G-L2-PS shall:

* change UE’s Delay State Machine (DSM) to state 1,
* if MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAugSrbPrioBoost is set to true, then boost priority of UL-DCCH transmission (SRB)  for at least one AUG SRB or more according to proactive UL grant (see 12532487),
* [CNI-161127] trace whole TTI event processingDelayInfo [End CNI-161127]

Move from any state to à (0) Disabled, transition G’ or (1) Enabled, transition F’

When

* HARQ retransmission timer (harqRrcMsgTimeoutTimer) expired, or
* NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to true (CB013742) and UE has triggered reRACH procedure, or
* 5G-L2-PS has received PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq message from L3 before RrcProcessingDelayTimer expiration

Then 5G-L2-PS shall check actual value of actRrcReconfProcessingDelay in UE DB, and it shall:

* For actRrcReconfProcessingDelay equal to False:
* change UE’s Delay State Machine (DSM) to state 0
* For actRrcReconfProcessingDelay equal to True:
* change UE’s Delay State Machine (DSM) to state 1
* [CNI-161127] trace whole TTI event processingDelayInfo [End CNI-161127]

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-7**  (ID: `12532487`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
Proactive UL grant and priority boost for RRC response

---

**2.4.16.0-7.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `12532488`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
When all conditions below are met:

* MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actAugSrbPrioBoost is set to true, and
* MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actRrcReconfProcessingDelay is set to true, and
* RRC Processing Delay timer is expired (UE in delay state machine 5, see 11170529), and
* additional RRC processing delay for RRC reconfiguration is not enough to bundle Control PDU and DATA PDU, and
* UE response on RRC reconfiguration request message only with RLC ACK (Control PDU) or just padding

Then 5G-L2-PS shall give additional SRB grants for UL scheduling and continue boosting priority of UL-DCCH transmission (SRB) until response from UE is received  **[Before PR833408] [Before PR830327] (when DATA PDU with BSR=0 [PR829206]or empty BSR containing just padding [End PR829206]) [PR830327] regardless of existing BSR and its value. [End PR830327] [PR833408] with Data PDU with SRB1 logical channel identity (SRB1 LCI) or Data PDU on any logical channel higher than SRB1 (SRB2 or any DRB) [PR837688] or when positive SR is received. [End PR837688].**

Note: In UL CA, UE can send SRB traffic like RRCReconfigurationComplete on SCell. PCell 5G-L2-PS can assume that UE response on SCell when Data PDU with LCI has been received on higher LCI than SRB1.[End PR833408]

CP2 Reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27948\_replaced([11194471](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv1DPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/11194471(DNG) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32746

---

### 2.4.16.0-8 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management Transient phase for the UE without dedicated configuration (with an explicit state of UE dedicated configuration) (ID: 11170534)

**2.4.16.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11170543`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
L2-PS shall start transient phase for the UE, when ueDedicatedConfigState is set to 'disabled' in PsUser\_UserSetupReq (5G\_L2\_11422\_replaced([11144261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_234_VPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Furthermore, the transient phase is to be continued when PsUser\_UserModifyReq message(s) is/are received with ueDedicatedConfigState set to 'disabled' or 'inProgress' (5G\_L2\_11424\_replaced([11144691](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_237bkPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In transient phase, during and after the completion of first RRC procedure after User Setup (5G\_L2\_11436\_replaced([11170481](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26KQOfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), following restrictions apply to UE scheduling, depending on the state the user context is currently in:

* 'disabled':
* only common downlink and uplink resources (PDCCH/PDSCH/PUCCH/PUSCH) configured in the SIB1 are used. The common resources that are available are/do affect the following:
* The Type1-PDCCH (common search space for DL/UL grants), specifically:
* Used for Search space selection for C-RNTI (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4053\_replaced([11168627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktxNufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Additional check for inclusion into CS2 list based on Type1-PDCCH availability (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4354\_replaced([11177973](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzIfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* DCI format 1\_0 to be used for RA-Msg4 with CRI MAC CE and DL-DCCH (SRB1) messages, the DCI format 1\_0 with CRC scrambled by C-RNTI (5G\_UP\_ALG\_16216\_replaced([11178050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuzvjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4453\_replaced([11178188](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku09pfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4463\_replaced([11178704](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LzPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Scheduling of RA-Msg4 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_18004\_replaced([11153469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmwEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Contention Based RA completion (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3441\_replaced([11153057](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjsy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Common TDRA resources for the PDSCH allocation broadcasted in the SIB1:
* Used for allocations during RA procedure (5G\_UP\_ALG\_6667\_replaced([11115365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpt0IfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Precoding and spatial mode settings (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3171\_replaced([11139234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krlca_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3178\_replaced([11139281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3183\_replaced([11139297](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12747\_replaced([11139457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krnRmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18139\_replaced([11153435](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* FD resource calculation for Msg4/SRB0 message in Initial BWP: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16523\_replaced([11179366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku5PHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* The number of parallel HARQ processes for PDSCH is restricted to 8 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4526\_replaced([11184119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvP0bPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Common PUCCH resources:
* HARQ feedback for any transmission (5G\_UP\_ALG\_18005\_replaced([11153496](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmwGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11091\_replaced([11177791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kux6VfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18087\_replaced([11187133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvY-UvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Number of short PUCCH symbols to be received for HARQ feedback on PUCCH (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4587\_replaced([11187257](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvZlhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* MU Trigger Condition with PRB Pooling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14781\_replaced([11146479](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksEkkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Additional restrictions for HARQ feedback for RA-Msg4/SRB0 in TDD with beamforming: (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4319\_replaced([11177575](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuwFK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* HARQ feedback for specific RRC procedures: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4582\_replaced([11187231](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvZlZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* The Common TDRA resources for the PUSCH allocation broadcasted in the SIB1 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_17753\_replaced([11115795](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpyFkvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* For MU Trigger Condition With PRB Pooling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14781\_replaced([11146479](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksEkkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* There is no request/decoding of CSI report.
* There is no SRS configuration, therefore L2-PS shall not request SRS from the L1 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_2151\_replaced([11154219](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksrolPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Minimum Transport Block Size to handle the DL-CCCH message and Link Adaptation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_18142\_replaced([11153451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJDvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Link Adaptation with DL-DCCH alone and UE dedicated configuration deactivated or during its activation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_18231\_replaced([11153658](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-NPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Additional restrictions when selecting UE for CS1 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4209\_replaced([11174627](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kudxRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4226\_replaced([11174914](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kue_aPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Additional restrictions when selecting UE for UL pre-scheduling candidate set, for UEs with PUSCH proactive scheduling/skippable proactive grants scheduling/TCP proactive grants (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4807\_replaced([11194840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv3fe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), Low Priority Proactive Scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4833\_replaced([11195231](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6iyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), [CB012810-D] DRB priority Proactive Grant Scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_30332\_replaced([11195558](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8_CfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB012810-D] ,
* Common PUCCH transmissions (with addition of UEs without activated dedicated configuration) determine PUSCH sub-areas (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4879\_replaced([11196950](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwGwCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* 'inProgess': the same restrictions as for 'disabled' state apply, however as Scheduling Requests (SRs) are configured for the UE, L2-PS shall support reception and handling SRs.

The transient phase ends when it is confirmed that dedicated configuration is active in the UE (see 5G\_L2\_11483\_replaced([11170571](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3SfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). When it is confirmed, all the above restrictions are not valid anymore and all resources configured for the UE can be used.

Furthermore, after the transient phase ends (or, there was no transient phase at all, e.g. ueDedicatedConfigState was not present in PsUser\_UserSetupReq), below requirements shall be followed by L2-PS (this is to highlight the fact that legacy behaviour is changed):

* Minimum Transport Block Size to handle the DL-CCCH message and Link Adaptation : 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18139\_replaced([11153435](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
* Link Adaptation with DL-DCCH and CRI MAC CE (5G\_UP\_ALG\_18230\_replaced([11153648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-MvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

### 2.4.16.0-9 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management Transient phase for the UE with a dedicated configuration (with an explicit state of UE dedicated configuration) (ID: 11170549)

**2.4.16.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11170557`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
As the current state is "activated" in the user context when it receives the PsUser\_UserModifyReq message with ueDedicatedConfigState set to 'inProgress', L2-PS moves the user context state to "inProgress", and it shall start transient phase for this UE.

Some of new configurations present in psUser\_UserModifyReq are not immediatly taken into account, they are temporary saved as pending configuration as long as the UE does not confirmed the reception of the RRC-RECONF. Refer to the requirements  5G\_UP\_ALG\_26845\_replaced([11153360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26846\_replaced([11153382](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksmJA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the list of new configurations impacting the DCI sizes.

In this transient phase, during and after the completion of RRC\_RECONF procedure after User Setup (5G\_L2\_11436\_replaced([11170481](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26KQOfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), following restrictions apply to UE scheduling due to the "inProgress" state:

* The UE specific PDCCH and the active BWP are used for DL/UL grants
* Additional restrictions when selecting UE for UL pre-scheduling candidate set, for UEs with PUSCH proactive scheduling/skippable proactive grants scheduling/TCP proactive grants (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4807\_replaced([11194840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv3fe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), Low Priority Proactive Scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4833\_replaced([11195231](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv6iyfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB012810-D], DRB priority Proactive Grant Scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_30332\_replaced([11195558](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv8_CfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB012810-D]. The UE dedicated configuration is not activated at this step.

as soon as UE has confirmed the reception of the RRC-RECONF by a MAC confirmation (HARQ), all the pending configurations are taken into account by L2PS to be used immediatly for the new dedicated BWP.

The transient phase ends when it is confirmed that dedicated configuration is active in the UE (see 5G\_L2\_11483\_replaced([11170571](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3SfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). When it is confirmed, all the restrictions are not valid anymore and all resources configured for the UE can be used.

Refer to the 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_5573\_replaced([11184464](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Be2eXvvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the call flow.

---

### 2.4.16.0-10 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management Transient phase for the UE with a dedicated configuration (without an explicit state of UE dedicated configuration) (ID: 11170562)

**2.4.16.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11170565`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
When L2-PS receives PsUser\_UserModifyReq message without ueDedicatedConfigState present, L2-PS shall not change UE's dedicated configuration activation status and may start a transient phase for the DL/UL scheduling during the time period between PsUser\_UserModifyReq reception and PsUser\_RrcReconfigCompleteReq reception. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8181\_replaced([11153814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksolSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) / 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4816\_replaced([11194889](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv4GhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for some detail about transient phase processing on DL / UL direction.

During transient phase, L2-PS shall also follow the DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion procedure specified by 5G\_L2\_22365\_replaced([11170588](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3TfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If **[Before CB013742-SR-C] rrmEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to ‘TRUE’ (5G\_L2\_22348\_replaced([11142460](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23unR_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_22350\_replaced([11143101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23yRqvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**[CB013742-SR-C] isMacLayerMonitorNeeded is evaluated as ‘TRUE’ according to 5G\_L2\_22352\_replaced([11145029](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_239QxPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) **[End CB013742-SR-C], transient phase is ended at the completion of DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion which may be a little earlier than the reception of PsUser\_RrcReconfigCompleteReq. If DCI format is forced to 1\_0 / 0\_0 within transient phase, L2-PS needs to resume DCI formatting 1\_1 / 0\_1 also upon the completion of DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion.**\*\*\*\*

---

### 2.4.16.0-11 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management Confirmation of activation of UE dedicated configuration (ID: 11170571)

**2.4.16.0-11.0-3**  (ID: `11170584`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
The L2-PS considers that the UE has received its dedicated configuration (DL and UL) when:

* PsUser\_UserSetupReq is received with ueDedicatedConfigState is not present (5G\_L2\_11422). In this case:
* There is no transient phase as described in 5G\_L2\_11428.
* After a Beam Failure Recovery (BFR), the L2-PS triggers the TCI update (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3444\_replaced([11153203](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksk64_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* After the DL-CCCH procedure, the L2-PS triggers the TCI update (5G\_UP\_ALG\_18000\_replaced([11153214](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksk65fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* If the UE has not dedicated configuration and PsUser\_UserModifyReq is received with ueDedicatedConfigState 'inProgress', and after that, the procedure for confirming that first DL-DCCH transmission is acknowledged by the UE, is completed, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18234\_replaced([11153699](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-OvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In this case:
* The transient phase described in 5G\_L2\_11428, ends.
* Before the end of the procedure, there is no TCI update even for a Beam Failure Recovery (BFR).
* At the end of the DL-DCCH procedure, the L2-PS applies the TCI update as defined in the 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18000\_replaced([11153214](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksk65fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18001\_replaced([11153190](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksk64fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* If the UE has already a dedicated configuration and PsUser\_UserModifyReq is received with ueDedicatedConfigState 'inProgress', and after that first DL-DCCH transmission is acknowledged by the UE,  confirming that the procedure is completed, as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26249\_replaced([11153730](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksn-PvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In this case, the transient phase described in 5G\_L2\_17046, ends.
* [CB013742-A] The activation of UE dedicated configuration is not affected by the reception of PsUser\_UserModifyReq without ueDedicatedConfigState present. Note: PsUser\_UserModifyReq without ueDedicatedConfigState present is a indication that UE dedicated configuration is activated already. [End CB013742-A]

---

### 2.4.16.0-12 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management Deferring DL-DCCH RRC messages that requires RRC complete (ID: 12782335)

**2.4.16.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `12782336`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
[04949764]

L2-PS shall delay the handling of PsUser\_UserModifyReq for 200 ms at most according to [12764030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_9F9DgYQcEfCOGMMH109ICw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) with below exception: "When MRBTS/NRBTS.actEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is enabled" condition is assumed to be satisfied if all below conditions are satisfied:

- rrmEnhRrcReconfUlPrbRandom is set to 'TRUE' (5G\_L2\_22348\_replaced([11142460](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23unR_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_22350\_replaced([11143101](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23yRqvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

- Current UE is for initial access (PsUser\_UserSetupReq::ueAddCause is set to “InitialAccess”)

- Current UE is not a SCell UE

[End 04949764]

---

### 2.4.16.0-13 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion (ID: 11170588)

**2.4.16.0-13.0-3**  (ID: `12793189`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
When PsUser\_UserModifyReq is received and isMacLayerMonitorNeeded is evaluated as ‘TRUE’ according to 5G\_L2\_22352, which means MAC Layer Monitoring for RRC Reconfiguration is needed after user modify request, L2-PS needs to execute the DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion procedure as specified by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Timer and MAC Layer monitoring control**

According to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 2 timers and 1 procedure are used for Type 1 RRC Complete Detection:

* harqRrcMsgTimeout timer

- Start trigger: reception of DL-DCCH data indication (SRB1) (5G\_L2\_11432) with rrcAction IE set to 'RRCReconfigOthers' or 'RRCReconfigCA'

- Stop trigger: see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

* rrcMsgProcessingDelay timer

- Start trigger: see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- Stop trigger: see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

* MAC Layer monitoring procedure

- Start trigger: harqRrcMsgTimeout timer start

- Stop trigger: harqRrcMsgTimeout timer stop

DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion phases:

Phase 1: After PsUser\_UserModifyReq reception and before MAC Layer monitoring procedure is started,

* Either DCI 1\_0 or DCI 1\_1 is used for DL scheduling (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8181\_replaced([11153814](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksolSvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Note: this is legacy scenario, not changed by CB013742.
* DCI 0\_1 shall be used for UL scheduling.

Phase 2: After harqRrcMsgTimeout timer is started

**Robust consideration:**

* If PsUser\_UserModifyReq to cancel or rollback an elderly triggered RRC reconfiguration (5G\_L3\_FLOW\_62574, 5G\_L3\_FLOW\_22681), i.e. in case of any of below conditions is satisfied, is received during Phase 1, L2-PS shall abort DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion procedure and print an information log.
* cancellationType is present in PsUser\_UserModifyReq
* the parameter MCS-table of PdschConfig or PuschConfig is set to ‘cancelMcsTableReconfiguration’
* [CB011022-CB] the parameter dmrsType of PdschConfig is set to ‘cancelDmrsTypeReconfiguration’ [End CB011022-CB]
* perSrConfigChangeRollback is present in PsUser\_UserModifyReq

To be noted that CG configuration / CG deconfiguration cancelation procedure is not considered. Rational: it could happen (but very rare) that RRC reconfiguration message containing UE configuration change(s) other than CG may still need to be transmitted to UE during CG configuration / deconfiguration cancelation procedure.

* If PsUser\_UserModifyReq to cancel or rollback an elderly triggered RRC reconfiguration is received during Phase 2, L2-PS shall ignore the new PsUser\_UserModifyReq and print an information log and response OK to client. Rational: 1. after MAC Layer Monitoring procedure is started, L2-PS needs to assure it run to complete; 2. NOK response is not expected by client for PsUser\_UserModifyReq to cancel or rollback an elderly triggered RRC reconfiguration.
* Within the whole DL-DCCH RRC Complete Detection for RRC Messages Requiring RRC Completion procedure specified by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29060\_replaced ([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Specifically, during the time period:
* start upon
  + PsUser\_UserModifyReq reception and when isMacLayerMonitorNeeded is evaluated as ‘TRUE’ according to 5G\_L2\_22352
* end upon:
  + reception of PsUser\_RrcReconfigCompleteReq, or
  + rrcProcessingDelay timer expiry or stopping, or
  + harqRrcMsgTimeout timer expiry, or
  + reception of PsUser\_UserModifyReq to cancel or rollback an elderly triggered RRC reconfiguration (5G\_L3\_FLOW\_62574, 5G\_L3\_FLOW\_22681)

L2-PS shall abort PsUsr\_ReconfigurationRequiredInd sending to avoid new PsUser\_UserModifyReq triggering from CPRT. Rational: L2-PS could not handle new PsUser\_UserModifyReq well during MAC layer monitoring ([11153872](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kspMVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note: only PsUsr\_ReconfigurationRequiredInd message sending is aborted, L2-PS still need to start the ReconfigurationRequiredInd timer (as if PsUsr\_ReconfigurationRequiredInd is sent out but ignored by CPRT).

---

### 2.4.16.0-14 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management DL-DCCH RRC Message Delivery Procedure (ID: 11170598)

**2.4.16.0-14.0-3**  (ID: `12688619`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
When LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd is received with rrcAction = RRCRelease [CNI-125276-A] or RRCReconfigHO [End CNI-125276-A], L2-PS needs start MAC layer monitoring for the RRC message delivery not requiring RRC completion according to the algorithm specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31674\_replaced(11153894).

[Before CNI-125276-UPD001][CNI-125276-A] If rrcAction = RRCReconfigHO, MCS reduction should not introduce additional fragments, MCS to be chosen between MCSx and max{MCS0, MCSx-rdMcsReductionRrcWoRlcAck} (refer to 11153894)[End CNI-125276-A][CNI-125276-A-UPD001] The NRCELL.actDlRankAdpAndSRB1McsLimDurHO flag is mandated to be true for rrcAction = RRCReconfigHO according to NRCELL.actEnhHOCommandHandling's description, and the DL Rank adaptation(refer to 11139510) and MCS limitation(refer to 11125340) for the SRB1 follows NRCELL.actDlRankAdpAndSRB1McsLimDurHO flag (refer to 11153894) [End CNI-125276-A-UPD001]

Start trigger:

* LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd is received with rrcAction=RRCRelease [CNI-125276-A]  or RRCReconfigHO [End CNI-125276-A]

End trigger [PR840987] for rrcAction = RRCReconfigHO [End PR840987]:

* all HARQ processes used SRBx (x=0,1,2,3) transmission are released (no pending HARQ is expected)

[PR840987]

End trigger for rrcAction = RRCRelease:

* all HARQ processes used SRBx (x=0,1,2,3) transmission are released (no pending HARQ is expected), and
* the additional anticipated uplink grant procedure is completed

[End PR840987]

Output:

* Send PsUser\_RrcDeliveryCompletedInd to CPRT

Algorithm:

* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31674\_replaced(11153894).

---

### 2.4.16.0-15 DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management UEs exclusion from CS1 list (ID: 11170605)

**2.4.16.0-15.0-1**  (ID: `11170610`)

DL-CCCH and DL-DCCH management
For cells configured with FDD non-DSS [Before PR771922] and NRBTS/actSlowPrbPoolingFddFr1Enh set to True, [End PR771922] where CSI-IM is scheduled on symbols other than CSI-RS for channel acquisition, the following conditions apply during DL Pre-Scheduling:

* If PsUser\_UserSetupReq is received and the ‘ueDedicatedConfigState’ is not present (as specified in 5G\_L2\_11422), L2-PS will exclude UEs with MSG 2/4 on the CSI-IM slot from being added to the CS1 list.
* If PsUser\_UserSetupReq is received and the ‘ueDedicatedConfigState’ is set to ‘disabled’ or ‘inProgress’, L2-PS will exclude UEs on the CSI-IM slot from being added to the CS1 list until the end of the transient phase defined in 5G\_L2\_11428.
* If PsUser\_UserModifyReq is received and the ‘ueDedicatedConfigState’ is set to ‘inProgress’, L2-PS will exclude UEs on the CSI-IM slot from being added to the CS1 list until the end of the transient phase defined in 5G\_L2\_11428.

---

## 2.4.17 SRS, CSI-RS and CSI Framework

### 2.4.17.0-1 SRS, CSI-RS and CSI Framework FA: SRS, CSI-RS and CSI Framework Document Owner: Shuyan Zhang (NSB); Yuanying Xie (NSB) (ID: 11170618)

### 2.4.17.1 SRS Framework

#### 2.4.17.1.1 Aperiodic SRS for UL SU-MIMO

##### 2.4.17.1.1.0-1 Aperiodic SRS for UL SU-MIMO APeriodic SRS for UL SU-MIMO for TAS UE (ID: 11170631)

**2.4.17.1.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11170634`)

Aperiodic SRS for UL SU-MIMO
Aperiod SRS can be enable/disable during UE wideband TAS and subband TAS switch,

PsUser\_UserModifyReq message used to trigger the update.

---

##### 2.4.17.1.2.0-2 Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO Periodic SRS Symbol configuration for Cell (ID: 11171058)

**2.4.17.1.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11171068`)

Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO
On cell level, the periodic SRS symbol number or symbol position are derived from PsCell\_CellSetupReq->bfSetup->srsSymbolPos[] or PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq-> srsSymbolPos[]. Only periodic SRS on latest cell’s periodic SRS symbols shall be received according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26832 and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2151. Examples are shown as bellow:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the concept of SRS (Sounding Reference Signal) symbol positions in a communication system. The diagram is divided into two rows, each depicting a sequence of symbols represented by colored rectangles.
In the first row:
- The initial state shows `srsSymbolPos[] = {11,12}`, represented by a series of light green rectangles followed by two purple rectangles.
- An arrow points to the next state, `srsSymbolPos[] = {12,13}`, which shows a similar sequence of symbols. However, the last symbol in this sequence is marked with a red 'X', indicating an issue.
- A blue speech bubble points to this red 'X' and contains the text: "periodic SRS on this symbol not received, no UlData\_SrsReceiveReq info for periodic SRS on this symbol".
In the second row:
- The initial state shows `srsSymbolPos[] = {12,13}`, again represented by light green and purple rectangles.
- An arrow points to the next state, `srsSymbolPos[] = {10,12}`, which also shows a sequence of symbols. The last symbol in this sequence is marked with a red 'X'.
- A blue speech bubble points to this red 'X' and contains the same text as in the first row: "periodic SRS on this symbol not received, no UlData\_SrsReceiveReq info for periodic SRS on this symbol".
The grid background suggests a structured representation, possibly related to time slots or resource blocks in a wireless communication system. The colors likely represent different types of symbols or states within the SRS transmission. The red 'X' and the accompanying text highlight a failure or absence of expected information for the SRS on specific symbols.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4316.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

And with periodic SRS symbol position changing with different configuration in different time duration, the cell may possibly occupy 4 periodic SRS symbols in one slot but only up to 2 latest periodic SRS symbols can be scheduled. One example is as bellow:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the configuration of SRS (Sounding Reference Signal) positions within a frame structure. The diagram shows a progression of configurations, indicated by arrows.
The top section of the image contains a header with the following information:
- **frame structure 1116, physical Cell ID = 2**
- **NRBTS/NRCELL-actMulSrsInSplSlot = true**
The diagram then presents three distinct configurations:
**Configuration 1:**
- **NRBTS/NRCELL-mulSrsPositions = twoSrsPos1**
- **NRCELL.actNrRim = false**
- **srsSymbolPos[] = {12,13}**
Below this text, a visual representation shows a sequence of 16 symbols. The first 14 symbols are colored light green, and the last two symbols are colored purple.
**Configuration 2:**
This configuration is indicated by a blue arrow pointing from Configuration 1 to Configuration 2.
- **NRBTS/NRCELL-mulSrsPositions = twoSrsPos2**
- **NRCELL.actNrRim = false**
- **srsSymbolPos[] = {10, 12}**
Below this text, a visual representation shows a sequence of 16 symbols. The first 10 symbols are light green. The 11th and 13th symbols are marked with a red 'X', and the 12th symbol is purple.
**Configuration 3:**
This configuration is indicated by a blue arrow pointing from Configuration 2 to Configuration 3.
- **NRBTS/NRCELL-mulSrsPositions = twoSrsPos3**
- **NRCELL.actNrRim = true**
Below this text, a visual representation shows a sequence of 16 symbols. The first 10 symbols are light green. The 11th and 13th symbols are marked with a red 'X', and the 12th symbol is purple.
Finally, a blue text box below Configuration 3 provides an explanation:
- **four symbols are occupied, but only latest cell configuration is effective, i.e. only periodic SRS on symbol 12, 11 can be received**[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4316.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

The editable file for the figure in this requirement is in link: <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/17.%20SRS,%20CSI-RS%20and%20CSI%20Framework/5G_L2_23783%20Cell%20pSRS%20symbol%20position.xlsx?d=w10ad4baf3a2c4d219ec133224a67d688&csf=1&web=1&e=56dZ5j>

Note: If pSRS is configured but pSRS symbol position for the cell is not informed to L2PS when NRBTS/NRCELL-actMulSrsInSplSlot = FALSE, all the configured pSRS shall be received.

---

##### 2.4.17.1.2.0-3 Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO SRS Frequency Hopping (ID: 11171073)

**2.4.17.1.2.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11171088`)

Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO
SubbandId calculation

---

**2.4.17.1.2.0-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11171126`)

Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO
If [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "b\_hop < B\_SRS". The text is in a light gray color against a white background. The expression is written in a standard mathematical font.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0], frequency hopping is enabled and the frequency position indices [image-1][image\_desc]The image is too blurry to describe in detail.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]:

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation defines a variable $n\_b$ using a piecewise function.
The equation is:
$n\_b = \begin{cases} \lfloor 4n\_{RRC}/m\_{SRS,b} \rfloor \pmod{N\_b} & b \le b\_{hop} \ (F\_b(n\_{SRS}) + \lfloor 4n\_{RRC}/m\_{SRS,b} \rfloor) \pmod{N\_b} & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

where [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N₂" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of blue, yellow, and orange, and have a glowing effect. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3], [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text that appears to be "m, SR, b". The text is in a light blue and yellow color palette.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] is given by Table 6.4.1.4.3-1 in 3GPP 38.211 6.4.1.4,

[image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula defines a function $F\_b(n\_{SRS})$ which is a piecewise function based on whether $N\_b$ is even or odd.
For the case when $N\_b$ is even, the formula is:
$$F\_b(n\_{SRS}) = \left(\frac{N\_b}{2}\right) \left[ \frac{n\_{SRS} \pmod{\prod\_{b'=b\_{hop}}^{b} N\_{b'}}}{\prod\_{b'=b\_{hop}}^{b-1} N\_{b'}} + \frac{n\_{SRS} \pmod{\prod\_{b'=b\_{hop}}^{b} N\_{b'}}}{2\prod\_{b'=b\_{hop}}^{b-1} N\_{b'}} \right]$$
For the case when $N\_b$ is odd, the formula is:
$$F\_b(n\_{SRS}) = \left(\frac{N\_b}{2}\right) \left[ \frac{n\_{SRS} \pmod{\prod\_{b'=b\_{hop}}^{b} N\_{b'}}}{\prod\_{b'=b\_{hop}}^{b-1} N\_{b'}} + \frac{\lfloor N\_b/2 \rfloor \lfloor n\_{SRS} / \prod\_{b'=b\_{hop}}^{b-1} N\_{b'} \rfloor}{\prod\_{b'=b\_{hop}}^{b-1} N\_{b'}} \right]$$
The formula uses the following notation:
- $F\_b(n\_{SRS})$: The function being defined.
- $n\_{SRS}$: An input variable.
- $N\_b$: A parameter, which can be even or odd.
- $b$: An index.
- $b\_{hop}$: A specific value of the index $b$.
- $\prod\_{b'=b\_{hop}}^{b} N\_{b'}$: The product of $N\_{b'}$ from $b'=b\_{hop}$ to $b$.
- $\prod\_{b'=b\_{hop}}^{b-1} N\_{b'}$: The product of $N\_{b'}$ from $b'=b\_{hop}$ to $b-1$.
- $\pmod{}$: The modulo operator.
- $\lfloor x \rfloor$: The floor function, which gives the greatest integer less than or equal to $x$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

and where [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression "N\_bhop = 1". The text is black and appears to be in a standard font. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] regardless of the value of [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains the text "N" followed by a subscript "h". The letters are stylized with a rainbow gradient effect, giving them a vibrant and somewhat blurry appearance. The background is white, and the text is enclosed within a black border.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]. The quantity [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains blurry, colorful text that is difficult to read. The text appears to be in a sans-serif font and is arranged horizontally. The colors are vibrant and include shades of blue, yellow, red, and purple. The background is white.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8]counts the number of SRS transmissions.

[image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula. The formula is:
$n\_{SRS} = \left( \frac{N\_{slot}^{frame,\mu} n\_f + n\_{s,f}^{\mu} - T\_{offset}}{T\_{SRS}} \right) \cdot \left( \frac{N\_{symb}^{SRS}}{R} \right) + \left\lfloor \frac{l'}{R} \right\rfloor$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.010.png[/image\_path][/image-9]

for slots that satisfy [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$(N\_{\text{slot}}^{\text{frame},\mu} n\_f + n\_{\text{s,f}}^\mu - T\_{\text{offset}}) \mod T\_{\text{SRS}} = 0$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.011.png[/image\_path][/image-10].

SubbandId of each hopping will be calculated by

[image-11][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a serif font. On the left side of the equation is the term "subbandId". To the right of "subbandId" is an equals sign. To the right of the equals sign is a summation symbol. The summation symbol has a lower limit of "b=0" and an upper limit of "B\_SRS". To the right of the summation symbol is the term "n\_b N\_b".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4321.012.png[/image\_path][/image-11]

##### 2.4.17.1.2.0-4 Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO Periodic SRS Scheduling for UL and DL WB BM (ID: 11171130)

**2.4.17.1.2.0-4.0-4**  (ID: `11171151`)

Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO
There may be hundreds of UEs are scheduled in one slot with 2 OFDM symbols. In order to suppress interference, only half of cs(cycle shift, odd or even values) can be configured in same comb same symbol same slotOffset. All UEs in particular cell shall be configured with same P SRS period by CPlane.

L2 Ps will store the max number of srs resource as the bellow for Uldata\_SrsReceiveReq according to the 5G\_UP\_8271\_replaced([11398824](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf9bXvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

100M BW, comb=2 with c\_srs=63, there will be up to 68 subband \* 2 comb \* 4 cs \* 2 symbols = 1088 UEs in case of nrofSRS-Ports=1.

100M BW, comb=2 with c\_srs=63, there will be up to 68 subband \* 2 comb \* 2 cs \* 2 symbols = 544 UEs in case of nrofSRS-Ports=2.

100M BW, comb=4 with c\_srs=63, there will be up to 68 subband \* 4 comb \* 4 cs \* 2 symbols = 2176 UEs in case of nrofSRS-Ports=1.

100M BW, comb=4 with c\_srs=63, there will be up to 68 subband \* 4 comb \* 2 cs \* 2 symbols = 1088 UEs in case of nrofSRS-Ports=2.

[Before CNI-130313]

[CB011117-B]

100M BW, comb=4 with c\_srs=63, there will be up to 68 subband \* 4 comb \* 6 cs (1cs per Ue) \* 2 symbols = 3264 UEs in case of nrofSRS-Ports=1.

100M BW, comb=4 with c\_srs=63, there will be up to 68 subband \* 4 comb \* 3 cs (2 cs per Ue) \* 2 symbols = 1632 UEs in case of nrofSRS-Ports=2.

Note: 6 cyclic shifts for comb 4 is only supported in Thor

Note:  after CNI-130313, CS = 4 from 6 in CB011117-B, need descope the above requirement in 5G\_L2\_17620  about 6CS , for the sake of adaption between L1 and L2, BM max number of srs resource in the interface of Uldata\_SrsReceiveReq is still kept

[End CB011117-B]

[End CNI-130313]

P SRS for BM shall be scheduled with symbolPosition, subbandId, combId and cycle shift as defined in Uldata\_SrsReceiveReq.

Due to L1 doesn't keep UE RNTI for BM, UE context is mapped with symbolPosition, subbandId, combId and cycle shift. One cycle shift in one comb in one subband on one symbolPosition shall be considered as one  scheduling resource of P SRS for BM measurement. One scheduling resource per UE if nrofSRS-Ports=1 and two scheduling resources per UE if nrofSRS-Ports=2.

Due to L1-L2 interface limitation of message size, up to 1088 SRS\_BM\_resource per slot is supported, see 5G\_L1\_IF\_250\_replaced([11514405](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR3PafvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

UEs are configured with P SRS for BM shall be scheduled in accending order of symbolPosition, subbandId, combId and cycle shift.

Note: only up to 10 UEs which is controlled by CPlane R&D parameter per symbol per slot is supported in 5GC001086-A/B due to L1 capacity unknown. Later in 5GC001086-G, up to 40 UEs is planned. How many UEs can be supported will be updated after testing/benchmark, updated in 5G\_UP\_3297\_replaced([11398176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf6YFfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_L2\_3443.

Note: When interworking with CDRX, UE will not transmit periodic SRS when it cant keep 4ms active before SRS reporting, Refer more to sector 5.7 of 3GPP 38.321. From software system arch level, it will cost many CPU to check the condition and nearly no benefit. The conclusion is: L2PS only schedules SRS when CDRX is active in this slot, UE may not report SRS. If L2PS receives SRS DTX, ignore this message.

Note: when interworking with measrue gap configured in 5G\_L2\_14660, L2PS shall not schedules periodic SRS resource and [CB011117-A]reset PeriodWbSrsSchData

used by UlData\_SrsReceiveReq defined 5G\_L2\_9827,

here is an example as 1T4R SRS port

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a spreadsheet with data organized into columns and rows. The top row contains headers like "1T4R", "SF", and "gap" followed by numbers. Below the "gap" headers are port names like "port0", "port1", "port2", and "port3". The first column contains labels such as "slot", "SRS ports startPatternId sent by L2(request)", "startPatternId sent by L1 resp", "Reset" bit, "Store" bit, "Calc" bit, and "Correlation Calc" bit.

##### 2.4.17.1.2.0-5 Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO Periodic SRS Scheduling Limitation according to L1 Capacity (ID: 11171156)

**2.4.17.1.2.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `11171168`)

Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO
Total number of scheduled UE is limited to [Before CB014465-A]maxNumOfPSrsPerSymbol[CB014465-A]maxNumOfPSrsPerSlot = maxNumOfPSrsWithWbBfPerSlot(when there is no SB BF UE)/maxNumOfPSrsWithSbBfPerSlot(When there is SB BF UE)[End CB014465-A](5G_L2_IF_115_replaced([10968891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UaBKvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

P SRS of UE configured with port1 for BM is counted as 1, P SRS of UE configured with port2 for BM is counted as 2, P SRS of UE (configured with port2) for SUMIMO only is counted as 20. See below table. Refer to L1 requirement 5G\_UP\_3297\_replaced([11398176](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf6YFfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Case | BM\_only\_port1 | BM\_only\_port2 | SUMIMO |
| Token | 1 | 2 | 20 |

If there are m UE configured with port1 and n UE configured with port2, (m+n\*2<=[Before CB014465-A]maxNumOfPSrsPerSymbol[CB014465-A]maxNumOfPSrsPerSlot[End CB014465-A]) which is guaranteed by Cplane. When m + n < [Before CB014465-A]maxNumOfPSrsPerSymbol[CB014465-A]maxNumOfPSrsPerSlot[End CB014465-A], there are still number of x=floor{([Before CB014465-A]maxNumOfPSrsPerSymbol[CB014465-A]maxNumOfPSrsPerSlot[End CB014465-A]– m \* 1 – n \* 2)/20}, 0<=x<=maxNumSuMimoPerSlot, possible UEs can be scheduled for SUMIMO. The maximum number of UE can be scheduled for SUMIMO per slot is maxNumSuMimoPerSlot=rdMaxNumOfSuMimoReports(default 8) (5G\_UP\_4254\_replaced([11398222](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf6YH_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). All UEs configured with BM shall be scheduled at same time.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Frame structure | 1070(slot 7, 17) | 1070(slot 3, 13) | 1208 | 1116 |
| SUMIMO\_Cap | 0 | 2 | 4 | 6 |

How many UEs will be scheduled for SUMIMO is decided by following calculation:

min{floor(([Before CB014465-A]maxNumOfPSrsPerSymbol[CB014465-A]maxNumOfPSrsPerSlot[End CB014465-A]– m \* 1 – n \* 2)/20)+ SUMIMO\_Cap, maxNumSuMimoPerSlot}.

For example, given that 1208 can support up to 4 SUMIMO and 136 BM, if there are only 10 UEs configured with 2 SRS ports, maximum number of SUMIMO per symbol is min{ floor((136-10\*2)/20)+4, 8 } = min{ floor(116/20)+4, 8 } = min{ 5+4, 8}=8.

Which UE shall be selected for SUMIMO is according to UE priority defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3246\_replaced([11155645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kswhHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: [Before CB014465-A]Since capacity verification for 2 symbols/slot is not covered in CB010887-D, it has not been decided whether limitation is taken into account per symbol or per slot. L1 limitation is done per slot in CB010887-D. That is to say, maxNumOfPSrsPerSymbol and maxNumSuMimoPerSlot are used as L1 resource limitation per slot in L2PS in CB010887-D. Description and/or implementation may need to be updated in the further feature. And this note can be deleted after updating.[CB014465-A]The SRS capacity is managed per slot even the slot can be configured with 2 pSRS symbols.[End CB014465-A]

---

##### 2.4.17.1.2.0-6 Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO Periodic wide band antenna switching SRS for DL Sub-band BM (ID: 11171173)

**2.4.17.1.2.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11171178`)

Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO
Overview

---

**2.4.17.1.2.0-6.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11171184`)

Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO
Period WB TAS Scheduling

---

**2.4.17.1.2.0-6.0-1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11171194`)

Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO
[CB009527-B] ( In eCPRI 7-2e (5G\_L2\_18130\_replaced([11155228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2432wvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),  extending the WB-SRS of suband-BF,  the SB/WB-SRS can both be  used for subband-BF.    Max Srs subband number  for  SB BF shall refer 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_14229\_replaced([11307007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tq5XCvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) )  [End CB009527-B]

[CB009527-A]  In eCPRI 7-2a (5G\_L2\_18130\_replaced([11155228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2432wvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), [End CB009527-A]

Since WB TAS will occupy whole BandWidth, in order to distinguish with SB TAS scheduling, L2-PS assign a special subbandId value(68) for WB TAS. When Schedule WB TAS UE, will get info list from subbandId 68 directly.

If UE just switch from SB SRS to WB SRS, in case not all ports of SRS resource received from the UE, L2-PS shall not ask L1H to calculate BWV for the UE to avoid SRS port power too lower.

L2-PS need to set LSB+3 to 1 for parameter "operation" in case it is first time to send srsBwv request for the UE to calculate subband beam.

---

**2.4.17.1.2.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `11171198`)

Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO
Periodic SRS Resource Distribution table

---

**2.4.17.1.2.0-6.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12419847`)

Periodic SRS for SU-MIMO
if UE is configured with pSRS(TAS/Codebook in usersetup\_NSA/HO or userModify\_SA) and dlShortTermBeamformingCapability = true in ueCapability, it shall be added/removed in the distribution table according to 5G\_L2\_12985\_replaced([11171210](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26MsePvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), Ue is added to the position where there is minimum UE number.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a flowchart illustrating a UE (User Equipment) distribution table. The table is organized into columns labeled with numbers from 0 to 6, and a final column labeled "rdPeriodSrsCheck-1".
Each numbered column contains one or more blue rectangular boxes, each labeled with "RNTI\_" followed by the column number and a letter (e.g., "RNTI\_0\_a", "RNTI\_1\_b"). Some columns have a hierarchical structure, with boxes connected by vertical lines. For instance, column 0 has "RNTI\_0\_a" connected to "RNTI\_0\_b", which is connected to "RNTI\_0\_c", followed by an ellipsis (...) and then "RNTI\_0\_x". Columns 1 and 2 show a similar two-level structure. Columns 4, 5, and 6 also have multi-level structures.
The last column, "rdPeriodSrsCheck-1", contains a single box labeled "RNTI\_rd-1\_a". An arrow originates from "RNTI\_6\_a" and points to a larger yellow box labeled "UE Context(RNTI\_XX)". Inside this yellow box, there is a smaller gray box containing the text "SrsResourceSwitchData", followed by an ellipsis (...), then several lines of text: "rrmMetricBufferHi", "rrmMetricBufferLo", "rrmMetricRlqGood", "rrmMetricRlqBad", another ellipsis (...), and finally "SrsState".
The overall diagram appears to represent a data structure or a system architecture where RNTI values are distributed across different categories or time periods, and each RNTI is associated with a UE Context containing specific data related to SRS (Sounding Reference Signal) resource switching.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4338.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note:

**1,    The cell capability of  max number of UEs that can be added to this table is determined by  rdMaxNumOfRtBeamCandidate  , detail refer 5G\_L2\_15947\_replaced([11182672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27dQwfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

2,    The size per slot can be determined by   rdMaxNumOfRtBeamCandidate  &  rdPeriodSrsCheck (5G\_UP\_ALG\_21349\_replaced([11158211](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktAYvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), example by default , 10 UE per slot.

3,     The size of table will be decided by  #UEs/slot \*  [Before CB012971-A ] rdPeriodSrsCheck [CB012971-A] rrmPeriodSrsCheck  ( see [11157342](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks65IfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB012971-A].
