###### 2.4.17.2.9.1.0-2 CL 8x4 MIMO periodic CSI decoding CSI decoding on PUCCH (ID: 11173065)

**2.4.17.2.9.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173068`)

CL 8x4 MIMO periodic CSI decoding
If dlMimoMode is set to ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4, L2-PS needs to support the decoding of UCI information on PUCCH (where PUCCH format 2 is always used) with the new CSI bit format specified by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3185\_replaced([11139392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmqi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For testing purpose, L2-PS also needs to support the CSI part overwritten of PUCCH format 2 based UCI according to rdDlRiPmi (5G\_L2\_1184) setting. In detail:

- In UL grant slot for UCI report on PUCCH, L2-PS needs to calculate the number of UCI bits with both A/N bit(s), SR bit and CSI bits counted and inform it to L1 via pucchUciConfig.numOfBitsOfUciInformation IE of UlData\_PucchReceiveReq message similar as before, but the number of CSI bits shall be calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3185\_replaced([11139392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmqi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For information type and number of information bits definition of supported PUCCH formats, refer to 5G\_L1\_5G21A\_1134.

- Upon UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs reception where UCI bits (uciBits) is expected, decode UCI bits normally, but for the decoding of CSI part of it, CSI format specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3185\_replaced([11139392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmqi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be followed.

- L2-PS needs to support the test mode specified by rdDlRiPmi also when the right most bit b0 is set to 1.

CP2 references: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3185\_replaced([11139392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmqi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3617\_replaced([11160013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktNNC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3626\_replaced([11160044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktN0EfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

###### 2.4.17.2.9.1.0-3 CL 8x4 MIMO periodic CSI decoding False CRC ok protection (ID: 11173073)

**2.4.17.2.9.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11173076`)

CL 8x4 MIMO periodic CSI decoding
L2-PS needs to support the False CRC ok protection functionality specified by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1996\_replaced([11112082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpS9ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note: similar mechanism for CSI for beam management is specified 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1992\_replaced([11112073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpS9Y_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), see CAS-276005-L7Z9 tagged content.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1996\_replaced([11112082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpS9ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

###### 2.4.17.2.9.1.0-4 CL 8x4 MIMO periodic CSI decoding Invalid LI value check (ID: 11173081)

**2.4.17.2.9.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11173086`)

CL 8x4 MIMO periodic CSI decoding
In case of CL 8x4 MIMO, L2-PS needs to support invalid LI value checking specified by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2205\_replaced([11113020](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Accordingly, following code logic needs to be applied before storing decoded LI value for later usage:

IF (LI > RI)

LI = 0

Print warning log to ease issue location

ENDIF

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2205\_replaced([11113020](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

###### 2.4.17.2.9.1.0-5 CL 8x4 MIMO periodic CSI decoding PMI pattern calculation based on CSI report (ID: 11173088)

**2.4.17.2.9.1.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11173099`)

CL 8x4 MIMO periodic CSI decoding
After periodic CSI is successfully decoded upon the reception of UlData\_PucchReceiveRespPs, L2-PS needs to calculate the pattern ID of the best PMI beam and 2nd best PMI beam (if reported) according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2194\_replaced([11112829](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2200\_replaced([11112965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and store it for later PDCCH / PDSCH scheduling usage.

[CB008133-B]

If cell is subject for massive MIMO muting for energy efficiency (as defined in 5G\_L3\_15819\_replaced([11022741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_A_Upnf_KEe-D3seUwaI0tg))) and cell is in power saving mode (PsCell\_PowerSavingConfigurationReq with energySavingState IE set to reducedEnergySaving was received) then:

startCsirsPmiPatternIdForNrLBmMimoRTXPowerSaving IE received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq message shall be used as startPatternId in pattern id calculation algorithm defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2194\_replaced([11112829](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

else

startCsirsPmiPatternId IE from PsCell\_CellSetupReq message shall be used as startPatternId.

[End CB008133-B]

Informatively, here is mapping between RI/i11/i12/i13 value reported by UE and PMI beam(s) deduced according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2194\_replaced([11112829](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and which beam is the best one in case of two PMI beams deduced. Note: “LI value” here refers to the LI value after Invalid LI value checking (5G\_L2\_788), where LI value will be forced to 0 if detected as invalid (i.e. decoded LI > RI).

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| If |  |  |  |  | Then |  |  |
| RI value | i11 value | i12 value | i13 value | LI value | Beam #1 reported? | Beam #2 reported? | Best beam ? |
| 0 | any | any (2x2) or n/a (4x1) | n/a | n/a | Yes | No | Beam #1 |
| 1 | Any | any (2x2) or n/a (4x1) | any | 0 | Yes | Yes | Beam #1 |
| 1 | Any | any (2x2) or n/a (4x1) | any | 1 | Yes | Yes | Beam #2 |
| 2 | Any | any (2x2) or n/a (4x1) | any | 0,2 | Yes | Yes | Beam #1 |
| 2 | Any | any (2x2) or n/a (4x1) | any | 1 | Yes | Yes | Beam #2 |
| 3 | any | any (2x2) or n/a (4x1) | any | 0,2 | Yes | Yes | Beam #1 |
| 3 | any | any (2x2) or n/a (4x1) | any | 1,3 | Yes | Yes | Beam #2 |

Both the sequence of two PMI beams and which one is the best and which one is the second best shall be stored in UE context, e.g. it could defined as pmiBeam[] array with size of 2:

pmiBeam[0] => first PMI beam

pmiBeam[1] => second PMI beam

pmiBeam structure content:

> patternID, u16, Pattern ID, set to 0xFFFF if valid PMI bream is not available

> bestOr2ndBest, bool, 0 - best PMI beam, 1 - 2nd best PMI beam

A dedicated flag (e.g. isPerCsiValid) needs to be defined to indicate whether UE’s periodic CSI reporting is valid or not:

- isPerCsiValid is initialized to FALSE after user setup

- After 1st periodic CSI for channel state feedback is successfully decoded, set isPerCsiValid to TRUE

- In case of periodic CSI for channel state feedback is outdated, e.g. after valid periodic CSI report is not received for a prolong time period, set isPerCsiValid to FALSE

- When periodic CSI for channel state feedback is successfully decoded again, set isPerCsiValid to TRUE

When retransmission is taken into consideration, PMI beam information needs also be stored per UE per reported Rank according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3185\_replaced([11139392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmqi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). And, it is not allowed to reset the patternID of PMI beam(s) used for ReTx to 0xFFFF when periodic CSI for channel state feedback is outdated, the reason is it is due to be used for retransmission that happens then after.

CP2 references: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2194\_replaced([11112829](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2200\_replaced([11112965](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpZrF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3185\_replaced([11139392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmqi_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

###### 2.4.17.2.9.1.0-6 CL 8x4 MIMO periodic CSI decoding Invalid PMI value check (ID: 11173104)

**2.4.17.2.9.1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11173107`)

CL 8x4 MIMO periodic CSI decoding
In case of CL 8x4 MIMO, if actCellCsirsTddOnSsbBf = true, L2-PS needs to support invalid PMI value checking specified by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9691\_replaced([11113298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpa5PfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Accordingly, following code logic needs to be applied before storing decoded LI value for later usage:

IF (i1,1 != 0 OR 4 OR 8 OR 12)

i1,1 and i1,3 and i2 and LI and RI is dropped.

Print warning log to ease issue location

ENDIF

Whenever PMI is dropped or not, CQI can be used in LA.

CP2 reference: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9691\_replaced([11113298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpa5PfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

#### 2.4.17.2.10 Multiplexing CSI Reports on PUCCH

##### 2.4.17.2.10.0-1 Multiplexing CSI Reports on PUCCH Identify Multiplexing CSI Reports (ID: 11173117)

**2.4.17.2.10.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173121`)

Multiplexing CSI Reports on PUCCH
If multiplex CSI reports are identified (5G\_UP\_ALG\_3639\_replaced([11160485](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktObIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), pucchResourceId (as defined by 3GPP 38.331) of multiplex PUCCH resource should be specified in multiCsiPucchResourceIdList (5G\_L2\_IF\_406\_replaced([10975219](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_3Cfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and corresponding PUCCH parameters should be specified in csiPucchResourceList (5G\_L2\_IF\_406\_replaced([10975219](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_3Cfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

NOTE: For multiplex reports, 5G-CP-RT shall use the same PUCCH resource which can be identified by pucchResourceId in multiCsiPucchResourceIdList.

If CSI reports are multiplexed but multiCsiPucchResourceIdList is empty, that is a failure case and shall be handled in 5G-L2-PS by rejecting the UserSetup or UserModify.

---

#### 2.4.17.2.11 Receiving first CSI Report

##### 2.4.17.2.11.0-1 Receiving first CSI Report Optimized Flow Control (ID: 11173130)

**2.4.17.2.11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173133`)

Receiving first CSI Report
When actOptimizedFlowControl = OptimizedAllUe and first CSI Report for given UE context is received, L2-PS shall recalculate estimated throughput (eTh) value (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4676\_replaced([11192682](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvmZtvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Calculated values:

 shall be sent to L2-LO using LoCtrl\_DlUuStatusUpdateInd message (5G\_L2\_IF\_4305\_replaced([10985413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2WH4Yfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 stored in UE context as lastSentEth to be used in 5G\_L2\_10486

L2-PS shall start rdEthUpdateTimer (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15747\_replaced([11193522](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvtucfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

##### 2.4.17.2.12 PCSI/BM report prcossing reduction to mitigate overload for FR1

###### 2.4.17.2.12.0-1 PCSI/BM report prcossing reduction to mitigate overload for FR1 No DL data detection (ID: 11173142)

**2.4.17.2.12.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173147`)

PCSI/BM report prcossing reduction to mitigate overload for FR1
To identify low priority UE which is not static UE as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29639\_replaced([11159298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), L2PS should record UE's PCell DL buffer status to check if there's no DL DRB buffer. The buffer change is recorded on LoCtrl\_DlBufferStatusInd as 5G\_L2\_IF\_869\_replaced([10984606](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2WBKwfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if the DRB data buffer is changed from zero to non-zero with bufferedBytes for each DRB. The buffer change is also recorded after PDSCH scheduling if the DRB data buffer is changed from non-zero to zero. So on every opportunity of UlData\_PucchReceiveReq for the UE, L2-PS can check how long is the UE's no DL DRB data duration.

If the reducing PCSI processing is enabled as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29643\_replaced([11159287](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and UE's low pri status due to no DL data or not as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29639\_replaced([11159298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the TA Grant timer should be changed as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4845\_replaced([11195726](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv-NIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: The UE's DL DRB buffer detection should be started on the time of its first RACH success.

---

###### 2.4.17.2.12.0-2 PCSI/BM report prcossing reduction to mitigate overload for FR1 Control the ratio of PCSI/BM report recieve request reduction (ID: 11173150)

**2.4.17.2.12.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `12790380`)

PCSI/BM report prcossing reduction to mitigate overload for FR1
If reducing PCSI processing is enabled as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29643\_replaced([11159287](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for low pri UE as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29639\_replaced([11159298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUj_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), L2PS should skip sending its UlData\_PucchReceiveReq to L1 for PCSI/BM report per according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29748\_replaced([11159309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktI7kPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The reduction ratio is according R&D parameter in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29641\_replaced([11160716](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPpTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), with some possible deviation according L2PS implementation as bellow:

Define a control *counter with range* [0:3] for each PCSI/BM report on each serving cell which is not skipping excluded by SR multiplexing in*[12571130: pCSI skipping Exclusion conditions for a UE in a serving cell](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_RlTCoS8EEfCRMdP0Nuo3Hg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for each UE, which is initialize as 3.*

For each value of rdCsiReportSkippingPercent in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29641\_replaced([11160716](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktPpTvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), define an increment step(*incStep) and a decrement step(*decStep) and skipping oppoertunity as bellow table:\*\*

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **rdCsiReportSkippingPercent** | **incStep** | **decStep** | **Skipping oppotunity** |
| 25 | 3 | 1 | counter = 0 |
| 50 | 3 | 3 | counter = 0 |
| 75 | 1 | 3 | counter < 3 |

On the opportunity for UlData\_PucchReceiveReq for UE's PCSI/BM report for each serving cell:

1. whenever reducing PCSI processing is disabled as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29643\_replaced([11159287](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), or the UE is not a low pri UE, counter shall be set as 3;

2.  when reducing PCSI processing is enabled as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29643\_replaced([11159287](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktIUjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and UE becames a low pri UE, if *counter value indicates a skipping opportunity as the table, then the UlData\_PucchReceiveReq for PCSI/BM report can be skipped.*

1. If the UlData\_PucchReceiveReq for PCSI/BM report is skipped, *counter =* counter + *incStep; and increase counter of 5G\_L2\_22153 by 1, and count the DTX for RLF according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8216\_replaced([11151674](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksbJ4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));*\*\*
2. If the the UlData\_PucchReceiveReq is not on the skipping opportunity, or if the UlData\_PucchReceiveReq is on the skipping opportunity but PCSI/BM report not skipped due to HARQ-D multiplexing or PUSCH scheduling(see note 3), c*ounter =* counter - *decStep if the result from L1 is nonDTX and the result is not dropped due to overload(see note 4);*\*\*
3. If DTX(see note 2) or unsuccessful PCSI/BM report due to overload or report/CSI-RS overlap with DRX/GAP, *counter =* counter + *incStep;*\*\*
4. Any transient phase including 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3490\_replaced([11159137](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktHtc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) due to reconfiguration, reset the counter to 3, and the skipped UlData\_PucchReceiveReq due to transient phase shall be ignored;
5. If BWP switch as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7857\_replaced([11111112](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKaifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), reset the counter for PCSI/BM to 3; and the skipped UlData\_PucchReceiveReq shall be ignored;
6. If the UlData\_PucchReceiveReq is for SCell, when the SCell is changed to be active from deactive, reset the counter for the SCell to 3.
7. After [Before CB014301-AA] UE PCell's down tilt is change as recorded [CB014301-AA] online modification of beamset NIDD parameters [End CB014301-AA]  in [12582669: Handling online modification of beamset parameters](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0nFmoTSEEfCKaNlcHOQWmg), before receiving first valid beam report for a serving cell which is skipping PCSI, stop skipping and reset the counter to 3.

**Note:**

1. *counter shall be kept in range of [0:3], so the calculation should be limited lower and upper value as 0/3, e.g, if* counter = 3, *counter =* counter + 3 will get the result as *counter = 3.*\*\*\*\*
2. When UL CA is active for the UE, if UlData\_PucchReceiveReq for PCSI/BM is sent to L1 but gets DTX due to PUSCH scheduling on SCell, the DTX should not be counted as a real DTX;
3. When UL CA is active for the UE, if on the ovelapped Ul slots, the SCell's PUSCH scheduling is not known by PCell when deciding if UlData\_PucchReceiveReq for PCSI/BM can be skipped, PCell assume PUSCH is not scheduled on UL SCell;
4. When overload PCSI/BM on PUSCH may be dropped, such dropping is ignored by the counter.

---

## 2.4.18 Broadcast Control Channel Management

### 2.4.18.0-1 Broadcast Control Channel Management FA: [Internal Domain] Common Channel Document Owner: TBD (WRO) (ID: 11173163)

### 2.4.18.1 MIB

### 2.4.18.2 SIB

#### 2.4.18.2.1 PDSCH SIB1 Slot Selection

**2.4.18.2.1.0-1**  (ID: `11173184`)

PDSCH SIB1 Slot Selection
SIB1 Slot selection:

SIB1 Slot selection details are explained as part of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3706\_replaced([11164184](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kteSwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For the step  : “slotSib10 is already used by previous SS/PBCH” : In case of SIB1 Slot mapping type = Type 1 - There would be no overlap between the previous-SIB1 n0+1 and current-SIB1 n (overlap at slot level).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays three different types of arrangements of cells, labeled "Type 1", "Type 2", and "Type 3". Each cell contains either "n0" or "n0+1". To the left of these arrangements, there are labels "SIB1n+3", "SIB1n+2", "SIB1n+1", and "SIB1n", arranged vertically.
**Type 1:**
This type consists of a staircase-like arrangement.
- The bottom row has two cells: "n0" and "n0+1".
- The row above it has one cell: "n0".
- The row above that has one cell: "n0+1".
- The top row has one cell: "n0".
**Type 2:**
This type also has a staircase-like structure, but with a different configuration.
- The bottom row has two cells: "n0" and "n0+1".
- The row above it has two cells: "n0" and "n0+1".
- The row above that has one cell: "n0".
- The top row has one cell: "n0+1".
**Type 3:**
This type is arranged in a 2x2 grid with an additional cell below.
- The top row has two cells: "n0" and "n0+1".
- The row below it has two cells: "n0" and "n0+1".
- The row below that has two cells: "n0+1" and "n0+1".
The labels on the left seem to indicate different states or versions related to the cell arrangements.
The image can be interpreted as representing different data structures or configurations, possibly in a computing or mathematical context, where "n0" and "n0+1" represent values or indices.
The text in the image is:
SIB1n+3
SIB1n+2
SIB1n+1
SIB1n
Type 1
Type 2
Type 3
The cell contents can be represented in markdown tables as follows:
**Type 1:**

| n0 | n0+1 |
| --- | --- |
| n0 | n0+1 |
| n0 |  |
| n0+1 |  |
| n0 |  |
| **Type 2:** |  |

| n0 | n0+1 |
| --- | --- |
| n0 | n0+1 |
| n0 | n0+1 |
| n0 |  |
| **Type 3:** |  |

| n0 | n0+1 |
| --- | --- |
| n0 | n0+1 |
| n0+1 | n0+1 |

Ex: FR2 SA with type0SearchSpaceConfigurationIndex=13 will use Type 1 SIB 1 slot mapping - In this scenario there would be no overlap between the previous-SIB1 n0+1 and current-SIB1 n

---

#### 2.4.18.2.2 PDSCH SIB1 transmissions

##### 2.4.18.2.2.0-3 PDSCH SIB1 transmissions SIB1 frequency and time domain setting (ID: 11173211)

**2.4.18.2.2.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11173249`)

PDSCH SIB1 transmissions
The 5G-L2-PS sets the frequency and time domain configuration for the SIB1 transmisisons as follows:

 The parameter “startSymbol” is set to start symbol of the Time Domain Resource Allocation  selected for the SIB1 transmission and defined in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3703\_replaced([11164172](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kteSwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 The parameter “numOfPdschSymbols” is set to length of the Time Domain Resource Allocation  selected for the SIB1 transmission and defined in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3703\_replaced([11164172](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kteSwPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 The parameter “startPrb” indicates the absolute position of the SIB1 transmission and takes into account the position of the CORESET(0) within the whole bandwidth. The first MSB bit set to 1 ([image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "msbPos" in a blurry, light blue font against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4677.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]) in the coresetFreqDomain parameter of the CORESET(0) and the RB shift ([image-1][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text that reads "coresetFreqDomainRblShlfc".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4677.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]) are received in the PsCell\_CellSetupReq message.

The starting PRB of the SIB1 transmission(s) is computed in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3748\_replaced([11165244](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktgH-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If actFdmSchedulingInitialBwp is True and cell parameter sibStartPrbRandomDisable is false , the starting RB of SIB1 shall be randomly selected with adjusted to multiples of minRbservedForNonSibPaging.

For example, CORESET#0 has 48 PRBs, SIB1 use 20 PRBs, this means the max value of 'startPrb' is 28. Meanwhile we configure minRbservedForNonSibPaging as 6 which is received in the PsCell\_CellSetupReq message. In this case, the startRbSib1 shall be randomly seleced from values of {0,6,12,18,24}

[image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical formula written in a blurry, light gray font against a white background. The formula is:
`startPrb = 6 × (63 – msbPos) + coreSetFreqDomainRbShift + startRbSib1_ssPbch`[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4677.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

 The parameter “numOfPrb” is set to number of RB compute for the SIB1 and saved in the SIB1 context as defined in the 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3716\_replaced([11164922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kte50_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) requirement.

 The parameter “rat0Bitmap” is set to 0x0000, as only the resource allocation type 1 is used for the SIB1 transmissions.

Note: Above contents refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13114\_replaced([11165385](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktgvAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

if actRat1Alloc is true, refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24003\_replaced([11165336](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktgH_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PDSCH resource allocation of SIB1.

---

##### 2.4.18.2.2.0-5 PDSCH SIB1 transmissions Low Density Parity Check configuration (ID: 12811410)

**2.4.18.2.2.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12811414`)

PDSCH SIB1 transmissions
The 5G-L2-PS sets Low Density Parity Check configuration, gets the following parameters through the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

[Before CB008949-A]

The parameter “baseGraph” is set to 2.

 The parameter “numOfCodeBlocks”.

 The parameter “codeBlockSize”.

 The parameter “numOfFillerBits”.

 The parameter “liftSize”.

 The parameter “liftSizeSetIndex”.

 The parameter “liftSizeColumnIndex”.

[CB008949-A]

The parameter “baseGraph” (BG) is set to 2.

 The parameter “numOfCodeBlocks” (C).

 The parameter “codeBlockSize” (K).

 The parameter “numOfFillerBits” (F).

 The parameter “liftSize” (Z).

 The parameter “liftSizeSetIndex” (set\_index).

 The parameter “liftSizeColumnIndex” (column\_index).

[End CB008949-A]

---

**2.4.18.2.2.0-10**  (ID: `11173281`)

PDSCH SIB1 transmissions
The 5G-L2-PS applies the following setting for beamforming management:

 numCeAxcIndex. The value corresponds to the number of Radio Unit Antenna ports available for the cell (2 or 4).

 ceAxcIndex. The Value corresponds to the indexes of Antenna-Carrier (AxC) containers selected for the PDSCH transmission in the cell between 0 to numCeAxcIndex-1.

 patternId. The value corresponds to the index of the pattern associated to the beam Index of the selected SS/PBCH for the transmission.

For CPRI configuration, the beamforming management is done by sending the DlData\_PatternConfigReq.

[5GC002200-A]

If dlMimoMode is CL 8x4 MIMO, only C4 is supported for subCell type. One x pol beam is used for SIB1 transmission. More detail of CPRI cells refer to 5G\_RAN\_L2\_497. The value of pattern ID for both CPRI and eCPRI corresponds to the index of the pattern associated to the beam Index of the selected SS/PBCH for the transmission.

For CPRI configuration, the beamforming management is done by sending the DlData\_PatternConfigReq.

numOfXpolBeams andpatternIdPolList shall be set according to 5G\_L2\_497.

[End 5GC002200-A]

[5GC002200-F]If dlMimoMode is CL 8x4 MIMO and for the eCPRI configuration, the parameters in streamConfig structure are common for CPRI and eCPRI. But for eCPRI only the 1st xpol stream shall be used. Note: FDM has no impact to streams of eCPRI and MU MIMO does not work for SIB.[End 5GC002200-F]

---

### 2.4.18.3 OSI

#### 2.4.18.3.1 Slot selection for SI message transmission

##### 2.4.18.3.1.0-1 Slot selection for SI message transmission OSI slot selection algorithm 5G_UP_ALG_3804_replaced( 11166172 ), 3805 explains the PDCCH occasion identification and eligible slot selection within the SI window. While selecting the slot that is eligible for the SI transmission:The slot selected should not be configured to handle SIB1 transmissions:-  While Identifying and ignoring slots configured for SIB1 – both n0 and n0+1 of the activated and not deactivated SIB1 slot need to be ignored for OSI transmission. (ID: 11173298)

#### 2.4.18.3.2 PDSCH OSI transmissions

##### 2.4.18.3.2.0-3 PDSCH OSI transmissions OSI frequency and time domain setting (ID: 11173317)

**2.4.18.3.2.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11173361`)

PDSCH OSI transmissions
The 5G-L2-PS sets the frequency and time domain configuration for the SI-Msg transmisisons as follows:

 The parameter “startSymbol” is set to start symbol of the Time Domain Resource Allocation  selected for the SI-Msg transmission and defined in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3825\_replaced([11166424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktlAePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 The parameter “numOfPdschSymbols” is set to length of the Time Domain Resource doors://esdooapv51.emea.nsn-net.net:36655/?version=2&prodID=0&view=00000002&urn=urn:telelogic::1-4017b9fe29051a61-O-9951-00025ecaAllocation  selected for the SI-Msg transmission and defined in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3825\_replaced([11166424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktlAePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 The parameter “startPrb” indicates the absolute position of the SI-Msg transmission and takes into account the position of the CORESET(0) within the whole bandwidth. The first MSB bit set to 1 ([image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the text "msbPos" in a blurry, light blue font against a white background.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4694.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]) in the coresetFreqDomain parameter of the CORESET(0) and the RB shift ([image-1][image\_desc]The image contains blurry text that reads "coresetFreqDomainRblShlfc".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4694.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]) are received in the PsCell\_CellSetupReq message.

The starting PRB of the SI-Msg transmission(s) is computed in the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3827\_replaced([11166434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktlAgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If actFdmSchedulingInitialBwp is True  and cell parameter sibStartPrbRandomDisable is false , the starting PRB of the SI-Msg shall be randomly selected with adjusted to multiples of minRbservedForNonSibPaging.

For example, CORESET#0 has 48 PRBs, SI-MSG use 20 PRBs, this means the max value of startRbSiMsg is 28. Meanwhile we configure minRbservedForNonSibPaging as 6 which is received in the PsCell\_CellSetupReq message. In this case, the starting PRB of the SI-Msg shall be randomly seleced from values of {0,6,12,18,24}

[image-2][image\_desc]startPrb = 6 × (63 – msbPos) + coresetFreqDomainRbShift + startRbSimsg[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4694.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

 The parameter “numOfPrb” is set to number of RB compute for the SI-Msg and saved in the SI-Msg context as defined in the 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3827\_replaced([11166434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktlAgPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) requirement.

 The parameter “rat0Bitmap” is set to 0x0000, as only the resource allocation type 1 is used for the SI-Msg transmissions.

Note: Above contents refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13718\_replaced([11166490](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktlnhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

if actRat1Alloc is true, refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24004\_replaced([11166457](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktlAhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PDSCH resource allocation of SI Messages.

---

##### 2.4.18.3.2.0-6 PDSCH OSI transmissions Low Density Parity Check configuration (ID: 12811426)

**2.4.18.3.2.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `12811432`)

PDSCH OSI transmissions
The 5G-L2-PS sets the following parameter for Low Density Parity Check configuration through the requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6874\_replaced([11192598](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvlypfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

[Before CB008949-A]

he parameter “baseGraph” to 2.

 The parameter “numOfCodeBlocks”.

 The parameter “codeBlockSize”.

 The parameter “numOfFillerBits”.

 The parameter “liftSizeSetIndex”.

**2.4.18.3.2.0-11**  (ID: `11173402`)

PDSCH OSI transmissions
For the eCPRI configuration, the following parameters are defined for beamforming management.

 numCeAxcIndex. The value corresponds to the number of Radio Unit Antenna ports available for the cell (2 or 4).

 ceAxcIndex. The Value corresponds to the indexes of Antenna-Carrier (AxC) containers selected for the PDCCH transmission in the cell between 0 to numCeAxcIndex-1.

 patternId. The value corresponds to the index of the pattern associated to the beam Index of the selected SS/PBCH for the transmission.

For CPRI configuration, the beamforming management is done by sending the DlData\_PatternConfigReq.

---

#### 2.4.18.4 Scheduling SS/PBCH blocks for above 6 GHz

###### 2.4.18.4.1 Configured, actived, muted SS/PBCH blocks

###### 2.4.18.4.1-1 Configured, actived, muted SS/PBCH blocks

**2.4.18.4.1-1.0-2**  (ID: `12565098`)

Configured, actived, muted SS/PBCH blocks
If 1 SS block active is active, then L2 PS shall set the activeSSBlocks and threeLsbSsbIndex in the following way:

* activeSsBlocks = 0x80
* threeLsbSsbIndex = 0

Valid for first slot only (slot 0 due to 0-based numbering).

Note:

1. Currently supported number of active SS blocks is indicated by rrmAbfSsbConf, which is set to either 0, 1 [CB008453-C], 20 [End CB008453-C] or 32. This requirement covers '0' and '1' cases.

---

###### 2.4.18.4.1-2 Configured, actived, muted SS/PBCH blocks

**2.4.18.4.1-2.0-5**  (ID: `12787024`)

Configured, actived, muted SS/PBCH blocks
If more than 1 SS block is active and ssBlockConfiguration equals to Case E (240 kHz), then L2 PS shall determine the activeSSBlocks and threeLsbSsbIndex in the following way:

For each sn120 slot in range [0..7], [10..17]:

* if slot is even AND related groupPresence is active then
* 4 MSB of activeSSBlocks shall be copied from 4 MSB of inOneGroup. **Additionally, each active bit from inOneGroup shall be compared with corresponding value from beamsMuted bitmask. In case of muted SSB, activeSSBlocks bit shall be set to 0.**
* threeLsbSsbIndex shall be set to 0
* if slot is odd AND related groupPresence is active then
* 4 MSB of activeSSBlocks shall be copied from 4 LSB of inOneGroup Additionally, each active bit from inOneGroup shall be compared with corresponding value from beamsMuted bitmask. In case of muted SSB, activeSSBlocks bit shall be set to 0.
* threeLsbSsbIndex shall be set to 4
* 4 LSB of activeSSBlocks shall be set to 0 in both cases.
* In case of all activeSSBlocks bits are set to 0, L2-PS shall not send out DlData\_SsBlockSendReq to L1.

where:

* Related groupPresence is active if:
* groupPresence[FLOOR(slot/2)] equals to 1 for slots in [0..7] range.
* groupPresence[FLOOR((slot-2)/2)] equals to 1 for slots in [10..17] range.
* Mapping between beamsMuted and SSB opportunity has been described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2237\_replaced([11113810](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpd8j_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Retrieving indices of muted beams and corresponding SSB opportunities'. [CB014301-B] In case of beamsMuted online modification, L2-PS shall receive PsCell\_BeamConfigUpdateReq from CP-RT ([12536044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_wLou8R9PEfCQJ5daVHVkgg)).[End CB014301-B]

Notes:

1. Slots are numbered in 120 kHz numerology, whereas ssBlockConfiguration is equal to 240 kHz. As a result, one DlData\_SsBlockSendReq (i slot) covers two 240 kHz slots (2*i and 2*i+1).
2. Currently supported number of active SS blocks is indicated by rrmAbfSsbConf, which is set to either 0, 1, 20 or 32. This requirement covers non-0 and non-1 cases.
3. Current implementation supports configurations of 32 or 56 SSBs, described in 5G\_UP\_94\_replaced([11347275](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubLB_PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) 'Time-Domain Allocation of SSBs for TDD FR2'.

---

**2.4.18.4.1.0-1**  (ID: `11173414`)

Configured, actived, muted SS/PBCH blocks
The IE numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks received in PsCell\_CellGroupSetupReq will be used as nActivatedSsPbch.

In the specification,

nConfiguredSsPbch :

 The configured SS/PBCH correspond to the ones configured in the UE by RRC (see 5G\_UP\_8335\_replaced([11347128](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

nActivatedSsPbch :

 The active SS/PBCH (see 5G\_UP\_7805\_replaced([11347149](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubKa4fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are the configured ones which are associated to a specific physical radio beam specified by the beamset configuration and to a dedicated configuration in the RRC connected UE in order to be a part of reference signals.

nConfiguredSsPbch >= nActivatedSsPbch

nMutedSsPbch :

 The number of muted SS/PBCH.

Ref: 5G\_UP\_82\_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

## 2.4.19 Public Warning System Management

### 2.4.19.0-1 Public Warning System Management FA: [Internal Domain] Common Channel Document Owner: TBD (WRO) (ID: 11173448)

## 2.4.20 Paging

### 2.4.20.0-2 Paging Initial PCCH Config reception and management (ID: 11173461)

**2.4.20.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11173474`)

Paging
5G-L2-PS shall expect Paging configuration only in case of SA Cell (cellDepType equals SA or both in PsCell\_CellSetupReq, see 5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

5G-L2-PS receives initial PCCH Config and Modification Period via PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq (5G\_L2\_IF\_2332\_replaced([10970613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UjyLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) from 5G-CP-RT. PCCH configuration is per cell basis and can be different in different cells managed by 5G-L2-PS.

5G-L2-PS obtains firstPdcchMonitoringOccasionOfPo if configured from pdcchConfigCommon structure within PsCell\_CellSetupReq message, which is only used for paging in dedicated BWP.

If 5G-L2-PS does not receive PCCH Config in PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq from 5G-CP-RT, 5G-L2-PS shall not trigger any action related to RRC paging and/or short message.

The received initial PCCH Config shall be validated as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3918\_replaced([11167942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kts8SPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If validation is not passed,  5G-L2-PS shall set corresponding fields of PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationResp as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7928\_replaced([11224070](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kyFF-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If validation on PCCH Config is passed, 5G-L2-PS shall maintain 2 Paging contexts per cell, and one is active at a time, and received initial PCCH Config shall be applied immediately and stored into active Paging context.

[CB010774-A] L2-PS shall also include maxNumPagingRecordsConfigured in PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationResp if needed according to description in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7784\_replaced([11167846](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktsVPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB010774-A]

---

### 2.4.20.0-3 Paging RRC Paging and system information change reception from 5G-CP-RT (ID: 11173478)

**2.4.20.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11173495`)

Paging
5G-L2-PS receives PsSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq message (5G\_L2\_IF\_571\_replaced([10978552](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VUnJfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with cell list where cells may belong to same or different cell group (in case more than one cell group is configured) from 5G-CP-RT for scheduling for RRC Paging and/or Short Message on those cells. 5G-L2-PS shall forward incoming PsSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq to multiple cell group scheduler if incoming message cell list has cells from different cell groups.

If pagingInd is present  within PsSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq message, this implies there is RRC paging to be scheduled:

[CB012544-C]

for eDRX paging, paging cycle/paging PF/PO calculation handling defined by 5G\_L2\_18802;

for non eDRX paging,  [End CB012544-C]

If PsSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq contains UE specific DRX value then L2-PS shall use the same for scheduling. Otherwise default paging DRX value shall be used which is received during cell setup [5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))].

5G-L2-PS derives the destination cells for the paging request. For each destination cell 5G-L2-PS calculates the paging occasion for the paging request as per [Before CB012544-C]5G\_UP\_ALG\_3918\_replaced([11167942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kts8SPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[CB012544-C]5G\_UP\_ALG\_11720\_replaced([11167694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktruIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB012544-C] and schedules the paging request during DL slot scheduling as per 5G\_L2\_823.

L2-PS shall schedule only one time [Before CB012544-C](SFN and slot computed as per 5G_UP_ALG_3918_replaced([11167942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kts8SPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB012544-C] in one beam and shall schedule in all not muted beams. L2-PS shall delete paging message after paging scheduling finishes for all not muted  beams.

If 5G-L2-PS does not receive PsSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq message from 5G-CP-RT (e.g. in case of CellDepType=NSA within PsCell\_CellSetupReq message, 5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall not trigger any action related to RRC paging and/or Short Message.

---

### 2.4.20.0-4 Paging Target Paging Frame and Paging Occasion selection for a paging request using UE-specific DRX cycle (ID: 11173500)

**2.4.20.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11173507`)

Paging
When the paging request has several target PF/PO buffers (2, 4 or 8) [CB008225-C-UPD001] and actPagingEnh=‘postponedPaging’ within PsCell\_CellSetupReq message(see 5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then[End CB008225-C-UPD001]5G-L2-PS applies the below algorithm. Subsequent paging requests are processed by this algorithm in order of entries in pagingRecord structure of PsSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq message (5G\_L2\_IF\_571\_replaced([10978552](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VUnJfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

This requirement completes 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11733\_replaced([11167613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktrHFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[docx\_desc]@startuml
Start
: Paging request of the UE \n from PsSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq<
if (\nUE-Specific DRX cycle < Default paging cycle ?\n) then (no)
:Executes handling for \nDefault paging cycle\n(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11731);
stop
else (yes)
:Takes the first PF/PO candidate \nfor the Paging request of the UE;
repeat
if (\nThe PF/PO buffer is already full ?\n) then (yes)
:Identifies the oldest paging record with \nthe lowest priority which is lower than \nthe priority of the paging request;
if (\nAn older Paging Record with lower\npriority is identified ?\n) then (yes)
:Removes the oldest paging record \nand stores the new paging \nrequest in the PF/PO buffer;
Stop
else (no)
endif
else (no)
:Stores the new paging\nrequest in the PF/PO buffer;
Stop
Endif
Backward: Takes the next PF/PO candidate;
repeat while (\nThe number of target PF/PO\nbuffers is reached ?\n) is (no)
->yes;
:Takes the first PF/PO candidate \nfor the Paging request of the UE;
repeat
:Identifies the oldest paging \nrecord with the same priority;
if (\nAn older Paging Record with the same\npriority is identified ?\n) then (yes)
:Removes the oldest paging record\nand stores the new paging\nrequest in the PF/PO buffer;
Stop
else (no)
endif
Backward: Takes the next PF/PO candidate;
repeat while (\nThe number of target PF/PO\nbuffers is reached ?\n) is (no)
->yes;
:Discards the processed \nincoming Paging request;
endif
stop
@enduml
[/docx\_desc][docx\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/attachments/m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file4726\_0.docx[/docx\_path]

Note1. 5G-L2-PS shall remove the paging record with the same paging priority only if it has older time stamp than the currently processed paging request thus it's impossible to remove an earlier stored paging request with the same priority received in the same PsSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq message.

---

### 2.4.20.0-5 Paging No PCCH config in PsCell_SystemInfoConfigurationReq (ID: 11173512)

**2.4.20.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11173519`)

Paging
If 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq (5G\_L2\_IF\_2332\_replaced([10970613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UjyLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) from 5G-CP-RT, which does not include PCCH Config (e.g. [Before 5GC001296-A]  in case of CellwithSib1 within PsCell\_CellSetupReq message is FALSE [5GC001296-A] in case of cellDepType = NSA within PsCell\_CellSetupReq message [End 5GC001296-A], see 5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall not trigger any action related to RRC paging and/or short message, including paging related parameter validation process refers to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3918\_replaced([11167942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kts8SPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.20.0-6 Paging Support of multiple paging messages in one slot (ID: 11173523)

**2.4.20.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11173528`)

Paging
When Common Search Space of Type-2 has more than one PMO (PDCCH monitoring occasion) within slot it is possible to schedule more than one paging message in slot, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12038\_replaced([11167698](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktruI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

[CB010774-A-UPD002]

PDL rule ensures that monitoringSymbolsWithinSlot can be set to value which indicates more than one PMO only when actPagingSsbOptimization is equal to twoBeamsPerSlot for common search space for Type2-PDCCH.

[End CB010774-A-UPD002]

If monitoringSymbolsWithinSlot indicates one PMO and actPagingSsbOptimization is equal to twoBeamsPerSlot, then L2-PS shall print warning in syslog and continue with one PMO per slot.

Note:

For monitoringSymbolsWithinSlot see 5G\_L2\_IF\_84\_replaced([10968165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UW90_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For actPagingSsbOptimization see 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (PsCell\_CellSetupReq).

---

### 2.4.20.0-7 Paging Scheduling UEs for paging messages (ID: 11173532)

**2.4.20.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11173535`)

Paging
[5GC000679-B] Not valid from this onwards for TDD. Decision on scheduling UEs is done in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4292\_replaced([11177281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End 5GC000679-B]

[CB005892-AC, CB005892-A] Also not valid for FDD. Decision on scheduling UEs is done in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4292\_replaced([11177281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuuP-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB005892-AC, CB005892-A].

During DL slot processing 5G-L2-PS shall check if there is paging message to be scheduled in current slot as received in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3908\_replaced([11167780](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktsVMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Based on paging scheduling use case as given below, 5G-L2-PS shall also decide if UE scheduling is possible in the slot.

Case 1: Paging for RRC\_IDLE UE (DCI 1\_0 in PDCCH for P-RNTI + paging data in PDSCH in initial BWP)

This use case is referred as RRC paging. In this case, L2-PS shall not schedule UE in PDSCH in the slot where paging is scheduled due to current L1 limitation of scheduling one RNTI in PDSCH.

Case 2: Paging for SI change notification (only DCI 1\_0 in PDCCH for P-RNTI in initial BWP; no PDSCH)

This use case is referred as Short Message. In this case, L2-PS shall not schedule UE in PDSCH in the slot where Short Message is scheduled.

Case 3: RRC Paging and Short Message both gets scheduled in a slot.

In that case, 5G-L2-PS shall not schedule UE in PDSCH in that slot due to current L1 limitation of scheduling one RNTI in PDSCH.

If 5G-L2-PS schedules RRC Paging and/or Short Message in the slot, then 5G-L2-PS shall send PDCCH/PDSCH information to 5G-L1-DL as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3917\_replaced([11167923](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kts8RfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.20.0-8 Paging RRC Paging and Short Message transmission to 5G-L1-DL (ID: 11173540)

**2.4.20.0-8.0-8**  (ID: `11173574`)

Paging
In case 5G-L2-PS schedules only Short Message (SI change notification) then 5G-L2-PS shall include one DCI 1\_0 for P-RNTI inside DlData\_PdcchSendReq message (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_73) and send to 5G-L1-DL. In this case no PDSCH resource shall be allocated. See MSC for SI change at 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_247\_replaced([11194202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfohevvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Paging message shall be mapped to the same subcell which was used for transmission of relevant SSB.

In case 5G-L2-PS schedules RRC-IDLE or RRC-INACTIVE UE paging (with or without Short Message) then 5G-L2-PS shall pack one DCI 1\_0 for P-RNTI inside DlData\_PdcchSendReq. 5G-L2-PS shall send DlData\_PdschSendReq (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_86) to 5G-L1-DL to request transmission of transport block over PDSCH for paging message. 5G-L2-PS shall send DlData\_PdschPayloadTbSendReq (5G\_L1\_5G21A\_96) to 5G-L1-DL to request transmision of encoded RRC Paging message [prepared as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11739\_replaced([11167674](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktrHIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))] to addressed UEs. See MSC for RRC paging at 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_245\_replaced([11194184](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BfohePvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The IEs in DlData\_PdcchSendReq are set as in 5G\_L2\_10447 with exceptions in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3921\_replaced([11168085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kttjXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and

IE polarizationSelection is set to “bothPolarizations" refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7117\_replaced([11171730](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuGk6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For IE pdcchPrecodingOption4x4 setting, [CB013440-A] if enhCtrlChannelTransMode is different than

'disabled' (see PsCell\_CellSetupReq, PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq) then follow 5G\_L2\_23660, otherwise [End CB013440-A] refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7117\_replaced([11171730](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuGk6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the scenarios without valid CSI and beam reports .

The IEs in DlData\_PdschSendReq are set as in 5G\_L2\_887 with the following exceptions:

The IE mcs is set based on MCS determined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3915\_replaced([11167805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktsVNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The IE rnti is set to P-RNTI.

The IEs startPrb shall be set according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3921\_replaced([11168085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kttjXfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The IE numOfPrbs shall be set to number of PRBs determined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3915\_replaced([11167805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktsVNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Spatial mode:

dlMimoMode == SingleAntennaTransmission\_1x1 then spatialMode = Single Antenna

dlMimoMode == ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 or ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4or4x2 or ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_8x4 then spatialMode = Closed loop spatial multiplexing

dlMimoMode == OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_2x2 or OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4or4x2 then spatialMode = Open loop spatial multiplexing

The IE 'numOfLayers' and 'antPort' are set according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12151\_replaced([11139277](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krmDc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The IE dlPtrsConfig shall be set to 0, meaning no PTRS is configured

The IE mcsTable is set to 64QAM

If 5G-L2-PS could not schedule a sTmsi due to some reason (e.g. more than maximum number of sTmsi mapped to one PO), then totalNoOfPagingDropped shall be incremented by 1 and 5G-L2-PS shall periodically trace it. On resetting totalNoOfIncomingPaging, totalNoOfPagingDropped shall also be reset.

If PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.actPagingEnh == pagingStratForRrcInact then 5G-L2-Ps shall not increase the counters totalNoOfPagingDropped or totalNoOfIncomingPaging  in case the paging record which was removed from the PF/PO buffer has several target PF/PO buffers (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27583\_replaced([11167644](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktrHHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details).

---

### 2.4.20.0-9 Paging On-line modification of PCCH Config (ID: 11173579)

**2.4.20.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11173583`)

Paging
[Before 5GC000733-I] PCCH Config Parameters are on-line modifiable. If they are on-line modified, 5G-L2-PS shall get modified PCCH config paramters via PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq message (5G\_L2\_IF\_2332\_replaced([10970613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UjyLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) sent from 5G-CP-RT.

[5GC000733-I] PCCH Config parameters are on-line modifiable. If they are on-line modified, 5G-L2-PS shall get modified PCCH config paramters via PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq message (5G\_L2\_IF\_2332\_replaced([10970613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UjyLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) sent from 5G-CP-RT. For this purpose, 5G-L2-PS shall maintain 2 Paging contexts per cell, and one is active at a time.

- No PCCH config in PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq -- refer to 5G\_L2\_820

- PCCH config online modification -- refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3928\_replaced([11168111](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kttjYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

### 2.4.20.0-10 Paging On-line modification of maxNumPagingRecords (ID: 11173589)

**2.4.20.0-10.0-2**  (ID: `11173598`)

Paging
Following procedure can be executed only for FR1, as maxNumPagingRecords is valid only for FR1.

After reception of new value of maxNumPagingRecords in PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq L2-PS shall apply new value of parameter.

Part of calculation from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7784\_replaced([11167846](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktsVPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is performed, value of maxNumPagingRecords is compared with number of paging records for which corresponding payload size is closest to and not larger than the TBS in mentioned requiremet.

Number of possible paging records to be sent in paging message can also change due to other parameter modification when maxNumPagingRecords is not present in request, in that scenario previously received value in PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationRe is treated as maxNumPagingRecords received in Req in further part of this requirement.

L2-PS shall store and use smaller of values as maximum number of paging records in paging message.

If stored value is lower than received maxNumPagingRecords, then stored value shall be sent back as maxNumPagingRecordsConfigured in PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationResp.

[PR817218-25R3] During maxNumPagingRecords modification already stored paging records can be dropped. [End PR817218-25R3]

---

### 2.4.20.0-11 Paging Online Modification of defaultPagingCycle (ID: 12795608)

**2.4.20.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `12795611`)

Paging
[CB014330-D] On reception of configured/reconfigured value of defaultPagingCycle in PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq, L2-PS shall apply the received value of parameter. L2-PS shall follow the system information parameter change handling as mentioned in [11167780](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktsVMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). Refer to [11167495](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktp5B_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for the usage of defaultPagingCycle when PSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq is received.[[CB014330-D]

---

### 2.4.20.0-12 Paging Online Modification of pagingTargetRateIndex (ID: 12795612)

**2.4.20.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `12795613`)

Paging
[CB014330-D] On reception of pagingTargetRateIndex in PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq message, L2-PS shall perform the following:

1. Re-calculate the maximum number of paging record as specified in [11167846](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktsVPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

2.  If re-calculated value is lower than configured maxNumPagingRecords, then L2-PS shall use the re-calculated value and sent it back as maxNumPagingRecordsConfigured in PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationResp.

If L2-PS receives PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq without pagingTargetRateIndex parameter during cell setup procedure, L2-PS shall initialize this parameter with default value as specified in [11167832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktsVOvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).[CB014330-D]

---

### 2.4.20.0-13 Paging Extended DRX (eDRX) (ID: 11173602)

**2.4.20.0-13.0-1**  (ID: `11173606`)

Paging
Overview

---

**2.4.20.0-13.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173610`)

Paging
5G-L2-PS receives PsSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq message (5G\_L2\_IF\_571\_replaced([10978552](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VUnJfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if pagingInd is present, for each pagingInfo(per UE ):

if nrPagingeDRXInformation is present, and [PR792293]active[End PR792293]actEdrx is NOT 'None':

5G-L2-PS shall handle this paging request as eDRX paging as following steps:

step1: check whether the pagingInfo(UE) is CN paging (sTmsi is present in pagingInfo) or RAN paging(iRnti is present in pagingInfo of PsSgnl\_PcchDataSendReq),and verify parameters as 5G\_L2\_19156.

Step2: calculate target PF/PO for :

eDRX CN paging - 5G\_L2\_18803 ;

[CB012544-D] eDRX RAN paging - 5G\_L2\_19773. [End CB012544-D]

Step3: put paging request into PF/PO buffer, as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11729\_replaced([11167569](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktqgCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Step4: scheduling paging.

if eDRX parameter check failed, 5G-L2-PS treat it as DRX paging.

---

**2.4.20.0-13.0-2**  (ID: `11173614`)

Paging
eDRX paging parameters validation

---

**2.4.20.0-13.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173619`)

Paging
For eDRX paging, 5G-L2-PS shall check [Before PR792293 ]latest[PR792293 ]active[End PR792293 ] actEdrx as:

For eDRX CN paging, actEdrx shall be "1: Idle eDrx support" or "2: Idle+inactive eDrx support".

For eDRX RAN paging, actEdrx shall be  "2: Idle+inactive eDrx support".

For each eDRX paging, nrPagingeDRXInformation must be present, 5G-L2-PS shall check eDRX paging related parameter in nrPagingeDRXInformation:

nrPagingeDRXCycleIdle: it is TeDrx-CN defined in 3GPP, it must be present for eDRX paging(for both eDRX CN paging and RAN paging);

if TeDrx-CN > 1 hyperframe, following IEs must be checked (used to calculate PTW):

nrPTW:  PTW(nrpaging-Time-Window) length,  it is mandatory for PTW calculation, if it not present in nrPagingeDRXInformation, default value is used;

hashedUeIdIndex: it is UE\_ID\_H defined in 3GPP, must be present for HF/PTW calculation.

[CB012544-D]

For eDRX RAN paging:

extendedUeIdIndex: must be present, it is enhancedUeIdIndex used to drive UE\_ID in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27711\_replaced([11167929](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kts8RvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

nrPagingeDRXCycleInactive: [Before PR788043]must be present[PR788043]It is optional [End PR788043], it is TeDrx-ran defined in 3GPP. It is used to calculate eDRX RAN paging cycle when present.

ueSpecificPagingDrxUpperLayer: It is UE specific DRX value configured by upper layers,  it is optional, used to calculate eDRX RAN paging cycle during PTW when present.

[End CB012544-D]

[PR792293]

notes: the reason to use active actEdrx(along with current SIB1 on air), is to try paging successul during actEdrx onlince change, since SIB1 take effective after 2MP when NIDD change, UE still worked in legacy eDRX mode during 2 MP.

[End PR792293]

---

**2.4.20.0-13.0-3**  (ID: `11173622`)

Paging
eDRX CN paging handling

---

**2.4.20.0-13.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11173626`)

Paging
5G-L2-PS handle eDRX CN paging as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27237\_replaced([11168024](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kttjU_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If TeDRX\_CN > 1024 frames,  UE only has PF inside PTW, and distance of 2 PTWs maybe be very long (max 1024 HF(hyper frame),  in order to reduce PF buffer length in 5G-L2-PS, if calculating PF acrossing 2 PTWs and the distance to next PTW is longer than 5s(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27237\_replaced([11168024](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kttjU_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the paging is discarded by 5G-L2-PS.

If TeDRX\_CN > 1024 frames, the min eDRX cycle is 2048 frames(here min TeDRX\_CN is 2 HyperFrames), 5G-L2-PS only find PF inside PTW using UE specific DRX cycle or default DRX cycle, the max DRX cycle is 256 frames. To simplify implementation,5G-L2-PS don't find all PF candidates accross 2 PTWs, If there is PF candidates found in current PTW, 5G-L2-PS don't check PF candidates in next PTW. If there is no PF candidates in current PTW or the last frame of current PTW is passed before Paging request received, then 5G-L2-PS need find PF candidates in next PTW.

If TeDRX\_CN <= 1024 frames, 5G-L2-PS find the nearest 1 PF candidate using TeDRX\_CN,  the max DRX cycle is 1024 frames.

If UE specific DRX cycle is used, the max DRX cycle is 256 frames(max default DRX cycle), there may be multiple PF candidates.

If TeDRX\_CN or default DRX cycle(in SIB1) is used, only nearest 1 PF candidate.

The target PF/PO is selected from PF candidates and put to PF buffer as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11729\_replaced([11167569](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktqgCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) defined.

notes: "current PTW" is defined as: Paging request is received between start frame of current PTW to next PTW's start frame.

---

**2.4.20.0-13.0-4**  (ID: `11173631`)

Paging
eDRX RAN paging handling

---

**2.4.20.0-13.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11173635`)

Paging
For eDRX RAN paging, 5G\_L2\_PS always find nearest possible PF candidates for UE. DRX cycle T is depended on TeDRX\_CN, TeDRX\_RAN,etc. details 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27238\_replaced([11168037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kttjVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If TeDRX\_CN > 1024 frames, T(DRX paging cycle) is different for inside PTW and outside PTW. Based on when paging request received, 5G\_L2\_PS maybe find nearest PF candidates across inside PTW and outside PTW, untill find 1 PF candidate for eDRX paging request. To simplify implementation,5G-L2-PS don't find all PF candidates across inside PTW and outside PTW:

For example, 5G\_L2\_PS start to find PF inside PTW when received paging request, if there is PF candidate inside PTW, 5G\_L2\_PS no need to find another PF in further frames outside PTW; if there is no PF candidate inside PTW, then 5G\_L2\_PS shall find PF candidate in further frames outside PTW using different T.

same case when start from outside PTW when received paging request.

Inside PTW, the T maybe UE specific DRX cycle which is less than max default DRX cycle(256frames).

If UE specific DRX cycle is less than max default DRX cycle(256 frames), there may be multiple PF candidates in 256 frames.

If TeDRX\_CN <= 1024 frames, no PTW is applied. 5G-L2-PS shall find the nearest PF candidates based on T, T is TeDRX\_RAN, the min T is 256 frames, there is nearest 1 PF candidate in T;

Here T: DRX cycle of the UE, refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27238\_replaced([11168037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kttjVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

## 2.4.21 Physical Downlink Control Channel Management

### 2.4.21.0-1 Physical Downlink Control Channel Management FA: [Internal Domain] PDCCH Document Owner: Patrick Moisy (Nokia) (ID: 11173643)

### 2.4.21.1 PDCCH Resource Management

#### 2.4.21.1.1 PDCCH Slot Pattern

##### 2.4.21.1.1.1 Initial Slot Pattern

###### 2.4.21.1.1.1.1 Initial Slot Pattern for TDD FR1 Frame Structure tdLte Config0

###### 2.4.21.1.1.1.1.0-1 Initial Slot Pattern for TDD FR1 Frame Structure tdLte Config0 Initial Slot Pattern for bandwidth &gt; 40 MHz (ID: 11173663)

**2.4.21.1.1.1.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173673`)

Initial Slot Pattern for TDD FR1 Frame Structure tdLte Config0
When the TDD FR1 frame structure is configured with tdLte Config0, in each half frame there are 6 UL slots which shall be scheduled within 4 DL slots.

If in special slot there's DL scheduling, the slot 2/12 or slot 3/13 will have 1 DL scheduling and 2 UL scheduling, refer to 5G\_L2\_23169 for the timing and K1/K2 values.

For cell bandwidth > 40 MHz, only 2 PDCCH occasions are available in the first 2 PDCCH symbols. Since 1 DL scheduler and 2 UL scheduler shall be allocated with PDCCH resource, CCE split is required.

The CCE split in slot 2/12, or slot 3/13 does not depend on the NIDD setting of NRCELL.actCceSplit and the CCE split condition described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12060\_replaced([11169840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt6Xo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CCE split shall always exist for the slots with 1 K1 and 2 K2 values.

For the CCE split index, there are some impacts need to be taken into account:

* CCE number allocated to each scheduler
* The location of PDCCH candidate
* The largest AL configured
* RA occasion for DL and UL in one slot
* Overlapping with Coreset 0

The final CCE split index for slot 2/12 or slot 3/13 within the 2 PDCCH symbol takes the different position, refer to 5G\_UP\_14028\_replaced([11362289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc7VePvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32469\_replaced([11169885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt6XrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

For slot 0/10, slot 1/11 legacy CCE split condition shall be followed according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12060\_replaced([11169840](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt6Xo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Then with below configurations:

* slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is false,
* beamforming is not activated,
* SSB number is 1,
* paging periodicity is T/8, paging offset is 1 and fPdcchMonOccsnPo[0] is 0,
* multiple BWP is not activated,
* NRCELL.actDynPdcchTdMode is set as disabled

The Coreset duration pattern for BW > 40 MHz will be as following examples:

* If NRCELL.actCceSplit is false or the CCE split condition not fulfilled,

- **Light Blue (U):** Uplink slot

The table illustrates a specific pattern of resource allocation across multiple frames, showing the distribution of downlink, uplink, and control channel resources.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4764.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

Common channels like SIB1, OSI, Paging shall not allow CCE split as legacy.

Note:

1. When CA with DPA is applicable, the first PDCCH symbol shall be for the DL direction, refer to the 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3962\_replaced([11168921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktzp9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Link to the table:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?cid=48b81bd8%2D88cc%2D4935%2D9365%2Db7631af4dc7c&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F21%2E%20PDCCH&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

---

###### 2.4.21.1.1.1.1.0-2 Initial Slot Pattern for TDD FR1 Frame Structure tdLte Config0 Initial Slot Pattern for bandwidth &gt;= 20 MHz and bandwidth &lt;= 40 MHz (ID: 11173678)

**2.4.21.1.1.1.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173687`)

Initial Slot Pattern for TDD FR1 Frame Structure tdLte Config0
For cell BW >= 20 MHz and BW <= 40 MHz, 3 PDCCH symbols can be supported, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_134\_replaced([11095535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmmOuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Then for slot 2/12, and slot 3/13 with 1 K1 and 2 K2 configured, no CCE split required any more, each scheduler can have one dedicated PDCCH symbol, the order of symbol allocated to each scheduler can refer to 5G\_UP\_14028\_replaced([11362289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc7VePvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The Coreset duration pattern example shall be as follows with below configurations:

* slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true,
* NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt is false,
* beamforming is activated,
* SIB1 is configured with 16 frames periodicity,
* OSI is configured with 8 frames periodicity sl10,0,3,
* TRS periodicity is 80ms,
* SSB number is 4,
* paging periodicity is T/2, paging offset is 1 and fPdcchMonOccsnPo[2] sl10,0,3,
* multiple BWP is not activated,
* NRCELL.actDynPdcchTdMode is set as disabled,
* NRCELL.actCceSplit is true and the CCE split condition fulfilled

The frame structure is represented by a sequence of symbols, with each symbol corresponding to a time slot. The PDCCH symbols are further categorized based on their function, such as downlink grants, uplink grants, or special purposes.
The table below summarizes the frame structure and PDCCH symbol allocations for each frame:

| Frame | Frame Structure | SSB | PDCCH Symbols/sl (Row 0) | PDCCH Symbols/sl (Row 1) | PDCCH Symbols/sl (Row 2) | CSI RS Pooling Group |

| 1 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 1 |
| 2 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 2 |
| 3 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 1 |
| 4 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 0 |
| 5 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 2 |
| 6 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 1 |
| 7 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 1 |
| 8 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 0 |
| 9 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 2 |
| 10 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 1 |
| 11 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 1 |
| 12 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 0 |
| 13 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 2 |
| 14 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 1 |
| 15 | DDDSUUUUUU |  | D, D, S | B, B, U | D, D, U | 1 |
| **Legend:** |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **D**: Downlink slot |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **U**: Uplink slot |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **S**: Special slot |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **SSB**: Synchronization Signal Block |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **PDCCH with DL grant**: PDCCH carrying a downlink grant. |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **PDCCH with UL grant**: PDCCH carrying an uplink grant. |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **PDCCH with both DL and UL grant, CCE split as CB009422 specified**: PDCCH carrying both downlink and uplink grants, with CCE split according to CB009422 specification. |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **PDCCH with both DL and UL grant, CCE split as legacy actCceSplit specified**: PDCCH carrying both downlink and uplink grants, with CCE split according to legacy actCceSplit specification. |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **CSI-RS for tracking**: Channel State Information Reference Signal for tracking. |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **SIB1 slot**: System Information Block 1 slot. |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **OSI slot**: Other System Information slot. |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| \* **Paging slot**: Slot for paging messages. |  |  |  |  |  |  |

The CSI RS Pooling Groups (0, 1, and 2) are also indicated, suggesting different groupings for CSI-RS resources. The frame structure itself is a repeating pattern of Downlink (D) and Uplink (U) slots, with some frames also containing Special (S) slots and SSB slots.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-04161808.png[/image\_path][/image-0] |  |  |  |  |  |  |

###### 2.4.21.1.1.1.1.0-3 Initial Slot Pattern for TDD FR1 Frame Structure tdLte Config0 Initial Slot Pattern for bandwidth = 10 MHz or bandwidth = 15 MHz (ID: 12533074)

**2.4.21.1.1.1.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12533075`)

Initial Slot Pattern for TDD FR1 Frame Structure tdLte Config0
For cell BW = 10 MHz or BW = 15 MHz, 3 PDCCH symbols can be supported, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_134\_replaced([11095535](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmmOuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and CORESET-0 with 2 symbols and CORESET-1 with 1 symbol shall be configured.

For slot 2/3/12/13 with 1 K1 and 2 K2 configured, CCE split shall be supported on CORESET-0 within the first 2 PDCCH symbols, each scheduler of different direction can have dedicated CCE resources, the order of symbol allocated to each scheduler can refer to 5G\_UP\_14028\_replaced([11362289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc7VePvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The Coreset duration pattern example shall be as follows with below configurations:

* slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true,
* NRBTS.actTddCsiRsOpt is false,
* beamforming is activated,
* SIB1 periodicity is 64 frames,
* OSI periodicity is 8 or 16 frames, with search space sl10/0/2,
* TRS periodicity is 80ms,
* SSB number is 2,
* numOfPagingFrames=T/2, paging offset=0,numPagingOccsnPagingFrame=one, fPdcchMonOccsnPo[0]=0, and with search space sl10/1/1,
* multiple BWP is not supported,
* NRCELL.actDynPdcchTdMode is set as disabled

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the structure of radio frames in a communication system, likely 5G NR, across multiple frames (0 through 7). The diagram shows the allocation of different types of slots within each frame, categorized by their purpose.
The top section of the diagram shows the "Frame Structure" with "SSB" (Synchronization Signal Block) indicated at the beginning of each frame. The frame is divided into 80 time slots, numbered from 0 to 79. Each slot is color-coded and labeled with a letter indicating its type:
- 'D' for Downlink slot (blue)
- 'U' for Uplink slot (light blue)
- 'S' for Special slot (grey)
- 'D' with a yellow border for SSB slot (yellow)
- 'D' with an orange border for CSI-RS for tracking (orange)
- 'D' with a green border for SIB1 slot (green)
- 'D' with a purple border for Paging slot (purple)
Below the frame structure, there are rows labeled "PDCCH symbols/s" which show the allocation of PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) symbols within specific groups. These groups are labeled as "CsiRsPoolingGroup0", "CsiRsPoolingGroup1", and "CsiRsPoolingGroup2". The PDCCH symbols are further categorized by their content:
- 'D' with a purple border for PDCCH with DL grant
- 'U' with a light blue border for PDCCH with UL grant
- 'B' with a green border for PDCCH with both DL and UL grant, CCE split as CB009422 specified
- 'B' with a light blue border for PDCCH with both DL and UL grant, CCE split as legacy actCceSplit specified
The diagram shows the pattern of these slots and PDCCH symbol allocations repeating or varying across the eight frames. For instance, the overall frame structure of Downlink, Uplink, and Special slots appears to be consistent across the frames, with variations in the placement of SSB, CSI-RS, SIB1, and Paging slots. The PDCCH symbol allocations within the CsiRsPoolingGroups also show a repeating pattern.
A legend at the bottom clarifies the meaning of the different colored blocks and labels used in the diagram.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05091013.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note:

1. When CA with DPA is applicable or A-CSI is activated, the first PDCCH symbol shall be for the DL direction, refer to the 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3962\_replaced([11168921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktzp9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. For slot 0/1/10/11, only when 1 CORESET is configured and associated with UE specific search space, the CCE split is allowed, refer to the 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8917\_replaced([11169023](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_iNqasPvNEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Link to the table:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?cid=48b81bd8%2D88cc%2D4935%2D9365%2Db7631af4dc7c&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F21%2E%20PDCCH&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>
