##### 2.4.21.1.1.2 Periodic Evaluation

###### 2.4.21.1.1.2.1 PDCCH Symbol Usage Control

###### 2.4.21.1.1.2.1.1 Modification of the initialized PDCCH slot pattern

###### 2.4.21.1.1.2.1.1.0-1 Modification of the initialized PDCCH slot pattern If increase of number of symbols fails in one direction (ID: 11173702)

**2.4.21.1.1.2.1.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173708`)

Modification of the initialized PDCCH slot pattern
Following requirement supplements 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4016\_replaced([11169667](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt4if_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4018\_replaced([11169689](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt5JhPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The increase of number of symbols may not always be possible for one direction due to SearchSpace configuration. If the increase fails for a slot then L2-PS shall consider the same slot still for potential PDCCH symbol increase of the other direction.

---

###### 2.4.21.1.1.2.1.2 Building the next PDCCH slot pattern

###### 2.4.21.1.1.2.1.2.0-1 Building the next PDCCH slot pattern Optimization of PDCCH slot pattern calculation (ID: 11173714)

**2.4.21.1.1.2.1.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12578424`)

Building the next PDCCH slot pattern
5G-L2-PS shall use the characteristics of slot pattern to optimize the construction of PDCCH slot pattern, during periodic pattern modification (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4000\_replaced([11169133](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt1fJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). To describe this optimization, let's introduce following terms, aligned with 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4001\_replaced([11169142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt1fKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

* construction slots (cSlots): slots in the second half of every odd SFN where 5G-L2-PS can construct the next PDCCH slot pattern
* PDCCH pattern slots (pSlots): slots in the two SFNs in which the PDCCH pattern is modified, following the construction SFN

When handling PsCell\_CellSetupReq, 5G-L2-PS shall create the strategy for periodic calculation of pSlots for all cells within the cell group:

1.5G-L2-PS distributes the calculation of pSlots across the maximum possible number of cSlots

2.5G-L2-PS prioritizes the cSlots without DL scheduling for pSlots calculation. This means more pSlots will be calculated in cSlots if there is no DL scheduling:

* For FR1 except for TDD tdLteConfig0 frame structure; or FR2 with <= 3 cells [CB008453-A] and TDD Periodic 5-slot frame structure (5G\_UP\_62\_replaced([11346979](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIlv_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008453-A]: The ratio of pSlots calculated in cSlots with DL scheduling and without DL scheduling is 1:25
* For FR1 TDD tdLteConfig0 frame structure: the cSlots and pSlots are described in [12639310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_8mkPwEDnEfCal_QUJn4xrg).
* For FR2 with > 3 cells and TDD Periodic 5-slot frame structure (5G\_UP\_62\_replaced([11346979](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIlv_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))); [CB008453-A]or FR2 and TDD Periodic 8-slot 4-4 frame structure ([11346993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMwPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) (i.e. ulDlDataSlotRatio is "4\_4\_ratio") [End CB008453-A] : 5G-L2-PS uses the tables specified in 5G\_L2\_22713

3.5G-L2-PS, for each cell, determines the cSlot where the DL scheduler will notify the UL scheduler about the modified PDDCH pattern. This is the cSlot where the complete pSlots pattern (2\*number of slots in SFN) is computed for given cell.

---

###### 2.4.21.1.1.2.1.2.0-2 Building the next PDCCH slot pattern FR1 TDD tdLteConfig0 PDCCH slot pattern calculation table (ID: 12639310)

**2.4.21.1.1.2.1.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12639311`)

Building the next PDCCH slot pattern
For tdLteConfig0 frame structure the passive PDCCH slot pattern build is still in subframe 5 of odd radio frame as legacy, but due to the dynamic slot offset deployed for DL and UL, the cSlots are only located in L2-PS slot 10/11/12 in DL to keep the PDCCH slot pattern sync between DL and UL and also the PDCCH slot pattern switch from passive to active in time.

By taking into account the PDCCH slots within 40 slots of 2 frames, the pSlots ratio in 3 cSlots are 11:11:18 which will keep the same number for real PDCCH slot pattern construction within 2 frames.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating a scheduling pattern for a wireless communication system, likely related to 5G New Radio (NR). The diagram is structured as a table with rows representing different scheduling parameters and columns representing time slots within frames.
**Key elements of the diagram:**
\* **SFN (System Frame Number):** Indicates the current frame number. The diagram shows SFNs 0, 1, 2, and 3.
\* **L2-PS DL Scheduling & L2-PS UL Scheduling:** These rows likely represent Layer 2 (L2) Physical Slot (PS) scheduling for Downlink (DL) and Uplink (UL) respectively. The colored cells (red, green, gray, blue) indicate different scheduling configurations or types. The red border highlights a specific period of scheduling.
\* **NRT Slots:** Denotes Non-Real-Time slots, which are marked with "NRT".
\* **K1 & K2:** These likely represent control parameters or indices used in the scheduling process.
\* **SlotOffsetDl (slots) & SlotOffsetUl (slots):** These rows indicate offsets in slots for DL and UL transmissions.
\* **TDD On Air Slot & Slot Type:** This section at the bottom shows the TDD (Time Division Duplex) configuration for each slot, with 'D' indicating Downlink, 'U' indicating Uplink, and 'S' indicating a special slot. The numbers above these rows represent the slot index within a larger sequence.
**Annotations and Visual Cues:**
\* **Blue Arrows:** Pointing to slots 10, 11, and 12 in SFN 1, with the text "Build passive PDCCH slot pattern". This suggests a specific pattern is being constructed for the Physical Downlink Control Channel (PDCCH) in these slots.
\* **Orange Arrow:** Points to the transition between SFN 1 and SFN 2, specifically at the beginning of SFN 2, with the text "Switch PDCCH slot pattern for next 2 frames in last slot for DL and UL". This indicates a change in the PDCCH slot pattern.
\* **Numbers above the diagram:** Indicate slot numbers within a frame (0-19) and also group numbers (e.g., 14,15,16/17,18,19 and 4,5,6/7,8,9) which might refer to specific scheduling groups or configurations.
\* **Red and Green Borders:** These borders highlight specific sections of the scheduling table, likely representing different operational phases or configurations. The red border encloses SFN 0 and parts of SFN 1, while the green border encloses parts of SFN 1 and SFN 2.
\* **Red and Yellow Circles:** Highlight specific cells in the L2-PS DL Scheduling and NRT Slots rows, drawing attention to particular scheduling events or states.
**Table Representation:**
| SFN | 0[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-08271757.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

###### 2.4.21.1.1.2.1.2.0-3 Building the next PDCCH slot pattern FR2 table for optimized PDCCH slot pattern calculation~~, ulDlDataSlotRatio 1/4~~ (ID: 11173724)

**2.4.21.1.1.2.1.2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12578425`)

Building the next PDCCH slot pattern
Table used in 5G\_L2\_22711 to determine how many pSlots will be constructed in every cSlot (see 5G\_L2\_22711 for pSlots, cSlots definition)

TDD Periodic 5-slot frame structure (5G\_UP\_62\_replaced([11346979](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIlv_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (i.e. ulDlDataSlotRatio is "1\_4\_ratio"):

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the structure of a half radio frame with 40 slots, along with a table showing data transmission parameters based on the number of cells.
The top part of the image shows a timeline of cSlot numbers from 40 to 79, indicating the type of transmission in each slot. The slots are categorized as Downlink (DL), Broadcast (BI), and Uplink (UL). Specifically:
- Slots 40, 41, and 42 are DL.
- Slot 43 is BI.
- Slots 44, 49, 54, 59, 64, 69, 74, and 79 are UL.
There are also dotted lines indicating a continuation of UL slots between the explicitly labeled UL slots.
The bottom part of the image is a table with the header "numOfCells" and rows representing different numbers of cells (8, 7, 6, 5, and 4). The columns represent the cSlot numbers, and the cells contain numerical values, likely representing data transmission quantities or configurations for each slot and number of cells.
Here is the table data:

| numOfCells | 40 | 41 | 42 | 43 | 44 | 49 | 54 | 59 | 64 | 69 | 74 | 79 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| numOfCells = 8 | 8 | 8 | 8 | 8 | 128 | 8, 8, 8, 8 | 128 | 8, 8, 8, 8 | 128 | 8, 8, 8, 8 | 128 | 8, 8, 8, 8 |
| numOfCells = 7 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 110 | 6, 6, 6, 6 | 110 | 6, 6, 6, 6 | 110 | 6, 6, 6, 6 | 110 | 6, 6, 6, 6 |
| numOfCells = 6 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 88 | 6, 6, 6, 6 | 88 | 6, 6, 6, 6 | 88 | 6, 6, 6, 6 | 88 | 6, 6, 6, 6 |
| numOfCells = 5 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 108 | 4, 4, 4, 4 | 82 | 4, 4, 4, 4 | 82 | 4, 4, 4, 4 | 82 | 4, 4, 4, 4 |
| numOfCells = 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 4 | 108 | 4, 4, 4, 4 | 82 | 4, 4, 4, 4 | 82 | 4, 4, 4, 4 | 82 | 0, 0, 0, 0 |

[CB008453-A]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a table illustrating the distribution of Downlink (DL), Uplink (UL), and Backhaul (BI) slots within a half radio frame containing 40 slots. The cSlot numbers range from 40 to 79.
The table has rows representing the number of cells, from 8 down to 1. Each cell in the table contains a numerical value, which appears to indicate the number of subcarriers allocated to that specific slot for a given number of cells.
The first row of the table, labeled "numOfCells = 8", shows the allocation for 8 cells. The subsequent rows show the allocations for 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, and 1 cell, respectively.
The top section of the image shows the slot type for each cSlot number.
- Slots 40, 41, 42 are DL.

| Slots 76-79: UL[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-10311926.png[/image\_path][/image-2] |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

[End PR854508]

[DNG\_12578425 how many pSlots will be constructed in every cSlot.xlsx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/21.%20PDCCH/DNG_12578425%20how%20many%20pSlots%20will%20be%20constructed%20in%20every%20cSlot.xlsx?d=w5058516239bd4040a5644d5519068d5b&csf=1&web=1&e=elyfAg)

[End CB008453-A]

Notes:

* Table was designed for [Before CB008453-A] tddFrameStructure.ulDlDataSlotRatio = 1/4 [CB008453-A] ulDlDataSlotRatio = "1\_4\_ratio" or "4\_4\_ratio" [End CB008453-A] and can not be used with other slot ratios.
* Number of pSlots calculated in cSlots is chosen in a way so that complete pSlots pattern (160 pSlots) calculation for a cell lands in a slot without DL scheduling, so the UL notification would be sent in that slot. Additionally, FR2 L2-RT supports max 4 cells with UL (see 5G\_UP\_8443\_replaced([11387846](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufHt1vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), which means total 4 Ul notifications are needed, allowing for further reduction of pSlots computed in some cSlots. Rationale: PDCCH pattern Ul notification was proven to be heavy operation

---

##### 2.4.21.1.1.3 Dynamic Evaluation

###### 2.4.21.1.1.3.1 Dynamic Evaluation for TDD FR1 tdLte Config0 Frame Structure

###### 2.4.21.1.1.3.1.0-1 Dynamic Evaluation for TDD FR1 tdLte Config0 Frame Structure Dynamic Evaluation for tdLte Config0 (ID: 11173742)

**2.4.21.1.1.3.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173747`)

Dynamic Evaluation for TDD FR1 tdLte Config0 Frame Structure
For the dynamic evaluation of Coreset duration pattern for tdLte Config0 frame structure, the algorithm from 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3984\_replaced([11169730](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt5JjfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be followed.

However, the slot 2/12 and slot 3/13 with 2 K2 shall be excluded for applying the Coreset duration pattern modification.

---

##### 2.4.21.1.1.4 PDCCH Slot Pattern for asymmetric DSS

###### 2.4.21.1.1.4.1 PDCCH Slot Pattern when a coreset overlaps shared LTE area

**2.4.21.1.1.4.1.0-2**  (ID: `13437696`)

PDCCH Slot Pattern when a coreset overlaps shared LTE area
A PDCCH OptionC ([12480718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_OD7H4A7qEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) was introduced to enable PDCCH behavior when a coreset > 0 overlaps shared LTE area:

* fixed PDSCH start in symbol position 3
* unconditional PDCCH occasion with CCE split on symbol 2
* specified UL/DL CCEs direction alternation between slots, symbols in slot and intra symbol

Note1. Slots with common channels are generated separately and have always DL on symbol 0.

Note2. Intra symbol alternation concerns symbol position 2 that has CCE split. The CCE split part mostly in LTE shared area has same direction as symbol 0 unsplit PDCCH occasion in same slot .

This option is set at cellSetup.

PDCCH optionC enables following PDCCH slot pattern generation/evaluation specificities:

* PDCCH framework switch to FDD NR with actCceSplit = true (regardless cell setup value) mode.
* Generation of appropriate additional PDCCH combinations having non CCE split symbol 0 & 1 occasions along with CCE split symbol two for slots with non-common channels (new combinations having mix of with/without CCE split PDCCH occasions).
* Force symbol 2 occasion (with CCE split) insertion in generated combinations for slots with common channels (non CCE split only combinations before).

[Before PR877389]

* Disable PDCCH pattern modification after re-evaluation in case of PDCCH lack ([12480718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_OD7H4A7qEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)).

[PR877389][End PR877389]

* Insertion of opposite direction PDCCH occasion in symbol 1 in case of search space configured with two monitoring symbols in coreset 0 with duration of one symbol during PDCCH slot pattern generation.
* Insertion of UL (same direction) PDCCH occasion in symbol 1 of SSB and/or TRS slots when numPdschPrbInSsbSlots = 0 and/or numPdschPrbInTrsSlots = 0 given that only UL scheduling is possible. Please note that in these conditions symbol 2 occasion with CCE split is not inserted in corresponding slots.

---

#### 2.4.21.1.2 PDCCH Power Resource Management

##### 2.4.21.1.2.0-1 PDCCH Power Resource Management PDCCH Power Resource (ID: 11173755)

**2.4.21.1.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173759`)

PDCCH Power Resource Management
When PDCCH power boost is activated, the PDCCH power resource shall be managed per PDCCH symbol for DL and UL direction individually.

In each slot, the power resource shall be re-initialized and re-allocated.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29308\_replaced([11171220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuCTf_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.21.1.3 Search Space Set Group Switching

##### 2.4.21.1.3.0-1 Search Space Set Group Switching UE SSSG Switching Configuration and Active Switching Algorithm (ID: 11173767)

**2.4.21.1.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173772`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
5G-L2-PS has SSSG switching configuration for UE level:

Dense SSSG: It is default SSSG when UE setup or [PR878479]new dedicate BWP reconfigured[End PR878479], configured in common pdcchConfig (5G\_L2\_IF\_84\_replaced([10968165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UW90_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in PsCell\_CellSetupReq, and bwpConfig when dedicate BWP is configured;

Sparse SSSG: It is configured with sparseSssgSwitchingConfig in PsUser\_UserModifyReq or PsUser\_UserSetupReq, along with dedicate BWP setup. [CB012631-SR-C]L2-PS get UE's sparse search space list from sparseSSGroupListPerBwp in cell according to sparseSSGroupIndex.

[End CB012631-SR-C]

SSSG switching algorithm related parameter([10970399](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UikDPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is common for all UEs in cell level, defined in PsCell\_CellSetupReq and PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

[CB012631-SR-D]When feature is enabled(actSssgSwitching is not disabled) for the UE,[End CB012631-SR-D]5G-L2-PS active SSSG switching evaluation algorithm from default SSSG, the timer measurementDurationToSparseSSSG is started when SSSG configuration is ready in UE side, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31126\_replaced([11168776](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktyb0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L2-PS run SSSG switching algorithm as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31058\_replaced([11168744](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktx0zfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L2-PS need keep track UE SSSG state always after sparse SSSG is configured.

---

##### 2.4.21.1.3.0-2 Search Space Set Group Switching SSSG Switching FSM Management (ID: 11173776)

**2.4.21.1.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173780`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
SSSG switching state FSM is UE based.

5G-L2-PS DL and UL scheduler both need maintain SSSG switching FSM respectively for each UE, as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31058\_replaced([11168744](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktx0zfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L2-PS UL report average UL throughput/UL buffer state to L2-PS DL.

5G-L2-PS DL combined UL report and make decision to trigger SSSG switching.

5G-L2-PS DL and UL need keep synchronization once states change as 5G\_L2\_23278.

---

##### 2.4.21.1.3.0-3 Search Space Set Group Switching SSSG Switching State Synchronization between DL and UL (ID: 11173784)

**2.4.21.1.3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11173789`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
SSSG Switching Evaluation Phase

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173793`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
5G-L2-PS DL and UL is Dense or Sparse SSSG state.

[CB012631-SR-D]5G-L2-PS DL enable evaluation SSSG switching for UE when:

**[Before PR867429](1)Cell feature flag actSssgSwitching is enabled;**[End PR867429]\*\*\*\*

(2)UE has sparseSssgSwitchingConfig **[Before PR867429]for both valid or invalid(due to parameter online changed)**[End PR867429]；\*\*\*\*

(3)Cell not in RIM mitigation active state(K2 changed), as [13313218: Search Space Set Group(s) and RIM mitigation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_lFEc4M6YEfCVOOJMfwsFiw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

[End CB012631-SR-D]

5G-L2-PS DL evaluation SSSG switching conditions based on:

DL throughput and buffer criteria(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31157\_replaced([11168793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktyb1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_UP\_ALG\_31157\_replaced([11168793](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktyb1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

UL throughput and buffer criteria(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31159\_replaced([11168813](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktyb2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G\_UP\_ALG\_31160\_replaced([11168822](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktyb3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

and Radio channel quality criteria(5G\_UP\_ALG\_31725\_replaced([11168832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktzC4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

When conditions reached to SSSG switching, [CB012631-SR-D]5G-L2-PS DL will:

(1)If UE has valid sparseSssgSwitchingConfig, start switching,[End CB012631-SR-D]change state to SSSG switching transient state, and notify 5G-L2-PS UL; [CB012631-SR-D]**[Before PR867429]Or else**

(2)If UE has invalid sparseSssgSwitchingConfig(Due to [12793706: Sparse SS set reconfiguration](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tm2UAJdeEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) ), trigger SSSG reconfiguration as [12798424: User Reconfiguration required for sparse SSSG](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Hr064Jj2EfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).**[End PR867429][End CB012631-SR-D]**

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11173797`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
SSSG Switching Transient State

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173802`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
5G-L2-PS DL and UL is in DenseToSparse or SparseToDense.

5G-L2-PS DL and UL schedule such UE in [Before CB012631-SR-C]sparse PDCCH only[CB012631-SR-C]common slots[End CB012631-SR-C].

5G-L2-PS DL and UL scheduler send DCI with new toggled "The PDCCH monitoring adaptation indication field" once there is scheduled for such UE, and track UE's feedback independently.

5G-L2-PS DL and UL scheduler shall work in transient state until SSSG switching is [PR869406]2nd[End PR869406] confirmed.

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11173806`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
SSSG Switching Confirm State

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11173811`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
5G-L2-PS DL or UL scheduler confirm SSSG switching based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31247\_replaced([11168850](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktzC5PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L2-PS DL notify confirm state to UL if DL scheduling result is confirmed.

5G-L2-PS UL notify confirm state to DL if UL scheduling result is confirmed.

5G-L2-PS DL or UL FSM change to SSSG switching confirm state when received notification from other direction scheduling or confirmed by itself;

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11173815`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
SSSG Switching 2nd Confirm

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11173819`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
To confirm the gNB and UE in same state, 2nd confirm is introduced when UE SSSG is switching [Before PR869406] sparse to dense [PR869406] after 1st ACK[End PR869406].

After SSSG switching confirm state,5G-L2-PS DL or UL need double track the next PDCCH result separately, to get the 2nd confirm.

SSSG switching 2nd confirm is successful when any 5G-L2-PS DL or UL is confirmed [PR869406] or 2nd confirm procedure is stopped after confirm timer is expired.[End PR869406] .

5G-L2-PS DL or UL notify 2nd confirm state to each other.

The UE is hold in SSSG switching transient state and [PR869406]active recovery procedure[End PR869406], scheduled only in [Before CB012631-SR-C]sparse[CB012631-SR-C]common slots[End CB012631-SR-C] again, if 2nd confirm is failure, refer to [11168860](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktzC5vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

---

##### 2.4.21.1.3.0-4 Search Space Set Group Switching SSSG online modification (ID: 12799006)

**2.4.21.1.3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12799007`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
SSSG switching online disabled

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12799008`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
**[Before PR867429]5G-L2-PS disable SSSG switching function  per UE type( eMBB and/or RedCap UEs ) based on latest value of actSssgSwitching by cell reconfiguration.**

For the SSSG disabled UE, 5G-L2-PS shall check UE SSSG state, then:

If the UE is in Dense SSSG, stop SSSG switching estimation to sparse;

If the UE is already in switching from Sparse to Dense, continue switching. After UE is switched to Dense, stop SSSG switching estimation to sparse;

If the UE in Sparse or in switching phase to Sparse,  trigger UE switching back to Dense; After UE is switched to Dense, stop SSSG switching estimation to sparse;

If there is UE not switched back to Dense, 5G-L2-PS wait switching completion for all UEs and delay handle PsCell\_CellReconfiguration as [12980770: Delay Cell reconfiguration response](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_6cEfILktEfCQT7hYUxERAg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).**[End PR867429]**

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12980772`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
SSSG switching online enabled

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12980777`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
**[Before PR867429]5G-L2-PS detect SSSG switching function enabler  per UE type( eMBB and/or RedCap UEs ) based on latest value of actSssgSwitching by cell reconfiguration.**

5G-L2-PS apply new actSssgSwitching, enable SSSG switching estimation for related UEs based on actSssgSwitching and UE type( eMBB and/or RedCap).**[End PR867429]**

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `13257359`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
Sparse SSSG configuration online modification

---

**2.4.21.1.3.0-4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13257360`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
**[Before PR867429]Cell level:**

sparse SSSG groups parameters online change procedure, refer to [12793706: sparseSSGroupListPerBwp modification](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tm2UAJdeEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

Per UE level:

For new UE, the new Cell sparse SSSG groups is used;

For existed UEs, once Cell level sparse SSSG groups changed, ALL UEs SSSG configuration(sparseSssgSwitchingConfig) are changed as invalid.

After SSSG switching is online enabled later, 5G-L2-PS trigger PsUser\_ReconfigurationRequiredInd procedure to CP-RT to sync UE's sparse SSSG configuration, when the UE reach the switching condition as [11173789: SSSG Switching Evaluation Phase](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26eZRvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

The sync/reconfiguration procedure refer to [12798424: User Reconfiguration required for sparse SSSG](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Hr064Jj2EfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

**[End PR867429]**

---

##### 2.4.21.1.3.0-5 Search Space Set Group Switching SSSG Switching IWK with CA (ID: 11173824)

**2.4.21.1.3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11173829`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
SSSG switching shall be configured in Pcell only, 5G-L2-PS Scell shall ignore sparseSssgSwitchingConfig when Scell UE setup(for both DL CA or UL CA).

Sparse SSSG(aimed to power saving) and active Scells(aimed to improve Tput) are not co-existed:

If UE is in sparse state or in transient sate,the scell activition shall be excluded until UE SSSG is switched back to dense.

If UE already has Scell actived or ongoing activing scells, the UE shall be excluded for SSSG switching evaluation,untill all scell is deactived.

---

##### 2.4.21.1.3.0-6 Search Space Set Group Switching SSSG Switching IWK with DC-GBR (ID: 11173831)

**2.4.21.1.3.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11173836`)

Search Space Set Group Switching
Sparse SSSG(for power saving, delay is expected) and DC-GBR(for delay critical service) are not co-existed:

For UE with DC-GBR configued(refer to delayCriticalGbrDrbParameters in 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) present in PsUser\_UserSetupReq or PsUser\_BearerSetupReq):

If the UE is in Dense SSSG, the UE shall be excluded for SSSG switching evaluation;

If the UE is already in Sparse SSSG, 5G-L2-PS shall bypass SSSG switching evaluation, and move UE to SSSG Switching Transient State**(5G\_L2\_23280), trigger UE SSSG switching SparseToDense;**

If the UE is in switching transient phase, 5G-L2-PS shall trigger UE go back to Dense SSSG if UE switched to  Sparse SSSG.

---

### 2.4.21.2 Aggregation Level Management

#### 2.4.21.2.0-1 Aggregation Level Management Reconfiguration of Dynamic Aggregation Level (ID: 11173845)

**2.4.21.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12795607`)

Aggregation Level Management
PDCCH Aggregation Level management can be online reconfigured via PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

For each BWP which is under re-configuration, an entry in vector cellBwpDynamicAl[] of PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq (5G\_L2\_IF\_232\_replaced([10971422](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UoDmfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) carries the reconfigurable parameters.

All parameters of PsCell\_CellSetupReq.pdcchConfig.cellBwpAlMgtAndSearchSpace[].dynamicAggregationlevelSet and dynamicAggregationlevelHrSet  
 can be online reconfigured [Before CB014330-F]except aggregationLevelList which required object-locking. [CB014330-F]

This implies that if dynamicAggregationlevelSet or dynamicAggregationlevelHrSet is present in PsCell\_CellSetupReq for a given BWP, it must be stored by L2-PS SW in the cell DB, even if aggregationLevelSelection = 'static' for that BWP.

If at least one parameter under dynamicAggregationlevelSet[CB014330-F]Reconfig[End CB014330-F]or dynamicAggregationlevelHrSet[CB014330-F]Reconfig[End CB014330-F] is reconfigured for the target Bandwidth Part, all the defined parameters are present in dynamicAggregationlevelSet[CB014330-F]Reconfig[End CB014330-F] or dynamicAggregationlevelHrSet[CB014330-F]Reconfig[End CB014330-F].

At reception of PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq, for each target BWP cellBwpId (cellBwpDynamicAl[.] present in PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq for the related BWP) L2-PS shall:

* store aggregationLevelSelection;
* store dynamicAggregationlevelSet[CB014330-F]Reconfig[End CB014330-F], and dynamicAggregationlevelHrSet[CB014330-F]Reconfig[End CB014330-F]if present;
* calculate cqiCorrectionDown (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4073\_replaced([11170495](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_QIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if aggregationLevelSelection = dynamic;
* Build the Set of allowed ALs for the dynamic AL selection (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4071\_replaced([11170399](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt-pGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if aggregationLevelSelection = dynamic;

If aggregationLevelSelection = dynamic, from the next slot, the dynamic AL selection algorithm shall be performed according to the new configuration. In order to keep the implementation simpler:

* related to the handling of missed CQIs (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4082\_replaced([11170623](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), timers which are ongoing with older *pCqiErasureActTimer value shall not be affected by the recofiguration;*

---

#### 2.4.21.2.0-2 Aggregation Level Management Reconfiguration of Paging and SI Aggregation Level (ID: 12795594)

**2.4.21.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12795595`)

Aggregation Level Management
PDCCH Aggregation Level management can be online reconfigured via PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

For each BWP which is under re-configuration, an entry in vector cellBwpDynamicAl[] of PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq  carries the reconfigurable parameters.

L2-PS retrieves IE pagingAndSiAggregationLevelSetReconfig from cellBwpDynamicAl[] of the initial BWP, as pagingAndSiAggregationLevelSetReconfig cannot differ between BWPs and SI/Paging transmissions are requested only if an initial BWP is configured in the cell (SA calls).

All parameters of PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.pdcchReconfiguration.cellBwpDynamicAl[].pagingAndSiAggregationLevelSetReconfig (for details refer to [12782413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_wBkG544yEfCrqOdGJPoCRA)), can be online reconfigured.

If at least one of the parameters corresponding to IEs  in pagingAndSiAggregationLevelSetReconfig is reconfigured for a particular Bandwidth Part, then all other IEs in the structure shall be present.

If dciSiAggregationLevel is reconfigured for a particular bandwidth part, L2-PS expects CP-RT to send PsCell\_SystemInfoConfigurationReq.

---

#### 2.4.21.2.0-3 Aggregation Level Management Reconfiguration of RA Aggregation Level (ID: 12795603)

**2.4.21.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12795604`)

Aggregation Level Management
PDCCH Aggregation Level management can be online reconfigured via PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

For each BWP which is under re-configuration, an entry in vector cellBwpDynamicAl[] of PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq  carries the reconfigurable parameters.

L2-PS retrieves IE raAggregationLevelSetReconfig  (dciRaMsg2AggregationLevel, dciRaCompletionAggregationLevel) from cellBwpDynamicAl[] of the dedicated BWP. raAggregationLevelSetReconfig is always configured in the dedicated BWP and a PDL rule in NIDD guarantees that raAggregationlevelSet parameters are configured to the same values in initial and dedicated BWP.

All parameters of   
PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.pdcchReconfiguration.cellBwpDynamicAl[].raAggregationlevelSetReconfig (for details refer to [12783423](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_eBjxYI5vEfCrqOdGJPoCRA)),  can be online reconfigured.

If at least one of the parameters corresponding to IEs  in raAggregationlevelSetReconfig, is reconfigured for a particular Bandwidth Part, then all other IEs in the structure shall be present.

---

#### 2.4.21.2.0-4 Aggregation Level Management Reconfiguration of Dedicated Aggregation Level (ID: 12795598)

**2.4.21.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12795599`)

Aggregation Level Management
PDCCH Aggregation Level management can be online reconfigured via PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

For each BWP which is under re-configuration, an entry in vector cellBwpDynamicAl[] of PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq  carries the reconfigurable parameters.

All parameters of   
PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.pdcchReconfiguration.cellBwpDynamicAl[].dedicatedAggregationlevelSetReconfig for details refer to [12783426](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rD_s8Y5vEfCrqOdGJPoCRA)  can be online reconfigured.

If at least one of the parameters corresponding to IEs  in dedicatedAggregationlevelSetReconfig is reconfigured for a particular Bandwidth Part, then all other IEs in the structure shall be present.

---

#### 2.4.21.2.0-5 Aggregation Level Management Reconfiguration of Dedicated HR Aggregation Level (ID: 12878319)

**2.4.21.2.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `12878320`)

Aggregation Level Management
PDCCH Aggregation Level management can be online reconfigured via PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

For each BWP which is under re-configuration, an entry in vector cellBwpDynamicAl[] of PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq  carries the reconfigurable parameters.

All parameters of   
PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.pdcchReconfiguration.cellBwpDynamicAl[].dedicatedAggregationlevelHRSetReconfig for details refer to [12783430](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_046jo45vEfCrqOdGJPoCRA)  can be online reconfigured.

If at least one of the parameters corresponding to IEs  in dedicatedAggregationlevelHRSetReconfig is reconfigured for a particular Bandwidth Part, then all other IEs in the structure shall be present.

#### 2.4.21.2.1 Dynamic Aggregation Level

##### 2.4.21.2.1.1 PDCCH Outer Loop Control and CQI management

###### 2.4.21.2.1.1.0-1 PDCCH Outer Loop Control and CQI management Downlink and Uplink feedbacks filtering (ID: 11173866)

**2.4.21.2.1.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173871`)

PDCCH Outer Loop Control and CQI management
- Relating to the filtering of the next DL-HARQ DTXs, following a CQI correction down triggered by a DL-HARQ DTX (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4079\_replaced([11170587](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_QLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) L2-PS implements the algorithm detailed here:

[Before PR575128]

* For each UE and DL serving cell, L2-PS shall maintain a variable which stores the xsfn of the latest DL HARQ DTX which has triggered a cqiCorrectionDown. The variable is intialized to an invalid value: xSfnLatestUsedDlDtx = invalid.
* Each time that a DL HARQ DTX report is received for the UE and DL serving cell, and for each expected HARQ bit of the report (in case of DL-HARQ bundling) L2-PS checks whether the DL HARQ DTX bit must be considered or not for pdcchCqiOffset calculation (i.e. has to trigger or not a down-correction of pdcchCqiOffset):
* First,L2-PS determines the xSfn of the corresponding DCI as xsfnDlDci = xsfnDlHarqReport – (k1 of the HARQ Process)
* Then, it takes the decision as specified below:

IF ( ( xSfnLatestUsedDlDtx == invalid ) OR ( xsfnDlDci > xSfnLatestUsedDlDtx ) )

{

xSfnLatestUsedDlDtx = xsfn of the DL HARQ DTX report

// use the DL HARQ DTX bit for cqiPdcchOffset update

}

ELSE

{

// do not use the DL HARQ DTX bit for cqiPdcchOffset update

}

Please note that each time that the calculation of pdcchCqiOffset is restarted, xSfnLatestUsedDlDtx has to be re-initialized.

* Relating to the filtering of the next UL-HARQ DTXs, following a CQI correction down triggered by a UL-HARQ DTX (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4079\_replaced([11170587](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_QLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS implements the same algorithm as above. The difference is that in UL there's no HARQ bundling. The handling in DL and UL are independent.

[PR575128]

The expected behaviour for each DL serving cell of the UE is that, after the pdcchCqiOffset is down-corrected following a DL HARQ = DTX, and the new pdcchCqiOffset is applied to the next PDCCH transmission L2-PS waits for the DL HARQ of the corrected PDCCH transmission before applying any further down-correction to the pdcchCqiOffset. This is implemeted in L2-PS SW with a state machine. It runs only if the dynamic AL Selection applies: aggregationlevelSelection = dynamic and a valid CQI is received.

* For each UE and DL serving cell, L2-PS shall maintain two new variables:
* pdcchCqiCorrectionDownState: the state of the state machine, initialized to applyCqiDownCorrectionAtNextDtxHarqReport;
* xsfnOf1stDownCorrectedCqiPdcch: xSfn of the 1st DL Grant transmission following a down-correction of the pdcchCqiOffset; it is initialized to an invalid value;

For a CA secondary cell (Scell) these variables have to be (re-)initialized each time that the Scell is activated. For a primary cell (Pcell) are (re-)initialized at each new RA of the UE.

* When a DL HARQ feedback is received for the UE and the DL Serving Cell:

IF((pdcchCqiCorrectionDownState == waitForHarqReportOf1stDownCorrectedCqiPdcch) AND

( (xsfnOf1stDownCorrectedCqiPdcch == xsfnGrant) OR (xsfnHarqFeedback - xsfnOf1stDownCorrectedCqiPdcch > k1max) )

)

{

pdcchCqiCorrectionDownState = applyCqiDownCorrectionAtNextDtxHarqReport;

}

IF((HARQ Report is DTX) AND (pdcchCqiCorrectionDownState == applyCqiDownCorrectionAtNextDtxHarqReport))

{

Apply correction down to cqiPdcchOffset;

pdcchCqiCorrectionDownState = transmitNextPdcchWithDownCorrectedCqi;

}

where xsfnGrant represents the xSfn on air of the corresponding DL grant, xsfnHarqFeedback is the xSfn on air of the HARQ feedback, k1max is calculated according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2473\_replaced([11116092](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* When a PDCCH is scheduled to grant a DL transmission:

IF(pdcchCqiCorrectionDownState == transmitNextPdcchWithDownCorrectedCqi)

{

pdcchCqiCorrectionDownState = waitForHarqReportOf1stDownCorrectedCqiPdcch;

xsfnOf1stDownCorrectedCqiPdcch = xsfnGrant;

}

where xsfnGrant is the xSfn on air of the PDCCH transmission.

Note: in case of HARQ bundling the HARQ feedback bits are either all DTX or all nonDTX.

* Relating to the filtering of the next UL-HARQ DTXs, following a CQI correction down triggered by a UL-HARQ DTX (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4079\_replaced([11170587](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_QLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS implements the same algorithm as above. Obviously k1max is replaced by k2max (calculated as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2484\_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) The difference is that in UL there's no HARQ bundling. The handling in DL and UL are independent.

[End PR575128]

* Relating to the filtring of the DL/UL notDTX when cqiAlSelection >= max( cqiDciCssAl[x] ) (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4079\_replaced([11170587](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_QLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2-PS implements the algorithm detailed here:

When the DL/UL grant is transmitted:

IF ( cqiAlSelection >= max( cqiDciCssAl[x] ) )

{

isSmallestPossibleAlUsed = true, where isSmallestPossibleAlUsed is a parameter of the DL/UL HARQ process

}

ELSE

{

isSmallestPossibleAlUsed = false, where isSmallestPossibleAlUsed is a parameter of the DL/UL HARQ process

}

When the corresponding DL/UL HARQ feedback is received:

IF( (HARQ feedback is not DTX) AND (isSmallestPossibleAlUsed == true) )

{

// do not use the nonDTX Feedback for cqiPdcchOffset update

}

ELSE

{

// do use the nonDTX Feedback for cqiPdcchOffset update

}

On DL and UL it applies in the same way but independently.

---

##### 2.4.21.2.1.2 reliability management

###### 2.4.21.2.1.2.0-1 reliability management Inner and Outer Loop in high Reliability (ID: 11173878)

**2.4.21.2.1.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173883`)

reliability management
The reliability of the PDCCH for a given bearer is independent for Downlink and Uplink. Therefore two Inner Loops and Outer Loop controls are needed, one for basic reliability and one for high reliability.

For simplification purpose, the inner loop parameter cqiPdcch, defined in req 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4082\_replaced([11170623](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), is calculated for both basic and high reliability as long as parameter DynamicAggregationlevelHrSet is defined for the bandwidth part.

Similarly, the outer loop parameter cqiPdcchOffset, defined in req 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4074\_replaced([11170531](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_QJ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), is calculated independently for basic and high reliability. When Uplink scheduler provide the Downlink scheduler with the DTX feedbacks to be used for Outer loop control, it also gives the reliability of the feedback.

When both Downlink and Uplink scheduler are calculing the aggregation Level to use based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4071\_replaced([11170399](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt-pGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),  the correct cqiPdcch and cqiPdcchOffset are chosen, basic or high reliability one, based on the relability of the UE for the given direction at the moment of the scheduling.

---

###### 2.4.21.2.1.2.0-2 reliability management Switch between High Reliability and Basic Reliability in Outer Loop Control (ID: 11173888)

**2.4.21.2.1.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173898`)

reliability management
Percentage of DL/UL PDCCH in HR OLC with low value of cqiAlSelection used for fallback and percentage of DL/UL PDCCH in BR OLC with high value of cqiAlSelection used for recovery counts valid samples as bellow:

[CB008246-H-UPD001]

L2-PS implementation of UPD001 in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16997\_replaced([11170693](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3PPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17138\_replaced([11170742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3QPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17263\_replaced([11170766](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3QvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18296\_replaced([11170717](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt_3PvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is done as followed:

* Initial monitoring period (no valid cqiAlSelection when period starts)

\* **Row 9-11**: These rows appear to be headers indicating different operational modes or states, with "HR" (likely High Reliability) and "BR" (likely Best Effort) being prominent.

| Feedback(noDTX,DTX) |  |  |  |  | \* | \* | \* | \* | \* | \* | \* |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| cqiPdcchOffset(t,BR) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

| cqiAlSelection (BR) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  | n+31 | n+32 |  |  |  |

* Other monitoring periods

Other cells contain an asterisk '\*' which might represent a default or undefined state.

\* **Colored Cells:** Cells within the table are colored differently (e.g., orange, blue, red outlines) to highlight specific periods or states. Red outlines are particularly prominent around certain blocks of cells.

\* The 'cqiPdcch(t,BR)' and 'cqiPdcch(t,HR)' rows show different patterns of values, suggesting distinct behaviors for BR and HR modes.

\* The red outlines seem to demarcate periods where specific configurations or events are active. For example, a red outline encloses 'n-10' values in the 'cqiPdcch(t,BR)' and 'cqiPdcch(t,HR)' rows from 'n-1' to 'n+11'. ### 2.4.21.3 DAI settings

### 2.4.21.3-1 DAI settings

### 2.4.21.4 Configuration for coresetFreqDomain and coresetDuration

#### 2.4.21.4.0-1 Configuration for coresetFreqDomain and coresetDuration Filling the numberOfRBs and numberOfSymbol parameters (ID: 11173915)

**2.4.21.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11173924`)

Configuration for coresetFreqDomain and coresetDuration
[Before CNI-109646-25R1] L2-PS shall store NumberOfRBs and NumberOfSymbols from CP-RT if temporary flag rdNumberOfRBsAndSymbolsFromCpRt is True.[End CNI-109646-25R1]

If CoresetId = 0 then L2-PS shall get from CP-RT the listed parameters:

* coresetFreqDomain from PsCell\_CellSetupReq\pdcchConfig for numberOfRBs where the number of RB corresponds to the number of bit set to 1 in coresetFreqDomain multiply by 6,  of the Control Resource Set used for the SIB1 transmissions;
* coresetDuration from PsCell\_CellSetupReq\pdcchConfig for numberOfSymbols of the Control Resource Set used for the SIB1 transmissions.

The parameter freqBandIndicatorNR is not used to identify the band indicator equal to “n79” and the frequency range (FR1: below n250, FR2: above n249).

If L2-PS not find CoresetId = 0 then L2-PS shall return error and the values: numberOfRBs, numberOfSymbols of the Control Resource Set 0 are not filled.

---

### 2.4.21.5 PDCCH Power Boost

#### 2.4.21.5.1 PDCCH Power Boost Overview

##### 2.4.21.5.1.0-1 PDCCH Power Boost Overview Overview (ID: 11173939)

**2.4.21.5.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173947`)

PDCCH Power Boost Overview
If PDCCH power boost is activated, and when there are un-configured CCEs or un-used CCEs in Coreset, then it probably brings potential gain to schedule more UE in one slot with downgraded AL but with higher PDCCH power.

The power boost gain has some dependency with bandwidth and Coreset size configuration, it also depends on the fragmentation in Coreset when allocating the PDCCH CCE resource.

Following figure is an example of TDD FR1 in 1 slot, the target AL for the 3 UE are all AL4 based on dynamic AL selection, with legacy PDCCH CCE allocation only 1 UE shall be scheduled.

With PDCCH power boost functionality activated, additional 2 UE will be scheduled as well in this slot with downgraded AL2 and 3 dB power offset from the un-configured and un-used CCEs.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2552\_replaced([11118347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqCkQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PDCCH power definition.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a 3D representation of PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) power allocation in a cellular network, likely 5G NR, illustrating how power is distributed among different User Equipments (UEs) within a slot. The x-axis represents time, marked by "symbol 0" and "1 slot". The y-axis represents PDCCH power.
The visualization shows several bars of varying heights and colors, representing PDCCH power allocated to different UEs (labeled UE-1, UE-2, UE-3). UE-1's PDCCH is shown in yellow, UE-2's in pink, and UE-3's in green. There's a clear indication of a "3 dB power offset" for UE-2 and UE-3 compared to UE-1, suggesting a power boost for these UEs.
Arrows point to different segments of the bars:
- A red arrow points to dashed red bars at the top, labeled "Unused PDCCH power".
- A green arrow points to a segment of UE-2's and UE-3's bars, labeled "PDCCH power with 3 dB boost in 1 CCE".
- A yellow arrow points to the base of UE-1's bars, labeled "PDCCH power in 1 CCE".
The diagram also indicates "Bandwidth" on the right side, with a bracket encompassing the range of the bars.
To the right of the main visualization, there is a table providing an "example of TDD FR1" configuration:

| Parameter | Value |
| --- | --- |
| BW | 30 MHz |
| Coreset size | 9 CCE |
| Coreset duration | 1 symbol |
| Monitor occasion | symbol 0 |
| numOfPdcchCandidatesInAL4 | n1 |
| numOfPdcchCandidatesInAL2 | n4 |
| UE-1 | 4 CCE |
| UE-2 | 2 CCE + 3 dB power boost |
| UE-3 | 2 CCE + 3 dB power boost |

Link in SharePoint:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B48F92348-84A1-4DA4-95CA-255D49D8E59E%7D&file=PDCCH%20power%20boost.xlsx&action=default&mobileredirect=true>

---

#### 2.4.21.5.2 PDCCH Power Boost Activation

##### 2.4.21.5.2.0-1 PDCCH Power Boost Activation (ID: 11173954)

**2.4.21.5.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173963`)

PDCCH Power Boost Activation
The PDCCH power boost is controlled by NIDD:

* NRCELL-actPdcchPowerBoost

When actPdcchPowerBoost is set to "enabled" or "enabledNonDTX", the power boost functionality is activated in the cell.

The AL downgraded level with power boost is controlled by 2 NIDD individually:

* NRBTS/NRDRB-allowedPdcchPowerBoostALDueCfg
* NRBTS/NRDRB-allowedPdcchPowerBoostALDueCce

For allowedPdcchPowerBoostALDueCfg, it targets for the UE which intended AL(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29330\_replaced([11171320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuC6kvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not configured, the power boost condition depends on:

* The allocated AL downgraded with only 1 level from the intended AL
* Any one of DRB on this UE configured with this allocated AL in allowedPdcchPowerBoostALDueCfg

For allowedPdcchPowerBoostALDueCce, it targets for the UE which got no CCE resource with calculated AL from dynamic AL selection, the power boost condition depends on:

* All of the DRB on this UE configured with the expected AL(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4071\_replaced([11170399](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt-pGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in allowedPdcchPowerBoostALDueCce

Note:

* The buffer status does not impact the power boost condition.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a network architecture diagram illustrating the relationship between a User Equipment (UE-i) and multiple Data Radio Bearers (DRB-1, DRB-2, DRB-3) through two Packet Data Unit (PDU) sessions.
UE-i is shown on the left side of the diagram. Two PDU sessions originate from UE-i: "PDU session-1" and "PDU session-2".
"PDU session-1" is represented by a light blue line connecting UE-i to a cluster of colored cylinders. This cluster is situated within a light blue oval shape. From this cluster, two lines extend to DRB-1 (orange line) and DRB-2 (dark blue line). The cluster itself contains several cylinders of different colors: orange, white, purple, green, and dark blue.
"PDU session-2" is represented by a yellow line connecting UE-i to a cluster of colored cylinders. This cluster is situated within a yellow oval shape. From this cluster, a single line extends to DRB-3 (dark blue line). This cluster contains three cylinders: red, black, and dark blue.
The diagram visually represents how a single UE can establish multiple PDU sessions, and each PDU session can be associated with one or more DRBs, facilitating different data flows or services.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4827.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Link in SharePoint:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B48F92348-84A1-4DA4-95CA-255D49D8E59E%7D&file=PDCCH%20power%20boost.xlsx&action=default&mobileredirect=true>

#### 2.4.21.5.3 Handle Power Boost for UE in WaitingList and CandidateList

##### 2.4.21.5.3.0-1 Handle Power Boost for UE in WaitingList and CandidateList Add UE to WaitingList or CandidateList (ID: 11173971)

**2.4.21.5.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11173978`)

Handle Power Boost for UE in WaitingList and CandidateList
When PDCCH power boost is activated, the PDCCH power resource shall be initialized and managed per slot in DL and UL direction.

If power boost condition due to configuration is fulfilled, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29324\_replaced([11171308](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuC6kPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when schedule UE in CS2 list, the UE shall be added to candidate list until the list is full. The candidate list size of DL and UL is controlled by:

* rdDlCandidateListForPDCCHPowerBoostingSize in DL, refer to 5G\_L2\_22520
* rdUlCandidateListForPDCCHPowerBoostingSize in UL, refer to 5G\_L2\_22522

If power boost condition due to CCE is fulfilled, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29326\_replaced([11171343](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuDhk_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), when schedule UE in CS2 list, the UE shall be added to waiting list until the list is full. The waiting list size of DL and UL is controlled by:

* rdDlWaitingListForPDCCHPowerBoostingSize in DL, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29364\_replaced([11171834](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuHzAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* rdUlWaitingListForPDCCHPowerBoostingSize in UL, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29366\_replaced([11171843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuHzA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The PDCCH power resource shall calculate the consuming PDCCH power for the UE with allocated CCE without power boost, and update the remaining PDCCH power resource accordingly.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the process of adding a User Equipment (UE) to a waiting list or candidate list. The flowchart begins with a start node and the title "Add Ue to waitingList or candidateList".
The process starts with "Schedule a Cell", followed by "Initialize PDCCH related resource". Then, it proceeds to "1 - Initialize PDCCH power resource per slot in each direction".
From this point, there's a decision point: "Is there UE to be scheduled in CS2 list?".
- If the answer is "no", the flow returns to the beginning of the loop, implying no UE is scheduled.
- If the answer is "yes", the process continues to "Get Aggeration Level".
After getting the "Aggeration Level", another decision point arises: "Is PDCCH resource available?".
- If the answer is "yes", the flow goes to "Save and update PDCCH CCE resource", then to "5 - Calculate UE's comsuming power and update remaining power resource".
- If the answer is "no", the flow goes to "2 - Is power boost waiting list full?".
- If "yes", it proceeds to "3 - Add UE to PDCCH waiting list".
- If "no", it proceeds to "4 - Downgrade AL with 1 level".
After "5 - Calculate UE's comsuming power and update remaining power resource", there's a decision: "6 - Is UE's AL marked as not intended?".
- If "no", the flow goes to "Go to select next UE in CS2 list".
- If "yes", it proceeds to "7 - Is power boost candidate list full?".
- If "yes", the flow goes to "Go to select next UE in CS2 list".
- If "no", it proceeds to "8 - Add UE to PDCCH candidate list".
After "3 - Add UE to PDCCH waiting list" and "4 - Downgrade AL with 1 level", both paths converge to a decision point which then leads to "Go to select next UE in CS2 list". Similarly, after "8 - Add UE to PDCCH candidate list", the flow also leads to "Go to select next UE in CS2 list".
The process then reaches "Go to select next UE in CS2 list", which is followed by "End loop UE in CS2 list", and finally a stop node.
The flowchart uses rounded rectangles for process steps, diamonds for decision points, and a filled circle for the start and end nodes. Arrows indicate the flow of the process. Some steps are highlighted in green, possibly indicating specific actions or states.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4830.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Descriptions:

1. Initialize PDCCH power resource in each direction in each slot, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29308\_replaced([11171220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuCTf_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29310\_replaced([11171229](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuCTgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. When UE with expected AL gets no CCE resource, condition shall be checked if this UE can be added to waiting list, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29322\_replaced([11171335](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuDhkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
3. Add this UE into waiting list for PDCCH power boost.
4. The expected AL shall be updated as 1 level downgraded.
5. For UE which have been allocated PDCCH CCE resource, the consuming PDCCH power shall be calculated and need to update the remaining power, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29314\_replaced([11171254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuCThPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
6. Check if this UE's selected AL is an intended AL or not.
7. When this UE's selected AL is an intended AL, check if candidate list is full, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29320\_replaced([11171300](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuC6iPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
8. Add this UE into candidate list for PDCCH power boost.

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=V8ishU&cid=9206de7d%2Df76b%2D4167%2D95a0%2D9ed94135ffa1&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F21%2E%20PDCCH%2FPDCCH%20power%20boost%20waitingList%20and%20candidateList&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

---

##### 2.4.21.5.3.0-2 Handle Power Boost for UE in WaitingList and CandidateList Handle UE in WaitingList and CandidateList (ID: 11173983)

**2.4.21.5.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11173991`)

Handle Power Boost for UE in WaitingList and CandidateList
The UE in waiting list have higher priority to get PDCCH resource allocation than the UE in candidate list.

Only when there's still PDCCH power remaining after handling UE in waiting list, allocation attempt for the UE with additional 3 dB power offset in candidate list shall be proceeded.

Each UE in waiting list or candidate list got the opportunity of power boost, the consuming power and remaining power shall be updated.

Note:

The UE in candidate list have already got the PDCCH CCE resource corresponding to the allocated AL even if there's no additional power boost granted.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process for handling User Equipment (UE) in a waiting list and a candidate list. The flowchart begins with a starting point, indicated by a black circle, followed by a yellow rounded rectangle labeled "Handle UE in waiting list".
From this initial step, a decision point is presented: "1 - Is there any UE in waiting list?".
- If the answer is "no", the flow continues to the right and then down, eventually leading to a loop back to the "Handle UE in waiting list" step.
- If the answer is "yes", the flow proceeds downwards to a green diamond shape labeled "2 - Is PDCCH CCE resource available with downgraded AL?".
From step 2:
- If the answer is "no", the flow goes to the right and then down, looping back to the "Handle UE in waiting list" step.
- If the answer is "yes", the flow continues downwards to a green diamond shape labeled "3 - Is PDCCH remaining power resource avaiable?".
From step 3:
- If the answer is "no", the flow goes to the right and then down, looping back to the "Handle UE in waiting list" step.
- If the answer is "yes", the flow proceeds downwards to a pink rounded rectangle labeled "4 - Save and update PDCCH CCE resource".
Following step 4, the flow moves to a pink rounded rectangle labeled "5 - Mark this UE with additional 3 dB power boost".
Next, it goes to a pink rounded rectangle labeled "6 - Calculate UE's consuming power and update remaining power".
After step 6, there are two green diamond shapes connected vertically. The first diamond has an arrow pointing down to the second diamond. The second diamond has an arrow pointing down to a gray rounded rectangle labeled "Go to select next UE in waiting list".
From "Go to select next UE in waiting list", the flow moves downwards to a yellow rounded rectangle labeled "Handle UE in candidate list".
From this step, a decision point is presented: "7 - Is there any UE in candidate list?".
- If the answer is "no", the flow goes to the right and then down, looping back to the "Handle UE in candidate list" step.
- If the answer is "yes", the flow proceeds downwards to a green diamond shape labeled "8 - Is PDCCH remaining power resource avaiable?".
From step 8:
- If the answer is "no", the flow goes to the right and then down, looping back to the "Handle UE in candidate list" step.
- If the answer is "yes", the flow continues downwards to a pink rounded rectangle labeled "9 - Mark this UE with additional 3 dB power boost".
Following step 9, the flow moves to a pink rounded rectangle labeled "10 - Calculate UE's consuming power and update remaining power".
After step 10, there are two green diamond shapes connected vertically, similar to the waiting list section. The first diamond has an arrow pointing down to the second diamond. The second diamond has an arrow pointing down to a gray rounded rectangle labeled "Go to select next UE in candidate list".
From "Go to select next UE in candidate list", the flow moves downwards to a yellow rounded rectangle labeled "End loop". The flowchart concludes with an ending point, indicated by a black circle.
The flowchart uses different shapes to represent different types of actions or decisions:
- Yellow rounded rectangles: Start/End points or main process steps.
- Green diamonds: Decision points.
- Pink rounded rectangles: Specific actions or operations.
- Gray rounded rectangles: Control flow steps.
- Arrows: Indicate the direction of the flow.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_4832.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Description:

1. Handle UEs in waiting list, and loop each UE in waiting list to check if the UE can be allocated with downgraded 1 level AL and additional 3 dB power offset, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29348\_replaced([11171360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuDhmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. Check if PDCCH CCE resource available for the downgraded AL.
3. Check if PDCCH remaining power resource is enough for power boost, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29350\_replaced([11171263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuC6gfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
4. Save and update the PDCCH CCE resource allocation.
5. Mark this UE with 3 dB additional power offset, which shall be used when filling DCI.
6. Calculate the UE's consuming power and update the remaining power, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29314\_replaced([11171254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuCThPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
7. Handle UEs in candidate list, loop each UE in candidate list to check if the UE can be allocated with additional 3 dB power offset, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29346\_replaced([11171327](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuC6lPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
8. Check if PDCCH remaining power resource is enough for power boost, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29350\_replaced([11171263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuC6gfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
9. Mark this UE with 3 dB additional power offset, which shall be used when filling DCI.
10. Calculate the UE's consuming power and update the remaining power, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29314\_replaced([11171254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuCThPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Link to SharePoint:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=V8ishU&cid=9206de7d%2Df76b%2D4167%2D95a0%2D9ed94135ffa1&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E4%20Algorithm%2F21%2E%20PDCCH%2FPDCCH%20power%20boost%20waitingList%20and%20candidateList&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>
