* prbDemandRimRs according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12045\_replaced([11160807](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3YfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* prbDemandForArtificialLoadForEmfValidation, as specified in 5G\_L2\_18383\_replaced([11174645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26luBPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* [CB014543-D,E] avgUsedPrbNonGbr historical load according to [12815922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_BYQ8YKfvEfCrANRYxAlR5A) [End CB014543-D,E]

2) In slot 0 of first SFN of bbPoolingPeriod N+1, DL and UL scheduler send previously calculated PrbDemands and [CB014543-D,E] non-GBR historical load [End CB014543-D,E](see 5G_L2_4650_replaced([11244424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B2zovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and [12815922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_BYQ8YKfvEfCrANRYxAlR5A)) **to BBRM.**

3) In slot 3 of each SFN, or slot 4 if cellTechnology is TDD and UL grants are available in slot 3 and PUSCH can be scheduled for slot 3 + k2, DL and UL schedulers request assignment of baseband resources from BBRM (even if new assignments/allocation are done only once per pooling period).

BBRM responds immediately with assigned baseband resources which includes CurrentActiveSubcellList through ulsubcells IE.

In first SFN of a bbPoolingPeriod, BBRM determines new distribution of baseband resources to all cells of the L1 subpool based on latest received prbDemandGbr, prbDemandNonGbr and prbDemandRim, prbDemandUlcgType2, prbDemandRimRs and [CB014543-D,E] avgUsedPrbNonGbr([12815922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_BYQ8YKfvEfCrANRYxAlR5A)) [End CB014543-D,E]

The result of the Loadbased algorithm, based on the different prbDemands is simulated in the following excel sharepoint:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5GdocRD/RD%20NPS%20documents/09%20-%20Features/24R1-SR/CB011028/Design/CB11028_CB10513_8940_LoadMetricAlgo%20TddFr1AlgEnh_v0.3.xlsx?d=w1837300d6ef945ecb2e22ddb1d5b38ef&csf=1&web=1&e=eRIqjz>

4) DL and UL scheduler apply new resource assignment in next SFN after reception of resource assignment..

**Exceptions:**

1. If, for the given cell, 5G-L2-PS could not receive PrbDemands [CB014543-D,E] and non-GBR historical load [End CB014543-D,E](see 5G_L2_4650_replaced([11244424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B2zovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and [12815922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_BYQ8YKfvEfCrANRYxAlR5A)) in its PRB pooling algorithm, 5G-L2-PS shall use PrbDemands [CB014543-D,E] and non-GBR historical load [End CB014543-D,E] received for this cell in the previous bbPoolEvalPeriod (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1555\_replaced([10966229](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UNM1Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in its PRB pooling algorithm.
2. If, for the given cell, 5G-L2-PS never received PrbDemands [CB014543-D,E] and non-GBR historical load [End CB014543-D,E] (see 5G\_L2\_4650\_replaced([11244424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B2zovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and [12815922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_BYQ8YKfvEfCrANRYxAlR5A)) in its PRB pooling algorithm, 5G-L2-PS shall set each of the PRB demands [CB014543-D,E] and non-GBR historical load [End CB014543-D,E] for this cell as equal to 0 in its PRB pooling algorithm.

If slowPrbPoolingFddFr1EnhAllowed is True(5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_7785\_replaced([11278788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNePvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),Load Metric based PRB pooling algorithm shall consider Layer PRB Capacity during stream Prb Calculation.

[Before CB014543-D,E]

For Stream Cell PRB in FDD and TDD, BBRM shall execute legacy PRB pooling algorithm as mention in [11212393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_QYQikPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

[CB014543-D,E]

For Stream Cell PRB in FDD and TDD, If histUsageAllowed is TRUE, BBRM shall execute enhanced PRB pooling algorithm for non-GBR as mention in [12707888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Y9YvcGLWEfC3KtQ77je1xA)(non-GBR), Else legacy PRB pooling algorithm for non-GBR as mentioned in [11212609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_RjHMIPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)(non-GBR)

[End CB014543-D,E]

BBRM pooling algorithm to balance subcell allocation between 2 L1 pools is introduced (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15387\_replaced([11212881](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxDyTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

When NRBTS/NRCELL.actUlCgType2 is set to TRUE and  slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed is TRUE, the demand estimations are done based on the layer PRB basis as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25712\_replaced([11214266](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxMVLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25732\_replaced([11214077](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxLuEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note: The above parameter prbDemandRim used is valid for only UL related scenarios.

Note: The parameter prbDemandUlcgType2 is applicable only for UL. For DL, it is always set to 0

**Note: The parameter prbDemandRimRs is applicable only for DL. For UL, it is always set to 0**

---

**2.4.23.4.1.3.5.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11174724`)

Metric Management
Load Metric based Subcell Deactivation Algorithm for UL

---

**2.4.23.4.1.3.5.0-8.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11174732`)

Metric Management
Subcell Deactivation Execution Steps -

Trigger below steps every BB pooling period (NRBTS.bbPoolEvalPeriod), just before the inter-L1 subpool UL PRB resource sharing algorithm.

1.1 [CB009890-B] When ulSubcellDeactForARMAllowed is TRUE, ARM capacity UL subcell deactivation procedure shall be executed. [End CB009890-B]

1.2 [CB009890-D] If ulSubcellDeactForARMAllowed is TRUE, when at least one of the cells in L1 pool has proposed UL secondary subcell after execution of ARM capacity UL subcell deactivation procedure and at least one of the cells configured in L1 pool does not support UL PRB muting and TddUlPrbMutingWorkaroundAllowed = TRUE, UL subcell deactivation procedure for cells not supporting UL PRB muting shall be executed.[End CB009890-D]

1.3 [CB009890-D] If ulSubcellDeactForARMAllowed is FALSE,[End CB009890-D] when at least one of the cells configured in L1 pool has configured UL secondary subcells and at least one of the cells configured in L1 pool does not support UL PRB muting and TddUlPrbMutingWorkaroundAllowed = TRUE, UL subcell deactivation procedure [CB009890-B] for cells not supporting UL PRB muting [End CB009890-B] shall be executed.

1. proposedActiveUlSubcellList(i) shall be updated for each cell within L1 Pool after end of UL subcell deactivation procedure.
2. proposedActiveUlSubcellList(i) shall be considered in inter-L1 subpool algorithm executed in the same BB pooling period for the calculation of maximum cell capacity for each of the cells in the L1 pool (Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7012\_replaced([11212774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxDLPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
3. Based on L1 Reconfig Procedure Result if its "Success" proposedActiveUlSubcellList(i) shall be considered as the new active UL subcell list which is updated to currentActiveUlSubcellList(i) and it will be considered in next BB pooling period Or if its "Failure" currentActiveUlSubcellList(i) shall be used for next pooling Period as well.
4. Send currentActiveUlSubcellList(i) to L2-PS.
5. In L2-PS - Update CurrentActiveSubcellList for each cell and it shall be used for UL MU MIMO Scheduling (Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6959\_replaced([11213357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxG1mfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14781\_replaced([11146479](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksEkkfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Exceptions:

If BBRM did not receive prbDemandGbr/prbDemandNonGbr from any of the cells, BBRM will use prbDemandGbr/prbDemandNonGbr from previous bbPoolingPeriod of this cell.

If BBRM never received prbDemandGbr/prbDemandNonGbr for a cell, it applies prbDemandGbr = 0 and prbDemandNonGbr = 0.

---

**2.4.23.4.1.3.5.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11174737`)

Metric Management
Load Metric based Subcell Deactivation Algorithm for DL

---

**2.4.23.4.1.3.5.0-8.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11174741`)

Metric Management
Subcell Deactivation Execution Steps -

Trigger below steps every BB pooling period (NRBTS.bbPoolEvalPeriod), just before the inter-L1 subpool DL PRB resource sharing algorithm.

1. [CB009890-A] When dlSubcellDeactForARMAllowed is TRUE, ARM capacity DL subcell deactivation procedure shall be executed. [End CB009890-A]

Steps 2 and on for DL are same as in 5G\_L2\_10203\_replaced([11174724](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26mVGvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for UL.

---

**2.4.23.4.1.3.5.0-8.0-4**  (ID: `13359842`)

Metric Management
**Load Metric based Partial ZAB algorithm for DL**

---

**2.4.23.4.1.3.5.0-8.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `13359843`)

Metric Management
If partialZABAllowed is TRUE for stream PRB in FDD or TDD then BBRM shall execute partial ZAB algorithm as mentioned in [12804289](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_UlH-8Z6QEfCkxIKMlo4L6w).

Else legacy ZAB privilege algorithm as mentioned in [11213394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_VjnNIPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ). For providing the ZAB privilege to a cell, the allocated cell PRB for partial ZAB shall be greater than zero.

Note: During RIM-RS PRB calculation, it is calculated for each cell but partialZabCapacityForAllCsiRsPoolingGroups is reduced by rimRsZabReservation for remaining PRB. Here rimRsZabReservation([12823121](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_c3kAmapUEfCrANRYxAlR5A)) is considered for all CsiRsPoolingGroup's cells except one CsiRsPoolingGroup's cell because they will be transmitting wideband signals instead of RIM-RS. Due to this it is possible that sometime total partialZabAllocatedPrb of all cells may be greater than partial zab capacity but in real it won't be transmitting more than partial zab capacity.

---

###### 2.4.23.4.1.3.6 Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling

###### 2.4.23.4.1.3.6.0-1 Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling Inter L1 Subpool Resource Reconfiguration Request (ID: 11174751)

**2.4.23.4.1.3.6.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11174763`)

Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling
If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed = TRUE (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rdSlowPrbPoolingRangeTddUl set to '1'(5G\_L2\_6312\_replaced([11188119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28GJ5vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB009215-B] or (slowPrbPoolingFddFr1EnhAllowed = TRUE (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_7785\_replaced([11278788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNePvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rdAllowInterL1SpPoolingFdd set to '1'(5G\_UP\_ALG\_19197\_replaced([11215293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxSbyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))))[End CB009215-B], and L1 address is known for the UL L1 pool, BBRM shall send UlPool\_BbResourceReconfigReq message to L1 UL at the slot3 of the second SFN of the pooling period after inter L1 subpool pooling algorithm (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10390\_replaced([11213243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxGOh_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for inter pooling algorithm) at each pooling period.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed = TRUE (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rdSlowPrbPoolingRangeTddDl set to '1'(5G\_L2\_6312\_replaced([11188119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28GJ5vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB009215-B] or (slowPrbPoolingFddFr1EnhAllowed = TRUE (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_7785\_replaced([11278788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNePvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rdAllowInterL1SpPoolingFdd set to '1'(5G\_UP\_ALG\_19197\_replaced([11215293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxSbyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))))[End CB009215-B], and L1 address is known for the DL L1 pool, BBRM shall send DlPool\_BbResourceReconfigReq message to L1 DL at the slot3 of the second SFN of the pooling period after inter L1 subpool pooling algorithm (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10387\_replaced([11213171](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxFnhvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for inter pooling algorithm) at each pooling period.

The SFN in which L1 shall apply the new reconfigurations is the second SFN of the next pooling period.

The value of addrBbPoolingResourceReconfResp shall be filled with the BBRM EqId that is defined internally in BBRM.

the content of the UlPool\_BbResourceReconfigReq message , see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14815\_replaced([11515370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR7g4vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

the content of the DlPool\_BbResourceReconfigReq message , see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14842\_replaced([11513342](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRv6rPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The changes under flag slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed are applicable for Large Baseband Pooling (CB007112) also.

---

###### 2.4.23.4.1.3.6.0-2 Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling Inter L1 Subpool Resource Reconfiguration Response (ID: 11174766)

**2.4.23.4.1.3.6.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11174785`)

Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling
L1 UL shall reply UlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message within the current pooling period by sending UlPool\_BbResourceReconfResp message to BBRM.

L1 DL shall reply DlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message within the current pooling period by sending DlPool\_BbResourceReconfResp message to BBRM.

In the case of receiving the response, BBRM shall check if the SFN received in UlPool\_BbResourceReconfResp corresponds to the SFN of UlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq, if no, BBRM shall discard this response message and consider this case to no response from L1 UL.  if yes, BBRM shall check the received status,  update the new configuration in case "success" status, or keep the current stream prb/layer capacity per L1 subpool in case of "failure" status (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10389\_replaced([11213186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxGOgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

-If TddUlPrbMutingWorkaroundAllowed = TRUE (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_9169\_replaced([11279052](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpTbm_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), In the case of receiving the response as "Success" in UL, BBRM updates currentActiveUlSubcellList for each of the cells in L1 pool with the proposedActiveUlSubcellList. The rationale is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15493\_replaced([11213691](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxJR1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) otherwise in the case of receiving the response as "Failure" in UL, BBRM keeps the currentActiveUlSubcellList for each of the cells in L1 pool unchanged. The rationale is described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15493\_replaced([11213691](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxJR1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In the case of receiving the response, BBRM shall check if the SFN received in DlPool\_BbResourceReconfResp corresponds to the SFN of DlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq, if no, BBRM shall discard this response message and consider this case to no response from L1 DL.  if yes, BBRM shall check the received status,  update the new configuration in case "success" status, or keep the current stream prb/layer capacity per L1 subpool in case of "failure" status (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10389\_replaced([11213186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxGOgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

BBRM shall apply the new capacity values in the next intra pooling algorithm. In case of no response from L1 to BBRM reconfiguration request within the current pooling period, BBRM shall use, in this intra pooling algorithm, the minimum of subpool capacities between the new proposed allocation and the current allocation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10389\_replaced([11213186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxGOgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

if BBRM does not send UlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message because UL EqId address is unknown, BBRM keeps the initial UL subpool capacities.

if BBRM does not send DlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message because DL EqId address is unknown, BBRM keeps the initial DL subpool capacities.

The changes under flag  slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed are applicable for Large Baseband Pooling (CB007112) also.

The changes under flag slowPrbPoolingFddFr1EnhAllowed are applicable for FDD FR1 Pooling Enhancement (CB009215) also.

NOTE :

* [PR805135]  BBRM shall alarm by ERR  syslog when the number of consecutive L1 Processing Resources Reconfiguration messages  failure  from L1 (DlPool\_BbResourceReconfResp / UlPool\_BbResourceReconfResp) reaches the R&D parameter rdNumOfConsecutiveL1ReconfFailThreshold (Defined in 5G\_L2\_23171\_replaced([11188249](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28HYAfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End PR805135]

---

###### 2.4.23.4.1.3.6.0-3 Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling Algorithm and BB Resource Reconfiguration trigger (ID: 11174790)

**2.4.23.4.1.3.6.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `11174837`)

Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling
If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed is true (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rdSlowPrbPoolingRangeTddUl set to '1'(5G\_L2\_6312\_replaced([11188119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28GJ5vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or (slowPrbPoolingFddFr1EnhAllowed = TRUE (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_7785\_replaced([11278788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNePvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rdAllowInterL1SpPoolingFdd set to '1'(5G\_UP\_ALG\_19197\_replaced([11215293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxSbyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))), and L1 address is known for this UL L1 pool, BBRM calculates the capacity of the subpools in UL within L1 pool with inter pooling algorithm (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10390\_replaced([11213243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxGOh_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the calculated subpool capacities are sent to L1 through UlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message.

When TddUlPrbMutingWorkaroundAllowed is True, the UL inter subpool pooling algorithm takes as input the output of the subcell deactivation mechanism for cells not supporting UL PRB muting (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7012\_replaced([11212774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxDLPPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15493\_replaced([11213691](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxJR1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). And when ulSubcellDeactForARMAllowed is True, the UL inter pooling algorithm takes as input the output of the ARM capacity subcell deactivation mechanism as well.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rdSlowPrbPoolingRangeTddDl set to '1'(5G\_L2\_6312\_replaced([11188119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28GJ5vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or (slowPrbPoolingFddFr1EnhAllowed = TRUE (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_7785\_replaced([11278788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNePvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and rdAllowInterL1SpPoolingFdd set to '1'(5G\_UP\_ALG\_19197\_replaced([11215293](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxSbyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))))  or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed is true, and L1 address is known for this DL L1 pool, BBRM calculates the capacity of the subpools in DL within L1 pool with inter pooling algorithm (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10390\_replaced([11213243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxGOh_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26501\_replaced([11214551](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for layer PRB ), the calculated subpool capacities are sent to L1 through DlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message.

When dlSubcellDeactForARMAllowed is True, the DL inter pooling algorithm takes as input the output of the ARM capacity subcell deactivation mechanism.

The following information are transferred to L1 ( see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14818\_replaced([11515379](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR8H4PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• The calculated subpool capacity:

* Maximum number of allocated stream PRB per subpool
* Maximum number of allocated layer PRB per subpool

• The SFN in which L1 shall apply the new reconfigurations; this SFN is the second SFN of the next pooling interval (see the below figure and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10389\_replaced([11213186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxGOgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In the second SFN of every pooling period (see the below figure), [CNI-127661] Upon the reception of PsInternal\_InterSubPoolsSynchroTriggerInd message  (see 5G\_L2\_22324\_replaced([11244146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B0-dfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CNI-127661], BBRM shall trigger the UL and DL inter L1 subpool pooling algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_10390\_replaced([11213243](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxGOh_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for all L1 pools to calculate the subpool capacity and send the UlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq to UL L1 after the inter pooling algorithm at the same SFN (second SFN).

The result of DL inter L1 subpool pooling algorithm  is simulated in the following excel sharepoint:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5GdocRD/RD%20NPS%20documents/09%20-%20Features/24R1-SR/CB011028/Design/CB011028-BBRM%20PRB%20allocation%20interL1subpool%20-%20V1.xlsx?d=wc3d3351e3f1c4a1e89fbc84769d95f06&csf=1&web=1&e=VJnEH1>

[Before CNI-127661]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a timeline of resource allocation and processing within a communication system, likely related to cellular networks. The diagram spans multiple SFN (System Frame Number) periods, from N-1 to N+6, and shows different layers of operation: SCH DL (Scheduling Downlink), SCH UL (Scheduling Uplink), BBRM (Baseband Resource Management), SCH (Scheduling), L1 DL (Layer 1 Downlink), L1 UL (Layer 1 Uplink), and L1 (Layer 1).
The diagram uses color-coding to represent different types of actions or states:
- Red: Indication
- Blue: Resource Req/Resp
- Green: L1 SP Reconf Req/Rsp
- Grey: New resource allocation processing intra L1SP based on L1 SP capacity calculated at previous pooling period
- Orange: New resource allocation processing inter L1SP
Key elements and their interactions are depicted:
**Timeline and Slots:**
The horizontal axis represents time, divided into SFNs, and further into slots (0 to 5, and 'x' which likely represents a special slot or end-of-frame marker).
**SCH DL and SCH UL:**
These rows show resource requests and responses represented by blue arrows. These occur at various slots across different SFNs.
**BBRM:**
This row indicates resource management activities.
- In SFN N-1, slot 3, there's a blue arrow indicating a Resource Req/Resp.
- In SFN N, slot 0, there's a red block labeled "Indication" with two red arrows pointing down, suggesting an event or trigger.
- In SFN N, slot 1, there's a blue arrow indicating a Resource Req/Resp.
- In SFN N+1, slot 2, there's an orange block labeled "New resource allocation processing inter L1SP" with blue arrows indicating Resource Req/Resp.
- In SFN N+1, slot 3, there's a grey block with the text "Apply new BBRM resources".
- In SFN N+4, slot 0, there's a red block labeled "Indication" with two red arrows pointing down.
- In SFN N+5, slot 2, there's a blue arrow indicating a Resource Req/Resp.
- In SFN N+6, slot 1, there's an orange block labeled "New resource allocation processing inter L1SP" with blue arrows indicating Resource Req/Resp.
- In SFN N+6, slot 2, there's a grey block with the text "Apply new RBRM".
**SCH:**
This row shows broader allocation periods.
- From SFN N+1, slot 3 onwards, there's a grey bar labeled "BBRM Allocation with L1 SP capacity available at previous pooling period".
- A green arrow originates from SFN N+1, slot 2, pointing to the right and upwards, with the text "Apply new L1 SP capacity". This arrow seems to indicate the application of new L1 SP capacity.
**L1 DL and L1 UL:**
These rows are not explicitly populated with specific events but are part of the layered structure.
**L1:**
This row represents the Layer 1 operations and capacity.
- A large green block spans from SFN N+1, slot 3, to the end of the diagram, labeled "L1 SP capacity calculated at previous pooling period".
- A green block appears in SFN N+1, slot 2, with a green arrow pointing downwards, labeled "L1 SP Reconf Req/Rsp".
- Another green block appears in SFN N+6, slot 2, with a green arrow pointing downwards, labeled "L1 SP Reconf Req/Rsp".
- A green arrow originates from SFN N+1, slot 2, pointing to the right and downwards, with the text "Apply new L1 SP capacity".
**Pooling Period:**
A red bracket at the bottom indicates a "Pooling Period" that spans from SFN N to SFN N+4.
The diagram visually represents how resource requests and indications trigger updates in BBRM and L1 SP capacity over time, with specific actions being applied at different points in the timeline. The concept of "L1 SP capacity calculated at previous pooling period" is central to the resource allocation strategy shown.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5010.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[CNI-127661]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation and processing within a system, likely related to telecommunications or network management. The diagram spans multiple time units, labeled as SFN (System Frame Number) N-1 through N+6, with each SFN divided into slots 0 through 5 and an 'x' slot.
The diagram is organized into several horizontal tracks, each representing a different aspect or layer of the system:
1. **Top Blue Bar:** This bar, labeled "8940/7113", appears to represent a high-level timeline or a specific operational period. It contains a subtle arrow indicating progression.
2. **SFN and Slot Labels:** This track clearly delineates the time units (SFN N-1 to N+6) and the slots within each SFN.
3. **SCH DL and SCH UL:** These tracks, likely representing Scheduling Downlink and Uplink, show colored blocks indicating resource allocation or events.
\* A red block appears in SFN N-1, slot 3, and SFN N+4, slot 0.
\* A light blue block appears in SFN N, slot 3, and SFN N+5, slot 2.
\* A yellow block appears in SFN N+1, slot 3, and SFN N+6, slot 3.
\* An orange block appears in SFN N+2, slot x.
\* Arrows are associated with some of these blocks, indicating directionality or triggers.
4. **BBRM:** This track, likely representing a Base Band Resource Management or similar function, shows a grey bar spanning from SFN N to SFN N+2, with text "Apply new BBRM resources" and an arrow pointing to the right. Another grey bar appears from SFN N+4 to SFN N+5, also with "Apply new BBRM resources". An orange block is present in SFN N+2, slot x, with arrows pointing up and down.
5. **SCH:** This track shows a grey bar from SFN N to SFN N+2, with the text "BBRM Allocation with L1 SP capacity available at previous pooling period". Another grey bar is present from SFN N+4 to SFN N+5, with the text "Apply new L1 SP capacity".
6. **L1 DL and L1 UL:** These tracks are mostly empty, suggesting no specific events are highlighted here.
7. **L1:** This track shows a dark green bar from SFN N-1 to SFN N+2, with the text "L1 SP capacity calculated at previous pooling period". A lighter green bar starts from SFN N+2 and extends to SFN N+6. An arrow originating from SFN N+4, slot 0, points to the right with the text "Apply new L1 SP capacity". Another arrow originates from SFN N+2, slot x, and points to the right with the same text. A green block appears in SFN N, slot 3, with arrows pointing down.
8. **Bottom Red Bar:** This bar indicates a "Pooling Period" and spans from SFN N-1 to SFN N+5.
**Legend:**
The legend at the top explains the meaning of the colors used in the diagram:
\* **Red:** Indication
\* **Light Blue:** Resource Req/Resp
\* **Green:** L1 SP Reconf Req/Rsp
\* **Yellow:** Inter-SubPools Synchro Trigger Indication
\* **Grey (in BBRM/SCH tracks):** New resource allocation processing intra L1SP based on L1 SP capacity calculated at previous pooling period
\* **Orange (in BBRM/SCH tracks):** New resource allocation processing inter L1SP
The diagram illustrates a process where L1 SP capacity is calculated, then used for BBRM allocation. New resource allocations are triggered and applied over time, with specific indications and requests/responses occurring at different SFNs and slots. The pooling period defines a significant timeframe for these operations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5010.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End CNI-127661]

Note: Reconfiguration request message is sent to L1 even if the inter L1 subpool algorithm has provided the same values for the L1 subpool sizes as in the current pooling period. A further optimisation could be introduced to send the request only in case of L1 subpool sizes modification.

The changes under flag slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed are applicable for Large Baseband Pooling (CB007112) also.

##### 2.4.23.4.1.4 PM Counter Support

###### 2.4.23.4.1.4.0-1 PM Counter Support Impacted Counters (ID: 11174844)

PM Counter Support

[CB009215-B]

If slowPrbPoolingFddFr1EnhAllowed is set 'true', no allocation could be allowed in particular slots due to allocation of signals on other cells see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2382\_replaced([11115299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kptNEPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19557\_replaced([11115143](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kprX5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

##### 2.4.23.4.1.5 PRB Pooling performances

###### 2.4.23.4.1.5.0-1 PRB Pooling performances PRBs availability for DL PDSCH Scheduling (ID: 11174866)

**2.4.23.4.1.5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11174875`)

PRB Pooling performances
The purpose of this requirement is informational. The goal is to help testing and debug teams to understand and analyze the PRB usage for DL scheduling in the scope of Slow PRB Pooling in FR1 TDD.

Depending on Cell bandwidth configuration and numAllocCellPrb, in every slot available for DL PDSCH scheduling, some PRBs available for DL scheduling among numAllocCellPrb alllocated by BBRM to the cell, can be wasted (i.e. can never be used for scheduling), and the number of RBGs that can be allocated varies.

The wasted PRBs, number of RBGs that can be allocated and the associated throughput degradation per DL slot can be computed using the excel tool at the following link:

[Before CB010513-D]

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/CB008940%20Tools/Wasted%20PRB%20compute.xlsx?d=w8170b7cd4ea74239a7d24dbfa302f0a1&csf=1&web=1&e=E7F0Rv>

[CB010513-D]

[https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS\_Tech\_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/CB010513%20Tools?csf=1&web=1&e=zaGrSc](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/CB010513%20Tools?csf=1&web=1&e=zaGrSc%20)

[End CB010513-D]

The algorithm run in this tool is described below:

 Input parameters:

 BWP: number of PRBs in bandwidth part

 P = number of PRBs in a full RBG of current BWP

 firstRbgSize = size of the first RBG (firstRbgSize = P if full ; firstRbgSize < P if partial)

 Intermediate Values:

 lastRbgSize = size of the last RBG (lastRbgSize = P if full ; lastRbgSize < P if partial)

 numPrb1 = number of remaining PRBs out of first RBG and out of all full RBGs that could be totally filled with allocated PRBs on first Subcell when numAllocCellPb < BWP

 numPrb2 = number of remaining PRBs out of first RBG and out of all full RBGs that could be totally filled with allocated PRBs on second Subcell when numAllocCellPb > BWP

 Output parameters:

 wastedPrb = number of PRBs among numAllocCellPrb provided by BBRM that cannot be allocated for scheduling

 numRbg1 = number of RBGs that can be allocated on subcell1

 numRbg2 = number of RBGs that can be allocated on Subcell 2

 If numAllocCellPRB = BWP

o WastedPrb = 0 (full BWP usable on 1st Subcell)

o numRbg1 = ((BWP – firstRbgSize -lastRbgSize) div P) + 2 // all RBGs usable on subcell1

o numRbg2 = 0

 Else if numAllocCellPrb = 2\*BWP

o numRbg1 = ((BWP – firstRbgSize -lastRbgSize) div P) + 2 // all RBGs usable on subcell1

o If (all UEs scheduled on a single Subcell)

 wastedPrb = N/A

 numRbg2 = 0

o else

 wastedPrb = 0 (full BWP usable on 1st Subcell and 2nd subcell)

 numRbg2 = ((BWP – firstRbgSize -lastRbgSize) div P) + 2 // all RBGs usable on both subcells

o endif

[Before CB010513-D]

 Else if numAllocCellPrb < BWP

o numPrb1 = (numAllocCellPrb – firstRbgSize) mod P)

o numRbg1 = (numAllocCellPrb – firstRbgSize) div P) + 1 //number of full RBGs + first RBG

o numRbg2 = 0

o If numPrb1 >= lastRbgSize

 If actMuMimoEnh = false

 wastedPrb = numPrb1 – lastRbgSize

 numRbg1 = numRbg1 + 1

 else if actMuMimoEnh = true

 wastedPrb = numPrb1 + (firstRbgsize mod P)

 numRbg1 = numRbg1 – 1 + (firstRbgSize div P) // the first RBG cannot be used either, except if full

o else if numPrb1 < lastRbgSize

 wastedPrb = numPrb1

o endif

 Else if numAllocCellPrb > BWP

o numRbg1 = ((BWP – firstRbgSize - lastRbgSize) div P) + 2 // all RBGs usable on subcell1

o If (all UEs scheduled on a single Subcell) // all RBGs usable on subcell1

 wastedPrb = N/A

 numRbg2 = 0

o else

 if (numAllocCellPrb – BWP < firstRbgSize)

 wastedPrb = numAllocCellPrb – BWP

 numRbg2 = 0 // no RBG can be allocated on Subcell 2

 else

 numPrb2 = ((numAllocCellPrb - BWP - firstRbgSize) mod P)

 wastedPrb = numPrb2 + (firstRbgSize mod P)

 numRbg2 = ((numAllocCellPrb - BWP – (firstRbgSize mod P)) div P) // number of full RBGs in (numAllocCellPrb – BWP)

 endif

o endif

 endif

[CB010513-D]

 Else if numAllocCellPrb < BWP

o numPrb1 = (numAllocCellPrb – firstRbgSize) mod P

o numRbg1 = (numAllocCellPrb – firstRbgSize) div P + 1 //number of full RBGs + first RBG

o numRbg2 = 0

o If numPrb1 >= lastRbgSize

o wastedPrb = numPrb1 – lastRbgSize

o numRbg1 = numRbg1 + 1

o else if numPrb1 < lastRbgSize

 wastedPrb = numPrb1

o endif

 Else if numAllocCellPrb > BWP // actDlMuMimoEnh = true => first RBG is full

o numRbg1 = ((BWP – firstRbgSize - lastRbgSize) div P) + 2 // all RBGs usable on subcell1

o If (all UEs scheduled on a single Subcell) // all RBGs usable on subcell1

 wastedPrb = N/A

 numRbg2 = 0

o else

o numPrb2 = ((numAllocCellPrb – BWP) mod P)

o numRbg2 = ((numAllocCellPrb - BWP) div P) -1 // count of full RBGs in (numAllocCellPrb – BWP) except first one. Note: numRbg2 can be -1 at this stage if (numAllocCellPrb – BWP < firstRbgSize).

o if numPrb2 >= lastRbgSize

 wastedPrb = numPrb2 – lastRbgSize

 numRbg2 = numRbg2 + 2 // last RBG can be used

o else if numPrb2 < lastRbgSize

 wastedPrb = numPrb2

 numRbg2 = numRbg2 + 1

o endif

o     endif

 endif

[End CB010513-D]

Notes:

(1) See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4459\_replaced([11178370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for first and last RBG size computation.

(2) The throughput degradation caused by these wasted PRB compared to theoretical throughput is tPutLoss = wastedPrb/numAllocCellPrb (for one DL slot)

---

##### 2.4.23.4.1.6 Interaction with other features

###### 2.4.23.4.1.6.0-1 Interaction with other features Implementation of DL artificial load (ID: 11174885)

**2.4.23.4.1.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11174888`)

Interaction with other features
From the L2-PS point of view, the interaction of the DL artificial load and the PRB pooling features is implemented in a generic way, more specifically, it is independent from the used technology (TDD or FDD)(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4497\_replaced([11183858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvN_MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Therefore, the activation or the deactivation of the DL artificial load with PRB pooling is controlled at OAM level.

---

###### 2.4.23.4.1.6.0-2 Interaction with other features DL artificial load in TRS/CSI-RS slot (ID: 11174892)

**2.4.23.4.1.6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11174898`)

Interaction with other features
From the L2-PS point of view, if slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is set to 'true' the mechanism of not scheduling any PDSCH in TRS/CSI-RS slot is applicable either if the DL artificial load is activated or deactivated (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4497\_replaced([11183858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvN_MfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 2.4.23.4.2 Layer PRB pooling

##### 2.4.23.4.2.1 Pooling Deployment

###### 2.4.23.4.2.1.0-1 Pooling Deployment Pooling deployment error cases (ID: 11174912)

**2.4.23.4.2.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11174916`)

Pooling Deployment
If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed == true, LayerPrbPooling is not yet managed when two L1Pools are configured, the configuration should be rejected by L2-PS in that case.

So when PsCnfg\_AddressDistributionReq received contained

- two L1Pool instances

- and bbPoolInfos.L1PoolLIst.L1Pool[0]. l1DlSubPoolList.slowPrbPoolingParameters

the configuration must be rejected by sending PsCnfg\_AddressDistributionResp with EStatus5G\_NotOk with cause InvalidParameter (2).

---

##### 2.4.23.4.2.2 Cell Management

###### 2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1 Cell Management Cell Setup (ID: 11174925)

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11174927`)

Cell Management
Overview

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11174932`)

Cell Management
If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed == true , cellSetup contains:

* l1PoolId and l1DlSubPoolId for DL subcell, handled by BBRM
* numMinLayerPrbAllocDl for DL subcell, handled by BBRM

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed == true , cellSetup contains:

* l1PoolId and l1DlSubPoolId for UL subcell, handled by BBRM
* numMinLayerPrbAllocUl for UL subcell, handled by BBRM

BBRM checks the L1 pool and L1 subpool mapping and rejects cellSetup in case of error.

Refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of the prbPooling flags

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11174937`)

Cell Management
Actions on cell setup reception - General

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12368091`)

Cell Management
Determination of Minimum layer PRB

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12466419`)

Cell Management
[CB010630-D] L2PS shall verify the minimum number of layer PRBs allocated as specified in [12466919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_AI5BYQu-EfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and [12466922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_E11Rowu-EfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

If the sum condition is not true, BBRM returns the Error STATUS5G\_NOTOK, cause NoUserPlaneResourcesAvailable in cellSetup response, and ignores/cancels cell Setup request, else BBRM return OK in cellSetup response.

In a second step, if the sum of minLayerPrb of all cells of the corresponding L1 subPool (including the added cell) is greater than its L1SpMaxNumLayerPrb, then BBRM will not consider this cell for prb allocation in intra pooling algorithm (0 PRB allocated to this new cell) until the next inter pooling algorithm allocates enough LayerPrb to the concerned L1 subpool.

Note that l1SpMaxNumLayerPrb is updated after each L1 subpool reconfiguration.

[End CB010630-D]

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11174945`)

Cell Management
Verification of Cell Setup Request

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11174960`)

Cell Management
In L2-PS signalling, upon reception of PsCell\_CellSetupReq, the following checking shall be performed to guarantee that current configuration is supported:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Checking case | Checking description | Action description |
| Check L1pool capacity is enough to allocate min layerPRB for all cells | If [(remainingNumDlLayerPrbL1Pool - numMinLayerPrbAllocDl) < 0 Or (remainingNumUlLayerPrbL1Pool - numMinLayerPrbAllocUl) < 0] Reject the cell setup Else remainingNumDlLayerPrbL1Pool -= numMinLayerPrbAllocDl remainingNumUlLayerPrbL1Pool -= numMinLayerPrbAllocUl Accept the cell setup   Note : remainingNumDlLayerPrbL1Pool  is initialized with maxNumDlLayerPrbL1Pool  & remainingNumUlLayerPrbL1Pool  is initialized with maxNumUlLayerPrbL1Pool.   remainingNumDlLayerPrbL1Pool : remaining L1Pool DL capacity in LayerPRBs after minimum allocation of every cell in the L1 pool. remainingNumUlLayerPrbL1Pool : remaining L1Pool UL capacity in LayerPRBs after minimum allocation of every cell in the L1 pool.   maxNumDlLayerPrbL1Pool: sum of every max number of DlLayerPrb for the L1 Subpools in the same L1 Pool.   maxNumUlLayerPrbL1Pool: sum of every max number of UlLayerPrb for the L1 Subpools in the same L1 Pool. | When the cell setup is rejected, the following actions shall be done: · PsCell\_CellSetupResp shall be returned to CP-RT with · Status = NotOk · Cause = InvalidParameter (2) · DiagnosticInformation[0..13] = 0x00000000 · Add an ERROR log with at least the following sentence: "L1Pool capacity is not enough to allocate min Layer-PRB to Cell" |
| 12605 ckeck feature flag activation bbPoolingInfoCell>numMinLayerPrbAllocDl | [Before CNI-136855-A] If (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed == true and numMinLayerPrbAllocDl== Present) Accept the cell setup else (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed == false and numMinLayerPrbAllocDl== notPresent)  Accept the cell setup Otherwise Reject CellSetup [CNI-136855-A] If (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed == true and numMinLayerPrbAllocDl == NOT Present) Reject the cell setup [End CNI-136855-A] | When the cell setup is rejected, the following actions shall be done: · PsCell\_CellSetupResp shall be returned to CP-RT with · Status = NotOk · Cause = InvalidParameter (2) · DiagnosticInformation[0..13] = 0x00000000 · Add an ERROR log with at least the following sentence: "Cell setup rejected because bbPoolingInfoCell is not valid, numMinLayerPrbAllocDl missing. " |
| 12605 ckeck feature flag activation bbPoolingInfoCell>numMinLayerPrbAllocUl | [Before CNI-136855-A] If (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed == true and numMinLayerPrbAllocUl== Present) Accept the cell setup else (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed == false and numMinLayerPrbAllocUl== notPresent)  Accept the cell setup Otherwise Reject CellSetup [CNI-136855-A] If (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed == true and numMinLayerPrbAllocUl == NOT Present) Reject the cell setup [End CNI-136855-A] | When the cell setup is rejected, the following actions shall be done: · PsCell\_CellSetupResp shall be returned to CP-RT with · Status = NotOk · Cause = InvalidParameter (2) · DiagnosticInformation[0..13] = 0x00000000 · Add an ERROR log with at least the following sentence: "Cell setup rejected because bbPoolingInfoCell is not valid, numMinLayerPrbAllocUl missing. " |

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12466919`)

Cell Management
Verification of Minimum Layer PRB for DL

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12468458`)

Cell Management
[CB010630-D]

Following are the verifications to be performed on Cell Setup

Step 1.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed set as TRUE and isInterSubPoolingActivated set as '0':

5G-L2-PS shall check that the numMinLayerPrbAlloc values (Ref: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25717\_replaced([11214316](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxM8M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for DL cells are available in the subpool associated to these cells.

That means it shall calculate for DL: the sum of minLayerPrb values of all cells using the same L1 subpool.

Note: When subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is TRUE 5G-L2-PS shall check numMinLayerPrbAlloc values for DL cells in the primary subpool only.

Primary subpool is subpool on which primary subcell is deployed.

Step 2.

Iif slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed set as 'TRUE' and isInterSubPoolingActivated set as '1':

5G-L2-PS shall check that the numMinLayerPrbAlloc values for DL cells are available in the L1 pool associated to these cells.

That means it shall calculate for DL: the sum of minlayerPrb values of all cells using the same L1 pool.

Note: When subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is TRUE 5G-L2-PS shall check numMinLayerPrbAlloc values for DL cells in the primary subpool only.

Primary subpool is subpool on which primary subcell is deployed.

Step 3.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed set as 'TRUE' and isInterSubPoolingActivated ser as '0':

The sum of minLayerPrb values of all cells for L1 subpool associated to the added cell shall be lower or equal to l1SpMaxNumLayerPrb associated to this L1 subPool (with l1SpMaxNumLayerPrb received in poolingDeploymentReq).

If the sum condition is not true:

5G-L2-PS shall return the error STATUS5G\_NOTOK, cause NoUserPlaneResourcesAvailable in cellSetup response, and shall ignore/cancel cell Setup request

else:

5G-L2-PS shall check ok in cellSetup response.

Step 4.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed set as 'TRUE' and isInterSubPoolingActivated set as '1':

The sum of minLayerPrb of all cells in L1 pool shall be lower or equal to L1MaxNumLayerPrb (sum of l1SpMaxNumLayerPrb, received in poolingDeployementReq).

[End CB010630-D]

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12466922`)

Cell Management
Verification of Minimum Layer PRB for UL

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `12469336`)

Cell Management
[CB010630-D]

Step 1.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed is configured TRUE and isInterSubPoolingActivated set as FALSE:

5G-L2-PS shall check that the numMinLayerPrbAlloc values for UL cells are available in the subpool associated to these cells.

That means it shall calculate for UL: the sum of minLayerPrb values of all cells using the same L1 subpool.

Step 2.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed is configured TRUE  and isInterSubPoolingActivated set as TRUE:

5G-L2-PS shall check that the numMinLayerPrbAlloc values for UL cells are available in the L1 pool associated to these cells.

That means it shall calculate for UL: the sum of minLayerPrb values of all cells using the same L1 pool.

Step 3.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed set as 'TRUE' and isInterSubPoolingActivated set as ‘FALSE’:

The sum of minLayerPrb values of all cells for L1 subpool associated to the added cell shall be lower or equal to l1SpMaxNumLayerPrb associated to this L1 subPool (with l1SpMaxNumLayerPrb received in poolingDeploymentReq).

If the sum condition is not true:

5G-L2-PS shall return the error STATUS5G\_NOTOK, cause NoUserPlaneResourcesAvailable in cellSetup response, and shall ignore/cancel cell Setup request

else:

5G-L2-PS shall return ok in cellSetup response.

Step 4.

If If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed set as 'TRUE' and isInterSubPoolingActivated set as ‘FALSE’:

The sum of minLayerPrb of all cells in L1 pool shall be lower or equal to L1MaxNumLayerPrb (sum of l1SpMaxNumLayerPrb, received in poolingDeployementReq).

[End CB010630-D]

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12469560`)

Cell Management
Layer PRBs allocated for a cell with DL artificial load generation for EMF validation

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12469577`)

Cell Management
[CB010630-D]

If prbDemandForArtificialLoadForEmfValidation (see 5G\_L2\_4650\_replaced([11244424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B2zovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) was present in PsInternal\_DlMetricInd (see 5G\_L2\_3876\_replaced([11244253](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B1lh_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall determine the minimum number of layer PRBs as follows:

artificialLoadForEmfValidation\_layerPrb = prbDemandForArtificialLoadForEmfValidation \* maxNumberOfLayerPerCellPrb

minLayerPrb = max(artificialLoadForEmfValidation\_layerPrb, minLayerPrb)

Where:

* artificialLoadForEmfValidation\_layerPrb - artificial load for electromagnetic field validation in layer-PRBs  (defined as ConfiguredLoadInLayerPRBForEmf  in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13342\_replaced([11212500](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxB9EPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) .
* prbDemandForArtificialLoadForEmfValidation - Requested PRB resources for DL artificial load generation for EMF validation (see 5G\_L2\_4650\_replaced([11244424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B2zovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* maxNumberOfLayerPerCellPrb - see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25734\_replaced([11214157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxLuHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
* minLayerPrb - minimum resource per Cell in layer-PRBs, calculated in 5G\_L2\_14643\_replaced([11174415](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26j41PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (defined as numMinLayerPRBAlloc in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29609\_replaced([11214330](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxM8NfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB010630-D]

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-7**  (ID: `12469698`)

Cell Management
Limitation of the number of layer-PRBs allocated for a cell with DL artificial load generation for EMF validation

---

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-1.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `12469700`)

Cell Management
[CB010630-D]

If in the given (sub)pool there is one cell with prbDemandForArtificialLoadForEmfValidation > 0 (see 5G\_L2\_4650\_replaced([11244424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B2zovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall check the number of allocated layer-PRBs in the given (sub)pool, as specified in [12466919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_AI5BYQu-EfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), for the sake of software robustness.

If the sum of the allocated layer-PRBs (numAllocCellLayerPrb, see: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13342\_replaced([11212500](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxB9EPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_18387\_replaced([11174429](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26j41_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of all the cells in given L1 (sub)pool exceeds the configured limit ([12466919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_AI5BYQu-EfCuhewgqkUVKQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), 5G-L2-PS shall reduce the  numAllocCellLayerPrb  for the cell with requested PRB resources for DL artificial load generation for EMF validation: the sum of  numAllocCellLayerPrb  for all the cells shall match the configured limit.

Note: As specified in the NIDD definition of NRCELL.actEmfTestMode, DL artificial load generation for EMF validation can be enabled for only one cell instance of the gNb.

[End CB010630-D]

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.2.0-2 Cell Management Cell Delete (ID: 11174966)

**2.4.23.4.2.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11174971`)

Cell Management
Same process introduced as for Cell Prb pooling, see 5G\_L2\_3322\_replaced([11174471](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26kf4PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

##### 2.4.23.4.2.3 Pooling Mechanisms

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.1 Cell Addition Handling

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.1.0-1 Cell Addition Handling New cell introduction (ID: 11174983)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11174986`)

Cell Addition Handling
BBRM has to take into account the new cell in the layer PRB pooling algorithm.

The PRB allocation for the current BB pooling period shall not be updated for the cells already running.

So the number of allocated PRBs for this new cell is 0 during the current pooling period.

If this new cell is added to an L1 subpool already having a cell, then BBRM shall not grant any PRB resources before next BB pooling period starts.

If instead, this new cell is the first cell of an L1 subpool, then BBRM shall grant at least the minimum number of layer PRBs, unless there are not enough  layerPrb available in which case no resources are granted.

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.2 UL and DL Scheduler applying numLayerPrb

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.2.0-1 UL and DL Scheduler applying numLayerPrb Processing of numLayerPrb received from BBRM (ID: 11174993)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175010`)

UL and DL Scheduler applying numLayerPrb
L2-PS will apply granted numAllocLayerPrb  on the slot 0 SFN N+1 for DL and UL Layer PRB resource allocation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_25709\_replaced([11214021](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxLHCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

DL  [CB012605-D]and UL[End CB012605-D] related actions, both actions on Cell Prbs & Layer Prbs should be done:

* Update the total number of usable Layer PRB  (numAllocLayerPrb defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25712\_replaced([11214266](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxMVLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* granted to the cell every bbPoolingPeriod if the value changed and  the total number of usable Cell PRB (numAllocCellPrb defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4278\_replaced([11176996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Check in each slot on DL and UL the sum of the Layer PRB resource allocation for all the UEs to-be-scheduled in the CS2 list for each cell not exceed the numAllocLayerPrb, and check in each slot on DL and UL the sum of the cell  PRB resource allocation for all the UEs to-be-scheduled in the CS2 list for each cell not exceed the numAllocCellPrb.
* Check to-be-scheduled retransmission on DL and UL, if the number of the Layer  PRB for a retransmission exceed the total usable Layer PRB of the cell (numAllocLayerPrb) this retransmission can't be scheduled and the HARQ process is freed, otherwise if there is insufficient layer PRB for a retransmission, the retransmission is skipped (5G\_UP\_ALG\_5082\_replaced([11203301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),(5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),(5G\_UP\_ALG\_4311\_replaced([11177467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).    Check to-be-scheduled retransmission on DL and UL, if the number of the PRB for a retransmission exceed the total usable PRB of the cell (numAllocCellPrb) this retransmission can't be scheduled and the HARQ process is freed, otherwise if there is insufficient PRB for a retransmission, the retransmission is skipped (5G\_UP\_ALG\_5082\_replaced([11203301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),(5G\_UP\_ALG\_4532\_replaced([11184193](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvQbg_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),(5G\_UP\_ALG\_4311\_replaced([11177467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuveGPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* L2-PS allocators consider numAllocLayerPrb is 0 Layer PRB and numAllocCellPrb is 0 cell PRB as long as BBRM did not grant resources

Only DL related actions:

As defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26612\_replaced([11183051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvH4kPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) number of usable RBGs need to be recomputed based on layer prb limitation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4278\_replaced([11176996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) based on slot type:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is:
$N\_{RBC} = \lceil (N\_{BW P,i}^{size} + (N\_{BW P,i}^{start} \mod P) / P \rceil$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5047.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] nominal number of RBG defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23474\_replaced([11178375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2Lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

UsableRBG=min(NRBG, numAllocCellPrb/P)

-   [image-1][image\_desc]The image is entirely black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5047.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1] the size of the Ue active bandwidth part (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1823\_replaced([11110957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJMY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

-  [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry mathematical expression. The expression appears to be a fraction with "N\_start" in the numerator and "BWP\_i" in the denominator.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5047.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] the start of the Ue active bandwidth part (5G\_UP\_ALG\_1823\_replaced([11110957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpJMY_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

-  P: RBG size in cellPrb

-  numAllocCellPrb:  resource allocation based on slot type (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4278\_replaced([11176996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kurzv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is completely black, with no discernible features or content.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5047.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]   defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23474\_replaced([11178375](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2Lw_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

if RBGsizeLast > 0

RemainPartialRbgNum= 1

else

RemainPartialRbgNum= 0

For each UE, required resources (numReqFdPrb = N­LayerPrb / Rank) are adressed based on UsableRBG.

Then if RemainPartialRbgNum is present, last partial RBG will be used if consumed entirely; depending on GBR and nGBR algorithm resource distribution (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4510\_replaced([11181328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-Hn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and can only be used once.

Each allocation have to check the limitation in Layer PRB.

* If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed flag is true, the DL related actions are to be considered.
* [CB012605-D]If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed flag is true , the UL related actions are to be considered.[End CB012605-D]

Refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of the prbPooling flags.

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.3 Metric Management

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.3.0-1 Metric Management GBR and nonGBR buffer occupancy of cell (ID: 11175017)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175022`)

Metric Management
The buffer occupancy of the cell (5G\_UP\_ALG\_6784\_replaced([11212073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw-5yvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is used to compute the rrmInstNewDataArrivalEst (5G\_UP\_ALG\_6785\_replaced([11212086](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw-5zPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the prbDemandGbr and prbDemandNonGbr (5G\_UP\_ALG\_6786\_replaced([11212141](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw_g1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) separately in DL and UL.

the GBR and non-GBR buffer occupancy of the cell is maintained as follow:

For DL:  If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed flag is true,  the gbrBufferLength (respectively nonGbrBufferLength) is updated each time the bufferedDataBytes of one GBR bearer (respectively non-GBR) of one UE in the cell is updated as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4561\_replaced([11186983](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvXwMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For UL: [CB012605-D]If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed flag is true, the gbrBufferLength (respectively nonGbrBufferLength) is updated each time the rrmBufferedDataUlLcg of one GBR/non-GBR bearer of one UE in the cell is updated as described in 5G\_L2\_372\_replaced([11151301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24hRefvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB012605-D]

Note, a bearer is considered as GBR at L2PS level if gbrQosSpecificParameters structure is present in the drbAddReqPs (5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) at the UserSetup or BearerSetup procedures.

At the reception of PsUser\_UserStopReq (During user delete procedure), the total amount of data for Gbr bearer(s) (respectively nonGbr bearer) in the associated UE buffer is decreased from gbrBufferLength (respectively nonGbrBufferLength) of the active serving cell associated to UE buffer.

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.3.0-2 Metric Management New DL Data Arrival Estimation per Cell (ID: 11175026)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175029`)

Metric Management
same process as Cell Prb pooling see req 5G\_L2\_4566\_replaced([11174607](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26lG-_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.3.0-3 Metric Management New UL Data Arrival Estimation per Cell (ID: 11175034)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175038`)

Metric Management
same process as Cell Prb pooling see req 5G\_L2\_4564\_replaced([11174616](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26lG_fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.3.0-4 Metric Management Historical Layer PRB Usage Bookkeeping (ID: 12877952)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12877953`)

Metric Management
[CB014543 -F, K]

For TDD 7.2e, if histUsageAllowed is TRUE and (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed is TRUE for DL or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed is TRUE for UL) then calculated average of layer non-GBR historical usage PRB([12721584](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_QhdH6W09EfCmnYHxzZN44w) and [12721585](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_e3LQRW09EfCmnYHxzZN44w)) shall be sent in dlMetricInd/ulMetricInd message.

Else average of layer non-GBR historical usage PRB shall be set to 0(zero) in dlMetricInd/ulMetricInd message.

Note1: In case only DL or UL PRB pooling is enabled then respective average of non-GBR historical usage PRB shall be calculated.

Note2:To calculate non-GBR PRBs, UE priority index of all DRB traffics shall be consider in DL and UL respectively. It might be possible that due to multiple bearers of an UE, priority index will be set as per data priority and UE will be chosen accordingly.

Note 3: It is clear and accepted that some UL data was initially already granted in BB pooling period N-1 but received in BB pooling period N. So counting of used PRB will be in BB pooling period N-1. Please refer [11174616](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_G2rmCfvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for rationale.

Note 4: In case of MU-MIMO, Scheduler shall consider all Layer PRBs used by real UE for calculating average used layer PRB

Example: A single virtual UE containing 3 real UEs then total layer PRB shall be calculated by considering individual layer PRBs of all 3 real UEs.

[End CB014543 -F, K]

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.3.0-5 Metric Management PRB Demand (ID: 11175043)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11175046`)

Metric Management
same process as Cell Prb pooling see req 5G\_L2\_4580\_replaced([11174638](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26luAvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

prbDemandGbr and prbDemandNonGbr computations in scope of Layer prb are defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25732\_replaced([11214077](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxLuEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.4 Load based Algorithm

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.4.0-1 Load based Algorithm Load based algorithm executed by BBRM (ID: 11175055)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12877954`)

Load based Algorithm
The load based algorithm of Layer  PRB pooling can be described by the following steps:

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed == true  and  bbPoolMode is set to "1: load based"

---> DL scheduler case

**[CB012605-H]**

---> If dlSubcellDeactForARMAllowed is true BBRM shall apply subcell deactivation algorithm specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27687\_replaced([11214642](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOxZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**[End CB012605-H]**

**[CB012605-D]**

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed == true

**[End CB012605-D] (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and  bbPoolMode is set to "1: load based" (UL scheduler case)**

---> UL scheduler case

Then

1) In last slot of last SFN of bbPoolingPeriod N, DL and  UL scheduler calculate the PRB demands [CB014543-F] and historical non-GBR Layer PRB load [End CB014543-F]:

* prbDemandGbr,
* prbDemandNonGbr
* [CB014543-F] avgUsedPrbNonGbr ([12815922)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_BYQ8YKfvEfCrANRYxAlR5A)[End CB014543-F]

2) In slot 0 of first SFN of bbPoolingPeriod N+1, DL and UL scheduler send previously calculated **[prbDemandGbr, prbDemandNonGbr, [CB014543-F] avgUsedPrbNonGbr ([12815922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_BYQ8YKfvEfCrANRYxAlR5A)) [End CB014543-F]**] to BBRM.\*\*\*\*

3) In slot 3 of each SFN, or slot 4 if cellTechnology is TDD and UL grants are available in slot 3 and PUSCH can be scheduled for slot 3 + k2, DL and UL schedulers request assignment of baseband resources from BBRM (even if new assignments/allocation are done only once per pooling period).

In first SFN of a bbPoolingPeriod, BBRM determines new distribution of baseband resources to all cells of the L1 subpool based on latest received prbDemandGbr, prbDemandNonGbr,  [CB014543-F] avgUsedPrbNonGbr ([12815922](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_BYQ8YKfvEfCrANRYxAlR5A)) [End CB014543-F].

4) DL and UL scheduler apply new resource assignment in next SFN after reception of resource assignment..

**Exceptions:**

1. If, for the given cell, 5G\_L2\_PS could not receive PrbAllocated (see 5G\_L2\_4650\_replaced([11244424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B2zovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in its PRB pooling algorithm, 5G-L2-PS shall use PrbAllocated received for this cell in the previous bbPoolEvalPeriod (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1555\_replaced([10966229](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UNM1Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in its PRB pooling algorithm.
2. If, for the given cell, 5G\_L2\_PS never received PrbAllocated(see 5G\_L2\_4650\_replaced([11244424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B2zovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in its PRB pooling algorithm, 5G-L2-PS shall set each of the PRB allocated for this cell as equal to 0 in its PRB pooling algorithm.

[Before CB014543-F]

For Layer Cell PRB in TDD eCPRI 7.2e, BBRM shall execute legacy PRB pooling algorithm as mention in [11214266](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_Z4F6EfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

[CB014543-F]

For Layer Cell PRB in TDD eCPRI 7.2e, If histUsageAllowed is TRUE, BBRM shall execute enhanced PRB pooling algorithm for non-GBR as mention in [12705617](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_q9WDI2FQEfCLrcnPCspVyQ)(non-GBR), Else legacy PRB pooling algorithm for non-GBR as mentioned in [11214383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_aZ4YgPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)(non-GBR)

[End CB014543-F]

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.4.0-2 Load based Algorithm Load Metric based Subcell Deactivation Algorithm for DL (ID: 11175063)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175067`)

Load based Algorithm
Subcell Deactivation Execution

Trigger below every BB pooling period (NRBTS.bbPoolEvalPeriod), just before the inter-L1 subpool DL PRB resource sharing algorithm.

When dlSubcellDeactForARMAllowed and slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed are TRUE, ARM capacity DL subcell deactivation procedure shall be executed refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27687\_replaced([11214642](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOxZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.5 Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.5.0-1 Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling Inter L1 Subpool Resource Reconfiguration Request (ID: 11175078)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175080`)

Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling
[CB012605-D]

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed == true (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  and L1 address is known for the UL L1 pool, BBRM shall send UlPool\_BbResourceReconfigReq message to L1 UL  at the end of inter L1 subpool pooling algorithm executed in the second SFN of the pooling period(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26501\_replaced([11214551](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for inter pooling algorithm).[End CB012605-D]

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed == true (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  and L1 address is known for the DL L1 pool, BBRM shall send DlPool\_BbResourceReconfigReq message to L1 DL  at the end of inter L1 subpool pooling algorithm executed in the second SFN of the pooling period(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26490\_replaced([11214449](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxNjR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for inter pooling algorithm).

The SFN in which L1 shall apply the new reconfigurations is the second SFN of the next pooling period.

The value of addrBbPoolingResourceReconfResp shall be filled with the BBRM EqId that is defined internally in BBRM.

the content of the UlPool\_BbResourceReconfigReq message , see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14815\_replaced([11515370](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR7g4vvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

the content of the DlPool\_BbResourceReconfigReq message , see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14842\_replaced([11513342](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRv6rPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.5.0-2 Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling Inter L1 Subpool Resource Reconfiguration Response (ID: 11175085)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175090`)

Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling
L1 UL shall reply UlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message within the current pooling period by sending UlPool\_BbResourceReconfResp message to BBRM.

L1 DL shall reply DlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message within the current pooling period by sending DlPool\_BbResourceReconfResp message to BBRM.

In the case of receiving the response, BBRM shall check if the SFN received in UlPool\_BbResourceReconfResp corresponds to the SFN of UlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq, if no, BBRM shall discard this response message and consider this case to no response from L1 UL.  if yes, BBRM shall check the received status,  update the new configuration in case "success" status, or keep the current stream prb/layer capacity per L1 subpool in case of "failure" status (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26623\_replaced([11214533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In the case of receiving the response, BBRM shall check if the SFN received in DlPool\_BbResourceReconfResp corresponds to the SFN of DlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq, if no, BBRM shall discard this response message and consider this case to no response from L1 DL.  if yes, BBRM shall check the received status,  update the new configuration in case "success" status, or keep the current stream prb/layer capacity per L1 subpool in case of "failure" status (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26623\_replaced([11214533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

BBRM shall apply the new capacity values in the next intra pooling algorithm. In case of no response from L1 to BBRM reconfiguration request within the current pooling period, BBRM shall use, in this intra pooling algorithm, the minimum of subpool capacities between the new proposed allocation and the current allocation (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26623\_replaced([11214533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

if BBRM does not send UlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message because UL EqId address is unknown, BBRM keeps the initial UL subpool capacities.

if BBRM does not send DlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message because DL EqId address is unknown, BBRM keeps the initial DL subpool capacities.

---

###### 2.4.23.4.2.3.5.0-3 Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling Algorithm and BB Resource Reconfiguration trigger (ID: 11175093)

**2.4.23.4.2.3.5.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11175111`)

Inter L1 Subpool PRB pooling
If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed is true (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  and L1 address is known for this UL L1 pool, BBRM calculates the capacity of the subpools in UL within L1 pool with inter pooling algorithm (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26623\_replaced([11214533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the calculated subpool capacities are sent to L1 through UlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message.

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed == true (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

and L1 address is known for this DL L1 pool, BBRM calculates the capacity of the subpools in DL within L1 pool with inter pooling algorithm (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26623\_replaced([11214533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the calculated subpool capacities are sent to L1 through DlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq message.

The following information are transferred to L1 ( see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14845\_replaced([11513352](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRv6rvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1\_IF\_14818\_replaced([11515379](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR8H4PvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• The calculated subpool capacity:

* Maximum number of allocated layer PRB per subpool

• The SFN in which L1 shall apply the new reconfigurations; this SFN is the second SFN of the next pooling interval (see the below figure and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26623\_replaced([11214533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In the second SFN of every pooling period (see the below figure),  [CNI-127661] Upon the reception of PsInternal\_InterSubPoolsSynchroTriggerInd message  (see 5G\_L2\_22324\_replaced([11244146](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B0-dfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CNI-127661], BBRM shall trigger the UL and DL inter L1 subpool pooling algorithm (5G\_UP\_ALG\_26623\_replaced([11214533](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxOKVfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for all L1 pools to calculate the subpool capacity and send the DlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq to DL L1 and UlPool\_BbResourceReconfReq to UL L1 after the inter pooling algorithm at the same SFN (second SFN).

[Before CNI-127661]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a timeline of resource allocation and processing within a communication system, likely related to cellular networks. The diagram spans multiple SFN (System Frame Number) periods, from N-1 to N+6, and shows different layers of operation: SCH DL (Scheduling Downlink), SCH UL (Scheduling Uplink), BBRM (Baseband Resource Management), SCH (Scheduling), L1 DL (Layer 1 Downlink), L1 UL (Layer 1 Uplink), and L1 (Layer 1).
The diagram uses color-coding to represent different types of actions or states:
- Red: Indication
- Blue: Resource Req/Resp
- Green: L1 SP Reconf Req/Rsp
- Grey: New resource allocation processing intra L1SP based on L1 SP capacity calculated at previous pooling period
- Orange: New resource allocation processing inter L1SP
Key elements and their interactions are depicted:
**Timeline and Slots:**
The horizontal axis represents time, divided into SFNs, and further into slots (0 to 5, and 'x' which likely represents a special slot or end-of-frame marker).
**SCH DL and SCH UL:**
These rows show resource requests and responses represented by blue arrows. These occur at various slots across different SFNs.
**BBRM:**
This row indicates resource management activities.
- In SFN N-1, slot 3, there's a blue arrow indicating a Resource Req/Resp.
- In SFN N, slot 0, there's a red block labeled "Indication" with two red arrows pointing down, suggesting an event or trigger.
- In SFN N, slot 1, there's a blue arrow indicating a Resource Req/Resp.
- In SFN N+1, slot 2, there's an orange block labeled "New resource allocation processing inter L1SP" with blue arrows indicating Resource Req/Resp.
- In SFN N+1, slot 3, there's a grey block with the text "Apply new BBRM resources".
- In SFN N+4, slot 0, there's a red block labeled "Indication" with two red arrows pointing down.
- In SFN N+5, slot 2, there's a blue arrow indicating a Resource Req/Resp.
- In SFN N+6, slot 1, there's an orange block labeled "New resource allocation processing inter L1SP" with blue arrows indicating Resource Req/Resp.
- In SFN N+6, slot 2, there's a grey block with the text "Apply new RBRM".
**SCH:**
This row shows broader allocation periods.
- From SFN N+1, slot 3 onwards, there's a grey bar labeled "BBRM Allocation with L1 SP capacity available at previous pooling period".
- A green arrow originates from SFN N+1, slot 2, pointing to the right and upwards, with the text "Apply new L1 SP capacity". This arrow seems to indicate the application of new L1 SP capacity.
**L1 DL and L1 UL:**
These rows are not explicitly populated with specific events but are part of the layered structure.
**L1:**
This row represents the Layer 1 operations and capacity.
- A large green block spans from SFN N+1, slot 3, to the end of the diagram, labeled "L1 SP capacity calculated at previous pooling period".
- A green block appears in SFN N+1, slot 2, with a green arrow pointing downwards, labeled "L1 SP Reconf Req/Rsp".
- Another green block appears in SFN N+6, slot 2, with a green arrow pointing downwards, labeled "L1 SP Reconf Req/Rsp".
- A green arrow originates from SFN N+1, slot 2, pointing to the right and downwards, with the text "Apply new L1 SP capacity".
**Pooling Period:**
A red bracket at the bottom indicates a "Pooling Period" that spans from SFN N to SFN N+4.
The diagram visually represents how resource requests and indications trigger updates in BBRM and L1 SP capacity over time, with specific actions being applied at different points in the timeline. The concept of "L1 SP capacity calculated at previous pooling period" is central to the resource allocation strategy shown.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5069.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
