[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a timeline of resource allocation and processing within a system, likely related to telecommunications or network management. The diagram spans multiple Super Frame Numbers (SFNs), labeled from N-1 to N+6, and within each SFN, it shows slots numbered 0 to 5, followed by an 'x'.
The diagram is divided into several horizontal tracks, each representing a different aspect of the system:
\* **Top Blue Bar:** This bar appears to represent a general timeline or a control signal, with an arrow indicating progression.
\* **SFN Labels:** The SFN labels are clearly displayed above the slot numbers.
\* **Slot Numbers:** Below the SFN labels, the slots within each SFN are numbered.
\* **SCH DL and SCH UL:** These tracks likely represent Downlink (DL) and Uplink (UL) scheduling control channels.
\* **BBRM:** This track seems to indicate a Bandwidth and Resource Management (BBRM) related activity.
\* **SCH:** This track, colored light grey, shows BBRM resource allocation and processing.
\* **L1 DL and L1 UL:** These tracks likely represent Layer 1 (L1) Downlink and Uplink activities.
\* **L1:** This track, colored dark green and then light green, represents L1 Specific Pooling (SP) capacity.
The diagram uses colored blocks and arrows to denote different events and processes:
\* **Red Blocks:** Labeled as "Indication," these appear at specific slots, signifying an indication event.
\* **Light Blue Blocks:** Labeled as "Resource Req/Resp," these indicate resource requests or responses.
\* **Green Blocks:** Labeled as "L1 SP Reconf Req/Rsp," these signify L1 SP reconfiguration requests or responses.
\* **Yellow Blocks:** Labeled as "Inter-SubPools Synchro Trigger Indication," these indicate synchronization triggers between sub-pools.
\* **Orange Blocks:** Labeled as "New resource allocation processing inter L1SP," these show inter-L1SP resource allocation processing.
\* **Grey Blocks:** Labeled as "New resource allocation processing intra L1SP based on L1 SP capacity calculated at previous pooling period," these indicate intra-L1SP resource allocation processing.
Arrows are used to show the flow of information or the application of resources:
\* **Blue Arrows:** Pointing upwards or downwards, often associated with resource requests/responses or indications.
\* **Green Arrows:** Indicate the application of new L1 SP capacity or new BBRM resources.
\* **Dark Grey Arrows:** Indicate the application of new BBRM resources.
Text annotations provide further context:
\* "Apply new BBRM resources"
\* "BBRM Allocation with L1 SP capacity available at previous pooling period"
\* "Apply new L1 SP capacity"
\* "L1 SP capacity calculated at previous pooling period"
A red horizontal line at the bottom marks a "Pooling Period," with red vertical lines indicating the start and end of this period.
The diagram illustrates a sequence of events where resource allocation and capacity calculations occur over time, influencing subsequent resource management and processing. For instance, L1 SP capacity calculated at a previous pooling period is shown to be used for BBRM allocation and new L1 SP capacity application. Resource requests and indications are also depicted at various points in the timeline.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5069.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[End CNI-127661]

Note: Reconfiguration request message is sent to L1 even if the inter L1 subpool algorithm has provided the same values for the L1 subpool sizes as in the current pooling period. A further optimisation could be introduced to send the request only in case of L1 subpool sizes modification.

##### 2.4.23.4.2.4 PM Counter Support

###### 2.4.23.4.2.4.0-1 PM Counter Support Introduced Counters (ID: 11175118)

**2.4.23.4.2.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175122`)

PM Counter Support
If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed == true (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) new counters should be updated:

- LAYER\_PRB\_USED\_PDSCH\_BB\_POOL (M55308C36006) see 5G\_L2\_17827\_replaced([11193039](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28oVhPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB012605-D]

If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed == true (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) new counters should be updated:

- LAYER\_PRB\_AVAIL\_PUSCH\_BB\_POOL (M55308C36007) see 5G\_L2\_19566\_replaced([11193794](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28ucBvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- LAYER\_PRB\_USED\_PUSCH\_BB\_POOL (M55308C36008) see 5G\_L2\_19567\_replaced([11193806](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28ucCPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB012605-D]

###### 2.4.23.4.2.4.0-2 PM Counter Support Impacted Counters (ID: 11175126)

**2.4.23.4.2.4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175129`)

PM Counter Support
If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed == true (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) existing counters should be impacted:

##### 2.4.23.4.2.5 PRB Pooling performances

##### 2.4.23.4.2.6 Interaction with other features

###### 2.4.23.4.2.6.0-1 Interaction with other features Interaction with non supported feature (ID: 11175142)

**2.4.23.4.2.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175147`)

Interaction with other features
After introduction of CB009514 eCpri 7-2e parity feature , several features remain not supported.

These features are listed in req 5G\_L2\_18423\_replaced([11174945](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26oKQ_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

As long as no checks are provided by OAM, L2PS will forbid their activation.

When those features will be supported with eCpr7-2e , or if OAM provides the checks, then L2PS will have to remove its internal checks.

When those features will be supported, they will have to handle IWK with CB012605.

---

### 2.4.23.5 Scheduled UEs per Slot Pooling

#### 2.4.23.5.1 Common

##### 2.4.23.5.1.0-1 Common Applicable Technologies (ID: 11175161)

**2.4.23.5.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175166`)

Common
Pooling of scheduled UEs per slot has been introduced first for FDD (CB007114, 22R2) and then for TDD FR1 (CB008847, 23R1).

The requirements in 5G\_L2\_3747\_replaced([11175152](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26p_cPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) are valid for both techonolgies, unless otherwise indicated in the requirement itself.

---

##### 2.4.23.5.1.0-2 Common Resources controlled by Scheduled UE pooling algorithm (ID: 11175171)

**2.4.23.5.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175175`)

Common
If scheUePoolingAllowed flag of PsCnfg\_AddressDistributionReq is true, BBRM shall distribute the below baseband pool resources to cells that belong in this baseband pool:

for DL

 Number of PDSCH UEs per slot

for UL

 Number of PUSCH UEs per slot

 Number of PUCCH F1 for HARQ A/N per slot

 Number of PUCCH F3 for HARQ A/N per slot

For the communication between DL/UL schedulers and BBRM, please refer to 5G\_L2\_4094\_replaced([11246077](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3CBLsfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.23.5.1.0-3 Common Scheduled UE Pooling algorithm mode (ID: 11175181)

**2.4.23.5.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12442785`)

Common
[CNI-136563] ~~There are two different pooling algorithm modes that shall be supported by BBRM, respectively~~~~：~~

~~     round-robin based~~

UE Pooling supports following pooling algorithm mode

[END CNI-136563]

 load-metric based

Pooling algorithm mode can be set by an O&M parameter NRBTS.bbPoolMode, and BBRM will gets this parameter from PsCnfg\_AddressDistributionReq message.

---

##### 2.4.23.5.1.0-4 Common Maximum Resources for each Cell (ID: 11175188)

**2.4.23.5.1.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11175198`)

Common
The maximum resource per cell (Rmax,x) is calculated as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7943\_replaced([11210499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw5aPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

BBRM will calculated/re-calculated the maximum resource per cell once receives PsCell\_CellSetupReq or PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq message for this cell.

[Before CNI-80481-D]

BBRM will start to use the new value in scheduled UEs pooling once receive the first resourceReq message after value change.

[CNI-80481-D]

BBRM will take the new value into use by the next allocation of the pooled resources.

[End CNI-80481-D]

---

##### 2.4.23.5.1.0-5 Common Minimum Resource Share for each Cell (ID: 11175201)

**2.4.23.5.1.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `12445785`)

Common
The minimum resource per cell (Rmin,x) is calculated as [11210423](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw5aNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) (5G\_UP\_ALG\_7942\_replaced([11210423](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw5aNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB014542-A]

When the NRBTS.actScheUePoolingEnh is 'TRUE', the requirement is to assign minimum UEs using R&D parameters. To protect incorrect assignment, a check shall be implemented in BBRM that total minimum resources from each cell shall be within the limit of L1 Pool.

[End CB014542-A]

BBRM will calculate/re-calculate each cell's minimun resource once receives PsCell\_CellSetupReq message or the maximum resource per cell changed for any cell in the L2 pool.

BBRM will take the new value into use by the next allocation of the pooled resources.

---

##### 2.4.23.5.1.0-6 Common Scheduled UE per slot Pooling for Supplemental DL Cell FR1 (ID: 11175215)

**2.4.23.5.1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11175219`)

Common
On calculation of:

1. minimum resources for PUSCH UEs per slot per cell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7942\_replaced([11210423](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw5aNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
2. shared resources for non-GBR bearers (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10366\_replaced([11211629](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw8djvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
3. shared resources for GBR bearers (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_10364\_replaced([11211368](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw72fPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
4. shared resources for Configured Grants (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20544\_replaced([11211144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw7PavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
5. PUCCH F1 for HARQ A/N per slot per cell (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8681\_replaced([11211775](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9EnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
6. PUCCH F3 for HARQ A/N per slot per cell for FR1 FDD (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7948\_replaced([11211803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9ro_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),
7. maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM, maxNumPucchF1PerSlotByBBRM and maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM in Round Robin Based Algorithm (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6361\_replaced([11210614](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw5aQPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

BBRM shall check if the given cell is Supplemental DL Cell (SDL cell), as specified in: 5G\_L2\_14855\_replaced([11141639](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23puzPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Rationale: BBRM shall not allocate UL resources for Supplemental DL Cells.

---

##### 2.4.23.5.1.0-7 Common Limit the Scheduled UE per slot per cell with the pooling result (ID: 11175225)

**2.4.23.5.1.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11175228`)

Common
If scheUePoolingAllowed flag of PsCnfg\_AddressDistributionReq is true, after Dl/Ul scheduler received the pooling result from BBRM, they need to limit the Scheduled UE number and PUCCH resource per slot per cell with the pooling result.

 rrmMaxNumUesFdmDl = maxNumPdschPerSlotByBBRM (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4478\_replaced([11178989](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2y2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl = maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4960\_replaced([11199244](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwMPk_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 rrmNumOfF1ForHarq = maxNumPucchF1PerSlotByBBRM (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2465\_replaced([11116066](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 rrmNumOfF3ForHarq = maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2465\_replaced([11116066](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot = maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2465\_replaced([11116066](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

 rrmNumCsiPart1OnPusch = rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot = maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2465\_replaced([11116066](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

Note:

While Scheduled UE pooling is disabled, L2-Ps will calculate the value of rrmNumOfF1ForHarq, rrmNumOfF3ForHarq and rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot based on L3 configuration from PsCell\_CellSetupReq message; but if Scheduled UE pooling is enabled, CP-RT will set corresponding IEs to 0 in PsCell\_CellSetupReq message,  then the limitation from BBRM will be used(refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2465\_replaced([11116066](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Considering the interwork with DPA (Distributed PUCCH Allocation), if Dl/Ul scheduler and DPA receive the new assignment at SFN N from BBRM, the new assignment shall take effect in scheduling at slot 0, SFN N+2. For the interworking with DPA, please see 5G\_L2\_4465\_replaced([11158802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25Mm6_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7357\_replaced([11211858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9rqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.23.5.2 Round Robin based Algorithm

**2.4.23.5.2.0-2**  (ID: `12442826`)

Round Robin based Algorithm
If bbPoolMode is set to "0: round-robin", BBRM shall distribute the pooled resource "Number of PDSCH/PUSCH UEs per slot" according to the round-robin algorithm (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6361\_replaced([11210614](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw5aQPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The round-down operation is used in the third & fourth step of round-robin based pooling algorithm (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6361\_replaced([11210614](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw5aQPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to make sure the assignment won't exceed the L1 subpool limitation, and this operation may cause a little bit waste of resource. This is acceptable in Round Robin based pooling algorithm, due to this pooling algorithm mode cannot been selected by customer, it just will be used for internal validation testing.

5G\_UP\_ALG\_6361\_replaced([11210614](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw5aQPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is the reference requirement also for the allocation of PUCCH F1/F3 for HARQ A/N, in round-robin mode.

[CNI-136563] bbPoolMode = "0: round-robin", is deprecated post CNI-136563. [END CNI-136563]

---

#### 2.4.23.5.3 Load Metric based Algorithm

##### 2.4.23.5.3.0-1 Load Metric based Algorithm Pooling of PUCCH F3 Resources within BBRM (ID: 11175246)

**2.4.23.5.3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11175259`)

Load Metric based Algorithm
For FDD FR1, requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7948\_replaced([11211803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9ro_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) specifies how to distribute the PUCCH F3 resources among the cells which report a metric rrmCAF3Ue(i) > 0.

It is possible that after that algorithm some PUCCH F3 resources are still left unassigned.

This requirement proposes an algorithm which allocates PUCCH F3 resources to Cells with rrmCAF3Ue(i) > 0 according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7948\_replaced([11211803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9ro_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and additionally allocates possible remaning PUCCH F3 resources to cells which received less than 2 F3 resources, as 2 is the maximum amount of PUCCH F3 resources which 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7948\_replaced([11211803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9ro_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) allocates to a cell. By distributing the remaining F3 resources we also want to give higher priority to cells reporting rrmCAF3Ue(i) = 1, compared to cells reporting rrmCAF3Ue(i) = 0.

The idea is not to go through all the cells mutiple times: a first time to assign 1 F3 to all the cells with rrmCAF3Ue(i) > 0, a second time to assign an additional F3 to all the cells with rrmCAF3Ue(i) > 1, and so on.., but to assign all the F3 resources in one loop and starting from a different cell at each pooling allocation.

Initializations:

numCellsWithF3Demand = "amount of cells with rrmCAF3Ue(i) > 0"

numCellsWithHighF3Demand = "amount of cells with rrmCAF3Ue(i) > 1"

numCellsDemanding1F3 = numCellsWithF3Demand - numCellsWithHighF3Demand

l1PucchF3Capacity = maxNumPucchF3HarqL1pool

leftCapacityAfterGive1ToCellsDemandingAtLeast1 = max(0, L1PucchF3Capacity - numCellsWithF3Demand), amount of F3 resource left after reserving one to each cell with rrmCAF3Ue(i) > 0

leftCapacityAfterGive2ToCellsDemandingMoreThan1 = max(0, leftCapacityAfterGive1ToCellsDemandingAtLeast1 - numCellsWithHighF3Demand), amount of F3 resource left after reserving additional one to each cell with rrmCAF3Ue(i) > 1

additonalCapacityForCellsDemanding1F3 = min(leftCapacityAfterGive2ToCellsDemandingMoreThan1, numCellsDemanding1F3)

additonalCapacityForCellsDemanding0F3 = max(leftCapacityAfterGive2ToCellsDemandingMoreThan1 - additonalCapacityForCellsDemanding1F3, 0)

startCellIndex: it can be initialized to 0 at start-up and incremented at each pooling period before running the algorithm below. It is used to start from a different cell, each pooling allocation.

Algorithm:

FOR(c = 0; c < numCells; c++)

{

i = (startCellIndex + c) MOD numCells;

maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM(i) = 0;

IF( rrmCAF3Ue(i) > 0 AND l1PucchF3Capacity > 0 )

{

maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM(i) += 1;

l1PucchF3Capacity -= 1;

}

IF( rrmCAF3Ue(i) > 1 AND leftCapacityAfterGive1ToCellsDemandingAtLeast1 > 0 )

{

maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM(i) += 1;

leftCapacityAfterGive1ToCellsDemandingAtLeast1 -= 1;

}

ELSE IF( rrmCAF3Ue(i) == 1 AND additonalCapacityForCellsDemanding1F3 > 0 )

{

maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM(i) += 1;

additonalCapacityForCellsDemanding1F3 -= 1;

}

ELSE IF ( rrmCAF3Ue(i) == 0 AND additonalCapacityForCellsDemanding0F3 > 0)

{

maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM(i) += 1;

additonalCapacityForCellsDemanding0F3 -= 1;

}

}

[CB008847-E] For TDD FR1 the distribution of PUCCH F3 resources is specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6551\_replaced([11211831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9rpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and applies to both round-robin and metric-based algorithm. How to allocate possible PUCCH F3 resources which are left unassigned by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6551\_replaced([11211831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9rpvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is left to the implementation. [End CB008847-E]

[CB007112-C] if subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is 'true'

The maximum PUCCH F3 resources capacity of one 'L1 Pool' will be considered even though there are two L1 pools.

[End CB007112-C]

---

##### 2.4.23.5.3.0-2 Load Metric based Algorithm Pooling of Non-GBR resources within BBRM (ID: 12479072)

**2.4.23.5.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12479088`)

Load Metric based Algorithm
[Before CB014542-B]

If NRBTS.actScheUePooling = TRUE, then L2PS (BBRM) needs to follow algorithm [11211400](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_LhMF8PvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)(5G\_UP\_ALG\_10365\_replaced([11211400](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw8dgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[CB014542-B]

For FR1 in Load Metric based, L2PS shall follow the following requirement for non-GBR pooling.

If NRBTS.actScheUePooling = TRUE and NRBTS.actScheUePoolingEnh = FALSE, then L2PS (BBRM) needs to follow legacy algorithm [11211400](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_LhMF8PvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)(5G\_UP\_ALG\_10365\_replaced([11211400](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw8dgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

and if NRBTS.actScheUePooling = TRUE and NRBTS.actScheUePoolingEnh = TRUE, then L2PS (BBRM) needs to follow enhanced algorithm [11211514](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_MFtIQPvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)(5G\_UP\_ALG\_32798\_replaced([11211514](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw8dhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For actScheUePoolingEnh pooling, GBR & non-GBR average shall be calculated as normal average with respective DL or UL slots of a pooling period and reset at the end of pooling period.

[Before CB014542-B-UPD001] For actScheUePoolingEnh pooling, pre-cs UE average shall be calculated as normal average with all slots of a pooling period and reset at the end of pooling period.

[CB014542-B-UPD001] For actScheUePoolingEnh pooling, pre-cs UE count shall be taken from the last slot before next pooling period, and reset at the end of pooling period. [END CB014542-B-UPD001]

[End CB014542-B]

---

##### 2.4.23.5.3.0-3 Load Metric based Algorithm Allocation of remaining pooled Resources (number of PDSCH/PUSCH UEs per slot) within BBRM (ID: 11175263)

**2.4.23.5.3.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11175278`)

Load Metric based Algorithm
[Before PR647478]

The allocation of the pooled resources "number of scheduled Ues per slot" happens according to the algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6363\_replaced([11210707](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw6BQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the output for each cell k is Rsum,x(k, t), as calculated in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6548\_replaced([11211690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9ElPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

This requirement proposes a way to round Rsum,x(k, t) to an integer value and to distribute the remaining resources, if any.

The idea is to prioritize the cells with higher decimal part in Rsum,x(k, t) and give them one additional resource, if available:

RsumInt,x(k, t) = roundDown( Rsum,x(k, t) )

RsumDec,x(k, t) = Rsum,x(k, t) – RsumInt,x(k, t)

The cells are sorted by decreasing RsumDec,x(k, t) and the sorted indexes are filled in sortedListOfCellIndexes[]

FOR(i = 0; i < numCell; i++)

{

k = sortedListOfCellIndexes[i];

IF( RsumInt,x(k,t) < Rmax,x(k,t))

{

givenRes = min(  Rleftpool,x(l1pool(k),t),

Rleftpool,x(l1subpool(k),t),

Rleftpool,x(l2subpool(k),t),

1);

Rleftpool,x(l1pool(k),t) -= givenRes;

Rleftpool,x(l1subpool(k),t) -= givenRes;

Rleftpool,x(l2subpool(k),t) -= givenRes;

RsumInt,x(k, t) += givenRes;

}

}

where:

* x indicates either "number of PDSCH UEs per slot" or "number of PUSCH UEs per slot" resources;
* Rmax,x(i,t) and Rsum,x(i,t) are defined respectively in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7943\_replaced([11210499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw5aPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6548\_replaced([11211690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9ElPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));
* Rleftpool,x(p,t) is the remaining capacity on the l1pool/l1subpool/l2subpool the cell belongs to;

RsumInt,x(k, t) is the final output.

The allocation of "number of PUCCH F1 per slot" according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8681\_replaced([11211775](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9EnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) has to happen after the distribution of the remaning resources for "number of PDSCH UEs per slot", as described in this requirement.

[PR647478]

The allocation will happen according to what is specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_6548\_replaced([11211690](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9ElPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End PR647478]

[CB007112-C]  if subcellDeactivationDlAllowed is 'true'

sortedListOfCellIndexes[] will contain all the cells from both the L1 Pools. The allocation of "number of PUCCH F1 per slot" happens according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8681\_replaced([11211775](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw9EnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), also for PUCCH F1 resource distribution capacity of one 'L1 Pool' is considered.

[End CB007112-C]

---

##### 2.4.23.5.3.0-4 Load Metric based Algorithm Missing metric values from DL and UL schedulers (ID: 11175282)

**2.4.23.5.3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175287`)

Load Metric based Algorithm
\* When BBRM is triggered to execute the pooling and no metric is available from a certain DL/UL scheduler, the BBRM shall assume value 0 for all the metrics of that scheduler and by this it shall allocate (at least) the minimum amount of resources (according to bbPoolMinResource) for the resources "number of PDSCH/PUSCH UEs per slot".

* If cell load metric values are not received by the BBRM for a certain DL/UL scheduler, which is not the first one for that scheduler, the BBRM shall not detect it and just use the latest received cell load metric values.

---

### 2.4.23.6 Throughput Pooling

#### 2.4.23.6.0-1 Throughput Pooling Overview (ID: 11175295)

**2.4.23.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175299`)

Throughput Pooling
Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28774\_replaced([11214770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxPYefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Throughput Pooling overview.

---

#### 2.4.23.6.0-2 Throughput Pooling Description: (ID: 11175303)

**2.4.23.6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175308`)

Throughput Pooling
Throughput Pooling Algorithm Procedure and Control Description:

1. L2PS BBRM Calculates the throughput grant given to a cell as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28774\_replaced([11214770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxPYefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
2. L2PS DL/UL scheduler restricts the combined cell throughput of all the UEs scheduled per slot in a cell as described in: 5G\_L2\_21246\_replaced([11150232](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24bK0PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_21258\_replaced([11152616](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24p0VvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Due to any exception, If PsInternal\_ResourceResp message is not successfully received at DL/UL scheduler then Minimum throughput allocation has to be applied for cell as mentioned in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28823\_replaced([11214909](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxQmkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
3. If scheduling a UE causes the throughput limits to be exceeded, L2PS DL/UL scheduler shall perform the necessary counter-measures as described in 5G\_L2\_21246\_replaced([11150232](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24bK0PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_21258\_replaced([11152616](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24p0VvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). L2PS DL/UL schedulers will increase the relevant R&D counters as described in: 5G\_L2\_21258\_replaced([11152616](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24p0VvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_21261\_replaced([11152666](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24p0XvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_21263\_replaced([11194108](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28w4RPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_21264\_replaced([11194120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28w4R_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.23.7 Power Pooling

#### 2.4.23.7.0-1 Power Pooling Overview (ID: 13444740)

**2.4.23.7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13444741`)

Power Pooling
The overview of Power Pooling and its interworking with PRB Pooling is described in [13237747:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_mGCzWL-SEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)and [12711586](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_XkY2tGa8EfCwWbD4x7Iu-g?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

L2-PS has three main functionality for Power Pooling

1. Power Pool Configuration in L2-PS

The creation/modification/deletion of Power Pools in L2-PS is handled by the Address Distribution , Cell Setup and Cell Reconfiguration messages from CPRT-L2-PS.

1. Intra Power Pooling Algorithm in L2-PS

The overview of Intra Power Pooling alorithm is described in 13242831

3.  Power Pooling Based DL Power Control in L2-PS

The overview of Power Pooling in DL power control is described here  13388904

---

#### 2.4.23.7.0-2 Power Pooling Power Pool Configuration in L2-PS (ID: 13444742)

**2.4.23.7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13444743`)

Power Pooling
The information about Power Pooling feature activation and applicability for a particular cell are received in the [10966028: Address Distribution](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UL-sfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) , [10966848: Cell Setup](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UQ3OPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and [10970911: Cell Reconfiguration](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UlnW_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

The interface from CP-RT carrying the Power pool information and configuration are in below requirements

1. Address Distribution - [10966124: L2RtPoolDeploymentInformations structure](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)
2. Cell Setup - [10967202: PsCell\_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

The handling of the message is covered in [13399497: Handling of Power Pooling Configuration for FR1-TDD in Ecpri7.2e](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_8f_PcQsCEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

1. Cell Reconfiguration - [10971056: PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

The handling of the message is covered in [13400637: Handling of Power Pooling Configuration for FR1-TDD in Ecpri7.2e](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_8-494QtCEfGu_4itz1o3RA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

---

#### 2.4.23.7.0-3 Power Pooling Intra Power Pooling Algorithm in L2-PS (ID: 13444745)

**2.4.23.7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13444746`)

Power Pooling
Power Pooling Algorithm Procedure and Control Description:

1. The Initialisation steps in L2PS BBRM are covered in the requirement 13242812 & 13248844
2. L2PS BBRM Calculates the Allocated Cell DL Power grant given to a cell using Static Allocation

Calculate MinPSD refer 13237865

Calculated MinPSD for LayerPRB refer 13237872

Static Algorithm refer 13244875

1. L2PS BBRM Calculates the Allocated Cell DL Power grant given to a cell using Middle Round Allocation

Calculate MinPSD refer 13237865

Calculated MinPSD for LayerPRB refer 13237872

Power Pooling Allocation for Power Pool refer 13388172

Calculate Minimum Power Demand of Cell refer 13237878

Calculate Additional Power Demand of Cell refer 13237902

Calculate Total Power Demand of Cell refer 13237942

Calculate Total Power Demand of Power Pool refer 13237948

Allocation of Remaining LayerPRB per Cell (Round-2) refer 13244514

Middle Round Algorithm refer 13387824

Calculate Excess DL PRB refer 13388173

4. L2PS BBRM Calculates the Allocated Cell DL Power grant given to a cell using Post Round Allocation

Calculate MinPSD refer 13237865

Calculated MinPSD for LayerPRB refer 13237872

Power Pooling Allocation for Power Pool refer 13388172

Calculate Minimum Power Demand of Cell refer 13237878

Calculate Additional Power Demand of Cell refer 13237902

Calculate Total Power Demand of Cell refer 13237942

Calculate Total Power Demand of Power Pool refer 13237948

Allocation of Remaining LayerPRB per Cell (Round-2) refer 13244514

Post Round Algorithm refer 13388145

Calculate Excess DL PRB refer 13388173

1. L2PS BBRM Shall pass the calculated Allocated Cell DL Power to  DL scheduler using PsInternal\_ResourceResp message. Due to any exception, If PsInternal\_ResourceResp message is not successfully received at DL scheduler then Minimum DL allocation has to be applied for cell as

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| allocCellDLPwr = minPSD W/Mhz \*1000 \* CellBW / NumofTrx |  |  |  |  |

1. Based on the L2PS BBRM provided information L2PS DL scheduler shall increase the relevant counters and update TTI Traces as in [11204427: dlAllocatedPooledResourcesData](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-A1d_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

---

#### 2.4.23.7.0-4 Power Pooling Power Pooling Based DL Power Control (ID: 13444747)

**2.4.23.7.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `13444748`)

Power Pooling
Power Pooling based DL Power Control impact

1. PDSCH Power Setting refer 13244503

Calculate Maximum DL PRB based Target PSD refer 13388906

Determine Used PDSCH PSD refer 13388907

Calculation of PDSCH power offset caused by power pooling allocation  refer 13388916

- The PDSCH Power offset due to power pooling shall be scaled ( multiplied by 10 and converted to int) inline to the pdschTbTransmitPower in [11512745:](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FRrpPfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Update usedPdschPSD counter (MAX\_USED\_PSD\_PDSCH (M55308C00177), MIN\_USED\_PSD\_PDSCH (M55308C00178),AVG\_USED\_PSD\_PDSCH(M55308C00179)

Note: The PSD counters listed above are computed at the end of FDM phase , there can be minor differences in the event of change in the used PRB in the current slot due to any UE Drop ,like in throughput limit based UE drop, from scheduling or PRB Reduction (Cell/Layer/Stream PRB) like in downgrade of UE rank or PRB shaving  in FD phase.

1. PDCCH resource constraint based on Power Pooling

PDCCH Layer PRB usage and constraint PDCCH Layer PRB usage and constraint  refer 13244504

---

## 2.4.24 Overheating Assistance Information

### 2.4.24.0-2 Overheating Assistance Information Overview (ID: 11175320)

**2.4.24.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11175331`)

Overheating Assistance Information
Overheating Assistance Information enhances the legacy overheating functionality which was introduced by PR499482, supporting the RRC Assistance Information procedure for overheating. gNB-CU configures a UE having the required capability to assist the RAN in managing an overheating condition by signalling, via the RRC: UE Assistance Information message, when it detects internal overheating or when it is no longer overheated.

The RRC:UE Assistance Information message contains the OverheatingAssistanceInformation (OAI) Information Element (IE) by which the UE experiencing internal overheating can provide to gNB-CU information about Maximum number of SCells that should be configured/activated in downlink: reducedMaxCCs/reducedCCsDL[CB006137-BB], and uplink: reducedMaxCCs/reducedCCsUL[End CB006137-BB].

---

### 2.4.24.0-3 Overheating Assistance Information Description (ID: 11175335)

**2.4.24.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11175351`)

Overheating Assistance Information
The procedure is as follow:

Steps:

1. UE signals that is in overheating condition.

2. OverheatingAssistanceInformation (OAI) Information Element (IE) contains field reducedMaxCCs/reducedCCsDL[CB006137-BB] and reducedCCsUL[End CB006137-BB].

3. CPlane sets number of UE’s SCells that can be active for overheating ([Before CB006137-BB]maxNumberOfActiveSCells[CB006137-BB]maxNumberOfActiveSCellsDl and maxNumberOfActiveSCellsUl[End CB006137-BB]).

4. L2-PS limits the maximum number of active SCells to value[Before CB006137-BB] MaxNumofActiveSCells [CB006137-BB] MaxNumofActiveSCellsDL for DL and MaxNumofActiveSCellsUL for UL [End CB006137-BB](if other than "not limited") by using MAC CE. [PR671242] In case there are any SCell activation processes ongoing, then 5G-L2-PS shall store the maxNumberOfActiveSCells and make sure limitation is no exceeded once Scell activation processes are finalized.[End PR671242]

5. SCell(s) shall be deactivated by using MAC CE and it shall happen within 100ms from the overheating assistance information received from UE.

6. Number of SCells dectivated as indicated in MAC CE.

7. UE signals that is still in overheating condition and decides to reduce more SCell(s).

8. OverheatingAssistanceInformation (OAI) Information Element (IE) contains field reducedMaxCCs/reducedCCsDL[CB006137-BB] and reducedCCsUL[End CB006137-BB].

9. CPlane sets number of UE’s SCells that can be active for overheating ([Before CB006137-BB]maxNumberOfActiveSCells[CB006137-BB]maxNumberOfActiveSCellsDl and maxNumberOfActiveSCellsUl[End CB006137-BB]).

10. L2-PS decides to deactivate more Scells.

11. Number of SCells dectivated as indicated in MAC CE.

12. Overheating state is over.

13. OverheatingAssistanceInformation (OAI) Information Element (IE) with empty IE.

14. CP starts timerPostOverheating and when it expires the gNB decides to activate all the SCells or some SCell(s) by removing the limitation in L2-PS

15. L2-PS shall activate new SCell(s) using configured activation method.

16. SCell(s) shall be activated by using MAC CE.

17. Number of SCells activated as indicated in MAC CE.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating the "Overheating Assistance Information" process in a telecommunications system. The diagram depicts interactions between three entities: UE (User Equipment), CP (Control Plane), and UP (User Plane).
The diagram shows the following sequence of events:
1. **UE is overheated**: The UE initiates the process by indicating it is overheated.
2. **RRC Overheating Assistance Information**: The UE sends RRC Overheating Assistance Information to the CP, including parameters like `reducedMaxCCs` and `reducedCCsDL`.
3. **PsUser\_UserChangeInd**: The CP sends a `PsUser_UserChangeInd` message to the UP, indicating a change in the maximum number of active SCells to `reducedMaxCCs`.
4. **L2-PS limits active SCells**: The UP (specifically L2-PS) limits the number of active SCells to the value specified by `MaxNumofActiveSCells`.
5. **SCell(s) deactivation**: The UE deactivates one or more SCells using MAC CE (Medium Access Control Control Element).
6. **SCell deactivation ACK**: The UE sends an acknowledgment for the SCell deactivation to the CP.
7. **Number of SCells activated**: The number of activated SCells is indicated in MAC CE.
The process then continues with the UE deciding to reduce more SCells if it continues to be overheated:
8. **UE overheating continue, UE decides to reduce more SCell(s)**: The UE, still overheated, decides to further reduce the number of active SCells.
9. **RRC Overheating Assistance Information**: The UE sends updated RRC Overheating Assistance Information to the CP.
10. **PsUser\_UserChangeInd**: The CP sends another `PsUser_UserChangeInd` to the UP with updated `reducedMaxCCs'`.
11. **L2-PS limits active SCells**: The UP again limits the number of active SCells.
12. **more Scell(s) deactivation**: The UE deactivates more SCells.
13. **SCell deactivation ACK**: The UE acknowledges the deactivation.
The diagram then shows the UE overheated state ending:
14. **UE Overheated state over**: The UE is no longer overheated.
15. **RRC Overheating Assistance Information, with empty IE**: The UE sends RRC Overheating Assistance Information with an empty IE to the CP.
16. **CP starts timerPostOverheating**: The CP starts a `timerPostOverheating`.
17. **gNB activates SCells**: When the timer expires, the gNB decides to activate all or some SCells by removing the limitation in L2-Ps.
18. **PsUser\_UserChangeind**: The CP sends a `PsUser_UserChangeind` to the UP with `maxNumberOfActiveSCells = MAX_NUM_SC_PER_USER`.
19. **L2-PS activates new SCell(s)**: The UP (L2-PS) activates new SCells using a configured activation method.
20. **Scell(s) activation**: The activation is confirmed via MAC CE.
21. **Number of SCells activated**: The number of activated SCells is indicated in MAC CE.
A vertical annotation on the right side indicates an "Expected maximum deactivation time is 100 ms". The "Overheating Indication" on the left side has a "Prohibit Timer (timerOverheatind Prohibit)" associated with it.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5122.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The fourth state, colored light orange, also features a self-transition arrow. The text inside says: "State applicable when actNrRim == {'framework1'}".

Remote Interference Management
5G-L2-Ps shall maintain following states and transitions when actNrRim is set to "RI detection only" or "RI detection and mitigation":

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a state machine diagram. It depicts three main states: STATE 0, STATE 1, and STATE 2, with a sub-state STATE 2a.
**STATE 0:**
\* This state is represented by a circle with an arrow indicating a self-loop.
\* The attributes within this state are: `riSuspected = false` and `riDetected = false`.
**Transitions from STATE 0:**
\* A transition labeled `riSuspected = false` leads back to STATE 0.
\* A transition labeled `riSuspected = true` leads to STATE 1.
\* A transition labeled `riDetected = false` leads to STATE 1.
**STATE 1:**
\* This state is represented by a blue circle with an arrow indicating a self-loop.
\* The attributes within this state are: `riSupected = true` and `riDetected = false`.
**Transitions from STATE 1:**
\* A transition labeled `riDetected = true` leads to STATE 2.
**STATE 2:**
\* This state is represented by a blue circle with an arrow indicating a self-loop.
\* The attribute within this state is: `riDetected = true`.
**Transitions from STATE 2:**
\* A dashed transition labeled `actNrRim == {'framework0', 'framework1'}` leads to STATE 2a.
**STATE 2a:**
\* This state is represented by a yellow-orange circle.
\* The text within this state is: `STATE 2a`.
The diagram illustrates the flow between different states based on the conditions of `riSuspected`, `riDetected`, and `actNrRim`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5129.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**2.4.25.0-2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175403`)

Remote Interference Management
5G-L2-Ps shall maintain following states and transitions in addition to states defined in requirement 5G\_L2\_16161\_replaced([11175383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26r0ovvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when actNrRim is set to "framework0" :

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating the transitions between different states based on specific conditions. The states are represented by circles, and the transitions are represented by arrows with associated conditions.
Here's a breakdown of the states and transitions:
**States:**
\* **STATE 0** (Black circle)
\* **STATE 2** (Blue circle)
\* **STATE 2a** (Green circle, with `riDetected = true`, `rs1Detected = false`)
\* **STATE 2a** (Orange circle)
\* **STATE 2** (Green circle, with `riDetected = true`, `rs1Detected = true`)
\* **STATE 2b** (Green circle, with `riDetected = false`, `rs1Detected = true`)
**Transitions:**
1. **From STATE 0 to STATE 2a:**
\* Condition: `riDetected = false`, `rs1Detected = false`
2. **From STATE 2 to STATE 2a:**
\* Condition: `actNrRim changed from 'framework0' to 'RI detection only' or 'RI detection and mitigation'` (Dashed blue arrow)
3. **From STATE 2a (Green) to STATE 2:**
\* Condition: `rs1Detected = false`
4. **From STATE 2a (Green) to STATE 2b:**
\* Condition: `rs1Detected = true`
5. **From STATE 2a (Orange) to STATE 0:**
\* Condition: `actNrRim changed from 'framework0' to 'framewok1'` (Dashed orange arrow)
6. **From STATE 2 (Blue) to STATE 2a (Green):**
\* Condition: `actNrRim changed from 'framework0' to 'RI detection only' or 'RI detection and mitigation'` (Dashed blue arrow)
7. **From STATE 2 (Green) to STATE 0:**
\* Condition: `riDetected = false`, `rs1Detected = false`
8. **From STATE 2 (Green) to STATE 2a (Green):**
\* Condition: `riDetected = false`, `rs1Detected = true`
9. **From STATE 2 (Green) to STATE 2b:**
\* Condition: `riDetected = true`, `rs1Detected = false`
10. **From STATE 2b to STATE 0:**
\* Condition: `actNrRim changed from 'framework0' to one of {'disabled', 'RI detection only' or 'RI detection and mitigation'} or rs1Detected = false or maxRsDetectDurationWithoutRi expired`
11. **From STATE 2b to STATE 2a (Green):**
\* Condition: `riDetected = true`, `rs1Detected = true`
12. **From STATE 2a (Green) to STATE 0:**
\* Condition: `actNrRim changed from 'framework0' to 'framewok1'` (Dashed orange arrow)
13. **From STATE 2a (Green) to STATE 2:**
\* Condition: `riDetected = true`, `rs1Detected = false`
14. **From STATE 2a (Green) to STATE 2b:**
\* Condition: `riDetected = false`, `rs1Detected = true`
The diagram visually represents a system's behavior, likely related to detection and mitigation processes, where different inputs (`riDetected`, `rs1Detected`, `actNrRim` changes) trigger state changes.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5131.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**2.4.25.0-2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175419`)

Remote Interference Management
5G-L2-Ps shall maintain following states and transitions in addition to states defined in requirement 5G\_L2\_16162\_replaced([11175397](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26r0pPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when actNrRim is set to "framework1" :

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating the transitions between different states based on various conditions. The states are represented by circles, and the transitions are depicted by arrows. Each transition is labeled with the conditions that trigger it.
Here's a breakdown of the states and transitions:
**States:**
\* **STATE 0:** Represented by a black circle.
\* **STATE 2:** Represented by a blue circle.
\* **STATE 2a:** Represented by a green circle.
\* **STATE 2b:** Represented by an orange circle.
\* **STATE 2c:** Represented by an orange circle.
\* **STATE 2ab, 2bc, 2ac:** Represented by a light orange circle with the labels "2ab", "2bc", and "2ac" inside.
**Transitions and Conditions:**
\* **From STATE 0 to STATE 2a:** Triggered by `actNrRim changed from 'framework1' to 'framework0'`.
\* **From STATE 2a to STATE 0:** Triggered by `actNrRim changed from 'framework0' to one of {'disabled', 'RI detection only' or 'RI detection and mitigation'} or rs1Detected = false or maxRsDetectDurationWithoutRi expired`.
\* **From STATE 2a to STATE 2:** Triggered by `actNrRim changed from 'framework1' to 'RI detection only' or 'RI detection and mitigation'`.
\* **From STATE 2 to STATE 2a:** Triggered by `riDetected = false, rs1Detected = false, rs2Detected = false`.
\* **From STATE 2 to STATE 2b:** Triggered by `rs2Detected = false`.
\* **From STATE 2 to STATE 2c:** Triggered by `rs1Detected = false, rs2Detected = true`.
\* **From STATE 2b to STATE 0:** Triggered by `actNrRim changed from 'framework1' to 'framework0'`.
\* **From STATE 2b to STATE 2:** Triggered by `riDetected = true, rs1Detected = true, rs2Detected = true`.
\* **From STATE 2b to STATE 2c:** Triggered by `rs1Detected = false, rs2Detected = true`.
\* **From STATE 2c to STATE 0:** Triggered by `actNrRim changed from 'framework0' to one of {'disabled', 'RI detection only' or 'RI detection and mitigation'} or rs2Detected = false or maxRsDetectDurationWithoutRi expired`.
\* **From STATE 2c to STATE 2:** Triggered by `riDetected = true, rs1Detected = true`.
\* **From STATE 2c to STATE 2b:** Triggered by `rs1Detected = true, rs2Detected = false`.
\* **From STATE 2ab, 2bc, 2ac to STATE 0:** Triggered by `riDetected = false`.
\* **From STATE 2ab, 2bc, 2ac to STATE 2:** Triggered by `riDetected = true, rs1Detected = true`.
\* **From STATE 2ab, 2bc, 2ac to STATE 2b:** Triggered by `rs1Detected = false, rs2Detected = true`.
\* **From STATE 2ab, 2bc, 2ac to STATE 2c:** Triggered by `rs1Detected = false, rs2Detected = true`.
The diagram also shows a state labeled "STATE 2" (blue circle) with a transition from STATE 0, triggered by `riDetected = false`. There are also transitions from STATE 2 to STATE 2ab, 2bc, 2ac, and vice-versa, with conditions involving `riDetected`, `rs1Detected`, and `rs2Detected`.
The diagram uses different colors for states: black for STATE 0, blue for STATE 2, green for STATE 2a, and orange for STATE 2b, 2c, and the combined states 2ab, 2bc, 2ac. The arrows are colored based on the transition type: yellow for conditions related to `rs1Detected` and `rs2Detected`, dashed blue for `actNrRim` changes, and dashed green for `actNrRim` changes.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5133.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating different states and transitions within a system. The states are represented by circles, and the transitions are represented by arrows. Each state has a name, such as "STATE 2", "STATE 2a", "STATE 2ab", "STATE 2ac", "STATE 2bc", and "STATE 0". Some states also have associated conditions or variables like "riDetected", "rs1Detected", and "rs2Detected" with their boolean values.
The transitions between states are triggered by specific events or conditions, which are labeled on the arrows. These labels describe changes in "actNrRim" or the values of the "riDetected", "rs1Detected", and "rs2Detected" variables.
Here's a breakdown of the states and some of their transitions:
**States:**
\* **STATE 2:** A blue circle.
\* **STATE 2a:** A green circle.
\* **STATE 2a, 2b, 2c:** An orange circle with these labels. There are multiple instances of this state representation.
\* **STATE 2ab:** An orange circle with "riDetected = true", "rs1Detected = true", "rs2Detected = false".
\* **STATE 2ac:** An orange circle with "riDetected = true", "rs1Detected = false", "rs2Detected = true".
\* **STATE 2bc:** An orange circle with "riDetected = false", "rs1Detected = true", "rs2Detected = true".
\* **STATE 0:** A black circle.
**Transitions (Examples):**
\* From a STATE 2a (green) to STATE 2ab (orange) when "actNrRim changed from 'framework1' to 'framework0'".
\* From STATE 2ab to STATE 2ac when "rs1Detected = false" and "rs2Detected = true".
\* From STATE 2ab to STATE 2bc when "riDetected = false" and "rs2Detected = true".
\* From STATE 2ac to STATE 2bc when "riDetected = false" and "rs1Detected = true".
\* From STATE 2bc to STATE 2ac when "riDetected = true" and "rs2Detected = false".
\* From STATE 2ac to STATE 0 when "riDetected = false" and "rs2Detected = false".
\* From STATE 2bc to STATE 0 when "actNrRim changed from 'framework0' to one of {'disabled', 'RI detection only' or 'RI detection and mitigation'}" or "(rs1Detected = false and rs2Detected = false)" or "maxRsDetectDurationWithoutRi expired".
\* From STATE 2 (blue) to STATE 2ac when "actNrRim changed from 'framework1' to 'RI detection only' or 'RI detection and mitigation'".
\* From STATE 2a (orange) to STATE 2ac with a dashed arrow, indicating a possible transition.
The diagram uses different colors for states (blue, green, orange, black) and arrow styles (solid, dashed) to convey different types of information or relationships. The orange states seem to represent a more detailed breakdown of a particular condition, possibly related to detection results.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5133.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

### 2.4.25.0-3 Remote Interference Management RIM Manager (ID: 11175424)

**2.4.25.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11175437`)

Remote Interference Management
After reception of PsCell\_CellSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988\_replaced([10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if actNrRim is set to 1 (riDetectionOnly) or 2 (riDetectionAndMitigation) or 3 (framework0) [CB009823-B] 4 (framework1)[End CB009823-B], 5G-L2-PS shall:

 store the RI monitoring pattern and mark Special and UL slots that are target for RI measurements reception via UlData\_RimReceiveReq (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14748\_replaced([11514602](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR4difvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in given RI states (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9328\_replaced([11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),

 identify and store startPRBs and numOfPRBs (rdRIMsubbandsize) for all subbands according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9328\_replaced([11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 maintain RI state machine and keep track of current RI state (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9180\_replaced([11161269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktTTqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). After rrmRDRImonitorTimer (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2293\_replaced([10969964](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgHyvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for nrRiMonitorWindow) expires, RI state machine shall apply transitions between RI states from the beginning of the next RI monitoring pattern.

If PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq modifies actNrRim or rimConfig (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2293\_replaced([10969964](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgHyvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall apply the modification after the next time rrmRDRImonitorTimer (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9180\_replaced([11161269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktTTqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) expires, except of the case actNrRim is reconfigured from 1 (riDetectionOnly) or 2 (riDetectionAndMitigation)  or 3 (framework0) [CB009823-B] or 4 (framework1)[End CB009823-B]to 0 (disabled), for which 5G-L2-PS shall deactivate the feature and close the state machine immediately and except of UL mitigation parameters (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9172\_replaced([11163373](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYzNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): nrRimRarTpcCommand (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2293\_replaced([10969964](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgHyvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and status of SRS mitigation activity which shall be modified immediately.

Note: RI State is not modified upon reconfiguration, unless the feature is being disabled (actNrRim is reconfigured to 0).

---

### 2.4.25.0-4 Remote Interference Management RIM-RS Management (ID: 11175442)

**2.4.25.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175446`)

Remote Interference Management
Activation and configuration

---

**2.4.25.0-4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11175458`)

Remote Interference Management
After reception of PsCell\_CellSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if actNrRim is set to value 3 (framework0) [CB009823-B] or 4 (framework1) [End CB009823-B] , 5G-L2-PS shall:

 store rimRsConfig (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2797\_replaced([10970007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgH0fu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) according to 5G\_L2\_9429\_replaced([11142157](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23syF_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_9430\_replaced([11142183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23syG_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

 initiate empty detected (confirmed) setID and suspected setID lists [CB009823-B] for both RS1 and RS2 signals [End CB009823-B] (see 5G\_L2\_11757\_replaced([11175520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26sbzPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[CB009823-A] During cell reconfiguration procedure when 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.actNrRim reconfiguration happens then 5G-L2-PS shall continue operation with old configuration until the end of current RIM RS period and then:

* for actNrRim change from "framework1" to "framework0" then 5G-L2-Ps shall clean up all recorded RIM RS1 and RS2 set ids while maintaining RI state 2.
* for actNrRim change from "framework0" to "framework1" then 5G-L2-Ps shall clean up all recorded RIM RS1 set ids while maintaining RI state 2.
* for actNrRim change from "framework1" or "framework0" to "RI detection only" or "RI detection and mitigation" then 5G-L2-Ps shall clean up all recorded RIM RS1 and RS2 set ids while maintaining RI state 2. [End CB009823-A]

After reception of PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988\_replaced([10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), if actNrRim is set to value 3 (framework0)  [CB009823-B] or 4 (framework1) [End CB009823-B] and any of the following IEs in rimRsConfig:

  [Before CB009823-A] repetitionRsType1  [CB009823-A]  rimRsTypeConfig.rsRepetition [End CB009823-A]

 numRbForRs

 startRbForRs

 [CB009823-F] rimRsSubCarrierOffset [End CB009823-F]

  [Before CB009823-A] rsType1NScId  [CB009823-A] rimRsTypeConfig.rsNScId  [End CB009823-A]

 rsPattern1Period

 rsPattern2Period

  [Before CB009823-A]  numRsType1SetId  [CB009823-A] rimRsTypeConfig.numRsSetId  [End CB009823-A]

 [CB009823-E] actRsEnoughIndication [End CB009823-E]

was modified, 5G-L2-PS shall modify rimRsConfig, clear (reset) all entries in detected (confirmed) setID and suspected setID lists and set status of all resources in RSDetectElements (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13188\_replaced([11162108](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktW-D_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13194\_replaced([11162620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to “unmeasured”.

If given UL slot/SS is a target (on any symbol) for reception of new detection of RIM-RS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13188\_replaced([11162108](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktW-D_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13156\_replaced([11161857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or revalidation of RIM-RS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15475\_replaced([11162234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall mark this slot for reception of RIM-RS.

While fetching available RIM-RS L1-UL capacity for the cell to to fulfil 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13156\_replaced([11161857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW9_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) according to 5G\_UP\_5450\_replaced([11399506](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ugBFuPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (after PsCell\_CellSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988\_replaced([10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with actNrRim >= 3 is received), 5G-L2-PS shall use the value of IE PsCnfg\_AddressDistributionReq.L2RtPoolDeploymentInformations.maxNumberOfCells (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_14\_replaced([10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

**2.4.25.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11175463`)

Remote Interference Management
Interaction with DL PRB pooling

---

**2.4.25.0-4.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175468`)

Remote Interference Management
PrbDemand for RIM-RS

---

**2.4.25.0-4.0-2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11175493`)

Remote Interference Management
if actBbPoolTddFr1Enh is true,

At the beginning of a pooling period (First SFN, Slot0) , the DL scheduler shall send the prb Demand for RIM-RS transmission(prbDemandRimRs) to BBRM via the DlMetricInd msg (5G\_L2\_12105\_replaced([11244248](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3B1lhvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The prbDemandRimRs value depends on whether RIM-RS needs to be transmitted or not during the period from the start of 2nd SFN to the end of first SFN of the next pooling period.

In the cases described below:

* The current config is the RIM-RS configuration currently applied at the bbPooling compute metric time.
* The pending config is the RIM-RS configuration stored in the L2PS, but not yet applied at the bbPooling compute metric time.

[CB009823-F] - numRbForRs includes additional PRB required in case of rimRsSubCarrierOffset not being equal to 0 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13075\_replaced([11118073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqAIC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_5348\_replaced([11360434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucuhJfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB009823-F].

Case1: RIM-RS from current  and pending  config need to be transmitted:

prbDemandRimRs= Max (Pending numRbForRs, Current numRbForRs)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a timeline illustrating the process of RIM-RS (Radio Interface Measurement - Radio Signal) configuration and resource allocation. The timeline is divided into two main horizontal bars.
The top bar represents time periods. The left portion is colored green and labeled "Current RIM-RS Period". To the right of this, there's a star icon, followed by a light green section labeled "Pending RIM-RS Period". A vertical dashed line separates the "Current RIM-RS Period" from the "Pending RIM-RS Period". An arrow originating from "Reception of next (pending) RIM-RS config" points to the boundary between these two periods, indicating the timing of configuration reception.
The bottom bar represents "bbPooling" periods, which are further subdivided into "SFN" (System Frame Number) slots.
- The first section, labeled "bbPooling N", shows SFN0, SFN1, SFN2, SFN3, and SFN4.
- The second section, labeled "bbPooling N +1", starts after the dashed line and shows SFN0, SFN1, SFN2, SFN3, and SFN4. This section is partially colored brown, indicating a different state or allocation.
- The third section, labeled "bbPooling N +2", shows SFN0, SFN1, SFN2, SFN3, and SFN4.
Several arrows indicate processes and data flows:
- An arrow labeled "Compute Metric (prbDemand)" originates from the "Current RIM-RS Period" and points towards the "bbPooling N" section, specifically towards SFN2.
- From SFN2 in "bbPooling N", an arrow labeled "Sending MetricInd" points downwards.
- From SFN0 in "bbPooling N +1", an arrow labeled "s11 s10" points towards "Sending MetricInd".
- From SFN3 in "bbPooling N", an arrow labeled "s13" points towards "Sending MetricInd".
- From SFN0 in "bbPooling N +1", an arrow labeled "s10" points downwards towards "Sending ResourceReq".
- An arrow labeled "Sending ResourceReq" originates from "Sending MetricInd" and points downwards.
- A large green arrow originates from "Sending ResourceReq" and points to the right, leading to the text "prbDemandRimRs=Max(Pending numRbForRs, Current numRbForRs)". This indicates the calculation of a resource demand.
- An arrow originating from SFN0 in "bbPooling N +1" and labeled "s10" points to the right, connecting to a horizontal yellow arrow.
- The yellow arrow extends to the right and is labeled "apply new BBRM allocation".
- Below the "Pending RIM-RS Period" label, there is text "Pending RIM-RS is applied", with a dashed line connecting it to the yellow arrow.
- A blue star icon is present in the "Current RIM-RS Period" and the "Pending RIM-RS Period", and is also labeled "★ RIM-RS transmission".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5146.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Case2: Only RIM-RS from one config (current or pending) need to be transmitted:

 From current config:

prbDemandRimRs= Current numRbForRs

[image-1][image\_desc]The image depicts a timeline illustrating the process of receiving and applying a new RIM-RS configuration. The timeline is divided into three main periods: "Current RIM-RS Period," "Pending RIM-RS Period," and a section showing the application of the pending configuration.
Below the main timeline, there are several "bbPooling" blocks, representing different time intervals. These blocks are further subdivided into "SFN" (Slot Frame Number) segments, each with associated "sl" (slot) numbers.
Key events and actions are indicated by arrows and text:
\* **Reception of next (pending) RIM-RS config:** An arrow points from this text to the beginning of the "bbPooling N" block, indicating when the new configuration is received.
\* **Compute DIMetric (prbDemand):** An arrow originates from the "SFN4" segment of "bbPooling N" and points to this text, suggesting that a metric calculation related to PRB demand occurs at this point.
\* **Sending MetricInd:** An arrow from "Compute DIMetric (prbDemand)" leads to "Sending MetricInd," indicating a subsequent action.
\* **Sending ResourceReq:** An arrow from "Sending MetricInd" points to "Sending ResourceReq," showing another step in the process.
\* **prbDemand= Current numRbForRs:** A thick green arrow originates from the "Sending ResourceReq" area and extends downwards, with this text indicating that the PRB demand is equivalent to the current number of RBs for RS.
\* **apply new allocation:** A yellow arrow originates from the "SFN0" segment of "bbPooling N +1" and points to the end of the "bbPooling N +1" block, signifying the application of a new allocation.
\* **Pending RIM-RS config is applied:** This text is placed at the end of the timeline, indicating when the pending configuration becomes active.
A blue star symbol is used to denote "RIM-RS transmission" and appears within the "Current RIM-RS Period" and at the beginning of the "Pending RIM-RS Period."
The "bbPooling" blocks are labeled as "bbPooling N," "bbPooling N +1," and "bbPooling N +2," suggesting a sequential progression of these pooling periods. The "Current RIM-RS Period" is shown to overlap with parts of "bbPooling N" and "bbPooling N +1." The "Pending RIM-RS Period" starts towards the end of "bbPooling N +1" and continues into "bbPooling N +2." A dashed vertical line marks the transition from the "Current RIM-RS Period" to the "Pending RIM-RS Period."[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5146.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

 From pending config:

prbDemandRimRs= Pending numRbForRs

[image-2][image\_desc]The image depicts a timeline illustrating the process of receiving and applying a new RIM-RS configuration. The timeline is divided into three main periods: "Current RIM-RS Period," "Pending RIM-RS Period," and a transition phase.
The "Current RIM-RS Period" is represented by a green bar, with a blue star indicating a "RIM-RS transmission." Below this, a series of colored blocks represent different "bbPooling" periods, each divided into "SFN" (System Frame Number) segments. For "bbPooling N," we see SFN0, SFN1, SFN2, SFN3, and SFN4.
An arrow labeled "Reception of next (pending) RIM-RS config" points from the top to a specific point in time, coinciding with the start of a new "bbPooling N+1" period. This new period is highlighted with a golden color for SFN0 and SFN1, followed by SFN2, SFN3, and SFN4 in blue.
Several actions are indicated by arrows and text:
- An arrow from SFN4 of "bbPooling N" points to "Compute DIMetric (prbDemand)."
- An arrow from "Compute DIMetric (prbDemand)" points to "Sending MetricInd."
- An arrow from "Sending MetricInd" points to "Sending ResourceReq."

Another set of actions are shown related to the application of the new configuration:
- An arrow from SFN0 of "bbPooling N+1" points to "apply new allocation."
- An arrow from "apply new allocation" points to the dashed vertical line, with the text "Pending RIM-RS is applied."
