The legend at the bottom indicates that a blue star represents "RIM-RS transmission."[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5146.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Case3: No RIM-RS needed to be transmitted or cellBW=10Mhz

prbDemandRimRs= 0

With numRbForRs [CB009823-F] and rimRsSubCarrierOffset [End CB009823-F]: received via PsCell\_CellSetupReq/PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq msg [CB009823-F] (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2797\_replaced([10970007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgH0fu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB009823-F].

If a new config RIMRS is received after the bbPooling compute metric time, please refer to 5G\_L2\_14843\_replaced([11175498](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26sbtvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

**2.4.25.0-4.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11175498`)

Remote Interference Management
RIM-RS transmission conditions

---

**2.4.25.0-4.0-2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11175507`)

Remote Interference Management
if actBbPoolTddFr1Enh is true,

A new RIM-RS config can be received (via cellReconfigurationReq) after the prbDemandRimRs computation is done (at the end of the last SFN of the pooling period).

In this case, if a RIM-RS with the new config needs to be transmitted during the period from the start of 2nd SFN to the end of first SFN of the next pooling period, this RIM-RS can be scheduled only in the following cases :

 cellConfig1 and newRimRsRequiredResources <= min (numAllocCellPrbDl \* 2, previous\_prbDemandRimRs)

 cellConfig2 and newRimRsRequiredResources <= min (numAllocCellPrbDl, previous\_prbDemandRimRs)

With :

 CellConfig1: (actBeamforming is true and dlSubcellType of Primary Subcell is "C4")

 CellConfig2: (actBeamforming is false and dlMiMoMode is "ClosedLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4 or OpenLoopSpatialMultiplexing\_4x4" (5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) or (dlSubcellType of Primary Subcell is "C2")

 numAllocCellPrbDl: number of DL PRB allocated by BBRM for the cell.

 previous\_prbDemandRimRs: the previous prbDemand needed for RIM-RS transmission (depends on whether a RIM-RS is needed to be transmitted during the pooling period or not) [CB009823-F], including additional PRB required in case of rimRsSubCarrierOffset (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2797\_replaced([10970007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgH0fu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) not being equal to 0 (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13075\_replaced([11118073](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqAIC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_5348\_replaced([11360434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucuhJfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB009823-F]

 newRimRsRequiredResources: resources required for RIM-RS transmssion in pending configuration [CB009823-F], including additional PRB required in case of rimRsSubCarrierOffset not being equal to 0 [End CB009823-F].

Otherwise, the RIMRS transmission will be blocked until the next pooling period.

Note: RIM-RS transmission may be blocked after the cellSetup until having a valid prbDemandRimRs and numAllocCellPrbDl.

For each blocked RIM-RS transmission, the PM counter RIM\_RS\_TX\_DISC (5G\_L2\_14813\_replaced([11195648](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_289FhfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is incremented.

---

**2.4.25.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `11175511`)

Remote Interference Management
Exception handling for RIM-RS transmission

---

**2.4.25.0-4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175515`)

Remote Interference Management
When a slot is used for RIM-RS transmission, 5G-L2-PS shall, as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13085\_replaced([11161241](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktTTpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) :

* stop scheduling PDSCH in this slot including scheduling UE, SIB, Paging.
* discard CSI-RS for tracking, measurement or beam management transmitted in the same slot

if actBbPoolTddFr1Enh is true, the RIMRS transmission can be blocked as specified in 5G\_L2\_14843\_replaced([11175498](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26sbtvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In this case, L2PS shall not stop scheduling of PDSCH, CSI-RS and other channels.

---

### 2.4.25.0-5 Remote Interference Management Internal setID list maintenance for RIM-RS detection (ID: 11175520)

**2.4.25.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `11175533`)

Remote Interference Management
This requirement serves as an elaboration of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13204\_replaced([11163050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for maintaining detected set ID list.

5G-L2-PS shall store circular buffers (separate set of buffers for RS1 and RS2 signals) **for detected (confirmed) setID list with 640 entries and suspected set IDs with 25000 entries**

Each entry of single setID shall store:

 sequence index of RIM-RS detection (3 bits),

 number of RIM-RS detections (minimum of 3 bits),

 frequency index of RIM-RS detection (2 bits),

 symbol distance of the detection in relation to the end of Guard Period (6 bits),

 signal power of the detection (16 bits) – signal power of RIM-RS is received as float on 32 bits (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14855\_replaced([11514639](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR4djvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), however the step is 0.1 with signed integral part fitting on maximum of 9 bits, so it can be safely stored as 16-bit signed integer after e.g. multiplying the input by 8 or 10.

Entries in circular buffers shall be stored sorted based on the repetition window they belong to, so only set of neighboring entries belong to the same repetition window.

During each repetition window 5G-L2-PS shall create temporary container with statistics for each possible setID belonging to given repetition window (maximum of 32 elements (up to 4 frequency resources \* up to 8 sequences)), extract elements from circular buffers belonging to given repetition window and propagate their properties to statistics. Statistics shall also include entry for number of attempts (number of Uplink-Downlink switching periods with RIM-RS reception request for given setID) during given repetition window. Those statistics shall be updated after each non-empty RIM-RS feedback from L1-UL belonging to given repetition window (see 5G\_L2\_11752\_replaced([11181108](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27RDffvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

After repetition window is closed, 5G-L2-PS shall merge detected (confirmed) and suspected setIDs back to circular buffers according to the rules outlined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13204\_replaced([11163050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Due to 5G-L1-UL possible feedback delay (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14878\_replaced([11514918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR6StfvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), repetition window shall not close earlier than one full repetition window after last symbol of target repetition window. Note: *This means, that 5G-L2-PS needs to maintain at least 2 set of repetition window statistics at once.*

5G-L2-PS shall keep track of additional information provided further to 5G-CP-RT via **[Before 05101473]**[Before CB014133-B] **[End 05101473]**PsCell\_RemoteInterferenceInd **(see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2280\_replaced([10973285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U0Q1Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))****[Before 05101473]**[CB014133-B] PsCell\_RimRsIndication (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_8011\_replaced([10973391](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U035vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[End CB014133-B]**[End 05101473] :***\*******\****\*\*

rimRsDetected (Enum) – if detected (confirmed) setID list is empty it is equal to "0: notDetected", otherwise it is equal to {1: Type1, 2: Type2, 3: Type1AndType2} depending on the detected RS signal types

**[Before 05101473]**

**[Before CB014133-B]**

**[End 05101473]**

 **rimRs1DetectedSetIds (List of U32) containing**[Before 05101473] up to MAX\_SET\_IDS RimRs1 setIDs **[05101473] from 1 to 640 RimRs setIDs (configurable via PsCell\_CellSetupReq.rimRsConfig.rimRsSetidListLength (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2797\_replaced([10970007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgH0fu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**[End 05101473] corresponding to any current entries from detected (confirmed) RimRs1 setID list***\****\*

 **rimRs2DetectedSetIds (List of U32) containing** [Before 05101473] up to MAX\_SET\_IDS RimRs2 setIDs **[05101473] from 1 to 640 RimRs setIDs (configurable via PsCell\_CellSetupReq.rimRsConfig.rimRsSetidListLength (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2797\_replaced([10970007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgH0fu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**[End 05101473] corresponding to any current entries from detected (confirmed) RimRs2 setID list (only when framework1 is activated)***\****\*

**[Before 05101473]**

**[CB014133-B]**

 **rimRsType (Enum) - indicating which Rim RS type is sent in rimRsDetectedSetIds list**

 **rimRsDetectedSetIds (List of U32) containing range from 1 to 640 RimRs setIDs (configurable via PsCell\_CellSetupReq.rimRsConfig.rimRsSetidListLength (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2797\_replaced([10970007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgH0fu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) corresponding to any current entries from detected (confirmed) RimRs1 or RimRs2 setID list (depending on RimRsType)**

**[End CB014133-B]**

**[End 05101473]**

**[Before CB014133-A-UPD003]**

**[Before CB014133-B]**

If any setID has been added to or removed from the detected (confirmed) setID list and it caused modification of *rimRsDetected state or rimRs1DetectedSetIds or rimRs2DetectedSetIds (only when framework1 is activated)* *5G-L2-PS shall initiate sending PsCell\_RemoteInterferenceInd towards 5G-CP-RT with their new values (all other IEs in the message shall also reflect the current status of 5G-L2-PS).*\*\*

**[CB014133-B]**

5G-L2-PS shall initiate sending PsCell\_RimRsIndication towards 5G-CP-RT at the end of RI  trasmission period \* rdRimRsUpdateInterval (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32376\_replaced([11163868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktcdk_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) respectively for each Rim Rs type (f.e. in framework 1 - RIM RS1 set ids update sent at the end of RIM RS1 transmission period, RIM RS2 set ids update sent at the end of RIM RS2 transmission period).

**[End CB014133-B]**

**[CB014133-A-UPD003]**

5G-L2-PS shall initiate sending PsCell\_RimRsIndication towards 5G-CP-RT at the end RI detection window(see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_32376\_replaced([11163868](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktcdk_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) respectively for each Rim Rs type (f.e. in framework1 both types sent on separate messages one after another).

**[End CB014133-A-UPD003]**

If any of the buffers is at its maximum capacity, 5G-L2-PS shall prevent adding any new entries and not override existing ones.

Notes: *Value 640 for number of detected (confirmed) set IDs is the effect of multiplication of maximum number of symbols in Uplink-Downlink switching period (4 + 14 + 14 = 32) with 20 setIDs (per symbol (distance)), however in practice, there is no limitation for number of setIDs remembered per symbol, only in total.*

*Value 25000 for number of suspected set IDs is the effect of attempt to provide more entries than 20792, which is rounded up multiplication of maximum number of supported possible setIDs (4194304) by 0.005, which is the estimated probability of false positive (single) detection of RIM-RS.*

5G-L2-PS shall clean the set ID list with respect to rsDistanceThreshold value after any cell reconfiguration - see 5G\_L2\_20312\_replaced([11142783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23wcfPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for details.

---

### 2.4.25.0-6 Remote Interference Management RIM with UL PRB Pooling (ID: 11175538)

**2.4.25.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11175542`)

Remote Interference Management
If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1UlAllowed is true (5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_4283\_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) the information from PSCell\_CellSetupReq for RIM setting (5G\_L2\_8280\_replaced([11175424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26r0qPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall be used by L2 PS to ensure that the PRB for RI monitoring shall follow the below condition

PRBs required for RI in unscheduled region per subband + PRBs in scheduled region should not exceed the usable PRBs from BBRM - (PUCCH PRB+PRACH+msg Ack/Nack)

The selection of PRB for RI monitoring shall follow the existing functionality as in 5G\_L2\_8282\_replaced([11175546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26tCyPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[PR704924]

When Aperiodic SRS are scheduled in a Slot , during RI monitoring allocation for a subband, it shall be validated on a per symbol level (preferably on each symbol) that UL PRB used on  given symbol (include PUSCH + apSRS + RIM unschedulerd) does not exceed the Usable PRB limit from BBRM.

If the PRB usable limit is exceeded for a symbol (across the slot) the RI monitoring scheduling for the subband is cancelled (RI Monitoring scheduling is skipped for the subband)

[End PR704924]

There is no Demand allocation  for RIM PRB across all subbands .

There shall be missed PRB allocation for RIM detection , if the allocated PRB from BBRM is exceeded .

---

### 2.4.25.0-7 Remote Interference Management Determination of RI measurement resources (ID: 11175546)

**2.4.25.0-7.0-5**  (ID: `11175568`)

Remote Interference Management
This requirement serves as an explanation and elaboration of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9328\_replaced([11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When 5G-L2-PS enters a slot which is marked for RI measurement reception (see 5G\_L2\_8280\_replaced([11175424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26r0qPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), after all other UL allocations are finished, 5G-L2-PS shall decide the resources applicable as follows:

If (5G-L2-PS is currently in RI state == 0 and rimRssiMeasInRiState0 is set to "False"): // (For "noRI" see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9180\_replaced([11161269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktTTqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for rimRssiMeasInRiState0 see 5G\_L2\_IF\_2293\_replaced([10969964](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgHyvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

startSymbol is assigned with the index of the first UL symbol appearing after Guard Period // (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2313\_replaced([11114697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnGefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for TDD slot format)  
 numOfSymbols = 1

Else: // rimRssiMeasInRiState0 is set to "True" or 5G-L2-PS is currently in RI state == 1 ("suspectRI") or RI state == 2 ("RIdetected")

startSymbol is assigned with the index of the first UL symbol in the slot  
 numOfSymbols is assigned with the number of UL symbols within the slot

If periodic SRS are scheduled within the slot and their symbols overlap with symbols of RI measurement, 5G-L2-PS shall:

 increase startPRB of the first subband within the slot by 1,

 decrease numOfPRBs of the last subband within the slot by 1,

 consider all PRBs on periodic SRS symbols as scheduled resources, regardless of the specific UE resources requested for SRS.

5G-L2-PS shall not allocate RI measurements on PUCCH and PRACH. PRBs of those channels shall be excluded from applicable subbands within the slot before proceeding with RI measurement resources allocation.

numOfPrbPerSubBand = 6

numOfSubbands = 0

For each subband [CNI-131193] selected in the current iteration of the loop (up to rdRiMonitorSubband, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29863\_replaced([11163755](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktbPe_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CNI-131193] in the primary subcell:

5G-L2-PS shall traverse in ascending order remaining PRBs of the subband to find possible for RI Measurement allocation with following priorities:

1. Attempt to find numOfPrbPerSubBand consecutive PRBs scheduled on any symbol for PUSCH and on any symbol for apSRS within RIM-RS reception PRB allocation span,

2. Attempt to find numOfPrbPerSubBand consecutive PRBs scheduled on any symbol for PUSCH within RIM-RS reception PRB allocation span,

3. Attempt to find numOfPrbPerSubBand consecutive PRBs scheduled on any symbol for apSRS that were not scheduled for PUSCH on any symbol within RIM-RS reception PRB allocation span,

4. Attempt to find numOfPrbPerSubBand consecutive PRBs scheduled on any symbol for RIM-RS reception that were not scheduled for PUSCH on any symbol,

5. Attempt to find numOfPrbPerSubBand consecutive PRBs scheduled on any symbol for PUSCH and on any symbol for apSRS that were not overlapping with any RIM-RS reception on any symbol,

6. Attempt to find numOfPrbPerSubBand consecutive PRBs scheduled on any symbol for PUSCH that were not overlapping with any RIM-RS reception on any symbol,

1. Attempt to find numOfPrbPerSubBand consecutive PRBs scheduled on any symbol for apSRS that were not overlapping with any RIM-RS and any PUSCH reception on any symbol,

8. Attempt to find numOfPrbPerSubBand consecutive PRBs that did not belong to any PUSCH and any RIM-RS on any symbol.

If such PRB allocation could be found, numOfSubbands shall be increased by 1 and startPrbPerSubBand shall be set to the first PRB of such allocation.

After RI Measurement resources are decided, 5G-L2-PS shall proceed with determination of Unscheduled Regions as per 5G\_L2\_8455\_replaced([11175573](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26tC0vvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.25.0-8 Remote Interference Management Determination of Unscheduled Regions (ID: 11175573)

**2.4.25.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11175583`)

Remote Interference Management
[Before CB006991-C]

This requirement serves as an explanation and elaboration of 5G\_UP\_ALG\_9328\_replaced([11161420](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhwfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If aperiodic SRS is present in the slot, its PRB allocation is calculated for an overlap check as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16799\_replaced([11161550](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. Check whether current slot contains any PUSCH or SRS allocations. If there are such allocations within the slot, proceed with step 2, else:

 create an entry for UnscheduledRegion (startSymbol, numOfSymbols) and increment numOfUnscheduledRegions by one if that entry did not already exist,

 allocate numOfPrbPerSubBand from currentStartPrb as startPrb within PrbRange and increment numOfPrbRange by one,

 increment numOfSubbands by 1,

 finish the procedure.

2. If current slot does not contain any PUSCH allocations (but contains SRS allocation), proceed with step 3, else iterate with currentStartPrb over PRBs of current subband in attempt to find numOfPrbPerSubBand consecutive PRBs allocated to PUSCH, starting from the currentStartSymbol. If no such allocation could be found, proceed with step 2.1, else:

 set startPrbPerSubBand to currentStartPrb where consecutive allocation of numOfPrbPerSubBand started,

 increment numOfSubbands by 1,

 assign symbol length of the PUSCH allocation to puschNumOfSymbols,

 if puschNumOfSymbols == currentNumOfSymbols, finish the procedure, else subtract puschNumOfSymbols from currentNumOfSymbols, increase currentStartSymbol by puschNumOfSymbols and proceed with step 2.2.

2.1 Reassign currentStartPrb with the first PRB of the subband and iterate with currentStartPrb over PRBs of current subband in attempt to find numOfPrbPerSubBand consecutive unscheduled PRBs, starting from the currentStartSymbol. If no such allocation could be found, finish the procedure with no RI measurement scheduled for this subband, else:

 assign symbol length of unscheduled resources before SRS allocation to unscheduledNumOfSymbols,

 create an entry for UnscheduledRegion (currentStartSymbol, unscheduledNumOfSymbols) and increment numOfUnscheduledRegions by one if that entry did not already exist,

 allocate numOfPrbPerSubBand from currentStartPrb as startPrb within PrbRange and increment numOfPrbRange by one,

 increment numOfSubbands by 1,

 if unscheduledNumOfSymbols == currentNumOfSymbols, finish the procedure, else subtract unscheduledNumOfSymbols from currentNumOfSymbols, increase currentStartSymbol by unscheduledNumOfSymbols and proceed with step 2.2.

2.2 If current slot does not contain any SRS allocation, proceed with step 2.4, else:

 assign symbol length of the SRS allocation to srsNumOfSymbols,

 if all numOfPrbPerSubBand PRBs from startPrbPerSubBand are all allocated for SRS, proceed with step 2.3, else (allocation is on the border of aperiodic SRS and unscheduled resources) continue with following steps,

 create an entry for UnscheduledRegion (currentStartSymbol, srsNumOfSymbols) and increment numOfUnscheduledRegions by one if that entry did not already exist,

 cut unallocated SRS PRBs from requested (startPrbPerSubBand, numOfPrbPerSubBand), propagate them to (startPrb, numOfPrb) within PrbRange and increment numOfPrbRange accordingly,

 proceed with step 2.3.

2.3 If srsNumOfSymbols == currentNumOfSymbols, shall finish the procedure, else subtract srsNumOfSymbols from currentNumOfSymbols, increase currentStartSymbol by srsNumOfSymbols and proceed with step 2.4.

2.4 Perform the following:

 create an entry for UnscheduledRegion (currentStartSymbol, currentNumOfSymbols) and increment numOfUnscheduledRegions by one if that entry did not already exist,

 allocate numOfPrbPerSubBand from startPrbPerSubBand as startPrb within PrbRange and increment numOfPrbRange by one,

 finish the procedure.

3. Perform the following:

 assign symbol length of unscheduled resources before SRS allocation to unscheduledNumOfSymbols,

 create an entry for UnscheduledRegion (currentStartSymbol, unscheduledNumOfSymbols) and increment numOfUnscheduledRegions by one if that entry did not already exist,

 allocate numOfPrbPerSubBand from currentStartPrb as startPrb within PrbRange and increment numOfPrbRange by one,

 increment numOfSubbands by 1,

 subtract unscheduledNumOfSymbols from currentNumOfSymbols, increase currentStartSymbol by unscheduledNumOfSymbols and proceed with step 2.2.

[CB006991-C]

If current slot is marked for RI measurement (see 5G\_L2\_8280\_replaced([11175424](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26r0qPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or RIM-RS reception (see 5G\_L2\_11755\_replaced([11175442](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26r0rPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall create a temporary resource grid with all PRBs across all symbols within the slot and propagate following scheduling information:

 all PRBs on all symbols that were scheduled for PUSCH shall be considered scheduled,

 all PRBs on all symbols that were scheduled for aperiodic SRS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16799\_replaced([11161550](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktUhyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for PRB calculation) shall be considered scheduled,

 all PRBs on symbols with periodic SRS shall be considered scheduled,

 all PRBs that are reserved for PUCCH shall be considered blanked on entire slot symbol span,

 all PRBs that are reserved for PRACH on any symbol shall be considered blanked on entire slot symbol span.

Each time RIM-RS and RI measurements are scheduled, their resources shall be propagated on all applicable PRBs and symbols and considered unscheduled without overriding entries previously considered as “scheduled” and “blanked”. If RIM-RS resource takes any symbol on given “half-slot” (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13194\_replaced([11162620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), entire half-slot shall be propagated as unscheduled resource.

After all RIM allocations are finished, each entry in the temporary resource grid considered as “unscheduled” shall be included exactly once in unscheduledRegionInfo (see 5G\_L2\_8302\_replaced([11181069](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27RDdvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in UlData\_RimReceiveReq (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14744\_replaced([11514580](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR4dhPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_8295\_replaced([11180991](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27QcaPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Consecutive unscheduled entries (same PRBs for consecutive number of symbols) shall be bundled, firstly in time domain, secondly in frequency domain as follows:

unscheduledRegionInfo consists of numOfUnscheduledRegions entries unscheduledRegion recognized by (startSymbol, numOfSymbols).

unscheduledRegion consists of numOfPrbRange entires PrbRange which are recognized by (startPrb, numOfPrb).

Each PRB span considered as “blanked” is further reported as Blanked Region (see 5G\_L2\_11748\_replaced([11181030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27RDcPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[PR697859] If numOfPrbRange exceeds the allowed range (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14744\_replaced([11514580](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR4dhPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall stop further processing or RI Measurements and RIM-RS within the slot and prevent sending of UlData\_RimReceiveReq for the target slot towards 5G-L1-UL (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16851\_replaced([11162029](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End PR697859]

After all allocations of RIM-RS and all allocations of RI Measurements on all subbands are finished and their respective unscheduledRegions determined, if numOfSubbands > 0 or any RIM-RS detection is expected, 5G-L2-PS shall proceed with sending the request towards 5G-L1-UL for RI measurement via UlData\_RimReceiveReq (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_14744\_replaced([11514580](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR4dhPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_8295\_replaced([11180991](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27QcaPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End CB006991-C]

---

### 2.4.25.0-9 Remote Interference Management PM Counters Support (ID: 11175588)

**2.4.25.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11175597`)

Remote Interference Management
When scheduling reception of new detections of RIM-RS within a slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13188\_replaced([11162108](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktW-D_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall:

 Update counter M55308C22008 (RIM\_RS\_NEW\_DET\_EXP) with the number of units belonging to all resources within the slot that were marked as “ongoing”,

 Update counter M55308C22009 (RIM\_RS\_NEW\_DET\_DROPPED) with the number of units belonging to all “ongoing” resources that were dropped and not scheduled for reception within the slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13188\_replaced([11162108](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktW-D_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16851\_replaced([11162029](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Note: This includes sequences within RSDetectElements that did not fit remaining L1Capacity.

When scheduling reception of revalidations of RIM-RS within a slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15475\_replaced([11162234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), 5G-L2-PS shall:

 Update counter M55308C22010 (RIM\_RS\_SETID\_REVAL\_EXP) with the number of units belonging to all revalidations of the detected (and suspected) setIDs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13204\_replaced([11163050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) within the slot. Only UL symbol ranges for revalidations belonging to the current slot shall be counted (as requested for reception), not their entire respective RSDetectElements,

 Update counter M55308C22011 (RIM\_RS\_SETID\_REVAL\_DROPPED) with the number of units belonging to all revalidations of the detected (and suspected) setIDs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13204\_replaced([11163050](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktYMLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) that were dropped and not scheduled for reception within the slot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15475\_replaced([11162234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16851\_replaced([11162029](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktWW_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Only UL symbol ranges for revalidations belonging to the current slot shall be counted (as requested for reception), not their entire respective RSDetectElements.

[CB009823-B] All above counters shall be incremented irrespective of the RIM-RS type. [End CB009823-B]

Unit for all above counters is RIM-RS capacity (detection) unit (see 5G\_UP\_5448\_replaced([11399455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ugBFsfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). One unit counts as one RIM-RS sequence (per one entry in [Before CB009823-A] rimRsConfig.rsType1NScId[] [CB009823-A]  rimRsConfig.rimRsTypeConfigList.rsNScId[]  [End CB009823-A], see 5G\_L2\_9430\_replaced([11142183](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23syG_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_IF\_2797\_replaced([10970007](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UgH0fu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) over one frequency resource (candidate, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13194\_replaced([11162620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), per one entry in rimRsConfig.startRbForRs[]) on one symbol.

---

## 2.4.26 Uplink Configured Grant Type 2

### 2.4.26.0-2 Uplink Configured Grant Type 2 Illustrations of this chapter are store under: https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/26%20Uplink%20Configured%20Grant%20Type%202?csf=1&amp;web=1&amp;e=qSXVOD (ID: 11175609)

### 2.4.26.1 CG overview

#### 2.4.26.1.0-1 CG overview RRC CG UE configuration (type 1 or 2) (ID: 11175620)

**2.4.26.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175626`)

CG overview
An initial CG PUSCH (Configured Grant) transmission as opposed to DG (Dynamic Grant) PUSCH transmission is a DCI  less PUSCH transmission on semi-persistent allocated resources in a so called CG slots. Although the retransmission of a CG PUSCH requires an UL DCI (Dynamic Grant), this document names it “CG PUSCH retransmission” for convenience to differentiate it from the retransmission of a DG PUSCH.

A UE is configured by RRC with the following parameters to prepare a semi-persistent scheduling (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14592\_replaced([11172878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more information):

* a CS-RNTI (in addition to the C-RNTI):   
  This specific value ensures that 5G-L1-UL cannot mistake a CG PUSCH for a standard DG PUSCH and that any associated PDCCH is unambiguously identified.

For PDCCH signaling associated to CS-RNTI, the C-RNTI is still used for PDCCH search space determination (5G\_UP\_ALG\_4036\_replaced([11168496](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktwmofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and PDCCH encoding (dciScramblingRnti field in DlData\_PdcchSendReq). Only the DCI CRC bits are scrambled with the CS-RNTI (rnti field).

* periodicity:  
  The number of slots between two consecutive CG slots associated to different HARQ Ids.  
  Note: In TDD, CP-RT ensures that forwarded value is greater than the minimum possible UL slot periodicity (refer to 5G\_UP\_5893\_replaced([11348488](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubWBFPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_8431\_replaced([11324329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TsmnRfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The association between a CG slot (SFN, slot) and its HARQ Id for an initial CG PUSCH transmission is implicit as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14922\_replaced([11202350](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwcuR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* nrofHARQ-Processes:

CG PUSCH (re)transmission shall use an HARQ Id in range [0 .. nrofHARQ-Processes-1].

In our implementation, those processes are reserved for CG PUSCH (i.e. cannot be used for an initial DG PUSCH transmission) as soon as CG has been configured.

* configuredGrantTimer per HARQ Id (Id lower than nrofHARQ-Processes):

A UE in CG active state starts or restarts this timer respectively on any PUSCH transmission or retransmission related to this HARQ Id (reserved for CG PUSCH in our implementation).

On the next CG slot occasion related to the same HARQ Id, if the timer is expired and in absence of any DCI requesting either a PUSCH transmission or a PUSCH retransmission in this slot (i.e. no overwriting of the CG slot for another purpose), the UE implicitly assumes that the gNB has correctly decoded the PUSCH and may transmits a new initial CG PUSCH. In our implementation, this timer is used to block subsequent init CG PUSCH transmission when previous CG PUSCH retransmission related to this HARQ id is too close to the next CG slot occasion to let a chance for L2-PS to ask for a subsequent retransmission if necessary (scheduling of CG PUSCH retransmission in less than configuredGrantTimer is nonetheless not guaranteed and risk of HARQ failure still exists, refer to chronogram in 5G\_L2\_1130\_replaced([11185035](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27u9j_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Note that on 5G-L2-PS this is not a timer but just a check on a duration.

In following sections, a CG slot related to HARQ Id X whose configuredGrantTimer is running is called a blocked CG slot. 5G-L2-PS handle it as a non-CG slot and therefore the associated periodic CG RBs resources are considered as free for any other UE (on 5G-L2-PS perspective, configuredGrantTimer is not a timer but just used a check on the duration since the last CG PUSCH retransmission of this HARQ Id).

The configuredGrantTimer (expressed as a multiple of periodicity) is computed by 5G-L2-PS at cell setup according to the minimum UL HARQ round trip delay (a little more than 6 slots in the example below assuming that the slot 0 is a CG slot, refer to the calculation in 5G\_L2\_9075\_replaced([11175685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26uQ4fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and depends on CG periodicity. UE configuredGrantTimer and nrofHARQ-Processes are sent to CP-RT in PsUser\_UserModifyResp or PsUser\_UserSetupResp:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a concept related to wireless communication, specifically concerning grant timers and HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat reQuest) feedback. The diagram is structured as a table with "Periodicity" on the left and a timeline represented by "Slots" from 0 to 19, and then wrapping around to 0, 1, 2. Above the timeline, "SFN" (System Frame Number) and "N" are indicated, suggesting a cyclical or frame-based system.
The rows under "Periodicity" are: 20 FDD, 20TDD, 10FDD, 10TDD, 5FDD, 5TDD, and 2FDD. Each row depicts a series of time slots, some of which are colored black, indicating transmission or activity, and others are white, indicating idle slots. Yellow boxes with numbers (0, 1, 2, 3, 4) are placed within some slots, representing specific HARQ IDs or grant timer values.
A blue bar spans across the top of the timeline, labeled "configuredGrantTimer of initial CG PUSH TX with HARQ Id 0". This likely represents the duration or scope of a configured grant timer for an initial CG (Carrier Grant) PUSH transmission with HARQ ID 0.
A dashed green vertical line is shown intersecting slot 3, with an arrow pointing to the right and the text "Earliest possible CRC feedback for HARQ Id 0". Below this, another dashed green line with arrows indicates "Minimum HARQ round trip delay".
A yellow box with an "X" is placed at the bottom, with the text "CG slot matching with HARQ Id 'X'". This serves as a legend, explaining that 'X' in a yellow box signifies a CG slot matching with a particular HARQ ID.
The table can be represented as follows:

#### 2.4.26.1.0-2 CG overview CG PUSCH scheduling and overwriting (ID: 11175631)

**2.4.26.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175638`)

CG overview
Basic scheduling without CG PUSCH retransmissions

The absence of DCI to schedule the initial CG PUSCH in CG slot induces the following:

* The UE implicitly toggles the NDI when it considers that a new CG PUSCH can be transmitted (refer to configuredGrantTimer description in 5G\_L2\_10781\_replaced([11175620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26tp0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and diagram below). Besides the NDI value signaled in DCI associated to CS-RNTI is used for another purpose (i.e. as a discriminator, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14809\_replaced([11172924](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPHyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), so internal NDI value is not updated for CG PUSCH.
* The advantage compared to Proactive scheduling is that the UE can transmit an initial CG PUSCH [CB008124-HA] during the DRX Inactivity period and  [End CB008124-HA] without consuming any PDCCH resource.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the behavior of a User Equipment (UE) in a cellular network, specifically focusing on its transmission of PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) data under different DRX (Discontinuous Reception) states and grant types. The diagram spans across multiple SFN (System Frame Number) and slot numbers, indicating a timeline of events.
The top section of the diagram is divided into two main regions: "DRX INACTIVE" and "DRX ACTIVE".
Under "DRX INACTIVE", it states "CG PUSCG TX independent of DRX cycle (unlike proactive sched)". This implies that when the UE is in DRX inactive mode, its Contention-based (CG) PUSCH transmissions are not directly tied to the DRX cycle and might follow a different scheduling logic.
Under "DRX ACTIVE", it states "Only a DRX active UE can receive a DCI for initial DG PUSCH". This indicates that when the UE is in DRX active mode, it can receive a Downlink Control Information (DCI) for a Dynamic Grant (DG) PUSCH transmission.
The diagram then presents several rows representing different views or types of transmissions:
1. **PDCCH (UL DCI) / PUSCH**: This row shows the scheduling of PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) which carries UL DCI, and the corresponding PUSCH transmissions. Black bars represent PUSCH transmissions, white bars represent no PUSCH transmission, and gray bars represent CG slots. Yellow boxes with numbers indicate specific HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) IDs. For example, a yellow '1' in slot 17 of SFN 0 indicates a PUSCH transmission with HARQ ID 1.
2. **CG PUSCH Harqld 0 (UE view)**: This row shows the UE's perspective on Contention-based PUSCH transmissions with HARQ ID 0. It depicts events like "ConfiguredGrantTimer0" and "At timer expiry UE doesn't free TB 0a".
3. **L2-PS view**: This row likely represents the perspective of Layer 2 (L2) and Packet Switched (PS) components, showing "CRC OK" and "NDI toggling" (New Data Indicator toggling).
4. **CG PUSCH Harqld 1 (UE view)**: Similar to the HARQ ID 0 view, this row shows the UE's perspective on Contention-based PUSCH transmissions with HARQ ID 1. It includes events like "ConfiguredGrantTimer1", "At timer expiry UE doesn't free TB 1a", and "UE frees TB 1a".
5. **L2-PS view**: This row, corresponding to HARQ ID 1, shows "PuschReceiveReq", "PuschReceiveRespHarqU (CRC OK)", and "processing of previous TB" and "processing of new TB". It also indicates "TX skipped as no data to send" and "DTX" (Discontinuous Transmission).
6. **DG PUSCH HarqiD >= 2**: This row indicates Dynamic Grant PUSCH transmissions with HARQ ID greater than or equal to 2. It shows a "DG 2a" transmission and "CRC OK".
Arrows in the diagram indicate the flow of events or dependencies. For instance, an arrow from "NDI toggling" points to "UE frees TB 0a", and another arrow from "NDI toggling (no DCI for reTX, timer1 not running)" points to "UE frees TB 1a".
The timeline is marked with SFNs (0, 1, 2) and slots within each SFN (0 to 19). Different colored backgrounds highlight the DRX INACTIVE and DRX ACTIVE periods.
Key events and states depicted include:
\* PDCCH/PUSCH scheduling with specific HARQ IDs.
\* Contention-based (CG) and Dynamic Grant (DG) PUSCH transmissions.
\* Timer expirations related to grants.
\* UE freeing Transmission Buffers (TB).
\* NDI toggling.
\* CRC (Cyclic Redundancy Check) status (OK).
\* Transmission skipping due to no data.
\* DTX (Discontinuous Transmission).
\* UE receiving new data.
\* UE having no more data to send.
\* Processing of previous and new Transmission Buffers.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5177.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Stored in "Scheduling chronograms.xlsx" under <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/26%20Uplink%20Configured%20Grant%20Type%202/Scheduling%20Chronograms.xlsx?d=wa6da18476c0d4cd98315c0175835da24&csf=1&web=1&e=C1uGZM>

Initial CG PUSCH transmission

C-Plane considers UL TX skipping as a mandatory UE capability for configuring a UE with CG. Therefore, a CG activated UE that has no data to send will not transmit any CG PUSCH and 5G-L1-UL will report a DTX indication to 5G-L2-PS.

CG PUSCH retransmission:

Such type of retransmission can take place in any CG or non-CG (including blocked CG slot) slots.

Note: CG PUSCH repetition (without previous CG PUSCH decoding feedback) is currently not supported (repK=1 in RRC ConfiguredGrantConfig configuration).

CG slot overwriting

A CG slot occasion may be overwritten (i.e. CG PUSCH replaced with a Dynamic Grant PUSCH) for the following reasons:

- To schedule a CG PUSCH retransmission on granted CG RBs whatever the HARQ Id of the CG slot in case it was not possible to schedule it earlier on a non-CG slot.

[CB008124-CB] Depending on rdEnableCGTrafficOverwriting, see 5G\_L2\_11589\_replaced([11188427](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28ImIfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

- To schedule a pending DG PUSCH retransmission.

[CB008124-CB-UPD001]

- To schedule an initial DG PUSCH with MAC-CE “Configured Grant Confirmation” for CG Deactivation confirmation when the UE may not be able to transmit a CG PUSCH anymore after a CG Deconfiguration with periodicity is equal to 1 slot in FDD (or UL slot periodicity in FR1-TDD) or a CG Activation or Modification failure (refer to 5G\_L2\_11263\_replaced([11175947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGGPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End CB008124-CB-UPD001]

- To schedule an initial DG PUSCH when UE traffic exceeds CG TBS (computed based on number of CG RBs to fit grantedPacketSize SDU packet according to CG Link Adaptation, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14664\_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB008124-D-UPD002] or when CG RB allocation is not able to accomodate multiplexing of UCI report with data (refer to 5G\_L2\_11066\_replaced([11176279](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiYfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008124-D-UPD002]. This overwriting capability is required to reach the peak throughput.

[End CB008124-CB]

---

#### 2.4.26.1.0-3 CG overview CG type 2 and associated Activation / Deactivation procedures (ID: 11175643)

**2.4.26.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175648`)

CG overview
Only CG type 2 is supported by L2-PS meaning that CG PUSCH resources in Time and Frequency domain are not signaled in RRC configuration and requires additional DCI signaling described below.

CG PUSCH Activation

A DCI 0 1 whose CRC is scrambled with CS-RNTI and compliant with 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14810\_replaced([11172938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPHy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) format is used to provide the UE with semi-persistent CG RB allocation (implicit CG PUSCH slot position ‘X’ with respect to K2, FDRA and TDRA) and activate the CG type 2.

Scheduling this CG activation in a candidate slot ‘X’ requires free identical RB resources from CG resource pool (refer to cgAllocationTable in 5G\_L2\_10925\_replaced([11176576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-lfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) on all associated CG slots ‘Y’ ≡ ‘X’ (mod periodicity).

The same DCI is used to schedule any CG re-activation on smaller or larger number of CG RB resources due to Link Adaptation. This procedure is later called CG Modification (without MAC prefix as CG Modification at RRC level is currently not supported).

Although UE may not receive the DCI, 5G-L2-PS shall consider the CG allocated RB as busy from DCI emission until confirmation of CG Deactivation, CG Modification, CG Deconfiguration completion or UE release. Therefore, UE is in a “firing state” and initial CG PUSCH decoding attempt shall be scheduled in every CG slot opportunity.

UE confirms the CG activation by sending a MAC-CE “Configured grant Confirmation”.

Please refer to CG Activation (5G\_L2\_11018\_replaced([11175737](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26uQ7fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or CG Modification (5G\_L2\_11017\_replaced([11175827](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfAfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) procedures for more information on procedure initiation, scheduling or failure cases.

CG Deactivation

A DCI 0 0 whose CRC is scrambled with CS-RNTI and compliant with 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14812\_replaced([11172948](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPHzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) format is used to Deactivate the CG type 2.

UE confirms the CG Deactivation by sending a MAC-CE “Configured grant Confirmation”.  
Notes:  
- There is no way to differentiate a CG (re)activation confirmation from a CG Deactivation confirmation, but overlapping of both procedures needs to be addressed.  
- An already deactivated UE is supposed to send a MAC-CE as well.

UE is deactivated (and semi-persistent allocation released) at the end of the slot containing the MAC-CE and pending CG retransmission needs to be flushed as no subsequent retransmission is possible.

If K2 slots later than DCI slot matches a CG slot occasion (non-blocked CG slot), 5G-L2-PS can rely on this CG PUSCH to transport the MAC-CE provided that the previous CG (re)Activation procedure has been confirmed and this is the first deactivation attempt (i.e. UE is still activated on expected CG RB resources). As a drawback compared to DG PUSCH, the associated HARQ process cannot be retransmitted if CRC is KO.

Otherwise, 5G-L2-PS needs to schedule a DG PUSCH for UE to transmit the MAC-CE. Note that the UE may still be activated and send it earlier than this DG PUSCH.

In short, 5G-L2-PS cannot make any assumption on the slot containing the MAC-CE and just relies on the isConfiguredGrantConfCe field of the LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd indication from L2-LO.

Please refer to CG Deactivation procedure (5G\_L2\_10374\_replaced([11175923](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGE_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for more information on procedure initiation, scheduling or failure cases.

Impact on DL scheduling:

In transient phases where UE CG state may not be consistent with expected state in 5G-L2-PS, the UE may still transmit UCI (CSI report or DL HARQ feedback) on PUCCH whereas 5G-L1-UL is decoding CG PUSCH  or conversely (refer respectively to chronograms in 5G\_L2\_11196\_replaced([11175805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u3_fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_11239\_replaced([11175886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfDfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as an example).

Therefore, the absence of double decoding capability in 5G-L1-UL may impact the DL traffic during these transitions.

---

#### 2.4.26.1.0-4 CG overview UE buffer management and Multi-DRB CG configuration (ID: 11175651)

**2.4.26.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175657`)

CG overview
Although 3GPP Release 16 allows several CG configurations per UE with an association to Logical channels, this is not used in current implementation. Thus C-Planes configures a single CG configuration per UE (typically the CG periodicity) according to the DRB used as the DRB driver (modification of CG configuration is currently not supported).

It means that a UE can transmit data of any bearer (including SRB) over the CG PUSCH.

Consequently, CG activation decision in 5G-L2-PS considers the Data rate of all bearers (i.e. not only the DRB driver one), refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15204\_replaced([11173172](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuRkA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008124-CB]

However, when checking whether DG scheduling in non-CG slot can be avoided, meaning that UE buffer can be drained in next CG slot occasion (with respect to CG RBs allocated based on configured grantedPacketSize), only the UE buffer related to the same LCG group as the DRB driver (GBR or non-GBR) is considered (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5121\_replaced([11203701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End CB008124-CB]

---

#### 2.4.26.1.0-5 CG overview UE CG state machine overview (ID: 11175661)

**2.4.26.1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11175664`)

CG overview
Only CG-WaitForActivationConfirm state is added compared to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15099\_replaced([11172972](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)):

• CG-Init:

Non-CG UE configuration

• CG-Configured:

CG is configured and UL scheduler has received the CS-RNTI allocation from the DL scheduler

Evaluation of CG Activation is started.

• CG-WaitForActivation:  
Wait for an opportunity to schedule the DCI for the CG Activation

• CG-WaitForActivationConfirm:  
Once the DCI for CG Activation has been scheduled (and the UE has possibly started sending CG PUSCH), wait for the MAC-CE “Configured grant Confirmation”

• CG-Activated:

UE is activated and synchronized on the allocated CG slot occasions and the CG RB resources after a CG activation or Modification

[CB008124-CA]

• CG-WaitForModification:   
Wait for an opportunity to schedule the DCI for the CG re-Activation

• CG-WaitForModificationConfirm:

Once the re-activation DCI has been scheduled, wait for the MAC-CE “Configured grant Confirmation”  
[End CB008124-CA]

• CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder,

Wait for an opportunity to schedule the DCI for the CG Deactivation

• CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling:  
Once the DCI for CG Deactivation has been sent, if required, wait for an opportunity to schedule the MAC-CE “Configured grant Confirmation”

• CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm:  
Wait for the MAC-CE “Configured grant Confirmation”

• CG-Error:

Used in case the maximum number of CG activation or CG Deactivation attempts has been reached

---

### 2.4.26.2 CG configuration procedure

#### 2.4.26.2.0-1 CG configuration procedure Minimum remaining PRBs in UL initial BWP (ID: 11175674)

**2.4.26.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12634874`)

CG configuration procedure
At cell setup [CB013388-C] or cell reconfiguration [End CB013388-C], 5G-L2-PS shall validate the remaining consecutive PRBs in initial UL BWP. Corresponding CP2 requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14782\_replaced([11172745](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuN5qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

If rdMinNumPrbsPusch (see 5G\_L2\_1195\_replaced([11185095](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27vkoPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))) is 0, remaining consecutive PRBs in UL initial BWP should be more than or equal to 3 after removal of already occupied PRBs. Otherwise, remaining PRBs shall be more than or equal to rdMinNumPrbsPusch. Already occupied PRBs in initial UL BWP are as follows:

· The blanked RBs, if PRB blanking feature is activated

· The dedicated PUCCH, if long PUCCH feature is activated

· The common PUCCH for SA or SA + NSA cell.

· The PRACH, if PUSCH multiplexing in PRACH slot is activated

· The Uplink Configured Grant RBs, if Uplink Configured Grant Type 2 feature activated.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the configuration of Uplink (UL) Bandwidth Parts (BWPs) in a cellular network. It depicts how different types of physical channels, such as PUCCH (Physical Uplink Control Channel) and PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel), are allocated within the available bandwidth.
The diagram shows a vertical representation of the UL bandwidth, divided into several segments. Each segment is labeled with its type and sometimes with specific parameters or examples.
Here's a breakdown of the elements:
**Vertical Axis (representing UL Bandwidth):**
\* **Blanked PRBs High Edge:** This indicates a region at the top of the bandwidth that is not used.
\* **Dedicated PUCCH:** A segment allocated for dedicated PUCCH resources.
\* **ulCgMaxNumPrb (Ex 3):** Represents the maximum number of Resource Blocks (RBs) for a Control Resource Set (CoReSet) in Example 3.
\* **Initial UL BWP (Ex 1):** Denotes the first Uplink Bandwidth Part configuration in Example 1.
\* **Common PUCCH NumPrbCommonPucch:** A segment for common PUCCH, with a specified number of RBs.
\* **StartCrbCommonPucchHighPart:** The starting Carrier Resource Block (CRB) for the high part of the common PUCCH.
\* **ulCgMaxNumPrb (Ex 2):** Represents the maximum number of RBs for a CoReSet in Example 2.
\* **Initial UL BWP (Ex 2):** Denotes the second Uplink Bandwidth Part configuration in Example 2.
\* **ulCgPrbStartOffset:** An offset for the starting CRB of a CoReSet.
\* **bandwidth:** This label indicates the total bandwidth.
\* **Prach Size (if PRACH mux with pusch):** A segment for PRACH (Physical Random Access Channel) size, specifically when PRACH is multiplexed with PUSCH.
\* **prach PRB Offset:** An offset for the PRACH PRBs.
\* **Common PUCCH NumPrbCommonPucch:** Another segment for common PUCCH.
\* **StartCrbCommonPucchLowPart:** The starting CRB for the low part of the common PUCCH.
\* **location:** A general indicator of position within the bandwidth.
\* **ulCgMaxNumPrb (Ex 1):** Represents the maximum number of RBs for a CoReSet in Example 1.
\* **longPucchStartPuschPrb:** The starting PRB for a long PUCCH that is also a PUSCH.
\* **Dedicated PUCCH:** Another segment for dedicated PUCCH.
\* **location:** Another general indicator of position.
\* **Blanked PRBs Low Edge:** This indicates a region at the bottom of the bandwidth that is not used.
**Horizontal Arrows and Labels:**
\* **bandwidth:** Indicates the total span of the bandwidth.
\* **longPucchNumPuschPrb:** Represents the number of PRBs for a long PUCCH that is also a PUSCH.
**Dashed Lines:**
Dashed lines are used to connect different parts of the diagram, showing relationships between parameters and segments, and indicating the extent of the Initial UL BWPs.
**Notes:**
At the bottom of the image, there are two important notes:
1. **NOTE: Only one CG config available per cell (BLUE)**: This highlights that there is a single configuration for Control Group (CG) available per cell, and this configuration is represented in blue.
2. **Need to check whether rdMinNumPrbsPusch Or 3 consecutive PRBs remained in initial BWP after removal of all occupied PRBs (White)**: This is a crucial check to be performed. It suggests that after removing all occupied PRBs, one needs to verify if a minimum number of RBs for PUSCH (rdMinNumPrbsPusch) or at least 3 consecutive RBs remain in the initial BWP. The "White" likely refers to the color of the segments representing occupied PRBs.
The diagram uses different colors to distinguish between various channel types and configurations:
\* **White:** Likely represents blanked or unused PRBs.
\* **Yellow:** Represents Dedicated PUCCH.
\* **Blue:** Represents ulCgMaxNumPrb and Initial UL BWP configurations.
\* **Orange:** Represents Common PUCCH.
\* **Green:** Represents PRACH Size.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5186.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Depending upon the configuration, 5G-L2-PS shall check for the required number of consecutive PRBs (white space) in initial UL BWP.

If there are no sufficient PRBs remained, 5G-L2-PS shall reply according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14786\_replaced([11172769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuN5rfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) in cell setup resp [CB013388-C] or cell reconfiguration response [End CB013388-C] message.

---

#### 2.4.26.2.0-2 CG configuration procedure ConfiguredGrantTimer and NrOfHarqProcesses (ID: 11175685)

**2.4.26.2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `13388122`)

CG configuration procedure
ConfiguredGrantTimer and nrOfHARQProcesses are computed by L2-PS according to req **5G\_UP\_ALG\_14592\_replaced([11172878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**

**ConfiguredGrantTimer is computed  at CellSetup for every possible CG periodicity received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_390\_replaced([10974651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and stored at a cell level in a table indexed by CG periodicity**

**ConfiguredGrantTimer is expressed in number of CG periodicities.**

**ConfiguredGrantTimer(CG\_Periodicity\_slot) = roundup(**harqRTTDelay\_us/(CG\_**periodicity\_slot \*** slotDuration\_us))***\****\*

Where

 **slotDuration\_us is the slot duration expressed in us.**

 CG\_Periodicity\_slot is the Configured Grant periodicity expressed in slots. Supported values are : 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 40, 80, 160. The correct periodicity will be selected by CP-RT depending upon cellTechnology FDD/TDD (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_8431\_replaced([11324329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TsmnRfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 **harqRTTDelay\_us  =** ULAdvanceTime\_us + maxK2\_us + PuschCRCMaxReceptionTime\_us+ BufferedUlDelay\_us\*\*\*\*

 **ULAdvanceTime\_us UL scheduler advance time to the starting boundary of the same DL slot number on the air interface defined**

**ULAdvanceTime\_us = OneWayHwDelayDl , received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq**

 **MaxK2\_us : Maximum k2 configured for PUSCH  (excluding the k2 for msg3 scheduled from RAR and k2aCsi computed for A-CSI scheduling in TDD) \* slotDuration\_us**

 **PuschCRCMaxReceptionTime\_us : Maximum time for CRC reception for PUSCH from 5G-L1-UL relative to the starting boundary of the same DL slot on the air interface, calculated according to** 5G\_L1\_IF\_283\_replaced([11514791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR5rpPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for **UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU.**\*\*\*\*

**PuschCRCMaxReceptionTime\_us  = (**D - **NTAOffset us +**T\_AntToBB1 us)***\****\*

 For each technology we use the worse D timing value between Loner and Loki/Thor [CB014419-SR-EB] /Odin [End CB014419-SR-EB] for message UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU. Requirements providing those timings are given in req  **5G\_L1\_IF\_283\_replaced([11514791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR5rpPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) .**

 **NTAOffset is defined in** 5G\_L1\_IF\_282\_replaced([11514759](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR5roPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))\*\*\*\*

 **T\_AntToBB1 is defined in** 5G\_L1\_21A\_121\*\*\*\*

 **BufferedUlDelay\_us : Additional 3 slots delay in TDD when buffered UL is enabled**

 **For FDD : BufferedUlDelay\_us = 0**

 **For TDD : BufferedUlDelay\_us = 1500 if  bufferedUlAllowed == TRUE or if rdBufferedUlMode == 2 (Test)**

bufferedUlAllowed parameter is received in message PsCnfg\_AddressDistributionReq

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating timing relationships in a TDD FR1 eCPRI system. It depicts the timing of Downlink (DL) and Uplink (UL) Layer 1 (L1) signals, as well as Downlink and Uplink scheduling. Several delay parameters are also indicated.
The diagram is structured into four main rows:
1. **DL L1 time**: This row shows the eCPRI signal and subsequent data slots labeled N-1 through N+5. It also indicates "Antenna + HW delay -NTA" and "Antenna - HW delay".
2. **UL L1 time**: This row shows the eCPRI signal and subsequent data slots labeled N-1 through N+7. It also indicates "Antenna" and "OneWayHWDelayDI", "Max K2", and "D = Max Reception time for UlData\_ReceiveRespHargU". A label "Buffered UL delay" and "rounding" are also present.
3. **DL Sched**: This row shows scheduled slots labeled N+1 through N+11.
4. **UL Sched**: This row shows scheduled slots labeled N+1 through N+11.
Additionally, there are several delay annotations:
\* **Antenna - HW delay**: A horizontal arrow indicating a delay.
\* **Antenna + HW delay -NTA**: A horizontal arrow indicating a delay.
\* **OneWayHWDelayDI**: A horizontal arrow indicating a delay.
\* **Max K2**: A label associated with a delay.
\* **D = Max Reception time for UlData\_ReceiveRespHargU**: A horizontal arrow indicating a delay.
\* **Buffered UL delay**: A horizontal arrow indicating a delay.
\* **rounding**: A double-headed arrow indicating a delay.
\* **T\_AntToBB1 - NTaOffset**: A horizontal arrow indicating a delay.
\* **HarqRTTDelay**: A blue horizontal arrow spanning a significant portion of the diagram, indicating a round-trip delay for HARQ.
The diagram uses a grid background to represent time slots. The slots are labeled sequentially with "N" and offsets like "N+1", "N+2", etc. Green colored blocks represent DL data, and gray colored blocks represent UL data.

**nrOfHARQProcesses  =** ConfiguredGrantTimer +1\*\*\*\*

When PsUser\_UserModifyResp with parameters (csRnti, configuredGrantTimer, **nrOfHarqProcesses) as 5G\_L2\_IF\_458\_replaced([10976517](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is OK, the range for harqProcesses index will be split in two Harq processes ranges, CG (configured grant) and DG (dynamic grant).**

---

#### 2.4.26.2.0-3 CG configuration procedure HarqSlotType and HarqId Range (ID: 11175697)

**2.4.26.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175702`)

CG configuration procedure
HarqSlotType: it is an attribute of the FD data which can have 2 values:

- cgSlot: when it is a slot where there is a cgReservation (startPrb and numOfPrbs) of the resources for CG activation or CG scheduling or CG retransmission

- standard: it is the default value and it is set when there is a CG deactivation which passes through the state CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling  or a retransmission of a CG UE which does not have cgReservation (standard PRB allocation is used in this case)

CG harq process index: range [0 ...nrOfHarqProcesses - 1]. This CG harq range is used when UE CG is on CG firing state 5G\_L2\_10291\_replaced([11176085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26xUM_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and harqSlotType = cgSlot. There are some particular cases where this range is not used, such as:

* CG-overwriting for DG
* Timeout of cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer during CG deactivation process

DG harq process index: range [nrOfHarqProcesses ... maxNumOfHarqProcesses - 1]. This DG harq range is used when harqSlotType = standard

---

### 2.4.26.3 CG deconfiguration procedure

#### 2.4.26.3.0-1 CG deconfiguration procedure CG Deconfiguration (ID: 11175709)

**2.4.26.3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12713072`)

CG deconfiguration procedure
The CG deconfiguration consists of receiving of the messages PsUser\_UserModifyReq and PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq with CG deconfiguration.

At the reception of PsUser\_UserModifyReq with CG deconfiguration:

[Before PR837907] , there is nothing to do (L2PS stays in its current state and continues the associated actions).

[PR837907] **UL:**

If the current state is different from CgError and CgConfigured, the processing of the PsUser\_UserModifyReq is postponed:

* A pending CG activation or Modification is canceled if the DCI has not been sent yet.
* if the CG state machine is not in a waiting state, the CG Deactivation is initiated.

Otherwise, the CG Deactivation is only initiated on the next transition to a stable state different from CgConfigured and CgError.

* When the CG has been successfully or unsuccessfully deactivated (i.e. CG state is equal to CgConfigured or CgError), the PsUser\_UserModifyReq is processed.

Otherwise, there is nothing to do (L2PS stays in its current state and continues the associated actions).

[End PR837907]

At the reception of PsUser\_RrcReconfCompletedReq with CG deconfiguration**, the actions for CG Configured Ue are:**

**UL:**

* stop all CG timers.
* update total Number Of Actived Cg Ues counter.

-**If a CG deactivation procedure was ongoing (UE in CGWaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling or in CGWaitForDeactivationConfirm state) counter  UL\_CG\_DEACT\_ATT\_SUCC M55308C00023 shall be incremented**

* Cancel all pending CG ReTx for this UE and free all CG HARQ Processes Id related to pending CG ReTx
* Use all harq process Ids from 0 to 15 for DG.
* Release CG PRBs (refer to 5G\_L2\_11173\_replaced([11176568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* Remove UE from CG allocation table.
* Delete associated state machine for this Ue.
* Remove CS-RNTI from indexer of ue database

-  "Proactive Grant Type" is set to initial value and start procact Timer

* Reset CS-RNTI to invalid (0) in ue CG database

**DL:**

* Release CS-RNTI from rnti pool.
* Remove CS-RNTI from indexer of ue database
* Reset CS-RNTI to invalid (0) in ue CG database

---

#### 2.4.26.3.0-2 CG deconfiguration procedure CG Deconfiguration Cancelation (ID: 11175718)

**2.4.26.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175720`)

CG deconfiguration procedure
The CG deconfiguration cancelation consists of receiving of the message PsUser\_UserModifyReq with ulCgConfig = 0 (CG deconfiguration) and receiving of the message PsUser\_UserModifyReq with ulCgConfig = 3 (CG deconfiguration cancelation).

At the reception of PsUser\_UserModifyReq with CG deconfiguration, the CG deconfiguration flag will be stored in the CG Ue context.

At the reception of PsUser\_UserModifyReq with CG deconfiguration cancellation, the cg deconfiguration flag will be cleared in CG Ue context, and CG Ue continues the associated actions.

---

### 2.4.26.4 CG UE release procedure

#### 2.4.26.4.0-1 CG UE release procedure CG UE release (ID: 11175729)

**2.4.26.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175733`)

CG UE release procedure
[PR697326]

At the reception of PsUser\_UserStopReq, CG activated UE  is moved to cgInit state, stop CG scheduling

[PR704875] and  update total Number Of Activated Cg Ues counter .[End PR704875]

[End PR697326]

At the reception of PsUser\_UserDeleteReq, following specific actions are done for CG configured UE:

* Release CG PRBs (refer to 5G\_L2\_11173\_replaced([11176568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* stop all CG timers.

[Before PR704875]- update total Number Of Activated Cg Ues counter.[End PR704875]

* Remove UE from CG allocation table.
* reset associated state machine for this UE.
* Release CS-RNTI from rnti pool.
* remove CS-RNTI from indexer of ue database

---

### 2.4.26.5 CG Activation

#### 2.4.26.5.0-1 CG Activation CG Activation decision (in CG-Configured state) (ID: 11175741)

**2.4.26.5.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12816687`)

CG Activation
When a UE is entering this state from any state, SINR average for CG Link Adaptation input is invalidated and the **cgSinrMonitoringPeriod timer is restarted (refer to rdCgSinrMonitoringPeriod\_slot** in **5G\_UP\_ALG\_15212\_replaced([11173526](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUAR_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**\*\*\*\*

CG Activation decision requires the following (as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15121\_replaced([11173004](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

- A reliable SINR average update after the **cgSinrMonitoringPeriod timer expiry that generates a “SINR update event” (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15178\_replaced([11131181](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krBbt_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) processed in the post slot scheduling.**

- If the UE is assigned to the Dedicated BWP supporting CG PUSCH (Large one), this event is used by the CG LA to compute the **nRbCgUl required number of** CG RBs based on **grantedPacketSize (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14664\_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**\*\*\*\*

- A minimum UE data rate (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15204\_replaced([11173172](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuRkA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) that worth CG activation.

-     UE DRX is disabled or enabled and already passed the beginning of first complete DRX cycle after DRX enabled.

-     CG activation process is not suspended by the cell level CG activation control state specified in 5G\_L2\_12197\_replaced([11176727](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_262MtfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* In case UL Buffer split is triggered CG pattern must not always conflict with SCell Ul slots as specified in 5G\_L2\_13265\_replaced([11176763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_262MvfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

-    No CG Deconfiguration is pending for this UE.  
  
      -    UE is not in PUSCH GBR Congestion State

-    [CB014088-B] the cell in which UE is attached is not in overload defined in [12689381: CG Activation During UP Overload](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_UiJR01ZiEfC45IKMrv0u7Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)  [End CB014088-B]

When all conditions above are fulfilled:

* ~~The attempt counter cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter is set to 0~~
* the UE state is changed to CG-WaitForActivation for the first CG activation attempt.

---

#### 2.4.26.5.0-2 CG Activation CG Activation scheduling attempt (in CG-WaitForActivation state) (ID: 11175748)

**2.4.26.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175752`)

CG Activation
CG Activation scheduling

---

**2.4.26.5.0-2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175756`)

CG Activation
 [CB008124-C] As a protection, when entering this state, the timer cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer is started (refer to its duration in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15620\_replaced([11173576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUnVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008124-C]

 Scheduling a CG activation for a candidate slot ‘X’ (DCI slot + K2) requires availability of free CG RB resources. This check has a high CPU cost, so the CG activation tries to preempt an initial DG PUSCH transmission that already satisfies the selection criteria i.e. a PDCCH is already allocated (an eviction is unlikely, this is more efficient than having a dedicated request class). The preemption criteria are the following (refer to “TD-SCH” event in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15123\_replaced([11173014](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

- Initial DG PUSCH to be preempted has a request class priority lower or equal than the one of the CG DRB driver.

- UE is not in a transient phase (CG activation requires a PDCCH allocated in USS)  
Note: The existence of a transient phase does not delay the Activation decision but just the Activation scheduling.

* [CB008124-E-UPD003] If  UL Buffer split is triggered for UL CA, CG pattern starting on slot 'X' must  not overlap any UL slot of the active Scell configured with UL CA (refer to 5G\_L2\_13271\_replaced([11176774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_262Mv_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008124-E-UPD003]

- [CB008124-D] CG slots ‘Y’ associated to candidate slot ‘X’ have a low enough collision ratio with P-CSI reports (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15942\_replaced([11173218](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuRkDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_10375\_replaced([11175760](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u38_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008124-D].

- Free CG RB resources for candidate slot ‘X’ can be allocated (refer to 5G\_L2\_11036\_replaced([11176528](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260XjfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB008124-C] without exceeding max FDM capability and UE pooling budget for CG UEs [End CB008124-C].

-     [CB008124-HA] UE DRX is disabled or enabled and already passed the beginning of first complete DRX cycle after DRX enabled and in DRX active state. [End CB008124-HA]

-     [CB008124-H-UPD001] CG activation process is not suspended by the cell level CG activation control state specified in 5G\_L2\_12197\_replaced([11176727](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_262MtfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008124-H-UPD001]

If the preemption succeeds, 5G-L2-PS (refer to the default call flow diagram in 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_2416\_replaced([11207661](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgtfjvvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):  
- sends the DCI 0 1 for CG activation (refer to specific format in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14810\_replaced([11172938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPHy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  
- starts the periodic sending of UlData\_PuschReceiveReq in the CG slot occasions.   
- [CB008124-C] stops cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer [End CB008124-C]  
- UE state is set to CG-WaitForActivationConfirm.

If the preemption is not confirmed, initial DG PUSCH transmission is carried out waiting for another preemption occasion.

 [CB008124-C] If the cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer expires, the activation procedure is aborted, the UE state is set to CG-Configured and a new decision may take place based on a later SINR estimation. [End CB008124-C]

---

**2.4.26.5.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11175760`)

CG Activation
UL slot eligibility algorithm based on collision rate between CG slots and P-CSI reporting slots

---

**2.4.26.5.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175766`)

CG Activation
Each time CG activation is attempted in CG-WaitForActivation state [CB008124-CA] or each time CG modification is attempted in CG-WaitForModification [End CB008124-CA], this algorithm is performed to compute the collisionRate of the given UL slot N, candidate for CG activation [CB008124-CA] or CG modification [End CB008124-CA], and based on the computed collisionRate return whether the UL slot N is eligible or not for CG activation [CB008124-CA] or CG modification [End CB008124-CA]:

Input parameters:

- Slot N candidate for CG activation [CB008124-CA] or CG modification [End CB008124-CA]

- For each p-CSI report (i.e. channel acquisition or beam management) of P(S)Cell [CB008124-E] or SCell(s) [End CB008124-E]

- p-CSI periodicity

- p-CSI slotOffset

- CG periodicity

- k2

Algo description:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to communication systems, likely in a cellular network context. The flowchart begins with a "Start" node and proceeds through several steps involving calculations and conditional checks.
Here's a breakdown of the flowchart's content:
**Initialization and Candidate Slot Identification:**
\* **Start:** The process begins.
\* **Compute investigation interval duration:** This is calculated as the Least Common Multiple (LCM) of all "p-CSI report periodicities" and "CG periodicity".
\* **Populate candidateSlotList[0]:** The first candidate slot N is added to `candidateSlotList[0]` if `N+k2` is an UL (Uplink) slot.
\* **Populate candidateSlotList[k]:** Other candidate slots M are added to `candidateSlotList[k]` if `N+k2+CG periodicity` is an UL slot.
\* **Initialize candidateSlot:** `candidateSlot` is set to `candidateSlotList[0]`.
**p-CSI Report Processing and Collision Detection:**
\* **For each p-CSI report:** A loop iterates through each p-CSI report.
\* **Initialize BitmapCollisionPcsix:** `BitmapCollisionPcsix` is set to `0x0000`.
\* **Populate pCsiSlotList[0]:** The first UL slot P is added to `pCsiSlotList[0]` if `P = slotOffset`.
\* **Populate pCsiSlotList[n]:** Other slots Q are added to `pCsiSlotList[n]` if `slotOffset+P CSI periodicity` is an UL slot.
\* **Initialize pCsiSlot:** `pCsiSlot` is set to `pCsiSlotList[0]`.
**Main Loop and Collision Marking:**
\* **Loop Condition:** The flowchart enters a loop with a condition: `candidateSlot + k2 == pCsiSlot?`
\* **If Yes:**
\* **Mark Collision:** For the current p-CSI report, `BitmapCollisionPcsix[candidateSlot+k2]` is set to `1`.
\* **Check for next candidate slot:** `Is next candidate slot M in candidateSlotList?`
\* **If Yes:** `candidateSlot` is updated to the next element in `candidateSlotList` (`candidateSlotList[++i]`). The loop continues.
\* **If No:** The loop terminates.
\* **If No:**
\* **Check for next p-CSI slot:** `Is next p-CSI slot R in pCsiSlotList?`
\* **If Yes:** `pCsiSlot` is updated to the next element in `pCsiSlotList` (`pCsiSlotList[++x]`). The loop continues.
\* **If No:** The loop terminates.
**Collision Rate Calculation and Eligibility Determination:**
\* **Calculate BitmapCollisionAllPcsi:** This is the bitwise OR of `BitmapCollisionPcsi1 OR BitmapCollisionPcsi2 OR ... BitmapCollisionPcsiN`.
\* **Calculate collisionRate:** This is the number of bits set to 1 in `BitmapCollisionAllPcsi` divided by the `investigation interval duration`.
\* **Conditional Check:** `collisionRate < 50%?`
\* **If Yes:** "Slot N is eligible for sending of CG activation".
\* **If No:** "Slot N is not eligible for sending of CG activation".
**End:**
\* **Stop:** The process concludes.
