#### 2.4.26.5.0-3 CG Activation CG Activation scheduling Confirmation (in CG-WaitForActivationConfirm state) (ID: 11175771)

**2.4.26.5.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175775`)

CG Activation
When entering this state for each attempt, the cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer is started (refer to its duration in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15211\_replaced([11173518](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUARfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 On reception of the MAC-CE “Configured grant Confirmation” (LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd indication with isConfiguredGrantConfCe set to True, refer to chronogram in 5G\_L2\_11185\_replaced([11175795](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u3-_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):  
- Stop the cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer timer  
- The UE state is changed to CG-Activated.

 On cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer expiry, the number of failed confirmation attempts, cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter, is incremented.

If all the CG PUSCH received in this state were DTX, release the allocated CG RBs (refer to 5G\_L2\_11173\_replaced([11176568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

* If the cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter has reached the max number of attempts (defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15207\_replaced([11173484](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZO_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the UE state is changed to CG-Error
* Else if at least one CG PUSCH was non-DTX, UE state is changed to CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder to initiate a CG Deactivation (refer to chronogram in 5G\_L2\_11200\_replaced([11175817](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u3__vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* Else, UE state is changed to CG-WaitForActivation to initiate a new activation attempt (refer to chronograms in 5G\_L2\_11196\_replaced([11175805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u3_fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 2.4.26.5.0-4 CG Activation CG Activation chronograms (ID: 11175778)

**2.4.26.5.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175782`)

CG Activation
Legend:

---

**2.4.26.5.0-4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175791`)

CG Activation
Legend

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram explaining the configuration of CG PUSCH and DG PUSCH scheduling, along with legends for DCI and PUSCH, and UE configuration details.
The diagram is structured into three main columns: "DCI legend", "PUSCH legend", and "UE configuration Configuration". It is further divided into two main rows: "CG PUSCH scheduling" and "DG PUSCH scheduling".
**DCI Legend:**
This section details different types of DCI (Downlink Control Information) for CG PUSCH scheduling.
- **A**: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH Activation (represented by a purple square)
- **r-A**: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH Re-Activation (represented by a red-purple square)
- **D**: DCI 0 0 for CG PUSCH Deactivation (represented by a red square)
- **X**: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH reTX of Harqld X (represented by a blue square)
- **no DCI available**
**PUSCH Legend:**
This section explains the meaning of different symbols and lines used in PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) diagrams.
- **UL slot line:**
- White rectangle: Non-CG slot
- Yellow rectangle with 'X': CG slot with implicit Harqld X (as a fn of SFN/slot)
- Orange rectangle with 'X': Blocked CG slot (UE configuredGrantTimer running or Measurement Gap overlaps CG slot)
- Dashed blue rectangle with 'X': Skipped Init CG PUSCH (DTX as UE has no data) (L1 decoding scheduled)
- **harq line:** (associated with CS-RNTI)
- Blue rectangle with 'X' above a blue rectangle with 'X': Init CG PUSCH & Harqld (same as CG slot)
- Blue rectangle with 'X' above a blue rectangle with 'X': CG PUSCH reTX & Harqld (any id) in CG slot
- Blue rectangle with 'X' above a blue rectangle with 'X': CG overwriting where CG RBs are reused
- Blue rectangle with 'X' above a blue rectangle with 'X': CG PUSCH reTX in non-CG slot (or blocked CG slot)
- Blue rectangle with 'X' above a blue rectangle with 'X': needs FDM RBs alloc (same num RB as CG slot)
**UE configuration Configuration:**
This section provides configuration parameters for the UE (User Equipment).
- **10 or 20**: CG slot periodicity (in slots)
- **2**: Num of Harqld received by CG
- **2**: ConfiguredGrantTimer (UE timer)
- **L1 feedback = DTX, CRC OK or NACK**
- Further details on timer length and space between PUSCH and CRC depending on:
- the L1 max decoding budget
- the L1 slot buffering (ABIL)
- and the '2 slot' advance in scheduler
**CG PUSCH scheduling (including CG PUSCH reTX):**
This row describes the DCI and PUSCH configurations related to CG PUSCH. The PUSCH legend symbols are applied here.
**DG PUSCH scheduling (DG stands for Dynamic Grant):**
This row describes the DCI and PUSCH configurations related to DG PUSCH.
- **X**: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH (initial or reTX) (represented by a green square)
- **harq line:** (associated with C-RNTI)
- Green rectangle with 'X' above a green rectangle with 'X': DG PUSCH (initial or reTx)
- Green rectangle with 'X' above a green rectangle with 'X': CG overwriting for DG PUSCH reusing CG RBs
- Green rectangle with 'X' above a green rectangle with 'X': i.e. UE data fits in CG RBs according to DG LA
- Green rectangle with 'X' above a green rectangle with 'X': CG overwriting for DG PUSCH with new RBs
- Green rectangle with 'X' above a green rectangle with 'X': needs FDM RBs alloc num RB > num in CG slot)
The table can be represented as follows:

| Category | Sub-Category | DCI Legend | PUSCH Legend | UE Configuration Configuration |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **CG PUSCH scheduling (including CG PUSCH reTX)** |  | A: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH Activation (Purple)   r-A: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH Re-Activation (Red-Purple)   D: DCI 0 0 for CG PUSCH Deactivation (Red)   X: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH reTX of Harqld X (Blue)   no DCI available | **UL slot line:**   White: Non-CG slot   Yellow X: CG slot with implicit Harqld X   Orange X: Blocked CG slot   Dashed Blue X: Skipped Init CG PUSCH   **harq line (CS-RNTI):**   Blue X / Blue X: Init CG PUSCH & Harqld   Blue X / Blue X: CG PUSCH reTX & Harqld   Blue X / Blue X: CG overwriting where CG RBs are reused   Blue X / Blue X: CG PUSCH reTX in non-CG slot   Blue X / Blue X: needs FDM RBs alloc | 10 or 20: CG slot periodicity (in slots)   2: Num of Harqld received by CG   2: ConfiguredGrantTimer (UE timer)   L1 feedback = DTX, CRC OK or NACK   Timer length depends on periodicity   Space between PUSCH and CRC depends on:   - L1 max decoding budget   - L1 slot buffering (ABIL)   - '2 slot' advance in scheduler |
| **DG PUSCH scheduling (DG stands for Dynamic Grant)** |  | X: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH (initial or reTX) (Green) | **harq line (C-RNTI):**   Green X / Green X: DG PUSCH (initial or reTx)   Green X / Green X: CG overwriting for DG PUSCH reusing CG RBs   Green X / Green X: i.e. UE data fits in CG RBs according to DG LA   Green X / Green X: CG overwriting for DG PUSCH with new RBs   Green X / Green X: needs FDM RBs alloc num RB > num in CG slot) |  |

Chronograms below are stored in "Scheduling chronograms.xlsx" under <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/26%20Uplink%20Configured%20Grant%20Type%202/Scheduling%20Chronograms.xlsx?d=wa6da18476c0d4cd98315c0175835da24&csf=1&web=1&e=C1uGZM>

Chrono:[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication sequence, likely within a cellular network context, focusing on Carrier Aggregation (CG) activation. The diagram spans across multiple Time-to-Live (TTL) slots, indicated by the "SFN slot" timeline at the top, which shows slots numbered from 0 to 19 for two Super Frame Numbers (SFNs).
The top section depicts the Physical Downlink Control Channel (PDCCH) and the corresponding CG slot in the Uplink (UL). This is represented by a series of black, white, and gray blocks, indicating different types of transmissions or states within those slots. Notably, a "DCI for activation" is highlighted in purple, occurring in slot 16 of the first SFN, with a parameter "k2=3".
Below this, several timelines represent different aspects of the CG process:
\* **CG PUSCH Harqld 0 & 1 (UE view):** These timelines, shown in blue, indicate the Hybrid Automatic Repeat reQuest (HARQ) feedback for the CG Physical Uplink Shared Channel (PUSCH) from the UE's perspective. A "MAC-CE CG Conf." (MAC Control Element CG Configuration) is shown in slot 17, with a value of "1", followed by "CG activated UE". A "0" is also indicated in slot 1 of the second SFN.
\* **gNB view:** This section is less detailed but provides context.
\* **DG PUSCH HarqiD >= 2:** This indicates a Downlink Grant (DG) for PUSCH with a HARQ ID greater than or equal to 2.
\* **5G-L2-PS states:** This section outlines the states of the 5G Layer 2 Protocol Stack (L2-PS).
\* **CG-Configured:** This state is indicated by a dashed blue box.
\* **Activation decision:** A dashed brown box points to this decision, occurring around slot 14.
\* **CG-WaitForActivation:** A dashed brown box represents this state.
\* **CG-WaitForActivationConfirm:** A dashed purple box indicates this state, which involves "Start Scheduling init CG PUSCH decoding".
\* **CG-Activated:** A dashed blue box signifies the final activated state.
The diagram also shows specific events like "CRC OK" and "LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd" occurring later in the timeline, suggesting successful reception and processing. Yellow boxes with "1" and "0" are present in the PDCCH/CG slot timeline, likely indicating specific control information or status.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5213.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**2.4.26.5.0-4.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175811`)

CG Activation
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a timing diagram illustrating a cellular network scenario, likely related to a feature called "CG" (Carrier Aggregation or similar). The diagram spans across multiple SFN (System Frame Number) slots, from slot 15 in SFN 0 to slot 19 in SFN 2.
The diagram is organized into several horizontal tracks representing different communication channels and states:
1. **SFN slot:** This is the top-most track, showing the SFN and slot numbers. It's divided into SFN 0, SFN 1, and SFN 2, with slots numbered from 0 to 19 within each SFN. The background of this track alternates between black, grey, and white, possibly indicating different types of slots or transmission periods.
2. **PDCCH (UL DCI) / CG slot (UL):** This track shows the Downlink Control Information (DCI) for CG activation and the corresponding CG slot for Uplink (UL) transmission. It highlights specific slots with colored blocks (purple, black, white, yellow) and labels like "A" and "1". There are vertical dashed lines indicating specific points in time.
3. **CG PUSCH Harqld 0 - UE view:** This track, from the UE's perspective, shows the status of CG PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) with HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) ID 0. It shows a "Ø" symbol and a dashed circle with "1" and "UE not activated".
4. **CG PUSCH Harqld 0 - gNB view:** This track, from the gNB's perspective, shows the status of CG PUSCH with HARQ ID 0. It's currently empty.
5. **CG PUSCH Harqld 1 - UE view:** This track, from the UE's perspective, shows the status of CG PUSCH with HARQ ID 1. It shows a dashed circle with "1" and "UE not activated".
6. **CG PUSCH Harqld 1 - gNB view:** This track, from the gNB's perspective, shows the status of CG PUSCH with HARQ ID 1. It indicates "UE buffer not null".
7. **DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 1:** This track shows a condition related to DG PUSCH HARQ ID being greater than or equal to 1.
8. **5G-L2-PS states:** This track shows the state transitions of the 5G Layer 2 Protocol Stack. It includes states like "CG-WaitForActivationConfirm", "Start Scheduling init CG PUSCH decoding", "CG-WaitForActivation", and "Free allocated CG RBs".
Throughout the diagram, there are various annotations and messages indicating events and their outcomes:
\* **DCI for CG activation k2=3:** Indicates the DCI used for CG activation.
\* **NEW DCI for activation:** Indicates a new DCI for activation.
\* **MAC-CE CG Conf. 0a CG activated UE:** Indicates a MAC Control Element (CE) for CG confirmation, with "0a" and "CG activated UE".
\* **CRC OK LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveind:** Indicates a successful CRC check and a reception indication.
\* **UE not activated:** Indicates that the UE is not activated.
\* **UE buffer not null:** Indicates that the UE's buffer is not empty.
\* **L1 DTX ⇒ NOK:** Indicates a Layer 1 Discontinuous Transmission (DTX) resulting in a "Not OK" outcome.
\* **CG Activation attempt failure on timer expiry:** Indicates a failure in CG activation due to timer expiry.
\* **cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer:** A message related to CG activation/deactivation timer confirmation.
\* **NOK:** Appears multiple times, indicating a negative outcome.
\* **CG-Activated:** Indicates that CG is activated.
\* **Possible loss of PUCCH (AN/CSI) in CG slots because gNB is only decoding CG PUSCH:** A concluding remark explaining a potential issue.
The diagram uses different line styles (solid, dashed, dotted) and colors (purple, blue, red, green, brown) to represent different signals, messages, and states. Arrows are used to indicate the direction of communication or the flow of events.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5215.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The UE has not decoded the first DCI for activation, so 5G-L2-PS:

* Detects the absence of confirmation at cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer expiry (5G\_L2\_11030\_replaced([11175771](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u39fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Have received a raw L1 DTX for all CG PUSCH occasions. Therefore it assumes that the UE has not received the DCI, so CG RBs can be released without prior CG Deactivation.
* Starts a new CG activation attempt.

The DL is impacted during the inconsistency period because the UE may still report a DL HARQ feedback over PUCCH in CG slots whereas 5G-L1-UL tries to decode a CG PUSCH.

Notes:

* As per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15135\_replaced([11173114](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQ88_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the CG PUSCH in (SFN 0, slot 19) is considered as CRC NOK (although L1 has reported DTX) because the estimated UE buffer was not empty at slot 17.
* In the very unlikely case where the UE has received the DCI but all CG PUSCH (could be single opportunity in 1:7 pattern) are seen as DTX, this UE will interfere the release CG RBs that may be allocated to another UE.

**2.4.26.5.0-4.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175822`)

CG Activation
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a timing diagram illustrating the process of Carrier Aggregation (CG) activation and deactivation in a cellular network, likely 5G NR. The diagram shows the timeline in terms of SFN (System Frame Number) and slots, from 0 to 19 for SFN 0, and then continuing for SFN 1 and SFN 2.
The diagram depicts the interactions between the UE (User Equipment) and the gNB (next-generation NodeB, the base station). Key elements shown are:
\* **PDCCH (UL DCI):** Physical Downlink Control Channel carrying Uplink Downlink Control Information. Different DCI messages are shown, including "DCI for activation" and "DCI for CG Deact".
\* **CG slot (UL):** Carrier Aggregation slot for uplink transmission.
\* **CG PUSCH Harqld 0/1:** Channel Grant Physical Uplink Shared Channel with HARQ ID 0 or 1. This is shown from both the UE's and gNB's perspective.
\* **DG PUSCH HarqiD >= 2:** Dual Grant Physical Uplink Shared Channel with HARQ ID greater than or equal to 2.
\* **MAC-CE CG Conf.:** MAC Control Element for Carrier Aggregation Configuration.
\* **SG-L2-PS states:** States related to the UE's Layer 2 (L2) and Packet Switched (PS) functionalities.
The diagram illustrates a scenario where CG is activated, then potentially fails, and then is deactivated.
**Key events and states shown:**
\* **CG Activation:**
\* A "DCI for activation" is sent by the gNB.
\* The UE receives this DCI and enters a "CG activated UE" state.
\* A "MAC-CE CG Conf." is sent by the gNB to confirm activation.
\* The UE enters a "CG-WaitForActivationConfirm" state, initiating CG PUSCH decoding.
\* **CG Activation Failure:**
\* A "NOK" (Not OK) is indicated, suggesting a failure in the activation process.
\* "CG Activation failure on timer expiry" is shown, implying a timer related to activation expired without successful confirmation.
\* A "cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer" is involved.
\* **CG Deactivation:**
\* A "DCI for CG Deact" is sent by the gNB.
\* The UE enters a "CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm" state.
\* The gNB flushes pending HARQ CG retransmissions and ignores subsequent CG-CRC.
\* A "MAC-CE CG Conf." with "non-active CG UE" is shown, indicating the UE MAC clears CG activation.
\* The UE enters a "CG-Configured" state, stopping the scheduling of initial CG PUSCH.
\* **Other states:**
\* "no more initial CG PUSCH" is indicated from the UE's perspective after deactivation.
\* "ACK LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd" is shown, likely an acknowledgment of a received PDU.
\* A "Possible loss of PUCCH (AN/CSI) because gNB is only decoding CG PUSCH" is highlighted as a potential issue.
The diagram uses different colored lines and shapes to represent different entities and events, with dashed lines indicating communication or state transitions. The timeline is clearly marked with slot numbers.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5217.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The UE has correctly decoded the first DCI for CG Activation but the gNB has not correctly decoded any CG PUSCH (re)transmission with MAC-CE (could be single opportunity in 1:7 pattern):

* 5G-L2-PS initiates a CG Deactivation because we don't want to block CG allocation of first attempt (and possible subsequent ones) until a successful CG Activation.

---

### 2.4.26.6 CG Modification

#### 2.4.26.6.0-1 CG Modification Single CG Modification scheduling attempt (in CG-WaitForModification state) (ID: 11175832)

**2.4.26.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175838`)

CG Modification
Refer to the conditions (CG Link Adaptation in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15651\_replaced([11172853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to trigger a CG Modification.

 As a protection, when entering this state, the cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer is started (refer its duration in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15620\_replaced([11173576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUnVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 Unlike CG activation, the cgModification request class is used to schedule the CG Modification for a candidate slot ‘X’ (DCI slot + K2) if following criteria are fulfilled (refer to “TD-SCH” event in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15622\_replaced([11173051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when UE is in cgModification request class:

- a PDCCH is available in the USS.  
It means that the UE is not in a transient phase of a RRC reconfiguration, otherwise the CG Modification scheduling is delayed.

- [CB008124-E-UPD003] If  UL Buffer split is triggered for UL CA, CG pattern starting on slot 'X' must  not overlap any UL slot of the active Scell configured with UL CA (refer to 5G\_L2\_13271\_replaced([11176774](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_262Mv_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008124-E-UPD003]

- [CB008124-D] The CG slots ‘Y’ associated to the candidate slot ‘X’ have a low enough collision ratio with P-CSI reports (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15942\_replaced([11173218](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuRkDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_10375\_replaced([11175760](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u38_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008124-D].

- Free CG RB resources for candidate slot ‘X’ can be allocated (refer to 5G\_L2\_11036\_replaced([11176528](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260XjfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) without exceeding the max FDM capability and UE pooling budget for CG UEs.

-     [CB008124-HA] UE DRX is disabled or enabled and already passed the beginning of first complete DRX cycle after DRX enabled and in DRX active state. [End CB008124-HA]

If UE is scheduled for CG Modification, 5G-L2-PS:

- Sends the DCI 0 1 for CG re-activation (same format as CG activation, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14810\_replaced([11172938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPHy_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

- Starts applying the new CG RB configuration from slot ‘X’ onwards (former RBs not yet released) i.e. 5G-L2-PS assumes that UE has correctly decoded the DCI and is going to sent the MAC-CE CG Confirmation in slot ‘X’ irrespective of configuredGrantTimer state (refer to Uplink Grant reception in 38.321 section 5.4.1).

- Therefore, for the HARQ Id ‘d’ associated to the new CG slot ‘X’, the slot number of last CG PUSCH (re)Transmission (used to check against the configuredGrantTimer(d) value) is reset. A possible CG HARQ retransmission waiting to be scheduled is released (retransmission count set to the maximum value) whereas the HARQ context waiting for an HARQ feedback is reset for possible immediate reuse (refer to the chronogram in 5G\_L2\_9153\_replaced([11175877](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfC_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

-  Besides, all pending HARQs or pending HARQ retransmissions with TBS based on the former CG allocation (Adaptive retransmission CB008662 not yet supported for CG PUSCH) are released whatever the value of 'periodicity'.

- Stops the cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer

- UE state is set to CG-WaitForModificationConfirm to wait for MAC-CE CG Confirmation.

Else if the UE is also assigned to the cgHarqRetrans request class, a CG PUSCH reTX is scheduled if the following criteria are fulfilled (refer to 5G\_L2\_11202):

- A PDCCH is available.

- nRbCgUl RB are available in case the slot ‘X’ is a non-CG slot (blocked CG slot included).

 If the cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer expires, the CG Modification procedure is aborted:

* If the CG modification was triggered for improved radio conditions, reset the CG Outer Loop LA (refer to “Enter event” in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15125\_replaced([11173036](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and change UE state to CG-Activated, waiting for new and possibly identical CG Modification decision.
* Else change UE state to CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder to trigger CG Deactivation

---

#### 2.4.26.6.0-2 CG Modification CG Modification confirmation (in CG-WaitForModificationConfirm state) (ID: 11175843)

**2.4.26.6.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175846`)

CG Modification
 When entering this state (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15624\_replaced([11173071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV5_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):  
- The cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer is started (refer to its duration in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15211\_replaced([11173518](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUARfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  
- Reset the monitoring window for CG Link Adaptation.

 On reception of the MAC-CE “Configured grant Confirmation” (LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd indication with isConfiguredGrantConfCe set to True), the Modification is confirmed (refer to chronogram in 5G\_L2\_11208\_replaced([11175865](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfCfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):  
- Release all or non-overlapping part of the CG RBs of the former CG allocation (refer to 5G\_L2\_11173\_replaced([11176568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  
- Stop the cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer timer  
- the UE state is changed to CG-Activated.

 On the cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer expiry (refer to all failure chronograms from 5G\_L2\_11239\_replaced([11175886](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfDfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):  
- UE state is changed to CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder to initiate the CG Deactivation.

Note: For simplification purpose, CG RB allocation will be released as part of CG Deactivation.

---

#### 2.4.26.6.0-3 CG Modification CG Modification chronograms (ID: 11175849)

**2.4.26.6.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175854`)

CG Modification
Legend:

---

**2.4.26.6.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175861`)

CG Modification
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram explaining different types of PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) scheduling and their corresponding configurations in a cellular network. It is divided into three main columns: "DCI legend", "PUSCH legend", and "UE configuration Configuration".
The "DCI legend" column describes different DCI (Downlink Control Information) messages related to CG (Configured Grant) PUSCH scheduling. These include activation, re-activation, deactivation, and retransmission of CG PUSCH.
The "PUSCH legend" column provides visual cues for different types of PUSCH slots and HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) lines. It differentiates between non-CG slots, CG slots with implicit HARQ, blocked CG slots, and skipped initial CG PUSCH. It also explains the meaning of different HARQ line representations, such as initial CG PUSCH, CG PUSCH retransmission within a CG slot, and CG PUSCH retransmission in a non-CG slot.
The "UE configuration Configuration" column lists various parameters related to UE (User Equipment) configuration for CG PUSCH. These include CG slot periodicity, the number of HARQ feedback received by CG, and the ConfiguredGrantTimer. It also specifies the possible L1 feedback values (DTX, CRC OK, or NACK) and factors influencing the timer length.
The diagram is further divided into two main sections based on the type of PUSCH scheduling: "CG PUSCH scheduling" (including CG PUSCH reTX) and "DG PUSCH scheduling" (DG stands for Dynamic Grant). Each section has its own set of DCI and PUSCH legend entries.
The table below summarizes the information presented in the image:

| Category | Sub-category | DCI Legend | PUSCH Legend | UE Configuration Configuration |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **CG PUSCH scheduling (including CG PUSCH reTX)** |  | A: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH Activation | **UL slot line:**   White: Non-CG slot   Yellow 'X': CG slot with implicit HarqId X (as a fn of SFN/slot)   Orange 'X': Blocked CG slot (UE configuredGrantTimer running or Measurement Gap overlaps CG slot)   Dashed Blue 'X': Skipped Init CG PUSCH (DTX as UE has no data)   (L1 decoding scheduled) | 10 : CG slot periodicity (in slots)   or 20 |
|  |  | r-A: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH Re-Activation | **harq line:**   Blue 'X' with Blue lines: Init CG PUSCH & HarqId (same as CG slot)   Blue 'X' with vertical lines: CG PUSCH reTX & HarqId (any id) in CG slot   ⇒ i.e. CG overwriting where CG RBs are reused   Blue 'X': CG PUSCH reTX in non-CG slot (or blocked CG slot)   ⇒ needs FDM RBs alloc (same num RB as CG slot) | 2 : Num of HarqId received by CG |
|  |  | D: DCI 0 0 for CG PUSCH Deactivation |  | 2 : ConfiguredGrantTimer (UE timer)   L1 feedback = DTX, CRC OK or NACK   - timer length depends on periodicity   - in diagrams, space between PUSCH and CRC depends on   . the L1 max decoding budget   . the L1 slot buffering (ABIL)   . and the '2 slot' advance in scheduler |
|  |  | X: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH reTX of Harqld X |  |  |
|  |  | no DCI available |  |  |
| **DG PUSCH scheduling (DG stands for Dynamic Grant)** |  | X: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH (initial or reTX) | **harq line:**   Green 'X' with Green lines: DG PUSCH (initial or reTx)   Green 'X' with vertical lines: CG overwriting for DG PUSCH reusing CG RBs   ⇒ i.e. UE data fits in CG RBs according to DG LA   Green 'X': CG overwriting for DG PUSCH with new RBs   ⇒ needs FDM RBs alloc num RB > num in CG slot) |  |

(duplicate of 5G\_L2\_11258\_replaced([11175782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u3-PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for readability reason)

Chronograms below are stored in "Scheduling chronograms.xlsx" under <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/26%20Uplink%20Configured%20Grant%20Type%202/Scheduling%20Chronograms.xlsx?d=wa6da18476c0d4cd98315c0175835da24&csf=1&web=1&e=C1uGZM>

**2.4.26.6.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11175865`)

CG Modification
Chronogram of a successful CG Modification with same CG slots (TDD pattern 1:4, UE periodicity = 10 slots)

**2.4.26.6.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175872`)

CG Modification
CG Modification is scheduled in a CG slot of former configuration, so the newly allocated RBs may be nested in (nRbCgUl decrease) or may fully overlap with (nRbCgUl increase) the former CG RB allocation (refer to 5G\_L2\_11163\_replaced([11176556](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-kfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a timing diagram illustrating a communication protocol, likely within a cellular network context. The diagram spans across multiple SFN (System Frame Number) slots, with a timeline marked from 0 to 19 for SFN 0 and then continuing from 0 to 19 for SFN 1, and further for SFN 2.
The top section depicts the "PDCCH (UL DCI)" and "CG slot (UL)" status. It shows a series of colored blocks (black, white, gray, and yellow) representing different states or events within each slot. Some slots are marked as "No DCI available...". Yellow blocks are labeled with numbers like "1", "0", and "1", indicating specific events or data.
Below this timeline, several parallel tracks represent different processes and states:
1. **CG PUSCH Harqld 0 (UE view / gNB view):** This track shows events related to "CG PUSCH Harqld 0" from both the UE (User Equipment) and gNB (next-generation NodeB) perspectives. Events like "NOK 0a" and "UE drops TBOa" are noted.
2. **CG PUSCH Harqld 1 (UE view / gNB view):** Similar to the above, this track tracks "CG PUSCH Harqld 1". It shows an event labeled "1a" and "UE & gNB apply new conf".
3. **DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2:** This track indicates "CG LA update" and "NOK".
4. **5G-L2-PS states:** This section details the state transitions and actions within the 5G Layer 2 Protocol Stack. It includes:
\* "CG-WaitForModification" (green dashed box)
\* "CG-WaitForModificationConfirm: L2-PS flushes all pending HARQ processes" (purple dashed box)
\* "CG-Activated: Free old CG RBs not overlapping with new CG RB config" (blue dashed box)
There are also several horizontal blue lines representing ongoing processes or states, with labels like "LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd" and "OK" indicating successful completion or status.
A red arrow with the text "Possible loss of UCI (AN/CSI)" points from the "CG-WaitForModificationConfirm" box towards the right, suggesting a potential consequence of certain actions.
The diagram uses color-coding and labels to convey complex interactions and state changes over time in a communication system.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5227.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**2.4.26.6.0-3.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175894`)

CG Modification
The UE and the gNB have inconsistent CG allocation and CG Modification fails due to absence of MAC-CE CG Confirmation.

They are not aligned on the same slots (and RBs), so 5G-L2-PS considers CG PUSCH as DTX (or NOK if UE buffer is not empty which is similar to 5G\_L2\_11243\_replaced([11175897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfD_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) case).

The use of "NOK" suggests negative acknowledgments or failures, while "DTX = skip" indicates Discontinuous Transmission or skipping of transmissions. The DL is impacted during the inconsistency period because the UE may still report a DL HARQ feedback over PUCCH in the new CG slots whereas the 5G-L1-UL tries to decode a CG PUSCH or conversely in the former CG slots.

**2.4.26.6.0-3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11175906`)

CG Modification
The UE and the gNB have inconsistent CG allocation and CG Modification fails due to absence of MAC-CE CG Confirmation.

In this case, due to common CG slots between former and new CG allocation, the DTX metric on DM-RS has reached the DTX threshold thanks to a significant RB overlapping. Initial CG PUSCH decoding fails but CG PUSCH retransmission is successfully decoded.

### 2.4.26.7 CG Deactivation

#### 2.4.26.7.0-1 CG Deactivation CG Deactivation decision (in CG-Activated state and all Firing states) (ID: 11175928)

**2.4.26.7.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12634855`)

CG Deactivation
The Deactivation is triggered for one of the following reasons:  
- when the number of consecutive DTX or UL MAC PDUs without data on the CG PUSCH occasions,  cgConsecutiveDtxOrWithoutDataCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation (refer to “HARQU event” in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15125\_replaced([11173036](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))), has reached the deactivation threshold (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15210\_replaced([11173510](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUAQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))).  
- when the number of consecutive HARQ failure on CG PUSCH, cgConsecutiveHarqFailureCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation (refer to “HARQU event” in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15125\_replaced([11173036](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV4PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))), has reached the deactivation threshold (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15209\_replaced([11173501](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUAQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)))  
- as a defense against a CG Activation or Modification failure to be sure that the UE has released its CG RB resources before reusing them.

* when CP-RT requests non-GBR UL CG type2 PRB load reduction as specified in 5G\_L2\_12008\_replaced([11176718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_262Ms_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
* when the UE pooling budget or/and the PRB pooling budget is/are exceeded as specified in 5G\_L2\_12687\_replaced([11175936](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGFvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

[CB013388-C]- when in cell reconfiguration procedure, according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_33244/[11173470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), the actUlCgType2 (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988/[10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is present and modified from TRUE to FALSE, or any of the IEs inside the structure ulCgResourceType2 (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988/[10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) is modified (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_33244/[11173470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).[End CB013388-C]

When one of above conditions is fulfilled:  
- The number of attempt counter cgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounter is set to 0  
- The UE state is changed to CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder for the first CG Deactivation attempt.

[CB013388-C]If the Deactivation was triggered with cell reconfiguration procedure, and the actUlCgType2 remains set to TRUE, and any of the IEs inside the structure ulCgResourceType2 is modified, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall follow 5G\_UP\_ALG\_33244/[11173470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and:

* start the rdCgType2DeactivationTimer (see [12634737](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_8T-FYD-eEfCal_QUJn4xrg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and follow [12646707](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_W4tMcULNEfCH-cG2YrVgkA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9),
* resume CG Activation according to 5G\_L2\_11018/[11175737](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26uQ7fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) once the CG Deactivation has been completed for all CG type UEs or once the timer has expired,
* stop and reset the timer if the CG Deactivation has been completed for all CG type UEs before the timer expires.

If the Deactivation was triggered with cell reconfiguration procedure, and the actUlCgType2 is modified from TRUE to FALSE, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall follow 5G\_UP\_ALG\_33244/[11173470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and:

* start the rdCgType2DeactivationTimer (see [12634737](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_8T-FYD-eEfCal_QUJn4xrg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and follow [12646707](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_W4tMcULNEfCH-cG2YrVgkA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9),
* stop and reset the timer if the CG Deactivation has been completed for all CG type UEs before the timer expires.

If additional CG Deactivation was triggered with cell reconfiguration procedure during the deactivation transient phase i.e. before the CG Deactivation has been completed for all CG type UEs, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall continue the previously started timer, and take into account the latest CG parameters received (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14663/[11172788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgsPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).[End CB013388-C]

---

#### 2.4.26.7.0-2 CG Deactivation Selection of UEs candidate for CG deactivation because of UE pooling bugdet or/and PRB pooling budget limitation (ID: 11175936)

**2.4.26.7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175944`)

CG Deactivation
The algorithm below is triggered upon reception of the internal::resourceResp:

step 1: to check whether some CG slots exceed or not the UE pooling budget or/and the PRB pooling budget

step 2: if the UE pooling budget or/and the PRB pooling budget is/are exceeded for some CG slots, select in UeDeactCandidateList the UEs candidate to CG deactivation

step 3: apply a penalty for the UEs in UeDeactCandidateList

step 4: sorting the UEs in UeDeactCandidateList from the highest penalty to the lowest penalty. If several UEs have the same penalty, the UEs with higher number of CG PRBs are prioritized first to trigger lower number of CG Deactivation

step 5: triggering the CG deactivation for UEs in UeDeactCandidateList up to obtain all CG slots of the cgAllocationTable with conditions C1 and C2 below respective budgets

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a spreadsheet with code and comments. The code appears to be related to resource management in a cellular network, specifically concerning CG (Carrier Grade) resource allocation and deactivation.
Here's a breakdown of the content:
**Key Variables and Concepts:**
\* **Budget:** Represents resource budgets, likely for PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) and PRB (Physical Resource Block) pooling.
\* **isPoolingBudgetExceeded:** A boolean flag indicating if the pooling budget has been exceeded.
\* **SlotOverbudgetBitmap:** A bitmap to identify slots where the pooling budget is exceeded.
\* **UeDeactCandidateList:** A list of candidate UEs (User Equipments) for CG deactivation.
\* **cgSlotAllocationTable:** A table containing slot-level allocation information.
\* **UEList:** A list of UEs within a specific slot.
\* **cgData.UeP:** UE-specific data related to CG resources.
\* **cgRbCountPerSlot:** The count of CG RBs allocated in a slot.
\* **cgUeCountPerSlot:** The count of CG UEs in a slot.
\* **budgetroh, budgetRe:** Specific budget thresholds.
\* **PenaltyPerUe:** A penalty value assigned to a UE, likely for sorting candidates for deactivation.
**Code Logic and Functionality:**
1. **Budget Calculation:** Budgets are calculated based on available PUSCH PRBs and other parameters.
2. **Pooling Budget Check:** The code checks if the pooling budget is exceeded by comparing current resource usage (`cgUeCountPerSlot`, `cgRbCountPerSlot`) against the defined budgets (`budgetroh`, `budgetRe`).
3. **Candidate Identification:** If the budget is exceeded, UEs are added to `UeDeactCandidateList`.
4. **Penalty Assignment:** Penalties are assigned to UEs based on how much they contribute to exceeding the budget. Higher penalties are assigned for exceeding `budgetroh` or `budgetRe`.
5. **Deactivation Prioritization:** The `UeDeactCandidateList` is sorted based on the assigned penalties, with higher penalties prioritized for deactivation.
6. **Resource Decrementation:** When a UE is considered for deactivation, its CG RBs and FDM tokens are treated as free, and the slot's resource counts are decremented.
7. **Slot Budget Reset:** If the slot's resource counts fall within the budget after considering deactivations, the `SlotOverBudgetBitmap` for that slot is reset.
8. **Deactivation Initiation:** UEs are moved to a "CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder" state if they are not already in a pending deactivation or deconfiguration state.
9. **Loop Termination:** The process breaks if the `SlotOverBudgetBitmap` indicates no slots are over budget.
**Comments and Explanations:**
The comments provide context and explanations for the code, including:
\* The purpose of different variables and calculations.
\* The logic behind penalty assignment and UE sorting.
\* Simplifications made for the purpose of the algorithm (e.g., considering old CG RBs as allocated).
\* The meaning of specific states or conditions (e.g., "pending CG Deact", "i.e. UE not included for resource decrementation only").
**Table Representation:**
The image does not contain a single, well-defined table in the traditional sense. Instead, it has data structures that are conceptually tabular, such as `cgSlotAllocationTable` and `UEList`, which are iterated over. The structure is more like nested lists or arrays within a spreadsheet.
```

# Conceptual representation of cgSlotAllocationTable and UEList structure:

cgSlotAllocationTable[ixSlot]
-> UEList[ixUE]
-> cgData.UeP
-> currentCgResource().numOfPrb()
-> Penalty
```[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5240.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

CG deactivation because of exceeded Pooling Budget.xlsx is stored under:

<https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/26%20Uplink%20Configured%20Grant%20Type%202?csf=1&web=1&e=qSXVOD>

---

#### 2.4.26.7.0-3 CG Deactivation CG Deactivation scheduling attempt (in CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder state) (ID: 11175947)

**2.4.26.7.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11175951`)

CG Deactivation
The cgDeactivation request class is used to schedule the PDCCH order for CG Deactivation (DCI 0 0 without associated PUSCH, refer to specific format described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14812\_replaced([11172948](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPHzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The only criterion is the allocation of the PDCCH in the CSS (refer to “Enter” event in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15127\_replaced([11173079](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB008124-HA]  and UE DRX is disabled or enabled and already passed the beginning of first complete DRX cycle after DRX enabled and in DRX active state. [End CB008124-HA]:

This procedure cannot be aborted in this state and the actual CG deactivation on UE side does not start on DCI reception but on emission of the MAC-CE CG Confirmation whose reception is used as a confirmation on 5G-L2-PS side (refer to the default flow diagram in 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_2576\_replaced([11207856](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BgutpfvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Thus, the remaining part of the procedure depends on whether or not the slot ‘X’ (DCI slot + K2) is a confirmed CG slot occasion, meaning that this is not a blocked CG-slot (refer to configuredGrantTimer in 5G\_L2\_10781\_replaced([11175620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26tp0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the UE state with the associated RB configuration is unambiguous (i.e. the CG Deactivation is not a defense against a failed CG Activation, [CB008124-CA] CG Modification [End CB008124-CA] or a first CG Deactivation attempt not confirmed) [CB008124-B-UPDXXX] and no CG Deconfiguration is pending [CB008124-B-UPDXXX]:

 In Confirmed CG slot ‘X’ case, no additional DG PUSCH scheduling is required for MAC-CE CG Confirmation:   
UE state is set to CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm to wait for the CG PUSCH containing the MAC-CE CG Confirmation in slot ‘X’ (refer to chronogram in 5G\_L2\_11271\_replaced([11175985](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wtIfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: Unlike the CG re-activation, the configuredGrantTimer of the HARQ associated to the CG slot is not reset on the reception of the DCI for CG deactivation (refer to Uplink Grant reception in 38.321 section 5.4.1), so it is important to exclude the blocked CG-slot.

 Otherwise:  
UE state is set to CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling to schedule a DG PUSCH for this purpose (refer to chronogram in 5G\_L2\_11284\_replaced([11175998](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wtI_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_L2\_11275\_replaced([11176009](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wtJfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_11277\_replaced([11176020](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wtJ_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) respectively for non-CG slot, blocked CG slot and after a CG procedure failure).

For failure case, if periodicity is equal to 1 (or minimum UL slot periodicity in TDD), a CG overwriting for DG PUSCH transmission is required, irrespective of CG PUSCH TBS.

---

#### 2.4.26.7.0-4 CG Deactivation Optional scheduling of DG PUSCH for MAC-CE CG Confirmation (in CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling state) (ID: 11175955)

**2.4.26.7.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11175958`)

CG Deactivation
When entering this state (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15129\_replaced([11173092](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV7PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), UE remains in cgDeactivation request class to schedule a DG PUSCH:  
- If the UE is in transient phase, the PDCCH could be allocated in CSS.  
- For RB calculation, MAC-CE CG confirmation size (header only) is added to the UE buffer size.

 Once the DG PUSCH has been scheduled, the CG state is changed to CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm to wait for the MAC-CE CG Confirmation.

 In case a previous CG Activation [CB008124-CA], CG Modification [End CB008124-CA] or CG Deactivation attempt has only failed on 5G-L2-PS perspective (MAC-CE CG Confirmation has previously been sent but not decoded), DG PUSCH scheduling may be useless (refer to second chronogram in 5G\_L2\_11277\_replaced([11176020](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wtJ_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

So the MAC-CE CG Confirmation could also be received in a CG PUSCH in this state and shall be processed as in CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm state.

---

#### 2.4.26.7.0-5 CG Deactivation CG Deactivation Confirmation (in CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm states) (ID: 11175963)

**2.4.26.7.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11175968`)

CG Deactivation
When entering this state (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15131\_replaced([11173098](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):  
- The cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer is started (refer to rdCgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer\_slot duration in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15211\_replaced([11173518](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUARfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))  
- HARQ with pending CG reTX are released. Later, on reception of HARQU feedback, HARQ are similarly released (refer to 5G\_L2\_10885\_replaced([11176377](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zwcvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 On reception of the MAC-CE “Configured grant Confirmation” (LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd indication with isConfiguredGrantConfCe set to True):

[Before CB008124-B-UPDXX]   
- Release the CG RB allocation [CB008124-CA] and the former RB allocation in case of CG Modification failure [End CB008124-CA] (refer to 5G\_L2\_11173\_replaced([11176568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  
- Stop the cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer timer  
- The UE state is changed to CG-Configured.

[CB008124-B-UPDXX]  
If the CG Deactivation has been triggered either by a failed CG Activation [CB008124-CA] or a failed Modification [EndCB008124-CA], a late reception of the MAC-CE CG Confirmation of the previous failed procedure could be mistaken for the one of the CG Deactivation (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15131\_replaced([11173098](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the chronogram in 5G\_L2\_11290\_replaced([11176060](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wtLfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), so a new CG deactivation attempt is started to remove the ambiguity :  
- The failing condition is cleared   
- cgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounter is not incremented  
- Stop cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer timer  
- The UE state is changed to CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder.

Otherwise, CG deactivation is complete:  
- Release CG RB allocation [CB008124-CA] including former RB allocation in case of CG Modification failure [End CB008124-CA] (refer to 5G\_L2\_11173\_replaced([11176568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).  
- Stop the cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer timer  
- The UE state is changed to CG-Configured.

[End CB008124-B-UPDXX]

 On cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer expiry:

* Increment the number of failed Deactivation attempts, cgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounter.
* If the maximum number of attempts (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15208\_replaced([11173491](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) has been reached, the UE state is changed to CG-Error
* Else, the UE state is changed to CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder to start a new CG Deactivation attempt.

---

#### 2.4.26.7.0-6 CG Deactivation CG Deactivation Timeout (ID: 12646707)

**2.4.26.7.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12646719`)

CG Deactivation
If the Deactivation was triggered with cell reconfiguration procedure, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall set the state to CG-Error for all CG type UEs for which the CG Deactivation has not been completed before the rdCgType2DeactivationTimer (see [12634737](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_8T-FYD-eEfCal_QUJn4xrg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) expired.

---

CG Deactivation

**2.4.26.7.0-7.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11175982`)

CG Deactivation
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram explaining different types of PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) scheduling and their corresponding configurations in a cellular network. It is divided into three main columns: "DCI legend", "PUSCH legend", and "UE configuration Configuration".
The "DCI legend" column describes different DCI (Downlink Control Information) messages related to CG (Configured Grant) PUSCH scheduling. These include activation, re-activation, deactivation, and retransmission of CG PUSCH.
The "PUSCH legend" column provides visual cues for different types of PUSCH slots and HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) lines. It differentiates between non-CG slots, CG slots with implicit HARQ, blocked CG slots, and skipped initial CG PUSCH. It also explains the meaning of different HARQ line representations, such as initial CG PUSCH, CG PUSCH retransmission within a CG slot, and CG PUSCH retransmission in a non-CG slot.
The "UE configuration Configuration" column lists various parameters related to UE (User Equipment) configuration for CG PUSCH. These include CG slot periodicity, the number of HARQ feedback received by CG, and the ConfiguredGrantTimer. It also specifies the possible L1 feedback values (DTX, CRC OK, or NACK) and factors influencing the timer length.
The diagram is further divided into two main sections based on the type of PUSCH scheduling: "CG PUSCH scheduling" (including CG PUSCH reTX) and "DG PUSCH scheduling" (DG stands for Dynamic Grant). Each section has its own set of DCI and PUSCH legend entries.
The table below summarizes the information presented in the image:

| Category | Sub-category | DCI Legend | PUSCH Legend | UE Configuration Configuration |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **CG PUSCH scheduling (including CG PUSCH reTX)** |  | A: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH Activation | **UL slot line:**   White: Non-CG slot   Yellow 'X': CG slot with implicit HarqId X (as a fn of SFN/slot)   Orange 'X': Blocked CG slot (UE configuredGrantTimer running or Measurement Gap overlaps CG slot)   Dashed Blue 'X': Skipped Init CG PUSCH (DTX as UE has no data)   (L1 decoding scheduled) | 10 : CG slot periodicity (in slots)   or 20 |
|  |  | r-A: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH Re-Activation | **harq line:**   Blue 'X' with Blue lines: Init CG PUSCH & HarqId (same as CG slot)   Blue 'X' with vertical lines: CG PUSCH reTX & HarqId (any id) in CG slot   ⇒ i.e. CG overwriting where CG RBs are reused   Blue 'X': CG PUSCH reTX in non-CG slot (or blocked CG slot)   ⇒ needs FDM RBs alloc (same num RB as CG slot) | 2 : Num of HarqId received by CG |
|  |  | D: DCI 0 0 for CG PUSCH Deactivation |  | 2 : ConfiguredGrantTimer (UE timer)   L1 feedback = DTX, CRC OK or NACK   - timer length depends on periodicity   - in diagrams, space between PUSCH and CRC depends on   . the L1 max decoding budget   . the L1 slot buffering (ABIL)   . and the '2 slot' advance in scheduler |
|  |  | X: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH reTX of Harqld X |  |  |
|  |  | no DCI available |  |  |
| **DG PUSCH scheduling (DG stands for Dynamic Grant)** |  | X: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH (initial or reTX) | **harq line:**   Green 'X' with Green lines: DG PUSCH (initial or reTx)   Green 'X' with vertical lines: CG overwriting for DG PUSCH reusing CG RBs   ⇒ i.e. UE data fits in CG RBs according to DG LA   Green 'X': CG overwriting for DG PUSCH with new RBs   ⇒ needs FDM RBs alloc num RB > num in CG slot) |  |

(duplicate of 5G\_L2\_11258\_replaced([11175782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u3-PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for readability reason)

Chronograms below are stored in "Scheduling chronograms.xlsx" under <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/26%20Uplink%20Configured%20Grant%20Type%202/Scheduling%20Chronograms.xlsx?d=wa6da18476c0d4cd98315c0175835da24&csf=1&web=1&e=C1uGZM>

**2.4.26.7.0-7.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11175994`)

[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a timing diagram illustrating the interaction between a base station (gNB) and a User Equipment (UE) in a cellular network, specifically focusing on Connected Mode (CG) functionalities. The diagram spans several SFN (System Frame Number) slots, from 0 to 2, with subdivisions into slots 0 through 19 within each SFN.
The top section shows the PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) and CG slot status. The PDCCH is represented by alternating black, white, and gray blocks, indicating different control information transmissions. The CG slot status is shown below, with black indicating no CG activity and yellow blocks highlighting specific CG events.
Below this, the diagram details the status of CG PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) IDs 0 and 1, as viewed by the UE and the gNB. It also shows the status of DG PUSCH HARQ ID greater than or equal to 2.
Several events and states are annotated with text and arrows:
\* **SFN 0, Slot 17-19:** A PDCCH transmission is indicated.
\* **SFN 0, Slot 18:** A yellow block in the "CG slot (UL)" row signifies a CG event.
\* **SFN 1, Slot 0:** A yellow block in the "CG slot (UL)" row signifies a CG event.
\* **SFN 1, Slot 8:** A yellow block in the "CG slot (UL)" row signifies a CG event.
\* **SFN 1, Slot 17-19:** A dashed blue oval encloses a sequence of events related to "sched CG PUSCHini".
\* **SFN 1, Slot 18:** A yellow block in the "CG slot (UL)" row signifies a CG event, labeled with "1" in a red box.
Key events and states described in the diagram include:
\* **"CG Deact > CG reTX"**: Indicates a condition where CG deactivation is followed by CG retransmission.
\* **"UE drops TBOa"**: The UE drops a Transport Block (TB) with ID 'a'.
\* **"Ob MAC-CE CG Conf."**: A MAC Control Element (CE) related to CG configuration is received.
\* **"non-active CG UE (UE MAC clears CG act)"**: The UE is non-active for CG, and its MAC layer clears the CG activity.
\* **"OK"**: A positive confirmation.
\* **"NOK 0a"**: A negative acknowledgment for HARQ ID 0a.
\* **"1a"**: Likely refers to a Transport Block with ID 'a' for HARQ ID 1.
\* **"NOK"**: A general negative acknowledgment.
\* **"LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd"**: An indication of receiving a MAC PDU.
\* **"11"**: Appears to be a HARQ feedback indicator.
\* **"Trash PuschReceiveRespHarqU / Ps"**: Suggests that PUSCH receive responses are being discarded.
\* **"CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm"**: A state where the system is waiting for confirmation of CG deactivation. This state involves the gNB flushing pending HARQ CG retransmissions (TB 1a) and ignoring subsequent CG-CRC checks.
\* **"CG-Configured"**: A state indicating that CG is configured and the system should stop scheduling initial CG PUSCH.
\* **"CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder"**: A state where the system is waiting for an order to deactivate CG.
\* **"Possible loss of PUCCH (AN/CSI) because gNB keeps scheduling init CG PUSCH instead of possible PUCCH"**: A critical observation indicating a potential issue where the gNB prioritizes scheduling CG PUSCH over PUCCH for AN/CSI, leading to potential data loss.
The diagram uses different colored lines and boxes to represent various signals, states, and events, providing a visual timeline of the CG operations and potential issues.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5251.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note:

* if gNB is not able to decode the initial CG PUSCH transmission containing the MAC-CE CG Confirmation, the UE will not be able to handle the HARQ retransmission whereas CG has already been deactivated.
* once 5G-L2-PS has prepared the reception of the initial CG PUSCH, the PuschReceiveReq is sent even if MAC-CE CG Confirmation has been received in between. It reduces the number cases to handle.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication protocol timeline, likely within a cellular network context (5G). It depicts the scheduling of data transmission slots (SFN slots) and the associated control channels (PDCCH, CG slot). The diagram also shows different states and events related to Carrier Aggregation (CG) and the UE (User Equipment) and gNB (next-generation NodeB) perspectives.
The top section of the diagram shows a timeline divided into SFN (System Frame Number) slots, labeled with numbers 0, 1, and 2. Within each SFN, individual slots are numbered from 0 to 19. The PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) is represented by colored blocks indicating different states or transmissions. The CG slot (likely a dedicated slot for Carrier Aggregation control) is also shown with colored blocks, some containing numbers (1, 2) or letters (D).
Below the timeline, different communication events and states are described and visually linked to specific points in the timeline using dashed lines.
**Key elements and their likely interpretations:**
\* **SFN slot:** Represents time intervals in the system.
\* **PDCCH:** Control channel used for scheduling and other control information.
\* Black blocks: Likely represent no transmission or a specific state.
\* White blocks: Likely represent a transmission or a specific state.
\* Gray blocks: Likely represent a transmission or a specific state.
\* Orange block with 'D': Possibly indicates a Downlink grant or a specific control message.
\* **CG slot:** Dedicated slots for Carrier Aggregation control.
\* Yellow block with '1': Likely indicates a CG-related control message or event for HARQ ID 1.
\* Green block with '2': Likely indicates a CG-related control message or event for HARQ ID 2.
\* Blue circle around a white block in slot 8 of SFN 1: Labeled "non-CG", suggesting this slot is not used for Carrier Aggregation.
\* **CG PUSCH Harqld 0, CG PUSCH Harqld 1, DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2:** These labels refer to different types of PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) transmissions with specific HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) identifiers. "CG" likely stands for Carrier Aggregation, and "DG" might refer to a different type of aggregation or a specific configuration.
\* **UE view / gNB view:** Differentiates the perspective of the User Equipment and the base station.
\* **5G-L2-PS states:** Refers to states within the 5G Layer 2 Protocol Stack.
\* **CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling:** A state where the system is waiting for a deactivation confirmation. The note "(no more CG reTx as prio < prio Deact req. class)" suggests a condition related to retransmissions and priority.
\* **CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm:** Another state related to deactivation confirmation. The notes "PNB flushes pending HARQ CG reTx" and "Ignore subsequent CG-CRC" indicate actions taken during this state.
\* **CG-Configured:** A state where Carrier Aggregation is configured. The note "Stop scheduling init CG PUSCH" indicates that PUSCH scheduling is stopped for this configuration.
\* **MAC-CE sched:** Likely refers to MAC Control Element scheduling.
\* **MAC-CE CG Conf.:** MAC Control Element for Carrier Aggregation Configuration.
\* **non-active CG UE (UE MAC clears CG act):** Indicates a state where the UE is not actively using Carrier Aggregation, and its MAC layer clears the CG activation.
\* **OK:** A confirmation or successful event.
\* **LoCtrl\_UIMacPduReceiveInd:** Likely an indication of receiving a MAC PDU (Protocol Data Unit) at the User Interface level.
\* **Possible loss of PUCCH (AN/CSI):** A warning or indication of a potential issue with the PUCCH (Physical Uplink Control Channel) for Access Network (AN) or Channel State Information (CSI) transmissions.
The diagram uses different colored dashed lines to connect events and states to specific points in the timeline, illustrating the flow of control and data. The overall diagram seems to be detailing a scenario where Carrier Aggregation is being deactivated or managed, with potential implications for uplink control channel transmissions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5253.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note that this case is much more robust as CG slot case because the UE can repeat the MAC-CE CG confirmation (DG HARQ) although CG has been already deactivated.

**2.4.26.7.0-7.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11176032`)

CG Deactivation
Due to a previous CG Activation, [CB008124-CA] CG Modification [End CB008124-CA] or CG Deactivation attempt failure, the UE may not be able to send the MAC-CE CG Confirmation in the CG slot [CB008124-CA] (or on new RB allocation of the new CG slot) [End CB008124-CA] (refer to 5G\_L2\_11263\_replaced([11175947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGGPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), so the 5G-L2-PS needs to schedule a DG PUSCH.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication protocol, likely within a cellular network context, focusing on the scheduling and state transitions of a "CG" (Carrier Aggregation or Connected Group) entity. The diagram uses a timeline format, with SFN (System Frame Number) slots marked along the top, ranging from 0 to 2, and individual slots numbered within each SFN.
The diagram depicts several layers of information:
1. **PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) / CG slot:** This row shows the status of PDCCH and CG slots. Black and white squares likely represent different types of slots or transmissions. Yellow-highlighted squares with numbers (0, 1, 2) indicate specific events or states related to HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) IDs.
2. **CG PUSCH Harqld 0 & 1:** These sections detail the UE (User Equipment) and gNB (next-generation NodeB, i.e., base station) views for PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) transmissions with HARQ IDs 0 and 1. The diagram indicates when a slot is no longer considered a "CG slot" for the UE and shows an "Unconfirmed state."
3. **DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2:** This section covers the UE view for PUSCH transmissions with HARQ IDs greater than or equal to 2.
4. **5G-L2-PS states:** This row outlines different states related to the 5G Layer 2 Protocol Stack. These states are represented by dashed boxes:
\* `CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder`: This state is indicated by a red dashed border.
\* `CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling`: This state, with a purple dashed border, includes a condition: "(no more CG reTx as prio < prio Deact req. class)".
\* `CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm`: This state, with an orange dashed border, describes actions: "gNB flushes pending HARQ CG reTx (TB 1a)" and "Ignore subsequent CG-CRC".
\* `CG-Configured`: This state, with a blue dashed border, indicates "Stop scheduling init CG PUSCH".
5. **Events and Transitions:** Various colored lines and text annotations indicate events and transitions:
\* A blue oval highlights "MAC-CE sched" (MAC Control Element scheduling) occurring around slots 9-11 of SFN 1.
\* A green rectangle labeled "2 MAC-CE CG Conf." signifies a MAC-CE CG Configuration event.
\* Text like "not a CG slot anymore for the UE" and "DTX" (Discontinuous Transmission) are placed to indicate specific conditions or states.
\* An arrow points from the "CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm" state to "LoCtrl\_UIMacPduReceiveInd", suggesting a trigger.
\* A red arrow at the bottom points to a concluding statement: "Possible loss of PUCCH (AN/CSI) because gNB keeps scheduling init CG PUSCH instead of possible PUCCH". This indicates a potential issue where the gNB prioritizes scheduling initial CG PUSCH over PUCCH (Acknowledgement/Negative Acknowledgement or Channel State Information) transmissions, leading to their loss.
The diagram visually represents a scenario where the gNB's scheduling decisions might lead to the loss of important control information (PUCCH) for the UE, particularly when the CG state is being managed or deactivated.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5257.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

but it may appear that the UE is able to transmit in this slot (and DG PUSCH allocation has been useless):

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication protocol, likely within a cellular network context, showing the timeline of events and states. The top section displays a timeline divided into Super Frame Numbers (SFN) and slots, with SFN 0, 1, and 2 visible, and slots numbered from 15 to 19 in SFN 0, and 0 to 11 in SFN 1, and 0 to 11 in SFN 2.
Below the timeline, there are several rows representing different communication channels and states:
1. **PDCCH CG slot**: This row shows the status of the Physical Downlink Control Channel (PDCCH) for Carrier Group (CG) slots. Black and white blocks indicate scheduled or unscheduled slots, with some slots highlighted with numbers (1, 2) and letters (D). A gray area is labeled "Useless MAC-CE sched".
2. **CG PUSCH Harqld 0**: This section details the UE (User Equipment) and gNB (next-generation NodeB) views for CG PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) with HARQ ID 0.
3. **CG PUSCH Harqld 1**: Similar to the above, but for HARQ ID 1. It shows an "Unconfirmed state" for the gNB view and a "NOK 0a" event.
4. **DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2**: This section covers the UE and gNB views for DG (Data Group) PUSCH with HARQ ID greater than or equal to 2.
5. **5G-L2-PS states**: This row outlines the states of the 5G Layer 2 Protocol Stack. It shows three distinct states represented by dashed boxes:
\* **CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder**: This state is associated with a purple dashed line.
\* **CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling**: This state is associated with a purple dashed line and includes a note about "no CG reTx as prio < Deact prio".
\* **CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm**: This state is associated with an orange dashed line and includes notes about "gNB flushes pending HARQ CG reTx" and "Ignore subsequent CG-CRC".
\* **CG-Configured**: This state is associated with a blue dashed line and indicates "Stop scheduling init CG PUSCH".
Various events and messages are annotated on the diagram with colored text and lines, indicating interactions and transitions between states and channels. Notable annotations include:
\* "UE drops TBOa"
\* "Ob MAC-CE CE Conf."
\* "non-active CG UE (UE MAC clears CG act)"
\* "OK"
\* "LoCtrl\_UIMacPduReceiveInd"
\* "2 just data"
\* "DTX"
A blue oval highlights a section of the timeline around slot 9-10 in SFN 1, encompassing the "Ob MAC-CE CE Conf." and "non-active CG UE" events.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5257.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

[CB008124-CB-UPD001]

If a CG Deconfiguration is pending, a Forced CG overwriting (refer to 5G\_L2\_12515\_replaced([11176267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiW_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for DG PUSCH transmission with CG RBs is required in case UE is no more CG configured.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline illustrating the behavior of Carrier Aggregation (CG) PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) and PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) slots, along with associated states and events. The timeline spans across SFN (System Frame Number) 0 and 1, with slots numbered from 15 to 19 in SFN 0, and 0 to 11 in SFN 1.
The top row represents the SFN and slot numbers. Below this, the PDCCH CG slot status is shown with black indicating a CG slot, white indicating a non-CG slot, and yellow and green indicating specific events or configurations.
The subsequent rows detail the status of CG PUSCH with different HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) IDs:
- **CG PUSCH HarqId 0**: Shows UE view and gNB view. In slot 18 of SFN 0, the gNB view indicates "not a CG slot anymore for the UE" and shows a '0' in a dashed blue box.
- **CG PUSCH HarqId 1**: Shows UE view and gNB view. In slot 1 of SFN 1, the gNB view shows a '1' in a dashed blue box.
- **DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2**: Shows UE view and gNB view.
Various events and states are marked on the timeline:
- **MAC-CE sched**: Appears above slots 9-11 in SFN 1.
- **Forced CG overwriting**: Appears above slots 13-19 in SFN 1.
- **CG overwriting for DG PUSCH**: An oval highlights this event around slot 12 in SFN 1, with "MAC-CE CG Conf." and "non-active CG UE (UE MAC clears CG act)" noted.
- **DTX**: Indicates Discontinuous Transmission, appearing at various points.
- **LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveind**: A dashed blue box indicates this event.
The bottom section outlines the **5G-L2-PS states**:
- **CG-WaitForDeactivation Order**: A dashed box.
- **CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm Scheduling**: A dashed box with "(no more CG reTx as prio < prio Deact req. class)".
- **CG-WaitForDeactivation Confirm**: A dashed box with "gNB flushes pending HARQ CG reTx" and "Ignore subsequent CG-CRC".

**2.4.26.7.0-7.0-6**  (ID: `11176036`)

CG Deactivation
Chronogram of a first CG Deactivation attempt failure due to loss of DCI ( TDD pattern 1:4, UE periodicity = 10 slots)

---

**2.4.26.7.0-7.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11176042`)

CG Deactivation
UE is not able to decode the PDCCH order and remains activated.

In absence of MAC-CE CG Confirmation, 5G-L2-PS keeps on scheduling initial CG PUSCH decoding.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a timeline of events related to cellular network communication, specifically focusing on Control Grant (CG) PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) and PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) interactions. The timeline is organized by SFN (System Frame Number) and slot numbers, ranging from SFN 0 to SFN 2, and slots 0 to 19 within each SFN.
The diagram depicts several parallel tracks representing different aspects of the communication:
1. **PDCCH (UL DCI)**: This track shows the status of the PDCCH, indicated by colored blocks (black, white, gray). Some slots are marked with specific values like 'D' (orange) and '>' (gray).
2. **CG slot (UL)**: This track indicates whether a slot is designated as a CG slot in the uplink, marked with a yellow square containing a number (0 or 1).
3. **CG PUSCH Harqld 0 (UE view / gNB view)**: This section details the status of CG PUSCH with HARQ ID 0 from both the UE's and gNB's perspectives. Events like "NOK 0a" (red) are noted.
4. **CG PUSCH Harqld 1 (UE view / gNB view)**: Similar to the above, but for CG PUSCH with HARQ ID 1. Events like "1a" (blue), "NOK" (red), and "HARQ 1a released" (blue) are shown.
5. **DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2**: This track is for DG (Data Grant) PUSCH with HARQ ID greater than or equal to 2.
6. **5G-L2-PS states**: This section outlines the state transitions of the 5G Layer 2 Protocol Stack. It shows states like "CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder" and "CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirm", along with associated events and conditions such as "gNB flushes pending HARQ CG reTx", "Ignore subsequent CG-CRC", and "Possible loss of PUCCH (AN/CSI)".
Key events and annotations observed in the diagram include:
\* "CG Deact > CG reTX" (orange text) above the PDCCH track.
\* "gNB keeps scheduling init CG PUSCH (instead of possible PUCCH)" (blue text) in the upper part of the timeline.
\* "UE drops TBOa" (orange text) and "Ob Data" (blue text) in the CG PUSCH Harqld 0 section.
\* "UE remains activated" (orange text) and "OK" (green text) in the CG PUSCH Harqld 1 section.
\* "1b" (blue text) in the CG PUSCH Harqld 1 section.
\* "cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer" (red dashed line with arrow) indicating a timer event.
\* "CG Deactivation attempt failure on timer expiry" (red text) with a dashed box.
\* A dashed box containing "CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder or CG-Error (max num of attempt reached)".
The diagram uses different colored lines and text to differentiate between various signals, states, and events. The timeline progresses from left to right, with SFNs and slots providing the temporal context.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5259.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

At cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer expiry, a new CG Deactivation is starting.

Note: 5G-L2-PS does not use possible reception of init CG PUSCH to try a new CG Deactivation attempt before cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer expiry.

* 5G-L2-PS cannot schedule a CG PUSCH retransmission (as explained in 5G\_L2\_11285\_replaced([11176005](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wtJPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the transmission of MAC-CE CG Confirmation over DG PUSCH is much more robust).
