**2.4.26.7.0-7.0-8**  (ID: `11176060`)

CG Deactivation
Chronogram of a CG Deactivation attempt failure due to MAC-CE CG Confirmation mistaken for the one of the previous procedure ( TDD pattern 1:4, UE periodicity = 10 slots)

---

**2.4.26.7.0-7.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11176068`)

CG Deactivation
A previous CG Activation has failed [CB008124-CA] (it could be a CG Modification) [End CB008124-CA] and CG Deactivation is triggered.

A late MAC-CE CG Confirmation of the failing procedure is mistaken for the confirmation of the CG Deactivation (no discriminator as no payload).

[CB008124-B-UPDXXX]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a timing diagram illustrating the signaling and state transitions related to Carrier Aggregation (CG) activation and deactivation in a 5G network. The diagram spans across multiple SFN (System Frame Number) slots, from 0 to 2, and within each SFN, it shows slots from 15 to 19.
The diagram is organized into several horizontal tracks, each representing a different aspect of the communication:
1. **SFN slot**: This is the top-most track, indicating the SFN and slot numbers.
2. **PDCCH (UL DCI)**: This track shows the Downlink Control Information (DCI) transmitted on the Physical Downlink Control Channel (PDCCH) in the Uplink (UL) direction. It displays symbols like 'A' (activation), 'D' (deactivation), '1' (data), and '>' (reserved or other control).
3. **CG slot (UL)**: This track indicates the status of the CG slot in the Uplink. Black indicates active, white indicates inactive, and colored blocks (purple, yellow, orange, red) highlight specific events.
4. **CG PUSCH Harqld 0 / 1**: These tracks represent the Hybrid Automatic Repeat reQuest (HARQ) feedback for the CG Physical Uplink Shared Channel (PUSCH) with HARQ IDs 0 and 1, from both the UE (User Equipment) and gNB (next-generation NodeB) perspectives.
5. **DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2**: This track shows HARQ feedback for DG PUSCH with HARQ ID 2 or greater, from the UE perspective.
6. **5G-L2-PS states**: This track depicts the states of the 5G Layer 2 Protocol Stack (PS).
Key events and messages depicted in the diagram include:
\* **DCI for activation**: A purple block in the PDCCH track at SFN 0, slot 15, labeled 'A', indicates a DCI for CG activation.
\* **MAC-CE CG Conf.**: A blue block labeled 'MAC-CE CG Conf.' appears in the CG PUSCH Harqld 1 track, indicating a MAC Control Element (CE) for CG configuration.
\* **CG activated UE**: A blue line indicates the UE is activated for CG.
\* **NOK**: This indicates a "Not OK" status, suggesting a failure or negative acknowledgment.
\* **DCI for CG Deact**: An orange block in the PDCCH track at SFN 1, slot 15, labeled 'D', signifies a DCI for CG deactivation.
\* **DCI not decoded**: A label indicating that a DCI was not successfully decoded.
\* **0a**: A label indicating a possible lack of decoding.
\* **LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd**: A red oval highlights a potential misinterpretation of a received PDU as a deactivation confirmation, leading to a new deactivation attempt.
\* **Just Data, UE remains activated**: A label indicating that only data was received, and the UE remains activated.
\* **CG Activation failure on timer expiry**: A red dashed arrow points to a CG Activation failure occurring when a timer expires.
\* **CG-WaitForActivationConfirm**: A purple dashed box indicates the UE is in a state waiting for activation confirmation, initiating CG PUSCH decoding.
\* **CG-WaitFor Deactivation Confirm/Order/Scheduling**: Purple and orange dashed boxes represent different states related to CG deactivation, including waiting for confirmation, order, and scheduling.
The diagram illustrates a scenario where an initial CG activation attempt is made, followed by a period where the UE is activated. Subsequently, a deactivation attempt is signaled, but there are indications of potential issues like DCI not being decoded or misinterpretations of control messages, leading to a "new deactivation attempt." The diagram also shows the progression through various waiting states for confirmation and order related to deactivation.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5263.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The UE behavior and the gNB behavior are no more consistent:

* The UE is still transmitting initial CG PUSCH.

If the 5G-L2-PS has prepared the corresponding decoding before scheduling the PDCCH Order, the CG PUSCH will be decoded. Later ones will not be decoded.

* The 5G-L2-PS considers the CG Deactivation as successful.

It is not possible for the 5G-L2-PS to identify a late MAC-CE CG Confirmation.

So, as protection, after each CG Activation [CB008124-CA] or CG Modification [End CB008124-CA] failure, the CG Deactivation procedure is executed twice (refer to 5G\_L2\_11267\_replaced([11175963](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGHPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

[End CB008124-B-UPDXXX]

---

### 2.4.26.8 CG State Machine

#### 2.4.26.8.0-1 CG State Machine CG state machine principles (ID: 11176075)

**2.4.26.8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176081`)

CG State Machine
CG state machine main principles are described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15099\_replaced([11172972](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

One state machine instance is created and initialized per UE, upon reception in UL Scheduler of internal intra-Scheduler message “SgnlToUlCsrntiAllocationInd” when CG activation is requested by CPRT with ulCgConfig = 1, thanks to UserModify  for CG activation during reconfiguration (5G\_L2\_11569\_replaced([11145077](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_239Qy_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [CB008124-E] or UserSetup for CG activation during Handover (5G\_L2\_11662\_replaced([11144332](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_234_a_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [End CB008124-E].

State machine instance is created in state cgInit.

State machine instance for a UE is deleted on reception of PsUser\_UserStopReq, meaning UE deletion, or on reception of PsUser\_RrcReconfCompleteReq with ulCgConfig = 0 & ulCgConfDeconfResult = success, meaning CG is DEconfigured for this UE.

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-2 CG State Machine CG Firing State Definition (ID: 11176085)

**2.4.26.8.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176088`)

CG State Machine
CG 'firing' state is defined in a sense that, from UE perspective, UE is CG activated and CG PUSCH shall be fired by the UE in a CG occasion.

The following states: cgWaitForActivationConfirmation, cgActivated, cgWaitForDeactivationOrder, cgWaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling, cgWaitForDeactivationConfirm, cgWaitForModification, cgWaitForModificationConfirm

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-3 CG State Machine CG state machine transitions (ID: 11176093)

**2.4.26.8.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11176100`)

CG State Machine
Some transitions are described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15117\_replaced([11172982](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Additional transitions are implemented, due to introduction of additional CG state(s) in CP3.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating a process flow. It consists of several rectangular boxes, each representing a state, and arrows connecting these states, indicating transitions. The states are labeled with text.
Here are the states and their corresponding labels:
\* **cqInit** (light purple box)
\* **cqConfigured** (light blue box)
\* **cqWaitForActivation** (light purple box)
\* **cqWaitForActivationConfirmation** (light purple box)
\* **cqActivated** (light green box)
\* **cqError** (yellow box)
\* **cqWaitForModification** (light yellow box)
\* **cqWaitForDeactivationOrder** (light purple box)
\* **cqWaitForDeactivationConfirm** (light purple box)
\* **cqWaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling** (light purple box)
\* **cqWaitForModificationConfirm** (yellow box)
The arrows represent transitions between these states. Some arrows are thick, indicating a more significant or frequent transition, while others are thin. The diagram shows a complex flow with multiple possible paths and feedback loops. For example, there are transitions from `cqInit` to `cqConfigured` and `cqWaitForActivation`. `cqConfigured` has transitions to `cqWaitForActivation` and `cqWaitForModification`. `cqWaitForActivation` transitions to `cqWaitForActivationConfirmation`. `cqWaitForActivationConfirmation` can lead to `cqActivated` or `cqError`. `cqActivated` can transition to `cqWaitForModification` or `cqWaitForDeactivationOrder`. `cqError` can transition back to `cqWaitForActivation`. `cqWaitForModification` can lead to `cqWaitForModificationConfirm` or back to `cqActivated`. `cqWaitForDeactivationOrder` can transition to `cqWaitForDeactivationConfirm` or `cqWaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling`. `cqWaitForDeactivationConfirm` and `cqWaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling` both have transitions that lead back to `cqConfigured`. Finally, `cqWaitForModificationConfirm` has a transition back to `cqConfigured`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5270.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The following CG states have been added:

[CB008124-C]

-cgWaitForActivation to cgConfigured

[End CB008124-C]

[CB008124-CA]

* cgWaitForModification
* cgWaitForModificationConfirm

[End CB008124-CA]

Drawing is stored under <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?csf=1&web=1&e=roJEom&cid=0e191e1f%2Db763%2D419b%2Dab69%2De2763efea843&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2FCB008124%20%2D%205G%5FL2%5FEntity%5FLevel%20Module%20%2D%20Drawings&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c>

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-4 CG State Machine How to describe cg State Machine (ID: 11176105)

**2.4.26.8.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11176107`)

CG State Machine
Below framework to describe a cg state

To be completed after cheking implementation of State Machine framework

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry | Whatever the state we come from, actions done each time entering in state |
| Actions on Exit | Whatever the state we go to, actions done each time exiting from state |
| Actions in State | Performed in state, not leading to a cg state change |
| Event 1: event leading to invoke the State Machine. Could be: - Message received - Message Sent - Timer Expiry - Counter Reaching a given vlaue | Conditions: condition to be checked on Event 1 |
| Actions: actions performed in case of Event 1 received, and if condition above fulfilled. |  |
| Target State: target state after actions above are performed |  |
| Event n: .... | Conditions: |
| Actions: |  |
| Target State: |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-5 CG State Machine cgInit state description (ID: 11176112)

**2.4.26.8.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11176116`)

CG State Machine
In cgInit state, gNB-DU (L2PS) has received CG configuration for the UE, and is waiting for CG configuration completion over RRC

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry | Define & Init to zero cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter cgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounter cgConsecutiveHarqFailureCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation [Before PR701262]cgConsecutiveDtxCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation [PR701262]cgConsecutiveDtxOrWithoutDataCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation[End PR701262]     Define timers: cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer cgSinrMonitoringPeriodTimer |
| Actions on Exit |  |
| Actions in State |  |
| Event: reception of Msg PsUser\_RrcReconfCompleteReq | Conditions: parameter ulCgConfig=1 and ulCgConfDeconfResult=“success” |
| Actions: |  |
| Target State: cgConfigured |  |
| [CB008124-E] Internal Event: RandomAccessInd [End CB008124-E] | Conditions: |
| Actions: [PR707635] Perform CG Harq reservation [End PR707635] |  |
| Target State: cgConfigured |  |
| [CB008124-C] Event: reception of Msg PsUser\_UserModifyReq [End CB008124-C] | Conditions: parameter ulCgConfigType2. ulCgConfig=2 “CG configuration cancelation”  (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_4008\_replaced([10976380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VF9q_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) |
| Actions: |  |
| Target State: Release state machine |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-6 CG State Machine cgConfigured state description (ID: 11176122)

**2.4.26.8.0-6.0-3**  (ID: `12822818`)

CG State Machine
In **cgConfigured state, CG is configured at both gNB-DU (L2PS) side and UE side, and UE is monitored for CG activation, waiting for fulfilled conditions for CG Activation.**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions **on Entry** | **Define & Init** cgAvgUlUeDataRate = 0 numOfSlotsSinceLastUlPdu = 1000 \* 2^u **Start timer *cgSinrMonitoringPeriodTimer = rdCgSinrMonitoringPeriod\_slot*** *isDeactivationConfirmToSchedule = False* |
| Actions **on Exit** | ~~Stop timer~~ ~~cgSinrMonitoringPeriodTimer~~ ~~(for robustness, in case of exit state when timer still running)~~ |
| Actions **in State** | Update cgAvgUlUeDataRate (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19256\_replaced([11173186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuRkBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) with the amount of non-padding data transmitted if a UL MAC PDU reception from L2 LO   After cgSinrMonitoringPeriodTimer expiry  an update in rrmCorrAvgSinr or a the expected rank change, nrUlCgPrb & MSC are calculated (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14664\_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) CG Data rate calculation and minimum CG data rate condition check (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15204\_replaced([11173172](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuRkA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) |
| Event: **SINR Update** | Conditions: *cgSinrMonitoringPeriodTimer timer is not running,* minimum CG Data rate is reached, determination of nrUlCgPrb & MCS (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14664\_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) succeeded, UE is in the largest dedicated BWP (5G\_UP\_ALG\_15121\_replaced([11173004](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **UE DRX is disabled or enabled and already passed the beginning of first complete DRX cycle after DRX enabled.,** CG activation process is not suspended by the cell level CG activation control state specified in 5G\_L2\_12197\_replaced([11176727](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_262MtfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case UL Buffer split is triggered CG pattern must not always conflict with SCell UL slots as specified in 5G\_L2\_13265\_replaced([11176763](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_262MvfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). UE is not in GBR congestion due to PUSCH [CB014088-B] the cell in which UE is attached is not in overload defined in [12689381: CG Activation During UP Overload](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_UiJR01ZiEfC45IKMrv0u7Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)  [End CB014088-B] |
| Actions: |  |
| Target State: **cgWaitForActivation** |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-7 CG State Machine cgWaitForActivation state description (ID: 11176132)

**2.4.26.8.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `12745643`)

CG State Machine
In **cgWaitForActivation state, an Initial DG TX is preempted to send a DCI 0.1 for CG activation.**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry | HARQ Process Id related to pending ReTx and belonging to the range [0..nrOfHARQProcesses-1] shall be released Start cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer to rdCgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer\_ms (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15620\_replaced([11173576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUnVPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))) |
| Actions on Exit |  |
| Actions in State | Actions done in UL FDM scheduler: 1. Preemption of Initial DG TX for CG activation 2. Check PDCCH availability for Initial DG Ul Transmission 3. Check whether slot N is eligible for sending the CG activation DCI. If slot N is not eligible, the sending of the CG activation DCI is delayed to a next UL slot 4. in every CG occasion slot of the CG pattern being started, the number of UEs with PRB booked for CG transmission  in the slot is < MAX(0, MIN (NIDD: ulCgMaxNumUeUl, rrmMaxNumUesFdmUl - rdMinNonCgSchedReservedPerSlot (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19199\_replaced([11173592](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuUnWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))))) Defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15123\_replaced([11173014](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuPu2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). If the CG resource allocation is performed between the ulMetricInd sending (i.e. slot 0 of the 1st SFN of the pooling period) and the resourceResp reception from BBRM (slot 3 of the 1st SFN of the pooling period), the number of UEs with PRB booked for CG transmission in those 4 slots (i.e. from slot 0 up to slot 3 of the 1st SFN of the pooling period) can't exceed the maxNcg value provided to BBRM in ulMetricInd 5. If CG Prb allocation fail or checks fails Activation is delayed to next UL slot and UE is considered for dynamic scheduling Else Continue 6. Set the FD data and add UE in the toBeScheduleList \* if the CG resource allocation is done between the ulMetricInd sending (slot0 of the 1st SFN of the pooling period) and the resource resp reception (slot3 of the 1st SFN of the pooling period) The total number of CG UEs in those 4 slots (slot 0 up to slot 3 of 1st SFN of the pooling period) can't exceed the maxNcg value provided to BBRM in ulMetricInd Actions done in FD scheduler: If DCI for CG Activation cannot be sent due to lack of HARQ Process Id Fire 'DciIsNotSent' event to release CG Prb resource and prevent leakage else Send DCI 0.1 for CG Activation Fire 'DciIsSent' event to change state |
| Event: DciIsNotSent | Conditions: |
| Actions: release CG PRB resource and prevent leakage |  |
| Target State: |  |
| Event: DciIsSent | Conditions: |
| Actions: stop cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer if cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter == 0 Counter UL\_CG\_ACT\_ATT M55308C00020 shall be incremented. |  |
| Target State: cgWaitForActivationConfirmation |  |
| Event: Internal Event: expiry of timer cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer | Conditions: |
| Actions: Invalidate CG validity as function of the SINR. |  |
| Target State: cgConfigured |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: expiry of timer rdCgType2DeactivationTimer [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: none. |
| Actions: \* Free CG PRB resources → update cgAllocationTable. \* Set cgErrorCause to cgDeactivationTimerExpired. |  |
| Target State: cgError |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-8 CG State Machine cgWaitForActivationConfirmation state description (ID: 11176139)

**2.4.26.8.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `12745651`)

CG State Machine
In **cgWaitForActivationConfirmation state, DCI 0.1 for CG Activation has been sent to the UE, waiting for reception of MAC CE Configured Grant Confirmation.**

In this state, CG Pusch scheduling is performed.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry | \* Set isNonDtxCG = false \* Start timer configuredGrantTimer = calculated value  (5G\_L2\_9075\_replaced([11175685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26uQ4fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))) \* Create and start timer cgMacCeConfirmationforCgActivationDeactivationtimer = rdCgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer\_slot \* Reset cgConsecutiveHarqFailureCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation \* "Proactive Grant Type" is set to Disabled and stop procact |
| Actions on Entry |  |
| Actions on Exit | ~~Set~~ ~~isNonDtxCG~~ ~~= false~~ |
| Actions in State | CG PUSCH Scheduling is performed as described in 5G\_L2\_10918\_replaced([11176220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiUPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) CG HARQ U is handled as described in 5G\_L2\_10884\_replaced([11176371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zwcfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) |
| Event: LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd received PeerCtrl\_ScellUlInfoUpdateInd received | Conditions: parameter isConfiguredGrantConfCe = true |
| Actions: reset isNonDtxCg Stop timer cgMacCeConfirmationforCgActivationDeactivationtimer doors://esdooapv51.emea.nsn-net.net:36655/?version=2&prodID=0&view=00000001&urn=urn:telelogic::1-4017b9fe29051a61-O-8232-00020a03 Counter UL\_CG\_ACT\_ATT\_SUCC M55308C00021 shall be incremented. |  |
| Target State: cgActivated |  |
| Event: Internal Event: expiry of timer cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer | Conditions: cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter < rdCgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounterMax  - 1 AND isNonDtxCG = false (all pusch in CG slot were DTX) |
| Actions: \* Increment cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter \* Free CG PRB resources => update cgAllocationTable (since slot offset changed) \* "Proactive Grant Type" is set to initial value and start procact Timer |  |
| Target State: cgWaitForActivation |  |
| Event: Internal Event: expiry of timer cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer | Conditions:  isNonDtxCG = true (at least 1 pusch in CG slot was Non DTX) |
| Actions: \* Increment cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter \* Set cgDeactivationCause = Standard \* Since MacCe has not been received CG is considered not activated. |  |
| Target State: cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| Event: Internal Event: expiry of timer cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer | Conditions: cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter = rdCgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounterMax - 1 AND isNonDtxCG = false (all pusch in CG slot were DTX) |
| Actions: Free CG PRB resources => update cgAllocationTable |  |
| Target State: cgError |  |
| Event: cgConsecutiveHarqFailureCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation = rdCgConsecutiveHarqFailureCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivationMax | Conditions: |
| Actions: Set cgDeactivationCause = Standard |  |
| Target State: cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq received [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: see [11175928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGFPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). |
| Actions: Set cgDeactivationCause to reconfigWithDeactivation. |  |
| Target State: cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: expiry of timer rdCgType2DeactivationTimer [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: none. |
| Actions: \* Free CG PRB resources → update cgAllocationTable. \* Set cgErrorCause to cgDeactivationTimerExpired. |  |
| Target State: cgError |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-9 CG State Machine cgActivated state description (ID: 11176150)

**2.4.26.8.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `12745653`)

CG State Machine
In **cgActivated state, the MAC-CE containing the Configured Grant Confirmation has been received. Scheduling of PUSCH is processed according to if time N+k2 correspond to a CG or non-CG slot. Moreover, there is a monitoring of the UL HARQ Feedback to keep the UE in** cgActivated state or to change it to **cgWaitForDeactivationOrder state**\*\*\*\*

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry | \* Reset cgConsecutiveHarqFailureCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation to zero \* Reset cgConsecutiveDtxOrWithoutDataCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation to zero \* Reset cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter \* - Reset the monitoring window for CG Link Adaptation (nCgHarqAckNackInd, nAckInd, nNackInd are set to 0) |
| Actions on Exit | None |
| Actions in State | Each time N+k2 is CG slot: \* CG PUSCH scheduling is handled as described in 5G\_L2\_10918\_replaced([11176220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiUPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) Each time N+k2 is non-CG slot: \* If UL data ready to be received, the UE is scheduled in DG PUSCH as usual CG HARQ U is handled as described in 5G\_L2\_10884\_replaced([11176371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zwcfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) CG Link Adaptation is triggered by the CG monitoring mechanism: \* If CG LA is not able to provide a valid MCS, number of RBs, UL rank \* Fire 'TriggerWaitForDeactivationOrderByLa' event to change state \* If CG LA is able to provide a new set of LA parameters \* if radio conditions improved (i.e. better MCS or higher UL rank) § deltaSinrCgStatus = stepUp \* else § deltaSinrCgStatus =stepDown \* Fire 'TriggerWaitForModificationByLa' event to change state \* If CG LA doesn’t change the current CG parameters (MCS/RB/UL rank) \* deltaSinrCgStatus = none \* stay in cgActuvated state |
| Event cgConsecutiveHarqFailureCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation = rdCgConsecutiveHarqFailureCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivationMax | Conditions |
| Actions |  |
| Target State: change state to cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| Event cgConsecutiveDtxOrWithoutDataCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation = rdCgConsecutiveDtxOrWithoutDataCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivationMax | Conditions |
| Actions |  |
| Target State: change state to cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| Event TriggerWaitForDeactivationOrderByLa | Conditions: None |
| Actions: None |  |
| Target State: change state to cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| Event TriggerWaitForModificationByLa | Conditions: None |
| Actions: None |  |
| Target State: change state to cgWaitForModification |  |
| Event triggerCgDeactivationDueToUlBufferSplit | Conditions: None |
| Actions: None |  |
| Target State: change state to cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq received [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: see [11175928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGFPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). |
| Actions: Set cgDeactivationCause to reconfigWithDeactivation. |  |
| Target State: cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: expiry of timer rdCgType2DeactivationTimer [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: none. |
| Actions: \* Free CG PRB resources → update cgAllocationTable. \* Set cgErrorCause to cgDeactivationTimerExpired. |  |
| Target State: cgError |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-10 CG State Machine cgWaitForDeactivationOrder state description (ID: 11176159)

**2.4.26.8.0-10.0-2**  (ID: `12745659`)

CG State Machine
In **cgWaitForDeactivationOrder state, conditions for CG Deactivation are fulfilled, waiting for DCI 0.0 for CG Deactivation sending**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry | Add UE in cgDeactivation request class |
| Actions on Exit | if cgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounter == 0 and UE is CG activated Counter UL\_CG\_DEACT\_ATT M55308C00022 shall be incremented. |
| Actions in State | Actions done in UL FDM scheduler: \* Allocate Pdcch resources to send  DCI 0\_0 addressed to CS-RNTI \* add UE in the toBeScheduleList Actions done in FD scheduler: \* Send DCI 0.0 for CG Deactivation Trigger 'DciForDeactivationSent' event CG HARQ U is handled as described in 5G\_L2\_10884\_replaced([11176371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zwcfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) |
| Event: DciForDeactivationSent | Conditions: N+K2 is not CG slot or configuredGrantTimer running or cgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounter != 0 or isDeactivationConfirmToSchedule == TRUE |
| Actions: |  |
| Target State: cgWaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling |  |
| Event: DciForDeactivationSent | Conditions: N+K2 is CG slot and configuredGrantTimer not running and isDeactivationConfirmToSchedule == FALSE |
| Actions: |  |
| Target State: cgWaitForDeactivationConfirm |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: expiry of timer rdCgType2DeactivationTimer [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: none. |
| Actions: \* Free CG PRB resources → update cgAllocationTable. \* Set cgErrorCause to cgDeactivationTimerExpired. |  |
| Target State: cgError |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-11 CG State Machine cgWaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling state description (ID: 11176168)

**2.4.26.8.0-11.0-2**  (ID: `12745660`)

CG State Machine
In **cgWaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling state , for the CG UE which has a request class = cgDeactivation it checks the availability of resources PDCCH and PUSCH to send a dynamic grant transmission addressed to a C-Rnti**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry |  |
| Actions on Exit | None |
| Actions in State | Actions done in UL FDM Scheduler For a CG UE with request class = cgDeactivation, UE is added to the toBeScheduledList if conditions below are succedded, \* Allocation of Pdcch resources to send DCI 0\_1 addressed to C-RNTI. \* Allocation of PRBs resources for UlData\_PuschReceiveReq if slot N+K2 is a non CG slot, otherwise, re-utilisation of CG PRBs resources. Actions done in UL FD Scheduler \* DCI 0\_1 is sent for DG transmission \* UlData\_PuschReceiveReq addressed to C-Rnti is sent. \* Trigger 'DGTransmissionForDeactivationSent' CG HARQ U is handled as described in 5G\_L2\_10884\_replaced([11176371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zwcfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) |
| Event: Reception of LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd | Conditions: isConfiguredGrantConfCe == true |
| Actions: \* Update cgAllocationTables in releasing CG PRB resources related to the deactivated CG \* Free HARQ Process Id related to the PUSCH scheduled to receive the MAC CE CG Confirmation \* Cancel all pending CG ReTx for this UE \* Free all CG HARQ Processes Id related to pending CG ReTx \* "Proactive Grant Type" is set to initial value and start procact Timer \* Leave request class cgDeactivation |  |
| Target State: change state to cgConfigured |  |
| Event: DGTransmissionForDeactivationSent | Conditions: None |
| Actions: None |  |
| Target State: change state to cgWaitForDeactivationConfirm |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: expiry of timer rdCgType2DeactivationTimer [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: none. |
| Actions: \* Free CG PRB resources → update cgAllocationTable. \* Set cgErrorCause to cgDeactivationTimerExpired. |  |
| Target State: cgError |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-12 CG State Machine cgWaitForDeactivationConfirm state description (ID: 11176176)

**2.4.26.8.0-12.0-2**  (ID: `12745662`)

CG State Machine
In **cgWaitForDeactivationConfirm state, DCI 0.0 for CG Deactivation has been sent to the UE, waiting for reception of MAC CE Configured Grant Confirmation.**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry | \* Start timer cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer with duration rdCgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer\_slot \* Remove UE from request class cgDeactivation \* Cancel all pending CG ReTx for this UE \* Free all CG HARQ Processes Id related to pending CG ReTx |
| Actions on Exit | None |
| Actions in State | CG HARQ U is handled as described in 5G\_L2\_10884\_replaced([11176371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zwcfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) |
| Event: Reception of LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd PeerCtrl\_ScellUlInfoUpdateInd received | Conditions: isConfiguredGrantConfCe == true AND cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter < rdCgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounterMax |
| Actions: \* Stop timer cgMacCeConfirmationforCgActivationDeactivationtimer \* Reset cgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounter \* Update cgAllocationTables in releasing CG PRB resources related to the deactivated CG \* ~~Free~~ ~~HARQ Process Id~~ ~~related to the PUSCH scheduled to receive the CE CG Confirmation~~ if UE CG is activated and UL\_CG\_DEACT\_ATT has been pegged \* Counter UL\_CG\_DEACT\_ATT\_SUCC M55308C00023 shall be incremented. \* "Proactive Grant Type" is set to initial value and start procact Timer. \* Release all PRBs related to the C-RNTI, including the PRBs of the previous pattern not overlapping with the allocation of the new pattern in case of CG modification failure |  |
| Target State: change state to cgConfigured |  |
| Event: Reception of LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd | Conditions: isConfiguredGrantConfCe == true AND cgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounter == rdCgConsecutiveActivationAttemptFailureCounterMax |
| Actions: \* Stop timer cgMacCeConfirmationforCgActivationDeactivationtimer \* Reset cgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounter \* Update cgAllocationTables in releasing CG PRB resources related to the deactivated CG \* ~~Free~~ ~~HARQ Process Id~~ ~~related to the PUSCH scheduled to receive the CE CG Confirmation~~ if UE CG is activated and UL\_CG\_DEACT\_ATT has been pegged \* Counter UL\_CG\_DEACT\_ATT\_SUCC M55308C00023 shall be       incremented. \* "Proactive Grant Type" is set to initial value and start procact Timer. \* Release all PRBs related to the C-RNTI, including the PRBs of the previous pattern not overlapping with the allocation of the new pattern in case of CG modification failure |  |
| Target State: change state to cgError |  |
| Event: expiry of timer cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer | Conditions: If cgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounter < rdCgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounterMax |
| Actions: Increment cgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounter |  |
| Target State: change state to cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| Event: expiry of timer cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer | Conditions: If cgConsecutivedeactivationAttemptFailureCounter == rdCgConsecutiveDeactivationAttemptFailureCounterMax |
| Actions: Update cgAllocationTables in releasing CG PRB resources related to the deactivated CG |  |
| Target State: change state to cgError |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: expiry of timer rdCgType2DeactivationTimer [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: none. |
| Actions: \* Free CG PRB resources → update cgAllocationTable. \* Set cgErrorCause to cgDeactivationTimerExpired. |  |
| Target State: cgError |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-13 CG State Machine cgWaitForModification state description (ID: 11176186)

**2.4.26.8.0-13.0-3**  (ID: `12745668`)

CG State Machine
In **cgWaitForModification state, an Initial DG TX is preempted to send a DCI 0.1 for CG modification.**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry | \* Start timer cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer with duration rdCgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer\_ms \* Add UE in request class cgModification |
| Actions on Exit | Remove UE from request class cgModification |
| Actions in State | Actions done in UL FDM scheduler: \* Check PDCCH availability for Initial DG UL Transmission \* If CG slot offset of the new CG pattern is different to the CG slot offset of the current CG pattern \* Check whether slot N is eligible for sending the CG Modification DCI as per 5G\_L2\_10375\_replaced([11175760](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u38_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) \* If slot N is not eligible, the sending of the CG Modification DCI is delayed to a next eligible CG slot \* if UE is not in transient state and if highest UE priority class in UL scheduler is cgModification \* Reserve CG PRBs for the new CG pattern as per 5G\_L2\_11102\_replaced([11176532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260XjvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).  If the CG resource allocation is performed between the ulMetricInd sending (i.e. slot 0 of the 1st SFN of the pooling period) and the resourceResp reception from BBRM (slot 3 of the 1st SFN of the pooling period), the number of UEs with PRB booked for CG transmission in those 4 slots (i.e. from slot 0 up to slot 3 of the 1st SFN of the pooling period) can't exceed the maxNcg value provided to BBRM in ulMetricInd \* If PRB reservation fails or If slot N is not eligible (same condition as for CG activation as per 5G\_L2\_10375\_replaced([11175760](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u38_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))) \* As long as cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer is running, UE shall be scheduled according to the UE highest priority class other than cgModification (e.g. cgHarqRetrans), If HarqId for which CG ReTx is pending \* Else \* Set the FD data and add UE in the toBeScheduledList as per 5G\_L2\_11311\_replaced([11176384](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zwdPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) \* Cancel all pending ReTx with TBS based on the old CG pattern and free all CG Harq Processes Id ( i.e. pending or requiring a retransmission) If slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE, the resources are considered in terms of Layer PRBs and handling is as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15622\_replaced([11173051](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuQV4_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). Actions done in FD scheduler: \* Send DCI 0.1 for CG Modification \* Send PUSCH Receive Req for MAC CE CG confirmation reception \* Increment by 1 PM counter M55308C00024 - No of UL CG modification attempts \* Fire 'DciIsSent' event to change state |
| Event: DciSent | Conditions: None |
| Actions: Stop cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer |  |
| Target State: change state to cgWaitForModificationConfirm |  |
| Event: expiry of timer cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer | Conditions: If improvedRadioCondition == TRUE |
| Actions: None |  |
| Target State: change state to cgActivated |  |
| Event: expiry of timer cgMaxWaitForCgActivationModificationSchedulingTimer | Conditions: If improvedRadioCondition == FALSE |
| Actions: isDeactivationConfirmToSchedule = TRUE |  |
| Target State: change state to cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq received [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: see [11175928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGFPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). |
| Actions: Set cgDeactivationCause to reconfigWithDeactivation. |  |
| Target State: cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: expiry of timer rdCgType2DeactivationTimer [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: none. |
| Actions: \* Free CG PRB resources → update cgAllocationTable. \* Set cgErrorCause to cgDeactivationTimerExpired. |  |
| Target State: cgError |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-14 CG State Machine cgWaitForModificationConfirm state description (ID: 11176201)

**2.4.26.8.0-14.0-2**  (ID: `12745670`)

CG State Machine
In **cgWaitForModificationConfirm state, DCI 0.1 for CG modification has been sent to the UE, waiting for reception of MAC CE Configured Grant Confirmation.**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry | Start cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer with duration rdCgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer\_slot |
| Actions on Exit |  |
| Actions in State | CG HARQ U is handled as described in 5G\_L2\_10884\_replaced([11176371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zwcfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) |
| Event: LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd received [PR724048] PeerCtrl\_ScellUlInfoUpdateInd received [End PR724048] | Conditions: if isConfiguredGrantConfCe = True |
| Actions: \* Stop cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer \* Release the PRBs of the previous pattern not overlapping with the allocation of the new pattern \* Increment by 1 PM counter M55308C00025 - No of successful UL CG modification attempts |  |
| Target State: change state to cgActivated |  |
| Event: expiry of timer cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer | Conditions: None |
| Actions: isDeactivationConfirmToSchedule = True |  |
| Target State: change state to cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq received [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: see [11175928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGFPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9). |
| Actions: Set cgDeactivationCause to reconfigWithDeactivation. |  |
| Target State: cgWaitForDeactivationOrder |  |
| [CB013388-C] Event: expiry of timer rdCgType2DeactivationTimer [End CB013388-C] | Conditions: none. |
| Actions: \* Free CG PRB resources → update cgAllocationTable. \* Set cgErrorCause to cgDeactivationTimerExpired. |  |
| Target State: cgError |  |

---

#### 2.4.26.8.0-15 CG State Machine cgError state description (ID: 11176210)

**2.4.26.8.0-15.0-1**  (ID: `11176215`)

CG State Machine
In cgError state, Ue is still CG Configured (from RRC point of view), has a CS-RNTI allocated, but no reserved PRBs for CG. In this state, UE is exclusively scheduled in DG mode, with usage of HarqId < nrOfHarqProcesses allowed for DG.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Actions on Entry | Cancel all pending CG ReTx for this UE Free all CG HARQ Processes Id related to pending CG ReTx [CB008124-C] -  "Proactive Grant Type" is set to initial value and start procact Timer [End CB008124-C] |
| Actions on Exit | NA |
| Actions in State |  |
| Event: NA | Conditions: NA |
| Actions: NA |  |
| Target State: NA |  |

---

### 2.4.26.9 CG PUSCH scheduling

#### 2.4.26.9.1 CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot

##### 2.4.26.9.1.0-1 CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot Building the cgUeListPerSlot (ID: 11176228)

**2.4.26.9.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176232`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
The cgUeListPerSlot stores all the CG UEs scheduled for initial CG PUSCH in the current slot.

Every slot, this list is build based on the cgSlotAllocationTable (giving a list of UE RNTI eligible to CG per slot, refer to 5G\_L2\_10932\_replaced([11176604](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-mvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and excluding UEs [CB008124-D] in Measurement Gap or [End CB008124-D] in a blocked CG slot (refer to configuredGrantTimer in 5G\_L2\_10781\_replaced([11175620](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26tp0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

This list is allocated at each slot during the FDM process at the preparation step.

When building the sub-areas, the CG RBs of the UEs are considered as busy and not included in sub-areas.

---

##### 2.4.26.9.1.0-2 CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot Initialization of the To-Be-Scheduled-List and update of cs2List with CG UEs (ID: 11176237)

**2.4.26.9.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176242`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
Initialization of the To-Be-Scheduled-List with cgUeListPerSlot and update of cs2List in case of suspected UCI mux issue

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12713169`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
5G-L2-PS loops on UE in the *cgUeListPerSlot  (refer to 5G\_L2\_10939\_replaced([11176228](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiUvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) during the preparation step of FDM process:*

For each UE in the list:

- In unlikely case UE Pooling budget or PRB pooling budget is exceeded (on polling budget reduction), then the UE is not added to the To-Be-Scheduled list and continue with the next UE, if any.

**- . If there is a suspicion that CG PRB allocation may not be not able to accommodate UCI multiplexing with data (refer to 5G\_L2\_12941\_replaced([11176255](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiWPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),** add this UE to the *cs2List (although UE is not part of cs1List) with the priority specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15617\_replaced([11198720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (so that FDM process can later check whether CG PRB allocation is less than minimum number of required PRB for multiplexing according to exact UCI size and evaluate CG overwriting in this case). Condition for suspicion and conditions for adding UE in cs2List are detailed in 5G\_L2\_12941\_replaced([11176255](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiWPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*\*\*

- If the UE is already in the *cs2List with a priority higher or equal to this priority, the UE is not added.*

- If  the UE is already in the *cs2List but with a lower priority than the one expected for UCI multiplexing issue, the UE is promoted i.e. moved from old to new priority.*

Note: if there is no more room in the cs2List, the UE with the lowest priority is removed from the *cs2List before.*

. Otherwise, if this UE is eligible to Forced CG overwriting (refer 5G\_L2\_12515\_replaced([11176267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiW_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), allocate PDCCH resource and **add this UE to the *To-Be-Scheduled-List***

- [Before PR837907] If not selected for Forced CG overwriting reason, [End PR837907] A**dd this UE to the *To-Be-Scheduled-List \*\*for scheduling of the initial CG PUSCH.***\*\*

Compute the HARQ Process Id associated to this CG slot and if any, release the pending HARQ process with same Process Id.

Note: On a CG Modification, once the DCI for CG re-activation has been sent, only the new CG slot is considered as a CG slot (refer to 5G\_L2\_11023\_replaced([11175832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfAvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11176255`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
UE added to the cs2List in case of suspicion of UCI and data multiplexing issue with current CG PRB allocation

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11176263`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
There is a suspicion of UCI and data multiplexing issue with current CG PRB allocation when:

* A PCSI report is expected in current slot or
* At least 4 Secondary Cells are configured for this UE (DL HARQ feedback).

In this case, if the UE is in DRX active state, the UE is inserted in the cs2list or promoted when already in cs2List with a lower prio (refer to position in 5G\_L2\_11066\_replaced([11176279](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiYfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [PR773509], but the UE is inserted only when conditions below are fulfilled (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15171\_replaced([11198700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwJzXPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for conditions to evaluate the UE for CG overwriting)

* request class is different from cgHarqRetrans => check on PriorityIndex
* request class is not cgdeactivation => check on cgState different from cgWaitForDeactivationConfirm
* request class is not cgModification => check on cgState different from cgWaitForModificationConfirm

[End PR773509].

Then, the default mechanism applies in the UE FDM selection :

* Compute the minimum number of PRB required for UCI multiplexing with data over init DG PUSCH (depending on the computed number of UCI bits i.e. PCSI, ACK/NACK bits)
* The CG overwriting should be scheduled only if this number is greater than CG PRB allocation (and the UE is not scheduled for a CG modification).

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11176267`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
Forced CG overwriting when CG deconfiguration is pending

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11176275`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
A UE is in 'Forced Overwriting' condition, meaning that it should be scheduled over DG PUSCH in CG slots (using CG RBs) irrespective of the UE need compared to the CG TBS, if a CG deconfiguration is pending (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15617\_replaced([11198720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) [Before PR815362] and the UE is not already in the CS2 list [End PR815362].

Notes: It addresses the case where the UE cannot transmit the RRC Reconfiguration complete if all UL slots are CG slots but UE is no more CG configured.

If [Before PR815362] UE is not in (uplink) Transient phase of RRC Reconfiguration [PR815362] the UE is not in cgDeactivation request class [End PR815362], a free PDCCH candidate is available and the UE is in DRX active state, the UE is scheduled on CG RBs considering the DG Link Adaptation.

It should be noted that other conditions equivalent to prescheduler checks (such as Measurement Gap) have been checked before adding the UE to the cgUeListPerSlot.

---

##### 2.4.26.9.1.0-3 CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot Scheduling a CG PUSCH according to cs2List in a CG slot (ID: 11176279)

**2.4.26.9.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11176285`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
Loop on cs2List content and check UE eligibility to CG overwriting

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12713148`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
It assumes that the *To-Be-Scheduled-List has been initialized and* *cs2List updated as per 5G\_L2\_13150\_replaced([11176237](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiVPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*\*\*

Loop on cs2List content:

- A non-CG UE is processed as usual.

- A CG UE in a non-CG slot (or blocked CG slot) is processed as per 5G\_L2\_11313\_replaced([11176346](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zJbPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- A CG UE in a CG slot UE has already been added to the *To-Be-Scheduled-List for initial CG PUSCH transmission* *[Before PR837907**] or Forced CG overwriting (refer to 5G\_L2\_12515\_replaced([11176267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiW_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))* *[End PR837907**] at list initialization* (refer to 5G\_L2\_13150\_replaced([11176237](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiVPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).***\****\*\*\*

According to the request class the UE is assigned to, the following CG overwriting reasons are possible:

. for CG PUSCH retransmission (5G\_L2\_11314\_replaced([11176301](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zJYvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

. for CG Deactivation (5G\_L2\_11317\_replaced([11176310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zJZPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

. **[CB008124-CA] for CG Modification** if **new CG RB can be allocated (5G\_L2\_11023\_replaced([11175832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfAvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))** [End CB008124-CA]***\****\*

. **[CB008124-CB] for initial DG PUSCH transmission (5G\_L2\_11316\_replaced([11176319](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zJZvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) if the UE buffer cannot be drained by the CG TBS.**

. for DG PUSCH retransmission (5G\_L2\_11457\_replaced([11176329](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zJaPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[End CB008124-CB]**

. **[CB008124-D] for A-CSI scheduling.**

Note: When k2Acsi is different from k2 (TDD), the scheduling of ACSI is not supported in CG slots, so the same UE cannot be inserted twice in the list. **[End CB008124-D]**

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the process of configuring Carrier Aggregation (CG) for a User Equipment (UE) in a 5G network. The flowchart starts with the initialization of `cgUeListPerSlot` and then branches based on various conditions related to CG slots, UE request classes, and the availability of PDCCH.
The flowchart details the steps involved in CG activation, deactivation, retransmission (reTX), and re-activation. It outlines the conditions under which CG overwrite occurs and the subsequent actions taken, such as computing resource requirements (`nRbMinOwn`, `nRbMaxOw`), allocating PDCCH, and distributing PRBs.
Key elements and processes depicted include:
\* **Initialization:** `cgUeListPerSlot` is built and initialized with `To-Be-Scheduled-List`.
\* **Loop on UEs:** The process iterates through UEs in `cs2List`.
\* **CG Slot Check:** A decision point determines if the current slot is a "CG slot".
\* **UE Request Class:** The flowchart branches based on the UE's request class (e.g., `cgDeactivate`, `cgHarqRetrans`, `cgModification`, others).
\* **CG Overwrite Conditions:** Various "CG overwrite" boxes indicate scenarios where specific configurations are applied.
\* **PDCCH Availability:** Checks for PDCCH availability in USS and whether the UE is in a transient phase are crucial decision points.
\* **Resource Calculation:** `nRbMinOwn` and `nRbMaxOw` are computed for DG reTX and UE eligibility in FDM.
\* **RB Allocation:** The flowchart describes the allocation of new RBs in the CG pool, including considerations for upscaling and potential overlaps.
\* **Standard UE Selection:** A step for "Standard UE selection in FDM" is shown.
\* **Finalization:** The process concludes with "End of processing for this UE" and a note that "Later UE rejection is not allowed".
Specific codes and references are mentioned throughout the flowchart, such as `5G_L2_10939`, `5G_L2_13150`, `8124-CB-UPD001`, `8124-D-UPD002`, `5G_L2_11313`, `5G_L2_11263`, `5G_L2_11457`, `8124-CB`, `5G_L2_11316`, `8124-CA`, `5G_L2_11023`, `8124-D2`, `5G_L2_11450`, `8124-D`, and `5G_L2_11317`.
The flowchart uses standard flowchart symbols like rectangles for processes, diamonds for decisions, and arrows to indicate the flow of control. The text is clear and legible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5312.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

If eligibility to CG overwriting is confirmed, the UE remains in the *To-Be-Scheduled-List  but the scheduling reason is updated.*

Refer to the call flow diagram in 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_2417\_replaced([11208119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Bgv7wvvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: On a CG Modification, once the DCI for CG re-activation has been sent, the CG RB of former CG allocation are considered as free for PRB allocator (refer to 5G\_L2\_11023\_replaced([11175832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfAvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11176301`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
CG Overwriting for CG Retransmission

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176305`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
A UE in cgHarqRetrans request class has at least one pending CG HARQ to retransmit. The one with the highest priority (considering sub-priority defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15182\_replaced([11194497](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv1qRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is selected regardless of the HARQ process Id of the current CG slot.

Apart from PDCCH allocation in USS, no new RB allocation is required as the HARQ TBS is consistent with the current CG RB allocation that is used, refer to the diagram in 5G\_L2\_13185\_replaced([11176285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zJYPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008124-C] Adaptive retrans is deactivated for CG HARQs. [CB008124-CA] Therefore, on a CG Modification, pending HARQ with a TBS associated to the former CG RB allocation are released, refer to 5G\_L2\_11023\_replaced([11175832](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfAvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008124-CA] [End CB008124-C]

[CB008124-CB-UPD001] The PDCCH resource possibly allocated in USS by a Force CG overwriting can be reused (refer to 5G\_L2\_12515\_replaced([11176267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiW_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) TBC. [End CB008124-CB-UPD001]

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11176310`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
CG Overwriting for CG Deactivation

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11176314`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
For a UE in cgDeactivate request class, 5G-L2-PS needs either (refer to the diagram in 5G\_L2\_13185\_replaced([11176285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zJYPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

* to schedule a PDCCH order (UE in CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder, refer to 5G\_L2\_11263\_replaced([11175947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGGPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

It requires a free PDCCH available in CSS.

[CB008124-CB-UPD001] In case UE has already been added to the To-Be-Scheduled-List for Force CG overwriting reason (CG Deconfiguration is pending, refer to 5G\_L2\_12515\_replaced([11176267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiW_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the CG overwriting for CG Deactivation reason is discarded (PDCCH resource allocated in USS cannot be reused) and CG Deactivation is delayed) TBC. [End CB008124-CB-UPD001]

Notes: The CG RBs cannot be considered as free in current slot.

* to schedule a DG PUSCH (UE in CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling, refer to 5G\_L2\_11265\_replaced([11175955](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGGvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) on the nRbCgUl CG RBs:

It requires a free PDCCH available in USS.

[CB008124-CB-UPD001] The PDCCH resource possibly allocated in USS by a Force CG overwriting can be reused (refer to 5G\_L2\_12515\_replaced([11176267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiW_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) TBC. [End CB008124-CB-UPD001]

For simplification, a DG PUSCH is scheduled in any case (although this is only required when the UE may not be able to transmit a CG PUSCH).

[CB008124-CB] Unlike other CG overwriting for DG PUSCH transmission, additional data in UE buffer and possibility of RB upscaling are not considered. [End CB008124-CB]

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11176319`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
CG Overwriting for Initial DG PUSCH transmission

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `12713162`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
For a CG UE in any initial traffic request class, if the following conditions are satisfied (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15617\_replaced([11198720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) :

* the CG TBS is not sufficient to drain the UE buffer i.e. (rmBufferedDataUlTotal\_bytes + max size of all possible MAC CEs)\*8

OR CG-PUSCH requires UCI multiplexing with a the minimum number of PRB that is larger than nRbCgUl

* the UE is in not a transient phase (DCI format change during RRC reconfiguration)
* there is one free PDCCH candidate available in USS **[Before PR837907**] or Forced CG overwriting has already allocated one (refer to 5G\_L2\_12515\_replaced([11176267](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiW_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) **[End PR837907**]***\****\*

Then:

If ‘NRB-MIN’ is greater than **nRbCgUl, an RB upscaling (refer to 5G\_L2\_11450\_replaced([11176647](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_261lpfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is tried with:  
  -** numRbsAlreadyAllocated = nRbCgUl  
        - **numRbsToAddMin = max (1, minimum number of PRB for UCI mux - numRbsAlreadyAllocated)**\*\*\*\*

- If selected request class is equal to drbTraffic   
      numRbsToAddMax = numRbsToAddMin

Else  
***\*numRbsToAddMax = ‘NRB’ –* \*nRbCgUl**\*\*\*\*

Where :

. ‘NRB-MIN’, the minimum number of RBs required for UE FDM selection (as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_11420\_replaced([11196526](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwDFrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), considering DG Link Adaptation and possible UCI multiplexing

. ‘NRB’ is the number of RBs to drain the UE buffer.

Note that the FDM PRB distribution may later distribute additional RBs.

CG overwriting for initial DG PUSCH is scheduled either with the new allocation if RB upscaling is required and successful or with the CG PRB otherwise (DG Link Adaptation is used).

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `11176329`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
CG Overwriting for DG PUSCH retransmission

---

**2.4.26.9.1.0-3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11176333`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
For a CG UE in any retransmission request class (except cgRetrans):

If ‘NRB-MIN’ is lower or equal than nRbCgUl  
  CG overwriting is scheduled on CG RBs  
Else   
  An RB upscaling (refer to 5G\_L2\_11450\_replaced([11176647](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_261lpfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is tried with:  
  - numRbsAlreadyAllocated = nRbCgUl  
  - numRbsToAddMin = numRbsToAddMax = min( ‘NRB-MIN’ – nRbCgUl, nRbCgUl+1)

If RB upscaling is successful  
    CG overwriting is scheduled on nez FDM RB allocation  
  Else  
    CG overwriting is not possible (initial CG PUSCH is scheduled)

Where :  
. ‘NRB-MIN’ is equal to the [CB008124-D-UPD001] minimum between [End CB008124-D-UPD001] the number of RBs of the initial transmission (or possibly less according to minPctResourcesReservedMcsUl if Adaptive retransmission 8662 is enabled) [CB008124-D-UPD001] and the minimum number of PRB required for UCI multiplexing, if any [End CB008124-D-UPD001]

Note: the RB upscaling is using a unique target i.e., there is an intermediate check with targetPctResourcesReservedMcsUl before using minPctResourcesReservedMcsUl.

See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15617\_replaced([11198720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.26.9.1.0-4 CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot Minimum TBS when OverWriting in pCsi slot (ID: 11176337)

**2.4.26.9.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11176343`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a CG slot
As described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15617\_replaced([11198720](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwKaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5102\_replaced([11203503](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwfKifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), minimum TBS to ensure is equal to cgTBS.

#### 2.4.26.9.2 CG PUSCH scheduling in a non-CG slot

##### 2.4.26.9.2.0-1 CG PUSCH scheduling in a non-CG slot UE eligibility and need of PRB allocation depending on the request class (ID: 11176351)

**2.4.26.9.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176358`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a non-CG slot
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to cellular network configurations, specifically focusing on Carrier Aggregation (CG) and its associated scheduling and resource allocation mechanisms. The flowchart uses various shapes to represent different steps and decisions within the process.
Here's a breakdown of the elements and their likely meanings:
**Shapes and their meanings:**
\* **Yellow Rectangles:** Represent actions or processes.
\* **Orange Diamonds:** Represent decision points.
\* **Green Rectangles:** Represent allocation or assignment of resources.
\* **Dashed Rectangles:** Indicate a group of related steps or a specific state.
\* **Arrows:** Indicate the flow of the process.
**Textual Content and Flow:**
The flowchart begins with "Get the UE from cs2List".
1. **Is CG slot?** (Decision)
\* **Yes:** Leads to "Refer to 5G\_L2\_11348".
\* **No:** Leads to "Non-CG activated UE Or non-CG slot (or blocked CG slot)".
2. **Non-CG activated UE Or non-CG slot (or blocked CG slot)** leads to a decision point: **switch on UE request class?**
\* **cgModification [8124-CA] 5G\_L2\_13262:** This path seems to be for specific modifications.
\* It then checks if **PDCCH available In USS? & UE not in transient phase & successful alloc in CG pool (refer to 5G\_L2\_11023)**.
\* **Yes:** Leads to "CG Modification", then "Slot N+k2", then "Allocate PDCCH (USS)", and then "Init DG sched".
\* **No:** Leads to "PDCCH available?".
\* **Others (Srb/gbr/drbTraffic, deprioritizedGbr/srResp):** This path is for other types of traffic.
\* It then checks if **PDCCH available?**
\* **Yes:** Leads to "Allocate PDCCH".
\* **No (end):** The process ends.
3. **CG deactivation:** This is a separate branch.
\* **cgDeactivate:** Initiates CG deactivation.
\* **Switch on CG state:** A step in the deactivation process.
\* **cgRetrans harqRetransSrb/Gbr/Drb [8124-CB] (same num of RBs than init Tx):** This seems to be related to retransmissions during deactivation.
\* It then checks **Yes (CG reTx)** and **UE also in cgHarqRetrans**.
\* **Yes:** Leads to "CG/DG reTX".
\* Then checks **PDCCH available In USS?**
\* **Yes:** Leads to "Allocate PDCCH for DCI 0 0 (CSS or USS)".
\* **No:** Leads to "No (end)".
\* **No:** Leads to "No (end)".
\* **CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling (DG PUSCH for MAC-CE CG Confirmation):** A waiting state.
\* It then checks **PDCCH available in CSS**.
\* **Yes:** Leads to "Allocate PDCCH for DCI 0 0 (CSS or USS)".
\* **No (end):** The process ends.
\* **CG-WaltForDeactivationOrder (PDCCH order):** Another step in deactivation.
\* Leads to "Bypass PRB distribution".
4. **From "Allocate PDCCH (USS)" (CG Modification path):**
\* **Init DG sched:** Leads to a complex decision block.
\* **No-ACSI:** Leads to "PDCCH available?". (This path seems to merge with the "Others" path).
\* **ACSI:** Leads to "Slot N+k2acsi A-CSI scheduling [8124-D]".
\* Then leads to "FDM PRB distribution: free RBs in CG pool cannot be allocated to avoid later limitation on CG (re)activation (on any UE using k2k2) & slot N+k2acsi is not a CG slot & A-CSI scheduling decision in slot N+k2acsi: **This is a condition.
\*** Yes: **Leads to "Slot N+k2acsi A-CSI scheduling [8124-D]".
\* Then leads to "FDM PRB distribution: free RBs in CG pool cannot be allocated to avoid later limitation on CG (re)activation (on any UE using k2<k2acsi)".
6.** From "Allocate PDCCH for DCI 0 0 (CSS or USS)" (CG deactivation paths):**\* Leads to "Bypass PRB distribution".
7.** From "Bypass PRB distribution":**\* Leads to "Standard FDM PRB distribution: any free CG RBs can be allocated".
8.** From "Standard FDM PRB distribution: any free CG RBs can be allocated":**\*** Min PRB alloc granted: **A decision point.
\*** Yes: **Leads to "Ue added to the To-Be-Scheduled-List".
\*** No (end): **The process ends.
9.** From "FDM PRB distribution: free RBs in CG pool cannot be allocated..." (ACSI paths):**\* Leads to "Standard FDM PRB distribution: any free CG RBs can be allocated". (This merges with the deactivation path).
10.** From "Init DG sched" (CG Modification path, complex decision block):**\*** Yes: **Leads to "CG activation".
\*** Init TX-w/ prio ≤ CG driver prio & CG UE in WaitForActivation & UE not in transient phase & successful alloc in CG pool (refer to other conditions in 5G\_L2\_11025): **This is a condition.
\*** Yes: **Leads to "CG activation".
\* Then leads to "Bypass PRB distrib".
\*** No: **Leads to "Bypass PRB distrib".
11.** From "Bypass PRB distrib":**\* Leads to "Standard FDM PRB distribution: any free CG RBs can be allocated".
12.** From "Ue added to the To-Be-Scheduled-List":\*\*
\* Leads to "End".
The flowchart details the complex logic for managing CG slots, including activation, deactivation, modification, resource allocation (PDCCH, PRB), and retransmissions, considering various conditions like UE state, slot availability, and scheduling decisions (ACSI). It also highlights specific reference documents or codes like `[8124-CB]`, `[8124-CA]`, `[8124-D]`, and `5G_L2_11348`, `5G_L2_11023`, `5G_L2_11025`.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5325.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

##### 2.4.26.9.2.0-2 CG PUSCH scheduling in a non-CG slot CG Deactivation Deactivation scheduing in non-CG slot (ID: 11176362)

**2.4.26.9.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176366`)

CG PUSCH scheduling in a non-CG slot
For a UE in cgDeactivate request class, 5G-L2-PS needs either (refer to the diagram in 5G\_L2\_13185\_replaced([11176285](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26zJYPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

* to schedule a PDCCH order (UE in CG-WaitForDeactivationOrder, refer to 5G\_L2\_11263\_replaced([11175947](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGGPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

It requires a free PDCCH available in CSS.

* to schedule a DG PUSCH (UE in CG-WaitForDeactivationConfirmScheduling, refer to 5G\_L2\_11265\_replaced([11175955](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGGvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

It requires a free PDCCH and minimum number of free RBs (not subject to restrictions applicable to CG slot).

---

#### 2.4.26.9.3 CG HARQ U handling

##### 2.4.26.9.3.0-1 CG HARQ U handling CRC and DTX handling in puschReceiveRespHarqU (ID: 11176377)

**2.4.26.9.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176379`)

CG HARQ U handling
After CG Pusch scheduling, for CG Initial Tx or for CG ReTx, L2PS will receive UlData\_PuschReceiveRespHarqU message.

L2PS will handle this message, taking into account parameters below:

* CRC parameter received in message
* DTX parameter received in message
* rrmBufferedDataUlTotal\_bytes that represents an estimation of the total buffered UL data amount awaiting transmission (See 5G\_L2\_359\_replaced([11151161](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24gqYPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[PR713963] If there is an UL Rlc Status Pdu pending (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22926\_replaced([11173208](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuRkC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for definition of RLC status Pending)  [End PR713963]

This handling is performed whatever the cgState of CG State Machine: it means in any state CG scheduling is performed, CG HarqU will be handled as below.

Case CG HARQU ACK: CRC=OK and DTX=NonDTX related to initial Tx or ReTx

- Set to invalid  "transmissionXsfn" related to the HARQ Process ID

- Free CG HARQ Process Id

- Reset cgConsecutiveHarqFailureCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation to zero

-    [Before PR701262]Reset cgConsecutiveDtxCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation to zero[End PR701262]

[CB008124-C]

If PUSCH CRC-OK and non-DTX detected by gNB for a CG initial transmission over PUSCH.

Counter CG\_INI\_TX\_PUSCH\_SUCC M55308C00026 shall be incremented.

If PUSCH CRC-OK and non-DTX detected by gNB for a CG retransmission over PUSCH

Counter CG\_RE\_TX\_PUSCH\_SUCC M55308C00029 shall be incremented.

[End CB008124-C]

Case CG HARQU NACK: CRC=NOK related to initial Tx or ReTx, OR DTX= DTX related to ReTx, OR DTX=DTX related to Initial Tx AND rrmBufferedDataUlTotal\_bytes > 0 [PR713963] AND  there is NO UL Rlc Status Pdu pending [End PR713963]

Check if maximum number of Transmission is reached or not (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12741\_replaced([11203324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwejePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

* if maximum number of Transmission  is NOT reached

o Add UE in cgHarqRetrans request class, to perform a ReTrans

o Set "transmissionXsfn" related to the HARQ Process ID

 if maximum number of Transmission is reached,

o Free CG HARQ Process Id

o Set to invalid the "transmissionXsfn" related to the HARQ Process ID

o increment cgConsecutiveHarqFailureCounterBeforeDeactivation

[CB008124-C]

If PUSCH CRC-NOK detected by gNB for a CG initial transmission over PUSCH.

Counter CG\_INI\_TX\_PUSCH\_UNSUCC M55308C00027 shall be incremented.

If PUSCH CRC-NOK detected by gNB for a CG retransmission over PUSCH

Counter CG\_RE\_TX\_PUSCH\_UNSUCC M55308C00030 shall be incremented.

If DTX detected by gNB and UE UL Data is not null for a CG initial transmission over PUSCH.

Counter CG\_INI\_TX\_PUSCH\_DTX M55308C00028 shall be incremented.

Counter PUSCH\_CP\_INI\_64TBL\_MCS\_xx   M55302C000xx shall be incremented.

Counter PUSCH\_CP\_INI\_256TBL\_MCS\_xx M55302C110xx shall be incremented.

If DTX detected by gNB for a CG retransmission over PUSCH

Counter CG\_RE\_TX\_PUSCH\_DTX M55308C00031 shall be incremented.

[End CB008124-C]

Case CG HARQU NormalDTX: DTX=DTX related to Initial Tx AND

rrmBufferedDataUlTotal\_bytes = 0 [PR713963] OR there is an UL Rlc Status Pdu pending [End PR713963]

  [Before PR701262]increment cgConsecutiveDtxCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation [PR701262]]increment cgConsecutiveDtxOrWithoutDataCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation [End PR701262]

[CB008124-C]

If DTX detected by gNB and UE UL Data is null for a CG initial transmission over PUSCH.

Counter CG\_INI\_TX\_PUSCH\_DTX M55308C00028 shall not be incremented.

Counter PUSCH\_CP\_INI\_64TBL\_MCS\_xx   M55302C000xx shall not be incremented.

Counter PUSCH\_CP\_INI\_256TBL\_MCS\_xx M55302C110xx shall not be incremented.

[End CB008124-C]

---

##### 2.4.26.9.3.0-2 CG HARQ U handling Incapacity for CG HARQ retransmission (ID: 11176384)

**2.4.26.9.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176389`)

CG HARQ U handling
A CG HARQ is also released:

* when a initial CG PUSCH transmission is scheduled with the same Harq Id because the UE has toggled its NDI (absence of DCI for any CG overwriting reason in this CG slot that is not blocked i.e. configuredGrantTimer(Harq Id)  not running)
* on reception of the puschReceiveRespHarqU, although the need of retransmission of CG PUSCH is detected, this retransmission is not possible because the UE is either in a transient phase of RRC Reconfiguration.

---

#### 2.4.26.9.4 CG HARQ D &amp; PS handling

##### 2.4.26.9.4.0-1 CG HARQ D &amp; PS handling CS-RNTI aspects (ID: 11176398)

**2.4.26.9.4.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176402`)

CG HARQ D & PS handling
- The DL scheduler receives the HARQ ack/nack is from L1 in the PuschReceiveRespHarqD with rnti = CS-RNTI. It needs to replace the CS-RNTI with the C-RNTI during DL scheduler processing.

[CB008124-E] For DL CA, HARQ ack/nack related to SCell are forwarded to Scell in ScellPuXchReceiveIndHarqD with rnti = C-RNTI.  [End CB008124-E]

* The UL scheduler receives the periodic CSI report in the PuschReceiveRespPs with rnti = CS-RNTI. It shall forward this report to DL scheduler in PuschReceiveRespPsLight as per 5G\_L2\_5746\_replaced([11242493](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_3BrNePvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with rnti = C-RNTI .

[CB008124-E] For DL CA, periodic CSI report related to SCell are forwarded to Scell in PsPeerCtrl\_ScellPuschReceiveIndPs with rnti = C-RNTI.  [End CB008124-E]

---

#### 2.4.26.9.5 UL Mac PDU Handling

##### 2.4.26.9.5.0-1 UL Mac PDU Handling (ID: 11176411)

**2.4.26.9.5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176414`)

UL Mac PDU Handling
If CG UE receives an UL Mac PDU with any UL Data

- Reset cgConsecutiveDtxOrWithoutDataCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation

If CG UE receives an UL Mac PDU without any UL data

- Increment cgConsecutiveDtxOrWithoutDataCounterBeforeTriggeringDeactivation

---

#### 2.4.26.9.6 CG Scheduling chronograms

##### 2.4.26.9.6.0-1 CG Scheduling chronograms Legend (ID: 11176424)

**2.4.26.9.6.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176431`)

CG Scheduling chronograms
[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram explaining different types of PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) scheduling and their corresponding configurations in a cellular network. It is divided into three main columns: "DCI legend", "PUSCH legend", and "UE configuration Configuration".
The "DCI legend" column describes different DCI (Downlink Control Information) messages related to CG (Configured Grant) PUSCH scheduling. These include activation, re-activation, deactivation, and retransmission of CG PUSCH.
The "PUSCH legend" column provides visual cues for different types of PUSCH slots and HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) lines. It differentiates between non-CG slots, CG slots with implicit HARQ, blocked CG slots, and skipped initial CG PUSCH. It also explains the meaning of different HARQ line representations, such as initial CG PUSCH, CG PUSCH retransmission within a CG slot, and CG PUSCH retransmission in a non-CG slot.
The "UE configuration Configuration" column lists various parameters related to UE (User Equipment) configuration for CG PUSCH. These include CG slot periodicity, the number of HARQ feedback received by CG, and the ConfiguredGrantTimer. It also specifies the possible L1 feedback values (DTX, CRC OK, or NACK) and factors influencing the timer length.
The diagram is further divided into two main sections based on the type of PUSCH scheduling: "CG PUSCH scheduling" (including CG PUSCH reTX) and "DG PUSCH scheduling" (DG stands for Dynamic Grant). Each section has its own set of DCI and PUSCH legend entries.
The table below summarizes the information presented in the image:

| Category | Sub-category | DCI Legend | PUSCH Legend | UE Configuration Configuration |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **CG PUSCH scheduling (including CG PUSCH reTX)** |  | A: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH Activation | **UL slot line:**   White: Non-CG slot   Yellow 'X': CG slot with implicit HarqId X (as a fn of SFN/slot)   Orange 'X': Blocked CG slot (UE configuredGrantTimer running or Measurement Gap overlaps CG slot)   Dashed Blue 'X': Skipped Init CG PUSCH (DTX as UE has no data)   (L1 decoding scheduled) | 10 : CG slot periodicity (in slots)   or 20 |
|  |  | r-A: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH Re-Activation | **harq line:**   Blue 'X' with Blue lines: Init CG PUSCH & HarqId (same as CG slot)   Blue 'X' with vertical lines: CG PUSCH reTX & HarqId (any id) in CG slot   ⇒ i.e. CG overwriting where CG RBs are reused   Blue 'X': CG PUSCH reTX in non-CG slot (or blocked CG slot)   ⇒ needs FDM RBs alloc (same num RB as CG slot) | 2 : Num of HarqId received by CG |
|  |  | D: DCI 0 0 for CG PUSCH Deactivation |  | 2 : ConfiguredGrantTimer (UE timer)   L1 feedback = DTX, CRC OK or NACK   - timer length depends on periodicity   - in diagrams, space between PUSCH and CRC depends on   . the L1 max decoding budget   . the L1 slot buffering (ABIL)   . and the '2 slot' advance in scheduler |
|  |  | X: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH reTX of Harqld X |  |  |
|  |  | no DCI available |  |  |
| **DG PUSCH scheduling (DG stands for Dynamic Grant)** |  | X: DCI 0 1 for CG PUSCH (initial or reTX) | **harq line:**   Green 'X' with Green lines: DG PUSCH (initial or reTx)   Green 'X' with vertical lines: CG overwriting for DG PUSCH reusing CG RBs   ⇒ i.e. UE data fits in CG RBs according to DG LA   Green 'X': CG overwriting for DG PUSCH with new RBs   ⇒ needs FDM RBs alloc num RB > num in CG slot) |  |

(duplicate of 5G\_L2\_11258\_replaced([11175782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26u3-PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for readability reason)

Chronograms below are stored in "Scheduling chronograms.xlsx" under <https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.4%20Algorithm/26%20Uplink%20Configured%20Grant%20Type%202/Scheduling%20Chronograms.xlsx?d=wa6da18476c0d4cd98315c0175835da24&csf=1&web=1&e=C1uGZM>
