##### 2.4.26.9.6.0-2 CG Scheduling chronograms CG PUSCH without reTX and implicit NDI toggling ( TDD pattern 1:4, UE periodicity = 10 slots) (ID: 11176436)

**2.4.26.9.6.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11176451`)

CG Scheduling chronograms
Basic scheduling without CG PUSCH retransmission:

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline illustrating the scheduling of PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) and PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) transmissions in a cellular network. The timeline spans across several SFN (System Frame Number) and slot numbers, starting from SFN 0, slot 15 and continuing to SFN 2, slot 19.
The top row indicates the SFN and slot numbers. Below this, the PDCCH (UL DCI) is represented by a series of colored blocks (grey, black, white) indicating different states or types of control information. The "CG slot (UL)" row shows the corresponding uplink slots for Carrier Group (CG) transmissions, marked with numbers 0, 1, and 2 in yellow and green boxes, indicating specific HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) IDs.
The subsequent rows detail the behavior of CG PUSCH and DG PUSCH (Discontinuous Grant PUSCH) from both the UE (User Equipment) and gNB (next-generation NodeB) perspectives, associated with different HARQ IDs (0, 1, and ≥2).
Key events and annotations are overlaid on the timeline:
- "k2=3" is noted above the PDCCH slots.
- For CG PUSCH HARQ ID 0, "0a" is indicated in a blue box, and "NDI toggling in UE (configuredGrantTimer0 not running)" is mentioned, along with "Ob" in a blue box and "NDI toggling in UE (configuredGrantTimer1 not running)".
- For CG PUSCH HARQ ID 1, "1a" is shown in a blue box. Text annotations describe "At timer expiry UE MAC doesn't release TB1a" and "OK (gNB releases the HARQ 1a context)". Further to the right, "ACK (New TB)" is noted.
- For DG PUSCH HARQ ID ≥ 2, the number "2" is highlighted in a green box.
- A general note at the bottom states: "For each CG/DG PUSCH, indicate to DL that UCI (AN, CSI report) is expected on PUSCH".
- A dashed blue box with "1" inside is present near the end of the timeline.
- Text like "OK" and "skipped(DTX) = no data" are also present.
The colored blocks in the "CG slot (UL)" row represent the actual PUSCH transmissions or grants. Yellow boxes indicate specific HARQ IDs being transmitted, while green boxes might represent a different type of grant or HARQ ID. Black and white blocks likely represent other transmission states or absence of transmission. The grey blocks in the PDCCH row might indicate specific DCI formats or states.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5344.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

On the next CG slot (HARQ Id ‘X’) after a (re)transmission of CG PUSCH HARQ Id ‘X’, the UE toggles its internal NDI value because its corresponding configuredGrantTimer’X’ has expired (and no DCI has been received). It means that the HARQ entity is ready to fi re a new TB.

The standard DG PUSCH scheduling applies to non-CG slots, [Before CNI-109641]possibly [End CNI-109641]considering a UE buffer adjustment according to the next CG slot TBS (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5121\_replaced([11203701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwgYqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_5010\_replaced([11201008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwTkUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), [Before CNI-109641] adjustment depending on rdEnableCgAdjustment (See 5G\_L2\_11597\_replaced([11188438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28ImI_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CNI-109641]. In case of Status PDU Indication is pending, if some conditions are fullfilled (See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22926\_replaced([11173208](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuRkC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), cgAdjustment is not applied.

Note: The UL scheduler signals any CG slot opportunity to DL scheduler because possible UCI reporting (CSI or HARQ feedback) would be sent over CG PUSCH and not PUCCH.

---

##### 2.4.26.9.6.0-3 CG Scheduling chronograms CG Overwrite for CG PUSCH retransmission in a CG-slot ( TDD pattern 1:4, UE periodicity = 10 slots) (ID: 11176455)

**2.4.26.9.6.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11176464`)

CG Scheduling chronograms
Basic scheduling with CG PUSCH retransmission in a CG slot (refer to 5G\_L2\_11334\_replaced([11176497](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260XiPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for non-GC slot):

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline illustrating the scheduling of different communication channels, specifically PDCCH and CG slots, along with associated HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) statuses and UE (User Equipment) and gNB (next-generation NodeB) views. The timeline is segmented into Super Frame Numbers (SFN) 0, 1, and 2, with each SFN further divided into slots numbered from 0 to 19.
The top row indicates the SFNs. Below this, the slot numbers are displayed.
The "PDCCH" row shows a sequence of colored blocks representing the status of the Physical Downlink Control Channel. Gray blocks likely indicate an idle or inactive state, white blocks might represent a control message transmission, and black blocks could signify a busy or occupied state. There's a label "k2=3" above slot 17 in SFN 0.
The "CG slot" row, directly below the PDCCH row, uses black and yellow blocks. Yellow blocks are labeled with numbers, indicating specific states or events within the CG (Carrier Group) slots. For instance, a yellow block labeled "1" appears in slot 17 of SFN 0, and another labeled "0" appears in slot 9 of SFN 1.
The "CG PUSCH Harqld 0" and "CG PUSCH Harqld 1" rows are intended to show HARQ statuses for CG PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) transmissions, with "0" and "1" likely representing different HARQ feedback values (e.g., ACK/NACK). However, these rows are mostly empty, with no specific markings visible in the provided image.
The bottom section presents two views: "UE view" and "gNB view".
The "UE view" shows a blue bar spanning across several slots, indicating a period of activity or a grant. Within this bar, "1a" is labeled in two locations. There's also a red oval labeled "NOK" below the blue bar, and a green label "OK" further to the right. A dashed blue box with "1" inside appears at the end of the blue bar.
The "gNB view" is represented by a blue text label "UE NDI toggling (configuredGrantTmer1 not running)" and dashed blue boxes containing "0" and "1" at different points in time, suggesting control signals or status updates from the gNB's perspective.
The overall visualization appears to be a timing diagram used to explain the interaction and scheduling between a UE and a gNB, particularly concerning control channel signaling and uplink data transmission with HARQ feedback.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5346.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

No PRB distribution is required as the CG PUSCH retransmission fits in the allocated CG RBs.

##### 2.4.26.9.6.0-4 CG Scheduling chronograms HARQ failure due to implicit UE NDI toggling ( TDD pattern 1:4, UE periodicity = 10 slots) (ID: 11176469)

**2.4.26.9.6.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11176480`)

CG Scheduling chronograms
When the 5G-L2-PS is not able to schedule the CG HARQ ‘X’ retransmission before the next CG slot of this HARQ ‘X’, the UE toggles its NDI value and the 5G-L2-PS releases the HARQ context.

The HARQ fails and the RLC will retransmit.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline illustrating the behavior of a cellular network, specifically focusing on HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat reQuest) processes and DCI (Downlink Control Information) availability. The timeline is segmented by SFN (System Frame Number) and slot numbers, ranging from 0 to 19 for SFN 0, and then continuing from 0 to 19 for SFN 1, and 0 to 19 for SFN 2.
The top section shows the PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) and CG (Control Grant) slot status. Different colors represent different states: black indicates no DCI available, white indicates DCI available, and gray indicates a specific condition related to DCI availability. Yellow boxes highlight specific slots with a number inside, likely representing a HARQ ID.
Below this, the image details the CG PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) HARQ IDs from the perspective of both the UE (User Equipment) and the gNB (next-generation NodeB).
Key events and observations are annotated:
\* **k2=3**: This is indicated near SFN 0, slot 16.
\* **"No DCI available..."**: This text appears multiple times, indicating periods where DCI is not present on the PDCCH.
\* **Yellow boxes with numbers**:
\* SFN 0, slot 19: Contains '1'.
\* SFN 1, slot 4: Contains '1'.
\* SFN 1, slot 8: Contains '0'.
\* SFN 2, slot 9: Contains '0'.
\* SFN 2, slot 19: Contains '1'.
\* **Blue boxes with numbers**:
\* SFN 1, slot 4: Contains '0' (dashed outline).
\* SFN 2, slot 9: Contains '0' (dashed outline).
\* SFN 2, slot 19: Contains '1' (dashed outline).
\* **UE View**:
\* A blue line extends from SFN 0, slot 17 to SFN 1, slot 5, labeled "1a".
\* Below this line, "NOK" is written, suggesting a failure.
\* A blue circle highlights an event: "UE NDI toggling of HARQ 1a (configuredGrantTmer1 not running) => UE releases HARQ 1a". This event is associated with "HARQ 1a failure => RLC reTX" and a blue box labeled "1b".
\* **gNB View**:
\* A blue line extends from SFN 1, slot 15 to SFN 2, slot 11.
\* Below this line, "OK" is written.
\* The text "gNB releases the HARQ 1a context new TB 1b" is also present.
\* **DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2**: This is a general condition noted at the bottom left.
The timeline visually represents the transmission and reception of data, control information, and the management of HARQ processes, including failures and retransmissions.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5348.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note that the UE toggles its NDI value even if it has no TB to send.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication system, likely a cellular network, showing the timing of various events across different slots within Super Frame Numbers (SFNs). The diagram is organized into rows representing different aspects of the communication and columns representing time slots.
**Rows:**
\* **SFN slot:** This row displays the SFN (Super Frame Number) and the corresponding slot numbers within each SFN. The SFNs are divided into sections labeled 0, 1, and 2. Within each SFN, slots are numbered from 0 to 19.
\* **PDCCH:** This row shows the status of the Physical Downlink Control Channel (PDCCH) for each slot. The blocks are colored black, white, and gray, with some slots marked with ">" symbols. Red text annotations indicate "No DCI available..." for specific slot ranges.
\* **CG slot:** This row indicates the status of the "CG slot" (likely Carrier Group slot) for each slot. Some slots are marked with a yellow background and the number "1" or "0", indicating specific events or states.
\* **CG PUSCH Harqld 0:** This row, labeled "CG PUSCH Harqld 0", shows a blue dashed square with "0" inside, indicating an event related to HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) ID 0.
\* **CG PUSCH Harqld 1:** This row, labeled "CG PUSCH Harqld 1", shows a blue dashed square with "1" inside, indicating an event related to HARQ ID 1.
\* **- UE view:** This section provides a "UE view" of events. A blue rectangle labeled "1a" is shown, followed by a blue line extending across several slots. Red text "NOK" appears later in this view.
\* **- gNB view:** This section provides a "gNB view" of events.
\* **DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2:** This row, labeled "DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2", indicates a condition related to Downlink Grant (DG) PUSCH HARQ ID greater than or equal to 2.
**Key Annotations and Events:**
\* **k2=3:** This annotation appears above slot 15 in SFN 0.
\* **"No DCI available..."**: These red text annotations indicate periods where no Downlink Control Information (DCI) is available.

\* **Blue rectangle "1a"**: This likely represents a grant or resource allocation for a specific transmission.
\* **"NOK"**: This red text indicates a failure or negative acknowledgment.
\* **"UE NDI toggling of HARQ 1a (configuredGrantTmer1 not running) → UE releases HARQ 1a"**: This is a detailed annotation describing a sequence of events from the UE's perspective, involving NDI (New Data Indicator) toggling, a timer not running, and the UE releasing HARQ 1a.
\* **"HARQ 1a failure ⇒ RLC reTX"**: This annotation indicates a HARQ 1a failure leading to RLC (Radio Link Control) retransmission.
\* **"gNB releases the HARQ 1a context"**: This annotation describes an action taken by the gNB (next-generation NodeB).

##### 2.4.26.9.6.0-5 CG Scheduling chronograms CG PUSCH reTX window extended thanks to the DCI of a CG Overwritting blocking the UE NDI toggling ( TDD pattern 1:4, UE periodicity = 10 slots) (ID: 11176485)

**2.4.26.9.6.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11176492`)

CG Scheduling chronograms
If the UE has received a DCI for the retransmission of the HARQ ‘X’ but the configuredGrantTimer’X’ has not yet been started, the UE doesn’t toggle its NDI value.

The possibility for 5G-L2-PS to retransmit the CG PUSCH is extended until the subsequent CG slot of the HARQ Id ‘X’.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline illustrating the behavior of PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) and CG slot (Control Grant slot) over several SFNs (System Frame Numbers) and slots. The timeline is segmented by SFNs 0, 1, and 2, with slots numbered from 15 to 19 within each SFN.
The PDCCH is represented by a series of colored blocks: white, gray, and black. White blocks indicate the presence of DCI (Downlink Control Information), gray blocks indicate no DCI available, and black blocks represent other states.
The CG slot is shown below the PDCCH, with yellow blocks indicating a HARQ ID (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request Identifier) of 0 or 1, and black blocks indicating other states.
Below the CG slot, there are three timelines representing different views: "CG PUSCH Harqld 0", "CG PUSCH Harqld 1", and "DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2". These timelines use blue and green text to denote events and statuses.
Key observations from the timeline:
- In SFN 0, slots 16 and 17 have white PDCCH blocks, indicating DCI presence. The CG slot shows a yellow block with '1' in slot 17, corresponding to CG PUSCH Harqld 1.
- In SFN 1, slots 6-8 and 11-13 have gray PDCCH blocks, indicating "No DCI available...". Slot 9 has a yellow CG slot block with '0', corresponding to CG PUSCH Harqld 0.
- In SFN 2, slots 6 and 7 have gray PDCCH blocks, followed by white blocks in slots 8 and 9. The CG slot shows a yellow block with '0' in slot 9.
- The "CG PUSCH Harqld 1" timeline shows a blue bar labeled "1a" starting around slot 17 of SFN 0 and extending to slot 5 of SFN 1. It also shows a blue bar labeled "1a" in slot 6 of SFN 2.
- The "DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2" timeline shows "NOK" in red text below the "1a" bar in SFN 1, and "OK" in green text below the "1a" bar in SFN 2.
- There are annotations like "k2=3", "DCI prevents HARQ failure", "No NDI toggling (ConfiguredGrantTmer1 not running but DCI received)", and "NDI toggling (timer1 not running) 1b new TB 1b".

##### 2.4.26.9.6.0-6 CG Scheduling chronograms CG PUSCH reTX window extended thanks to the configuredGrantTimer blocking the UE NDI toggling ( TDD pattern 1:4, UE periodicity = 10 slots) (ID: 11176497)

**2.4.26.9.6.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11176504`)

CG Scheduling chronograms
The UE cannot toggle the NDI value in a blocked CG slot ‘X’ i.e. when the configuredGrantTimer’X’ is running.

The possibility for 5G-L2-PS to retransmit the CG PUSCH is also extended until the subsequent CG slot of the HARQ Id ‘X’.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication system's scheduling and HARQ (Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request) feedback. It depicts a timeline divided into SFN (System Frame Number) and slots, with different layers representing PDCCH (Physical Downlink Control Channel) and CG (Carrier Aggregation) PUSCH (Physical Uplink Shared Channel) HARQ IDs.
The top row shows the SFN and slot numbers, ranging from 15 to 19 for SFN 0, and then slots 0 to 19 for SFN 1, and slots 0 to 19 for SFN 2.
The second row, labeled "PDCCH", shows a sequence of colored blocks representing different states or events within each slot. Black blocks likely indicate no transmission or a specific state, white blocks might represent a control channel transmission, gray blocks could indicate a different type of transmission or state, and colored blocks (yellow, green, orange, blue) represent specific HARQ IDs or events.
The third row, labeled "CG slot", shows a similar pattern of colored blocks, aligning with the PDCCH row, indicating the status of CG slots.
Below these rows, there are three sections detailing the behavior of CG PUSCH HARQ IDs:
1. **CG PUSCH Harqld 0**: This section shows a yellow block labeled "1" in slot 18 of SFN 0, and a yellow block labeled "0" in slot 8 of SFN 1. There's also a blue dashed square with "0" below the yellow "0" in slot 8 of SFN 1.
2. **CG PUSCH Harqld 1**: This section shows a blue block labeled "1a" in slot 17 of SFN 0. A blue line extends from this point, with "NOK" written below it. Further along, a yellow line indicates "No NDI toggling (configuredGrantTimer1 running)". There's also a blue dashed square with "1" in slot 10 of SFN 1.
3. **DG PUSCH HarqiD ≥ 2**: This section shows a green block labeled "2" in slot 16 of SFN 1. A blue oval labeled "CG reTX in non-CG slot (FDM RB distrib)" is positioned below slot 7 of SFN 1. Another blue oval labeled "Standard Sched (FDM RB distrib)" is positioned below slot 8 of SFN 1, with a green block labeled "2" above it. Text "OK" appears next to both of these ovals.
Additional annotations include:
\* "k2=3" below slot 16 of SFN 0.
\* "No DCI available..." above slots 5-8 of SFN 1.
\* "Blocked CG slot 1" above slots 17-19 of SFN 1.
\* "NDI1 toggling (timer1 not running)" to the right of slot 19 of SFN 1.
\* "CG RBs no more reserved" below the yellow line in SFN 1.
The diagram visually represents the timing of control and data channel transmissions, HARQ feedback, and specific events like retransmissions, NDI toggling, and timer statuses within the context of Carrier Aggregation.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5352.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note: For a CG UE, a blocked CG slot is not considered as a CG slot. Therefore, the 5G-L2-PS can allocate the corresponding CG RBs to any UE.

---

##### 2.4.26.9.6.0-7 CG Scheduling chronograms CG PUSCH reTX window extended thanks to the DCI of a CG Overwritting ( TDD pattern 1:4, UE periodicity = 10 slots) (ID: 11176510)

**2.4.26.9.6.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `11176517`)

CG Scheduling chronograms
For a CG UE, a blocked CG slot is not considered as a CG slot and associated CG RBs no more reserved.

Therefore, this UE requires a DG PUSCH with standard FDM PRB distribution to be scheduled in a blocked CG slot.

### 2.4.26.10 CG resource pool management

#### 2.4.26.10.1 CG RB allocation

##### 2.4.26.10.1.0-1 CG RB allocation (ID: 11176532)

**2.4.26.10.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11176550`)

CG RB allocation
Implementation of RB allocation in CG pool (5G\_UP\_ALG\_14663\_replaced([11172788](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgsPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is based on the cgAllocationTable (in time and frequency domain, refer to 5G\_L2\_10925\_replaced([11176576](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-lfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):  
- The pattern length is 8 (frames) \* 10 \* 2µ slots wide  
- Each slot entry keeps track of allocated chunks of 4 RBs in a bitmap of 64 bits (max 256 RBs), bit 0 associated to CRBs in range [ulCgPrbStartOffset .. ulCgPrbStartOffset+3]  
- and the list of UEs that require CG PUSCH decoding in the slot (assuming no CG overwriting).

Scheduling a CG activation in a candidate slot { SFN ‘N’, slot ‘X’ (DCI slot + K2) } requires availability of same nRbCgUl RB resources (refer to CG LA in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14664\_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in all candidate CG slot indexes ‘Y’ ≡ ‘N’ \* 10 \* 2µ  + ‘X’ (mod periodicity) in the slot pattern.

In below example, blue, yellow and purple colors respectively represents already allocated chunks, free chunks and new allocation (white slots are DL slots that cannot be used):

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a resource map illustrating the concept of Common Gateway (CG) resource allocation in the context of FR1 TDD 2.5ms frame structure with configurable periodicities of 5ms and 10ms.
The resource map is presented in a grid format, with the vertical axis representing "Max PRB allocation per cell for CG" and the horizontal axis representing "Time-domain in slot". The frequency domain is indicated on the left side as "Frequency domain in CG PRB group".
The map shows a pattern of resource allocation across time slots. Different colors and symbols (X and 0) are used to denote the allocation status of Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs).
Above the main grid, there are indicators for periodicity.
- "Periodicity 5 slots" is shown with red downward arrows pointing to specific time slots, suggesting a repeating pattern every 5 slots.
- "Periodicity 10 slots" is shown with blue downward arrows pointing to specific time slots, suggesting a repeating pattern every 10 slots.
The main grid itself is divided into columns representing time slots. Within each time slot column, there are rows representing PRBs. The allocation is visualized by colored blocks and 'X' or '0' symbols.
- Teal colored blocks appear in some time slots, spanning multiple PRBs.
- Purple colored blocks appear in some time slots, also spanning multiple PRBs.
- Yellow colored blocks appear in some time slots, spanning multiple PRBs.
- 'X' symbols are present in many PRB slots, indicating occupied resources.
- '0' symbols are present in many PRB slots, indicating free resources.
The bottom of the image indicates that the allocation is "Defined with the maximum periodicity size (160 slots)". This suggests that the entire resource map shown or implied covers a duration of 160 slots, and the periodic patterns are defined within this larger timeframe.
The overall visualization aims to explain how CG resources are scheduled and allocated over time, considering different periodicity configurations.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5359.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

The search for these RBs (refer to 5G\_L2\_11163\_replaced([11176556](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_260-kfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) uses the chunk allocation bitmap that is the result of an “OR” bit operation between the bitmaps of all target CG slots ‘Y’ of the full pattern (due to absence of the longest configured periodicity in the Cell configuration) to consider already allocated UEs.

If free RBs are found, the allocation bitmap is updated and the UE added to the list of CG UEs to schedule for all CG slots of the pattern.

In TDD, DL slots are set as "busy" to forbid allocation in slots 3:13 fro semi-static pattern 3:7 (5GC001070).

Note: CP-RT converts in slots and rounds up any NIDD periodicity (ms) to the lower multiple of UL slot pattern length (deduced from tddFrameStructure field), so 5G-L2-PS needn’t check on the minimum supported periodicity.

[CB010630-E] For TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e, when slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed = TRUE and slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eUlAllowed = TRUE, the PRB allocations per cell are consiered as Layer PRB allocated per cell as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25713\_replaced([11214271](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxMVL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB010630-E]

---

##### 2.4.26.10.1.0-2 CG RB allocation Searching strategy (ID: 11176556)

**2.4.26.10.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176563`)

CG RB allocation
numChunksNew = nRbCgUlNew / 4

In case of CG activation [CB008124-CA] or CG Modification [End CB008124-CA] in a slot not matching a CG slot of former configuration, the search starts from the lower CRB index and stops at the first match.

For (i = 0; (i+ numChunksNew) < ulCgMaxNumPrb/4; i++) …

[CB008124-CA]  
In case of CG Modification attempt in a slot matching a CG slot of former configuration:

Former allocation starts at CRB = ulCgPrbStartOffset+CgPoolRbIndexOld (index within the CG pool)  
Associated chunkIndexOld” (4 RB granularity) = CgPoolRbIndexOld / 4  
and         numChunksOld = nRbCgUlOld / 4

* On a nRbCgUl decrease, the lowest nRbCgUl CRBs indexes of former allocation are allocated.

chunkIndexNew = chunkIndexOld

* On a nRbCgUl increase, the allocator first tries to have the former allocation nested in the new one:

The “nested search” starts from chunkIndexLow = max(0, chunkIndexOld – (numChunksnew – numChunkslold))

For (i = chunkIndexLow; (i+numChunksNew) < min(chunkIndexOld+ numChunksNew, ulCgMaxNumPrb/4); i++) …

If nested search fails, the default search continues below nesting search:

For (i = 0;  i+numChunksNew < chunkIndexOld-1; i++) …

If no allocation is found below, try above:

For (i = chunkIndexOld+ numChunksOld+1; (i+ numChunksNew) < ulCgMaxNumPrb/4; i++) …

[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a visual representation of a chunk allocation algorithm, illustrating different search strategies. It is divided into four main sections: "Former Allocation", "Nested search", "Below search", and "Above search". Each section contains a vertical bar representing a series of chunks, numbered from 0 to 64.
The "Former Allocation" section shows an initial state where chunks 7, 8, and 9 are allocated (marked in yellow), and chunk 5 is also allocated (marked in brown). The variable `chunkIndex_old` is set to 8, and `numChunks_old` is 3.
The "Nested search" section, with `numChunks_New = 5`, shows two vertical bars representing a search range. The left bar shows chunks from an unspecified starting point down to a limit of `chunkIndex_old + numChunks_New = 12`. The right bar shows chunks from an unspecified starting point down to the same limit. In the right bar, chunks 7, 8, and 9 are shown as allocated (yellow), and chunks 10, 11, and 12 are shown as newly allocated (brown). The variable `chunkIndex_Low` is set to 5.
The "Below search" section also shows two vertical bars. The left bar represents a search range from an unspecified starting point down to a limit of `chunkIndex_old + numChunks_old = 11`. The right bar shows a similar range. Below these bars, there's a note: "Optimistic assumption from Nested search Failure comes from adjacent chunk 'X' & 'Y' already allocated". The right bar in this section shows chunks 5, 6, and 7 allocated (brown), and chunk 8 is marked with a question mark and a grey box labeled 'X'. Chunk 9 is labeled 'Y'. The limit is indicated as `chunkIndex_old - 1 = 6`.
The "Above search" section displays two vertical bars. The left bar shows a search range from an unspecified starting point down to a limit of `uICgMaxNumPrb/4 = 18`. The right bar shows a similar range. In the right bar, chunks 17 and 18 are shown as newly allocated (brown). Below these, chunk 16 is marked with a question mark and a grey box labeled 'X'.
A legend at the bottom clarifies the color coding: yellow represents "Chunk of old allocation" and brown represents "Chunk of new allocation".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5361.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[End CB008124-CA]

---

##### 2.4.26.10.1.0-3 CG RB allocation CG RB release (ID: 11176568)

**2.4.26.10.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11176572`)

CG RB allocation
In case of confirmed CG Deactivation or CG Deconfiguration completion (or UE release), the allocation bitmap is updated with released RBs and the UE is removed from the list of CG UEs to schedule for all CG slots of the pattern.

[CB008124-CA]  
In case a former CG allocation is still reserved due to pending CG Modification confirmation:  
- If CG slots of both allocations are different, same update applies to former CG allocation for all former CG slots.  
- Otherwise, if same CG slots, the allocation bitmap is also updated with non-overlapping CG RBs of former allocation.

[End CB008124-CA]

---

#### 2.4.26.10.2 cgAllocationTable

##### 2.4.26.10.2.0-1 cgAllocationTable Definition (ID: 11176580)

**2.4.26.10.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176586`)

cgAllocationTable
The cgAllocationTable is a two-dimension map in time domain and frequency domain per CG activated cell:

* Time-domain dimension of the map is in unit of slot and ranges up to maximum period duration (5G\_L2\_IF\_4000\_replaced([10976426](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkt_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).
* Frequency-domain dimension of the map is in unit of CG PRB (one bit corresponding to 4 PRBs) and ranges up to the maximum cell level CG PRB number in term of cell level CG configuration.

---

##### 2.4.26.10.2.0-2 cgAllocationTable Structure (ID: 11176590)

**2.4.26.10.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176599`)

cgAllocationTable
The cgAllocationTable is defined as a table of cgSlotAllocationTable, each table element corresponding to the CG PRBs of a dedicated slot. The number of cgSlotAllocationTable in cgAllocationTable is 160 (maximum possible slot period).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a data structure diagram illustrating an `AllocationTable` which is an array of `cgAllocationTable` elements, with the length determined by `CGPatternLength`. The diagram shows several `cgSlotAllocationTable` entries, indexed by `slot 0`, `slot 1`, `slot 2`, and extending up to `CGPatternlength - 160`.
Each `cgSlotAllocationTable` consists of two parts: an "Array of Rntis" and an "AllocationTable". The "Array of Rntis" contains entries like "Rnti 0", "Rnti 1", and "Rnti 2", each represented as a small table with multiple rows. The "AllocationTable" for each slot is a vertical array of cells, some of which are colored blue or gray, indicating allocated resources.
At the bottom left, there's a `CgData` block containing `currentCgResource` and `oldCgResource`. These two resources are linked to separate blocks, each containing `numOfPrb`, `startPrb`, and `firstSlot` fields.
The diagram uses lines to show relationships between these components. Specifically, `currentCgResource` and `oldCgResource` are connected to the `numOfPrb`, `startPrb`, and `firstSlot` fields of two distinct resource allocation descriptions. The `cgSlotAllocationTable[slot 0]`'s "Array of Rntis" is connected to the `CgData` block.
The overall structure suggests a system for managing resource allocations over time slots, where each slot can have multiple RNTI (Radio Network Temporary Identifier) assignments, and each assignment is tracked within an allocation table. The colored cells in the allocation tables likely represent occupied resource blocks (PRBs).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5368.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

---

#### 2.4.26.10.3 cgSlotAllocationTable

##### 2.4.26.10.3.0-1 cgSlotAllocationTable Definition (ID: 11176608)

**2.4.26.10.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176611`)

cgSlotAllocationTable
A cgSlotAllocationTable stores the CG PRB allocation for one slot and all associated CG UEs being able to send CG traffic in this slot. Its dimension is defined by the maximum possible bandwidth allocated to CG traffic.

---

##### 2.4.26.10.3.0-2 cgSlotAllocationTable Structure (ID: 11176616)

**2.4.26.10.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176621`)

cgSlotAllocationTable
The cgSlotAllocationTable structure contains a 64bits bitset whose use is defined in accordance with the maximum number of CG PRBs allocable to CG (5G\_L2\_IF\_3986\_replaced([10970126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Ugu3_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Each bit of the bitset corresponds to a CG PRB (equivalent to 4 PRBs). This structure also contains a list of RNTIs corresponding to the CG UEs having allocated CG PRBs in this slot.

---

#### 2.4.26.10.4 Total CG activated UEs PM counter

##### 2.4.26.10.4.0-1 Total CG activated UEs PM counter (ID: 11176629)

**2.4.26.10.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11176638`)

Total CG activated UEs PM counter
Counter M55308C00034 (TOT\_CG\_ACT\_UE) should be incremented on every UL slot by the [Before PR758983] sum [PR758983] total number [End PR758983] of CG activated UEs.

-A UE is considered as CG activated after UL grant Type 2 scheduling sent via PDCCH in DCI format 0\_1 for activation.

[Before PR758983] Counter M55308C00034 (TOT\_CG\_ACT\_UE) [PR758983] Total number of CG activated UEs [End PR758983] should be decremented for CG deactivated UEs, CG bearer released or deletion of UE still CG activated.

* A UE is considered as CG deactivated after UL grant Type 2 scheduling sent via PDCCH in DCI format 0\_0 for deactivation.

[PR758983]- A UE is considered as CG deactivated in case the Mac CE was not received before expiry of cgMacCeConfirmationForCgActivationDeactivationTimer at the last CG activation attempt. [End PR758983]

[Before PR758983]- This counter should be decremented by the number of lapsed UEs in case the MAC CE is not received after DCI format 0\_1 for CG activation was sent.[End PR758983]

---

### 2.4.26.11 Impact on UE FDM allocation when feature is enabled

#### 2.4.26.11.0-1 Impact on UE FDM allocation when feature is enabled RB upscaling for CG UEs (ID: 11176647)

**2.4.26.11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176651`)

Impact on UE FDM allocation when feature is enabled
When a UE already has already numRbsAlreadyAllocated number of CG RBs allocated in current CG slots, the RB upscaling of FDM allocator tries to allocate no less than numRbsAlreadyAllocated + numRbsToAddMin and up to numRbsAlreadyAllocated + numRbsToAddMax contiguous RB(s). For a UE in a transient phase, if this maximum exceeds CORESET0 size, this size is considered as the maximum number of RBs to allocate.

For this search, the CB RBs currently allocated to this UE are considered as available and are not necessarily nested in the new allocation.

For a UE in a transient phase, if numRbsAlreadyAllocated + numRbsToAddMin is greater than the CORESET0 size, the RB upscaling is considered as failed.

For a CPU reason, the total number of RB upscaling attempts per slot (all UEs considered) is limited to a maximum of rdMaxRbUpscalingAttemptPerSlot attempts per slot (refer to 5G\_L2\_11453). RB upscaling is considered as failed if this maximum has been reached.

If numRbsAlreadyAllocated + numRbsToAddMin are available, RB upscaling is successful and the CG RB allocation is considered as free for FDM allocator in current slot (for same or different UE):

* The CG RBs are assigned to an adjacent sub-area, if any.
* In case CG RBs are adjacent to two sub-areas, the two subareas are merged in a subarea containing the two sets of RBs and the CG RBs.
* In absence of any adjacent sub-area, a new sub-area is created to host the CG RBs

Default FDM PRB allocator algorithm applies meaning that allocated PRB may not overlap with CG RB allocation of this CG UE and CG RBs could be allocated to another UE in current slot.

---

#### 2.4.26.11.0-2 Impact on UE FDM allocation when feature is enabled RB allocation for ACSI scheduling for non-CG UEs or non-CG slots (ID: 11176656)

**2.4.26.11.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176661`)

Impact on UE FDM allocation when feature is enabled
When k2acsi != k2 (i.e. TDD only), the A-CSI scheduling cannot allocate free RBs in the CG pool because they should remain available for a possible later CG Activation.

---

### 2.4.26.12 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling and Configured Grant Type 2

#### 2.4.26.12.0-1 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling and Configured Grant Type 2 PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling enable/Disable with CG type 2 (ID: 11176673)

**2.4.26.12.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176681`)

PUSCH Low Priority Proactive Scheduling and Configured Grant Type 2
According to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_4839\_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the "Proactive Grant Type" is set to Disabled and proact timer stopped when UE enters en firing state (see 5G\_L2\_10291\_replaced([11176085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26xUM_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and set to the value as before when UE gets out of firing state and proact timer started.

If "Proactive Grant Type" is updated during the UE in firing state (via user modify message), the new value (if Proactive Grant Type is other than Disabled) is applied when UE gets out of firing state.

Following is the states transitions to manage Proactive Grant Type with CG.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the "Configured Grant Type 2 and UL proactive Scheduling" process. It depicts various states and transitions between them, triggered by specific events.
Here's a breakdown of the states and transitions:
**States:**
\* **CG\_WaitForActivation:** The system is waiting for activation.
\* **CG\_WaitForActivationConfirm:** The system is waiting for confirmation of activation.
\* **CG\_Error:** An error state.
\* **CG\_WaitForDeactivationConfirm:** The system is waiting for confirmation of deactivation.
\* **CG\_Configured:** The system is configured.
\* **CG\_WiitForDeactivationConfirmSched:** The system is waiting for scheduled deactivation confirmation.
\* **CG\_Deconfiguration:** The system is undergoing deconfiguration.
**Transitions:**
\* From **CG\_WaitForActivation** to **CG\_WaitForActivationConfirm**: Triggered by "Disable proactive grant after DCI 01".
\* From **CG\_WaitForActivationConfirm** to **CG\_WaitForActivation**: Triggered by "Enable proactive grant".
\* From **CG\_WaitForActivationConfirm** to **CG\_Error**: Triggered by "Enable proactive grant".
\* From **CG\_Error** to **CG\_WaitForActivationConfirm**: Triggered by "Enable proactive grant".
\* From **CG\_Error** to **CG\_WaitForDeactivationConfirm**: Triggered by "Enable proactive grant".
\* From **CG\_WaitForDeactivationConfirm** to **CG\_Error**: Triggered by "Enable proactive grant".
\* From **CG\_WaitForDeactivationConfirm** to **CG\_WiitForDeactivationConfirmSched**: Triggered by "Enable proactive grant".
\* From **CG\_WiitForDeactivationConfirmSched** to **CG\_Configured**: Triggered by "Enable proactive grant".
\* From **CG\_Configured** to **CG\_WiitForDeactivationConfirmSched**: Triggered by "Enable proactive grant".
\* From **CG\_Deconfiguration** to **CG\_Configured**: Triggered by "Enable proactive grant after RRC reconf complete".
The flowchart uses rectangular boxes to represent states and arrows to indicate transitions, with text labels on the arrows describing the events that trigger these transitions. The background appears to be a grid, suggesting it might be from a spreadsheet or a similar diagramming tool.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5385.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

---

### 2.4.26.13 Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC

#### 2.4.26.13.0-1 Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC UL non-GBR PRB Load associated to Configured Grant Type2 active UEs for RAC (ID: 11176690)

**2.4.26.13.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176696`)

Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC
UL non-GBR PRB Load associated to Configured Grant Type2 for UEs in firing state (see 5G\_L2\_10291\_replaced([11176085](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26xUM_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is reported to CP-RT in new message .

The non-GBR CG load report message   is sent after existing GBR load report  to CP-RT.

Associated CP2 requirement: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15449\_replaced([11201433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwWAnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

#### 2.4.26.13.0-2 Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC UL CG Non-GBR Time Domain Load Monitoring for RAC (ID: 11176701)

**2.4.26.13.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176705`)

Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC
As per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8174\_replaced([11201280](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwUyffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), UL CG non-GBR Td load reporting is part of existing message  between L2-PS and CP-RT.

---

#### 2.4.26.13.0-3 Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC Housekeeping average non GBR CG PRB load (ID: 11176709)

**2.4.26.13.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11176713`)

Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC
According to UL CG periodicity specified in 5G\_L2\_IF\_4000\_replaced([10976426](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkt_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and UL CG PRB allocation process specified  in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14664\_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), UE's average non GBR CG PRB load is an integer multiple of a minimum CG PRB load which could be defined as:

-  minUlCgPrbLoad = minUlCgPrbNumber / (ulWeight \* maxUlCgPeriodicity) where:

-  minUlCgPrbNumber: 4 PRBs according to CG PRB allocation step specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14664\_replaced([11172802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuOgs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

-  ulWeight: refers to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17871\_replaced([11173234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

-  maxUlCgPeriodicity: 160 slots according to UL CG periodicity specified in 5G\_L2\_IF\_4000\_replaced([10976426](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkt_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))

A data structure with flexible size could be used to maintain the UE list specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17871\_replaced([11173234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) due to the theoretical CG firing and not pending deactivation UE number could be very big. The integer multiple of  'minUlCgPrbLoad' could be stored into the list instead of the decimal PRB load 'avgCgNonGbrPrbLoadUe' in order to save memory and to simply relevant calculation.

UE average non GBR CG PRB load shall be updated once CG modification process specified in 5G\_L2\_11017\_replaced([11175827](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26vfAfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  finished and number of CG PRB changed.

---

#### 2.4.26.13.0-4 Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC Non GBR CG PRB load reduce (ID: 11176718)

**2.4.26.13.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11176724`)

Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC
CP-RT may requests L2-PS to reduce non-GBR UL CG type2 PRB load as described in 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_3195\_replaced([11208384](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Bgwi3fvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

L2-PS will calculate final PRB load reduction value 'prbToReduce' based on 'percentToReduce' value received from CP-RT as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17873\_replaced([11173250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

L2-PS shall select minimum number of non GBR UL CG UEs to fulfill PRB load reduction value 'prbToReduce'. A natural expectation is to minimize SUM(avgCgNonGbrPrbLoadUe) based on mininum UE number. But the algorithm complexity for such kind of issue is too high. No obvious benefit expected to cost large CPU load to minimize SUM(avgCgNonGbrPrbLoadUe). A simple UE selection algorithm proposed as below:

from the largest 'avgCgNonGbrPrbLoadUe' to the smallest one in the list:

if SUM + avgCgNonGbrPrbLoadUe <= prbToReduce:

SUM = SUM + avgCgNonGbrPrbLoadUe;

select current UE;

else

from the smallest 'avgCgNonGbrPrbLoadUe' to the largest one in the list:

if SUM + avgCgNonGbrPrbLoadUe > prbToReduce:

select current UE;

return;

CG deactivation shall be triggered for selected UEs. If all UEs in the list are selected but still can not match 'prbToReduce', a warning info shall be generated accordingly.

---

#### 2.4.26.13.0-5 Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC CG activation control (ID: 11176727)

**2.4.26.13.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `12734560`)

Non GBR CG PRB load reporting for RAC
CP-RT may requests L2-PS to suspend CG activation for GBR or non-GBR as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17873\_replaced([11173250](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLFfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L1L2\_FLOW\_3195\_replaced([11208384](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Bgwi3fvREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). L2-PS shall maintain [Before CB008250-F] two independent [End CB008250-F] flags to record and update GBR and non GBR CG activation control state [CB008250-F] on cell level as well as per Plmn [End CB008250-F], the flags can refer to L2L3 interface definition 5G\_L2\_IF\_4620\_replaced([10973873](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U37P_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and initial value shall be 0 (resume).  These [Before CB008250-F] two [End CB008250-F] flags are updated according to cell level message PsCell\_UlCGType2ActivationControlReq refer to 5G\_L2\_IF\_4617\_replaced([10973857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U37PPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [CB008250-F] If UL Plmn Aware Scheduler is off only cell level flags are used (updated and used to control UE CG activation), otherwise only per plmn flags are used. Upon online activation/deactivation of Plmn Aware Scheduler flags are not reset (values are preserved until next activation or deactivation). [End CB008250-F]

The function of cell level CG activation control is enabled/disabled according to on online changable GBR admission control parameter actGBRAdmissionControl and gbrLoadUpdatePeriod as specified in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_8746\_replaced([11234566](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TmTx9PvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988\_replaced([10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When either actGBRAdmissionControl or gbrLoadUpdatePeriod changed to 0 (off),  cell level [CB008250-F] and per plmn [End CB008250-F] CG activation control flags in L2-PS shall be reset to 0 (resume). And the cell level [CB008250-F] or per plmn [End CB008250-F] CG activation control flags shall not be updated if actGBRAdmissionControl or gbrLoadUpdatePeriod is 0 (off).

---

### 2.4.26.14 Power Saving BWP switching with CG

#### 2.4.26.14.0-1 Power Saving BWP switching with CG Power Saving BWP switching (ID: 11176741)

**2.4.26.14.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176744`)

Power Saving BWP switching with CG
UE can not switch from large BWP to the power saving BWP (with DL grant format DCI 1\_1) if configured with CG profile, i.e. CS-RNTI is allocated.

Associated CP2 requirement 5G\_UP\_ALG\_1872\_replaced([11111200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

### 2.4.26.15 CG interworking with UL CA

#### 2.4.26.15.0-1 CG interworking with UL CA Overview (ID: 11176755)

**2.4.26.15.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11176759`)

CG interworking with UL CA
When UL Buffer split is triggered for CG UE configured with UL CA, CG pattern overlapping TDD Scell UL slots is not supported.

Behaviour is specified in requirements 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22286\_replaced([11173280](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22288\_replaced([11173327](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

#### 2.4.26.15.0-2 CG interworking with UL CA CG permanent conflict with SCell UL Slots (ID: 11176763)

**2.4.26.15.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176768`)

CG interworking with UL CA
if PCell technology is FDD ans SCell technology is TDD and CG periodicity < 5 ms, CG pattern always conflicts with SCell UL slots.

Otherwise it does not.

---

#### 2.4.26.15.0-3 CG interworking with UL CA CG pattern overlap with UL lots of the Scell configured with UL CA (ID: 11176774)

**2.4.26.15.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11176781`)

CG interworking with UL CA
CG pattern overlap with shortened UL slots of Pcell with UL Tx switching

---

**2.4.26.15.0-3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `12507294`)

CG interworking with UL CA
For UE configured with FDD PCell + FR1 TDD SCell [Before CB0013553-C] R16 [End CB013553-C] UL Tx switching option 1 (switchedUL), CG pattern is not supported if any of the following conditions is fulfilled:

Any of CG occasion is in overlapping UL slot;

In non-overlapping UL slot, any of CG occasion has different SLIV than start symbol 0 and length defined as the smallest length of PUSCH in TDRA list in cell setup procedure, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22286\_replaced([11173280](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If CG pattern is not supported:

For UL buffer split for CG UE configured with UL CA, UL buffer split is not accepted (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22286\_replaced([11173280](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

For CG activation/modification for UL CA UE with active UL SCell, CG activation/modification is not allowed (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_22288\_replaced([11173327](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuSLHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

#### 2.4.26.15.0-4 CG interworking with UL CA UL Buffer Split status (ID: 11176789)

**2.4.26.15.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11176794`)

CG interworking with UL CA
Boolean variable isUlBufferSPlitInUse indicates whether UL Buffer split was triggered since latest Scell activation with UL CA configure.

isUlBufferSPlitInUse  is set to false at CG Init, ScellDeactivation or ScellRelease.

isUlBufferSplitInUse is set to true the first time UL Buffer split is triggered after ScellActivation of an Scell with UL CA configured.

---

### 2.4.26.16 CG transient phase handling

#### 2.4.26.16.0-1 CG transient phase handling CG Deactivation (ID: 12732803)

**2.4.26.16.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12732804`)

CG transient phase handling
If the Deactivation was triggered with cell reconfiguration procedure according to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_33244/[11173470](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuTZOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_tauaapvfee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconfiguration%2f9&artifactinmodule=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fbi_kutzopvsee-aqvopbp1qhq&componenturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2frm-projects%2f_t-lvkj5eee2a-5u7tzedra%2fcomponents%2f_ubjxej5eee2a-5u7tzedra") due to online modification of actUlCgType2 (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988/[10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_mohdapu3ee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconfiguration%2f9&artifactinmodule=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fbi_2um1c_u6ee-aqvopbp1qhq&componenturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2frm-projects%2f_cp08qo3yee-rl47x_rccmw%2fcomponents%2f_cqjvao3yee-rl47x_rccmw")) from TRUE to FALSE, then the 5G-L2-PS instance shall continue apply CG PUSCH scheduling (see 5G\_L2\_10918/[11176220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26yiUPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/bi_26yiupvtee-aqvopbp1qhq?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9")) for the eligible UEs during the transient phase (see also [12646707](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_W4tMcULNEfCH-cG2YrVgkA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/bi_w4tmculnefch-cg2yrvgka?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9")).

CG transient phase handling
If the CG Deactivation (see [11175923](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26wGE_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_tauaapvfee-aqvopbp1qhq&artifactinmodule=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fbi_kutzopvsee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconfiguration%2f9&componenturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2frm-projects%2f_t-lvkj5eee2a-5u7tzedra%2fcomponents%2f_ubjxej5eee2a-5u7tzedra") due to online modification of actUlCgType2 (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_1988/[10971056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Um1c_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_mohdapu3ee-aqvopbp1qhq&artifactinmodule=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fbi_2um1c_u6ee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconfiguration%2f9&componenturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2frm-projects%2f_cp08qo3yee-rl47x_rccmw%2fcomponents%2f_cqjvao3yee-rl47x_rccmw")) "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showartifactpage&artifacturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fmd_xf2dofvlee-aqvopbp1qhq&oslc_config.context=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2fgc%2fconfiguration%2f9&artifactinmodule=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2fresources%2fbi_w4tmculnefch-cg2yrvgka&componenturi=https%3a%2f%2fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2frm%2frm-projects%2f_44aj0j5dee2a-5u7tzedra%2fcomponents%2f_5j4kaj5dee2a-5u7tzedra")) ## 2.4.27 Slice Aware Scheduler

### 2.4.27.0-2 Slice Aware Scheduler Introduction (ID: 11176805)

**2.4.27.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11176809`)

Slice Aware Scheduler
Please check 5G\_UP\_ALG\_7386\_replaced([11188670](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd20_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

### 2.4.27.0-3 Slice Aware Scheduler Configured and Unconfigured Slices (ID: 11176812)

**2.4.27.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11176821`)

Slice Aware Scheduler
[CB008709-A]In DL SAS, [End CB008709-A] L2PS should keep two list of [Before CB008828-C] slices [CB008828-C] resource groups [End CB008828-C]:

-list of configured [Before CB008828-C] slices [CB008828-C] resource groups [End CB008828-C] (originating from Cell Setup and Cell Reconfiguration)

-list of unconfigured [Before CB008828-C] slices [CB008828-C] resource groups [End CB008828-C] (originating from User Setup and Radio Bearer Setup)

For every [Before CB008828-C] slice [CB008828-C] resource group [End CB008828-C] (configured and unconfigured) L2PS should keep relevant information, e.g. sum of schedule weights of all DRBs associated with given [Before CB008828-C] slice [CB008828-C] resource group [End CB008828-C].

[CB008828-C] The default resource group should belong to the configured resource groups. [End CB008828-C]

For further details refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17581\_replaced([11188712](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvd22_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.27.0-4 Slice Aware Scheduler Resource groups and default resource groups (ID: 11176826)

**2.4.27.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11176831`)

Slice Aware Scheduler
ulResourceGroupConfig received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_61\_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) contains:

* resource group list
* default resource group list

Default resource group is a mapping of artificial id (1-12) to resource group id (1-32).

Resource group id in above mapping must be one of ids of configured resource groups.

Each newly added DRB via drbAddReqPs (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in PsUser\_UserSetupReq / PsUser\_BearerSetupReq contains:

* ul resource group id (optional)
* ul default resource group id (mandatory, must belong to configured resource groups if UL slice aware scheduler is active)

If resource group id belongs to configured resource groups, then DRB shall be mapped to this resource group, otherwise DRB shall be mapped to default resource group.

---

### 2.4.27.0-5 Slice Aware Scheduler UL Slice Aware Scheduler bookkeeping (ID: 11176836)

**2.4.27.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11176846`)

Slice Aware Scheduler
For every bearer in bearerList in LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_875\_replaced([10984687](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2WBxzvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) 5G-L2-PS shall calculate the instantaneous resource share of the DRB as specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23256\_replaced([11207913](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0AvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using the receivedDataAmount parameter.

Note: Unlike as in DL, UL UE resources distribution to DRBs is not controlled by 5G-L2-PS scheduler (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24753\_replaced([11208033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwt0BfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). It is important to note that SAS UL bookkeeping is typically done several slots after the actual PUSCH scheduling.

5G-L2-PS shall update the average resource shares for each RG as described in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23263\_replaced([11207786](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwtM-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using instantaneous resource share calculated for all the DRBs which were scheduled at some previous UL slot. Since 5G-L2-PS cannot guarantee the arrival of all the DRBs receivedDataAmount data in timely manner, it shall update the average resource shares after the max time defined as below:

maxWaitTime = rdSasBookkeepingDelay + l1PuschDelay + bufferedUlDelay [PR815634]+ dynamicSasBookkeepingDelay[End PR815634],

where:

- rdSasBookkeepingDelay see 5G\_L2\_16392\_replaced([11188460](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28ImKPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

- l1PuschDelay is derived from L1 UlData\_PuschReceiveRespLo latency (see 5G\_L1\_IF\_283\_replaced([11514791](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR5rpPvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and equals:

TDD (SCS=30KHZ) HW variant Loner - 6 slots

TDD (SCS=30KHZ) other HW variants - 4 slots

FDD (SCS=15KHZ) - 3 slots

- bufferedUlDelay is derived from buffered ul delay feature (5G\_UP\_ALG\_5216\_replaced([11209990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw298_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and equals 5 slots if buffered ul is active, otherwise 0 slots

[PR815634]

- dynamicSasBookkeepingDelay is used only if rdEnableDynamicSasBookkeepingDelay is set to True; it is calculated as follows: when 5G-L2-PS receives LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd after maxWaitTime, then the scheduler shall increment the dynamicSasBookkeepingDelay by 1 slot, otherwise when 5G-L2-PS receives LoCtrl\_UlMacPduReceiveInd in consecutive rdMinDynamicSasBookkeepingDelaySlots before maxWaitTime, then the scheduler shall decrement the dynamicSasBookkeepingDelay by 1 slot; the valid range for dynamicSasBookkeepingDelay is 0 to rdMaxDynamicSasBookkeepingDelayValue.

[End PR815634]

Amount of slots used to compare against maxWaitTime shall be measured between the scheduled PUSCH onAir time and current radio time.

---
