### 2.4.31.0-15 Nominal Bitrate Bearer (NBR) NBR Overload Counters for DL and UL (ID: 12682555)

**2.4.31.0-15.0-1**  (ID: `12682556`)

Nominal Bitrate Bearer (NBR)
TOT\_CNT\_MEAS\_DL\_TYPE3 & TOT\_CNT\_MEAS\_UL\_TYPE2 Counter update during respective counter measure active period

**[PR839866-26R1]** **The counters are updated as per the following scenarios**\*\*\*\*

1. Increment when feature enable with NBR bearer
2. Increment when feature enable and no-NBR bearer
3. Increment when feature disable with NBR bearer
4. No increment when feature disable and No NBR bearer

**[End PR839866-26R1]**

Note :The counter measure counters could be updated even after disabling of NBR feature ,similar to legacy behavior of NBR counters.

---

## 2.4.32 Guaranteed Bitrate Bearer (GBR)

### 2.4.32.1 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR

#### 2.4.32.1.0-1 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR Activation of GBR Congestion control (ID: 12470204)

**2.4.32.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12470206`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
NonVoNR GBR Congestion Control can be activated individually for

GBR PDSCH Congestion Control (gbrPdschCongHandling in 5G\_L2\_IF\_1830\_replaced([10969409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UcdaPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

GBR PUSCH Congestion Control (gbrPuschCongHandling in 5G\_L2\_IF\_1830\_replaced([10969409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UcdaPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

GBR DL PDCCH Congestion Control (gbrPdcchCongHandlingDl in in 5G\_L2\_IF\_1830\_replaced([10969409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UcdaPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

GBR UL PDCCH Congestion Control (gbrPdcchCongHandlingUl in in 5G\_L2\_IF\_1830\_replaced([10969409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UcdaPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

The Congestion detection period for each of GBR Congestion control is derived from congDetectPeriod in 5G\_L2\_IF\_7698\_replaced([10970415](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UikEvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

GBR Congestion control is done on cell level or BWP level as per requirements in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31281\_replaced([11183557](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (DL GBR), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31352\_replaced([11201668](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (UL GBR)

In a CongDetectPeriod ( 5G\_L2\_IF\_7698\_replaced([10970415](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UikEvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) not more than one GBR congestion control operation are performed in a particular slot (to avoid heavy processing on a single slot) .

GBR DL PDCCH Congestion Detection and  GBR PDSCH Congestion Detection shall be performed in the same slot.

GBR UL PDCCH Congestion Detection and GBR PUSCH Congestion Detection shall be performed in the same slot.

The prioritisation between each of the GBR Congestion control algorithms applied in the same slot shall be as defined in [12525150: Interworking of congestion control Algorithms](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Zd1dUBqZEfC3upM2pE5-4w?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-2 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR Deactivation of GBR Congestion control (ID: 12472604)

**2.4.32.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12472606`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
GBR Congestion Control shall be considered to be deactivated when all of the below GBR congestion control parameter are not Enabled

GBR PDSCH Congestion Control (gbrPdschCongHandling in 5G\_L2\_IF\_1830\_replaced([10969409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UcdaPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

GBR PUSCH Congestion Control (gbrPuschCongHandling in 5G\_L2\_IF\_1830\_replaced([10969409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UcdaPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

GBR DL PDCCH Congestion Control (gbrPdcchCongHandlingDl in in 5G\_L2\_IF\_1830\_replaced([10969409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UcdaPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

GBR UL PDCCH Congestion Control (gbrPdcchCongHandlingUl in in 5G\_L2\_IF\_1830\_replaced([10969409](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UcdaPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

When GBR Congestion Control is in deactivated state : Default Parmeter handling and priority parameters are not included in the interface from CP-RT and L2-PS ( as in 5G\_L2\_IF\_390\_replaced([10974651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

priorityLevelArp (in 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) - L2-PS initialize to lowest priority vaue of 14

arpPrioPerVul (in 5G\_L2\_IF\_397\_replaced([10974876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U-o4vu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) - L2-PS initialize to default value 1 (Preemptable)

Note: When the GBR Congestion control state changes from Activated -> Deactivated or Deactivated ->Activated the existing UE/ bearer's shall continue to use the configured (Activated -> Deactivated ) or  default (Deactivated->Activated) Priority values untill any bearer modify request is triggered by CP-RT

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-3 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR Modification of GBR Congestion control (ID: 12472627)

**2.4.32.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `12472631`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
Modified GBR Congestion control parameters can be notified to L2-PS from CP-RT using defined interface message (5G\_L2\_IF\_559\_replaced([10977908](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VPHnvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , 5G\_L2\_IF\_220\_replaced([10970911](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UlnW_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

For Cell, BWP congestion control L2-PS shall take into effect the updated parameters from the subsequent Congestion control period and re-initialize the congestion control operations (de-prioritization and re-prioritisation of DL bearer or UL LCG of UE).

The modification of GBR Congestion Weight  Algorithm as in CongWeightAlg (in 5G\_L2\_IF\_7698) shall be used as a trigger to change the L2-PS internal data structure "key" for different GBR Congestion Control as in 5G\_L2\_

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-4 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR GBR Congestion monitering and detection (ID: 12478167)

**2.4.32.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `12478168`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
When Congestion Control is enabled GBR PDSCH, PUSCH, PUCCH(DL,UL) PRB load is monitered and congestion detection performed

GBR PDSCH PRB load monitering is performed as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8168\_replaced([11183175](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvIfqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and congestion detection as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31298\_replaced([11183605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

GBR PUSCH PRB load monitering is performed as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8172\_replaced([11201209](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwUycfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and congestion detection as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31359\_replaced([11201708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

GBR PDCCH-DL PRB load monitering is performed as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31283\_replaced([11183567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvLi_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and congestion detection as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31285\_replaced([11183590](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

GBR PDCCH-UL PRB load monitering is performed as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31354\_replaced([11201675](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and congestion detection as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31356\_replaced([11201693](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

GBR PDSCH and PUSCH PRB Load monitering need to be performed for Power Saving (PS) BWP when PS-BWP is configured and PRB Load monitering is performed (as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8168 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8172) for other BWP's for RAC load reporting.

Note: PS BWP PRB load is not reported to CPRT

[CB008118-E]

DC-GBR PDCCH-DL load is added along with GBR PDCCH DL load for congestion detection as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31285\_replaced([11183590](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

DC-GBR PDCCH-UL load is added along with GBR PDCCH UL load for congestion detection as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31356\_replaced([11201693](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

DC-GBR PDSCH Load 5G\_UP\_ALG\_30857\_replaced([11183283](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvJGvvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) considered along with other GBR PDSCH load for PUSCH congestion detection as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31298\_replaced([11183605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

DC-GBR PUSCH Load 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31516\_replaced([11201392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwWAlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) considered along with other GBR PUSCH load for PUSCH congestion detection as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31359\_replaced([11201708](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End CB008118-E]

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-5 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR GBR Congestion Control - De-prioritized Bearer Handling (ID: 12478173)

**2.4.32.1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `12478174`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
De-prioritisation of "GBR Bearers for DL" OR "LCG for UL" - per cell, BWP shall be done according to the requirements in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31361\_replaced([11201715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (UL) , 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31321\_replaced([11183615](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKBvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(DL)

The maximum number of bearers / Ues for DL GBR Congestion control or LCGs/Ues for UL GBR Congestion control, that can be de-prioritized (or Re-Prioritized) in an Iteration of a Congestion period (congDetectPeriod), shall be be controlled by rdMaxNumOfDeprioReprioGbrPerIterInCongPeriod.

The limit rdMaxNumOfDeprioReprioGbrPerIterInCongPeriod ([11188599: rdMaxNumOfDeprioReprioGbrPerIterInCongPeriod](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28J0QfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) shall be used across all BWP where GBR Congestion Control actions are performed.

When GBR congestion is detected and 'one of the GBR bearer' OR LCG of the UE is picked for de-prioritization i.e moved to “drbTraffic (prioXlDrbTraffic)” class then:-

L2PS will continue updating the token bucket of the GBR UE bearer :  
   a.    token bucket could remain positive and reach max - (i.e as 5G\_UP\_ALG\_8026\_replaced([11175497](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kujQ1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)): If Bi, j is larger than bucket size j, it shall be set equal to the bucket size)

b.    token bucket could go negative – i.e case when this de-prioritized UE gets scheduled. In this case – L2PS shall consider UE belonging to “deprioritizedGbr (prioDlDeprioritizedGbr/prioUlDeprioritizedGbr)” traffic class and not “drbTraffic”. L2PS will keep the UE in to-be re-prioritized eligible list till congestion period.

If a new bearer is added to an already de-prioritized LCG due to GBR UL PUSCH Congestion Control , LCG shall continue to act de-prioritized

Note:   
1) Scheduling weight "rdCongDepriorGbrSchedulWeight  (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31697) " need to be used for the congestion deprioritized DRB bearers   
2) GBR UE's current active average data rate can be continued to be used when the GBR UE is congestion deprioritized to DRB bearer

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-6 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR GBR DL/UL PDCCH Congestion control - De-Prioritized UE Handling (ID: 12492831)

**2.4.32.1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12492832`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
De-prioritization for GBR DL / UL  PDCCH for Congestion control shall be done at a UE level as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31285\_replaced([11183590](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKAfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (DL) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31356\_replaced([11201693](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) (UL)

If a new bearer is added to an already de-prioritized UE due to GBR DL / UL PDCCH Congestion Control , the new bearer shall act as de-prioritized bearer

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-7 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR GBR DL/UL PDCCH Congestion Control - PDCCH Resource calculation per UE (ID: 12492833)

**2.4.32.1.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `12521593`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
The GBR DL/UL PDCCH Resource utilisation per UE shall be done as in [12495956](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_M5bgsBQ6EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)  DL and  [12496156](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-fhWtBRLEfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) UL

For PDCCH load CCE used shall be considered as zero under following condition  
      - When UE is not scheduled in a Slot  
      - When UE is scheduled with priority class higher than GbrTraffic    
      - GBR De-prioritized UE's scheduling from DRB traffic class

Common filterCoefficient shall be used for all GBR Congestion Control algorithms (No separate filter co-efficient definition needed)

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-8 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR GBR Congestion Control - Re-prioritized Bearer Handling for Multiple BWP (ID: 12492834)

**2.4.32.1.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `12492835`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
In case of multiple BWP , the GBR Congestion status for each of the BWP shall be maintained on a per BWP basis and reprioritization as in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31323\_replaced([11183626](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvMKCPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31366\_replaced([11201724](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is performed

Reprioritize should be done for UEs from higher priority (i.e. with lower values) to lower priority (i.e. with higher values).

Re-prioritization of DL bearer or UL LCG of UE of a Lower BWP shall not be done if that BWP or any of the upper BWP (including Larger BWP) of the cell is in Congestion status.

If BWP Switch is executed for a UE and the UE is in PXSCH Reprio Eligible list, it will be reprioritized and removed from that list.

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-9 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR GBR UL Congestion Control - GBR LCG/UE de-prioritization in UL congestion using Power Limitation (ID: 12494467)

**2.4.32.1.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `12494468`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
GBR LCG/UE de-prioritization in UL congestion is handled in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31361\_replaced([11201715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwYc2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The first step of using  LCGs of UEs in power limitation for de-prioritisation shall consider the status of UE power limitation at its first scheduling slot within the congestion control period . The dynamic state of the UE power limitation shall not be updated during the congestion control period in order to simplify the processing of congestion control.

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-10 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR GBR DL/UL Congestion Control - Counter Update (ID: 12494478)

**2.4.32.1.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `12494484`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
The detection of GBR UL Congestion Control is on a per LCG basis , however the GBR UL Congestion Control Counter shall be counted on a per bearer level.

A cell level accNumOfDeprioUlGbrBearers shall be maintained.

Whenever an LCG is selected for deprioritisation / Re-prioritisation based on the GBR UL Congestion Control , the number of bearer attached to the LCG shall be updated (increment / decrement respectively) for the counter

The cell level accNumOfDeprioUlGbrBearers shall be updated for bearer addition / deletion and GBR Congestion control reconfigurations.

In GBR DL/UL PDCCH Congestion control the control action (de-prioritization/re-prioritization) is performed at a UE level . The counter update for DL and UL shall include all the GBR bearers of the de-prioritized/re-prioritized.

DL, UL Counters are NUM\_GBR\_DRB\_DEPRIO\_DL\_CONG, NUM\_GBR\_DRB\_DEPRIO\_UL\_CONG

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-11 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR GBR Congestion weight Algorithm based Key derivation - for Congestion Control (ID: 12525023)

**2.4.32.1.0-11.0-2**  (ID: `13460601`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
The folowing Key identifier shall be used in GBR Congestion handling of de-prioritisation and re-prioritization :

[CB013501-SR-A-B2]

1) Of bearer for GBR PDSCH Congestion Control

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a table that defines the structure of a data packet or message, likely in a telecommunications or networking context. The table is organized by bit positions, ranging from 31 down to 0. Each row represents a different aspect of the data, with columns indicating the bit range and the name of the field.
Here's a breakdown of the table's content:
**Header Row:**
\* **1-bit:** Indicates a field occupying 1 bit.
\* **3-bit:** Indicates a field occupying 3 bits.
\* **7-bit:** Indicates a field occupying 7 bits.
\* **16-bit:** Indicates a field occupying 16 bits.
\* **5-bit:** Indicates a field occupying 5 bits.
**Bit Position Row:**
This row shows the specific bit numbers corresponding to each field, from most significant bit (31) to least significant bit (0).
**Field Name Row:**
This row labels the different fields within the data structure.
\* **De/Re:** A 1-bit field.
\* **BWPID:** A 3-bit field.
\* **PRIORITY:** A 7-bit field.
\* **RNTI:** A 16-bit field.
\* **LCHID:** A 5-bit field.
**Value Row:**
This row provides example hexadecimal values for each field.
\* **0x1u:** Value for De/Re.
\* **0x7u:** Value for BWPID.
\* **0x7Fu:** Value for PRIORITY.
\* **0xFFFFu:** Value for RNTI.
\* **0x1Fu:** Value for LCHID.
**Sub-field Row (for bits 28-21):**
This row further breaks down the 7-bit PRIORITY field into sub-fields:
\* **ArpVal:** Occupies bits 28-26 (3 bits).
\* **Spare:** Occupies bits 25-24 (2 bits).
\* **ArpPrio:** Occupies bits 23-21 (3 bits).
**Top Label:**
\* **New Scenario:** This label appears in a pink rectangle above the table, suggesting this data structure might be associated with a specific scenario or configuration.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-04061301.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

2) Of LCG for GBR PUSCH Congestion control

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a table that describes a "New Scenario" with different bit fields. The table has two rows and multiple columns, with the top row indicating the size of each bit field (1-bit, 3-bit, 7-bit, 16-bit, 4-bit) and the bottom row showing the bit numbers and the names of the fields.
Here is the table represented in markdown:

| Bit Size | Bit Numbers | Field Name |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1-bit | 31 | DeOrReprio |
| 3-bit | 30-28 | BWP |
| 1-bit | 27 | PL |
| 7-bit | 26-20 | Priority |
| 16-bit | 19-4 | rnti |
| 4-bit | 3-0 | lcg |
| There is also a sub-table below the "Priority" field, which seems to be a breakdown of some bits within that range: |  |  |

| Field Name | Bit Numbers |
| --- | --- |
| Arp Val | (Implied within 26-20) |
| Spare | (Implied within 26-20) |
| ArpPrio | (Implied within 26-20) |

3) Of UE for GBR DL PDCCH Congestion Control and GBR UL PDCCH Congestio control

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a table representing a data structure labeled "PDCCH DL". The table is organized into rows and columns, with cells containing numerical indices and descriptive labels.
The top row of cells indicates bit lengths: "1bit", "3 bit", "7bit", and "16bit". Below these, a row of numbers from 31 down to 0 represents bit indices.
The cells are colored to denote different fields:
- The "1bit" field, corresponding to indices 27, is colored yellow and labeled "de/reprio".
- The "3 bit" field, corresponding to indices 26, 25, and 24, is colored orange and labeled "WP ID".
- The "7bit" field, corresponding to indices 23 through 17, is colored light blue and labeled "priority".
- The "16bit" field, corresponding to indices 16 through 0, is colored light orange and labeled "RNTI".
Above the table, a green text box contains the text "updated Congestion key bit map".
The table structure is as follows:

| Field Name | Bit Indices | Color |
| --- | --- | --- |
| PDCCH DL | 31-28 | White |
| de/reprio | 27 | Yellow |
| WP ID | 26-24 | Orange |
| priority | 23-17 | Light Blue |
| RNTI | 16-0 | Light Orange |

[image-3][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the structure of an "updated Congestion key bit map" for PDCCH UL. It is presented as a table with rows and columns representing bits and their corresponding fields.
The table has the following structure:

| Field Name | Bit Length | Bit Range | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PDCCH UL |  | 31-0 |  |
|  | 1bit | 31-28 |  |
|  | 1bit | 27 | de/reprio |
|  | 3bit | 26-24 | bwp id |
|  | 1bit | 23 | we |
|  | 7bit | 22-16 | priority |
|  | 16bit | 15-0 | RNTI |

Where,

- congAlgo = 0 , indicates congWeightAlg(5G\_L2\_IF\_7698) set as '0:priorityLevel'

- congAlgo = 1, indicates congWeightAlg(5G\_L2\_IF\_7698) set as '1:arpPrioPerVul'

- ArpVul ARP Vulerability bit

- ArpPrio is ARP Priority

- PL is Power Limiting bit

- Spare are unused bits

The BWPID field remains 3 bits wide, the PRIORITY field remains 7 bits (including ArpVal, Spare, and ArpPrio), and the RNTI field continues to be 16 bits. Their sizes and meanings are unchanged; only their starting bit positions move due to the addition of the De/Re Prio bit.

[End CB013501-SR-A-B2]

---

#### 2.4.32.1.0-12 Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR Interworking of congestion control Algorithms (ID: 12525150)

**2.4.32.1.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `12525154`)

Congestion Control for non-VoNR GBR
a) GBR DL PDCCH and PDSCH congestion control interworking

GBR DL PDCCH is prioritized over GBR PDSCH Congestion Control handling during de-prioritization.

De-prioritization is treated at a higher priority than re-prioritization during GBR DL PDCCH and GBR PDSCH Congestion control

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GBR DL PDCCH congestion | GBR PDSCH congestion | Action |  |
| Congestion detected | Congestion detected | Only PDCCH deprioritization ([12492831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-yXVUBL6EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) No PDSCH deprioritization |  |
| Congestion detected | No congestion | Only PDCCH deprioritization ([12492831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-yXVUBL6EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) No PDSCH reprioritization |  |
| No congestion | Congestion detected | No PDCCH reprioritization Only PDSCH deprioritization [12478173](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_V3Qo8Q5IEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) |  |
| No Congestion | No Congestion | PDCCH reprioritization ([12492831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-yXVUBL6EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) PDSCH reprioritization [12492834](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_VsbLYBL9EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) |  |

b) GBR UL PDCCH and PUSCH congestion control interworking

Similarly GBR UL PDCCH is prioritized over GBR PUSCH Congestion control handling during de-prioritization.

De-prioritization is treated at a higher priority than re-prioritization between GBR UL PDCCH and GBR PUSCH Congestion Control

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GBR UL PDCCH congestion | GBR PUSCH congestion | Action |  |
| Congestion detected | Congestion detected | Only PDCCH deprioritization ([12492831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-yXVUBL6EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) No PUSCH deprioritization |  |
| Congestion detected | No congestion | Only PDCCH deprioritization ([12492831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-yXVUBL6EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) No PUSCH reprioritization |  |
| No congestion | Congestion detected | No PDCCH reprioritization Only PUSCH deprioritization [12478173](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_V3Qo8Q5IEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) |  |
| No congestion | No congestion | PDCCH reprioritization ([12492831](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_-yXVUBL6EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) PUSCH reprioritization [12492834](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_VsbLYBL9EfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) |  |

The congestion control on each BWP shall  work independently  . For instance in a cell assume there are 2 BWPs, Large  and Small, Small is congested and Large is having no congestion:

In such a case , the UEs in Small BWP shall be deprioritized and the UEs in large BWP can be reprioritized. (similar to the PDSCH/PUSCH congestion iwk with multibwp).

PDCCH congestion interworking with multibwp:

If a PDCCH congestion is detected on any of the BWP, we declare PDCCH congestion. Now in such a case, the table mentioned above shall be referred for iwk with PDSCH/PUSCH congestion

The congestion control on each BWP shall  work independently. For instance in a cell assume there are 2 BWPs Large  and PS is congested and large is having no congestion.

In such a case , the UEs in PSBWP shall be deprioritized and the UEs in large BWP will be reprioritized. (similar to the PDSCH/PUSCH congestion iwk with multibwp).

However Unlike the case of PDSCH congestion, we will not add up the load on the large BWP due to UE released/added due to deprio/Reprio on the smaller BWP.  
This is because the coresets can be overlapping and not aligned like the BWPs.

Max number of action taken (Deprio or RePrio) for PDSCH/PUSCH will be restricted by rdMaxNumOfDeprioReprioGbrPerIterInCongPeriod  [11188599](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28J0QfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

[CB008118-CB]

c) GBR DL/UL and NBR DL/UL congestion control interworking

If GBR congestion is detected in a slot (for any BWP) then NBR (including NBR RG) level congestion control would not be performed at same slot Ref: 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31627\_replaced([11183415](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvKU2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Rational:

- Reducing GBR load will reduce NBR load (GBR Load is considered in NBR Congestion detection)

- this also help avoid high CPU consumption in a slot.

[End CB008118-CB]

---

## 2.4.33 Management of Downlink Resource Allocation Type1

### 2.4.33.0-1 Management of Downlink Resource Allocation Type1 Overview (ID: 11177336)

**2.4.33.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13245426`)

Management of Downlink Resource Allocation Type1
CB008927 feature introduces Downlink RAT1(Contiguous PRB Allocation) support that helps in efficient utilization of PRBs in turn improves the spectral efficiency.

This feature introduces the concept of PDSCH subareas in downlink to support contiguous  allocation.

We will have following implementation impacts respect to Type1 Allocations

1. PDSCH SubArea Creation.(TD Scheduler/FDM)

2. FDM-selectUes Impacts (FDM).

 PDSCH Resource Availability Check.

3. Subarea Assignment.

4. RAT1 PDSCH Resource Reservation.(FDM).

 Needed Resource based on 2PrbBasedProposedMcs.

5.   MU-MIMO Resource Reservation and Allocation.(FDM)

6.   RAT0 Dynamic Switch Resource Reservation/Allocation.(FDM).

 After Dynamic Switch RAT0 if remaining prbs > 0, Redistribute remaining prbs as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26460\_replaced([11180766](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku8SZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

7**. RAT1 Resource Allocation.**

 Start PRB Randomization.

8.   TBS Calculation.(FD Scheduler)

 Scaled MCS based on 2PrbBasedProposedMcs .

9. L2-L1 Interface Update and DCI Content Preparation. (FD Scheduler).

[CB014861-A]

10. Exhaustive DL Scheduling replace WRR in (4) and (5).[End CB014861-A]

The high level flow of Scheduler Impacts in terms of DL RAT1 & Dynamic Switch RAT0 is shown below

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to 5G network scheduling. The flowchart starts with a "Start" node, followed by "TD Scheduling".
The main part of the flowchart is enclosed in a dashed rounded rectangle and contains several sequential steps:
1. **SubArea Management** (5G\_L2\_15211, 5G\_L2\_15531)
2. **SelectUes**
3. **Distribute Resource(SU-WRR)** (5G\_L2\_15363)
4. **MU-MIMO Algo** (5G\_L2\_20176)
5. **Dynamic Switch Eligible UE List**
6. **Dynamic Switch For Initial-Tx** (5G\_L2\_16532)
7. **Artificial Load**
8. **Fake UE Sched lability Decision**
9. **SU MIMO Allocate Resource** (5G\_L2\_15322)
There are also several parallel processes and inputs/outputs connected to this main flow:
\* **Dynamic Switch For Re-Tx** (5G\_L2\_16534) is connected to the "SubArea Management" step.
\* A purple box on the left lists several steps:
\* Step 1: Required RBG Calculation (5G\_L2\_16530)
\* Step 2: Available RBG Calculation (5G\_L2\_16530)
\* Step 3: Dynamic Switch Schedulability Check (5G\_L2\_16528)
\* Step 4: Allocate RBG Pre-Book (5G\_L2\_16593)
\* Step 5: Create/Update Subareas
This purple box is connected to the "Dynamic Switch For Initial-Tx" step.
\* To the right of the main flow, there are several rectangular boxes representing external processes or data:
\* **Resource Reservation** (5G\_L2\_15358) is connected to "SubArea Management".
\* **Fake UE Sched lability Check based on Target Load** is connected to "Artificial Load".
\* **Start PRB Randomization** (5G\_L2\_15656) is connected to "SU MIMO Allocate Resource".
Below the main dashed rounded rectangle, there is another section enclosed in a dashed rounded rectangle, labeled "FD" (likely Frequency Domain or similar):
1. **TBS Calculation**
2. **PDSCH and DCI Preparation**
3. **Artificial Load (5G\_L2\_1811)**
This second section also has external connections:
\* **Proposed and Scaled MCS Calculation** (5G\_L2\_15410) is connected to "TBS Calculation".
\* **DCI 1\_1 and PdschSendReq** (5G\_L2\_15461) is connected to "PDSCH and DCI Preparation".
The flowchart ends with a circular node after the "Artificial Load (5G\_L2\_1811)" step. Arrows indicate the flow of the process.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5511.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

**[CB014861-A]**If NRCELL.actExhaustiveScheduling is set to DL or DLAndUL, Exhaustive DL Scheduling to replace WRR(SU-WRR/MUMIMO algorithm), PDSCH Resource Reservation/Allocation is done by [12483324: Exhaustive Scheduling for RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qbsGEQ-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)**[End CB014861-A]**

The Dynamic switch functionality supported for MU-MIMO new tx users. Note: The dynamic switch functionality is not supported for MU-MIMO ReTx users.

**[Before CB014861-A]Subarea assignment shall be performed(Subarea Management) before Resource Reservation and PRBs will be allocated with in selected Subarea.**[CB014861-A]Subarea assignment shall be performed **after Exhaustive DL Scheduling step1 as [12483324: Exhaustive Scheduling for RAT1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qbsGEQ-XEfCvOvZoOJBSnQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)**[End CB014861-A]***\****\*

Subarea In case of TDD mode, when PDSCH scheduling in TRS/SSB slots, if NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True then L2-PS shall update the startPrb and the size of the PDSCH Subarea as a multiple of PRB granularity for each PDSCH subarea.

Prb granularity is equals to,

·    PRB\_GRANULARITY = 4 PRBs if the number of physical PRBs of the carrier bandwidth is greater than 200 PRBs

·    PRB\_GRANULARITY = 2 PRBs if 100 PRBs < dlBandwidth <= 200 PRBs

·    PRB\_GRANULARITY = 1 PRBs if dlBandwidth <= 100 PRBs

**Note: There is no DCI 1\_0 data scheduled in TRS/SSB slots**

**Note: In other cases (in FDD, TDD non FD beamforming, or in other slots), the PRB\_GRANULARITY is always set to 1.**

In FDM , if NRCELL.actConfDlInterferenceGen is set to a non-zero value, based on targetLoad , Fake UE Schedulability shall be performed and If it is decided to schedule Fake UE such slots Start PRB Randomization shall not be performed.

In FD , If its decided to schedule Fake UE , for such slots , based on needed resource to achieve targetLoad , FD scheduler shall decide and continue whether RAT1 or Dynamic Switch RAT0 based resource allocation for single Fake UE. Refer to 5G\_L2\_1811\_replaced([11212138](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-joAfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.33.0-2 Management of Downlink Resource Allocation Type1 Creation of PDSCH Sub Area (ID: 11177364)

**2.4.33.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11177387`)

Management of Downlink Resource Allocation Type1
[CB008927-H] Static SubAreas shall be created based on [Before CB008927-Q]Inner Gurad Band[CB008927-Q]For a FDD cell shared with LTE (FDD DSS) and when LTE CAT-M is enabled (NRCELL/NRDSSLTE.actLteNrDssLteCatM is set to true) ,gNB-DU shall create several PDSCH sub areas in the slot according to the number of the CAT-M NBs PRBs areas [End CB008927-Q] configuration on every slots.Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24780\_replaced([11175888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7M_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End CB008927-H] PDSCH Sub-Areas shall be additionally created either before selectUes in the case of Common Channel Scheduling or during the DCI 1\_0 scheduling while executing selectUes itself.

End of Sub-Area creation , Each Sub-Area shall be initialized with subAreaSize, startrb,reservedrb and remainingrb.

[Before CB008927-H]

If SA #0 is selected as largest subarea then FreePrb will be computed as, For Mono BWP, totalAvailableRb = totalRbs in SA #0 - { InitialBwpReservedRBs in SA #0 + DedicatedBwpReservedRbs in SA #0}

Note 1: Bundle 1 -> we will be using largest sub-area respect to largest bwp ,so sub-area selection is out of scope and largest subarea shall be either SA #0 or SA #1 according to the size.

[CB008927-H]

If SA 'i' is selected as subarea then FreePrb will be computed as, For single Largest BWP, totalAvailableRb = totalRbs in SA 'i' - { InitialBwpReservedRBs in SA 'i' + DedicatedBwpReservedRbs in SA 'i'}.

Note 1: Subarea assignement shall be performed before UE continue to resource reservation and selction of subarea according to 5G\_L2\_15531\_replaced([11177393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_266eLvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008927-H]

Note 2: In case, Resource Reservation happens for retransmission UE's before of Initial BWP UE's , PDSCH Sub Area creation happens only if any DCI 1\_0 UE request happens in subject to resources available in Initial BWP otherwise PDSCH Sub Area creation shall not proceed further.Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23607\_replaced([11176609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlkPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB008927-B]

Note 3: when the PRB blanking is enabled, according to low edge and/or high edge balnking configuration ,if Created Subarea is inclusive/partial part of blanking PRB's, size of PDSCH Sub Area should consider blanked prbs and get subtracted from actual subarea size.Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23605\_replaced([11175956](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7PfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

Note 4: In case of Specific Bandwidth, the start and size of carrier bandwidth to be used is specified and this specific bandwidth of carrier would be considered as PDSCH Sub Area for further calculations and schedulability.Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23605\_replaced([11175956](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7PfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

[Before CB008927-H]

Note 5: PDSCH Scheudling in TRS/SSB slot , For 24R2 only,max number of PRB's restricted such that minimum of largest PDSCH SubArea size or numPdschPrbInTrsSlots/numPdschPrbInSsbSlots shall be considered. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24048\_replaced([11177116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kutB1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB008927-H]

Note 5: PDSCH Scheudling in TRS/SSB slot , max number of PRB's restricted such that minimum of sum of all PDSCH SubArea size or numPdschPrbInTrsSlots/numPdschPrbInSsbSlots shall be considered. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24048\_replaced([11177116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kutB1PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008927-H]

[CB008927-I]

Note 6: In case of TDD mode, PDSCH Scheudling in TRS/SSB slot , if NRCELL.actBeamforming = True and NRBTS.actFdmEnhancedScheduling = True then L2-PS shall update the startPrb and the size of the PDSCH subarea as a multiple of PRB granularity for each PDSCH subarea. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24010\_replaced([11175959](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7PvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

[End CB008927-I]

[End CB008927-B]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to 5G network scheduling. The flowchart begins with a "Start" node, followed by "Scheduler Initialization."
The process then branches into two main paths within a rounded rectangle labeled "FDM Scheduler." The first path checks "Is Preempt Resources Due to SSB." If "Yes," it proceeds to a timeline visualization. If "No," it moves to the next check.
The second path within "FDM Scheduler" checks "Is Preempt Resources Due to SIB/Osi/Paging." If "Yes," it also leads to a timeline visualization. If "No," it proceeds to a section labeled "Select Ues."
Within "Select Ues," the flowchart checks "IsAnyUeInInitialBWP." If "Yes," it proceeds to "UeSchedulabilityChecker::IsInitialBwpResourceCheckIsOk." If "No," it branches to a timeline visualization.
If "UeSchedulabilityChecker::IsInitialBwpResourceCheckIsOk" returns "Yes," the process checks "areSubAreasMade==false." If "No," it leads to "Resource Reservation." If "Yes," it proceeds to "SubArea Helper::makeSubAreas."
The "Resource Reservation" node has a dashed arrow leading to "SubAreaHelper::updateSubAreas" and "SubAreaHelper::addUeToBeAllocatedList."
The "SubArea Helper::makeSubAreas" node has a dashed arrow leading to "Create UeInitialBwpSubAreas SubAreasOfBwps." This node, in turn, has an upward arrow leading to a blue rectangle stating "After made subareas, Update areSubAreasMade == true."
There are several timeline visualizations on the right side of the flowchart, each associated with specific conditions or steps. These timelines show segments labeled with terms like "SG\_UP\_ALG\_23455," "SG\_UP\_ALG\_23606," "SG\_UP\_ALG\_23462," "SSB," "SIB/Paging," "Coreset0 SA #1," "Coreset0 SA #0," "SG\_UP\_ALG\_2343," "SG\_UP\_ALG\_23468," "SG\_UP\_ALG\_23633," "DCI\_10," and "Coreset O SA #0." These timelines likely represent the allocation or scheduling of resources over time.
Finally, there is a blue rectangle at the bottom labeled "Update Reserved PRB in Selected SubArea within UE BWP SubAreasOfBwps."
The flowchart uses standard flowchart symbols: rounded rectangles for start/end points, rectangles for processes, diamonds for decisions, and arrows for flow. Dashed arrows indicate a different type of connection, possibly asynchronous or informational.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5513.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[CB008927-D]

Multi BWP , Sub-Area creation will be only once based on Largest BWP and along with that other BWP Sub-Area's also created according to the BWP size.

End of Sub-Area creation , Each BWP will have list of Sub-Areas (Max 2 so far) and Each Sub-Area shall initialized with subAreaSize, startrb,reservedrb and remainingrb.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a timeline with three parallel horizontal bars, representing different processes or states. The bars are colored in shades of gray, yellow, and red. The top bar is entirely gray. The middle bar is gray for the first portion, then yellow, and then gray again. The bottom bar is gray, then yellow, and then gray.
Overlapping the first portions of the middle and bottom bars are three red rectangular blocks, each labeled "SSB". These red blocks are aligned vertically and occupy the same horizontal segment. The yellow segments of the middle and bottom bars extend further to the right than the red "SSB" blocks.
Below the horizontal bars, there are several vertical lines with arrows, indicating time intervals or durations. These lines are labeled with text that is too small to be legible in the provided image. However, the structure suggests a comparison of durations or phases across the three parallel tracks. The overall diagram appears to be a Gantt chart or a similar timeline visualization.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5513.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

Note: Bundle1 , Largest SubArea Index refers respect to Largest BWP which is constent for Small and Other BWP's.

[End CB008927-D]

[CB008927-A]

In case of, one or more than one UE is in Transient Phase which uses[Before CB008927-H] N-GBR Traffic or [End CB008927-H] other than N-GBR traffic , such UE's shall be considered and reserved through non-distributable resource.

[End CB008927-A]

[CB008927-H]

In case of, Subareas are not already created , either single or multiple UE's in Transient Phase which uses N-GBR Traffic , such UE's shall paritcipate in WRR then during distribution phase ,subarea shall be created then distributable resources shall be reserved within coreset0 available RB's.

[End CB008927-H]

[CB008927-I]

In case of TDD mode ,either mono or multi BWP configured, In SSB slot ,  If beam refinement / management is activated (NRCELL.beamSet.nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 = true) then subarea shall be updated as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24904\_replaced([11176320](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kupXePvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) such cases subarea where CSI-RS is being transmitted shall not be used for PDSCH Scheduling [PR769456] when slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed set as False otherwise subarea shall be created above or below based on csirsBmMgmtSubband configuration [End PR769456] .

[End CB008927-I]

[CB008927-L]

In a slot where Dynamic Switch RAT0 for Re-Tx is being scheduled , additional subareas creation is possible along with that existing subarea also shall get updated.Refer to  5G\_UP\_ALG\_23623\_replaced([11175867](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kumULfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25183\_replaced([11176710](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlmPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If Dynamic Switch RAT0 UE From BWP #1, then
