For UsableRbg ,availableRbg in selected Subarea and UE's Required RBG ,Refer to 5G\_L2\_16530\_replaced([11177672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_267sT_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Rank for each UE shall be calculated this way

Gain = MIN{Remaining Required RB, Sum of AvailableRB’s in Each Subarea within UE’s BWP}

If Gain is equal for one or multiple UE’s such cases priority shall considered this order

* UEs from Largest BWP then
* Service Class as N-GBR compare GBR
* select Random UE

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25657\_replaced([11181438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_Vs_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note: Dynamic Switch Eligible UE List shall have GBR and/or NBR and/or N-GBR Service Class UE's

Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24995\_replaced([11181383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24998\_replaced([11181413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-urvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for dynamic switch support for MU-MIMO new tx users.

**[CB014474-A]  
With actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, more than one initial transmission UE part of vUE can get resource allocated by dynamic switch RAT0.**

The procedure to select one SU-MIMO or vUE among multiple of those for dynamic switch RAT0 allocation is based on RANK algorithm ([13257945](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_gy0R2cnoEfCYBIz6acpPHw))

**[End CB014474-A]**

**2.4.33.0-7.0-5**  (ID: `11177723`)

Management of Downlink Resource Allocation Type1
Execution Steps For Retransmission RAT0 UE

---

**2.4.33.0-7.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `13244939`)

Management of Downlink Resource Allocation Type1
**[Before CB014474-A] Selection of Dynamic Switch RAT0 Retransmission shall performed for only Initial Tx as Dynamic Switch RAT0 UE's only.**

Note: In case of retransmission gNB-DU shall not consider a MU-MIMO user for dynamic switch functionality. Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25093\_replaced([11181634](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_VvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  
**[End CB014474-A]**

**[CB014474-A]**

When actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, MU-MIMO retransmission user is eligible for dynamic switch RAT0 resource allocation ([12678834)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_QMNNoU2hEfCeeMlEb49MrQ)

The selection of retransmission UE as a candidate for dynamic switch RAT0 is done during the DL FDM reservation phase.

The criteria to select retransmission UE as candidate for dynamic switch RAT0 as mentioned below,

- needed PRB for retransmission UE is more than available PRB in the largest subarea and

- needed PRB for retransmission UE is less than the sum of available PRBs in all the subarea the UE is mapped to.

If other MU-MIMO UE's paired (vUE) along with the dynamic switch candidate retransmission UE, those UE's are eligible for dynamic switch RAT0 resource allocation, if it has more buffer to drain after RAT1 allocation and the UE is dynamic switch capable.

More than one MU-MIMO retransmission UE passes the schedulability check in DL FDM reservation phase based on MU available resources and assuming to be paired during vUE creation. If the retransmission UE does not pair with other UE's and if it does not get its needed resource during vUE creation, the retransmission UE will be downgraded to new transmission UE. The new transmission downgraded to retransmission will be still retained as MU eligible.  
**[End CB014474-A]**

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to scheduling and resource allocation for a User Equipment (UE). The flowchart begins with a "Start" oval.
From the start, a rectangular box contains the text: "schedulableUe, required Rb, RBG Size, maxCell AllocRb, num Rato Ue Scheduled and subArea List for UE's selected BWP". An arrow points from this box to another rectangular process box.
The flowchart then proceeds with a series of decision points (diamond shapes) and process steps (rectangular shapes) connected by arrows. The decision points are labeled with "Yes" or "No" branches.
There are two circular markers labeled "A" (green) and "B" (red) on the left side of the flowchart. Marker "A" is connected to a rectangular box containing three numbered steps:
"1. Update Pre-Booked RBG Bitmap into Reserved RBG Bitmap.
2. Update num Rat0UeScheduled++.
3. Add UE into ratOToBeAllocated List"
Marker "B" is at the end of a path in the flowchart.
On the right side of the flowchart, there is a rectangular box with dashed arrows pointing to and from it. This box contains the text: "Update Pre-Book RBG Bitmap Position according Subarea Direction of BWP1 or BWP2. Refer to 5G\_L2\_16594".
The overall flow seems to represent a scheduling or resource allocation logic, possibly within a telecommunications context, given the terms like "UE", "RB", "RBG", and "BWP".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5551.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

---

**2.4.33.0-7.0-6**  (ID: `13257945`)

Management of Downlink Resource Allocation Type1
Algorithm to select UE or vUE for for Dynamic Switch RAT0 resource allocation

---

**2.4.33.0-7.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `13257954`)

Management of Downlink Resource Allocation Type1
**[CB014474-A]  
With actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, all UE's within a virtual UE can undergo dynamic RAT0 resource allocation ( [12681502)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VqoMFDnEfCeeMlEb49MrQ)**

The selection of a vUE or SU-MIMO UE for dynamic RAT0 allocation is done through a algorithm based on Transport Block Size (TBS) as described below,

For a MU paired UE (vUE),  
 - TBS for each UE part of a vUE was calculated during virtual UE formation

-

vUEₖ Rank =  ∑  Needed PRBᵢ for UEᵢ in a vUEₖ after RAT1 allocation \* Rank of UEᵢ  
                     i∈u

SUₖ Rank =  Needed PRBᵢ for SU UEₖ after RAT1 allocation \* Rank of  UEₖ

where,

UEᵢ = Dynamic Switch Eligible UE in vUEₖ and has more buffer to drain in the current slot after its RAT1 allocation.

Rank of UEᵢ = The calculated RANK of UEᵢ part of vUEₖ

∑   =  Sum of all the Dynamic Switch Eligible UE's in vUEₖ which has more buffer to drain in the current slot after its RAT1 allocation

i∈u

Above algorithm is used for any SU-MIMO UE's to get it selected for dynamic RAT0 resource allocation along with other vUE's.

Whichever UE (SU-MIMO or vUE) gets the highest RANK is selected for dynamic RAT0 resource allocation.

When the calculated RANK is same for more than one vUE, select the vUE top in the vUE list.

When the calculated RANK is same SU and vUE, select vUE for getting higher resource utilization.

**[End CB014474-A]**

---

#### 2.4.33.0-7.1 Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch (ID: 13336150)

**2.4.33.0-7.1-1.0-1**  (ID: `13311090`)

Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch
**[CB014474-A]**

With actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, txPowerFactor to be calculated ([11145571](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_uIb1gPvLEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the new vUE(s) that are formed in another PDSCH sub-area due to dynamic switch RAT0 allocation

MU-SINR need to recalculated ([11143425](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_lLfIoPvLEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using the new txPowerFactor for all the UE's part of a vUE undergone dynamic switch RAT0 resource allocation.

Recalculate new average MU-SINR ([11143701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_mSJOsPvLEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the MU paired UE's which under-went dynamic RAT0 resource allocation to calculate the final MCS.

**[End CB014474-A]**

---

**2.4.33.0-7.1-2.0-3**  (ID: `13245018`)

Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch
For Prebooking Candidate RBG's for Dynamic Switch UE's shall be followed 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25096\_replaced([11181674](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8xvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Initial Tx  and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25644\_replaced([11176733](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqlm_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Re-Tx UE.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a timeline with four parallel tracks. Each track is divided into segments of varying lengths, indicated by vertical dashed lines. The segments are colored white, blue, yellow, and gray, with some tracks featuring dotted patterns within their white segments.
The diagram includes several horizontal arrows and labels indicating durations and relationships between different parts of the timeline.
Specifically, the tracks are labeled from top to bottom. The top two tracks are identical, showing a white segment followed by a blue segment labeled "DCIIO" repeated twice, then a yellow segment, and finally a white segment. The third track starts with a white segment, followed by two "DCIIO" segments, a yellow segment, and then a gray segment. The bottom track begins with a white segment with a dotted pattern, followed by two "DCIIO" segments, a yellow segment, and a gray segment with a dotted pattern.
The diagram also shows several time markers and duration indicators. For example, there are arrows indicating durations for the "DCIIO" segments and the yellow segments. There are also overall duration indicators for the entire timeline.
The text "DCIIO" appears multiple times within the blue segments.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5554.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[CB014474-A]

For Re-Tx Case , All RBGs shall be pre-booked starting from the largest subarea. Subsequently, based on the remaining RBGs, subareas shall be selected according to the availability of RBGs in each subarea.

For SU-MIMO Re-Tx Case, if rat1Alloc = true and actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true, UE's belonging to Large, Other BWP, Small and Power Saving BWP's are eligible for Dynamic RAT0 allocation

For Initial Tx Case , after WRR ,If  rat1Alloc = true and actEnhDynSwitchRat0 = true,  UE's belongs to either the Large, Other BWP, Small and Power Saving BWP's ([11181383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_tuAb8PvOEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and is reserved for NBR or N-GBR service, along with the required PRBs being higher than the largest subarea and based on UE's Rank in decreasing order shall checked as per 5G\_L2\_16528\_replaced([11177647](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_267sTPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and then UE selected for dynamic switch RAT0, then, based on UE's reserved RB count in subarea 'x', valid RBGs shall be pre-booked as per direction rules and then based on the remaining required RBGs, subareas shall be chosen according to the availability of RBGs in each subarea and continued RBG Pre-Booking.

[End CB014474-A]

RBG Pre-Book direction shall be decided based on subarea location either Large or  Other BWP or Small or  power saving BWP and by default direction is low2high for bottom or high2low for top.

Note : If Cell has Asymmetric Configuration where max three subareas shall be created and subarea selection shall be performed for pre-booking candidate RBG's.

Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24995\_replaced([11181383](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku-uqPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25094\_replaced([11181651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku_8wvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for dynamic switch support for MU-MIMO new tx users.

---

**2.4.33.0-7.1.0-1**  (ID: `13336152`)

Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch
Sub cell limit will be applied in the other sub-area for the dynamic switch RAT0 allocations for the MU paired UE's.

Limit is applied by taking MIN(rmMuAvailableNumSubcellPrb, availablePrbsPerBw)  
 - availablePrbsPerBwp = total available cell PRB in the large BWP after RAT1 vUE allocation allocation

-rmMuAvailableNumSubcellPrb = total available sub cell PRB in the large BWP after RAT1 vUE allocation allocation

---

#### 2.4.33.0-8 Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch FD Resource Allocation (ID: 11177767)

**2.4.33.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `12620825`)

Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch
,If actDlInterferenceAvoidance (see 5G\_L2\_IF\_73\_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is enabled, HP UE's allocation shall be done as per 5G\_L2\_17718\_replaced([11177588](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_267FP_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**If actDlMuMimoEnh is enabled and if actDlInterferenceAvoidance is enabled, then disable interworking with DL interference avoidance feature in current slot. please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23918\_replaced([11182175](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvBx8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

Note: When there is no MU paired users in as slot then consider these non paired MU users as SU users and then start the allocation from low to high.

If rat0ToBeAllocatedList is not empty then RAT0 Allocation shall be peformed based on prebookRbgBitmap(Refer to 5G\_L2\_16593\_replaced([11177744](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_268TVfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and update start and availableRb's for Initial BWP Allocation.

FD shall allocate resources as defined in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23918\_replaced([11182175](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvBx8PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If Randomization is disabled

Initial BWP Allocation shall performed during SelectUes Execution itself and shall maintain existing Approach.

Result  : {startPRb,numPRb after Coreset0 Allocation in SA0 and startPRb,numPRb after Coreset0 Allocation in SA1}

Dedicated BWP UE's Allocation shall perform like

If actDlMuMimoEnh is enabled, the dedicated large BWP UEs allocation shall perform as,

[image-1][image\_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a timeline or sequence of events, likely related to wireless communication scheduling. It is divided into several colored blocks, each representing a different component or state.
From left to right, the blocks are:
1. **SA 1 Available RB's**: This block is colored light green and labeled "SA 1 Available RB's". It appears to represent a period where Resource Blocks (RB's) are available for a specific service or scenario (SA 1).
2. **DCI 1\_0**: This block is colored light blue and labeled "DCI 1\_0". DCI typically refers to Downlink Control Information, and "1\_0" might indicate a specific format or version.
3. **CC**: This block is colored red and labeled "CC". The meaning of "CC" is not specified but could represent Carrier Component, Control Channel, or another relevant term.
4. **DCI 1\_0**: Another light blue block labeled "DCI 1\_0", similar to the second block.
5. **SU DCI 1\_1**: This block is colored gold and labeled "SU DCI 1\_1". "SU" might stand for Single User, and "DCI 1\_1" another format of Downlink Control Information.
6. **Free RBs**: This block is colored light green and labeled "Free RBs". It indicates a period where Resource Blocks are not allocated or are available.
7. **MU DCI 1\_1**: This block is colored dark orange and labeled "MU DCI 1\_1". "MU" could stand for Multi-User, and "DCI 1\_1" again refers to a format of Downlink Control Information.
The diagram also includes several blue arrows and brackets indicating durations, relationships, or time spans between different parts of the sequence. Specifically:
\* A long double-headed arrow spans the entire diagram, suggesting the total duration or scope of the illustrated sequence.
\* Shorter double-headed arrows indicate the duration of specific blocks or groups of blocks.
\* A bracket with an upward-pointing arrow highlights the combined duration of the "DCI 1\_0", "CC", and the second "DCI 1\_0" blocks.
\* A blue arrow pointing to the right is positioned below the "SU DCI 1\_1" block, possibly indicating a progression or a signal flow.
\* A short blue double-headed arrow is positioned below the "MU DCI 1\_1" block, indicating its duration.
The overall presentation suggests a structured allocation or transmission of control information and resources over time.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5556.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

As part of virtuval UE construction while allocation of resources for constructed virtval UE then the step size should be followed as,

Calculate the smallest value of (reqPrbUe - reqPrbVirtualUe), and this will be the stepSize.

For +ve case, allowedPrbUe will be incremented in order starting from rootUe.

For -ve case, allowedPrbUe will be decremented in reverse order, retaining the allowedPrbUe of rootUe intact (to ensure this remains the rootUe in the next iteration)

Input :

SA#0 startPRbInSa0 = {startPRb + numPRb} , numPRbInSa0 = numPRbInSa0 - {startPRb + numPRb}

SA#1 startPRbInSa1 = CRB0 , numPRbInSa1 = numPRbInSa1 - {startPRb + numPRb}

Allocation :

//select Bwp --> select from Low to High (Initial-->Small--> Other -> Large)

For(BwpIndex : BwpList)

{

//Retrive subAreaListInBwp -> According to BWP Index

For(subArea 'i': subAreaListInBwp)

{

If remainingRb in Cell or Bwp is Empty

Retain  startPRbInSa[x] for selected subArea 'i'

Else

startPRbInSa[x] shall computed according to 5G\_L2\_15659\_replaced([11177605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_267sQvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Endif.

//Get toBeAllocatedList from SubArea

while(toBeAllocatedList is not empty)

{

//select UE from toBeAllocatedList

Ue[i].startPRb = startPRbInSa[x]; //i is Ue Index in selected subArea , x -> SA#0 startPRbInSa0 ,SA#1 startPRbInSa1

Ue[i].numPRb = toBeAllocatedList[i].reservedPRb;

startPRbInSa#x += Ue[i].numPRb;

}

}

}

For Multi BWP , Allocation starts from Smaller BWP SubArea's to Larger BWP SubAreas { Initial -> Small -> Other -> Large}

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a timeline or schedule, likely related to telecommunications or data transmission, illustrating the allocation of resources over time. It is structured into three rows, each labeled "SA1 Available RB's," suggesting these are different streams or users of available Resource Blocks (RB's).
The timeline is divided into distinct segments, each representing a period of time and assigned a specific function or data type. These segments are color-coded and labeled with abbreviations.
The first segment across all three rows is labeled "SA1 Available RB's" and is colored light green. This indicates the initial availability of resources.
Following this, there are segments labeled "DCI1\_0" in light blue. These appear to be control information or data packets.
Next, there are segments labeled "CC" in red, which could represent Carrier Components or some form of data transmission.
After the "CC" segments, there are more "DCI1\_0" segments in light blue, followed by yellow segments labeled "DCI1\_0".
The timeline then progresses to segments labeled "DCI1\_1" in purple and brown, and a light green segment also labeled "DCI1\_1". There are also segments colored light grey.
Arrows are used to indicate the direction of time flow and to denote the duration or start/end points of certain segments. Specifically, a large blue arrow spans a significant portion of the timeline, indicating a general progression. Smaller blue arrows with arrowheads point to specific time points or durations associated with the segments.
Below the main timeline, there are horizontal lines with arrowheads, likely representing different time intervals or measurement points. These are not explicitly labeled with text but are visually distinct.
The overall impression is a representation of how resources are scheduled and utilized over time, with different types of data or control information being transmitted in distinct time slots.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m24dng\_5G\_L2\_Entity\_Level\_file\_5556.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

If actDlMuMimoEnh is enabled and multi BWP configured then Allocation starts like,

1) In large BWP, if any MU paired users are present then start allocation from higher to lower direction

2) In large BWP, if there is no MU paired users are present but there is non paired MU users in a slot then keep track these non MU paired users and allocate these users after allocation of SU users.

3) SU users allocation shall start from smaller BWP to larger BWP users with low to high direction.

4) In large BWP, if any non paired MU users are present then start allocation from low to high direction.

Result :

Allocated UE List with each UE's startRb ,numRb

If actDlMuMimoEnh is enabled and if Randomization is enabled, please refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_24810\_replaced([11182413](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvDAHPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**[CB012971-C]**

If  ZF\_MU is enabled (actDlMuMimoEnh=True, actDlMuPairingGroup = True) ,{

If SB BF UEs are paired with SVD-RZF in virtualUE(A),  due to L1's restriction, ~~L2PS shall align virtual UE's start PRB to BF subband's boundary.  It is difficult for L2PS to make sure that single subband BF UE is allocated in  a BF subband and and allocate PRB efficiently.~~

~~With the direction for MU UE's PRB allocation, Uei  in same ZF\_MU virtual UE shall have same  endPrb , Then L2Ps will only need to adjust  ZF\_MU UEs' startPrb to align with BF subband's boundary by reducing some PRB which is allocated in FDM ([12620818: ZF\_MU Virtual UE PRB Allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_o-zvMDlWEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)) , this will cause some PRB's are useless.~~

~~The UEin a ZF\_MU virtual UE, with allocated PRB  = reqPrbVirtualUeA .~~

~~IF  this UE with max number of PRB allocation ,  it's startPrb is aligned with BF subband's boundary , then other UEs in this Virtual UE doesn't need to do the adjustment.~~

~~Else   all UEs in this virtual UE,   # PRB\_diff = (reqPrbVirtualUeA -  reqPrbUei)~~

~~if #PRB\_diff >= bfsubbandSize, there is no need to adjust this UEi 's startPrb~~

~~else  reducePrb= [Rounddown (UEi\_startPrb /bfsubbandSize ) +1 ]\*bfsubbandSize  - UEi\_startPrb~~

~~adjust\_startPrb=startPrb+reducePrb~~

~~end if~~

~~End IF~~

~~where~~

~~For reqPrbVirtualUeA,reqPrbUei see [12620818: ZF\_MU Virtual UE PRB Allocation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_o-zvMDlWEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)~~

~~For bfsubbandSize  and PRB to BF subband mapping see [11171483: PRB to BF SB mapping](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26OhpPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).~~

~~SFS see [11182170: Without DL FD resource Randomization](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvBK7vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)~~

and with Option 2 is selected, see [12627592: Force SU Check for ZF\_MU](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ZKCXITrQEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9),  L2PS shall make sure the UEs in the same ZF\_MU virtual UE have same startPrb.

Note, If a ZF\_MU eligible UE is not paired due to non MU candidate is found , this UE will be scheduled as SU virtual UE rather than SU. This doesn't impact the UE's  MCS and TBs calculation, because one  subband beamfroming UE can only be in single ZF\_MU virtual UE, also can't be allocated of reaming PRBs then no necessary to consider the MU\_SINR averaging across multi virtual UE.

}

[End CB012971-C]

#### 2.4.33.0-9 Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch Interface Impacts (ID: 11177790)

**2.4.33.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11177795`)

Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch
L2 -> L1 (PdschSendReq)

For every to-be-scheduled UE,

the following information that needs changes for DL RAT1 to the L1 DL using DlData\_PdschSendReq rat0Bitmap  is set to 0 to enable L1 for RIV based processing.

Note:Start Prb is the first PRB used by the UE's Active BWP

L2->UE (DCI11 Payload)

a. Total number of bits should be computed  to carry RIV => Compute and Store in CellDB -> BWP   Context.

b. RIV Computation.

c. [CB008927-J]If UE is ResourceAllocation Type is Dynamics Switch then MSB bit shall be set in Frequency Domain Resource Assignment field according to dynamic switch either RAT1 or RAT1 allocation. {1-RAT1 and 0-RAT0}. [End CB008927-J]

Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23475\_replaced([11178569](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku2LyPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details

[CB008927-B]

In case of PRB blanking, as we consider allocation from Low to High, Startprb will be the first PRB offset by blanked prbs from bottom of carrier bandwidth.

In case of specific Bandwidth, startprb will be the first PRB of this specific bandwidth.Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23456\_replaced([11175946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kum7O_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008927-B]

[CB008927-Q]

In case of FDD with DSS , in a slot where LTE CRS REs/LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH REs are present , such slots numLteCrsMappingRes calculated based on 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2995\_replaced([11125090](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqsrlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB008927-Q]

---

#### 2.4.33.0-10 Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch DL PDCCH Scheduling (ID: 11177799)

**2.4.33.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11177815`)

Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch
While scheduling the FD resources using the RAT1, please refer to the 5G\_L2\_335\_replaced([11150303](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24bK3PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for handling the DL PDCCH scheduling.

Below mentioned changes are the specific to RAT1 while handling the DL PDCCH scheduling.

If the NIDD parameter NRCELL.actRat1Alloc = True, while determination of PDSCH start symbol for multiple dedicated BWP in TDD FR1, L2-PS shall calculate the PDSCH resources from available PDCCH symbol with granularity of PRBs instead of RBGs. This PRB granularity resources are assigned from each PDSCH subarea. Refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17072\_replaced([11178235](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku09rvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

While determine the PDSCH resource from PDCCH symbol, there are three different CORRESET BWP overlapping are possible while configured multiple BWPs.

1. CORESET of smaller BWP is fully overlapping with the CORESET of larger BWP

In any case while allocating the PRbs for UEs belonging to the corresponding BWP, if the prbs reserved from PDCCH symbol 0 is not overlapped with prbs reserved in PDCCH symbol 1 then those prbs from PDCCH symbol 1 are considered as available for PDSCH scheduling in the slot.

---

#### 2.4.33.0-11 Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch CPU improvement for RAT1 feature (ID: 11177820)

**2.4.33.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `11177824`)

Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch
Skip PRB Randomization and DL Interference Avoidance

---

**2.4.33.0-11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11177829`)

Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch
As of now L2-PS skipping the PRB randomization and DL Interference Avoidance functionality when the channel BWP or largest BWP is utilized 80% of PRBs.

In SW side its implemented such a way that in re distribution phase where the availablePrbs is less than 20% of largest BWP then skipping the PRB randomization and DL Interference Avoidance functionality.

Please refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_31988\_replaced([11182115](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvBK6_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

Note: As of now in SW side L2-PS is not handling the perfect proposed threshold value in case of SSB, TRS slots and also prb pooling enabled scenarios due to SW complexity and further these extra checks cause CPU impacts.

---

**2.4.33.0-11.0-2**  (ID: `11177835`)

Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch
Skip PDSCH Sub Area Selection Algo

---

**2.4.33.0-11.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11177840`)

Sub cell Limit For Dynamic Switch
L2-PS skip the PDSCH Sub Area selection algo when the pdsch sub area size is less than 20 PRBs.

Please refer 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26464\_replaced([11176645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuqllvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details

---

## 2.4.34 Overload Protection

### 2.4.34.0-1 Overload Protection Overload Protection description was moved to the 2.13 Load control chapter. New Id requirement for Overload Protection is 5G_L2_21780_replaced( 11214379 ). (ID: 11177849)

## 2.4.35 Buffer monitoring

### 2.4.35.1 Buffer monitoring for Inter-RAT handover purposes

#### 2.4.35.1.1 Starting DL buffer status monitoring

**2.4.35.1.1.0-1**  (ID: `13317485`)

Starting DL buffer status monitoring
When 5G-L2-PS receives PsUser\_BufferMonitoringInd ([10978152](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VRj0Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for UE with bufferMonitoring.tputBasedHoActivateDl the following steps are taken:

1. Check if 5G-L2-PS CPU is not overloaded. If the 5G-L2-PS CPU is overloaded, 5G-L2-PS shall queue the request to start DL buffer status monitoring ([13282370: CPU overload based suspend/resume handling](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2O3zkcrAEfCYBIz6acpPHw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
2. Update UE's bufferMonitoring IE value, save dlConfigLevel and dlConfigLevelNum IE at DL in UE database.
3. If the request is received when the respective buffer monitoring is already running, 5G-L2-PS shall ignore the request about DL buffer monitoring activation.

Note:

1. 5G-L2-PS shall monitor non-GBR DL data buffer.
2. Detailed description of DL buffer status monitoring algorithm ([13282360: Algorithm](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_JIL4ZMrAEfCYBIz6acpPHw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).
3. rrmTputBasedIratHoDlBufferThresh(k) for PCell is calculated as  = MIN(estimatedFullBufferMaxTbs, SUM(estimatedFullBufferTbs)), where:

estimatedFullBufferTbs is delivered to P(S)Cell 5G-L2-PS only for the DL non-GBR DRBs, for which some data is still buffered in P(S)Cell. For DL non-GBR CA eligible DRBs, 5G-L2-PS checks also if rdActNoEstimatedFullBufferTbsForDrbWithNoDlData is enabled ([11124437: rdActNoEstimatedFullBufferTbsForDrbWithNoDlData](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqq2YvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). If yes, 5G-L2-PS starts verifying if any data is buffered in 5G-L2-PS P(S)Cell.  
estimatedFullBufferMaxTbs is used as a buffer size limiter as estimatedFullBufferTbs is not calculated every TTI and its value might be overstimated.
4. estimatedFullBufferMaxTbs is introduced by PR703192 for CA Scell addition scenarios and is used also in CB012554 in UE buffer monitoring for inter-RAT purposes.
5. When UE buffer monitoring is not started for any UE, and 5G-L2-PS subsequently starts UE buffer monitoring while a CPU overload is active, a 160ms evaluation delay prevents 5G-L2-PS from immediately recognizing the CPU overload. Consequently, if a buffer overload happens on that UE within this delay, 5G-L2-PS might send a PsUser\_BufferCriterionInd message before acknowledging the CPU overload condition.

---

##### 2.4.35.1.2 Suspending/resuming DL buffer status monitoring

###### 2.4.35.1.2.0-1 Suspending/resuming DL buffer status monitoring When 5G L2-PS detects overload in the CPU core on which the DL scheduler is running as described in 13282370: CPU overload based suspend/resume handling , L2-PS shall suspend DL buffer status monitoring with following actions: When DL buffer status monitoring is suspended 5G-L2-PS shall: Reset number of passed buffer monitoring checks. Suspend DL data buffer check for the UE; If the PsUser_BufferMonitoringInd with bufferMonitoring.tputBasedHoActivateDl is received. The request shall be ignored as described in 13284489: Starting DL buffer status monitoring ; If the PsUser_BufferMonitoringInd with bufferMonitoring.tputBasedHoDeactivateDl is received, 5G-L2-PS shall follow the procedure of stopping buffer status monitoring as described in 13285486: Stopping DL bufer status monitoring . (ID: 13347613)

#### 2.4.35.1.3 Stopping DL bufer status monitoring

##### 2.4.35.1.3.0-1 Stopping DL bufer status monitoring 5G-L2-PS shall stop buffer monitoring for the given UE if any of the following conditions are met: 5G-L2-PS determines the conditions to trigger PsUser_BufferCriterionInd message, as specified in 13287190: Triggering User Buffer Criterion Indication . Upon reception of PsUser_BufferMonitoringInd with bufferMonitoring.tputBasedHoDeactivateDl. Upon reception of PsUser_UserDeleteReq message and if the request message is valid. Note: If the request to stop DL monitoring is received when the respective monitoring is not running, the request shall be ignored. (ID: 13326874)

#### 2.4.35.1.4 Triggering User Buffer Criterion Indication

##### 2.4.35.1.4.0-1 Triggering User Buffer Criterion Indication 5G-L2-PS shall trigger PsUser_BufferCriterionInd.bufferCriterion set to tputBasedHoDl for the given user, when the conditions specified in 13282360: Algorithm are passed the following steps shall be applied: 5G-L2-PS stops buffer monitoring for inter-RAT handover purposes. 5G-L2-PS sends DL 10978239: PsUser_BufferCriterionInd message content to 5G-CP-RT. (ID: 13330236)

## 2.4.36 Load reporting

### 2.4.36.1 Cell non-GBR load reporting

#### 2.4.36.1.1 Starting non-GBR load monitoring

##### 2.4.36.1.1.0-1 Starting non-GBR load monitoring When 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell_LoadReportingReq ( 13390759 ) for cell with DlReportSettings and/or UlReportSettings.LoadReportSettings.command = start, DlReportSettings and/or UlReportSettings.LoadReportSettings.configBitRate filled the following steps are taken: Proceed with load monitoring algorithm for: DL: 13313700 UL: 13314453 If the request is received when the respective load reporting is already running, 5G-L2-PS shall reply to 5G-CP-RT with PsCell_LoadReportingResp. (ID: 13464320)

#### 2.4.36.1.2 Stopping non-GBR load monitoring

##### 2.4.36.1.2.0-1 Stopping non-GBR load monitoring 5G-L2-PS shall stop non-GBR load monitoring if any of the following conditions are met: Upon reception of PsCell_LoadReportingReq with DlReportSettings and/or UlReportSettings.LoadReportSettings.command = stop. Upon reception of PsUser_UserDeleteReq message and if the request message is valid. Note: If the request to non-GBR load monitoring is received when the respective monitoring is not running, 5G-L2-PS shall reply to 5G-CP-RT with PsCell_LoadReportingResp. (ID: 13467484)

#### 2.4.36.1.3 Updating non-GBR load monitoring

##### 2.4.36.1.3.0-1 Updating non-GBR load monitoring When 5G-L2-PS receives PsCell_LoadReportingReq ( 13390759 ) for cell with DlReportSettings and/or UlReportSettings.LoadReportSettings.command = update, DlReportSettings and/or UlReportSettings.LoadReportSettings.configBitRate filled the following steps are taken: Update configBitRate IE value which is used in load monitoring algorithm for DL: 13313700 , UL: 13314453 Note: Change of configBitRate IE is applied at the beginning of the next bbPoolEvalPeriod. (ID: 13467490)

#### 2.4.36.1.4 Triggering non-GBR load report

##### 2.4.36.1.4.0-1 Triggering non-GBR load report Every 500 msc 5G-L2-PS shall trigger: PSCell_DlLoadInd with nonGbrLoad IE filled to 5G-CP-RT and/or 2. PSCell_UlLoadInd with nonGbrLoad IE filled 5G-CP-RT. (ID: 13467500)

## 2.4.37 Recommended Bit Rate

### 2.4.37.0-1 Recommended Bit Rate Supported bearers and their addition/removal (ID: 13471780)

**2.4.37.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13471782`)

Recommended Bit Rate
L2-PS shall manage Recommended Bit Rate (RBR) functionality. This functionality applies to the following bearer types:

* GBR VoNR (5QI-1, [CB006093-C] 5QI-65/66 [End CB006093-C])
* [CB006093-B] GBR ViNR (5QI-2, [CB006093-C] 5QI-67 [End CB006093-C]) [End CB006093-B]

L2-PS shall update RBR index calculations according to the following rules:

1. When a new trigger is detected, then L2-PS shall include any newly added bearers in the RBR index calculation.
2. When a new trigger is detected, then L2-PS shall recalculate the RBR indexes for the remaining bearers after one or more bearers have been removed.

Triggers are defined in [13394312](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_nRBp0AaNEfGozuGzRUXHpg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/266).

For the bearer types specified above, L2-PS shall permanently stop RBR functionality for the UE's lifetime under the following conditions:

* When a bearer is created with an `rbrConfigProfileId` that does not correspond to an existing profile.
* When a bearer is created without an `rbrConfigProfileId`.

---

### 2.4.37.1 Configuration parameters

### 2.4.37.1-1 Configuration parameters

**2.4.37.1-1.0-1**  (ID: `13405907`)

Configuration parameters
L2-PS shall recalculate RBR indexes for UEs bearers when PHR report is received for this UE.

For the first time L2-PS shall perform calculation when minimal required number of PHR values is received.  
L2-PS determine the minimal number of samples based on value of rbrMeasTime, see [12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for details.

L2-PS shall also recalculate RBR indexes when RBR query is received, see [13437568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_4Cuk0CIKEfGbZ5_vsUrUNw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/266).

---

### 2.4.37.1-2 Configuration parameters

**2.4.37.1-2.0-1**  (ID: `13427214`)

Configuration parameters
L2-PS shall calculate average ABR based on instantaneous ABR samples, see [12799609](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_sjLOUpnAEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

1. If number of calculated samples of ABR is lower than Nabr, L2-PS shall calculate average ABR as mean value of ABR samples.
2. If number of calculated samples of ABR is equal or greater than Nabr, L2-PS shall calculate average ABR via exponential filter.

See [12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for Nabr and filter definition.

Notes:

L2-PS shall stop calculation of average ABR when there is no VoNR, ViNR bearers, and reinitialized it when new one is created.

---

### 2.4.37.1-3 Configuration parameters

**2.4.37.1-3.0-1**  (ID: `13394200`)

Configuration parameters
L2-PS shall distribute calculated average ABR between bearers of types listed in [13426802](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_jtDsWRugEfGX7t-UNMeXGQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

1. L2-PS shall allocate average ABR to VoNR bearer (5QI-1 [CB006093-C], 5QI-65/66 [End CB006093-C]), there is always at least one VoNR bearer configured.  
   [CB006093-A-UPD003]  
   For each VoNR bearer L2-PS shall use full average ABR for calculation of RBR index.  
   [End CB006093-A-UPD003]

[CB006093-B]
2. If UE has configured ViNR bearer(s) (5QI-2 [CB006093-C], 5QI-67 [End CB006093-C]).  
L2-PS shall convert [CB006093-A-UPD003] the highest [End CB006093-A-UPD003] RBR index from point 1 to corresponding bit rate, subtract it from full average ABR.
3. L2-PS shall use remaining average ABR calculated in point 2 and distribute it equally among existing ViNR bearers.  
L2-PS shall use result of this distribution for RBR index calculation for each ViNR bearer.  
[End CB006093-B]

Notes:  
L2-PS shall perform conversion between  bit rates and RBR index in both directions, based on standardized RBR table in 6.1.3.20-1(3GPP TS 38.321).  
See [12801244](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_K488QZq5EfCahcI91JQtLg) for RBR index calculation.

---

### 2.4.37.1-4 Configuration parameters

#### 2.4.37.1-4.0-1 Configuration parameters L2-PS shall manage RBR index calculation: For uplink, L2-PS shall determine RBR indexes based on ABR (absolute bit rate) available for given bearer, see 12799843 for details. L2-PS shall convert ABR to RBR index, which has the highest bit rate not bigger than ABR, based on standardized RBR table in 6.1.3.20-1 (3GPP TS 38.321). For downlink, L2-PS shall determine value of RBR index based on parameter rbrDlQueryRespMode: UL_RBR, L2-PS shall reply with RBR index calculated based on granted ABR in UL [CB006093-A-UPD003] , limited by GFBR for DL [End CB006093-A-UPD003] . noRecommendation, L2-PS shall reply with 0, which means that there is no recommendation for bitrate. See 12815741 , 12816238 for RBR index calculation for solicited message. See 12801244 for RBR index calculation for unsolicited message. See 12816232 for RBR index calculation in case of multi ViNR bearers. (ID: 13364809)

### 2.4.37.1-5 Configuration parameters

**2.4.37.1-5.0-1**  (ID: `13366994`)

Configuration parameters
When one of the following events occurs, L2-PS shall inform L2-LO to send a bit rate recommendation message:

1. Unsolicited Trigger (UL only): A change in the value of the calculated RBR index for a given GBR bearer fulfills the condition defined by the parameters  
   rbrStepSizeDown, rbrStepSizeUp, rbrIndxAudioLoThreshold, rbrIndxVideoLoThreshold, and GFBR.  
   See [12801188](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qeBtkJquEfCahcI91JQtLg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [12801244](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_K488QZq5EfCahcI91JQtLg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for details.
2. Solicited Trigger (UL or DL): A bit rate recommendation query is received for a given bearer, identified by its LCID and direction.

L2-PS shall consider RBR index sent in last message as previous value of RBR index in future calculation.

L2-PS shall send a LoCtrl\_PduMuxReq message that includes one entry in the rbrMacCe dynamic array per RBR message.  
Filled in with the following parameters:

* LCID of the bearer
* Direction of transmission (UL or DL)
* RBR index

Furthermore, L2-PS shall adhere to the following constraints:

* L2-PS shall not trigger PDSCH scheduling solely for the transmission of the bit rate recommendation message.
* L2-PS shall only add the bit rate recommendation message to an existing UE transmission for which the recommendation is sent. See 12817115 for details.

---

### 2.4.37.1-6 Configuration parameters

#### 2.4.37.1-6.0-1 Configuration parameters L2-PS shall overwrite existing RBR message, which waits for transmission, with new one for the same LCID and direction by updating RBR index. If unsolicited overwrites solicited, then L2-PS shall update counters related to solicited messages, when message is sent. (ID: 13471223)

### 2.4.37.1-7 Configuration parameters

#### 2.4.37.1-7.0-1 Configuration parameters L2-PS receives information about queries in LoCtrl_UlMacPduReceiveInd, as entry in rbrMacCe array. Each query contains information about LCID of bearer for which query was received and direction (UL, DL). L2-PS shall reply for each query by sending bit rate recommendation message, see 12803195 , 13340274 . (ID: 13353122)

#### 2.4.37.1.0-1 Configuration parameters Recommended Bit Rate activation/deactivation and configuration parameters (ID: 13426802)

**2.4.37.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13354348`)

Configuration parameters
1. Feature activation/deactivation.

L2-PS shall support feature initial enabling with actRbr in PsCell\_CellSetupReq and runtime modification with parameter actRbr in PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.  
When the feature is deactivated in runtime, L2-PS shall stop all ongoing RBR-related activities and shall reject any RBR queries.   
When the feature is activated in runtime, L2-PS shall apply it only to UEs that are setup after feature activation.

1. Configuration parameters

L2-PS shall receive configuration related to RBR in PsCell\_CellSetupReq and later shall support updates via PsCell\_CellReconfigurationReq.

There are three parameters configured at cell level:

* rbrMeasTime
* rbrOperMode
* rbrRatio

other parameters are configured per RBR configuration profiles in mentioned messages.

L2-PS shall use rbrConfigProfileId, received in  PsUser\_UserSetupReq/PsUser\_BearerSetupReq, to select and apply corresponding RBR profile configuration.

1. Supported messages

L2-PS shall determine messages supported by given UE based on its capability and cell configuration indicated by rbrOperMode.  
L2-PS shall obtain information about UE capability via IE rbrSupportedMessages from ueCapability in  PsUser\_UserModifyReq.

UE can support only unsolicited or both messages (solicited and unsolicited).

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| rbrOperMode / rbrSupportedMessages | gNbUnsolicitedAllowed | bothAllowed |
| UeQueryAllowed | no message | solicited |
| gNbUnsolicitedAllowed | unsolicited | unsolicited |
| bothAllowed | unsolicited | unsolicited/solicited |

---

#### 2.4.37.1.0-2 Configuration parameters Online modification (ID: 13426819)

**2.4.37.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13377944`)

Configuration parameters
L2-PS shall support online modification of parameters related to RBR as described below.

1. rbrRatio, L2-PS uses this parameter to scale calculated ABR for UE.  
   It is used as multiplier of ABR, and result is used to calculate RBR Indexes for UEs.  
   Because of that after change of this parameter L2-PS shall reinitialize calculation of ABR, what means that required number of PHR samples has to be received before new recommended RBR will be calculated, see [12801188](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_qeBtkJquEfCahcI91JQtLg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).
2. rbrMeasTime, defines filter constant and indicates how many samples has to be collected before average ABR is used for RBR index calculation, see [12799843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_rtCZEJnXEfC7Wp-sWxcfJw?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).  
   If number of collected samples is lower than new Nabr, then L2-PS shall wait with calculation of RBR until required number is received,  
   otherwise L2-PS shall calculate RBR index.  
   If L2-PS was using filter to calculate "average ABR" or new Nabr is not higher than already received number of samples,   
   then continue calculation using new filter coefficient, otherwise L2-PS continue calculation of average value of ABR.  
   [CB006093-A-UPD002]
3. If RBR profile is deleted then L2-PS shall stop functionality related to RBR for all UEs that have bearer configured with this RBR profile, for its life time.
4. actRbr, when FAF modified from True to False then L2-PS shall stop functionality related to RBR for all existing UEs, for their life time.  
   When modified from False to True, only UEs created after this modification have active RBR functionality.  
   [End CB006093-A-UPD002]

In case of modification of any other parameter related to RBR L2-PS shall use its new value.

---

## 2.4.38 Tranform Precoder activation (TP On)

### 2.4.38.1 TP On activation on Msg3

#### 2.4.38.1.0-1 TP On activation on Msg3 TP On on Msg3 (ID: 13381541)

**2.4.38.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13372906`)

TP On activation on Msg3
[Before CB008148-A-UPD006]

All the Msg3 and ReTx for Msg3 should be set to tpOn (DFTs Transform Precoder) when the bit Msg3\_TpOn is set to 1 in field ulTransPrecoder/actUlTransPrecoderEnh in CellSetupReq or CellReconfigurationReq ( [10969528](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UdEffu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)).

[CB008148-A-UPD006]

All the Msg3 and ReTx for Msg3 are set to tp On (DFTs Transform Precoder) if actMsg3TP is set to TRUE in cellSetupReq ([10967202: PsCell\_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_wZAhEPu3Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), on line modification of actMsg3TP is not supported, and need a cell reset.

[End CB008148-A-UPD006]

---

### 2.4.38.2 TP On activation on DCI0_0

#### 2.4.38.2.0-1 TP On activation on DCI0_0 TP On on DCI0_0 (ID: 13381159)

**2.4.38.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13372909`)

TP On activation on DCI0\_0
[Before CB008148-A-UPD006]

TpOn activation for DCI0\_0 is a UE based activation to be aligned with the information received by UE from RRC messages. L2-PS follow the activation flags given by CP-RT on UE messages.

It will be initialized by the bit Msg3\_TpOn received in field ulTransPrecoder/actUlTransPrecoderEnh in CellSetupReq/CellReconfigurationReq ( [10969528](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UdEffu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)  ) but could be overwritten by the value actDCI00TpOn received in message UserSetupReq( [10974651](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw) )/UserModifyReq ( [10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw) ) if that field is present in those messages. ( See [12977641](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2g3-ULXfEfCQT7hYUxERAg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for system explanation )

[CB008148-A-UPD006]

TP On activation for DCI0\_0 is enabled if actMsg3TP is set to TRUE in cellSetupReq ([10967202: PsCell\_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_wZAhEPu3Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), independently Msg3/DCI0\_0 TP ON setting is not allowed, on line modification is not supported, need a cell reset.

[End CB008148-A-UPD006]

---

### 2.4.38.3 TP on activation on DCI0_1

#### 2.4.38.3.0-1 TP on activation on DCI0_1 TP On on DCI0_1 (ID: 13381163)

**2.4.38.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13373612`)

TP on activation on DCI0\_1
TP On activation for DCI0\_1 is a UE based activation,  L2-PS follows the activation flag sent by CP-RT during during UserSetupReq ([10974645: PsUser\_UserSetupReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) or UserModifyReq ([10976067: PsUser\_UserModifyReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) procedures for an UE

the DCI01 TP On activation flag is intialized to disable and will be ovewritten via the actDCI01TpOn parameter in the puschConfig structure([10975648: puschConfig structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VCTQPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) during UserSetupReq ([10974645: PsUser\_UserSetupReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) or UserModifyReq ([10976067: PsUser\_UserModifyReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_CqlKNu3yEe-rL47x_rCCmw)) procedures.

[Before CB008148-SR-B-UPD003]

For Initial Access: Stock the actDCI01TpOn flag if that field is present during UserSetupReq , This setting will be overwritten by actDCI01TpOn during a UserModifyReq. if the actDCI01TpOn flag is set enable in UserModifyReq, L2 PS will start DCI01 with TP ON after receiving the RRC Reconfiguration completed

for Handover (HO),  RRC Reestablishment (RRE) and RRC Resume: If the actDCI01TpOn flag is present and set to enable in userSetupReq, L2 PS will set DCI01 TP ON , irrespective of the source cell's TP state.

[CB008148-SR-B-UPD003]

For initial access,  RRC Reestablishment (RRE) and RRC Resume, the flag actDCI01TpOn is not present in userSetupReq,  if the actDCI01TpOn flag is set enable in UserModifyReq, L2 PS will stock this flag and start DCI 01 with TP on after receiving RRC Reconfiguration completed.

For Handover (HO) and NSA, the flag actDCI01TpOn is present in UserSetupReq, L2 PS will stock this flag and start DCI01 TP on after Ue attached if the actDCI01TpOn flag is set enable in UserSetupReq.

[End CB008148-SR-B-UPD003]

---

#### 2.4.38.3.0-2 TP on activation on DCI0_1 Interworking with High Reliabilty (ID: 13441620)

**2.4.38.3.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13441621`)

TP on activation on DCI0\_1
During UE setup processing, if UE starts with the TP on per ([13381163: TP On on DCI0\_1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_XwakIgAVEfGhFrIQm2O4Ew) ) :

* if UE is already in HR (High Reliabilty) mode in Uplink, fallback to BR (Basic Reliabilty) mode in Uplink ([11163997: Overview](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_H-FIYPvNEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and start DCI 01 TP on.
* if UE is configured in HR with adding a first DRB(s)  associated to HR ([10974876: drbAddReqPs structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_kODxsPu4Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) during UE setup processing, keep BR mode in Uplink ([11125162: Enable/Disable high reliability Link adaptation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_CympYPvKEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and start DCI 01 TP on.
* After UE setup processing, if a first DRB(s) associated to HR is added ([1097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_kODxsPu4Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)[4876: drbAddReqPs structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_kODxsPu4Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and UE is still in TP on,  keep the BR mode in Uplink ([11125162: Enable/Disable high reliability Link adaptation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_CympYPvKEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

## 2.4.39 Absolute timing advance

### 2.4.39.1 Absolute TA calculation

#### 2.4.39.1.0-1 Absolute TA calculation (ID: 13376677)

**2.4.39.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13376682`)

Absolute TA calculation
the absoluteTA is the value rrmAccumulatedTimingOffset defined in [11150386: Accumulated Timing Offset](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksTOFvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

the timingOffset is calculated from rrmUeLongTermTimingOffset, refer to[11150211: Long-Term TO Computation for TAC](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksSnB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

This timingOffset is sent to the UE in the TAC command to adjust the current timing advance of the UE. refer to [11150011Uplink Time Alignment Control](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksRY6fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

When the UE confirms a Timing Advance Command (TAC) by sending an ACK, then 5G-L2-PS shall update the value in Ue.taData.absoluteTa  in UE database.

---

### 2.4.39.2 Monitoring of UE absoluteTA

#### 2.4.39.2.0-1 Monitoring of UE absoluteTA Detection of UEs having a absoluteTA greater than the threshold taDiscThreshold (ID: 13376735)

**2.4.39.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13376755`)

Monitoring of UE absoluteTA
Basic principle

---

**2.4.39.2.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13376736`)

Monitoring of UE absoluteTA
if the flag actTaDisconnect is true, 5G-L2-PS shall monitor the absoluteTA of the connected UEs.  
if the absoluteTa value of a UE is greater or equal to the threshold taDiscThreshold for a duration greater or equal to taDiscTimer, that means UE has an exceededTA, then 5G-L2-PS shall send the message PsUser\_TaExceededInd message to 5G-CP-RT, containing the identity of this UE.

Note: The parameters actTaDisconnect, taDiscThreshold and taDiscTimer are received in PsCell\_CellSetupReq and PsCell\_cellReconfigurationReq messages.

---

**2.4.39.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13376757`)

Monitoring of UE absoluteTA
case of online configuration change

---

**2.4.39.2.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13376759`)

Monitoring of UE absoluteTA
The parameters actTaDisconnect, taDiscThreshold, and taDiscTimer are all online modifiable via the PsCell\_cellReconfigurationReq message.  
5G-L2-PS shall behave as follows:

* When the 'actTaDisconnect' parameter is set to 'false', 5G-L2-PS shall stop the TA disconnection monitoring.
* When the 'actTaDisconnect' parameter is set to 'true', 5G-L2-PS shall start the TA disconnection monitoring.
* When a new value for the 'taDiscTimer' parameter is received while 'actTaDisconnect' is 'true', 5G-L2-PS shall apply the new value the next time the TA disconnection timer is armed.
* When a new value for the 'taDiscThreshold' parameter is received while 'actTaDisconnect' is 'true', 5G-L2-PS shall use the new value for the next comparison between the absolute TA and the 'taDiscThreshold'.

---

**2.4.39.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13376792`)

Monitoring of UE absoluteTA
Sending the message PsUser\_TaExceededInd

---

**2.4.39.2.0-1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `13376796`)

Monitoring of UE absoluteTA
To reduce the frequency of the transmission of the message PsUser\_TaExceededInd. 5G-L2-PS shall send this message every 100ms minimum even if several UEs are in waiting list for this indication. If there is no UE candidate to the message, the message is not sent.

PsUser\_TaExceededInd message contains only one UE identity due to Cplane constraint. If several UEs have an exceeded absoluteTA, the selected UE for the message is the first UE detected in exceededTA state.

If only one UE has its absoluteTA >= taDiscThreshold for a long period, 5G-L2-PS shall send the message PsUser\_TaExceededInd periodically(note1) containing the UE identity.

if several UEs have their absoluteTA >= taDiscThreshold for a long period,  5G-L2-PS shall send the message PsUser\_TaExceededInd periodically(note1) and the identity of all these UEs shall be transmitted periodically one by one.

note1: the period is (taDiscTimer+taRetryDelay+ (+/-100ms)). taRetryDelay shall be well below minimal value of taDiscTimer =1second.

---

**2.4.39.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13378137`)

Monitoring of UE absoluteTA
Design overview

---

**2.4.39.2.0-1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `13378138`)

Monitoring of UE absoluteTA
if the feature flag actTaDisconnect is true, L2-PS manages a virtual state of the UE according the different steps.  
the states and the steps are defined in the state diagram below.  
the state is not stored in UE data base. three FIFO lists contain the list of UEs in the respective states 'taExceeded', 'ReadyToSent' and  'ExceededSent'.  
5G-L2-PS timer 'taRetryDelay' is set to 500ms.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating the states of a User Equipment (UE) in a connected state. The diagram is enclosed within a large rectangle labeled "UeConnected".
The diagram starts with an initial state represented by a filled circle, from which an arrow points to a state labeled "User Setup (1)".
From "User Setup (1)", an arrow leads to a diamond-shaped decision node. Two outgoing transitions from this node are labeled:
1. "[initialTa < taDiscThreshold (1)]" leading to the "Monitoring" state.
2. "[initialTa >= taDiscThreshold (1)]" leading to the "taExceeded" state.
The "Monitoring" state is a rectangular box with rounded corners. Inside, it has the following information:
- "Monitoring"
- "- UE absoluteTA < taDiscThreshold"
- "- no timer"
From the "Monitoring" state, there are two outgoing transitions:
1. An arrow labeled "[absoluteTa >= taDiscThreshold (2)]" leads to the "taExceeded" state.
2. An arrow labeled "[absoluteTa < taDiscThreshold (2)]" leads to the "ReadyToSend" state.
The "taExceeded" state is a rectangular box with rounded corners. Inside, it has the following information:
- "taExceeded"
- "- UE absoluteTA >= taDiscThreshold"
- "- taDiscTimer timer running"
From the "taExceeded" state, there are two outgoing transitions:
1. An arrow labeled "[taDiscTimer timeout (3)]" leads to the "ReadyToSend" state.
2. An arrow labeled "[absoluteTa >= taDiscThreshold]" leads to the "ExceededSent" state.
The "ReadyToSend" state is a rectangular box with rounded corners. Inside, it has the following information:
- "ReadyToSend"
- "- UE is candidate for next taExceededind message"
From the "ReadyToSend" state, there are two outgoing transitions:
1. An arrow labeled "[absoluteTa < taDiscThreshold]" leads to the "Monitoring" state.
2. An arrow labeled "[taExceededind message sent to Cplane (3)]" leads to the "ExceededSent" state.
The "ExceededSent" state is a rectangular box with rounded corners. Inside, it has the following information:
- "ExceededSent"
- "- taRetryDelay timer running"
From the "ExceededSent" state, there is one outgoing transition:
1. An arrow labeled "[timeout (3)]" leads to a diamond-shaped decision node.
From this diamond-shaped decision node, there is an arrow leading to the "Monitoring" state.
Finally, there is a transition labeled "User Delete (4)" originating from the "UeConnected" state and leading to a final state represented by a filled circle.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-02101542.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

source file: stored in attribute 'description' of this artifact.

(1): treatment done at userSetup event, using the initialTa received from L1 before the userSetup.

(2): treatment done when TAC command is confirmed by the UE, when 5G-L2-PS receives the ACK confirmation.

(3): treatment done every 100ms in postScheduling treatement.

(4): treatment done at userDelete event.

this implementation should allows to prevent an unexpected impact on 5G-L2-PS timing performance, for the treatment 1,2,3 and 4.  
However, as any other features, the activation of the feature syslog trace may have an impact due to the needed supplementary treatments to add some syslog traces.

---

**2.4.39.2.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13437714`)

Monitoring of UE absoluteTA
Design restriction

---

**2.4.39.2.0-1.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `13437716`)

Monitoring of UE absoluteTA
The size of the list containing the UE identities of the UEs having the absoluteTA >= taDiscThreshold is limited to 50 in the L2-PS design for performance reasons.  
if  a new UE has an absoluteTA >= taDiscThreshold, it can not be inserted in this list as long as the list is full.   
it is inserted in the list next time its TA is updated and if the list is not full.

---
